**Page 38 (Left)**

The contempt in which the old government held mercantile pursuits, and the obloquy that attached on merchants and manufacturers, contributed not a little to its embarrassments, and its eventual destruction; and, strange to tell, though the mischiefs arising from this mode of conduct are so obvious, yet an article is proposed for your adoption, which has a manifest tendency to restore a defect inherent in the monarchy.

I shall now proceed to the second article of the fame title, with which I shall conclude my remarks.

The second article says, "Every French soldier, who shall have served one or more campaigns in the cause of liberty, is deemed a citizen of the Republic, without any respect or reference to other qualifications."

It would seem, that in this Article, the Committee were desirous of extricating themselves from a dilemma into which they had been plunged by the preceding article. When men depart from an established principle, they are compelled to resort to trick and subterfuge, always shifting their means to preserve the unity of their objects; and as it rarely happens that the first expedient makes amends for the profusion of principle, they must call in aid a second of a more flagrant nature to supply the deficiency of the former.

In this manner legislators go on accumulating error upon error, and article upon article, until the mast becomes to bulky and incongruous, and their embarrassment to desperate, that they are compelled, as their last expedient, to resort to the very principle they had adopted.—The Committee were precisely in this predicament, when they framed this article; and to now I confess, their conduct appears specious rather than efficacious.

It was not for himself alone, but for his family, that the French citizen, at the dawn of the revolution (for then indeed every man was considered a citizen) marched soldier-like to the frontiers, and repelled a foreign invasion. He did not in his contemplation, that he should enjoy liberty for the residue of his earthly re-

**Page 39 (Right)**

veer, and by his own act preclude his offspring from that ineffaceable blessing. No! He wished to leave it as an inheritance to his children, and that they might hand it down to their latest posterity. If a republican, who united in his person the character of a Soldier and a Citizen, was now to return from the army to his peaceful habitation, he must address his small family in this manner:

"Sorry I am, that I cannot leave to you a small portion of what I have acquired by exposing my person to the ferocity of our enemies, and defeating their machinations. I have established the republic; and, painful the reflection, all the laurels I have won in the field are blasted, and all the privileges to which my exertions have entitled me, extend not beyond the period of my own existence!" Thus it stands at present that the framers of it speculated upon; for in conclusion of the Soldier, they have subjected the Father to the most pungent tentations, by obliging him to adopt a generation of Slaves.

Citizens, a great deal has been urged respecting inheritances. I am confident no man has a greater abhorrence of them than myself, and I am sorry that any insinuations should have been thrown out upon me as a promoter of violence of any kind. The whole tenor of my life and conversation gives the lie to clandestine, and proves me to be a friend to order, truth and justice.

I hope you will attribute this effusion of my sentiments to my anxiety for the honor and success of the revolution. I have no intereft distinct from that which has a tendency to meliorate the situation of mankind. The revolution, as far as it respects myself, has been productive of no lost and perfection than it is not ible for me to describe, or for you to indemnify. But with respect to the [subject] under consideration, I could not refrain from declaring my sentiments.

In my opinion, if you [subvert] the basis of the revolution,

lution, if you dispense with principles, and substitute expedients, you will extinguish that enthusiasm and energy which have hitherto been the life and soul of the revolution; and you will substitute in its place nothing but a cold indifference and self-interest, which will again degenerate into intrigue, cunning, and effeminacy.

But to discard all considerations of a personal and subordinate nature, it is essential to the well-being of the republic, that the practical or organic part of the constitution should correspond with its principles; and as this does not appear to be the case in the plan that has been presented to you, it is absolutely necessary that it should be submitted to the revision of a committee, who should be instructed to compare it with the Declaration of Rights, in order to ascertain the difference between the two, and to make such alterations as shall render them perfectly confident and compatible with each other.

title: Proposed Lottery Scheme, London
description: This printed broadside by Jacob Henriques of London, dated April 1753, proposes a new lottery scheme of 100,000 tickets at four pounds each, to be drawn by quadruple numbers so that 25,000 numbers will determine the lottery. The prize scheme offers 4 prizes of £10,000, 8 of £5,000, 40 of £1,000, 80 of £500, 340 of £100, 800 of £50, 2,000 of £20, and 12,000 of £10 — totalling 15,272 prizes worth £394,000 against 84,728 blanks, a ratio of only five and a half blanks per prize. A further £6,000 goes to the first and last drawn tickets. Twelve per cent is to be deducted from the prizes, paid in money or in 3-per-cent annuities, the proceeds going toward reducing the national debt — the document's stated public purpose, and the pitch by which Henriques, one of many private projectors of the age, sought Parliament's favour.
creator: Jacob Henriques
notes: Jacob Henriques, proposed lottery scheme broadside, London, April 1753. 100,000 tickets at £4 each.
transcription:
# Transcription

THO' I have already taken the Trouble and Pains to fettle the following Lottery Scheme of four Pound each Ticket, that they may have a Choice either for the prefent or the future yet I conceive the fame will be a Detriment to many People ; whereas my publifhed Guinea Lottery Scheme delivered laft Week, can hurt none, but will pleafe every body ; and though there are nine Blanks to a Prize, thofe who will take four Tickets will make it hard only to one and half Blanks a Prize ; befides, that to have one Ticket of four Pound, or four at one Guinea each, is four Chances to one ; and with four Guineas they have the Chance to 4000l. and be a prudent Pattern to fhew the Probability of my publifhed great Propofal for this Nations Welfare and Happinefs, which I have much at heart, &c.

SCHEME

100,000 Tickets (either more or lefs in equal Proportion) at four Pound each Ticket to depofit one Pound, and one Pound every Month afterwards, is 400,000l.

Thofe 100,000 Tickets to be drawn by quadruple Numbers, with quadruple Prizes, fo that 25000 Numbers will determine the whole Lottery, and fave Time, Trouble and Expence.

4 Prizes of     10000 each, is     40000
8               5000              40000
40              1000              40000
80              500               40000
340             100               34000
800             50                40000
2000            20                40000
12000           10                120000

15272 Prizes                amounts to £ 394000
84728 Blanks (is only five and half Blanks to a Prize)

100000 Tickets

For the firft Drawn at 500l. 2000
For the laft Drawn at 1000l. 4000

Amounts to £ 400000

Note, From the Prizes are to be deducted 12 per Cent. for the intended Purpofe, and to be paid either in Money, or in Annuities of 3 per Cent.

My Propofal for leffening the national Debt is real Good, and my Intention for it is ftill better, and if the Parliament and Government are pleafed for the Sake of their Community to encourage me with their prudent public Approbation, I will find out other great Matters for the Glory, Honour, and Welfare of his excellent Majefty and this Kingdom, and which is my chief Intention, moft earneft Defire, and humbly pray for it, &c.

London, April 1753.                JACOB HENRIQUES.

title: Ancient Papyrus Greek or Coptic Financial Document
description: A fragment of ancient papyrus, torn and holed, bearing several lines of cursive script in Greek or Coptic — the everyday administrative hand of Egypt under Ptolemaic, Roman, or Byzantine rule (roughly the third century BCE to the seventh century CE). The content cannot be read with confidence from the fragment, but documents of this form are typically financial or administrative: a tax receipt, grain account, loan agreement, or land lease. Papyri like this are the oldest surviving form of financial record-keeping in the Western tradition, among the earliest physical evidence of organised credit, taxation, and contractual obligation. It is in all likelihood the oldest item in the collection.
creator: Unknown (Egyptian administrative scribal office)
notes: Ancient papyrus fragment with cursive Greek or Coptic script. Likely a financial or administrative document (tax receipt, grain account, or land lease). Ptolemaic, Roman, or Byzantine Egypt, ca. 1st–7th century CE. Oldest item in the collection.
transcription:
# Fragmentary Egyptian Papyrus Document (Greek/Coptic Cursive) — Transcription

## Original:

Script: a rapid documentary cursive of the Greco-Roman/Byzantine Egyptian type. Several letterforms resemble Coptic rather than pure Greek majuscule, so the hand is best described as Greek or Coptic cursive; it cannot be assigned with confidence from this fragment.

Layout: the sheet is torn along the top and holed throughout, laid out in TWO COLUMNS of text. Each column preserves roughly 20–25 lines; the ink is faded and abraded over much of the surface. A modern cataloguer has drawn ink circles/underlines around a few individual words (an editorial annotation, not part of the ancient text).

Legibility: continuous reading is NOT possible. Only disconnected clusters of letters can be made out, and even these are uncertain. No complete word or phrase can be transcribed with confidence.

Tentative isolated letter-sequences (uncertain, offered only as scattered traces — not a running text):
- One line in the upper right column shows a cluster resembling: ο υ μ ν τ … ρ ε υ … (?) [uncertain]
- A circled word in the left column opens with what may be κ ε φ … / ⲕⲉⲫ … (?) [uncertain]

Everything else: [illegible]

Right column: [illegible — ~20–25 lines of cursive, mostly abraded]
Left column: [illegible — ~20–25 lines of cursive, mostly abraded]

---

## English Translation:

The text is not confidently legible. Beyond identifying it as a two-column documentary papyrus in a Greek/Coptic cursive hand, no reliable words or phrases can be read, and therefore no honest translation can be given. The catalogue classification (a Roman-period Egyptian financial/administrative document) is plausible for a papyrus of this form but cannot be confirmed from the surviving, abraded text itself.

title: New York Central Rail Road Company Bond
description: An engraved $1,000 bearer bond of the New York Central Rail Road Company, paying 6% annual interest, issued in August 1858 and later cancelled (struck with a red CANCELLED overprint). The sheet is worked in the fine steel-engraved manner of mid-century American bond production: an allegorical vignette of seated female figures with the national shield and eagle across the top, and a locomotive down the right margin — the railroad advertising its own modernity in the document's very ornament. The New York Central had been formed in 1853 by consolidating the lines linking Albany to Buffalo, and became a flagship of the Vanderbilt railroad empire. Its 6% coupon reflects the high returns American railroads were paying to draw capital in the 1850s; the cancellation marks redemption before the 1869 merger into the New York Central and Hudson River Railroad.
creator: New York Central Rail Road Company
notes: New York Central Rail Road Company. $1,000 bond, 6%. No. 1890. August 1858. CANCELLED.

title: Spanish Colonial Government Payment Order
description: A handwritten libramiento (treasury payment order) of the Spanish colonial Real Hacienda at Lima, dated July 1816, promising payment one year hence of 500 silver pesos (pesos fuertes) drawn against the Empréstito Patriótico — an emergency forced loan levied on the population of Lima to finance the royalist war effort during the South American wars of independence. It carries 5% annual interest, the twenty-five pesos named on its face being that five per cent of the 500-peso principal. The crowned Spanish royal arms and ornate penwork border at the head assert colonial authority on what is otherwise a manuscript instrument, and a later 1817 notation records further processing. Issued from one of the last strongholds of Spanish rule in South America — Peru would not gain independence until 1821 — it is a rare financial trace of the colonial order's final phase.
creator: Spanish Colonial Treasury, Lima (Tesorería de Lima)
notes: Spanish colonial libramento No. 444. Lima, Peru, October 1816. Empréstito Patriótico (forced loan). 500 pesos at 25% annual interest. Additional notation from 1817.

title: Imperial Russian Internal Prize Loan
description: A printed bearer bond of the Imperial Russian 5% Internal Prize Loan of 1864, with a capital value of 100 rubles yielding an annual 5 rubles, set bilingually in Russian (Билетъ внутренняго 5% съ выигрышами займа) and German (Obligation der Russischen 5% Inneren Anleihe mit Prämien-Verlösungen). The Romanov double-headed eagle sits at the centre within finely engraved pink-and-green guilloche — imperial authority joined to the era's anti-counterfeiting ornament. A prize loan (заём съ выигрышами) married a fixed coupon to periodic lottery draws for large cash prizes (выигрыши), a device European governments used to make state debt appealing to small and middling savers. The parallel German text shows the loan was meant to circulate in German financial markets as well as at home.
creator: Imperial Russian Government
notes: Imperial Russian Government Internal 5% Prize Loan 1864. 100 rubles. Series 15871, No. 45. Bilingual Russian/German. Lottery prize-bond format.

title: Thomas C. Jenkins, Wholesale Dealer in Flour & Groceries Invoice to Offutt & Son (Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania)
description: A printed billhead invoice of Thomas C. Jenkins — styling itself "The Largest Flour and Grocery House in the World" — issued at Pittsburgh on 19 December 1902 to Offutt & Son. The engraved letterhead is a piece of corporate boosterism in its own right: a commanding perspective view of the firm's multi-storey "Checkered Front" warehouse on Penn and Liberty Streets, wreathed in advertising for flour, teas, roast coffees, and tobaccos, with a discount table and the insistence "No Goods Sold Families… No Retail Store." The handwritten lines below bill two lots of plug tobacco. As a strictly wholesale house disclaiming any retail trade, Jenkins exemplifies the specialised distribution networks that organised American food commerce in the Gilded Age, when the printed billhead doubled as a tool of corporate self-promotion.
creator: Thomas C. Jenkins (Wholesale Dealer in Flour & Groceries)

title: Holladay Overland Mail and Express Company Blank Check, Virginia City, Montana Territory
description: An unused bank check of the Holladay Overland Mail and Express Company, printed for issue at Virginia City, Montana, and payable on order in dollars. The form is blank: the payee, sum, and date all remain open, the printed date reading only "186_". It bears the imprint of John E. Russell, Esq., 35 William St., New York, as Treasurer and Agent of the company, and was engraved by Slote & Janes of Fulton Street, New York. Ben Holladay's enterprise was the foremost stagecoach and express carrier of the trans-Mississippi West in the 1860s, drawing its revenue from federal mail contracts; Holladay sold it to Wells, Fargo & Co. in 1866. The two vignettes advertise that business and its frontier setting: a galloping mail coach at right, and at left a trapper with a seated companion and dog amid the wilderness the line professed to bridge.
creator: Holladay Overland Mail and Express Company
notes: Blank check No. 224S. Holladay Overland Mail and Express Company. Virginia City, Montana Territory. Signed by John Russell & Co., William, N.Y., Treasurer. Ben Holladay's stagecoach enterprise; sold to Wells Fargo 1866.

title: States of Gelderland (Quarter of Zutphen) Redeemable Bond, Arnhem, c. 1647-1650
description: This printed parchment bond, with its day and year completed by hand, is a redeemable obligation of the Quarter of Zutphen within the province of Gelderland. It opens with the formula of Willem, Prince of Orange (Willem II), as Stadholder, together with the Raden acting in the name of the Heeren Staten, and recites a resolution taken at the Landdag held at Zutphen in April 1644. It invokes the late Frederick Henry, Prince of Orange, described as of laudable memory and "in leven mede Stadtholder, Heer Vader van ons Stadtholder" (in life also Stadholder, father of our Stadholder), which places the actual issue after his death in March 1647. The body promises to pay interest on the principal at six per hundred (6%) yearly, from year to year, until redemption (aflossinge), securing both capital and interest, the sum reckoned in guilders of twenty stivers each. Given at Arnhem and sealed with the great seal countersigned by the Court's clerk, the instrument shows a provincial States borrowing against its tax revenues: the credit of the assembled Estates, not the prince personally, stands behind the loan. The text is unadorned, dominated by an ornate woodcut initial; no art-historical movement is named.
creator: Prince of Orange-Nassau (Willem / Frederick Henry)
notes: Archaic Dutch princely bond/obligation. "Y VVILHELM BY DER GRATIE GODES, Prince van [?]..." References FREDERICUS HENRICX (Frederick Henry), Prince of Orange. Tresorier General and Rentmeester noted. Mid-17th century Dutch Golden Age fiscal document.
transcription:
# Redeemable Bond of the States of Gelderland (Quarter of Zutphen), Arnhem, 1648 — Transcription

## Original:

(Printed archaic Dutch on parchment; a large ornamental woodcut initial fills the upper-left corner. Blanks left in the printed text are filled in by hand — these handwritten insertions are shown here {in curly braces}. Handwriting is difficult; uncertain readings are marked [?] or [illegible].)

**(large ornamental woodcut initial at left)**

**VVILHELM, BY DER GRATIE GODES,**
Printze van Oraignen, &c. Stadtholder, mitsgaders die Raeden uyt den naem van de Heeren Staten des Vor-
stendoms Gelre ende Graefschaps Zutphen, Doen condt in crachte deses, Alsoo 't den wel gedachten Heeren Staeten belieft heeft te resolvieren
op den Lantdach in April 1644 binnen de Stadt Zutphen gehouden, by advijs ende uytsprake van Sijne Hoogheyt, den oock Doorluch-
tigen en Hooghgebooren Vorst ende Heere **FREDERIJCK HENRIJCK,** Printze van Oraignen, &c. Hooghloflijcker memorie,
in leven mede Stadholder, Heer Vader van ons Sadtholder, gedatiert den 13/25 vande voornoemde Maent Aprilis, datmen tot meesten wel-
stant ende reddinghe van den voorsz: Vorstendom ende Graeffschap by jegenwoordige gelegentheyt, doen lichten ende op Interesse negotieren
sal seeckere sommen van penningen, om bekeert ende geemployeert te worden, tot betalinge van der Landtschaps nootelijcke schulden ende
lasten, naerder verhaelt by de voorsz: uytspraecke, ende de voornoemde Resolutie daer op gevolght vande wel gemelde Heeren Staten. Soo
bekennen wy in krachte deses dat {Evert[?] van Beckhoven[?] tot [Cletelborst?]} op huyden dato onderschreven in goeden ganckbaren
gelde, ten behoeve van welgedachte Heeren Staten getelt ende gelevert heeft de somma van {ses hondert vijftich gulden} tot
twintich stuyvers 't stuck. Ende willende mits dien de voorn: {Evert van Beckhoven[?]} sijn erven, ofte Actie van {[geen?]} hebbende
behoorlijck bewaren, Hebben wy in name van welghedachte Heeren Staten, uyt krachte van de voornoemde resolutie van hare Ed: Mog: inhererende de voorghemelde uyt-
spraecke, belooft en beloven mits desen die vooraengetogene summa van {ses hondert vijftich gulden} te verrenten met ses en een oort ten hondert, maeckende jaerlijcx
{veertich guldens[?] ...} te betalen van jaer tot jaer, daer van 't eerste jaer verscheenen sal zijn op den {xvj·[?] [maand — illegible]} des naestvolgende jaers {[illegible handwritten date]}
{[illegible]} ende soo voorts van jaer tot jaer, tot d'aflossinge toe. Daer op wy verseeckeren de voorn: {Evert van Beckhoven[?]}, soo voor 't Capitael als Interesse, mitsgaders
d'onkosten, die by gebreck van prompte betalinge op de voorsz: termijnen, of veerthien dagen daer na onbegrepen tot taxatie van den Hove, na gedane maninge soude mogen com-
men te loopen. Verbindende daer voor alle die gemeyne middelen, Imposten, Verpondingen, ende Comptoiren van ontfange van den voorn: Vorstendom ende Graeffschap,
gheen uytgesondert, ende een voor al, tot verwaringe ende schadeloos holdinge van de voornoemde {Evert van Beckhoven[?] sijn} Erven ofte actie van {[geen?]} heb-
bende, om die selve voor den Hove gedachtes Vorstendoms ende Graeffschaps, in val van misbetalinge, by reele executie te vervolgen, tot effective betalinge toe, sonder dat daer
tegens sal worden gedaen, ofte toegelaten eenighsins gedaen te worden. Tot welcken eynde mits desen van alle exceptien ende beneficien van rechten gerenuncieert wort, Mits
dat soo wel aen d'een als d'ander zijde vry sal staen, soo wel die loesse te doen als 't Capitael wederom te vorderen; Invoegen nochtans dat 't versoeck ende opkondige van de losse
respective een half jaer te vooren gheschiede. Ende dat in sulcken val, als oock vande rente die betalinge gedaen sal worden met goeden ganckbaren ende gepermitteerden gelde.
Willende dat desen onsen brief, Vidimus ofte Copie authentijcq, mitsgaders assignatie op eenigh Comptoir des voornoemden Vorstendoms ende Graeffschap van ons, voor d'eerste
reyse met quitantie, ende daer na quitantie alleenlijck, soo dickwils als 't van nooden sal zijn over gebracht wesende, die betalinge aenden voorn: {Evert van Beckhoven[?]}
geschieden sal, welcke betalinge den Lantrentmeester generael gedachtes Vorstendoms ende Graeffschaps, ofte dien sulcx anders te laste geleyt sal mogen wesen, in uytgave sonder
swarigheyt sal worden geleden; Alles sonder argh of list; In oorkonde hebben wy 't groote Segel gedachtes Vorstendoms ende Graeffschaps onder jegenwoordige verschryvinge
laten hangen, ende deselve door des Hoves Griffier doen beteeckenen. **Gegeven t'Arnhem den** {xvj·[?] [maand — illegible] xvjᶜ· [achtenveertich? = 1648]}

(handwritten signature of the Court's clerk/griffier, lower right:) {C. Luyst[...][?] — illegible flourish}

(archival docket number, lower right corner:) {1648}

---

## English Translation:

(Large ornamental initial at left)

**WILLIAM, BY THE GRACE OF GOD,** Prince of Orange, etc., Stadholder, together with the Councillors, in the name of the Noble Lords the States of the Principality of Guelders and the County of Zutphen, make known by virtue of these presents: Whereas it has pleased the said Noble Lords the States to resolve, at the Diet (Landdag) held at the town of Zutphen in April 1644, by the advice and pronouncement of His Highness, also the most Illustrious and High-born Prince and Lord **FREDERICK HENRY,** Prince of Orange, etc., of laudable memory, in life likewise Stadholder, the Lord Father of our Stadholder — [that pronouncement being] dated the 13/25 of the aforesaid month of April — that, for the greatest welfare and relief of the said Principality and County, in the present circumstances, certain sums of money shall be raised and negotiated at interest, to be applied and employed for the payment of the necessary debts and burdens of the Province, as more fully set forth in the aforesaid pronouncement, and in the said Resolution thereupon following, of the said Noble Lords the States:

So do we acknowledge, by virtue of these presents, that {Evert[?] van Beckhoven[?], of [Cletelborst?]} has, on the day this is subscribed below, in good current money, for the benefit of the said Noble Lords the States, counted out and delivered the sum of {six hundred and fifty guilders}, at twenty stivers the [guilder]. And accordingly, wishing to properly safeguard the said {Evert van Beckhoven[?]}, his heirs, or holders of his claim, we have, in the name of the said Noble Lords the States, by virtue of the aforesaid resolution of their Noble Mightinesses, and adhering to the aforementioned pronouncement, promised and do promise by these presents to pay interest on the said sum of {six hundred and fifty guilders} at six and a quarter per hundred, making yearly {forty guilders[?] ...}, to be paid from year to year, whereof the first year shall fall due on the {[16th?] of [month — illegible]} of the next following year {[illegible date]}, and so forth from year to year, until redemption.

Whereupon we assure the said {Evert van Beckhoven[?]}, as to both the capital and the interest, together with the costs which, in default of prompt payment on the said terms, or within fourteen days thereafter (undefined, subject to assessment by the Court), after due summons, might arise. Binding thereto all the common means, imposts, land-taxes (Verpondingen), and the revenue-offices of the said Principality and County, none excepted, and each and all, for the security and indemnification of the said {Evert van Beckhoven[?]}, his heirs or holders of his claim, so as to prosecute the same before the Court of the said Principality and County, in case of non-payment, by real execution, up to effective payment, without anything being done, or suffered to be done in any way, to the contrary. To which end all exceptions and benefits of law are hereby renounced. Provided that it shall be free to either side, the one as well as the other, both to make redemption and to demand the capital back again; on the understanding, however, that the request and notice of redemption be given respectively half a year beforehand. And that in such case, as also of the rent, the payment shall be made in good current and lawful money.

Willing that this our letter, an authentic vidimus or copy, together with an assignation upon any revenue-office of the said Principality and County by us, for the first time with a receipt (quittance), and thereafter with a receipt only, as often as need be, being delivered over, the payment shall be made to the said {Evert van Beckhoven[?]}; which payment the Land-Receiver-General of the said Principality and County, or whoever else shall be charged therewith, shall have allowed in his account (uytgave) without difficulty. All without fraud or guile. In witness whereof we have caused the great Seal of the said Principality and County to be appended to the present instrument, and the same to be signed by the Court's Clerk (Griffier). **Given at Arnhem, the** {[16th?] [month — illegible], 16{48}}.

(signature of the Court's clerk, illegible)

(archival docket: 1648)

title: Kingdom of Sweden Royal Loan Bond (Amsterdam, 1785)
description: A printed sovereign loan bond of King Gustavus III of Sweden, opening with the royal style "Wy GUSTAVUS, door Gods Genade Koning van Zweeden, der Gothen en Wenden." The Crown raises Twee Millioenen Guldens, or acht maal honderd Duizend Rijksdaalders Hollandsch Courant Geld (two million guilders, equal to 800,000 Rijksdaalders Holland current money), bearing interest at four and a half per cent per year, payable every six months, redeemable after the first year on six months' notice; the whole capital is to be reimbursed within fifteen years reckoned from the first of December. The loan is secured on the effects and revenues of the Crown, backed further by four Swedish State Treasury (Staats Comptoir) obligations of 200,000 Rijksdaalders each deposited as special hypothec by Hope en Compagnie and Fizeaux, Grand en Compagnie, and ranks after an earlier loan of 800,000 Rijksdaalders; it may itself be divided into obligations of smaller amounts for negotiation. It is contracted at Amsterdam through the houses Hope en Compagnie and Fizeaux, Grand en Compagnie, granted under the king's hand at Gripsholm Castle on 20 October 1784 and countersigned by J. Liljencrants. This Dutch sheet is a sworn translation, made at Amsterdam on 9 February 1785 by the sworn translator Johannes Vergeel, of a French original. The instrument shows how Gustavus III drew on the Amsterdam capital market, then the chief lender to northern courts, to fund a treasury strained by his policies. The text is unornamented, its authority resting on the engrossed royal style and the seal rather than on pictorial device.
creator: King Gustav III of Sweden
notes: Swedish royal loan bond, King Gustav III. 400,000 Rijksdaalders Hollandsch Courant. Amsterdam, February 9, 1782. Issued through Amsterdam money market. Johannes Verge, agent/translator. Gustav III borrowed extensively in Amsterdam to finance Swedish policy.
transcription:
# Royal Loan Bond of King Gustavus III of Sweden, Contracted at Amsterdam (Gripsholm, 20 October 1784; sworn Dutch translation, Amsterdam, 9 February 1785) — Transcription

## Original:

**Wy GUSTAVUS, door Gods Genade Koning van Zweeden, der Gothen en Wenden &c. &c. &c., Erf Heer van Noorwegen, Hertog van Schleeswyk, Holstein, Stormarn en Ditmarsen, Grave van Oldenburg, Delmenhorst &c. &c.**

Doen te weeten: Dat wy, goedgevonden hebbende ten welzyn van onze Staaten een Leening te doen van Twee Millioenen Guldens, of acht maal honderd Duizend Ryxdaalders Hollandsch Courant Geld, geevende tot zeekerheid alle de Effecten en Revenuen van de Kroon, welke niet reeds zyn verhypotheequeerd en Speciaal de Inkomsten van onze Douanen en Tollen, aan de Heeren *Hope* en *Compagnie* en *Fizeaux*, *Grand* en *Compagnie*, te Amsterdam, last gegeeven hebben en dezelve uit krachte van deeze tegenwoordige, Volmacht geeven om voor ons en ons Koningryk dezelve Leening te negotieeren.

En vermits wy van bovengemelde Heeren tot ons volkomen genoegen en ten behoeve van ons Koningryk, ontvangen hebben eene Somma van Acht maal Honderd Duizend Ryxdaalders Hollandsch Courant geld, om aan ons te worden geleend, tegens Een Interest van vier en een half ten Honderd Jaarlyks, en voor den tyd gestipuleerd in de Vier Obligatien van ons Staats Comptoir, ieder groot Tweemaal Honderd Duizend Ryxdaalders Hollandsch Courant geld, gedeposeerd by de voorzeide Heeren als Speciaale Hypotheecq van deeze Leening, welke Somma van Achtmaal Honderd Duizend Ryxdaalders Hollandsch Courant geld, aan ons en ons Koningryk deugdlyk is gefourneerd geworden.

Zo verbinden wy ons, voor ons, onze Erfgenaamen en Opvolgers in het Koningryk Zweeden, om niet alleen aan opgemelde Heeren te Amsterdam, alle zes Maanden te doen betaalen de Interessen van bovengemelde Somma van Achtmaal Honderd Duizend Ryxdaalders Hollandsch Courant geld, tegens vier en een half ten Honderd Jaarlyks, maar ook het geheele Capitaal op de vervaltyd dewelke binnen Vyftien Jaaren zyn zal, te rekenen van den Eersten December aanstaande, te doen rembourseeren, ten zy met gezaamentlyke overeenstemming mogte worden goedgevonden, den Termyn van het zelve Remboursement te prolongeeren, in welk laatst geval zulks zes Maanden te vooren zal worden gedeclareerd; ons echter de Faculteit voorbehoudende om deze Leening naa verloop van de Eerste Elf Jaaren geheel of gedeeltelyk te doen rembourseeren.

Teffens permitteren wy mits dezen aan opgemelde Heeren, om de voorgemelde Hoofdsomma in Obligatien van mindere Sommen te verdeelen, en dezelve onder hunne Handtekening aan wien zy zullen goedvinden te vernegotieren, mits dezelve Obligatien door een beëedigd Openbaar Notaris werden gecontrasigneerd, zullende dezelve in zodanig geval van dezelfde kracht en waarde zyn als deeze tegenwoordige Hoofd Obligatie, zodanig, dat aan de Eigenaars of Houders van dezelve Obligatien van zes tot zes Maanden ten Comptoire van bovengemelde Heeren zal worden betaald de Interest tegens vier en een half ten Honderd Jaarlyks, en by expiratie van de vervaltyd der Leening, het Capitaal zelve, ten waare dat men wegens een Ulterieure Prolongatie mogte zyn overeengekomen, alles zonder eenige korting.

Tot zekerheid van het Remboursement dezer Geldleening, zo wel ten opzichte van het Capitaal als de Interessen, verbinden en verhypothequeeren wy alle de Effecten en Revenuen van ons Koningryk, welke niet reeds zyn verhypothequeerd, van wat naam en Hoedanigheid dezelve ook mogen zyn en Speciaal de Inkomsten van onze Douanen en Tollen, en daar en boven de voorschreven vier Obligatien van ons Staats Comptoir, ieder groot Tweemaal Honderd Duizend Ryxdaaldaars, Hollandsch Courant geld, Authoriseerende de meergedachte Heeren, hunne Erfgenaamen en Opvolgers, ingeval van non betaaling van het Capitaal of de Interessen op Plaats en Termyn voorschreeven, als dan direct de gemelde Obligatien van ons Staats Comptoir te verkoopen en vernegotieeren tot de Concurrentie van het gunt hen verschuldigd zyn zal.

Na dat het Capitaal en de Interessen dezer Leening, van Achtmaal Honderd Duizend Ryxdaalders Hollandsch Courant geld, volgens den Inhoud van deeze tegenwoordige Hoofd-Obligatie door ons geheel zullen zyn Gerembourseerd, zo zal deeze zelve Obligatie mitsgaders de Obligatien van ons Staats Comptoir voor deze Leening Special verhypothequeerd, aan ons en onze Kroon, vry en ontslagen van alle verbintenis hoe genaamd, moeten worden te rug gegeeven en overgeleverd.

Approberende wy voor het overige al het geene meergedachte Heeren zullen nodig vinden by deze Leenings Negotiatie, ten beste en voordeele van ons Koningryk, overeenkomstig onze hier voren uitgedrukte Intentien, te reguleeren.

Ten Oirkonde waarvan, wy deeze tegenwoordige Authorisatie en Obligatie eigenhandig hebben geteekend, en ons Koninglyk Zegel daar by doen Apposeeren.

Gedaan op het Kasteel Gripsholm, deezen Twintigsten October des Jaars Een Duizend Zeven Honderd Vier en Tachtig.

(Was getekend)
GUSTAF. (L. S.)

(Lager was getekend)
J. LILJENCRANTS.

Accordeerd met het Fransch Origineel getekend N°. 4155. ne varietur.

Amsterdam den 9 February 1785.

JOHANNES VERGEEL Luc. Zn.
*Beëedigd Translateur.*

---

## English Translation:

**We GUSTAVUS, by the Grace of God King of Sweden, of the Goths and Wends &c. &c. &c., Hereditary Lord of Norway, Duke of Schleswig, Holstein, Stormarn and Dithmarschen, Count of Oldenburg, Delmenhorst &c. &c.**

Be it known: That we, having deemed it good for the welfare of our States to contract a Loan of Two Million Guilders, or eight times one hundred thousand Rijksdaalders in Dutch Current Money, giving as security all the Effects and Revenues of the Crown, which are not already mortgaged, and especially the Incomes of our Customs and Tolls, have given full charge and power to the Messrs. Hope and Company and Fizeaux, Grand and Company, at Amsterdam, and by virtue of these presents do grant them full authority to negotiate the said Loan for us and our Kingdom.

And whereas we have received from the aforesaid Messrs., to our full satisfaction and for the benefit of our Kingdom, a Sum of Eight times One Hundred Thousand Rijksdaalders Dutch Current Money, to be lent to us, at an Interest of four and one half per Hundred yearly, and for the term stipulated in the Four Obligations of our State Treasury (Staats Comptoir), each amounting to Twice One Hundred Thousand Rijksdaalders Dutch Current Money, deposited with the aforesaid Messrs. as Special Security (Hypothec) of this Loan — which Sum of Eight times One Hundred Thousand Rijksdaalders Dutch Current Money has been duly furnished to us and our Kingdom.

Thus we bind ourselves, for ourselves, our Heirs and Successors in the Kingdom of Sweden, not only to cause to be paid to the aforesaid Messrs. at Amsterdam, every six Months, the Interest on the abovementioned Sum of Eight times One Hundred Thousand Rijksdaalders Dutch Current Money, at four and one half per Hundred yearly, but also the whole Capital at the maturity which shall fall within Fifteen Years, reckoned from the coming First of December, to be reimbursed — unless by mutual agreement it should be found good to prolong the term of the said Reimbursement, in which latter case such shall be declared six Months beforehand; reserving to ourselves, however, the Faculty to reimburse this Loan wholly or in part after the lapse of the first Eleven Years.

At the same time we hereby permit the aforesaid Messrs. to divide the abovementioned Principal Sum into Obligations of smaller amounts, and to negotiate the same, under their Signature, to whomsoever they shall think fit, provided that the said Obligations be countersigned by a sworn Public Notary; in which case the same shall be of the same force and value as this present Principal Obligation, so that to the Owners or Holders of the said Obligations there shall be paid, from six to six Months at the Office of the abovementioned Messrs., the Interest at four and one half per Hundred yearly, and at the expiration of the maturity of the Loan, the Capital itself — unless an Ulterior Prolongation should have been agreed upon — all without any deduction.

For the security of the Reimbursement of this Money-loan, both as regards the Capital and the Interest, we bind and mortgage all the Effects and Revenues of our Kingdom, which are not already mortgaged, of whatever name and quality they may be, and especially the Incomes of our Customs and Tolls, and furthermore the aforesaid four Obligations of our State Treasury, each amounting to Twice One Hundred Thousand Rijksdaalders Dutch Current Money, authorizing the aforesaid Messrs., their Heirs and Successors, in case of non-payment of the Capital or the Interest at the place and term aforesaid, then directly to sell and negotiate the said Obligations of our State Treasury up to the amount of what shall be owed to them.

After the Capital and the Interest of this Loan, of Eight times One Hundred Thousand Rijksdaalders Dutch Current Money, according to the tenor of this present Principal Obligation, shall have been wholly Reimbursed by us, then this same Obligation, together with the Obligations of our State Treasury specially mortgaged for this Loan, shall have to be returned and delivered up to us and our Crown, free and discharged of every obligation whatsoever.

Approving for the rest all that the aforesaid Messrs. shall find necessary to arrange in this Loan Negotiation, for the best advantage of our Kingdom, in conformity with our intention expressed above.

In witness whereof, we have signed this present Authorization and Obligation with our own hand, and caused our Royal Seal to be affixed thereto.

Done at Gripsholm Castle, this Twentieth of October in the Year One Thousand Seven Hundred Eighty-four.

(Was signed)
GUSTAF. (L. S.)

(Below was signed)
J. LILJENCRANTS.

Agreeing with the French Original signed No. 4155, ne varietur.

Amsterdam, the 9th of February 1785.

JOHANNES VERGEEL, son of Lucas.
*Sworn Translator.*

title: Potosian Land Grant (Reverse)
description: This is the reverse of a Potosian Land Grant certificate conveying 400 acres, issued in London circa 1825 by a British company promoting mineral and land ventures in the Potosí region following Spanish America's wars of independence. The certificate denominated landholding in acres rather than in pounds, packaging speculative title to distant ground as a transferable instrument; the engraved face here serves chiefly to carry handwritten endorsements and initialled signatures by which the grant passed from hand to hand. The printed heraldic device dominates: a crowned shield charged with a saltire, flanked by two rampant horses and a pair of flags, set above the Latin mottoes NESCIUS VINCI ('not knowing how to be conquered') and PRO LIBERTATE DEFENDENDA SOCIORUM ('for defending the liberty of the partners'). The borrowed chivalric arms lend a fictive antiquity and respectability to a venture floated amid the London mania for South American mining shares.
creator: Potosian Land Grant Company (London)
notes: Potosian Land Grant, reverse/back side of 400-acre certificate. Shows printed header with coat of arms and handwritten endorsements. Complements front face (goetzmann0506). London, ca. 1825. British speculative investment in Bolivian Potosí region.

title: Bourbon Princes in Exile Loan Certificate, Amsterdam, 1793
description: This is a bond certificate raising a loan in the name of the exiled French Bourbon princes. Its authorization, given at Hamm in Westphalia on 25 September 1793 and dated to the first year of the King's reign, bears the names of Louis Stanislas Xavier (the future Louis XVIII) and Charles Philippe, Comte d'Artois, countersigned by the Maréchal Duc de Broglie as Ministre d'État (Minister of State), with a separate signature at the foot, "Par ordre de leurs Altesses Royales," by the Garde du Trésor Royal. The Amsterdam house of J. Bourcourd and the widow E. Croese & Comp. acknowledges, on behalf of the Princes of France, receipt of een duizend guldens Hollands Courant (f. 1,000 Dutch current guilders) for a share in the obligation, with coupons attached. Drawn at Amsterdam on 28 September 1793, the instrument shows the royalist cause, deprived of French revenues after the execution of Louis XVI in January 1793, seeking credit in the deep capital market of the Dutch Republic. The plain engraved text, free of heraldry or vignette, lends the émigré loan the sober appearance of an ordinary Amsterdam obligation.
creator: Louis Stanislas Xavier (Louis XVIII); Charles Philippe (Comte d'Artois); Maréchal Duc de Broglie
notes: French émigré princes' bond No. 34, f. 1,000 Dutch Guilders. Authorized by Louis Stanislas Xavier (future Louis XVIII) and Charles Philippe (Comte d'Artois). Duc de Broglie, Hamm en Westphalie, September 25, 1793. Amsterdam, J. Bourcourd & Wedowe F. Croese & Comp., September 28, 1793. French Royalist exile finance.
transcription:
# Transcription

## Original Text (French and Dutch):

DONNÉ À HAMM EN WESTPHALIE (sous nos sceaux ordinaires & tius le contrefacing du Maréchal Duc de Broglie, Ministre affEtat, le vingt cinquième jour de Septembre l'an de Grace Mille fait cent quatrevingt treize, & du Regne du Roi le premier.

LOUIS STANISLAS XAVIER,
CHARLES PHILIPPE.

(L. S.)

LE MARECHAL, DUC DE BROGLIE.

---

N° 34

Wy ondergestechtevene I. BOURCOURD en Weduwe E: CROEESE & COMP., als daar toe, gelyck in vorenstaande Obligatie breeder staat gemeld, geauthhoriseert zynde, bekennen voor rekening van hunne Koninglycke Hoogheden de Prineen van Vrankryk, ontfangen te hebben uit handen van

de Somma van EEN DUIZEND GULDENS Hollands Courant voor Aandeel in dezelve Obligatie, en zyn de Coupons hiertoe behorende geteekend

*Actum Amsterdam, den 28. September; 1793.*

ƒ 1000:--:

Deze recontrafiaerd en ten Prohocolle aangetekend, belopende de door my getekende Aandeelen niet boven het getal van Twaaif Duyzend Nummers. En zyn de *Lettres Patentes* dato 7 September 1793, benevens de-Originele Obligatie van dato 25 September 1793 gedeponeerd en/ berustenде onder my.

Par ordre de leur Altesses Royales.

Garde du Tréfor Royal.

## English Translation:

GIVEN AT HAMM IN WESTPHALIA (under our ordinary seals & under the countersigning of the Marshal Duke of Broglie, Minister of State, on the twenty-fifth day of September in the year of Grace One Thousand Seven Hundred Ninety-three, & of the Reign of the King the first.

LOUIS STANISLAS XAVIER,
CHARLES PHILIPPE.

(L. S.)

THE MARSHAL, DUKE OF BROGLIE.

N° 34

We undersigned I. BOURCOURD and Widow E: CROESE & COMP., as thereto authorized, as appears stated more fully in the foregoing Obligation, hereby acknowledge receipt for the account of Their Royal Highnesses the Princes of France, from the hands of

the Sum of ONE THOUSAND GUILDERS Dutch Currency for a share in the said Obligation, and the Coupons belonging hereto are signed

*Done in Amsterdam, the 28th of September; 1793.*

ƒ 1000:--:

This is re-authenticated and registered in the Protocol, the number of Shares signed by me not exceeding the total of Twelve Thousand Numbers. And the *Letters Patent* dated 7 September 1793, together with the Original Obligation dated 25 September 1793 are deposited and rest in my custody.

By order of Their Royal Highnesses.

title: Ricardo & De Lara Forward Contract for British Consolidated Stock
description: This printed-and-completed forward sale contract, in Dutch, is issued on a form supplied by the brokers Ricardo en De Lara, on the Weesperveld over het Werkhuis, Amsterdam. The undersigned acknowledges having sold £4,000 sterling capital of the Geconsolideerde 3 pct. Annuities of England (British Consols) at a price of 59½, these being remainders of the dividend (Uitdeling) issued the previous January, for delivery in London at the coming August Rescontre (settlement) at the named price. Intervening dividends accrue to the buyer and are deducted from the price; all terms follow the Reglement of December 1803. Done at Amsterdam, 13 May 1805, with the seller's signature. The form documents Amsterdam's role as a forward market in British government debt during the Napoleonic wars, when Consols traded near 59 and Dutch capital priced and dealt in London stock across the North Sea. The sheet is plainly utilitarian: an engraved italic heading and ruled lines, no allegory, the commercial instrument as its own ornament.
creator: Ricardo & De Lara (Amsterdam)
notes: Amsterdam forward contract for British Consolidated Stock (Consols). Ricardo en De Lara, No. 70 Amsterdam. Governed by December 1803 Reglement. Amsterdam, May 13, 1805. Ricardo family Amsterdam firm; Anglo-Dutch financial intermediation.

# Ricardo & De Lara Forward-Sale Contract for British Consolidated 3% Annuities — Transcription

## Original:

(Engraved italic heading, two lines, centered:)

**Deze CONTRACTEN zyn te bekomen by RICARDO en DE LARA op het
Weesperveld over het Werkhuis, N°. 70. Te Amsterdam.**

(Printed body with handwritten insertions shown in [ ]:)

Ondergetekende bekenne Verkogt te hebben aan [d'Heer *handwritten:* H:L: Louvrix] [Vier Duyzend —] Ponden Sterlings Capitaal Actien van de [Geconſolideerde 3 pC. Ann. —] van Engeland, ten Pryze van [Negen en vyftig en een Half — 59½] zynde Restanten van de Uitdeling, uitgegeven in de Maand [Januarij —] laatstl., welken [4000 —] aanneme, mits daarentegen de Rekening te geven, op de Rescontre van [Augustus] aankomende te laten Leveren, binnen London tot den gemelden Prys. En, zo middelerwyl eenige Uitdelingen gegeven worden, zullen dezelven zyn ten profyte van den Koper, en tot afkorting van den bovengemelden prys, en in tegendeel alle Bylagen en Inroepingen ten zynen laste, naar den gemeenen styl: alles op zodanige voorwaarden, als by het Reglement in de Maand December 1803 gearresteerd, staan vermeld, waarnaar Ondergetekende aanneeme zig te gedragen, en voorts ter goeder trouwe.

Gedaan in Amsterdam den [*handwritten:* 13 Mey] A°. 1805

(Handwritten signature with heavy flourish, lower right — [illegible])

## English Translation:

(Engraved heading:)

**These CONTRACTS may be obtained from RICARDO and DE LARA on the
Weesperveld, opposite the Workhouse, No. 70. In Amsterdam.**

The undersigned acknowledges having sold to [Mr. H:L: Louvrix] [Four Thousand —] Pounds Sterling capital Stock of the [Consolidated 3 per Cent. Annuities —] of England, at the price of [Fifty-nine and one Half — 59½], being remainders of the Dividend distributed in the month of [January —] last, which [4,000 —] he accepts, provided that on the other hand the account be rendered, to be delivered at the coming [August] Rescontre (settlement) within London at the aforesaid price. And if in the meantime any dividends are distributed, they shall be to the benefit of the Buyer, and in reduction of the abovementioned price; and on the contrary all Calls and Reclamations shall be at his charge, according to common practice: all upon such conditions as are set out, fixed in the Regulation of the month of December 1803, whereto the undersigned undertakes to conform, and further in good faith.

Done in Amsterdam on the [13 May] A.D. 1805

title: Conditions of the Daniel Changuion Plantation Loan, Essequibo and Demerara, 1816
description: Printed conditions (Conditien van Negotiatie) of a loan fund raised under the direction of Daniel Changuion for planters in the Rio Essequebo and Rio Demmerary, dated 1816. The heading sets the terms: a total sum of f 400,000 in Dutch guilders, advanced for ten years at 6 per cent interest yearly, to continue and improve their plantations. The articles that follow establish the security. Borrowers must have their estates valued by sworn appraisers — grounds, buildings, works, and the enslaved people ("Slaven en Slavinnen"), excluding furniture and movables — and the resulting inventory and mortgage deed (Verbandbrief) registered with the colony's secretary. Against this, a planter is advanced at most five-eighths of the appraised value. The document reveals how Dutch colonial plantation finance mortgaged enslaved labour itself as collateral, and how subscribers' capital was channelled into Guiana's sugar economy. Plain administrative typography; no pictorial ornament.
creator: Daniel Changuion
notes: Plantation negotiatie for Essequibo and Demerara (now Guyana) planters; f.400,000 at 6% for 10 years; Article 1 of conditions text; see related title page
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original:**

Conditien van Negotiatie, tot
cen Fonds waar uit, onder Directie van
DANIEL CHANGUION, aan cenige
Planters in Rio Essequebo en Rio Demmerary, tot voorzetting en verbeete-
ring hunner Plantagien een Somma van
ƒ 400000:- voor 10 Jaaren zal werden
gefourneert, teegens den Intreft van 6
pC⁰. 's Jaars.

**ART. 1.**

De Planters in opgemelde Colonien, welke geneegen zyn eenige Gelden ter verbeete-ringe en voorzetting hunner Plantagien op te neemen, en daar toe door den Directeur zyn geaccepteerd, zullen gehouden zyn ten hunnen kosten, gemelde Plantagien door be-diedige Prisfeurs doorluyrig te laaten Inventariseeren, 200 te Grondstoe beboord, met uitsluitinge van Meubelen en andere Zaken, onnodig tot het be-werken van een Plantagie, en den volien en onbelesten Eygendom van dien Inventaris door hun beboeld, door gemelde Taxateurs doen in waarde brengten.

**2.**

Den Inventaris al hier voren bepaald, opgemaakt en geprijceert weesnde, zal aan Daniel Changuion, moeten werden gezonden, beneevens de behooflyke Hypotheeck of Verbandbrier, waar uit zal moeten blijken dat deselve Hypotheeck, ter Secretary van de Colonie behoorfyk gereustreerd is, en dat geen andere Speciale of Generale hypotheeck op deselve Effecten zyn leggenda, als miede dat geen verbauden... [illegible section]... in deeds.

**3.**

Aan de Planters welke den Inventaris en Verbandbriof als boven zullen overmaken, zal worden gelooten de helft of uyterlyck vyf agtde der waarde waar op derzelvér Plantagien, met hun toebehooren, zyn geteixed, teegens den Intreft van Ses pCento in 't jaar, die zy zig verbinden jaarlyks boven alle andere Lasten te voldoen, op Paene in de Verbandbrier vermeld.

**4.**

De Geld-opneemers, welke Jaarlyks met Valaable bewyzen kunnen aantconen, dat hunne bezittingen daar door zyn verbeeterdt, zullen vryheid hebben, op een nieuwe Prisfatie als boven genaeld te doen, van het meerder bedragen der waarde, nog op te neemen de helft van de verbeeteringe, mits dezelve aan voorn: Daniel Changuion mede Veryhpothequeerde.

**5.**

De Respective Planters Geld-opneemers, verbinden zig alle hunne zendende Producten te addresseeren aan, nodige Provitien te ontbieden van, en brieven te trecken op Daniel Changuion, als miede by afzending hunner Producten, aan hem in te gee boorflyke opgaave ter besorging der noodige afflurantie te geeven, met overzichte dat in gevoalle zy daar aan niet mogten voldoen of ook hunne Producten in de respective Colonien Verkoopen, of op andere wyze te divergere, en in ghebroken blijven jaarlyks hunne Intreft te voldoen, het by hun genoog Capital met de Intraffen direckteyк zal konnen worden opgeeyschr, lyk ten kolten van zodainge Planters, by Executie verhault, aan hunne Personen, Plantagien niet derselver toebebooren, en verdere zoo roerends als onroerende Goederen, zig hier toe voor de Hove van Justitie in de Colonie, of waar het anders zonde mogen verreycscht werden, by vrywillige Condemnatie verbindendc.

**6.**

De Geldgeeverz behoulen aan zig de vryheid, zodanige en zo veele Persoenen in de Rivieren van Essequebo en Demerary aan te fallen en te qualificeeren, als zy zul-

[Page continues with "A" at bottom]

---

# English translation:

**Conditions of Negotiation for a Fund under the Direction of DANIEL CHANGUION, for certain Planters in Rio Essequebo and Rio Demerara, for the continuation and improvement of their Plantations, a Sum of ƒ400,000 for 10 Years shall be furnished, at an Interest of 6% per annum.**

**ARTICLE 1.**

The Planters in the aforementioned Colonies, who are inclined to take up certain Monies for the improvement and continuation of their Plantations, and who are accepted for this purpose by the Director, shall be bound to cause said Plantations to be inventoried at their own cost by competent Appraisers, namely with respect to the Land, buildings, with the exclusion of Furniture and other Articles unnecessary to the working of a Plantation, and the full and unencumbered Ownership of such Inventory shall be brought to value by the aforementioned Assessors.

**2.**

The Inventory as determined above, having been drawn up and assessed, shall be sent to Daniel Changuion, together with the necessary Deed of Hypothecation, from which it must appear that such Hypothecation has been properly registered at the Secretary of the Colony, and that no other Special or General hypothecation on such Effects is in place [illegible section].

**3.**

To the Planters who shall submit the Inventory and Deed of Hypothecation as above, shall be loaned half, or at most five-eighths of the value upon which their Plantations, with their appurtenances, are assessed, at an Interest of Six percent per annum, which they bind themselves to pay yearly in addition to all other Charges, on penalty as mentioned in the Deed of Hypothecation.

**4.**

The Money-borrowers, who can annually prove by means of Valuable Certificates that their possessions have thereby been improved, shall have the liberty to have a new Appraisal made as above of the additional value, and to take up half of the improvement, provided the same be hypothecated to: Daniel Changuion.

**5.**

The Respective Planters Money-borrowers bind themselves to address all their produce to, offer necessary Provisions from, and draw Bills on Daniel Changuion; and at the time of sending their Produce, to furnish him with a proper account for the arrangement of the necessary supply, with the proviso that if they should fail therein, or should sell their Produce in the respective Colonies in any other manner, and should fall behind in paying their annual Interest, the Capital with the Interest may be immediately collected from their Persons, Plantations with all appurtenances, and all other moveable and immoveable Goods, before the Court of Justice in the Colony, or wherever it may be required, by voluntary Condemnation.

**6.**

The Money-lenders reserve to themselves the liberty to appoint such and as many Persons in the Rivers of Essequebo and Demerara and to qualify them as they...

title: Gemeente Warnsveld Municipal Loan Certificate
description: This Bewijs van Aandeel (certificate of share) represents a f.250 participation in a f.12,000 loan charged to the Gemeente (municipality) of Warnsveld in the Dutch province of Gelderland, bearing interest of four and a half per cent yearly, payable against surrender of attached coupons. The loan rested on resolutions of the municipal council of 16 September and 21 October 1882, approved by the Provincial States of Gelderland on 25 October 1882 for a maximum of f.14,000. Given at Warnsveld on 30 April 1883 over the signatures of the burgemeester and wethouders (mayor and aldermen). Redemption proceeds by annual lottery: one share drawn each of the first twelve years, two in the following ten, three thereafter, paid the next 30 April at the municipal receiver's office, after which interest ceases. Such small-town borrowing shows how Dutch municipalities funded local works through subscribed shares rather than a single lender. A BETAALD (paid) cancellation marks its eventual redemption; the plain typographic sheet bears only the municipal arms.
creator: Gemeente Warnsveld
notes: Municipal loan; f.12,000 total; f.250 denomination; 4.5% interest; lottery redemption; Warnsveld, Gelderland
transcription:
# ORIGINAL TEXT:

BEWIJS van AANDEEL

IN DE

GELDLEENING

groot f 12,000,

ten laste der Gemeente WARNSVELD,

vastgesteld bij besluiten van den Gemeenteraad van den 16 September 1882 en van den 21 October d.a.a., goedgekeurd door Heeren Afgeputeerde Staten van Gelderland bij besluit van den 25 October 1882, nr. 29 [or 39] voor het bedrag van hoogstens f 14,000.

BURGEMEESTER en WETHOUDERS der gemeente WARNSVELD verklaren hierbij aan toonder schuldig te zijn de somma van **Twee honderd vijftig Gulden, f 250,—** als aandeel in de bovengenoemde geldleening en zulks tegen eene rente van vier en een half ten honderd in het jaar, te voldoen op en tegen inlevering van bijgevoegde coupons.

De eerste twaalf jaren zal telken jare een aandeel worden uitgoloot, de daarop volgende tien jaren twee aandeelen en de overige jaren drie aandeelen, bij loting te voren aan te wijzen.

De uitgelote aandeelen zullen op den dertigsten April na de uitloting ten kantore van den gemeente-ontvanger worden uitbetaald, tegen intrekking van dit bewijs en de onverschenen interest-coupons.

Zullende de rente der uitgelote aandeelen niet langer loopen dan tot den Dertigsten April van het jaar waarin de uitloting heeft plaats gehad.

Gegeven te WARNSVELD den 30 April 1883.

Burgemeester en Wethouders voornoemd,

[Signatures]

Burgemeester.
Wethouder.

---

# ENGLISH TRANSLATION:

CERTIFICATE OF SHARE

IN THE

MUNICIPAL LOAN

amounting to f 12,000,

charged to the Municipality of WARNSVELD,

established by resolutions of the Municipal Council of September 16, 1882 and October 21 of the same year, approved by the Appointed Representatives of the States of Gelderland by resolution of October 25, 1882, no. 29 [or 39] for the amount of at most f 14,000.

BURGEMEESTER [Mayor] and WETHOUDERS [Aldermen] of the municipality of WARNSVELD hereby declare themselves indebted to the bearer in the amount of **Two hundred fifty Guilders, f 250,—** as a share in the aforementioned municipal loan and at an interest rate of four and one half percent per year, payable upon and against submission of the attached coupons.

During the first twelve years, one share shall be drawn by lot each year; during the following ten years, two shares; and during the remaining years, three shares, to be determined beforehand by lottery.

The drawn shares shall be paid at the office of the municipal receiver on the 30th of April following the drawing, against surrender of this certificate and the unsubmitted interest coupons.

The interest on the drawn shares shall cease to accrue as of the 30th of April of the year in which the drawing takes place.

Given at WARNSVELD on April 30, 1883.

Burgemeester and Wethouders aforementioned,

- Stamps/seals visible (partially illegible circular stamps on document)

title: Stolkert Surinam Plantation Mortgage to Du Plessis & Taunay, Amsterdam, 1777
description: A notarial copy (Copia), opening a deed of mortgage (Acte van Hypotheecq) passed before Cornelis van Homrigh, public notary at Amsterdam, on 29 December 1777. It records a transaction first executed on 22 July 1777 before the council of civil justice of the colony of Surinam: Fredrik Cornelis Stolkert, owner of one-third of the named plantations, acting also for the owners of the remaining two-thirds (Isaac Stolkert, Hendrik Boudewyn Stolkert, and Dorothea Elizabeth Werner), acknowledges a debt of 320,000 guilders Holland current money to the Amsterdam merchants Reynier Isaac du Plessis and Jan Fredrik Taunay. Of this, 108,000 guilders was newly advanced and 212,000 guilders redeemed an earlier mortgage. The principal is repayable over fifteen years from 10 April 1777 at five per hundred yearly interest, the creditors to serve as sole correspondents and consignees for the plantations' produce sold at Amsterdam. The deed binds the estates as security and obliges the debtor to maintain a sufficient number of enslaved labourers, replacing the dead, maimed, aged, and runaways. The page breaks off before the plantations are named, the upright notarial hand reflecting Amsterdam's role financing colonial estates.
creator: Cornelis van Homrigh (Notaris); Fredrik Cornelis Stolkert; Reynier Isaac du Plessis en Jan Fredrik Taunay
notes: Amsterdam notarial copy; Plantation Paaschal, Dominica; Heshuysen & Comp. / James Balmer; mortgage £7,100; life annuities £1,269.10/yr; notary Hermanus de Wolff Junior; December 21, 1777
transcription:
# Copy of a Deed of Mortgage on Surinam Plantations — Fredrik Cornelis Stolkert to Reynier Isaac du Plessis and Jan Fredrik Taunay, Amsterdam, 29 December 1777 — Transcription

## Original:

**Copia.**

(Faint pencil annotations across the top margin, largely illegible; the figure "1000 fl" and a bracketed "(1000 fl)" are discernible, with further illegible pencil notes. At upper right an embossed tax stamp reading **ZEGEL 6** with a crowned lion coat of arms, and a handwritten flourish/signature.)

Op den negen en twintigsten December des jaars zéventien honderd zeven en zeventig, compareerden voor my CORNELIS VAN HOMRIGH, openbaar Notaris te Amsteldam, by den Hove van Holland geadmitteerd, De Heeren REYNIER ISAAC DU PLESSIS en JAN FREDRIK TAUNAY, Kooplieden wonende binnen deze Stad.

Te kennen gevende: Dat by Acte van Hypotheecq op den 22sten July dezes Jaars 1777. ten overstaan van de Edele Achtbaare Heeren CHRISTOFFEL NAGEL en HENDRIK ROTARIUS, Raden in den Hove van Civile Justitie der Colonie van Surinamen, &c. &c. door den Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, zo voor zich, als Eigenaar voor een derde part der na te meldene Plantagien, mitsgaders als, ingevolge twee onderscheidene by de zelve Acte van Hypotheecq vermelde Procuratien, Gemagtigde zo van den Heer ISAAC STOLKERT, als mede van den Heer HENDRIK BOUDEWYN STOLKERT, en Vrouwe DOROTHEA ELIZABETH WERNER, Echtelieden, als Eigenaren der overige twee derde parten van de zelve Plantagien, gepasseerd, en ten zelven dage met condemnatie van welgemelden Hove bekragtigd, dezelve Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, alzo in zyn privé en nog in opgemelde zyne qualiteit, en dus ook in de namen der voornoemde Constituanten heeft bekend, respectivelyk te zamen en ieder in het byzonder en voor het geheel, zonder eenige smaldeeling, onder behoorlyke renunciatie, wel en deugdelyk schuldig te zyn aan ende ten behoeven van hen Heeren Comparanten, een Somma van Driemaal honderd en twintig duizend Guldens Hollands Courant Geld, spruitende, eerstelyk uit hoofde en ter zake van een Somma van Een honderd en agt duizend Guldens, Hollands Courant Geld, door hen Heeren Comparanten ten behoeven van hem Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qualitatequa opgeschoten en verstrekt; en wyders van een Somma van Twee honderd en twaalf duizend Guldens, Hollands Courant Geld, door hen Heeren Comparanten mede ten behoeven van hem Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qualitatequa uit te schieten ter voldoening van het Hypotheecq ten behoeven van de Heeren P. HARTSINCK, S. DU PLESSIS, J. W. PICHOT en A. WALLYEN gepasseerd, (zo als ook de zelve Somma van Twee honderd en twaalf duizend Guldens, door hen Heeren Comparanten uitgeschoten, en dienvolgende ook het evengezegde Hypotheecq geroyeerd is,) Hebbende dezelve Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qualitatequa derhalven afstand gedaan van de exceptie van onaangetelden Gelde of niet genoegzame waarde genoten te hebben, En wyders beloofd de in voegen voorschreven opgenomen en verschuldigde Capitale Somma van Driemaal honderd en twintig duizend Guldens Hollands Courant Geld, aan hen Heeren Comparanten of derzelver regt verkrygende kost- en schadeloos binnen deze Stad te zullen restitueren en voldoen over vyftien Jaren, gerekend zederd den 10den April 1777. en inmiddels ook binnen deze Stad de Interessen te betalen op het verschynen van ieder Jaar, ook gerekend zederd den zelven 10den April 1777. tegens vyf ten honderd in het Jaar, vrygeld, zonder eenige korting hoe genaamd, en zulks tot de volle en effectueele voldoening van het Capitaal toe, Des dat aan hem Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, privé en qq., zal vrystaan, om de voorschreven Capitale Somma eerder, dan de gezegde vyftien Jaren af te leggen in termynen, het zy met het eindigen van het zesde Jaar, en zo vervolgens Jaarlyks met tien Percent, ofte met het eindigen van het elfde Jaar, en zo vervolgens ook Jaarlyks met twintig Percent, en dat in beiden gevallen wel meerder, maar niet minder, en mits van zodanige vroegere aflossing zes Maanden te voren te waarschouwen; Met verdere beloften, dat zy Heeren Comparanten of derzelver Opvolgers in hun Comptoir van Negotie, geduurende de voormelde tyd en tot de dadelyke aflossing van het voorschrevene geheele Capitaal toe, zullen zyn de eenige Correspondenten van de na te meldene Plantagien, en dat mitsdien aan hen of derzelver gedagte Opvolgers van tyd tot tyd alle de Producten en Gewasschen van dezelve Plantagien zullen werden verzonden en geconsigneerd, ten einde alhier te Amsteldam door de Heeren Comparanten of derzelver Opvolgers in hun Comptoir van Negotie te werden verkogt, zonder iets het allerminste aldaar in de Colonie te mogen verkopen of aan iemand anders, of na eenige andere plaatzen te mogen afzenden of verdebiteren, direct nog indirect; Dat ook van het afzenden der gemelde Producten en Gewasschen van tyd tot tyd behoorlyke en tydige advisen aan hen Heeren Comparanten of derzelver gedagte Opvolgers zullen werden gegeven, tot het doen der Assurantien waar toe zy Heeren Comparanten by dezelve Acte van Hypotheecq speciaal zyn gequalificeerd, en des noods op Schip of Schepen, gelyk mede alle Provisien en noodwendigheden van tyd tot tyd ten behoeven van de na te meldene Plantagien, en het onderhoud der zelven vereischt werdende, van hen Heeren Comparanten of derzelver gedagte Opvolgers, en van niemand anders zullen werden ontboden, en dat alles op den voet der gewone Provisien volgens Costume, na aftrek van welke Provisien uit het zuivere overschietende Saldo, voor zo verre het zelve zal kunnen toereiken, door de Heeren Comparanten of derzelver Opvolgers zullen werden voldaan de voorschreven Jaarlyksche Interessen, zo mede in cas van aflossing by Termynen als voren, dezelve Termynen, zullende over het meerdere beloop van het zelve Saldo door hem Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qualitatequa mogen werden gedisponeerd, dog in cas van te kort komende, het zelve door hem privé en qq. aanstonds in geld moeten worden gesuppleerd. Dat hy Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq. zal gehouden zyn, de rekening, welke Jaarlyks aan hem door de Heeren Comparanten of derzelver Opvolgers zal moeten werden overgezonden of ter hand gesteld binnen twee of uitterlyk drie Maanden na den ontfangst of overgifte van dezelve schriftelyk te approberen, of de erreuren daar in aan te wyzen, en die tyd verstreken zynde zonder dat dezelve rekening zal zyn gecontradiceerd, hy Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq. heeft verklaard, die alsdan te houden voor accoord, en door hem geapprobeerd, Hebbende voorts hy Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq. zig verpligt, om de na te meldene Plantagien geduurende de voorschreven tyd behoorlyk te administreeren of doen administreeren, mitsgaders altoos een toereikend getal Slaven op de zelve te houden, en voor de Stervende, verminkte of door Ouderdom of anderzints tot den dienst onbekwaam gewordene, als mede voor de weglopers van tyd tot tyd wederom nieuwe aan te koopen, en voorts in alles dezelve Plantagien in goeden staat te onderhouden, en ook van dezelve administratie en zulks van den staat der gedagte Plantagien, en al het geen daar omtrent komt voor te vallen, van tyd tot tyd een behoorlyk blyk over te zenden aan hen Heeren Comparanten of derzelver Opvolgers, die ook ten einde hun Edelens zullen kunnen verzekerd zyn, dat alles ter goeder trouwe geschied, dezelve Plantagien, des noods op nieuw zullen mogen doen Inventariseren en tauxeren, en zulks alles ten kosten van hem Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq.

Ter voldoeninge en naarkoming van al het welke hy Heer FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT privé en qq. heeft verbonden, niet alleen in het generaal de Personen en Goederen van die genen zoo hy repræsenteerde, en dat ieder in solidum en voor het geheel, ten bedwang van alle Regten ende Regteren, maar ook als een Legaal en Speciaal Hypotheecq derzelver, in gemelde Colonie gelegene Plantagien, te weten:

De

## English Translation:

**Copy.**

(Faint pencil annotations across the top margin, largely illegible; the figure "1000 fl" and a bracketed "(1000 fl)" can be made out. At upper right an embossed tax stamp reading "ZEGEL 6" [Seal 6] with a crowned lion coat of arms, and a handwritten flourish/signature.)

On the twenty-ninth of December in the year seventeen hundred seventy-seven, there appeared before me, CORNELIS VAN HOMRIGH, public Notary at Amsterdam, admitted by the Court of Holland, the Gentlemen REYNIER ISAAC DU PLESSIS and JAN FREDRIK TAUNAY, Merchants residing within this City.

Making known: That by Deed of Mortgage on the 22nd of July of this Year 1777, in the presence of the Honourable Worshipful Gentlemen CHRISTOFFEL NAGEL and HENDRIK ROTARIUS, Councillors in the Court of Civil Justice of the Colony of Surinam, &c. &c., by the Gentleman FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, both for himself, as owner of a one-third part of the plantations hereinafter to be named, and also, pursuant to two separate Powers of Attorney mentioned in the said Deed of Mortgage, as authorized agent both of the Gentleman ISAAC STOLKERT, as well as of the Gentleman HENDRIK BOUDEWYN STOLKERT, and Madam DOROTHEA ELIZABETH WERNER, married couple, as owners of the remaining two-third parts of the same plantations, passed and on the same day confirmed by decree (condemnation) of the aforesaid Court, the said Gentleman FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, thus in his private capacity and further in his aforesaid capacity, and therefore also in the names of the aforementioned Principals, has acknowledged, respectively together and each severally and for the whole, without any division, under proper renunciation, to be well and truly indebted to and for the benefit of the Gentlemen Appearers, in a Sum of Three times one hundred and twenty thousand Guilders Holland Current Money [320,000], arising, firstly, out of and by reason of a Sum of One hundred and eight thousand Guilders [108,000], Holland Current Money, advanced and furnished by the Gentlemen Appearers for the benefit of the Gentleman FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT in his private and representative capacity; and further of a Sum of Two hundred and twelve thousand Guilders [212,000], Holland Current Money, likewise to be advanced by the Gentlemen Appearers for the benefit of the Gentleman FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT in his private and representative capacity, in satisfaction of the Mortgage passed for the benefit of the Gentlemen P. HARTSINCK, S. DU PLESSIS, J. W. PICHOT and A. WALLYEN, (as also the same Sum of Two hundred and twelve thousand Guilders, advanced by the Gentlemen Appearers, and consequently the aforesaid Mortgage having also been cancelled,) the said Gentleman FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT in his private and representative capacity has therefore renounced the exception of money not counted out or of not having received sufficient value; And further has promised to restore and satisfy the Capital Sum thus taken up and owed as aforesaid, of Three times one hundred and twenty thousand Guilders Holland Current Money, to the Gentlemen Appearers or their assigns, without cost or loss, within this City, over fifteen Years, reckoned from the 10th of April 1777; and in the meantime also to pay the Interest within this City at the falling due of each Year, likewise reckoned from the same 10th of April 1777, at five per hundred per Year, free of charge, without any deduction of whatever kind, and this until the full and effective satisfaction of the Capital. So that the Gentleman FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, in his private and representative capacity, shall be at liberty to pay off the aforesaid Capital Sum sooner than the said fifteen Years, in instalments, whether at the ending of the sixth Year, and so on thereafter yearly at ten per cent, or at the ending of the eleventh Year, and so on thereafter yearly at twenty per cent, and this in both cases indeed more, but not less, and provided that six Months' notice of such earlier repayment be given beforehand; With further promises, that the Gentlemen Appearers or their successors in their Counting-house of Trade, during the aforesaid term and until the actual repayment of the whole aforesaid Capital, shall be the sole Correspondents of the plantations hereinafter to be named, and that accordingly to them or their said successors from time to time all the Produce and Crops of the same plantations shall be sent and consigned, in order to be sold here at Amsterdam by the Gentlemen Appearers or their successors in their Counting-house of Trade, without selling the very least of it there in the Colony or to anyone else, or sending or vending it to any other places, directly nor indirectly; That also of the sending of the said Produce and Crops proper and timely advices shall from time to time be given to the Gentlemen Appearers or their said successors, for the effecting of the Insurances for which the Gentlemen Appearers are specially qualified by the same Deed of Mortgage, and if need be upon Ship or Ships; likewise that all Supplies and necessaries required from time to time for the benefit of the plantations hereinafter to be named, and for the maintenance thereof, shall be ordered from the Gentlemen Appearers or their said successors, and from no one else, and all this upon the footing of the usual Commission according to Custom; after deduction of which Commissions, from the net remaining Balance, so far as the same may suffice, the Gentlemen Appearers or their successors shall pay the aforesaid Yearly Interest, as also in case of repayment by Instalments as above, the said Instalments; and the surplus of the said Balance may be disposed of by the Gentleman FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT in his private and representative capacity, but in case of a shortfall, the same must at once be supplemented by him in cash, privately and in his representative capacity. That the Gentleman FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, privately and in his representative capacity, shall be bound to approve in writing the account which must yearly be sent or handed to him by the Gentlemen Appearers or their successors, within two or at most three Months after its receipt or delivery, or to point out the errors therein; and that time having elapsed without the said account being contradicted, the Gentleman FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, privately and in his representative capacity, has declared then to hold it as agreed and approved by him. Furthermore the Gentleman FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, privately and in his representative capacity, has bound himself properly to administer or cause to be administered the plantations hereinafter to be named during the aforesaid term, and always to keep a sufficient number of Slaves upon them, and for the Dying, the maimed, or those become unfit for service through Old age or otherwise, as well as for the runaways, from time to time to purchase new ones again, and further in every respect to maintain the same plantations in good condition; and also, of the said administration and thus of the state of the said plantations, and of everything that comes to pass concerning them, from time to time to send a proper account to the Gentlemen Appearers or their successors, who, in order that their Honours may also be assured that everything is done in good faith, may, if need be, have the same plantations newly Inventoried and appraised, and all this at the cost of the Gentleman FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, privately and in his representative capacity.

For the satisfaction and fulfilment of all which the Gentleman FREDRIK CORNELIS STOLKERT, privately and in his representative capacity, has bound, not only in general the Persons and Goods of those whom he represented, and that each in solidum and for the whole, subject to the constraint of all Laws and Judges, but also as a Legal and Special Mortgage thereof, the plantations situated in the said Colony, namely:

The

title: Heshuysen & Compagnie / James Balmer Mortgage on Plantation Vauxhall, Dominica (Notarial Copy, Amsterdam, 1777)
description: This notarial copy ("Copie"), executed before Hermanus de Wolff Junior, notary public admitted by the Hof van Holland and resident at Amsterdam, records a mortgage transaction on the twenty-first of March 1777. Frans Jacob Heshuysen, acting for the Haarlem merchant house Adolf Jan Heshuysen en Compagnie, exhibits a mortgage ("Mortgage of Hypotheecq") granted on 13 August 1776 by James Balmer, merchant in London, in favour of Adolf Jan, Floris Visscher, and Frans Jacob Heshuysen for 7,100 pounds Sterling, secured on Plantation Vauxhall in Dominica. The same plantation already carried, per that mortgage, a life annuity ("Lyfrente") on diverse lives in Great Britain of 1,269 pounds 10 shillings Sterling yearly. Balmer had purchased the estate for 9,000 pounds above that annuity charge, then granted a fresh special mortgage of 7,100 pounds. The document shows how Dutch capital financed West Indian plantation ownership through London intermediaries, the New World estate pledged across the Atlantic to Holland creditors. The plain notarial hand, names italicised, fixes the chain of title in legally exact, unornamented form.
creator: Hermanus de Wolff Junior (Notaris); Adolf Jan Heshuysen en Compagnie
notes: Amsterdam notarial copy; Plantation Paaschal, Dominica; Heshuysen & Comp. / James Balmer; earlier phase of December 1777 transaction; notary Hermanus de Wolff Junior; March 21, 1777
