On this day, the twenty-first of March in the year seventeen hundred and seventy-seven, appeared before me *Hermannus de Wolff Junior*, Notary Public, admitted by the Court of Holland, residing in Amsterdam, in the presence of the aforementioned witnesses,

The Gentleman *Frans Jacob Heshuysen*, on behalf of his Company calling itself *Adolph Jan Heshuysen and Compagnie*, Merchants within this City and in Haarlem and known to me Notary, who likewise exhibited to me Notary a Mortgage or Hypothec, by the Gentleman *James Ballmer*, Merchant in London, on the thirteenth of August in the year seventeen hundred and seventy-six for the benefit of the Gentlemen *Adolph Jan Heshuysen, Floris Visscher Heshuysen* and him, the Gentleman Present *Frans Jacob Heshuysen*, together, namely as he, the Gentleman Present *Frans Jacob Heshuysen*, [represents] the Members of his Gentleman Present's said Company of *Adolph Jan Heshuysen and Compagnie*, guaranteed, for a sum of seven hundred pounds Sterling, levied on the Plantation *Vauxhall*, located in the Island of Dominica, which Plantation according to the aforementioned Mortgage or Hypothec mentioned in the year seventeen hundred and seventy-two has been provided with a Life Annuity on diverse Lives title: Hope & Co. Russian Bank Assignation Funds Certificate
description: This bilingual Dutch-and-French certificate (Certificaat / Certificat d'Inscription Russe) entitles the bearer to an inscription of one thousand roubles in 6 per cent Russian funds payable in bank assignations (Bank-Assignatien / Assignations de Banque), entered in the Grand Livre (Groot-Boek) at St. Petersburg under the Amsterdam administration office directed by Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, and Wed. W. Borski. It is dated Amsterdam, 7 September 1827, and carries ten semi-annual coupons running from 1 July 1829. The instrument shows how Amsterdam's banking houses repackaged the Russian crown's paper-rouble debt into bearer certificates marketed to both Dutch and French speakers, letting Continental investors hold a foreign sovereign's obligations through a trusted local intermediary. The plain typographic frame, mirrored columns, and engraved signatures convey the sober commercial formality of the issuing houses rather than any decorative program.
creator: Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Wed. W. Borski
notes: Russian 6% Bank Assignation Funds; No. 3074; 1,000 roubles; bilingual Dutch/French; Hope & Co., Ketwich & Voombergh, Wed. Borski; Amsterdam September 7, 1827; 10 semi-annual coupons from July 1, 1829

# Transcription

## Original Text (Dutch/French Bilingual):

**LEFT SIDE (DUTCH):**

CERTIFICAAT
Van 6 per Cents
RUSSISCHE FONDSEN
IN
Bank-Assignatien.

No. 3074

HOUDER dezes is geregtigd tot eene Inschrijving van DUIZEND ROEBELS 6 per Cents Russische Fondsen, betaalbaar in BANK-ASSIGNATIEN, ingeschreven in het Streef-Boek te St. Petersburg, ten name van het ADMINISTRATIE-KANTOOR te Amsterdam, onder directie van

HOPE en COMP.,
KETWICH en VOOMBERGH,
EN
WED. W. BORSKI.

Waarvan de Interessen voor rekening en risico van den HOUDER ontvangen en uitbetaald zullen worden op Couvert, ingevolge Art. 6 van het hierboven gestelde Reglement. De HOUDER van dit Certificaat zal ten allen tijde geregtigd zijn de originele INSCRIPTIE terug te vorderen, tegen teruggeving van dit Certificaat, met de onverschenen Coupons, en het daarbij afgegeven Bewijs voor nieuwe COUPONS, en tegen betaling van de ontvangen Interessen volgens Art. 10 van het Reglement.

Amsterdam, den 7 September 1827.

Gezien bij mij
[Signature] Joannes Brouwgh
Notarius

Hierbij afgegeven Tien Halfjaarige COUPONS, tot is Juli 1829.

---

**RIGHT SIDE (FRENCH):**

CERTIFICAT
D'INSCRIPTION RUSSE
à 6 pour Cent
EN
Assignations de Banque.

No. 4000:—

LE PORTEUR à droit à une Inscription de MILLE ROUBLES capital à 6 pour Cent, de la Dette publique de Russie, payable en ASSIGNATIONS DE BANQUE, inscrit sur le Grand-Livre à St. Petersburg, au nom du BUREAU D'ADMINISTRATION, établi à Amsterdam, sous la direction de

HOPE et COMP.,
KETWICH et VOOMBERGH,
ET
Veuve W. BORSKI.

Dont les Intérêts seront reçus pour compte et risque du PORTEUR et payés contre la remise des Coupons, suivant Art. 6 du Règlement. Le PORTEUR de ce Certificat pourra en tout temps réclamer L'INSCRIPTION originale, contre restitution du Certificat, avec les Coupons non échus y appartenans, et la RECONNAISSANCE y annexée pour obtenir de nouveaux COUPONS, moyennant le payement des Intérêts mentionnés dans l'Art. 10 du Règlement.

Amsterdam, den 7 September 1827.

[Signatures] Veuve W. Borski
M. W. Borskei

Délivré Dix COUPONS de Rentes de Six Mois, jusqu'es Juillet 1829.

Geleschend door     Signes par
[Signature]

---

## English Translation:

**LEFT SIDE (DUTCH):**

CERTIFICATE
Of 6 Per Cents
RUSSIAN FUNDS
IN
Bank Assignations.

No. 3074

THE HOLDER of this certificate is entitled to an Inscription of ONE THOUSAND ROUBLES 6 per Cents Russian Funds, payable in BANK ASSIGNATIONS, inscribed in the Grand-Book at St. Petersburg, in the name of the ADMINISTRATION OFFICE in Amsterdam, under the direction of

HOPE and COMP.,
KETWICH and VOOMBERGH,
AND
WED. W. BORSKI.

Of which the Interest shall be received for the account and risk of the HOLDER and paid against the return of the Couvert, according to Art. 6 of the Regulation stated above. The HOLDER of this Certificate shall at all times be entitled to claim the original INSCRIPTION back, against return of this Certificate, with the un-cashed Coupons, and the Evidence given with it for new COUPONS, and against payment of the Interests received according to Art. 10 of the Regulation.

Amsterdam, the 7th September 1827.

Seen by me
[Signature] Joannes Brouwgh
Notary

Herewith issued Ten Semi-annual COUPONS, until July 1829.

CERTIFICATE
OF RUSSIAN INSCRIPTION
at 6 Per Cent
IN
Bank Assignations.

No. 4000:—

THE BEARER is entitled to an Inscription of ONE THOUSAND ROUBLES capital at 6 per Cent, of the Public Debt of Russia, payable in BANK ASSIGNATIONS, inscribed in the Grand-Book at St. Petersburg, in the name of the ADMINISTRATION BUREAU, established in Amsterdam, under the direction of

HOPE and COMP.,
KETWICH and VOOMBERGH,
AND
Widow W. BORSKI.

Of which the Interest shall be received for the account and risk of the BEARER and paid against the return of the Coupons, according to Art. 6 of the Regulation. The BEARER of this Certificate may at any time claim the original INSCRIPTION, against restitution of the Certificate, with the non-matured Coupons belonging thereto, and the ACKNOWLEDGMENT attached thereto to obtain new COUPONS, by means of payment of the Interests mentioned in Art. 10 of the Regulation.

Amsterdam, the 7th September 1827.

[Signatures] Widow W. Borski
M. W. Borskei

title: Russian Public Debt (Assignation Funds) Certificate
description: This bilingual Dutch and French bearer certificate (Certificaat / Certificat) records an inscription of one thousand roubles in assignats, yielding 6 percent, entered in the Grootboek der Publieke Schuld of the Imperial Commission of Amortisation (Keizerlijke Commissie van Amortisatie) at St. Petersburg. The fund was held in custody by the Algemeen Kantoor van Administratie at Amsterdam, managed by a syndicate of Amsterdam houses — Stadnitski & van Heukelom, Jacob van Beeck Vollenhoven, Samuel & David Saportas, Lamaison & Bouwer, Johannes Samuel Wurfbain, Hendrik Oyens & Zoon, and de Lanoy & Burlage — and issued with twelve half-yearly coupons. Dated Amsterdam, 31 December 1825, under a prospectus of 30 June 1824, it belongs to the Russian crown's external borrowing channelled through the Amsterdam market after 1815, when the tsarist treasury raised capital abroad against its inconvertible paper rouble. The engraved frame is plain, the printing letting the financial instrument speak for itself.
creator: Stadnitski & van Heukelom; Ovens & Zoon; de Lanoy & Burlage et al.

title: England War Loan Dutch Certificate
description: This is a Dutch bearer certificate (Certificaat aan Toonder) for £100 of the British 3½% England War Loan 1932, marked "SPECIMEN" and printed with proof serial numbers (000000). It was issued in Amsterdam by the Administratiekantoor van De Twentsche Trust-Maatschappij, the administration office channelling Dutch capital into a sterling-denominated British government obligation; coupons fell due each 1 June and 1 December, on terms freely available at the issuing office. The instrument belongs to the moment of Britain's 1932 conversion of its costly 5% War Loan—the legacy of the Great War—into a cheaper 3½% perpetual, then the largest voluntary debt conversion ever undertaken. Its sober engraved frame and restrained guilloche border, devoid of allegory, advertise security and creditworthiness rather than ornament, fitting an obligation marketed to cautious foreign rentiers.
creator: Administratiekantoor van De Twintigste Trust-Maatschappij, Amsterdam
notes: SPECIMEN; 3.5% England War Loan 1932; £100; Dutch bearer certificaat No. 15145; De Twintigste Trust-Maatschappij Amsterdam; coupons Jun 1 & Dec 1; WWI debt conversion

title: Austrian Construction Lottery Loan (Bau-Los), Vienna, 1921
description: This is a premium debenture (Prämienschuldverschreibung) of the Austrian Construction Lottery Loan — Österreichisches Bau-Los Em. 1921 — a federally guaranteed and mortgage-secured lottery loan payable to bearer (auf den Überbringer), with a face value of one thousand two hundred Kronen (K 1.200). Issued at Vienna on 1 September 1921 by the Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds (Federal Housing and Settlement Fund) under the law of 15 July 1921, B.G.Bl. Nr. 383, the loan totalled K 600,000,000, split into 450,000 whole tickets of K 1,200 and 200,000 quarter-tickets of K 300. It carries no fixed coupon; capital is returned over forty years through periodic draws, winning tickets taking prize premiums free of tax. Issued amid the housing famine and runaway inflation of post-war Austria, the loan harnessed the lottery's lure to fund dwelling construction when ordinary credit had collapsed. The engraved vignettes of modest suburban cottages advertise its avowed purpose: bricks-and-mortar security offered against a depreciating Krone.
creator: Österreichischer Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds
notes: Austrian housing lottery bond; K 1,200; Series 1,790 No. 060; federal government guaranteed; mortgage registered; Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds; post-WWI housing finance; cottage vignettes
transcription:
# Austrian Housing Lottery Bond (Bau-Los) 1921

## Original (German):

**Header:**
Vom Bunde garantiertes und grundbücherlich einverleibtes Lotterieanlehen von 1921 für Wohnungszwecke.

**Main Title:**
Österreichisches Bau-Los Em. 1921
auf den Überbringer lautend über

**Denomination:**
Tausendzweihundert Kronen | K 1200.- | Tausendzweihundert Kronen

**Series and Number:**
Serie 1,790. | N° 060.

**Body Text (Left Column):**
Diese Prämienschuldverschreibung bildet einen Teil des auf Grund des Gesetzes vom 15. Juli 1921, B. G. Bl. Nr. 383, ausgebgebenen Lotterieanlehens vom Jahre 1921 des Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds in Betrage von K 600,000,000.— eingeteilt in 450,000 ganze Lose à K 1200.— Nominale und 200,000 Viertel-Lose à K 300.— Nominale, und wird nach Maßgabe des beigefügten Tilgungs- und Prämienzinzungs-Planes innerhalb 40 Jahren durch Verlosung zur Rückzahlung aufgefordert werden.

Die Gewinste des Prämienlehens sind ohne jeden Abzug von Steuern und Gebühren auszuzahlen und in die Bemessungsgrundslage der Einkommensteuer nicht einzubeziehen.

Der Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds haftet für den Dienst dieses Prämienlehens mit seinem gesamten Vermögen und insbesondere mit den aus dem Ertragsnisse dieses Anlehens gewährten Hypothekardarlehen, die als Kaution für die Befriedigung der Ansprüche der Besitzer der Prämienschuldverschreibungen zu bestellen sind. Subsiäre haftet für diese Verbindlichkeiten des Fonds der Bund.

Die Teilschuldverschreibungen dieses Prämienlehens sind mit Ausnahme der Festsetzung vom 15. April 1921, B. G. Bl. Nr. 222, und fruechtfolgenden Anlegung von Kapitalien der Stiftungen, der ganz öffentlich Anstalten des Spaarkaasse-Amtes, dann von Populär-, Fidelkommis- und Depositenfonds und Diöces- und Geschäfts-kautionen verwendbar.

Wien, 1. September 1921.

Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds.

[Signature]

**Body Text (Right Column - English Version):**
This is a premium loan bond issued on the strength of the law of July 15, 1921, Federal Statute Book Nr. 383, of the Federal 1921 Housing and Settlement Fund (Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds) and amounts to 600,000,000.— crowns divided into 450,000 whole bonds of 1200.— crowns each and 200,000 quarter bonds of 300.— crowns each, and will, according to the enclosed plan of amortization and drawing of premiums, be redeemed within 40 years by lottery.

The sums due on winning numbers of the premium loan are to be paid without any deduction for rates and taxes and are not to figure on the statement for the assessment of income tax.

The Federal Housing and Settlement Fund serves as security for the Federal State guarantees these obligations of the Fund in a supplementary manner.

The sums due on winning numbers of the premium loan are available for investment in accordance with § 23 of Federal Law of April 25, 1921, Federal Statute Book Nr. 222, and as further bearing investment of funds or capital belonging to: The Benevolent Foundations, Public institutions, Austrian Post Office Savings Bank, further of Popularly, Entail, and Deposit Monies, as well as of Fidelity Guarantees and Baldness Surplus.

**French Text (Center/Left Section):**
Cette obligation à prime représente une partie de l'emprunt à lots du Budget fédéral du 15 juillet 1921, Journal Fédéral des Lois, N° 383, pour financer la construction de Colonies suburbaines (Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds) dont l'émission se fait autorisée par la loi du 15 juillet 1921, Bulletin des Lois fédérales N° 383, emprunt se montant à K 600,000,000.— réparti en 450,000 lots entiers à K 1200.— et 200,000 quarts de lots à K 300.— de nominal, et sera appelé au remboursement dans un des tirages au sort établis par le plan d'amortissement et de tirage à primes ci-joint.

[Continues with French legal text...]

title: City of Baku 5% Loan Bond, 1910
description: This is a bearer bond (Облигация / Bond / Obligation) of the 5% Loan of the City of Baku of 1910, its text set in parallel Russian, English, and French. The note carries a face value of 189 roubles, fixed against 20 pounds sterling and 504 French francs, and bears interest at 5 percent per annum, payable semi-annually. It forms one share of a municipal loan of 26,999,973 roubles (£2,867,340; 71,999,928 francs), authorised by imperial sanction of 8/15 October 1909 and signed by the city's mayor (Городской Голова) and members of the municipal council. Issued at Baku, then the oil-rich capital of the Baku Governorate in the Russian Empire and now Azerbaijan, the bond shows a booming Caspian port reaching past its own borders to London and Paris money markets to fund municipal works. The trilingual frame and the prominent sterling and franc equivalences make the instrument's purpose plain: to court Western European capital. The municipal arms and restrained typographic ornament keep the design sober and administrative rather than allegorical.
creator: City of Baku Municipal Council
notes: Baku municipal bond; No. 00603; 189 roubles / £20 / 504 francs; total loan 26,999,973 roubles; authorized October 1909; trilingual Russian/English/French; signed by Mayor of Baku; Russian Imperial Azerbaijan

title: Bolivian Interior Loan Bond
description: This is a vale, or interest-bearing debt certificate, for one hundred pesos, issued by the República Boliviana under its Empréstito Interior (Internal Loan) of one million pesos authorized by the Constituent Congress law of 16 November 1826. It promises an annual rent of six pesos—six percent—payable in thirds each January, May, and September. The certificate is declared negotiable and receivable at the Republic's treasuries at face value toward the purchase of public properties and the redemption of censos (perpetual rent-charges on land), per the decrees of 12 June 1827. A stern line, "Pena de muerte al falsificador y cómplices," threatens death for forgers. Drawn at Chuquisaca on 24 July 1827, it bears a departmental endorsement of 7 February 1828—the seventeenth year of Independence—to the order of Colonel Ramón Molina, recorded at folio 22. Issued in the first years of the young republic, the vale shows the new state funding itself by monetizing colonial-era land obligations, turning the dismantling of censos into a fiscal instrument. The austere, text-dominant sheet, framed only by an engraved border at the right margin, reflects the documentary sobriety of early republican administration rather than any decorative program.
creator: República Boliviana, Ministerio de Hacienda
notes: Bolivian domestic loan; 100 pesos; 6% annual; payable in thirds Jan/May/Sep; Chuquisaca 1827; law of November 16, 1826; endorsed to Ramon Molina; death penalty for counterfeiting

title: Bolivian Government Loan of 1872 Trust-Fund Certificate (London)
description: This is a trustees' certificate issued in London under the Bolivian Government Loan of 1872, a £6 per cent loan raised in 1872 by the Republic of Bolivia and secured on the net proceeds set aside for the public works named in its prospectus. Signed by the trustees John Horatio Lloyd and Alfred James Lambert and dated June 1880, it certifies that the holder of bond No. 442, dated 17th February 1872 for £400, is entitled to a rateable proportion of the ultimate balance of the trust fund, distributions to be announced by advertisement. A printed note records that the certificate issues pursuant to the Court of Appeal's judgment of 20 June 1879 in Wilson v. Church and the House of Lords' judgment of 23 March 1880 in National Bolivian Navigation Company v. Wilson. A purple cancellation stamp confirms payment of £2 6s and the document's surrender. The certificate is a relic of one of the era's notorious foreign-loan failures: the proceeds, tied to a Madeira-Mamoré navigation scheme, were largely never applied, leaving creditors to recover only fragments of the trust fund through years of litigation.
creator: Trustees: John Horatio Lloyd & Alfred James Lambert
notes: Bolivian 5% Government Loan 1872; trust certificate; Bond No. 242, £400, dated February 12, 1872; trustees Lloyd & Lambert; legal references to Wilson v. Church (Court of Appeal 1879, House of Lords 1880)
transcription:
# BOLIVIAN GOVERNMENT LOAN OF 1872 - CERTIFICATE TRANSCRIPTION

**BOLIVIAN GOVERNMENT LOAN OF 1872.**

**CERTIFICATE.**

**IN RESPECT OF RATEABLE PROPORTION OF ULTIMATE BALANCE OF TRUST FUND.**

We the undersigned John Horatio Lloyd & Alfred James Lambert, the Trustees of that portion of the net proceeds of the 5 per Cent Government Loan raised in the year 1872 by the Republic of Bolivia, which was intended and appropriated towards payment for the works in the Prospectus of that Loan mentioned as they proceeded.

**Hereby Certify** that the holder of this Certificate is in respect of the undermentioned Bond and Loan named below that is to say

| No. of Bond | Date of Bond | Amount of Bond |
|---|---|---|
| 242 | 12th February 1872 | £400 |

entitled to a rateable proportion of the ultimate balance of the said trust fund. Payment of the said rateable proportion of the said ultimate balance of the said trust fund will be notified by Advertisement when the same shall become available for distribution.

Dated this 17th day of June 1880.

[Signatures]
**TRUSTEES.**

John Horatio Lloyd
J. Lambert

title: Bolivian Republic Fondo Público Bond, Sucre, 1846
description: A public-debt certificate (vale) of the Bolivian Republic's Fondo Público, the funded debt instituted by the Law of 1 June 1843. The note pledges six pesos of annual income on a registered capital of one hundred pesos, the yield to be collected in four-month installments by thirds ("a percibir en cuatrimestres por tercias partes"). It was issued at Sucre, the seat of government, on 1 August 1846, and carries the signatures of the Ministro de la Corte Suprema, the Ministro de Hacienda, and the Presidente del Consejo, with an endorsement to a named holder and entry in register folio 70. The instrument belongs to the young republic's effort to consolidate and service its obligations through a formal funded debt, converting scattered claims into a transferable security backed by the state. Its severe, text-driven typography, devoid of allegory, lets the printed law and the officials' autographs stand as the guarantee of value.
creator: República Boliviana, Ministerio de Hacienda
notes: Fondo Publico; No. 2657; 6 pesos income on 100 peso capital; 402 thirds payable; Law of June 1, 1843; Sucre April 1, 1846; endorsed to Venancio Nair; libro 70

title: Bosnia-Herzegovina 5% Provincial Investment Loan Bond, Sarajevo and Vienna, 1914
description: This is a bearer bond for 480 Kronen from the 5% Bosnian-Herzegovinian Provincial Investment Loan (Landesinvestitionsanleihe) of 1914, issued at Sarajevo and Vienna on 1 April 1914. Its face value is set out in five further currencies — 408 marks, 504 francs, 240 Dutch florins, 20 pounds sterling — advertising the instrument to investors across the Continental and London markets. The title is repeated in six languages (Serbo-Croatian in both Latin and Cyrillic, German, Hungarian, French and English), at once mirroring the province's mixed population and courting foreign capital. Bosnia-Herzegovina was then administered for Austria-Hungary through the Imperial and Royal Common Ministry of Finance, whose minister signs alongside Governor Oskar Potiorek. Dated weeks before the Sarajevo assassination that ignited the World War, the loan funded provincial public works on the eve of catastrophe. The dense engraved guilloche and rosette borders, with a stamp-duty overprint, project fiscal solidity.
creator: Kingdom of Yugoslavia
notes: Yugoslav 4% Public Works Loan; No. 05,155; six languages (Serbo-Croatian, Cyrillic Serbian, German, Hungarian, French, English); Kingdom of Yugoslavia; revenue stamp
transcription:
# Transcription of Kingdom of Yugoslavia 4% Public Works Loan Bond No. 05,155

## Header Information (Multilingual Titles)

**Original:**
- Serija - čepela / Serie - Sortecat / Serie - Sorcesat / A
- No. - No. / Број - Broi / 05155

**English translation:**
- Series / Series / Series / A
- Number / Number / 05,155

---

## Six-Language Bond Titles

1. **OBVEZNICA** (Serbo-Croatian)
2. **ОБВЕЗНИЦА** (Serbian Cyrillic - Cyrillic script)
3. **SCHULDVERSCHREIBUNG** (German)
4. **KÖTVÉNY** (Hungarian)
5. **OBLIGATION** (French)
6. **BOND** (English)

---

## Bond Specifications (Multilingual)

**Original (Serbian/Serbo-Croatian):**
"Perpositotal bosansko-hercegovački zemljalski investicioni zajam od godine 1914."

**English translation:**
"Perpetual Bosnia-Herzegovinian land investment loan from the year 1914."

Additional details visible:
- 480 kruna = 408 maraka
- 464 francs = 240 hollandských
- forinti = 30 Sterlina

**German version header:**
"Fünfprosentige Landesinvestitionsanleihe vom Jahre 1914"
(Five percent land investment loan from the year 1914)

**Hungarian version header:**
"KÖTELEZEVÉNY"

**French version header:**
"5% Public Works' Loan of Bosnia-Herzegovina of the year 1914"

**English version header:**
"5% Public Works' Loan of Bosnia-Herzegovina of the year 1914"

---

## Bond Value Information

- 480 Coronals = 408 märkädi (or equivalent denominations)
- Multiple currency equivalents listed across the document in local currencies

---

## Detailed Terms and Conditions

The document contains extensive legal text in all six languages describing:
- Bond terms and conditions
- Interest payment schedules
- Redemption procedures
- Conversion rates between currencies
- Annual interest rates (4-5%)

**Key visible sections include:**
- Sarajevo date markings
- Multiple signature lines for officials
- Government authorization statements

---

## Notable Markings:

**Signatures visible (though individual names are partially illegible):**
- Multiple official signatures at bottom in cursive script
- Titles beneath signatures indicating various government and financial officials:
  - "C.I.k. sanačrni minister financija" (Minister of Finance)
  - "Der k.k. gemeinsame Finanzminister" (Joint Finance Minister)
  - "Le Ministre des Finances" (Minister of Finance)
  - "The Governor of Bosnia-Herzegovina"

**Visual Elements:**
- Red/pink ornamental border throughout
- Circular medallions at four corners with sun/radial patterns
- Revenue stamp affixed at upper left
- Serial number: **05155**
- Date markings: References to 1914
- Central circular seal/watermark area (blank circle in center-bottom)
- Decorative geometric patterns and scrollwork

**Language attestations at bottom:**
- Sarajevo (multiple date references: "15 Aprilа 1914" / "1st of April 1914")

---

## Historical Context Notes:

This bond represents Yugoslavia's (then Bosnia-Herzegovina's) attempt to secure investment capital through a multi-lingual, internationally-tradeable security instrument. The presence of six languages reflects the document's circulation across Austro-Hungarian, German, French, and British financial markets in the pre-World War I period.

title: Bosnia-Herzegovina 4½% Railway Land Loan Bond, Sarajevo and Vienna, 1902
description: This is a bearer obligation for 1,000 Kronen from the 4½ per cent Bosnia-Herzegovina railway land loan of 1902 (Series C), its heading and denomination set in five languages — Croatian, Cyrillic Serbian, German, Hungarian, and French. The text grounds the issue in the law of 8 June 1902 (Reichsgesetzblatt No. 118) and Hungarian Law Article XIII of 1902, and dates the bond to Sarajevo and Vienna, 1 July 1902. It was issued for the common k.u.k. Ministry charged with the affairs of Bosnia and Herzegovina and signed by the common Minister of Finance, the territory then administered jointly by Austria and Hungary after its 1878 occupation. Proceeds, capped at 75 million Kronen, funded the provincial railways, including a narrow-gauge line reaching toward the Sandžak frontier. The quintilingual face and Sarajevo-Vienna imprint declare the loan an instrument of imperial development in an occupied, multi-ethnic land; the engraved blue underprint frames a wreathed coat of arms.
creator: Austro-Hungarian Government or Crown Land Authority
notes: Austro-Hungarian bond; Series C No. 17,496; 4.5%; 1,000 Kronen; five-language: Croatian/Cyrillic/German/Hungarian/French; blue and silver printing; likely Crown Land loan

## English Translation:

**Series C — № 17,496 — 1000** (in each corner)

**Heading (all five languages read alike):**
Four-and-a-half per cent Bosnian-Herzegovinian railway provincial (land) loan of the year 1902.
**BOND**
to bearer, over / of
**ONE THOUSAND KRONEN** (Croatian/Serbian "one thousand kruna"; German "one thousand Kronen"; Hungarian "of one thousand koronas"; French "one thousand crowns").

**Body:**

By virtue of the Law of 8 June 1902, Imperial Law Gazette (R.G.Bl.) No. 118, the Imperial-Royal Government of the Kingdoms and Lands represented in the Reichsrath, and — by Law Article XIII of 1902 — the Royal Hungarian Government, were empowered to give their consent that the Bosnian-Herzegovinian Provincial Government take up a loan, to be amortised in sixty annual instalments, in the maximum amount of 75 million Kronen, to meet the costs arising from the construction of a narrow-gauge railway — laid out, however, on the alignment of a normal-gauge main line — from Sarajevo to the Sanjak frontier in the Lim valley near Uvac, with a branch via Višegrad to the Serbian frontier in the Rzava valley near Vardište.

On the basis of the above laws, sanctioned by the Highest Resolution (Imperial approval) of 8 June 1902, and on the basis of the authorisation granted by both responsible governments, the Bosnian-Herzegovinian Provincial Government issues, for a loan of a nominal amount of 75 million Kronen, bearer bonds, namely:
20,000 pieces at 200 Kronen; 16,000 at 500; 18,000 at 1,000; 15,000 at 2,000; 3,000 at 5,000; 300 at 10,000. *(schedule partly obscured)*

One of these bonds constitutes the present instrument, and the drawing (redemption by lot) and amortisation of the aforesaid debt take place in accordance with the provisions set out on the second and third pages, which form an integral part of the present bond.

Sarajevo / Vienna, 1 July 1902.

**Signatures / authorities:**
For the Imperial and Royal Joint (common) Ministry in charge of the affairs of Bosnia and Herzegovina:
The Common Minister of Finance: *(facsimile)* Kállay
The Bosnian-Herzegovinian Chief Financial Councillor: *(signature illegible)*

For the Provincial Government in Sarajevo:
The Provincial Chief / Governor: *(facsimile)* [J.] FZM. (Feldzeugmeister) von Appel

title: Serbian Economic Development Loan Bond
description: A bearer bond (Обвезница) of 100 dinars issued by the Socialist Republic of Serbia to fund the republic's economic-development loan (за уплаћени износ зајма за привредни развој у Социјалистичкој Републици Србији). The certificate is payable to the bearer (Платива доносиоцу) and retains, at left, its full sheet of detachable interest coupons, each redeemable on a fixed half-yearly date — the visible coupons maturing on 30 June and 31 December through 1996, 1997 and 1998. The legend at lower right is dated Belgrade, February 1990, and bears the signature of the Republican Secretary for Finance. Issued in the closing months of socialist Yugoslavia, as the federation strained toward dissolution, the bond shows the republic borrowing from its own citizens to finance development. At its head stands the arms of socialist Serbia — a red star above a wheat wreath enclosing the cross-and-firesteels shield, with an industrial cogwheel at the base — fusing agriculture, industry and party in a single emblem, set against finely engraved guilloche meant to defeat forgery.
creator: Socijalistička Republika Srbija

title: Bosnia and Herzegovina Employment Loan Bond, Sarajevo, 1987
description: This is a bearer bond (obveznica / обвезница) for ten thousand dinars (deset hiljada / десет хиљада, 10000), issued by the Socialist Republic of Bosnia and Herzegovina under an Employment Loan (zajma za zapošljavanje / зајма за запошљавање) and signed at Sarajevo on 1 October 1987 by the Republican Secretary for Finance. Along the left edge run four detachable annuity coupons (anuitetski kupon / ануитетски купон) of 3,190 dinars each, payable in turn on 1 October of 1990, 1991, 1992 and 1993 — a self-amortising schedule that returns principal and interest over the bond's life. The instrument speaks to Yugoslavia's late-socialist fiscal predicament: a republic borrowing from its own citizens to fund jobs amid mounting unemployment and inflation. The frontal photograph of earnest young people, rendered in the documentary manner of socialist realism, casts the loan as an investment in labour itself. Text appears throughout in Latin and Cyrillic scripts.
creator: Socijalistička Republika Bosna i Hercegovina
notes: SFR Yugoslavia; Socialist Republic of Bosnia & Herzegovina; Employment Loan (Zajam za Zapošljavanje); 10,000 dinars; No. CC 04238717; 4 annuity coupons of 3,190 dinars; photo portrait

title: Imperial Brazil Loan Conversion Circular
description: This printed circular is a subscription prospectus for a sovereign debt conversion: the offer of £20,000,000 in new Imperial Brazilian 4 per cent. bonds, the proceeds to be applied exclusively to converting and redeeming the Empire's outstanding 5 per cent. loans of 1865, 1871, 1875, and 1886. It issues under the authority of the Emperor of Brazil, sanctioned by Laws No. 3,329 (3 September 1884) and Nos. 3,396 and 3,397 (24 November 1888), with N.M. Rothschild & Sons of London receiving subscriptions. The terms are the document's substance: the new 4 per cent. stock is offered at 90, holders of every £100 of old 5 per cent. stock receiving £100 nominal of new bonds plus a small cash difference, with interest running from the first half-yearly coupon due 1 April 1890. The plain, untitled typographic sheet, free of vignette or allegory, exemplifies the sober house style of late-nineteenth-century London finance, where a great loan was a matter of figures and law rather than ornament. The operation reflects a familiar fiscal motive: refunding costlier obligations at a lower coupon to ease the burden of the Empire's external service, transacted through the City house that had long managed Brazil's account abroad.
creator: N.M. Rothschild & Sons (agents for Imperial Brazil)
notes: Imperial Brazil debt conversion circular; £20,000,000 new 4% bonds; converts 5% loans of 1865, 1871, 1875, 1886; N.M. Rothschild & Sons; Laws 3,329, 3,396 and 3,397 (1884/1888)
transcription:
# CONVERSION AND REDEMPTION
## OF THE

# IMPERIAL BRAZILIAN FIVE PER CENT. LOANS OF 1865, 1871, 1875 AND 1886.

Issue of £20,000,000 4 per. cent. Brazilian Bonds, the proceeds to be applied exclusively to the conversion and redemption of the above Loans.

His Majesty the Emperor of Brazil having in conformity with the Laws No. 3,329 of the 3rd September, 1884, and Nos. 3,396 and 3,397 of the 24th November, 1888, authorized the negotiation of the above Loan, Messrs. N. M. ROTHSCHILD & SONS, have to announce that they are ready to receive subscriptions at their office.

Subscriptions may be made either in Cash, or in Bonds of the above-mentioned Loans.

Applications for the new Bonds, *in exchange for Bonds of the above-mentioned Loans*, will be received on Wednesday, the 2nd inst., and on the five following days, viz., until Wednesday, the 9th October, at 4 p.m., when the List will be finally closed. No subscriptions will be received on Saturday.

The conversion will be effected as follows:—

Subscribers in Bonds will receive allotment in full.

The 4 per cent. Bonds are issued at the price of 90. In the conversion, every £100 5 per cent. Stock will be reckoned at par, exclusive of accrued interest, and in addition to this, a bonus of £1. 10s. will be given on every £100 converted. Thus every holder of £100 5 per cent. Stock will on converting, receive in exchange a £100 4 per cent. Bond and £11. 10s. in cash, but for cash differences on large amounts of Bonds, subscribers may elect to have new Bonds, the fractional surplus only being regulated by a cash payment; this option must be exercised when subscribing. Interest at 5 per cent. will be paid on the old Bonds from the date of the last dividend to the 1st October, 1889, from which date the 4 per cent. Bonds will bear interest, the first half-yearly Coupon being due the 1st April, 1890.

The repayment at par, but without the Bonus of £1. 10s. per cent., of all the Bonds of the above Loans not presented for conversion will be officially notified at the earliest possible date.

The 5 per cent. Bonds to be converted must be listed on forms which can be obtained on application, and must be furnished with the Coupons not yet due.

Application must be made on the form provided herewith, and accompanied by a deposit of £5 per cent. in money, or an approximate amount in convertible Bonds, *unless subscribers prefer to deposit the whole of their Bonds when making application*. The failure to deliver the Bonds converted when the Scrip is ready to be given in exchange, will render the deposit on application liable to forfeiture.

---

**Notable markings:** 
Red annotation marks visible on the right margin of the document.

title: Hungarian Kingdom Annuity Loan Bond
description: This bearer bond (Bemutatóra Szóló Államadóssági Kötvény) of the Lands of the Hungarian Crown (A Magyar Korona Országai) belongs to a 4½% annuity loan (Járadékkölcsön) authorized by Acts XIV of 1911, V of 1912, and LXVI of 1912. The face value of 480 Korona is fixed simultaneously in 408 German Imperial Marks, 504 Francs, and 20 Pounds Sterling, with coupons payable semi-annually on 1 April and 1 October in Budapest and Vienna (crowns), Berlin and Frankfurt (marks), Brussels and Zürich (francs), and London (sterling), the loan not redeemable before 1 April 1923 (call protection). Dated Budapest, 12 March 1913, and carrying a German "Abgestempelt" stamp, the note reveals how the Hungarian treasury tapped foreign capital markets on the eve of war, binding its credit to several currencies at once. The restrained engraved frame, with the crowned arms at center, projects sober official authority rather than ornamental display.
currency: Austro-Hungarian krone|German mark|French franc|British pound sterling
creator: A Magyar Korona Országai (Lands of the Hungarian Crown)
notes: Hungarian Kingdom 4.5% Annuity Loan; No. 00,919; 480 Korona / 408 Marks / 504 Fcs / £20; Acts XIV-1911, V-1912, LXVI-1912; Budapest March 12, 1913; lottery redemption from 1923; Abgestempelt (German market stamp)
transcription:
# Document Transcription

## Original (Hungarian):

**Abgestempelt in Deutschland geltend** [stamp in corner]

Betti. A. ............................010.477 ........................Nr. 00.919
**SERVICE CENTRALE**

**A MAGYAR KORONA ORSZÁGÁI.**

**4½%-KAL KAMATOZÓ**

**JÁRADÉKKÖLCSÖN.**

KIBOCSÁTATOTT AZ 1911. ÉVI XIV. TÖRVÉNYCZIKK 15. §-A, AZ 1912. ÉVI V. TÖRVÉNYCZIKK 11. §-A ÉS AZ 1912. ÉVI LXVI. TÖRVÉNYCZIKK 11. §-A ALAPJÁN.

**BEMUTATÓRA SZÓLÓ**

**ÁLLAMADÓSÁGI KÖTVÉNY**

480 KORONÁRÓL = 408 NÉMET BIRODALMI MÁRKARÓL = 504 FRANKRÓL = 20 FONT STERLINGRŐL.

A magyar királyi pénzügyministerium elismeri, hogy ez a

**NÉGYSZÁZNYOLCZVAN KORONÁRÓL**
= NÉGYSZÁZNYOLCZ NÉMET BIRODALMI MÁRKARÓL = ÖTSZÁZAGY FRANKRÓL = HÚSZ FONT STERLINGRŐL.

szóló államadósági kötvény a magyar korona országai adómentas járadékadósságának egy részét alkotja. Az említett töke után 4½% évi kamat fizettetik minden év áprilís 1-én és október 1-én, és pedig a következő helyeken: a kamatot a tijekom beszolgattatásu ellenében szintűg a kötvények visszaváltása esetén annak tőkeértéke a kötvények és felmentoda határozotag nz le nem járt kamatszerzödtetnek s szelvenyultályának beszolgáltatása ellenben — a szelvény illetve a kötvény birtokosának választása szerint felvetételok Budapesteń a m. kir. központi állampenztárnal és más, a szelvényelynéplain megadott, valamint a magyar korona által telj utódoli felújüre teendő fizetelsi helyeken, és pedíg Budapesteń és Bécsben koronában, Berliben és M. Frankfurtban német birodalmi márkabon, Brüsselen és Zürichbon frankban, Londonban font sterlingben. Ez a kölcsön 1923. évi április 1-jét megelőző időpontru nen fog visszavásárolni felmutujtani. A kamatck, valamint — visszenlatótatok esetén — a járadékkötvelny tőkeértéke minden fennható ú és jövőben behozandó magyarországi adó, bélyeg és illétsk levonása nélkül fizettetnek ki. E kötvény egy szelvenyultalvánnyul és húsz czre szóló szelvényekkel van ellátra, melyeknek tejratán a szelvenyultalvány bemutatójának a szelvények egyu sorozata és új szelvenyultalvány fog kiadatni.

Kelt Budapesteń, 1913. márczius hó 12-én.

[Signatures follow]

m. kir. pénzügymínister.

a m. kir. pénzügymínísteri államvezeség hiteleigyl coursultjának fönadke.

Elenőrzőr alairása

a m. kir. központi állampenztár ügyzatóája.

M. kir. állami nyomda. Budapest, 1913.

## English Translation:

**Stamped/Accepted in Germany** [stamp in corner]

Receipt. A. ............................010.477 ........................No. 00.919
**CENTRAL SERVICE**

**OF THE LANDS OF THE HUNGARIAN CROWN.**

**4½% INTEREST-BEARING**

**ANNUITY LOAN.**

ISSUED PURSUANT TO SECTION 15 OF ACT XIV OF 1911, SECTION 11 OF ACT V OF 1912, AND SECTION 11 OF ACT LXVI OF 1912.

**BEARER STATE DEBT BOND**

480 CROWNS = 408 GERMAN IMPERIAL MARKS = 504 FRANCS = 20 POUNDS STERLING.

The Royal Hungarian Ministry of Finance hereby acknowledges that this

**FOUR HUNDRED EIGHTY CROWN**
= FOUR HUNDRED EIGHTY GERMAN IMPERIAL MARKS = FIVE HUNDRED FOUR FRANCS = TWENTY POUNDS STERLING

Bearer state debt bond constitutes a portion of the tax-exempt annuity debt of the Lands of the Hungarian Crown. Interest on the principal mentioned above at the rate of 4½% per annum is payable on April 1st and October 1st of each year at the following places: the interest shall be paid upon presentation of the voucher, and similarly upon redemption of the bond the principal shall be paid, and the right to interest and the coupon interest shall be at the discretion of the bondholder and shall be payable in Budapest at the Royal Hungarian State Treasury and at other designated payment places by the Royal Hungarian Crown, namely in Budapest and Vienna in Crowns, in Berlin and Frankfurt in German Imperial Marks, in Brussels and Zurich in Francs, and in London in Pounds Sterling. This loan shall be redeemed by lottery on or before April 1st, 1923. Interest coupons, and when redeemed, the principal amount of the annuity bond, shall be paid free of any Hungarian tax, stamp duty, and fee, whether now existing or hereafter imposed. This bond is accompanied by a coupon sheet and twenty coupons for interest payments; upon presentation of the coupon sheet, the bondholder shall be issued new coupons and a new coupon sheet for such series.

Dated Budapest, March 12th, 1913.

[Signatures follow]

Royal Hungarian Minister of Finance.

Secretary of the Royal Hungarian Ministry of Finance with authorization for financial responsibilities.

Checking Officer's signature

Official of the Royal Hungarian State Treasury Administration.

Royal Hungarian State Printing Office. Budapest, 1913.

title: City of Budapest Obligation
description: A bilingual Hungarian-French bearer obligation (Kötelezvény / Obligation au porteur) of the City of Budapest's 4% loan of 1911, issued by the Budapest Székesfőváros, the royal capital city. Each bond carries a face value of 400 Korona, equated on the certificate with 420 French Francs, and bears interest at 4 per cent payable on the first of May and the first of November. It forms one of 250,000 such obligations dividing a total loan of 100,000,000 Korona (105,000,000 Francs). Dated Budapest, 1 May 1911, the document is signed by the chief mayor and the city comptroller. The dual currencies and parallel French text reveal a municipality reaching beyond Hungary to the Paris capital market to fund its rapid pre-war expansion. The engraved vignette presents a sweeping panorama of Budapest along the Danube, framing the loan in the imagery of a confident metropolis.
creator: Budapest Székesfőváros (City of Budapest)

title: Province of Buenos Aires Gold Loan Bond
description: This is a bilingual English-French bearer bond of the Province of Buenos Aires, issued under its Five per Cent Consolidation Gold Loan of 1915. Printed in blue, it carries a face value of £20 sterling, equivalent to 504 French francs, and is styled a "Bond to Bearer / Obligation au Porteur," payable in gold. The provincial arms of Buenos Aires crown the sheet, and the engraved conditions appear in parallel English and French columns; the certificate is dated September 1915 and signed by an authorised representative of the Province. The consolidation loan refunded the provincial government's earlier external debts into a single uniform issue placed on the London and Paris markets, the dual sterling-franc denomination reflecting the Anglo-French capital on which Argentine provinces then depended. A later violet overstamp records the bond's modification and conversion under a debt-adjustment scheme, marking it a "Converted Bond."
creator: Province of Buenos Aires
notes: 5% Consolidation Gold Loan 1915; No. 048151; £20 / Frs. 504; CONVERTED BOND; bilingual English/French; Provincial arms; Buenos Aires provincial debt consolidation

title: Tungsram Share Certificate
description: This is a share certificate (részvény) of the Egyesült Izzólámpa és Villamossági Részvénytársaság (United Incandescent Lamp and Electrical Company), the Hungarian electrical concern known internationally as Tungsram. It represents ten ordinary shares of 100 pengő each, one thousand pengő (egyezer pengőről) in all, issued at Újpest near Budapest and dated 1 September 1930. The pengő, introduced in 1927 to stabilise Hungarian money after the post-war hyperinflation of the krone, here underwrites the capital of a firm that had grown into one of the world's foremost makers of tungsten-filament lamps. The engraved vignette of the sprawling Újpest works, smoke rising from its chimneys, places the certificate squarely in the descriptive realism favoured by interwar securities, where the factory itself stands as collateral for the promise. Stylised incandescent bulbs frame the corners, naming the source of the company's value.

title: Companhia da Zambezia Provisional Share Certificate, Lisbon, 1900
description: This is a provisional bearer share certificate (Certificado Provisorio / Certificat Provisoire) of the Companhia da Zambezia (Compagnie du Zambèze / Zambezi Company), the Lisbon-chartered concession granted authority to administer and exploit the Zambézia district of Portuguese Mozambique. The trilingual text — Portuguese, French, and English — addresses the holder as Portador / Porteur / Bearer, advertising the share to the Lisbon, Paris, and London markets at once. The capital is stated as 2.700.000$000 Réis, or 15.000.000 Francs, or £600.000, divided into 600.000 fully paid shares of 4$500 Réis (25 Francs / £1), issued in four series. Drawn at Lisbon and dated by hand the 20th of December 1900, the certificate stands in for definitive titles not yet delivered. Its restrained engraved frame and arched lettering convey the sober respectability such a colonial venture sought to project to foreign investors.
creator: Companhia da Zambezia
notes: Provisional share certificate; Companhia da Zambezia; Mozambique (Portuguese East Africa) concessionaire; capital £600,000; 600,000 shares at £1; four series 1898-1900; Lisbon December 25, 1900
transcription:
# Companhia da Zambezia - Certificado Provisorio de Acções

## Original Text:

**Header:**
Nº de Certificado: 21667
Nº de Acções: 1

title: Principality of Bulgaria State Mortgage Loan Bond
description: This bearer certificate represents two obligations (Двѣ Облигации / Deux Obligations) of the 6% State Mortgage Loan of 1892 issued by the Principality of Bulgaria (Княжество България / Principauté de Bulgarie). Each obligation bears a face value of 500 leva = 500 francs, with stated equivalents of 20 pounds sterling, 405 reichsmark, and 200 Austrian gold florins. Authorised by ukase of 27 October (8 November) 1892, the loan comprised 285,560 obligations for a total nominal capital of 142,780,000 francs, redeemable at par over thirty-three years and secured by a first mortgage on the State railways Kaspitchan—Sofia—Kustendil and Roustchouk—Varna and on the ports of Varna and Bourgas. Printed at Sofia and dated 3/15 December 1892, the bilingual text (the French declared authoritative) is signed for the Ministry of Finance by minister Ivan Salabashev (Иван Салабашевъ). Issued fourteen years after Bulgaria gained autonomy from Ottoman rule, the loan pledged the young principality's strategic transport assets to draw West European capital; its restrained green guilloche frame and lion-crowned arms project the fiscal seriousness of a state courting the foreign bond market.
creator: Principality of Bulgaria, Ministry of Finance
notes: Bulgaria 6% State Mortgage Loan 1892; TWO obligations Nos. 073,741–073,742; 500 leva / 500 francs each; 688,500 total obligations; signed Leon Sabbaitscheff; green/cream printing; Sofia November 15, 1892
transcription:
# Principality of Bulgaria — 6% State Mortgage Loan of 1892, Bond for Two Obligations (500 leva / 500 francs) — Transcription

## Original:

*(Bilingual bond, printed in green. Bulgarian text in the left column, French text in the right column. Corner counters read "2" at upper left and upper right; the crowned Bulgarian lion arms appear at lower left. Serial appears in the top centre.)*

**Nummer 073,741 – 073,742**

**Княжество България** — **Principauté de Bulgarie**

**6% Дьржавенъ ипотекаренъ заемъ отъ 1892 година,**
издаденъ съгласно закона, утвърденъ съ Княжески указъ № 204 отъ 27 Октоврий/8 Ноемврий 1892 год., обнародванъ въ Държавенъ Вѣстникъ брой 247 отъ 10/22 Ноемврий 1892 год.,

**ПРЕДСТАВЕНЪ ОТЪ 285.560 ОБЛИГАЦИИ**
за единъ имененъ капиталъ отъ
142,780.000 Лева = 5,711.200 Лири Стерлинги = 115,651.800 Марки = 57,112.000 Австрийски Флорини Златни
изплатими по именната имъ стойность въ 33 години. Заема е обезпеченъ съ първа ипотека:
а) на Държавнитѣ желѣзници Каспичанъ—София—Кюстендилъ и Русе—Варна,
б) на пристанищата въ Варна и Бургазъ.

*(Right column, same content in French:)*

**Emprunt hypothécaire d'État 6% de l'année 1892,**
[émi]s en vertu de la loi sanctionnée par Ukase en date du 27 Octobre (8 Novembre) 1892, No. 204, et publiée dans le Journal officiel No. 247 du 10/22 Novembre 1892,
**représenté par 285.560 Obligations**
portant sur un capital nominal total de
142,780.000 Francs = 5,711.200 Livres Sterl. = 115,651.800 R.-Mark = 57,112.000 Florins d'Autriche or
remboursables au pair en 33 ans. L'emprunt est garanti par inscription en première hypothèque:
sur les chemins de fer de l'État Kaspitchan—Sophia—Kustendil et Roustchouk—Varna,
sur les ports de Varna et Bourgas.

---

**ДВѢ ОБЛИГАЦИИ** — **DEUX OBLIGATIONS**

*(central denomination band)*

на носителя отъ по
**ПЕТЬ СТОТИНЪ ЛЕВА**
= Лири Стерлинги 20 = Марки 405 = Австрийски Флорини 200 Златни. Всѣка една.

au porteur de
**CINQ CENTS FRANCS**
= Livres Sterling 20 = Reichsmark 405 = Florins d'Autriche or 200, chacune.

---

**Body text — Bulgarian (left column):**

Настоящий заемъ е издаденъ въ 61,550 отрѣзка, А№ 000.001—061.550 по 1 облигация,
„ 30,005 „ 061.551—121.560 „ 2 облигации,
„ 24,000 „ 121.561—241.560 „ 5 „
„ 1,760 „ 241.561—285.560 „ 25 „

Облигациитѣ на този заемъ носятъ годишна лихва 6% на именния капиталъ, платима шестмесечно на 2/14 Януарий и на 1/13 Юлий всѣка година срѣщу предаването на купонитѣ.

Тоя заемъ ще се изплати въ 33 години, споредъ поместената въ облигацията таблица за погашение, чрѣзъ шестмесечни тѣгления, които ще стават въ София два мѣсеца прѣди падежа на купонитѣ — първа пѫть на 1/13 Май 1893 година, но при това Правителството си запази правото да изплати по [именната] имъ стойность въ всѣко врѣме, но не прѣди 2/14 Януарий 1898 год., всичкитѣ облигации останали въ обращение.

Изтеглените облигации ще бѫдатъ изплатени по именната имъ стойность на шестмесечния лихвенъ падежъ, при който ще стане тѣглението, срѣщу предаването имъ, придружени съ всичкитѣ неизтечени купони [...].

Изплащането на изтеглените купони и на изтеглените облигации ще става съ злато и по изборъ на носителя:
въ София у касата на съкровището,
въ Виена у Импер. Кралска привилегирована Банка на Австрийскитѣ земи,
въ Лондонъ, Парижъ, Берлинъ, Амстердамъ, Женева,
и други градове у учрежденията и банкеритѣ, които отпосле ще бѫдатъ показани отъ Българското Правителство.

Изплащането става въ София съ златни левове, въ Виена съ Австрийски флорини златни, въ Лондонъ съ лири стерлинги, въ Берлинъ и други Германски мѣста, гдѣто се плаща, съ марки, въ Парижъ и други градове съ златни франкове.

Купонитѣ и изтеглените облигации отъ настоящий заемъ сѫ и ще бѫдатъ свободни отъ всичкия видъ налози, такси или други данъкъ прѣдвидени или които не се прѣдвидятъ отъ Българскитѣ закони.

Изтеглените купони както и изтеглените облигации отъ настоящий заемъ ще се приематъ по именната имъ стойность въ всичкитѣ правителствени касси на Княжество България за изплащане на такси, мита, налози и други данъци, прѣдвидени или които не се прѣдвидятъ отъ бюджета на Княжеството.

Неизвадените купони губятъ стойностьта си слѣдъ петь години, а изтеглените облигации — слѣдъ тридесеть години отъ падежа имъ.

Плащането на купонитѣ и на изтеглените облигации е обезпечено съ първа ипотека:
а) на желѣзно-пѫтнитѣ линии Каспичанъ—София—Кюстендилъ и Русе—Варна,
б) на пристанищата въ Варна и Бургазъ и на всичкитѣ приходи на такси, които се събиратъ или ще се събиратъ въ тия пристанища.

За тая цѣль Българското правителство е установило на 18/30 Ноемврий 1892 год. подъ № 123, съгласно гражданскитѣ Български закони, първа ипотека върху казанитѣ желѣзнопѫтни линии съ всичкитѣ имъ станции, постройки, принадлежности и подвиженъ и неподвиженъ материалъ, както и върху дветѣ гореспоменати пристанища съ всичкитѣ имъ постройки, сгради и сѫорѫжения. Ипотеката е дадена на Императорската Кралска привилегирована Банка на Австрийскитѣ земи въ нейно име притежателка на облигациитѣ.

Ако Българското правителство не плати въ разстояние на шесть мѣсеци слѣдъ падежа изтеглените купони или изтеглените облигации, притежателя на облигациитѣ ще имать властьта и правото да вземать въ своя рѫка експлоатацията на ипотекиранитѣ желѣзници и пристанища за да се изплатятъ [...].

Ако Българското правителство не изплати въ разстояние на двѣ години происходящи [...] [линиитѣ] Каспичанъ—Шуменъ—Търново—Севлиево—Ловечъ—[...] пристанищата въ Варна и Бургасъ и неможе подъ [никаква форма да отклони тѣзи приходи отъ] специалното имъ назначение [...] да служи за основа. *(fine print; several clauses [illegible])*

**Body text — French (right column):**

Le présent emprunt est émis en 61.550 coupures No. 000.001—061.550 de 1 Obligation,
„ 30.005 „ 061.551—121.560 „ 2 Obligations,
„ 24.000 „ 121.561—241.560 „ 5 „
„ 1.760 „ 241.561—285.560 „ 25 „

Les obligations de cet emprunt portent intérêts à 6% l'an du capital nominal, [pay]ables semestriellement les 2/14 Janvier et 1/13 Juillet de chaque année contre remise coupons.

L'amortissement de cet emprunt se fera au pair en 33 ans suivant le tableau d'amortissement annexé aux obligations, par voie de tirages semestriels qui auront lieu à Sophia deux mois avant chaque échéance de coupons — la première fois le 1/13 Mai 1893; toutefois le Gouvernement se réserve la faculté de rembourser au pair à toute époque, mais pas avant le 1/13 Janvier 1898, toutes les obligations restant en circulation.

Les obligations sorties au tirage seront remboursées au pair à l'échéance semestrielle d'intérêts postérieure à leur tirage, contre remise des dites, munies de tous les coupons non encore échus après le terme de remboursement et du talon y afférent.

Le paiement des coupons et des obligations arrivées à remboursement a lieu en or, [au choix] du porteur:
à Sophia: à la Caisse du Trésor,
à Vienne: à la Banque Impériale Royale Privilégiée des Pays Autrichiens,
à Londres, Paris, Berlin, Amsterdam, Genève,
[et] autres villes auprès des établissements et banquiers qui seront ultérieurement désignés par le Gouvernement Bulgare.

Le paiement a lieu à Sophia en Levs or, à Vienne en Florins d'Autriche or, [à Londres en Livres Sterling, à Berlin et autres places d'Allemagne en Marks, à Paris et autres villes en Francs or]. *(remaining French clauses mirror the Bulgarian text; portions [illegible])*

[...] Varna et ne pourra sous aucun prétexte [être détourné de sa destination spéciale] (Art. II de la loi d'Emprunt.)

---

**Signatures / date (foot):**

София, 3/15 Декемврий 1892 година. — SOFIA, le 3/15 Décembre 1892.

Министръ на финанситѣ: — Le Ministre des Finances:
*(signed)* Iwan Salabascheff

Контролирано: Ковчежникътъ. — Pour contrôle: Le trésorier.
*(signature [illegible])*

[Делегатъ] на българското правителство. — Le délégué du Gouvernement de Bulgarie.
*(signature [illegible])*

---

## English Translation:

**Nummer 073,741 – 073,742**

**Principality of Bulgaria**

**6% State Mortgage Loan of the year 1892,** issued in conformity with the law confirmed by Princely Ukase No. 204 of 27 October / 8 November 1892, promulgated in the State Gazette No. 247 of 10/22 November 1892,

**REPRESENTED BY 285,560 OBLIGATIONS** for a total nominal capital of 142,780,000 Leva = 5,711,200 Pounds Sterling = 115,651,800 Marks = 57,112,000 Austrian Gold Florins, redeemable at their nominal value over 33 years. The loan is secured by a first mortgage:
a) on the State railways Kaspichan—Sofia—Kyustendil and Ruse—Varna,
b) on the ports of Varna and Burgas.

**TWO OBLIGATIONS** at the bearer of **FIVE HUNDRED LEVA** each = 20 Pounds Sterling = 405 Marks = 200 Austrian Gold Florins each (French: **FIVE HUNDRED FRANCS** each).

**Body:** The present loan is issued in 61,550 certificates (Nos. 000,001—061,550) of 1 obligation, 30,005 (Nos. 061,551—121,560) of 2 obligations, 24,000 (Nos. 121,561—241,560) of 5, and 1,760 (Nos. 241,561—285,560) of 25.

The obligations of this loan bear interest at 6% per annum on the nominal capital, payable half-yearly on 2/14 January and 1/13 July each year against surrender of the coupons.

This loan shall be repaid over 33 years, according to the amortization table printed on the obligation, by half-yearly drawings held at Sofia two months before the coupon due date — the first on 1/13 May 1893; however the Government reserves the right to repay at nominal value at any time, but not before 2/14 January 1898, all obligations remaining in circulation.

Obligations drawn shall be repaid at their nominal value on the half-yearly interest due date at which the drawing takes place, against their surrender, accompanied by all coupons not yet due [...].

Payment of the drawn coupons and obligations shall be made in gold, at the bearer's choice: at Sofia, at the Treasury cashier's office; at Vienna, at the Imperial Royal Privileged Bank of the Austrian Lands; at London, Paris, Berlin, Amsterdam, Geneva; and in other towns at establishments and bankers to be designated later by the Bulgarian Government. Payment is made at Sofia in gold leva, at Vienna in Austrian gold florins, at London in pounds sterling, at Berlin and other German places in marks, at Paris and other towns in gold francs.

The coupons and drawn obligations of this loan are and shall be free from every kind of tax, duty or other levy foreseen or not foreseen by the Bulgarian laws. The matured coupons and drawn obligations shall be accepted at their nominal value at all government treasuries of the Principality of Bulgaria for payment of fees, customs, taxes and other duties. Uncollected coupons lose their value after five years, and drawn obligations after thirty years from their due date.

Payment of the coupons and of the drawn obligations is secured by a first mortgage: a) on the railway lines Kaspichan—Sofia—Kyustendil and Ruse—Varna; b) on the ports of Varna and Burgas and on all the revenues and fees collected or to be collected in those ports. To this end the Bulgarian Government established, on 18/30 November 1892 under No. 123, in conformity with the Bulgarian civil laws, a first mortgage upon the said railway lines with all their stations, buildings, appurtenances and movable and immovable material, as well as upon the two aforesaid ports with all their buildings, structures and installations. The mortgage is granted to the Imperial Royal Privileged Bank of the Austrian Lands, in its name as holder of the obligations.

Should the Bulgarian Government fail to pay within six months after the due date the matured coupons or drawn obligations, the holder of the obligations shall have the power and right to take over the operation of the mortgaged railways and ports in order to obtain payment [...]. *(Further clauses concerning the lines Kaspichan—Shumen—Tirnovo—Sevlievo—Lovech—[...] and the ports of Varna and Burgas, which may under no pretext be diverted from their special purpose — Art. II of the Loan Law; portions illegible.)*

**Foot:** Sofia, 3/15 December 1892. The Minister of Finance: *(signed)* Ivan Salabashev. For control: The Treasurer *(signature illegible)*. The Delegate of the Bulgarian Government *(signature illegible)*.

title: Chrysler Corporation Share Certificate, Amsterdam, 1926
description: This is a Dutch bearer certificate (Certificaat), marked SPECIMEN, representing ten ordinary shares without nominal value (zonder nominale waarde) in the Chrysler Corporation, incorporated under the laws of the State of Delaware, U.S.A. The instrument was issued in Amsterdam by the administratiekantoor of Hubrecht, van Harencarspel & Vas Visser N.V., established by notarial deed of 16 July 1926 before notary R. Nicolaï. The certificate let Dutch savers hold an American equity through a familiar local bearer instrument, the holder's underlying shares kept in custody in America and dividends collected and remitted by the trust office. It speaks to the inflow of Dutch capital into booming 1920s American industry, with Amsterdam's old commission houses repackaging Wall Street shares for the home market. An attached coupon sheet (dividends No. 27 to No. 40, with a renewal talon) and the printer's mark of De Bussy reflect the practical machinery of cross-border share ownership. The engraved frame is plain and commercial, prioritising legibility over ornament.
creator: Hubrecht, van Harrenraegel & Van Vlisser N.V., Amsterdam (Administratietrust)
notes: SPECIMEN; Chrysler Corporation; Dutch bearer certificaat for 10 ordinary shares; Hubrecht, van Harrenraegel & Van Vlisser N.V.; notarial deed July 16, 1926; dividend coupons 29–40 and talon attached
transcription:
# Chrysler Corporation Certificate Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

CHRYSLER CORPORATION
OPCERIEGHT ONDER DE WETTEN VAN DEN STAAT DELAWARE (U.S.A.)

CERTIFICAAT
VOOR TIEN GEWONE AANDELEN
(ZONDER NOMINALE WAARDE)

HOUDER dezes is gerechtigd tot Tien Gewone Aandelen (zonder nominale waarde) in de Chrysler Corporation, met meer andere ingeschreven op naam van de Maatschappij tot beheer van het Administratiekantoor, opgericht door Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas V-sser N.V. te Amsterdam, en waarvan de aandelewijzen zijn gesteld onder neder-legging van den onder-geteekenden Notaris.

De uitdelingen op de originele Aandelen zullen voor rekening van de Houder in Amerika geldaangesend, naar hier opgemaagd en inilteide worden.

De voorwaarden, waaronder de Certificaten voor Gewone Aandelen in de Chrysler Corporation worden uitgegeven, zijn vastgesteld bij Acte, den 16e juli 1926 voor den Notaris R. Nicolai te Amsterdam verleiden.

Gezien door mij:

AMSTERDAM, 19___

De Maatschappij tot beheer van het Administratiekantoor voornoemd:

SPECIMEN
Notaris.

HIERBIJ AFGEGEVEN DIVIDENDBEWIJZEN VAN No.___ TOT No. 40 MET EEN TALON.

---

TALON
Na betaling van het Veertigste Dividendbewijs vervalt dit CERTIFICAAT VOOR TIEN GEWONE AANDELEN (zonder nominale waarde) in de Chrysler Corporation, ingeschreven op naam van de Maatschappij tot beheer van het Administratiekantoor, opgericht door Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas Vlisser N.V. te Amsterdam, zal na aankondiging en te:zen inrekking van dit Bewijs, een nieuw zal Dividendebewijs worden uitgegeven.

---

[Dividend coupons numbered 40, 39, 38, 37, 36, 35, 34, 33, 32, 31, 30, 29 with repeated text]:

Van het CERTIFICAAT _________ uitgegeven door de Maatschappij tot beheer van het Administratiekantoor, opgericht door Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas Vlisser N.V. te Amsterdam, voor Tien Gewone Aandelen (zonder nominale waarde) in de Chrysler Corporation, zal na inschrijving, inrekking en betaling, het Derde en Dertugste Dividendbewijs worden betaald in Amsterdam.

Betaalbaat mei /

---

**English Translation:**

CHRYSLER CORPORATION
INCORPORATED UNDER THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF DELAWARE (U.S.A.)

CERTIFICATE
FOR TEN ORDINARY SHARES
(WITHOUT NOMINAL VALUE)

HOLDER hereof is entitled to Ten Ordinary Shares (without nominal value) in Chrysler Corporation, among others registered in the name of the Company for the administration of the Administrative Office, established by Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas Vlisser N.V. in Amsterdam, and whereby the share certificates are placed under deposit by the undersigned Notary.

The distributions on the original Shares shall be paid to the Holder in America by mail, as they are received and sent here.

The conditions under which the Certificates for Ordinary Shares in Chrysler Corporation are issued have been established by Deed, dated July 16, 1926 before Notary R. Nicolai in Amsterdam.

Seen by me:

AMSTERDAM, 19___

The Company for the administration of the Administrative Office aforesaid:

SPECIMEN
Notary.

HEREBY ISSUED DIVIDEND COUPONS FROM No.___ TO No. 40 WITH A TALON.

---

TALON
After payment of the Fortieth Dividend Coupon, this CERTIFICATE FOR TEN ORDINARY SHARES (without nominal value) in Chrysler Corporation, registered in the name of the Company for the administration of the Administrative Office, established by Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas Vlisser N.V. in Amsterdam, shall, after notification and cancellation of this Coupon, a new Dividend Coupon will be issued.

---

[Dividend coupons 29-40 with repeated text]:
Of this CERTIFICATE _________ issued by the Company for the administration of the Administrative Office, established by Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas Vlisser N.V. in Amsterdam, for Ten Ordinary Shares (without nominal value) in Chrysler Corporation, shall, after registration, cancellation and payment, the Third and Rightful Dividend Coupon be paid in Amsterdam.

Payable on /

---

**Notable Markings:**

- **"SPECIMEN"** stamped in large red letters across the main certificate
- Ornamental decorative border throughout
- Notarial certification reference: **R. Nicolai**, Amsterdam, July 16, 1926
- Attached coupon sheet with numbered dividend coupons (29-40) and TALON
- Printing credit: "DRUK DE RUBY, AMSTERDAM" (bottom right)
- Administrative trust entity: **Hubrecht, van Harrenraepel & Vas Vlisser N.V., Amsterdam**

title: Republic of Colombia Funding Certificate
description: This is a bearer scrip certificate for £2 16s. 8d., representing a holder's share in the Republic of Colombia's 4% Funding Certificates, 1934/46, a sterling issue limited to a nominal amount not exceeding £155,892 14s. 0d. in accordance with a plan announced in "The Times" of 22 February 1934. It was issued in London by Lazard Brothers & Co., Ltd., of 11 Old Broad Street, and is dated 1 September 1934. The certificate funds the arrears of a tangle of older Colombian sterling obligations it enumerates: the 5% Bogotá-Sabana Railway Loan of 1906, the 6% External Gold Loan of 1911, the 6% External Debt of 1913 and its French Issue, the 5% Gold Bonds of 1916, the 6% (1920) Bonds, and the Agricultural Mortgage Bank of Colombia's 6½% Guaranteed Mortgage Bonds. Issued amid the debt defaults that swept Latin America after 1931, it consolidates lapsed interest into a new low-rate instrument, the era's characteristic remedy for sovereign distress. The plain typographic frame and the issuer's restraint suit a document of rescheduling rather than fresh credit; a red French "Valeurs Étrangères" revenue stamp dated 1938 records its later passage through a Paris portfolio.

title: Republic of Cuba External Gold Bond
description: A specimen of a 4½ per cent gold bond of the Republic of Cuba, the Bono de la Deuda Exterior Pagadero en Oro en 1949, an external loan due 1949. It binds the Republic to pay the bearer on 1 August 1949, with semi-annual interest from 1 August 1909, a single principal expressed at once in four currencies—500,000 dollars in United States gold coin, £102,880 13s. 2d. sterling, 2,100,000 German marks, and 2,590,000 French francs—payable at the holder's option in New York, London, Frankfurt or Berlin, or Paris. Dated at Havana on 2 August 1909, it forms part of a $16,500,000 loan authorized by decree of the Provisional Governor of Cuba and arranged through the New York house of Speyer & Co. The multi-currency face and American-overseen issue mark a young republic funding itself, after the second United States intervention, on foreign capital markets while binding repayment to gold and exempting the bond from all Cuban taxes. The text is plain and legalistic, its authority resting on terms rather than ornament.
currency: United States dollar|British pound sterling|German mark|French franc

# Transcription of Cuban External Gold Bond

**Header Information:**
- $500,000 U.S. Gold | F2124 | £102,880.13/2 Sterling
- M. 2,100,000 D.R.W. | No. 00000 | 2,590,000 Fca.

---

## Main Document Text

**REPUBLICA DE CUBA**

Bono de la Deuda Exterior Pagadero en Oro en 1949

Four and One-half Per Cent. Gold Bond, Due 1949 [External Loan]

The Republic of Cuba, for value received, promises to pay to the bearer hereof on the first day of August, 1949, in the City of New York, United States of America, Five Hundred Thousand Dollars, in gold coin of the United States of America of the present standard of weight and fineness, or, at the holder's option, in the City of London, England, £2 880 13s. 2d. Sterling, or in Frankfurt a.M. or Berlin, Germany, 2,100,000 German Marks, or in Paris, France, 2,590,000 Francs, and to pay interest on the above named sums, in any of said cities in the said respective currencies, at the rate of four and one-half per centum per annum from the first day of August, 1909, semi-annually on the first days of February and August in each year upon presentation of this bond for endorsement of statement of payment hereon.

The amount represented by this temporary bond forms a part of the loan of sixteen million, five hundred thousand dollars ($16,500,000) in gold coin of the United States of America, or its equivalent in foreign moneys as above stated, issued in pursuance of the decree dated January 25, 1909, of the Provisional Governor of Cuba and

in pursuance of the agreement between the Republic of Cuba and Speyer & Co., dated August__________, 1909, which decree together with extracts from said agreement is printed on the back hereof.

The principal, as well as the interest of this bond is forever exempt from all kinds of Cuban taxes, stamps, duties or assessments (whether of the Republic of Cuba or of any province or municipality therein) which exist or may be imposed hereafter. The Republic of Cuba will provide a sinking-fund for the retirement of said bonds as prescribed in said agreement with Speyer & Co. This bond is subject to be drawn for redemption at par and interest as stated in Article VI. of said agreement, and is also subject to redemption, at the option of the Republic of Cuba, at one hundred and five per cent. and accrued interest, as stated in Article VIII. of said agreement. At the date fixed for the redemption of this bond the interest thereon shall cease.

This bond shall not be valid or obligatory until the same shall have been duly authenticated by the execution by Speyer & Co. of the certificate endorsed hereon.

**HAVANA, CUBA, August 2, 1909.**

---

## Notable Markings:

- **SPECIMEN** (printed in red, bottom right)
- Serial number: **F2124 / 00000**
- Date stamp: August 2, 1909
- Vertical text on left margin (sideways): "*Temporary Republic of Cuba for Four and One-half Per Cent. Gold Bond dues 1949. [External Loan] Exchange for a like amount of Engraved Bonds.*"

title: Czechoslovak State 8% Sterling Bond, Prague, 1924
description: A bearer bond of the Czechoslovak State (Československý stát) for One Hundred Pounds (£100), part of an issue of £2,050,000 Eight Per Cent Sterling Bonds bearing the series name "1922," authorised by Laws No. 470 of 19 December and No. 110 of 31 March 1922 sanctioning borrowing of up to £10,000,000 (or $50,000,000). Bilingual in English and Czech, it was dated at Prague on 23 May 1924, with principal and interest payable in London through Baring Brothers & Co. and at Amsterdam through Hope & Co. The state reserved the right to redeem after 1 May 1932 at 108 per cent. A youthful republic, Czechoslovakia turned to the London and Amsterdam markets to fund post-war consolidation, pledging sterling rather than its own koruna to reassure foreign lenders. The engraved arms of the lion rampant and the restrained classical lettering project the solvency and permanence the new state needed to advertise. A large red overprint cancels the instrument, dated 1946, marking its retirement after the loan's settlement.
creator: Czechoslovak State

*(Left column — English body:)*

**BOND**
of the Czechoslovak State to Bearer for
**One Hundred Pounds**

comprised in the above mentioned Loan repayable by means of a cumulative Sinking Fund of one per cent. per annum operating half yearly by purchase in the market or drawings as mentioned in the Supplemental General Bond, a copy of which is endorsed hereon.

Upon the 1st October 1952 or upon the 1st April or 1st October in any earlier year commencing 1st April 1925 on which this Bond shall be drawn for payment under the provisions contained in the above mentioned Supplemental General Bond, the Bearer hereof upon presentation and against surrender hereof with all un-matured coupons (if any) attached hereto will be entitled to receive payment in London of the sum of One Hundred Pounds Sterling at the office of Messrs. Baring Brothers & Co., Limited, or at the office of Messrs. Hope & Co., Amsterdam, at the equivalent in Guilders at sight exchange on London, the rate for which will be fixed by Messrs. Hope & Co. from time to time.

On the 1st April and 1st October up to and including the day on which the principal sum shall be payable, interest at the rate of Eight per cent. per annum on the principal sum will be paid on presentation and surrender of the corresponding Coupon, in London in Pounds Sterling at the office of Messrs. Baring Brothers & Co., Limited, or at the office of Messrs. Hope & Co., Amsterdam, at the equivalent in Guilders at sight exchange on London, the rate for which will be fixed by Messrs. Hope & Co. from time to time.

If this Bond be drawn for payment interest will cease upon the date when the principal sum is payable and might have been received had this Bond been presented for payment.

The Bearer of this Bond is entitled to the benefits and bound by the provisions of the above mentioned Supplemental General Bond.

The Czechoslovak State shall have the right at any time after 1st May 1932 to repay all outstanding Bonds of the whole of the Loan of 1922 at 108 per cent. and accrued interest on giving three months notice by advertisement in two London, two American, and two Amsterdam newspapers once a week for four weeks.

Prague, 23rd May, 1924.

*(Right column — Czech body:)*

**DLUŽNÍ ÚPIS**
Československého státu znějící na doručitele a na
**Jedno sto Liber št.**

tvořící díl výše jmenované půjčky, splatné z kumulativního umořovacího fondu jedním procentem ročně, což bude prováděno pololetně koupěmi na trhu nebo slosováním podle znění dodatečného generálního dlužního úpisu, jehož opis jest na rubu uveden.

Prvního října 1952 anebo prvého dubna neb prvého října některého předcházejícího roku, počínajíc 1. dubnem 1925, ke kterému bude tento dlužní úpis tažen za účelem splacení podle ustanovení obsažených ve shora zmíněném dodatečném generálním dlužním úpisu, bude jeho doručitel, předloží-li a vrátí-li jej se všemi nedospělými (jsou-li které) k němu náležejícími kupony, [míti] nárok na vyplacení částky Jedno sto Liber šterlingů v Londýně u pokladny firmy Baring Brothers and Co., Ltd., neb u pokladny firmy Hope and Co. v Amsterdamě na částku stejné hodnoty v holandských zlatých dle devisy à vistà na Londýn, jejíž kurs bude čas od času stanoven firmou Hope and Co.

Dne 1. dubna a 1. října budou placeny ze základní jistiny úroky ve výši osmi procent ročně, do dne a inkl. dne, kdy bude základní jistina splatna, na předložení a odevzdání příslušného kuponu v Londýně v librách šterlingů u pokladny firmy Baring Brothers and Co., Ltd., nebo u pokladny firmy Hope and Co. v Amsterdamě v holandských zlatých v částce stejné hodnoty dle devisy à vistà na Londýn, jejíž kurs bude čas od času stanoven firmou Hope and Co.

Bude-li tento dlužní úpis vylosován, zastavuje se zúročení dnem, kdy jest základní jistina splatna a byla by vyplacena, kdyby byl dlužní úpis předložen k výplatě.

Doručitel tohoto dlužního úpisu má nárok na výhody výše citovaného dodatečného generálního dlužního úpisu a jest ustanoveními jeho také vázán.

Československý stát jest oprávněn splatiti kdykoli po 1. květnu 1932 všechny nesplacené dlužní úpisy celé této půjčky 108 procenty s přibylými úroky, ohlásí-li to tři měsíce napřed ve dvou londýnských, dvou amerických a dvou amsterdamských denících jednou v týdnu po dobu čtyř neděl.

V Praze, dne 23. května 1924.

*(Signature block, bilingual, across foot:)*

On behalf of the Czechoslovak Government: / Za Československý stát:

Countersigned,
Vice-President in charge of the Supreme Accounting Control Office. / Úřadující Vice-President Nejvyššího účetního kontrolního úřadu.
*(signature)*

Minister of Finance. / Ministr financí.
*(signature)*

Recorded in the book of State Debts. / Zapsáno v knize státního dluhu.
Director of State Debts. / Ředitel státního dluhu.
*(signature)*

Countersigned for identification for Baring Brothers & Co., Ltd. / Spolupodepsáno za účelem identifikace za Baring Brothers & Co., Ltd.
*(signature)*

*(Imprint, foot of certificate:)* BRADBURY, WILKINSON & Co. Ld. ENGRAVERS, NEW MALDEN, SURREY, ENGLAND.

*(Diagonal red overprint across the face:)*
**THE HOLDER OF THIS BOND IS BOUND BY THE TERMS OF THE NOTICE DATED THE 27th SEPTEMBER 1946, SIGNED ON BEHALF OF THE CZECHOSLOVAK GOVERNMENT AND PUBLISHED IN THE LONDON AND AMSTERDAM PRESS ON THE 30th SEPTEMBER 1946**
