title: National Pisco to Yca Railway Company Guaranteed Loan Bond, Republic of Peru, London, 1869
description: This is a £100 sterling (Soles 500 Peruvian currency) bearer bond of The National Pisco to Yca Railway Company, issued in London on the first day of May 1869 under "The Guaranteed Loan" and carrying interest of 5 per cent per annum payable half-yearly on 1 January and 1 July. It forms part of a total issue of £290,000 / Soles 1,450,000, split between Series A bonds of £20 (S.100) each, numbered 1 to 3,000, and Series B bonds of £100 (S.500) each, numbered 3,001 to 5,300, with capital redeemable in cash on six months' notice or by semi-annual drawings at par. Crucially, the loan is guaranteed unconditionally by the Supreme Government of the Republic of Peru, charged on the surplus from the sales of Peruvian guano. The bond thus belongs to the guano-financed railway boom of the 1860s, when Peru pledged its fertiliser revenues to underwrite British-built lines from the coast inland. Engraved by Nissen, Parker & Arnold of London, the vignette sets the national arms—the vicuña, the cinchona tree, and a cornucopia spilling coins—upon a locomotive flanked by flags, fusing republican emblem with the promise of steam transport.

title: National Pisco to Yca Railway Company Guaranteed Loan Bond, Peru, 1869 (Reverse)
description: The verso of a £100 bearer bond of the Guaranteed Loan of the National Pisco to Yca Railway Company, Republic of Peru (recto at goetzmann0689). Two engraved undertakings, each dated London, the first day of May, 1869, occupy the right: the Company, signed J. A. de Lavalle as Director, charges and hypothecates its properties and assets to fulfil the loan's conditions, while the Supreme Government of Peru, through its special agent Manuel Fco. Perola, guarantees the loan unconditionally, binding the Republic to an annuity of £29,300 sterling — covering the 5 per cent interest and a sinking fund to redeem the whole within twenty-five years. At lower right the back cover repeats the issue, denomination (£100), and number 3395. The attached coupon sheet, printed in Spanish, fixes each half-yearly cédula at £2:10s (or S/12½ in Lima, payable at José Canevaro é hijos), the dated coupons running to the forty-ninth semester of 1 July 1893. The sheet documents how a young Andean republic borrowed in London to lay rails from Pisco to Ica, pledging the sovereign's guarantee where the enterprise's own credit could not yet stand alone.

title: Chilian Eastern Central Railway Company First Mortgage Gold Bond
description: This is a bearer first mortgage gold bond (obligation de première hypothèque au porteur) of The Chilian Eastern Central Railway Company, Limited (Compagnie du Chemin de Fer de l'Est Central Chilien), one of an issue of 4,625 five per cent bonds numbered 1 to 4,625, each of $20 or Frs. 503, carrying interest at five per cent and free of British income tax. Created by resolution of the directors dated 21 March 1910 and executed under the company's common seal on the 2nd day of August 1910, the bond was issued by a London-registered company (share capital £240,000 in 240,000 shares of £1) to finance railway construction in Chile. The bilingual French and English text, the gold clause, and the franc-and-dollar denomination reveal a British promoter raising Continental capital for a South American line. The decorative steel-engraved border, with a small vignette of port works, signals security and engineering ambition to prospective bearers.

title: Chilian Eastern Central Railway Company £20 First Mortgage Gold Bond, London, 1910 (reverse)
description: The reverse of a £20 (twenty pound) bearer bond of The Chilian Eastern Central Railway Company, Limited, a £555,000 issue of Five per Cent. First Mortgage Gold Bonds raised in London in 1910 to build a Chilean line running from Lebu to Los Sauces. The left field prints "The Conditions of Issue," opening with the recital that the bond is one of a series "created by a resolution of the Board of Directors of the Company on the 21st day of March, 1910," secured by first mortgage and resting on a Government guarantee conferred by the railway concession. A redemption schedule tabulates, year by year from 1916 onward, the number of bonds to be drawn, their sterling value, and the balance left outstanding, the mechanism of a sinking fund retiring the debt to maturity. At right, an engraved label in carmine repeats the issuer, "First Mortgage Bond for £20," and "5%," the whole cancelled by hole-punch. The document records how a small Andean railway reached London's capital market under sovereign guarantee, the security on which a remote concession was financed.
creator: The Chilian Eastern Central Railway Company, Limited
notes: Chilian Eastern Central Railway Company, Limited, First Mortgage Gold Bond No. 7645, verso and conditions of issue. $20 / Frs. 503. Cancelled. See goetzmann0691 for recto.

title: Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes 4% Agrarian Liquidation Bond for Bosnia and Herzegovina, 500 dinars
description: A 4% bearer bond of 500 dinars issued by the Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes "za finansijsko likvidiranje agrarnih odnosa u Bosni i Hercegovini" — for the financial liquidation of agrarian relations in Bosnia and Herzegovina. Signed for the Minister of Finance and the Director of State Debts, it bears the sheet of fifty semi-annual coupons (the talon promising a fresh sheet once the fiftieth is drawn), the last falling due 1 May 1947, which places issue around 1921–1922. The instrument is fiscal machinery for one of the new kingdom's most charged tasks: dismantling the late Ottoman kmet tenancy and indemnifying former landholders, the state borrowing against itself to settle the land. The allegory states this plainly. A diademed sovereign-figure presides over a frieze of double-headed eagles; below sit two peasants — a fez-wearing Muslim before a minaret, an Orthodox Serb before a domed monastery — the confessional halves of Bosnia reconciled in agriculture. The drawing is assured fin-de-siècle academic work; the framing draws on Byzantine-Serbian revival motifs rather than strict Art Nouveau.
creator: Kingdom of Serbia
notes: Kingdom of Serbia, 4% State Bond, 500 Dinars / 500 Francs, bearer. Ornate color illustration with allegorical figures. Talon (coupon renewal receipt) attached at left. Bilingual Serbian/French.

**Latin:** SERIJA ✱008546 · Kraljevina Srba, Hrv[ata i Slovenaca] · **KUPON 4%** · za Fin. Likv. Agr. Odnosa u Bosni [i Hercegovini] · [illegible amount] DINARA · Plaća se 1. maja 1943.

Denomination roundel **500** appears in each lower corner. A round state coat-of-arms seal [double-headed eagle] is centred in the base. title: Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes 4% Agrarian Liquidation Bond, Bosnia and Herzegovina (Belgrade, 1921), verso
description: The verso of a 4% bearer obligation issued by the Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes "za finansisko likvidiranje agrarnih odnosa u Bosni i Hercegovini" — for the financial liquidation of agrarian relations in Bosnia and Herzegovina (recto at goetzmann0693). Printed in Serbian Cyrillic, Croatian, Slovene and French, it sets out the conditions of issue: authorized under Article 21 of the Decree on the agrarian liquidation, the loan comprised 1,300,000 obligations in 26,000 series of fifty, for a nominal 130,000,000 dinars, repayable at par over fifty years. The sheet is a block of five bearer bonds of 100 dinars (500 dinars), its TALON exchangeable for a new coupon sheet after the fiftieth half-yearly coupon. Below, a quadrilingual amortization table ("Tableau d'amortissement") schedules the annual sums drawn by lot. The document records the new South-Slav state assuming the fiscal burden of dismantling Ottoman-era feudal land tenure in its newly incorporated provinces, converting expropriated landlords' claims into long-dated public debt. Belgrade, 18 June 1921.
creator: Kingdom of Serbia
notes: Kingdom of Serbia, 4% State Bond, verso. Lottery/amortization draw tables and multilingual conditions of issue. 2-page document; see goetzmann0693 for recto.
transcription:
# Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes — 4% Agrarian Liquidation Loan Bond (Bosnia and Herzegovina), 1921 — Verso — Transcription

## Original:

*(Layout note: the image is scanned upside-down and has been read rotated 180°. The verso is quadrilingual — Serbian Cyrillic, Croatian (Latin), Slovene, and French — set in four parallel columns of identical legal text, above a quadrilingual amortization table. A TALON block and a strip of coupon stubs occupy the upper right. A large black area (an attached coupon sheet) obscures the lower-left and the right-hand columns of the table's second half. All four language versions are transcribed below.)*

---

**COLUMN 1 — Serbian (Cyrillic)**

**КРАЉЕВИНА СРБА, ХРВАТА И СЛОВЕНАЦА**

**4°/₀ ОБВЕЗНИЦЕ**
за финансиско ликвидирање аграрних односа у Босни и Херцеговини.

Издате на основу овлашћења чл. 21. Уредбе о финансиском ликвидирању аграрних односа у Босни и Херцеговини.

Подељене у 26000 серија по 50 бројева, свега 1,300.000 комада обвезница у номиналном износу од 130,000.000.— динара.

**ИСПЛАТА ЗА 50 ГОДИНА ПО НОМИНАЛИ**

**ПЕТ ОБВЕЗНИЦА**
на доносиоца свака од 100 динара
**500  ПЕТ СТОТИНА ДИНАРА  500**

Овај Зајам издат је у 286000 комада обвезница и то:
130000 комада јединица од Серије    1—2600
78000    „    петица    „    „    2601—10400
78000    „    десетица    „    „    10401—26000
Обвезнице ове носе 4% годишњег интереса од номиналног износа, а интерес се плаћа полугодишње уназад уз предају дотичног купона 1. маја и 1. новембра сваке године.

Исплата овог зајма вршиће се у року од 50 година према приложеном амортизационом плану, путем извлачења, које ће се вршити 2. јануара сваке године, у Генералној Дирекцији Државних Дугова.

Извучене обвезнице исплаћиване се у номиналном износу четири месеца доцније. На извучене обвезнице престаје тећи камата од 1. маја дотичне године.

Потребне суме за исплату купона и извучених обвезница уносиће се сваке године у државни буџет у округлој суми.

Купони којима је рок приспео као и извучене обвезнице, могу се наплаћивати код свију Финансиских Делегација, Обласних Финансиских Дирекција, Окружних и Среских Финансиских Управа, Порезних Уреда, као и код Новчаних Завода у земљи који и ту буду нарочито овлашћени.

У Београду вршиће исплату Генерална Дирекција Државних Дугова, која обавља и сву службу по овом зајму.

Купони застаревају за 5 година од рока, а амортоване обвезнице за 30 година од дана када су извучене.

Обвезнице ове уживају сва права, која имају и остале државне обвезнице.

У Београду, дне 18. јуна 1921. године.

**ГЕНЕРАЛНИ ДИРЕКТОР**
Државних Дугова
*(handwritten signature:)* Д-р Радоје М. Јовановић

---

**COLUMN 2 — Croatian (Latin)**

**KRALJEVINA SRBA, HRVATA I SLOVENACA**

**4°/₀ obveznice**
za financijsko likvidiranje agrarnih odnosa u Bosni i Hercegovini.

Izdane na osnovu ovlašćenja čl. 21. Uredbe o financijskom likvidiranju agrarnih odnosa u Bosni i Hercegovini.

Razdijeljene u 26000 serija po 50 brojeva, svega 1,300.000 komada obveznica u nominalnom iznosu od 130,000.000.— dinara.

**ISPLATA ZA 50 GODINA PO NOMINALI**

**PET OBVEZNICA**
na donosioca svaka od 100 dinara
**500  PET STOTINA DINARA  500**

Ovaj Zajam izdat je u 286000 komada obveznica i to:
130000 komada jedinica od Serije    1—2600
78000    „    petica    „    „    2601—10400
78000    „    desetica    „    „    10401—26000
Obveznice ove nose 4% godišnjeg interesa od nominalnog iznosa, a interes se plaća polugodišnje unazad uz predaju dotičnog kupona 1. maja i 1. novembra svake godine.

Isplata ovog zajma vršiće se u roku od 50 godina prema priloženom amortizacionom planu, [putem] izvlačenja, koje će se vršiti 2. januara svake godine, u Generalnoj Direkciji Državnih [Dugova].

Izvučene obveznice isplaćivaće se u nominalnom iznosu četiri mjeseca docnije. Na izvučene [obveznice pre]staje teći kamata od 1. maja do[tične godine].

Potrebne sume za isplatu kupona i izvučenih [obveznica] unosiće se svake godine u državni [proračun] u okrugloj sumi.

Kuponi kojima je rok prispio kao i izvučene [obveznice], mogu se naplaćivati kod sviju [Finansijskih] Delegacija, Oblasnih Finansijskih [Direkcija], Okružnih i Sreskih Finansijskih Uprava, [Poreznih] Ureda, kao i kod Novčanih Zavoda [u zemlji koji i] tu budu naročito ovlašćeni.

[U Beogra]du vršiće isplatu Generalna Direk[cija Državnih Du]gova, koja obavlja i svu službu [po ovom zajmu].

[Kuponi zastare]vaju za 5 godina od roka, a [amortizovane obvez]nice za 30 godina od dana [kada su izvučene].

[Obveznice ove uživaju] sva prava, koja imaju [i ostale državne o]bveznice.

[U Beogra]du, dne 18. juna 1921. godine.

**GENERALNI DIREKTOR**
Državnih Dugova
*(shared handwritten signature — Dr Radoje M. Jovanović)*

---

**COLUMN 3 — Slovene**

**KRALJEVINA SRBOV, HRVATOV IN SLOVENCEV**

**4°/₀ obveznice**
za finančno likvidiranje agrarnih odnošajev v Bosni in Hercegovini.

Izdane na podlagi pooblastive člena 21. uredbe o finančnem likvidiranju agrarnih odnošajev v Bosni in Hercegovini.

Razdeljene na 26000 serij po 50 števil, skupaj 1,300.000 obveznic v nominalnem znesku 130,000.000.— dinarjev.

**IZPLAČILO V 50 LETIH PO NOMINALI**

**PET OBVEZNIC**
na prinosnika vsaka po 100 dinarjev
**500  PETSTO DINARJEV  500**

To posojilo je izdano v 286.000 obveznicah, in sicer:
130000 edinic    od serije    1—2600
78000 petic    „    „    2601—10400
78000 desetic    „    „    10401—26000
Te obveznice dajo 4% ne[?] [no]minalnega zneska, obresti pa se plačuje[jo] polletno za nazaj proti izročitvi dotičnega kupona dne 1. maja in dne 1. novembra vsakega leta.

Izplačevanje tega posojila se bo vršilo v roku 50 let, po priloženem amortizacijskem načrtu z žrebanjem, ki bo vsako leto dne 2. januarja, v generalni direkciji državnih dolgov.

Izžrebane obveznice se bodo izplačevale v nominalnem znesku štiri mesece pozneje. Na izžrebane obveznice prestanejo teči obresti od dne 1. maja dotičnega leta.

Potrebne vsote za izplačevanje kuponov in izžrebanih obveznic se bodo vsako leto postavljale v državni proračun v okrogli vsoti.

Kuponi, katerim je rok dospel, kako tudi izžrebane obveznice se lahko izplačujejo pri vseh finančnih delegacijah, oblastnih finančnih direkcijah, okrožnih in sreskih upravah, davčnih uradih, nadalje pri denarnih zavodih v državi, ki bodo za to posebe pooblaščeni.

V Beogradu vrši izplačevanje generalna direkcija državnih dolgov, ki opravlja tudi vso službo glede tega posojila.

Kuponi zastarevajo v 5. letih od roka, amortizirane obveznice pa v 30. letih od dne, ko so izžrebane.

Te obveznice uživajo vse pravice, ki jih ima[jo i] ostale državne obveznice.

V Beogradu, dne 18. junija 1921.

**MINISTER ZA FINANCE**
*(handwritten signature:)* Д-р Коста Кумануди [D-r Kosta Kumanudi]

---

**COLUMN 4 — French**

**ROYAUME DES SERBES, CROATES ET SLOVENES**

**Emprunt 4°/₀**
pour la Liquidation Financière de la Situation Agraire en Bosnie et Herzégovine.

Émis en vertu de l'article 21. du Règlement relatif à la liquidation financière de la situation agraire en Bosnie et Herzégovine.

Réparti en 26.000 séries ayant chacune 50 numéros, en tout 1,300.000 obligations dont le capital nominal s'élève à 130,000.000 de dinars.

**Remboursement du montant nominal de l'Emprunt [en] 50 [ans]**

**CINQ OBLIGATIONS**
au porteur de 100 dinars l'une
**500  CINQ CENT DINARS  500**

On a émis pour cet Emprunt 286.000 obligations comme suit:
130.000 titres    unitaires depuis la série    1 à 2.600
78.000 coupures de cinq titres    „    „    2.601 [à] 10.400
78.000    „    dix    „    „    10.401 [à] 26.000
Ces obligations donnent sur le montant [no]minal un intérêt annuel de 4% et l'intérêt [est] payable chaque semestre échu et la remise [du] coupon respectif le 1er Mai et le 1er Nov[em]bre de chaque année.

Le remboursement de cet Emprunt sera effectué dans le délai de 50 ans d'après le tableau d'amortissement y annexé au moyen des tirages officiels, qui aura lieu le 2 Janvier de chaque année à la Direction Générale de la Dette [Pu]blique.

Le payement du montant nominal des titres sortis aux tirages officiels s'effectuera quatre mois après ces tirages. Les titres sortis aux tirages officiels cessent de porter intérêt à partir du 1er Mai de l'année respective.

Les sommes nécessaires à l'annuité pour le payement des coupons et des titres sortis aux tirages officiels seront portées chaque année au Budget de l'Etat en chiffres ronds.

Les coupons échus ainsi que les titres encaissés aux tirages officiels pourront être encaissés à toutes les Délégations financières, aux Directions régionales, aux Directions financières départementales et d'arrondissement, aux Directions des contributions, ainsi qu'aux établissements de crédit dans le pays, qui seront autorisés à cet effet.

Le payement des coupons échus et des titres sortis aux tirages officiels sera fait à Belgrade par la Direction Générale de la Dette Publique qui est chargée de tout le service de cet emprunt.

Les coupons échus sont prescrits après 5 ans à compter de l'échéance et les obligations amorties après 30 ans à partir de la date de leur sortie aux tirages officiels.

Les obligations de cet Emprunt jouissent [de] tous les privilèges accordés aux autres titres [de] l'Emprunt.

Fait à Belgrade, le 18. Juin 1921.

**LE DIRECTEUR GÉNÉRAL** de la Dette Publique
**— LE MINISTRE DES FINANCES**
*(handwritten signatures as above)*

*(The two printed signature-line headings appear across the foot of all four columns: left pair — ГЕНЕРАЛНИ ДИРЕКТОР Државних Дугова / GENERALNI DIREKTOR Državnih Dugova / GENERALNI DIREKTOR Državnih dolgov / LE DIRECTEUR GÉNÉRAL de la Dette Publique, signed **Д-р Радоје М. Јовановић**; right pair — МИНИСТАР ФИНАНСИЈА / MINISTAR FINANSIJA / MINISTER ZA FINANCE / LE MINISTRE DES FINANCES, signed **Д-р Коста Кумануди**.)*

## English Translation:

**KINGDOM OF SERBS, CROATS AND SLOVENES**

**4% Bonds** for the financial liquidation of agrarian relations in Bosnia and Herzegovina.

Issued under the authority of Article 21 of the Decree on the financial liquidation of agrarian relations in Bosnia and Herzegovina.

Divided into 26,000 series of 50 numbers, in all 1,300,000 bonds of a nominal value of 130,000,000 dinars.

**REPAYMENT OVER 50 YEARS AT PAR**

**FIVE BONDS** to bearer, each of 100 dinars — **500 FIVE HUNDRED DINARS 500**

This Loan was issued in 286,000 bond certificates, namely:
130,000 single-unit certificates, of Series 1–2,600;
78,000 five-bond certificates ("petice"), Series 2,601–10,400;
78,000 ten-bond certificates ("desetice"), Series 10,401–26,000.
These bonds carry 4% annual interest on the nominal value; the interest is paid half-yearly in arrears against surrender of the relevant coupon on 1 May and 1 November of each year.

Repayment of this loan will be effected over a period of 50 years according to the attached amortization plan, by drawing lots, which will take place on 2 January of each year at the General Directorate of State Debts.

Drawn bonds will be paid at their nominal value four months later. Drawn bonds cease to bear interest from 1 May of the relevant year.

The sums required to pay coupons and drawn bonds will be entered each year in the state budget in round figures.

Matured coupons and drawn bonds may be cashed at all Financial Delegations, Regional Financial Directorates, District and County (srez) Financial Administrations, Tax Offices, as well as at credit institutions in the country specially authorized for that purpose.

In Belgrade the payment is made by the General Directorate of State Debts, which also performs all service relating to this loan.

Coupons lapse (are time-barred) 5 years after maturity, and amortized bonds 30 years after the date on which they were drawn.

These bonds enjoy all the rights enjoyed by the other state bonds.

Belgrade, 18 June 1921.

**GENERAL DIRECTOR of State Debts** — signed Dr Radoje M. Jovanović
**MINISTER OF FINANCE** — signed Dr Kosta Kumanudi

**AMORTIZATION PLAN / TABLEAU D'AMORTISSEMENT** — a 50-year schedule giving, for each year (Echéance): the annual Interest, the Amortization (principal repaid), the Number of bonds and Series drawn/redeemed, and the remaining Balance of the debt (Solde de la dette). The debt runs from 130,000,000 dinars down to zero over 50 years; totals: interest 172,584,600 dinars, amortization 130,000,000 dinars, 1,300,000 bonds redeemed.

**TALON:** After payment of the last coupon (No. 50), the bearer of this talon will be issued a new coupon sheet.

**COUPONS:** interest coupons of 10 dinars each, payable 1 May / 1 November of successive years.

*Notes / conflicts vs. verified facts:*
- *Currency: the document (including the French column) states all amounts in **dinars** ("100 dinars l'une", "CINQ CENT DINARS", "130,000.000 de dinars"). The verified-facts list "Serbian dinar; French franc" — no "franc" figure appears on this verso; the franc may pertain to the recto (goetzmann0693).*
- *Named individuals: the facts list none, but two officials sign the verso — **Dr Radoje M. Jovanović** (General Director of State Debts) and **Dr Kosta Kumanudi** (Minister of Finance). Kosta Kumanudi was indeed Yugoslav Finance Minister in 1921, consistent with the date.*
- *Table, year 25 balance: printed "96,540.000" but the amortization sequence (year 26 interest 3,781,600 = 4% × 94,540,000; year 26 solde 92,270,000 + amort 2,270,000 = 94,540,000) requires **94,540,000**. Transcribed as printed with this flag; likely an original typographic error (or a smudged "4" read as "6").*
- *"286000 komada obveznica" (physical certificates) is internally consistent: 130,000 singles + 78,000 fives (=390,000 bonds) + 78,000 tens (=780,000 bonds) = 1,300,000 bonds total, matching the facts.*

title: Kingdom of Serbia 4% Amortizable Loan of 1895 — Amortization Plan (Belgrade)
description: This is the official amortization plan for the Kingdom of Serbia's 4% amortizable loan of 1895, a state bond with a nominal capital of 355,292,000 francs. Printed in three parallel languages — French (Tableau d'Amortissement), Serbian Cyrillic (Амортизациони план), and German (Tilgungs-Plan) — it sets out the schedule of half-yearly redemption payments, falling each 13 January and 13 July (new style), beginning in 1896. The conditions panels explain that service of the loan is secured on the newly reorganized Administration des Monopoles, which independently manages the state's tobacco, salt, and petroleum monopolies together with stamp, spirits, customs, and railway receipts, and which is bound to forward these revenues to designated banking houses. The instrument records Serbia's large 1895 conversion operation, by which scattered earlier obligations — including the lottery loans of 1881 and 1888 — were consolidated and refinanced under foreign-supervised monopoly security. Dated Belgrade, 1/13 August 1895, and signed by government delegates and bank representatives (among them Schröder). The austere ruled tables and unadorned typography present the bond as an exercise in fiscal discipline rather than display.
currency: French franc; Serbian dinar; German mark
creator: Kingdom of Serbia
notes: Kingdom of Serbia, 4% Bond, Amortization/Redemption Lottery Plan. Trilingual: Tableau d'Amortissement (French) / Amortizacioni Plan (Serbian) / Tilgungs-Plan (German). Separate schedule sheet accompanying the bond.
transcription:
# Kingdom of Serbia 4% Loan of 1895 — Amortization Plan — Transcription

## Original:

*(The sheet is printed upside-down relative to the scan; when uprighted, the layout is: three parallel conditions-text columns at top [French | Serbian Cyrillic | German], a signature/date band, then the three trilingual title blocks, and beneath them one continuous half-yearly amortization table divided into six paired sub-columns spanning all three language panels.)*

### Title blocks (three parallel languages)

**TABLEAU D'AMORTISSEMENT**
de Francs 355,292.000 capital nominal de l'emprunt amortissable 4% du Royaume de Serbie de l'année 1895.

**АМОРТИЗАЦИОНИ ПЛАН**
за краљ. Српски 4% Зајам од 1895. године у номиналној вредности од 355,292.000 динара.

**TILGUNGS-PLAN**
für das Königlich Serbische 4%ige Anlehen vom Jahre 1895 im Nominalbetrage von 355,292.000 Francs.

### Amortization table

*(Column headers, per panel:)*
- French: **Dates (n. st.) des Payements | Amortissement**
- Serbian: **Рок плаћања | Отплата**
- German: **Datum (n. St.) der Zahlungen | Tilgung**

*(Each date cell is itself trilingual, e.g. "13 Janvier — 1. Јануар — 13. Jänner" and "13 Juillet — 1. Јули — 13. Juli". Two payments per year. The table is a single continuous schedule running from 1896 to 1967; running carry-forward subtotals are labelled "Report — Пренос — Uebertrag Frcs." and "A reporter — За пренос — Fürtrag Frcs.", and it closes with a grand total "Total — Укупно — Summe Frcs. 355,292.000".)*

Representative opening rows (French/leftmost pair of sub-columns):

| Year | 13 Janvier (Amort.) | 13 Juillet (Amort.) |
|------|--------------------:|--------------------:|
| 1896 | 432.000 | 440.000 |
| 1897 | 460.000 | 460.000 |
| 1898 | 470.000 | 480.000 |
| 1899 | 490.000 | 500.000 |
| 1900 | 510.000 | 520.000 |
| 1901 | 530.000 | 540.000 |
| 1902 | 560.000 | 560.000 |
| 1903 | 570.000 | 590.000 |
| 1904 | 600.000 | 610.000 |
| 1905 | 620.000 | 640.000 |
| 1906 | 640.000 | 660.000 |
| 1907 | 680.000 | 680.000 |

*(A reporter — За пренос — Fürtrag Frcs. **13,242.000**)*

Next pair of sub-columns continues 1908–1919 (700.000 … 1,100.000), carrying to **34,552.000**; the schedule proceeds through the Serbian and German panels (intermediate carries 68,832.000 … 123,972.000 … 212,652.000).

Representative closing rows (German/rightmost pair, final years):

| Year | 13 Janvier | 13 Juillet |
|------|-----------:|-----------:|
| 1956 | 4,690.000 | 4,780.000 |
| 1957 | 4,880.000 | 4,970.000 |
| 1958 | 5,080.000 | 5,170.000 |
| … | … | … |
| 1966 | 6,970.000 | 7,100.000 |
| 1967 | 7,250.000 | 7,400.000 |

*(Closing line: **Total — Укупно — Summe Frcs. 355,292.000**)*

*(Individual mid-schedule figures between the sampled rows are legible in the image but are not reproduced exhaustively here; each rises by roughly 10.000 Frcs per half-year.)*

### Conditions text — French panel

8/20 Juillet 1895. Elle administre en toute indépendance les monopoles et les recettes qui ont été donnés en gage à cet emprunt, savoir: le monopole des tabacs, le monopole du sel, le monopole du pétrole, de même que les autres monopoles d'État existants et les recettes du timbre et des taxes sur les boissons. De plus, les recettes nettes des chemins de fer, les recettes des douanes et le produit de l'Obrt devront être également versés à la caisse de l'Administration des monopoles. A cet effet, la Direction du chemin de fer de l'État et celles des douanes ont reçu l'ordre irrévocable de verser, la première chaque mois, les autres tous les dix jours leurs recettes nettes directement à l'Administration des Monopoles.

Le Conseil de l'Administration des monopoles est obligé par la loi à employer les recettes nettes des monopoles du tabac et du pétrole, du monopole du sel après le remboursement des bons du sel, le produit du timbre et des taxes sur les boissons, enfin les recettes nettes des chemins de fer, des douanes et de l'Obrt, en premier lieu et exclusivement au paiement des coupons échus et des obligations sorties de cet emprunt, ainsi qu'au paiement des coupons de l'emprunt à lots de 1881 et des titres sortis au tirage des emprunts à lots de 1881 et de 1888. Il est également obligé par la loi à faire parvenir les recettes précitées aux établissements ou maisons de banque désignés pour le service de ces emprunts de telle façon que les dits établissements ou maisons de banque soient en possession 15 jours au plus tard avant chaque échéance de la somme entière nécessaire au paiement des coupons et des obligations sorties. En conséquence, les recettes et revenus précités ne peuvent recevoir aucune affectation autre que celles ci-dessus mentionnées.

Dans le cas où les recettes de l'Administration des monopoles ne seraient pas suffisantes pour assurer le service annuel de cet emprunt, le Gouvernement Royal Serbe est tenu de parfaire, au moyen d'autres ressources, la somme qui pourrait éventuellement manquer pour un semestre.

Le texte français des obligations fait foi.

Les coupons non présentés à l'encaissement sont prescrits après cinq ans et les obligations sorties après trente ans de leur échéance.

*(The French column as printed in this copy begins mid-sentence at "8/20 Juillet 1895."; the opening clause carried by the German and Serbian columns — naming the autonomous Monopoly Administration set up under the law of that date — is absent/not present above this line.)*

### Conditions text — Serbian panel (Cyrillic)

Гарантијама датим за овај зајам управља Самостална Монополска Управа, установљена на основу закона од 8/20. Јула 1895. Она управља самостално Монополима и приходима заложеним за овај зајам, т. ј. Монополом Дувана, Соли, Петролеума, као и осталим државним Монополима који постоје и приходима од таксених и крчмарских марака.

Даље се имају предавати благајници Монополске управе чист Железнички и Царински приход, као и приход од Обртне порезе. Тога ради упућене су неопозиво Дирекција српских државних Железница, као и све српске царинарнице, да све своје чисте приходе сваког месеца, односно сваких десет дана, предају непосредно Монополској Управи.

Управни одбор Монополске управе законом је обавезан, да све чисте приходе Дуванског и Петролумског Монопола, а по исплати Соњских Обавезница, и Соњског Монопола, даље од таксених и крчмарских Марака и напослетку све Железничко и Царински приход, као и приход од Обртне порезе употребљава првенствено и искључиво на исплату купона и извучених Обавезница из овога зајма, као и купона лутријског зајма од 1881 године и згодитака за исти зајам и Дуванских лозова од 1888 године, и да их за службу овог зајма одређеним исплатним местима предаје тако, да ова најдаље 14 дана пре сваког рока прими потпуну суму, коју за исплату дотичних купона и извучених Обавезница потребе.

Ако приходи Монополске Управе не би били довољни за годишњу службу овога зајма, обавезна је краљевска српска влада, да могући полугодишњи недостатак другим приходима попуни.

Француски текст на Обавезницама меродаван је.

На исплату неподнесени купони застаревају после пет, а извучене Обавезнице после тридесет година, од дана њиховог рока плаћања.

Београд, 1/13. Августа 1895.

### Conditions text — German panel

Mit der Verwaltung der Sicherheiten, welche für dieses Anlehen bestellt sind, ist eine autonome Monopolverwaltung betraut, welche auf Grund des Gesetzes vom 8./20. Juli 1895 eingesetzt worden ist. Dieselbe verwaltet selbstständig die Monopole und die Einnahmen, welche für dieses Anlehen als Pfand gegeben sind, nämlich: das Tabakmonopol, das Salzmonopol, das Petroleummonopol, sowie auch die übrigen bestehenden Staatsmonopole und die Einnahmen aus den Stempel- und Getränkegebühren. Ferner sind die Reineinnahmen der Eisenbahnen, die Zolleinnahmen und das Erträgniss der Obrtsteuer ebenfalls an die Casse der Monopolverwaltung abzuliefern. Zu dem Behufe sind die Direction der Serbischen Staatseisenbahnen, sowie sämmtliche Zollämter Serbiens unwiderruflich angewiesen, ihre ganzen Reineinnahmen jeden Monat, respective jede zehn Tage, unmittelbar an die Monopolverwaltung abzuführen.

Der Verwaltungsrath der Monopolverwaltung ist gesetzlich verpflichtet, alle Reineinnahmen aus den Tabak- und Petroleummonopolen, eventuell nach Einlösung der Salzbons auch aus dem Salzmonopol, dann aus den Stempel- und Getränkegebühren, endlich alle Eingänge der Eisenbahnen, dann aus den Zoll- und Obrtgebühren, in erster Reihe und ausschliesslich zur Bezahlung der fälligen Coupons und der verlosten Obligationen dieses Anlehens, wie auch der Coupons des Losanlehens v. J. 1881 und der Treffer dieses Losanlehens und des Losanlehens v. J. 1888 zu verwenden und an die für den Dienst dieser Anlehen bestimmten Zahlstellen in der Weise abzuführen, dass letztere spätestens 14 Tage vor jedem Termine in den Besitz der vollen, jeweilig zur Auszahlung der fälligen Coupons und der verlosten Obligationen erforderlichen Betrages gesetzt sein sollen. Demnach dürfen diese Einnahmen und Erträgnisse zu gar keinem anderen Zwecke als für den vorerwähnten Dienst verwendet werden.

Falls die Einnahmen der Monopolverwaltung für den jährlichen Dienst dieses Anlehens nicht ausreichen sollten, ist die Königlich Serbische Regierung verpflichtet, den eventuellen halbjährigen Fehlbetrag aus anderen Einkünften zu ergänzen.

Der französische Text der Obligation ist massgebend.

Die nicht zur Einlösung gelangten Coupons verjähren 5 Jahre, und die verlosten Obligationen 30 Jahre nach ihrer Fälligkeit.

Belgrad, am 1./13. August 1895.

**Pour contrôle: — За контролу: — Controllirt:**
[handwritten signature] Schröder

**Le Délégué du Gouvernement Royal Serbe: — Пуномоћник краљевске Српске владе: — Der Delegirte der Königlich Serbischen Regierung:**
[handwritten signature, illegible]

Dated: Belgrade, le 1/13 Août 1895 / Београд, 1/13. Августа 1895 / Belgrad, am 1./13. August 1895.

## English Translation:

### Titles
**AMORTIZATION TABLE** — Of the 355,292,000 Francs nominal capital of the amortizable 4% loan of the Kingdom of Serbia of the year 1895.
**AMORTIZATION PLAN** — For the Royal Serbian 4% Loan of 1895, in a nominal value of 355,292,000 dinars.
**REDEMPTION PLAN** — For the Royal Serbian 4% loan of the year 1895, in the nominal amount of 355,292,000 Francs.

### Table
Columns: "Dates (new style) of the Payments — Amortization." Two payments each year, falling 13 January and 13 July (each date printed in French, Serbian, and German). A single continuous half-yearly schedule running from 1896 to 1967, with increasing redemption amounts (roughly +10,000 Fr. per half-year), running subtotals labelled "Carried forward," and a final grand total of **355,292,000 Francs**. First-year payments are 432,000 (13 Jan 1896) and 440,000 (13 Jul 1896); final-year payments reach 7,250,000 and 7,400,000 in 1967.

### Conditions (composite of the three parallel texts)

The security furnished for this loan is entrusted to an autonomous Monopoly Administration, established under the law of 8/20 July 1895. It independently administers the monopolies and revenues pledged for this loan, namely: the tobacco monopoly, the salt monopoly, the petroleum monopoly, as well as the other existing State monopolies, together with the revenues from stamp duties and beverage (spirits) taxes. Furthermore, the net receipts of the railways, the customs receipts, and the yield of the Obrt (trade) tax must likewise be delivered to the treasury of the Monopoly Administration. To that end, the Directorate of the State Railways and the customs offices are irrevocably instructed to remit their entire net receipts — the former each month, the latter every ten days — directly to the Monopoly Administration.

The Administrative Council of the Monopoly Administration is bound by law to apply all net receipts of the tobacco and petroleum monopolies (and, after redemption of the salt bonds, of the salt monopoly), the yield of stamp and beverage taxes, and finally the net receipts of the railways, customs, and Obrt tax, first and exclusively to the payment of the matured coupons and drawn bonds of this loan, as well as the coupons of the 1881 lottery loan and the winnings (drawn prizes) of the lottery loans of 1881 and 1888; and to forward these sums to the paying agents/banking houses designated for the service of these loans, in such a way that the latter are, at least 14–15 days before each due date, in possession of the full amount required to pay the coupons and drawn bonds. Consequently, these receipts may be applied to no purpose other than the aforementioned service.

Should the receipts of the Monopoly Administration prove insufficient for the annual service of this loan, the Royal Serbian Government is obliged to make up any half-yearly shortfall out of other revenues.

The French text of the bonds is authoritative.

Coupons not presented for collection lapse (are time-barred) after five years, and drawn bonds after thirty years from their due date.

Belgrade, 1/13 August 1895.

### Signatures
The Minister of Finance: [signature, illegible]. — For control / Verified: [signature] Schröder. — The Delegate of the Royal Serbian Government: [signature, illegible].

*(Note on the description: the document denominates in Francs and dinars; the German panel also uses "Francs," so the description's reference to a "German mark" currency is not borne out by the sheet. All other verified facts — 355,292,000 Fr. nominal, 13 Jan / 13 Jul dates, tobacco/salt/petroleum + stamp/spirits/customs/railway security, consolidation of the 1881 & 1888 lottery loans, dating at Belgrade 1/13 Aug 1895, and Schröder's control signature — are confirmed by the image. This is an amortization/redemption payment schedule, not a lottery draw table.)*

title: Kingdom of Serbia 4% Amortisable Gold Bond
description: A bearer amortisable bond (Obligation / Обавезница) of the Kingdom of Serbia, denominated 500 Francs = 500 Dinars in gold, equivalent to R.M. 405 and 200 Austrian gold florins, bearing 4 per cent interest payable semi-annually and redeemable at par in gold over seventy-two years. It is one of 710,584 obligations forming a nominal capital of 355,292,000 Francs, created under the law of 8/20 July 1895. The trilingual text in French, Serbian (Cyrillic) and German, with payment places at Belgrade, Paris, Berlin and Vienna, marks this as Serbia's great unification loan, consolidating earlier debts and pledging the net revenues of the state railways together with the tobacco, salt and petroleum monopolies and the customs, all administered by the Autonomous Monopolies Administration. The instrument's appeal to foreign capital rested less on its restrained engraved guilloche border and small royal crown than on this elaborate pledge of revenue, a sovereign borrower binding its fiscal apparatus to reassure the Berlin and Paris markets. Printed in Vienna.
currency: French franc; Serbian dinar; German mark
creator: Kingdom of Serbia
notes: Kingdom of Serbia, 4% Amortisable Bond, No. 033,596. 500 Francs / 500 Dinara / 500 Franken, bearer. Trilingual layout: French / Serbian / German. Total issue: 118,516 obligations = 59,258,000 francs. Cancelled with red and orange stamps.

# Kingdom of Serbia 4% Amortisable Gold Bond of 500 Francs (Law of 8/20 July 1895) — Transcription

## Original:

(Trilingual bond printed in three parallel columns — French [left], Serbian Cyrillic [centre], German [right]. The figure "4%" appears in each of the four corner medallions and at the top centre. A red circular German fiscal stamp "REICHS-STEMPEL-ABGABE / SECHS VOM TAUSEND / 9" overlaps the upper-left; an orange circular Austrian stamp overlaps the upper-right.)

(Centre top, over all three columns:)
**No. / Броj } 033,596**

### Left column (French)

**ROYAUME DE SERBIE**
**Emprunt 4% amortissable**
créé en vertu de la loi du 8/20 Juillet 1895,
représenté par 710.584 obligations formant un capital nominal de
Francs 355,292.000 = R. M. 287.786.520 = Flor. autr. or 142,116.800,
remboursables en or, au pair, en 72 ans,
*garanti par les revenus affectés par la loi à l'Administration autonome des Monopoles.*

**OBLIGATION**
au porteur de

**CINQ CENTS FRANCS**

Capital nominal
= R.M. 405 = Flor. autr. or 200.

Le présent emprunt est émis en:
355.304 titres de   1 obligation  No. 000.001 à 355.304
 50.000    "     "    5 obligations   "   355.305 " 605.304
 10.528    "     "   10     "          "   605.305 " 710.584

Les obligations de cet emprunt rapporteront annuellement 4% sur leur capital nominal et les intérêts en seront payés semestriellement les 1/13 Janvier et 1/13 Juillet de chaque année, contre remise des coupons.

Le remboursement de cet emprunt aura lieu conformément au tableau d'amortissement annexé aux obligations, dans le délai de 72 ans, par voie de tirages semestriels à effectuer les 1/13 Avril et 1/13 Octobre de chaque année.

Les obligations sorties au tirage seront remboursées à leur valeur nominale, en or, à la première échéance de coupon postérieure au tirage.

Les obligations et coupons du présent emprunt sont exempts de tous impôts, taxes ou retenues, existants ou futurs en Serbie.

Le paiement des coupons échus et des obligations sorties aura lieu, au choix des porteurs:
à **Belgrade** } à raison de 10 Francs en or pour chaque coupon et de 500 Francs or
» **Paris** }    pour chaque obligation de 500 Francs nominal.
» **Berlin**  à raison de R. M. 8.10 en or, pour chaque coupon et de R. M. 405 pour chaque obligation de R. M. 405 nominal,
» **Vienne**  à raison de 4 Flor. autr. or pour chaque coupon et de 200 Flor. autr. or pour chaque obligation de 200 Florins nominal,
auprès de tous les établissements et maisons de banque qui seront désignés par le Ministre des Finances du Royaume de Serbie.

Pour assurer le paiement régulier des coupons et des obligations de cet emprunt, le Gouvernement Serbe affecte comme garanties
a) les revenus nets des Chemins de fer de l'État, lignes Belgrade-Vranje, Nisch-Pirot, Semendria-Velika Plana et Lapovo-Kragujevatz,
b) les recettes du timbre et des taxes sur les boissons,
c) le produit du monopole des tabacs,
d) les recettes des douanes,
e) le produit de l'impôt de l'Obrt et
f) le produit du monopole du sel, après remboursement des bons du sel; enfin,
g) le produit du monopole du pétrole.
La gestion des garanties affectées au présent emprunt est confiée à une Administration autonome des Monopoles, qui a été établie en vertu de la loi du [line ends here on the sheet]

### Centre column (Serbian Cyrillic)

**КРАЉЕВИНА СРБИЈА**
**4% Зајам с отплатом**
Закључен на основу закона од 8/20. Јула 1895,
подељен на 710.584 Обавезнице у укупном номиналном износу од Динара 355,292.000 у злату = Марака 287,786.520 = аустријских форината у злату 142,116.800
**ИСПЛАТАН ЗА 72 ГОДИНЕ — АЛ ПАРИ — У ЗЛАТУ**
обезбеђени приходима који су по закону о овоме зајму уступљени Самосталној Монополској Управи.

**ОБАВЕЗНИЦА**
на доносоца од

**ПЕТ СТОТИНА ДИНАРА**

номиналног капитала
= Марака 405 = аустријских форината у злату 200.

Овај зајам издат је у:
355.304 дела   Бр. 000.001—355.304 од по  1 Обавезнице
 50.000 делова   "  355.305—605.304   "   "  5 Обавезница
 10.528    "      "  605.305—710.584   "   " 10     "

Обавезнице овога зајма укамаћују се са 4% годишње од номиналног капитала, а интерес ће се плаћати полугодишње, уз предају дотичног купона, 1/13. Јануара и 1/13. Јула сваке године.

Исплата зајма вршиће се по приложеном амортизационом плану у току 72 године путем полугодишњих вучења, која ће се обављати 1/13. Априла и 1/13. Октобра сваке године.

Извучене обавезнице исплаћиваће се у номиналном износу у злату и то о првом року који је за исплату купона утврђен.

Обавезнице и купони овога зајма ослобођени су од свију пореза, такса и других дажбина, како садашњих тако и будућих, које у Србији постоје.

Имаоцима купона, којима је истекао рок, као и имаоцима извучених Обавезница, остављено је на вољу да их наплаћују:
у **Београду** } са 10 Динара у злату за сваки купон и са 500 Динара у злату
» **Паризу** }  за сваку Обавезницу од номиналних 500 Динара,
» **Берлину**  са Марака 8.10 у злату за сваки купон и са Марака 405 у злату за сваку Обавезницу од номиналних 405 Марака,
» **Бечу**  са 4 аустријске златне форинте за сваки купон и са 200 аустријских златних форината за сваку Обавезницу од номиналних 200 аустријских златних форината,
и код свију завода и банака, које краљевско српски Министар финансија буде наименовао за исплату овога зајма.

За обезбеду исплате купона и извучених Обавезница овога зајма даје краљевско српска влада као гарантије:
а) чист приход државних железничких пруга Београд-Врање, Ниш-Пирот, Смедерево-Велика Плана и Лапово-Крагујевац,
б) приходе од таксених и крчмаринских марака,
в) чист приход Дуванског Монопола,
г) Царински приход,
д) чист приход од Обртне порезе, и
ђ) чист приход Монопола Соли, по исплати Солских Обавезница; даље
е) чист приход Монопола Петролеума.

### Right column (German)

**KÖNIGREICH SERBIEN**
**4% amortisable Anleihe**
emittirt auf Grund des Gesetzes vom 8./20. Juli 1895,
bestehend aus 710.584 Obligationen über ein Gesammt-Nominal-Capital von
Francs 355,292.000 = R. M. 287,786.520 = österr. Goldgulden 142,116.800,
rückzahlbar innerhalb 72 Jahren al pari in Gold,
*sichergestellt durch die der autonomen Monopolverwaltung für diese Anleihe gesetzlich überwiesenen Einkünfte.*

**OBLIGATION**
auf den Inhaber über

**FÜNFHUNDERT FRANCS**

Nominal-Capital
= R.M. 405 = österr. Goldgulden 200.

Die gegenwärtige Anleihe ist ausgegeben in:
355.304 Abschnitten Nr. 000.001 bis 355.304, à 1 Obligation
 50.000    "    " 355.305 " 605.304, " 5 Obligationen
 10.528    "    " 605.305 " 710.584, " 10   "

Die Obligationen dieser Anleihe werden mit 4% jährlich vom Nominal-capitale verzinst und die Zinsen halbjährlich am 1./13. Jänner und 1./13. Juli jedes Jahres gegen Einlieferung der betreffenden Coupons bezahlt.

Die Tilgung der Anleihe erfolgt gemäss dem beigedruckten Tilgungsplane innerhalb 72 Jahren, im Wege halbjährlicher Ziehungen, die am 1./13. April und 1./13. October jedes Jahres stattfinden.

Die Auszahlung der gezogenen Obligationen geschieht zum Nominalbetrage in Gold, und zwar an dem auf die Auslosung folgenden nächsten Couponzahlungstermine.

Die Obligationen und Coupons dieses Anlehens sind befreit von allen in Serbien bestehenden und in Zukunft noch einzuführenden Steuern, Abgaben und Abzügen.

Die Zahlung der fälligen Coupons und der gezogenen Obligationen findet nach Wahl der Inhaber statt:
in **Belgrad** } mit 10 Francs in Gold für jeden Coupon und mit 500 Francs in Gold
» **Paris** }   für jede Obligation im Nominalbetrage von 500 Francs.
» **Berlin,** mit Mark 8.10 in Gold für jeden Coupon und mit Mark 405 in Gold für jede Obligation im Nominalbetrage von 405 Mark,
» **Wien,** mit österr. Goldgulden 4 für jeden Coupon und mit österr. Goldgulden 200 für jede Obligation im Nominalbetrage von österr. Goldgulden 200,
bei allen Anstalten und Bankhäusern, welche von dem Kön. Serbischen Finanzminister als Zahlstellen für dieses Anlehen designirt werden.

Zur Sicherstellung der Zahlung der Coupons und der verlosten Obligationen dieses Anlehens bestellt die Königlich Serbische Regierung als Pfänder:
a) die Reineinnahme der Staatseisenbahnlinien Belgrad-Vranje, Nisch-Pirot, Semendria-Velika Plana und Lapovo-Kragujevatz,
b) die Einnahmen aus den Stempel- und Getränkegebühren,
c) das Erträgniss des Tabakmonopoles,
d) die Zolleinnahmen,
e) das Erträgniss der Obrtsteuer, und
f) das Erträgniss des Salzmonopoles, nach Rückzahlung der hierauf haftenden Salzbons; ferner
g) das Erträgniss des Petroleummonopoles.

## English Translation:

(Trilingual bond in French, Serbian and German; the three columns carry identical text. A single English rendering follows.)

**No. 033,596**

**KINGDOM OF SERBIA**
**4% Amortisable Loan**
created by virtue of the law of 8/20 July 1895,
represented by 710,584 obligations forming a nominal capital of
Francs 355,292,000 = R.M. 287,786,520 = Austrian gold florins 142,116,800,
repayable in gold, at par, over 72 years,
*guaranteed by the revenues assigned by law to the Autonomous Administration of the Monopolies.*

**OBLIGATION (BOND)**
to bearer for

**FIVE HUNDRED FRANCS** (centre column: FIVE HUNDRED DINARS)

Nominal capital
= R.M. 405 = Austrian gold florins 200.

The present loan is issued in:
355,304 certificates of  1 obligation   No. 000,001 to 355,304
 50,000 certificates of  5 obligations   No. 355,305 to 605,304
 10,528 certificates of 10 obligations   No. 605,305 to 710,584

The obligations of this loan shall yield 4% annually on their nominal capital, and the interest shall be paid half-yearly on 1/13 January and 1/13 July of each year, against surrender of the coupons.

The repayment of this loan shall take place in conformity with the amortisation schedule annexed to the obligations, within the term of 72 years, by way of half-yearly drawings to be carried out on 1/13 April and 1/13 October of each year.

The obligations drawn in the lottery shall be repaid at their nominal value, in gold, at the first coupon maturity following the drawing.

The obligations and coupons of this loan are exempt from all taxes, dues or deductions, existing or future, in Serbia.

Payment of matured coupons and of drawn obligations shall take place, at the holders' choice:
at **Belgrade** } at the rate of 10 Francs in gold for each coupon and 500 Francs gold for each obligation of 500 Francs nominal.
» **Paris** } (as above)
» **Berlin** at the rate of R.M. 8.10 in gold for each coupon and R.M. 405 for each obligation of R.M. 405 nominal,
» **Vienna** at the rate of 4 Austrian gold florins for each coupon and 200 Austrian gold florins for each obligation of 200 florins nominal,
at all establishments and banking houses that shall be designated by the Minister of Finance of the Kingdom of Serbia.

To secure the regular payment of the coupons and obligations of this loan, the Serbian Government pledges as guarantees:
a) the net revenues of the State Railways, the Belgrade-Vranje, Nisch-Pirot, Semendria-Velika Plana and Lapovo-Kragujevatz lines,
b) the receipts of the stamp duty and of the taxes on beverages,
c) the proceeds of the tobacco monopoly,
d) the customs receipts,
e) the proceeds of the Obrt (trade/business) tax, and
f) the proceeds of the salt monopoly, after redemption of the salt bonds secured upon it; and finally,
g) the proceeds of the petroleum monopoly.
The management of the guarantees assigned to this loan is entrusted to an Autonomous Administration of the Monopolies, established by virtue of the law of [text ends at the foot of the sheet].

title: Chinese Imperial Government 4½% Gold Loan Bond, Berlin, 1898
description: A £100 sterling bearer bond of the Chinese Imperial Government's 4½ per cent Gold Loan of 1898, printed bilingually in English and German and styled as both a "Bond for £100 Sterling" and an "Obligation über £100 Sterling." Interest runs from 1 March 1898 at four and a half per cent, payable each 1 March and 1 September; the principal is repayable by annual drawings beginning January 1899, redeemable in sterling at the Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation in London and at the Deutsch-Asiatische Bank in Berlin, which acted as agent for the emission. The bond forms part of a total nominal loan of £16,000,000 sterling, divided among denominations of £25, £50, £100, and £500. Authorised by Imperial Edict of 2 March 1896 and secured upon the revenues of the Imperial Maritime Customs of the Treaty Ports together with a first charge on the likin of certain ports and districts, the issue records how the late Qing state, pressed by indemnity and reform after the war with Japan, raised capital through Anglo-German banking houses and pledged its customs receipts to foreign creditors. The ornate engraved frame, executed at the Reichsdruckerei in Berlin, joins European scrollwork with Chinese motifs and a red seal in seal-script characters, the visual idiom of a loan negotiated between empires.
creator: Chinese Imperial Government / Deutsch-Asiatische Bank
notes: Chinese Imperial Government, 4½% Gold Loan of 1898, £100 Sterling bearer bond. Deutsch-Asiatische Bank. Total issue: 1,600,000 Sterling. Cancelled (holes punched). 2-page document; see goetzmann0698 for verso and conditions.

# Transcription of Chinese Imperial Government 4.5% Gold Loan Bond (1898)

## Main Text (English Side - Left):

**CHINESE IMPERIAL GOVERNMENT**

**4.5% GOLD LOAN OF 1898**

**BEARER BOND**

**£100 STERLING**

[Ornate decorative border with imperial Chinese motifs and geometric patterns]

**KAISERLICH CHINESISCHE**

**4½% STAATSANLEIHE IN GOLD VON 1898**

**OBLIGATION ÜBER £100 STERLING**

## Body Text (Bilingual - Center):

**English version includes:**
- Bond denomination: £100 Sterling
- Interest rate: 4.5% per annum
- Reference to Deutsch-Asiatische Bank
- Total loan amount: £1,600,000 Sterling
- Payment terms and conditions regarding coupon interest
- [Extensive small print terms and conditions - partially legible but largely illegible due to image resolution]

**German version includes:**
- Corresponding terms: Obligation über £100 Sterling
- Interest rate specification
- Bank intermediary: Deutsch-Asiatische Bank
- Loan specifications and conditions
- [Extensive small print conditions - partially legible]

## Notable Markings:

- **Cancellation:** Bond cancelled by systematic hole-punch marks around the border
- **Stamps:** Red circular seals/stamps visible on left margin (Chinese Imperial Government seals)
- **Red Label:** Red rectangular label affixed to center of document (covering some text)
- **Serial/Reference Numbers:** Visible in corners (£100 denominations marked at corners)

title: Chinese Imperial Government Gold Loan Bond (Reverse)
description: The reverse of a £100 sterling bond of the Chinese Imperial Government 4½% Gold Loan of 1898 (Littera C), a bilingual sheet whose English and German columns reproduce extracts from the loan agreement ("Extracts" / "Auszug ... aus der Obligation erwähnten Vertrage") and, at right, the Table of Drawings or Amortisations-Plan. Interest fell due half-yearly on 1 March and 1 September. The amortisation schedule begins with the drawing of 1 March 1899, when £115,225 became repayable against an outstanding balance of £15,884,775, figures that fix the total issue at £16,000,000. Floated in 1898 through the Deutsch-Asiatische Bank, the loan belongs to the wave of European-secured Chinese borrowing that followed the indemnity owed to Japan after the war of 1894–95; the gold-denominated, sterling-fixed terms and the prominent redemption table reveal creditors' demand for security and predictable amortisation in lending to a hard-pressed empire. The engraved frame is restrained, its corner ornament conventional rather than emblematic.
creator: Chinese Imperial Government / Deutsch-Asiatische Bank
notes: Chinese Imperial Government, 4½% Gold Loan of 1898. Littera C, No. 076230. £100 Sterling. Verso: bilingual English/German extracts, Table of Drawings (amortization schedule), conditions. Interest payable 1st March and 1st September. 2-page document; see goetzmann0697 for recto.

title: Chinese Imperial Government Coupon Sheet (Upper Row)
description: This is a horizontal strip of interest coupons detached from a £100 bearer bond of the Chinese Imperial Government 4½% Gold Loan of 1898. Fifteen coupons, numbered 76 through 90 and all keyed to Bond C. No. 076230, each promise £2 5s.—a half-year's interest at 4½ per cent—falling due at successive March and September dates, the printed examples running from 1 March 1936 to 1 March 1943. Each is payable in London at the Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation or in Berlin at the Deutsch-Asiatische Bank. The loan, raised to fund the indemnity China owed Japan after the war of 1894–95, bound the empire's revenues to European creditors; the twin London-Berlin paying agents register the Anglo-German banking rivalry over China's finances. Printed in orange on engraved guilloche borders, the coupons are cancelled by hole-punch, marking interest already drawn.
creator: Chinese Imperial Government / Deutsch-Asiatische Bank
notes: Chinese Imperial Government, 4½% Gold Loan of 1898. Interest coupon sheet (upper portion), 14 cancelled coupons. Belongs to bond No. 076230; see goetzmann0697–0698.

title: Government of Honduras State Railway Loan Bearer Obligation, Paris, 1869
description: This is a bearer obligation of the Government of Honduras for its 1869 State Railway loan (Emprunt du Gouvernement de Honduras 1869 / Obligation des Chemins de Fer de l'État), a 300-franc bond carrying coupons and bearing interest from 1 March 1869. The text appears twice on the sheet, in French above and Spanish below, with the great "300 Francs au Porteur" / "300 Francos al Portador" numerals dominating each half and a block of coupons attached at the right. The bond was issued at Paris and dated 1 February 1869, payable in gold francs free of tax, secured by a mortgage on the State railway, with the loan partly guaranteed by France and England. The obligation belongs to the wave of European-placed railway loans by which a small Central American state sought to finance an interoceanic line, drawing on Paris and London capital it could not raise at home. The restrained engraved frame and the national arms favour the sober probity expected of a sovereign credit instrument rather than ornament for its own sake.
creator: Republic of Honduras
notes: Republic of Honduras, Government Railway Loan of 1869. Two 500-franc bearer bonds, No. 74444. Emission of 20,000 obligations of 500 francs. French/Spanish bilingual. Paris, January 30, 1869. Large coupon sheet at right. 2-page document; see goetzmann0702 for verso.
transcription:
# Government of Honduras Loan — Bearer Obligation of the State Railways, 300 Francs, Paris 1869 — Transcription

## Original:

*(Single sheet. LEFT PANEL carries two engraved bonds — the FRENCH text in the upper half, the SPANISH text in the lower half — each with a large ornamental "300" as the central device. A block of coupons fills the RIGHT PANEL. Printed throughout in blue; the running legal text is set in very light blue and is only partly legible, the numeral/heading strokes and the bold words being the clearest.)*

---

### FRENCH BOND (upper half)

*(Vertical banner along the left edge, wrapped on a scroll:)*

**EMPRUNT DU GOUVERNEMENT DE HONDURAS · 1869**

*(Heading lines, centred to the right of the banner:)*

**OBLIGATION DES CHEMINS DE FER DE L'ÉTAT**

**ÉMISSION DE 207,509 OBLIGATIONS DE 300 FRANCS**

[…] SOIT 12 LIVRES STERLING

*(Large central numeral device, with cursive title overlaid:)*

**300** — *Obligation de 300 Francs au Porteur*

**JOUISSANCE DU 1ᵉʳ MARS 1869.**

*(Body text, in light print and partly obscured by the numerals — legible fragments only; [illegible] marks gaps:)*

[Le porteur a droit à] un intérêt annuel de **20 francs**, payable par semestre, et au remboursement en [illegible ; se termine par « -sept »] années, suivant le tableau ci-contre. [Les] paiements [se feront] en or, à Paris, sans impôt ni déduction [quelconque].

[La présente Obligation est garantie par première] hypothèque sur le chemin de fer [en voie d'exécution, bois] et forêts de l'État, hypothèque [consentie pour …] des deux emprunts de 1867 [et 1869]. [La neutralité de la voie … la France, l'Angleterre] et les États-Unis d'Amérique. Les [produits …] inter-océanique […] les titres de la même émission […] par une Action de jouissance, portant [le même numéro] et donnant [droit …] revenus nets de la ligne pendant une durée [de quinze années après le rem]boursement. […Cortez…] [illegible]

**Paris, le 28 février 1869.**

*(Attestation and signature, right side:)*

**LE MINISTRE DE HONDURAS À P[ARIS],**
Agissant en son nom et en celui de son collègue,
en vertu de leurs pleins pouvoirs.

*(large cursive signature)* — [illegible signature]

---

### SPANISH BOND (lower half)

*(National arms of Honduras at upper left — cornucopias, wreath, the legend "…REPUBLICA DE HONDURAS…" and the date "1821"; the word "…LIBRE" visible. Serial number on a scroll:)*

**Nº 7854**

*(Vertical banner along the left edge:)*

**EMPRÉSTITO DEL GOBIERNO DE HONDURAS · 1869**

*(Heading lines:)*

**OBLIGACIONES DE LOS FERRO CARRILES DEL ESTADO**

**EMISION DE 207,509 OBLIGACIONES DE 300 FRANCOS**

IGUALES Á 60 CUPONES, Ó SEA Á 12 LIBRAS ESTERLINAS

*(Large central numeral device, with cursive title overlaid:)*

**300** — *Obligacion de 300 Francos al Portador*

**GOCE DEL 1º DE MARZO DE 1869.**

*(Body text, light print, legible fragments only:)*

[El portador tiene derecho á] un interés anual de **veinte francos**, pagadero por semestre, y al reembolso [en … años], con arreglo al [cuadro] al margen. [Los] pagos [se harán] en oro, en Paris, sin impuesto ni deduccion [alguna].

[La presente Obligacion está garantida con primera hipoteca sobre] el ferro carril que va eje[cutándose, bosques y montes del Estado], hipoteca otorgada [para …] la amortizacion de los dos [empréstitos de 1867 y 1869 …] la Francia, la Inglaterra [y los Estados-Unidos de América …] especialmente [para] servir integral[mente …] quedarán reemplazados con [una Acción de goce …] en el tercio del producto [… una parte proporcional en el terce]ro [… hasta el reembolso] por completo de este empréstito.

**Paris, á 28 de febrero de 1869.**

*(Attestation and signature:)*

**EL MINISTRO DE HONDURAS,**
Obrando en su propio nombre y en [el de su colega],
en u[so de sus plenos poderes].

*(large cursive signature — same as French)* — [illegible signature]

*(Circular hand-stamp near the arms, largely [illegible]; the name "L. GUY[…]" and a place — apparently MARSEILLE — can be partly made out.)*

---

### COUPON SHEET (right panel)

*(A grid of detachable coupons, printed in blue, each of identical form. Bilingual French/English text; representative coupons read:)*

**GOUVERNEMENT DE HONDURAS**
Intérêt du 1ᵉʳ [Septembre / Mars] 18[..] — Interest due 1st [March / September] 18[..]
**8 sh.**
**EIGHT SHILLINGS**
payable in London

*(Each coupon is stamped with the bond serial, e.g. 7854. Coupons are semi-annual (1st March / 1st September) and each pays 8 shillings; dated examples visible include "1st March 1882" and "1st September 1884". Individual coupon dates otherwise [illegible] at this resolution.)*

---

## English Translation:

**GOVERNMENT OF HONDURAS LOAN, 1869**

**OBLIGATION [BOND] OF THE STATE RAILWAYS**

**ISSUE OF 207,509 OBLIGATIONS OF 300 FRANCS**

[…] equal to 12 pounds sterling

**300** — *Obligation of 300 Francs to Bearer*

**INTEREST RUNNING FROM 1 MARCH 1869.**

[The bearer is entitled to] an annual interest of **20 francs**, payable half-yearly, and to redemption over [illegible; ends in "-seven"] years, in accordance with the table opposite. [The] payments [shall be made] in gold, at Paris, free of any tax or deduction [whatsoever].

[The present Obligation is secured by a first] mortgage on the railway [under construction, the woods] and forests of the State, a mortgage [granted for …] the two loans of 1867 [and 1869]. [The neutrality of the line … France, England] and the United States of America. The [proceeds …] interoceanic […] the bonds of the same issue […] by a "jouissance" (participation) Share, bearing [the same number] and giving [the right to a share in] the net revenues of the line for a period [of fifteen years after re]demption. […Cortez (Puerto Cortés)…] [illegible]

**Paris, 28 February 1869.**

**THE MINISTER OF HONDURAS AT PARIS,**
Acting in his own name and in that of his colleague,
by virtue of their full powers.

*(signature — illegible)*

**Coupon (each):**
**GOVERNMENT OF HONDURAS** — Interest due 1st [March/September] 18[..] — **8 sh. / EIGHT SHILLINGS — payable in London.**

*(The Spanish half of the sheet repeats the same text: "Empréstito del Gobierno de Honduras 1869 — Obligations of the State Railways — Issue of 207,509 Obligations of 300 Francos, equal to 60 coupons, i.e. to 12 pounds sterling — Obligation of 300 Francos to Bearer — Interest running from 1 March 1869 — annual interest of twenty francs, payable half-yearly … in gold, at Paris, free of tax or deduction … secured by a first mortgage on the railway under construction and the forests of the State … France, England and the United States … Paris, 28 February 1869 — The Minister of Honduras." Serial No. 7854.)*

---

*Notes on conflicts with the supplied description / prior transcription:*
- *Denomination is **300 francs** (large numeral, cursive title, and both heading lines all read 300), confirming the verified fact and correcting the earlier garbled "500."*
- *Emission count reads **207,509 obligations of 300 francs** (in both the French and Spanish heading lines). 207,509 × 300 fr = 62,252,700 fr ≈ £2,490,000 at 25 fr/£ — matching the known 1869 Honduras loan — which corroborates the reading. This replaces the earlier fabricated "2,059" / "301,004."*
- *Serial number is **7854** (stamped on the Spanish bond and on the coupons), not the earlier "74444."*
- *Printed **date is 28 February 1869**; the supplied description's "1 February 1869" appears incorrect. Interest/"jouissance" runs from **1 March 1869**.*
- *The clause on guarantees names **France, England AND the United States of America** as guaranteeing the line's neutrality; the description mentions only France and England.*
- *Coupons are engraved **8 shillings** each, semi-annual, "payable in London" (bilingual French/English), consistent with 20 fr annual interest (≈16 sh/yr ⇒ the sheet's "12 livres sterling / 60 coupons" describes the full 300-fr / 30-year run).*

title: Honduras Government Loan Bond, Paris, 1869 (verso)
description: The verso of a bearer bond for the Honduras Government Loan of 1869 (Emprunt du Gouvernement de Honduras / Staats-Anleihe der Regierung von Honduras), a sovereign loan floated at Paris by Victor Herran, the Republic's minister there. The upper portion sets out the loan's conditions in parallel English, French, and German, declaring an emission of 207,509 obligations of 300 francs (£12) on the bearer, dated Paris, 28 February 1869, the proceeds pledged to complete the State Railways; interest is payable 1 March and 1 September, with triennial drawings on 1 February and 1 August. A printed amortization table (Tableau d'Amortissement) schedules redemptions every March and September from 1869 to 1886, summing to the full 207,509 bonds. Backed by railway revenues and citing the neutrality of England and the United States, the issue exemplifies the speculative European lending to small Latin American states that culminated in Honduras's notorious default. Ornament is confined to engraved blue lettering and patterned borders; the cover panels reprise the denomination and terms.

notes: Republic of Honduras, Government Loan of 1869, £12 bond No. 74444, verso and conditions of issue. 2-page document; see goetzmann0701 for recto.

title: Republic of Bolivia Bearer Bond with Coupon Sheet
description: A bearer bond (bono al portador) of the Republic of Bolivia for one thousand pesos fuertes (Mil Pesos Fuertes), issued under the Empréstito de 1870 and dated at La Paz de Ayacucho on 1 January 1870. The attached coupon sheet pays interest semi-annually, each coupon promising forty pesos fuertes — eighty per year, an effective rate of eight per cent — with maturities running to 1889 and 1890, so a term of roughly twenty years; principal and interest were payable in Santiago de Chile. The engrossed text reproduces the enabling decree of the Asamblea Nacional Constituyente: the loan was authorised expressly to fund the conversion of the debased "feble" silver coinage into moneda de buena ley, the State bearing the conversion loss and the holder no charge beyond five per cent toward minting costs. The bond thus speaks to Bolivia's mid-century monetary disorder and its recourse to foreign credit to restore a sound silver standard. The central vignette presents an allegory of abundance — a seated figure amid sheaves of wheat, fruit, and grapes — engraved in the neoclassical manner; the imprint identifies the printer as the National Bank Note Company of New York (Compañía Nacional de Billetes de Banco, Nueva York).

title: City of Naples Unified Debt Bond
description: A bearer bond (Cartella al Portatore / Titre au Porteur) of the Unified 5% Debt of the City of Naples (Debito Unificato 5% della Città di Napoli), of 1,000 lire nominal capital bearing a 5% annual rente payable half-yearly from 1 January 1881. It forms part of a consolidated municipal loan of 86,953,310 lire, guaranteed by the Royal Government under the Law of 14 May 1881 and authorised by council and provincial resolutions of late 1880. It was issued at Naples on 30 November 1881 over the signatures of the Sindaco and Delegato Governativo. The instrument folds a southern municipality's debt into the fiscal architecture of the young Italian kingdom, the sovereign guarantee lending a provincial loan the credit of the state. The engraved frame, a winged putto cradling the crowned Savoy arms amid classicising scrollwork, binds civic borrowing to dynastic legitimacy. An attached sheet of 98 coupons, dated 1931 to 1979, attests the loan's long life.
creator: Municipalita della Citta di Napoli
notes: Debito Unificato 5% della Città di Napoli, Serie D, No. 20027. Capitale Nominale Lire 1,000. Bilingual Italian/French. Emission of L.It. 80,053,310. Naples, November 20, 1880. Stamped "Debito Nuovo Foglio" 1931–1975. New coupon sheet of 98 coupons valid July 1, 1931 to December 31, 1979 attached.

title: Liberian Government 7% Loan Arrear Interest Certificate, London, 1899
description: A certificate of arrear interest issued by the Council of Foreign Bondholders in London, certifying £176 3s 4d of unpaid interest accrued on a £100 bond of the Liberian Government 7% External Loan of 1871. The arrears span 1 February 1874 to 31 March 1899, and the certificate is made redeemable under the Arrangement approved by the law of the Liberian Congress of 23 January 1899, an extract of which is printed on the reverse. Dated at Council House, 17 Moorgate Street, on 1 October 1899, and signed by the Council's Secretary. The document records the settlement of a sovereign default: Liberia's first foreign loan, raised in the London market, fell into arrears within three years, and a quarter-century of unpaid coupons was here converted into a fresh redeemable claim brokered by the bondholders' own protective committee. Printed by Bradbury, Wilkinson & Co. within a finely engraved green border, the certificate's restrained classical lettering conveys the institutional authority of late-Victorian London finance.
creator: Council of Foreign Bondholders, London
notes: Liberian Government 7% External Loan of 1871. Certificate of arrear interest (£176:3:4) on Bond No. 0865 for £100. Period: February 1, 1874 to March 31, 1899. Redeemable per Arrangement approved by Liberian Congress, January 23, 1899. For the Council of Foreign Bondholders. London, October 1, 1899. Printed by Bradbury, Wilkinson & Co.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Liberian Government 7% External Loan of 1871.**

---

This Certificate represents the interest in arrear (amounting to £176 : 3 : 4) on Bond No. 0865 for £100 of the above Loan, from 1st February, 1874, to 31st March, 1899, and is redeemable in accordance with the conditions of the Arrangement approved by the Law of the Liberian Congress of 23rd January, 1899, an extract from which is printed on the back hereof.

FOR THE COUNCIL OF FOREIGN BONDHOLDERS,

[Signature]
Secretary.

COUNCIL HOUSE,
17, MOORGATE STREET, LONDON, E.C.,
1st October, 1899.

---

**Notable markings:**

- Ornate decorative green border on left margin with text "COUNCIL OF FOREIGN BONDHOLDERS" running vertically
- Handwritten signature of Secretary
- Printed by Bradbury, Wilkinson & Co., Limited, London (noted in margins)
- Bond No. 0865

title: República Mexicana 5% Consolidated External Debt Gold Bond, 1899
description: This is a bearer bond (bono al portador) of the República Mexicana for the five-per-cent Consolidated External Debt loan of 1899 (Bono de la Deuda Consolidada Exterior Mexicana del 5% del año de 1899). Its face value is stated in parallel across the markets that took the loan: £200 sterling, $970 United States gold dollars, and 4,080 German Reichsmark, with capital and interest expressly payable in gold ("Capital é Intereses pagaderos en oro"). The bond forms one of 198,000 pieces, in series of £20, £100 and £200, of a total issue of about $22,700,000, raised under contracts of 2 June and 3 July 1899 and serviced in Berlin, Paris, London and Mexico. Issued under President Díaz and finance minister Limantour, it consolidated and refunded Mexico's older external obligations on the strength of the regime's restored credit, the gold clause reassuring European and American holders. The engraved Mexican eagle on cactus, grasping a serpent, asserts sovereign authority over the instrument.
creator: Republica Mexicana / Ministerio de Hacienda
notes: República Mexicana, Bono de la Deuda Consolidada Exterior Mexicana, Serie C, No. 621768. £200 / $970. 3% of 1899. Mexico City, 1899.

The Spanish and headings are given here; the German, English and French bodies follow, and the English column serves as the official translation reproduced below.)*
