*(Upper left and upper right corners, repeated:)* **SÉRIE C** — **Nº 024766**

*(Engraved arch over the eagle vignette:)* **REPÚBLICA MEXICANA**

*(Denomination box, left:)* **£200**  *(Denomination box, right:)* **$970 U.S. GOLD**

**Bono de la Deuda Consolidada Exterior Mexicana**

**DEL 5 P%. DEL — AÑO DE 1899.**

**CAPITAL É INTERESES PAGADEROS EN ORO**

**ESTE BONO PERTENECE Á UNA DE LAS SÉRIES DE 198,000 BONOS DE LA NUMERACIÓN Y VALORES SIGUIENTES:**

*(Series table — columns: series · numbering · pieces · £ value · Mark value (D.R.W.) · U.S. gold $ value)*

- SÉRIE A Nºˢ 000.001 Á 004.000. — 4.000 BONOS DE £ 1.000. Ó M. 20.400. D.R.W. Ó $ ORO E.U. 4.850.
- " B " 004.001 Á 016.000. — 12.000 " " £ 500. Ó M. 10.200. " " Ó $ 2.425.
- " C " 016.001 Á 041.000. — 25.000 " " £ 200. Ó M. 4.080. " " Ó $ 970.
- " D " 041.001 Á 098.000. — 57.000 " " £ 100. Ó M. 2.040. " " Ó $ 485.
- " E " 098.001 Á 198.000. — 100.000 " " £ 20. Ó M. 408. " " Ó $ 97.

**198,000 BONOS REPRESENTANDO UN VALOR TOTAL DE £ 22.700.000. Ó M. 463.080.000. D.R.W. Ó $ ORO E.U. 110.095.000.**

**[Column 1 — Spanish body:]**

Los Estados Unidos Mexicanos reconocen deber por este bono al portador la suma de Lstg 200 ó 4.080 Marcos del Imperio Alemán, al cambio fijo de 20.40 Marcos por libra esterlina, ó 970 pesos americanos oro al cambio fijo de Dollars 4.85 centavos oro E.U. por libra esterlina.

Esta suma forma parte del Empréstito de Lstg 22.700.000 ó 463.080.000 Marcos del Imperio Alemán é 110.095.000 pesos oro de los Estados Unidos del América, emitido de conformidad con la ley del 2 de Junio de 1899 y con el contrato celebrado en Berlín, París y Londres con las casas de S. Bleichroder, Deutsche Bank y Dresdner Bank de Berlín; J. S. Morgan y Cía de Londres, J. P. Morgan y Cía de Nueva York y Banco Nacional de México; contrato que en lo conducente se inserta al respaldo de este bono.

El Tesoro Federal pagará intereses sobre la expresada suma de Lstg 200 ó 4.080 Marcos del Imperio Alemán é Dollars 970 oro E.U. á razón de 5% anual, por trimestres vencidos y la redimirá por compras ó por sorteos á la par con arreglo al artículo 4º del citado contrato.

Todos los pagos tendrán lugar á elección del portador, en Berlín, Londres, Nueva York y en Francfort s/M en la moneda correspondiente y en Amsterdam á otro lugar que las casas de emisión designen, en la moneda de curso al cambio á la vista sobre Berlín.

El capital que este bono representa y los intereses que devengue, estarán siempre exentos de toda clase de contribuciones é impuestos mexicanos existentes ó futuros cualquiera que sea su naturaleza, y están garantizados con la consignación del 62% de las contribuciones sobre la importación y exportación que se recauden en la República como se fija en el artículo 5º del precitado contrato.

**[Column 2 — German body:]**

Die Vereinigten Staaten von Mexico anerkennen durch diese Obligation, dem Inhaber derselben die Summe von £ 200 oder zum festen Course von Mark 20.40 für ein Pfund Sterling 4.080 Mark D.R.W. oder zum festen Course von Doll. 4.85 für ein Pfund Sterling 970 Ver. St. Gold Dollars zu verschulden.

Diese Summe bildet einen Theil der Anleihe von £ 22.700.000 oder 463.080.000 Deutsche Reichsmark oder 110.095.000 Ver. St. Gold Dollars, welche ausgegeben ist in Ausführung des Gesetzes vom 2 Juni 1899 und des Vertrages vom 2 und 3 Juli 1899, der in Berlin, Paris [und] London mit den Bankhäusern S. Bleichröder, Deutsche Bank und Dresdner Bank zu Berlin, J. S. Morgan & Co. zu London, J. P. Morgan & Co. zu New York und der Nationalbank zu Mexico abgeschlossen und auszugsweise anstehend abgedruckt ist.

Das Bundes-Schatzamt verzinst die vorbezeichnete Summe von £ 200 oder 4.080 Deutsche Reichsmark oder 970 Ver. St. Gold Dollars mit 5% jährlich in dreimonatlichen Terminen und amortisirt die Anleihe durch Rückkauf im Markt oder Auslosung zum Nennwerthe nach Massgabe des Artikel 4 vorbezeichneten Vertrages.

Alle Zahlungen finden nach Wahl des Inhabers in Berlin, London, New York und Frankfurt a/M in entsprechender Landeswährung, in Amsterdam und anderen durch die Emissionsbanken zu bezeichnenden Plätzen ebenfalls in Landeswährung, der Betrag der Mark zum Sichtwechselcurse auf Berlin umgerechnet, statt.

Der durch diese Obligation dargestellte Capitalbetrag sowie die daraus erwachsenden Zinsen sind für immer von jeder itzt bestehenden oder zukünftiger mexicanischer Steuer oder Auflage befreit und sind sichergestellt durch die Überweisung von 62% der in der Republik erhobenen Ein- und Ausfuhrzölle, wie dies in Art. 5 des oben bezeichneten Vertrages festgestellt ist.

**[Column 3 — English body (as printed):]**

The United States of Mexico acknowledge themselves by this Bond to be indebted to the Bearer thereof in the sum of Lstg 200 or at the fixed exchange of 20 M. 40 pf per £ sterling 4.080 Marks currency of the German Empire, or at the fixed exchange of Doll. 4.85 U.S. gold Doll. 970 U.S. gold Dollars.

This sum forms part of the Loan of Lstg 22.700.000 or Marks 463.080.000 currency of the German Empire or Doll. 110.095.000 U.S. gold Dollars, issued in pursuance of the law of the 2nd June 1899 and of the Agreement dated 1st, 2nd and 3rd July entered into in Berlin, Paris and London with the Banking firms of S. Bleichroder, Deutsche Bank and Dresdner Bank of Berlin, J. S. Morgan & Co. of London, J. P. Morgan & Co. of New York and the National Bank of Mexico, extracts from which Agreement are printed at the back hereof.

The Treasury of the Federal Government will pay interest on the above named sum of £ 200 or 4.080 Marks currency of the German Empire or Doll. 970 U.S. gold Dollars at the rate of 5 per centum per annum quarterly, and will redeem the same by purchase in the market or by drawings at par, in accordance with Art. 4 of the aforesaid Agreement.

All payments will be made either in Berlin, London, New York or Frankfort on Main, at the option of the holder, in the above stated currencies, and at Amsterdam and other places appointed by the issuing Banks, in their respective currencies, the amount in Marks being calculated at the rate of exchange at sight on Berlin.

The capital represented by this Bond, as well as the interest thereon, are for ever exempt from any Mexican taxes or assessments which may at present exist or may hereafter be imposed, and are secured by the assignment of 62 per cent of the export and import dues levied by the Republic, as stated in Art. 5 of the aforesaid Agreement.

**[Column 4 — French body:]**

Les États-Unis du Mexique reconnaissent par la présente obligation devoir au porteur la somme de Lstg 200 ou au change fixe de 20 m.40 par Lstg 1, 4.080 marks monnaie courante de l'Empire Allemand, ou au change fixe de Doll. 4.85 or É.U. par Lstg 1, 970 dollars américains or.

Cette somme forme partie de l'Emprunt de Lstg 22.700.000 ou Marks 463.080.000 monnaie courante de l'Empire Allemand, Dollars or É.U. 110.095.000, émis en vertu de la loi du 2 Juin 1899 et du contrat passé le 1er et 3 juillet 1899 à Berlin, Paris et Londres avec les maisons de Banque S. Bleichröder, Deutsche Bank & Dresdner Bank de Berlin, J. S. Morgan & Co. de Londres, J. P. Morgan & Co. de New York et la Banque Nationale du Mexique, des extraits duquel se trouvent imprimés au dos de la présente.

Le Trésor du Gouvernement Fédéral paiera les intérêts sur la somme précitée de Lstg 200 ou Marks 4.080 monnaie courante de l'Empire Allemand, ou Dollars or É.U. 970 au taux de 5 pour cent par an, trimestriellement et l'amortira, soit au moyen d'achats en Bourse, soit au moyen d'achats par tirages au pair conformément à l'article 4 du contrat précité. Tous paiements auront lieu à Berlin, Londres, New York, Francfort s/M au choix du porteur et dans les monnaies courantes précitées, et à Amsterdam ou toutes places désignées par les Banques d'émission dans les monnaies courantes de l'endroit, le montant des marks calculé au cours du change à vue sur Berlin.

Le montant du capital représenté par cette obligation, aussi bien que l'intérêt en provenant, sont exempts à tout jamais de toute taxe ou impôt mexicains existants et à venir, de quelque nature qu'ils soient, et sont garantis par l'assignation de 62 pour cent des droits d'importation et exportation perçus par la République, ainsi qu'il est prévu par l'article 5 du susdit contrat.

**[Signature captions, bottom, left to right:]**

EL TESORERO GENERAL DE LA FEDERACION.  —  EL CONTADOR DE LA TESORERIA GENERAL.  —  EL DELEGADO ESPECIAL DE HACIENDA.

*(Three manuscript signatures below the captions; one legible-ish at right ends "C. R[omero?]" — otherwise [illegible].)*

*(Red diagonal overprint stamp across lower-right of the body, mostly [illegible]; fragments legible: "…main office…", "Received", "…10 … Street…", "…thereof and the rights…".)*

*(Ornate green-and-black engraved border; central vignette of the Mexican eagle on a cactus grasping a serpent.)*

## English Translation:

*(Header cartouche:)* **M. 4,080 D.R.W.** (Deutsche Reichs-Währung / German imperial currency)
*(Corners:)* **SERIES C — No. 024766**
*(Arch:)* **UNITED MEXICAN STATES / MEXICAN REPUBLIC**
*(Denomination boxes:)* **£200** | **$970 U.S. GOLD**

**Bond of the Mexican Consolidated External Debt**

**OF 5 PER CENT — YEAR 1899.**

**CAPITAL AND INTEREST PAYABLE IN GOLD**

**THIS BOND BELONGS TO ONE OF THE SERIES OF 198,000 BONDS OF THE FOLLOWING NUMBERING AND VALUES:**

- Series A, Nos. 000,001 to 004,000 — 4,000 bonds of £1,000, or M. 20,400 D.R.W., or U.S. gold $4,850.
- Series B, Nos. 004,001 to 016,000 — 12,000 bonds of £500, or M. 10,200, or $2,425.
- Series C, Nos. 016,001 to 041,000 — 25,000 bonds of £200, or M. 4,080, or $970.
- Series D, Nos. 041,001 to 098,000 — 57,000 bonds of £100, or M. 2,040, or $485.
- Series E, Nos. 098,001 to 198,000 — 100,000 bonds of £20, or M. 408, or $97.

**198,000 bonds representing a total value of £22,700,000, or M. 463,080,000 D.R.W., or U.S. gold $110,095,000.**

**[Body of the obligation — the official English text printed on the bond:]**

The United States of Mexico acknowledge themselves by this Bond to be indebted to the Bearer thereof in the sum of £200 sterling, or at the fixed exchange of 20 Marks 40 pfennig per £ sterling, 4,080 Marks currency of the German Empire, or at the fixed exchange of $4.85 U.S. gold, 970 U.S. gold Dollars.

This sum forms part of the Loan of £22,700,000, or 463,080,000 Marks currency of the German Empire, or 110,095,000 U.S. gold Dollars, issued in pursuance of the law of the 2nd of June 1899 and of the Agreement dated the 1st, 2nd and 3rd of July [1899] entered into in Berlin, Paris and London with the banking firms of S. Bleichroder, Deutsche Bank and Dresdner Bank of Berlin, J. S. Morgan & Co. of London, J. P. Morgan & Co. of New York, and the National Bank of Mexico — extracts from which Agreement are printed on the back hereof.

The Treasury of the Federal Government will pay interest on the above-named sum of £200, or 4,080 Marks currency of the German Empire, or 970 U.S. gold Dollars, at the rate of 5 per centum per annum, quarterly in arrears, and will redeem the same by purchase in the market or by drawings at par, in accordance with Article 4 of the aforesaid Agreement.

All payments will be made, at the option of the holder, in Berlin, London, New York or Frankfort-on-Main in the corresponding currency, and at Amsterdam or such other place as the issuing houses shall designate, in the current currency, the amount in Marks being converted at the rate of exchange at sight on Berlin.

The capital represented by this Bond, as well as the interest it earns, shall at all times be exempt from every class of Mexican tax or impost, present or future, of whatever nature, and are secured by the assignment of 62 per cent of the import and export duties collected in the Republic, as fixed in Article 5 of the aforesaid contract.

**[Signature captions:]** The Treasurer General of the Federation. — The Comptroller of the General Treasury. — The Special Delegate of the Treasury (Hacienda).

*(Note: the German, French and Spanish columns are word-for-word equivalents of the English text above.)*

title: Mexico Gold Treasury Bond
description: A bearer treasury bond (Bono del Tesoro al Portador) of the Federal Government of the United Mexican States, styled "6% Oro, 10 Años, de 1913." This certificate represents the smallest tranche of the Primera Serie "A": a face value of $Mex.195, rendered in gold and quoted simultaneously in francs (Fr.505), pounds (£20), marks (RMK.409), United States dollars ($EU.97), and Dutch guilders (Fl.P-B.242), and paying six per cent annually. The whole series comprised 222,358 bonds across three denominations—4,050 of $Mex.1,950, 10,298 of $Mex.975, and 208,010 of $Mex.195—for a total of $Mex.58,500,000. Dated México, 8 June 1913, the loan was floated by the Huerta government to fund a treasury strained by revolution, courting European and American capital through its multi-currency promise. The engraved national arms—the golden eagle upon a nopal, devouring a serpent—asserts sovereign credit at a moment when that sovereignty was bitterly contested.
currency: Mexican peso; French franc; British pound sterling; German mark; US dollar; Dutch guilder
creator: Gobierno Federal de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos / Tesoreria General de la Federacion
notes: República Mexicana, Bono del Tesoro, 6% Oro 10 años de 1913. Primera Serie A, No. 054614. $Mex.195 / Fr.505 / £20 / RMk.409 / $EU.97 / Fl.P.B.242. Bearer. Total issue: 222,358 bonds. Mexico City, January 15, 1913. Signed by Tesorero General and Delegado Especial de Hacienda.

title: Provisional Bearer Receipt for the 4% Ottoman Unified Converted Debt, Paris, 1933
description: A provisional bearer receipt (récépissé provisoire au porteur) issued by the Conseil de la Dette Publique Répartie de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman, delivered against the surrender of one unit obligation of the 4% Emprunt Ottoman Dette Convertie Unifiée, nominal Frs. 500 (or £20, or Ltqs 22). It was issued through the Hamburg branch of the Deutsche Bank und Disconto-Gesellschaft and dated Paris, 1 October 1933. The receipt rests on the Commission decision of 9 June 1933, taken under Article 49 of the Treaty of Lausanne, which apportioned the nominal capital of the old Ottoman public debt among the empire's successor states. A central table sets out those États Débiteurs — Greece, Bulgaria, Yugoslavia, Albania, Transjordan, the Hedjaz, Asir, Yemen and others — with each one's proportion and share of capital. The document thus records the financial liquidation of a vanished empire: a debt once owed by Constantinople, redistributed by treaty across the territories carved from it, its engraved frame and guilloche borders lending the settlement the gravity of a sovereign bond.
creator: Conseil de la Dette Publique Repartie de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman
notes: Conseil de la Dette Publique Répartie de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman, Reçu Provisoire au Porteur No. 0,951,240. Emprunt Ottoman Dette Convertie Unifiée 4%. German paying agent: Deutsche Bank und Disconto-Gesellschaft Filiale Hamburg. Coupon sheet attached below with orange cancellation stamps.
transcription:
# Provisional Bearer Receipt for the 4% Ottoman Unified Converted Debt, Paris, 1933 — Transcription

## Original (French):

**CONSEIL DE LA DETTE PUBLIQUE RÉPARTIE DE L'ANCIEN EMPIRE OTTOMAN**

**RÉCÉPISSÉ PROVISOIRE AU PORTEUR N° 0,951,240**

Délivré contre remise d'une obligation unitaire d'un capital nominal de Frs. 500 ou £ 20 ou Ltqs. 22

**DE L'EMPRUNT OTTOMAN DETTE CONVERTIE UNIFIÉE 4°/o.**

Conformément à la décision en date du 9 Juin 1933 de la Commission pour la répartition du capital nominal de la Dette Publique de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman, créée en vertu de l'Article 49 du Traité de Lausanne, le présent récépissé a été délivré, contre retrait de l'obligation susvisée, par l'entremise de la Banque

**DEUTSCHE BANK UND DISCONTO-GESELLSCHAFT FILIALE HAMBURG**

pour constater :

a) que la quote-part de la Turquie a fait l'objet d'une émission spéciale ; que les quotes-parts de l'Italie (Dodécanèse), de la Palestine, de l'Irak et des États du Levant sous Mandat français n'ont été entièrement amorties ;

b) que, par suite de ces règlements, à la date d'émission du présent récépissé, le capital nominal non amorti de l'emprunt susvisé se trouve réduit aux montants indiqués dans le tableau ci-dessous, incombant aux États qui en sont les débiteurs à raison des montants énoncés en regard de chacun d'eux.

**ÉTATS DÉBITEURS**

| ÉTATS DÉBITEURS | Proportions % | Capital non amorti sur les 1,371,021 titres unitaires de l'emprunt susvisé restant en circulation à la date d'émission du présent récépissé — Frs. | ou £ | ou Ltqs. | Capital non amorti de chaque titre unitaire de l'emprunt susvisé — Frs. | ou £ | ou Ltqs. |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| GRÈCE | 48,46 | 84.518.841 | 3.380.754 | 3.718.829 | 61,65 | 2,47 | 2,71 |
| BULGARIE | 7,75 | 13.518.659 | 540.746 | 594.821 | 9,86 | 0,40 | 0,44 |
| YOUGOSLAVIE | 24,17 | 42.153.955 | 1.686.138 | 1.854.774 | 30,75 | 1,23 | 1,35 |
| ALBANIE | 7,27 | 12.677.818 | 507.113 | 557.824 | 9,25 | 0,37 | 0,41 |
| TRANSJORDANIE (y compris la Caza de Maan) | 2,88 | 5.024.250 | 200.970 | 221.067 | 3,66 | 0,14 | 0,16 |
| HEDJAZ | 5,01 | 8.736.652 | 349.467 | 384.414 | 6,37 | 0,25 | 0,28 |
| ASSYR | 0,08 | 152.295 | 6.092 | 6.701 | 0,11 | 0,01 | 0,01 |
| YEMEN | 3,95 | 6.887.250 | 275.490 | 303.039 | 5,02 | 0,20 | 0,22 |
| NEDJD | 0,43 | 752.477 | 30.099 | 33.109 | 0,55 | 0,02 | 0,02 |
| TOTAL | 100,— | 174.422.227 | 6.976.389 | 7.674.578 | 127,22 | 5,09 | 5,60 |

Le présent récépissé provisoire est exclusivement émis pour constater les droits des porteurs des titres retirés de la circulation sur les États débiteurs indiqués ci-dessus. Ces États ne pourront procéder, par le rachat de ces récépissés, à l'amortissement du capital nominal à leur charge dans la Dette Ottomane. Le Conseil de la Dette, détenteur des titres ottomans retirés de la circulation, aura seul qualité pour procéder aux paiements ou autres opérations qui pourront être effectués au moyen du présent récépissé provisoire, en vue de l'application des Accords conclus avec les États pour le règlement de leurs quotes-parts dans la Dette Ottomane. Les coupons n°s 1 à 12 attachés au présent récépissé serviront à constater les dits paiements ou opérations, dont les modalités seront portées, en temps utile, à la connaissance des porteurs, par des avis publiés par le Conseil de la Dette.

**POUR CONTRÔLE,**

Par Délégation du Conseil :

[signature illegible]

**POUR LE CONSEIL DE LA DETTE PUBLIQUE RÉPARTIE DE L'ANCIEN EMPIRE OTTOMAN,**

Le Président :

[signature illegible]

Paris, le 1er Octobre 1933.

## English Translation:

**COUNCIL OF THE APPORTIONED PUBLIC DEBT OF THE FORMER OTTOMAN EMPIRE**

**PROVISIONAL BEARER RECEIPT No. 0,951,240**

Issued in exchange for the surrender of one unit bond of a nominal capital of Frs. 500 or £ 20 or Ltqs. 22

**OF THE OTTOMAN LOAN, UNIFIED CONVERTED DEBT 4%.**

In accordance with the decision dated 9 June 1933 of the Commission for the apportionment of the nominal capital of the Public Debt of the Former Ottoman Empire, created by virtue of Article 49 of the Treaty of Lausanne, the present receipt has been issued, against withdrawal of the above-mentioned bond, through the agency of the Bank

**DEUTSCHE BANK UND DISCONTO-GESELLSCHAFT HAMBURG BRANCH**

to certify:

a) that the share of Turkey has been the subject of a special issue; that the shares of Italy (Dodecanese), of Palestine, of Iraq and of the Levant States under French Mandate have not been entirely amortized;

b) that, as a result of these settlements, at the date of issue of the present receipt, the unamortized nominal capital of the above-mentioned loan is reduced to the amounts indicated in the table below, falling to the States which are the debtors thereof, in the amounts set out opposite each of them.

**DEBTOR STATES**

| DEBTOR STATES | Proportions % | Unamortized capital on the 1,371,021 unit bonds of the above loan remaining in circulation at the date of issue of the present receipt — Frs. | or £ | or Ltqs. | Unamortized capital of each unit bond of the above loan — Frs. | or £ | or Ltqs. |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| GREECE | 48.46 | 84,518,841 | 3,380,754 | 3,718,829 | 61.65 | 2.47 | 2.71 |
| BULGARIA | 7.75 | 13,518,659 | 540,746 | 594,821 | 9.86 | 0.40 | 0.44 |
| YUGOSLAVIA | 24.17 | 42,153,955 | 1,686,138 | 1,854,774 | 30.75 | 1.23 | 1.35 |
| ALBANIA | 7.27 | 12,677,818 | 507,113 | 557,824 | 9.25 | 0.37 | 0.41 |
| TRANSJORDAN (including the Caza of Maan) | 2.88 | 5,024,250 | 200,970 | 221,067 | 3.66 | 0.14 | 0.16 |
| HEDJAZ | 5.01 | 8,736,652 | 349,467 | 384,414 | 6.37 | 0.25 | 0.28 |
| ASSYR | 0.08 | 152,295 | 6,092 | 6,701 | 0.11 | 0.01 | 0.01 |
| YEMEN | 3.95 | 6,887,250 | 275,490 | 303,039 | 5.02 | 0.20 | 0.22 |
| NEDJD | 0.43 | 752,477 | 30,099 | 33,109 | 0.55 | 0.02 | 0.02 |
| TOTAL | 100.— | 174,422,227 | 6,976,389 | 7,674,578 | 127.22 | 5.09 | 5.60 |

The present provisional receipt is issued exclusively to establish the rights of the holders of the bonds withdrawn from circulation against the debtor States indicated above. These States may not proceed, by the repurchase of these receipts, to the amortization of the nominal capital chargeable to them in the Ottoman Debt. The Debt Council, holder of the Ottoman bonds withdrawn from circulation, shall alone be qualified to carry out the payments or other operations that may be effected by means of the present provisional receipt, with a view to the application of the Agreements concluded with the States for the settlement of their shares in the Ottoman Debt. Coupons Nos. 1 to 12 attached to the present receipt shall serve to record the said payments or operations, the terms of which shall be brought, in due course, to the knowledge of the holders, by notices published by the Debt Council.

**FOR CONTROL,**

By Delegation of the Council:

[signature illegible]

**FOR THE COUNCIL OF THE APPORTIONED PUBLIC DEBT OF THE FORMER OTTOMAN EMPIRE,**

The President:

[signature illegible]

Paris, 1 October 1933.

title: Société Ottomane du Chemin de Fer de Damas-Hamah Bond, Constantinople, 1910
description: This is a bearer bond (obligation au porteur) of the Société Ottomane du Chemin de Fer de Damas-Hamah et Prolongements, the Ottoman company chartered to build and operate railways radiating from Damascus and Hamah. It carries a face value of 500 francs, or 22 livres turques, and pays 4 percent annual interest — 20 francs, or 88 piastres — by half-yearly coupons of 10 francs falling due on 1 March and 1 September, with redemption at par by lot beginning 1 September 1910. Issued at Constantinople and dated 1 February 1910, it belongs to the "Emprunt 1909 — Homs-Tripoli" loan, a series of 43,747 obligations sanctioned by the shareholders' assembly of 16 December 1909 to finance the line's extension toward Tripoli on the Syrian coast. The bilingual French and Ottoman Turkish text, the recital of imperial firmans granting the concession, and the dense violet engraving register the close entanglement of European finance and Ottoman state infrastructure on the eve of the empire's final decade.
creator: Societe Ottomane des Chemins de Fer de Damas-Hamah et Prolongements

title: Ottoman Public Debt Bearer Receipt, Amsterdam, 1930
description: A bearer receipt (Reçu au Porteur) issued by the Conseil de la Dette Publique Répartie de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman, recording a nominal claim of Ltqs 2.20, equivalent to £2.0.0 (deux livres sterling) or Frs fr. 50. The document does not bear interest; it is exchangeable (Échangeable) for a bearer title of the Bons Représentatifs des Annuités Arriérées de la Dette Publique Ottomane, "Série A" 1928, with holders invited to combine receipts up to Ltqs 22 / £20 / Frs fr. 500 to claim unitary bonds. Issued at Amsterdam on 22 April 1930 by the Banque de Paris et des Pays-Bas, par procuration, and bearing a Dutch 20-centime revenue stamp. It was created under Article IV of the contract of 13 June 1928 between the Turkish Republic and the bondholders, ratified by Turkish law of 1 December 1928, which apportioned the late Ottoman Empire's debt among its successor states. The trilingual face and Parisian printing (Imprimerie Chaix) attest to the international administration of a defunct empire's obligations; unredeemed receipts were to lapse to the benefit of the debtor states within one year.

title: Ottoman Public Debt Bearer Receipt, Amsterdam, 1930
description: This bearer receipt (Reçu au Porteur) was issued by the Conseil de la Dette Publique Répartie de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman for a nominal amount of Ltqs 4.40 / £4 / Frs fr. 100, the figures stated in Turkish, sterling and French currencies side by side. It records a claim under article IV, paragraph 9 of the contract of 13 June 1928 between the Turkish Republic and the bondholders' representatives, ratified by the Grande Assemblée Nationale de Turquie by the law of 1 December 1928, and is exchangeable for a bearer title of the "Bons Représentatifs des Annuités Arriérées de la Dette Publique Ottomane 'Série A' 1928" once holders assemble receipts totalling Ltqs 22, £20 or Frs fr. 500. Issued at Amsterdam on 22 April 1930 by the Banque de Paris et des Pays-Bas, par procuration, and bearing a Dutch 40-centime revenue stamp, the document is a fragment of the protracted apportionment of the Ottoman public debt among the empire's successor states. Created solely for the mechanics of conversion, it expressly bore no interest and lapsed within a year, a plain administrative instrument marking the orderly liquidation of a vast inherited liability.

title: Republic of Peru 100-soles bond for the Arica customs house, Lima, 1871
description: A bearer bond (bono al portador) of the República del Perú with a face value of one hundred soles (cien soles), bearing six per cent annual interest and five per cent amortization, payable to the holder under the terms of the law of 4 February 1869. Issued at Lima on 17 July 1871, the bond was floated to fund construction of the customs house at Arica (la Aduana de Arica), authorized by the law of 28 January 1869, its proceeds earmarked for that single public work. The instrument illustrates how the guano-era Peruvian treasury raised dedicated capital for port and fiscal infrastructure on the southern coast, pledging customs revenue to service the loan. The engraved head bears the national arms flanked by flags, cannon, and a cornucopia; at right a seated female figure in classical drapery raises a staff topped by a Phrygian cap and holds a laurel wreath and sun-emblazoned shield, an allegory of liberty and the republic in the neoclassical idiom favored by nineteenth-century state securities.
creator: Republica del Peru / Direccion del Credito
notes: República del Perú, 100 Soles bearer bond, No. 165. Construcción de la Aduana de Arica, per law of January 28, 1869. 6% interest, 5% amortization. Lima, July 17, 1871. Signed by Felipe Medina and José Félix García, El Director del Crédito.

title: City of Warsaw Bearer Bond
description: A bearer bond (Obligation / Obligacja) issued by the Municipality of the City Capital of Warsaw for its 4½% loan of 1931. The face value is payable, at the holder's choice, in any of three currencies: 284 French francs, 99.188 Polish złoty, or 57.666 Swiss francs, with interest of 4½% per annum paid free of Polish income tax on securities. Drawn at Warsaw on 1 June 1931, the instrument forms part of a total emission of 55,912,500 French francs (equivalently 19,527,243.75 złoty or 11,352,993.75 Swiss francs), authorized by the Municipal Council on 10 June 1930 and sanctioned by the Government Commissioner overseeing the conversion of the city's earlier bonds. The triple-currency clause and the tax exemption reveal a municipality courting foreign capital while shielding it from currency risk in the unsettled financial climate following the 1929 crash. Crowning the green frame is Warsaw's heraldic Syrenka, the sword-bearing mermaid, set above the city's mural crown; engraved vignettes of the capital's monuments fill the lower corners.

title: Republic of Poland Dollar Premium Loan Bond, Warsaw, 1931
description: A bearer bond (obligacja) of the Republic of Poland (Rzeczpospolita Polska), issued as Series III of the Dollar Premium Loan (Premjowa Pożyczka Dolarowa). Its face value is five United States dollars — five dollars "STANÓW ZJEDNOCZONYCH AMERYKI" — fixed at the equivalent of 44.57 złotych, and it carries interest at 4% per annum. Drawn up at Warsaw and dated 1 February 1931 under the law of 1 February 1930, the instrument belongs to the inter-war Polish treasury's effort to fund itself by dollar-denominated borrowing from a domestic public, hedging against the złoty's frailty by pegging the obligation to a foreign currency and sweetening it with a lottery premium. The engraving is austere and architectural: a large numeral 5, restrained classical borders and a guilloche field, the typographic discipline of state credit rather than ornamental flourish.
creator: Republic of Poland

**REPUBLIC OF POLAND.**

**BOND**

**OF SERIES III OF THE DOLLAR PREMIUM LOAN**

**OF THE VALUE OF 5 DOLLARS**

**OF THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA**

**THAT IS, 44.57 ZŁOTYCH.**

(Body paragraph:)

Series III of the Dollar Premium Loan is issued by virtue of the Act of 1 February 1930 (Journal of Laws of the Republic of Poland No. 10, item 66). Interest on this loan, at the rate of 4 per hundred per annum, is payable semi-annually in arrears on 1 February and 1 August of each year against surrender of the appropriate coupons.

This bond participates in the drawings of premiums, held publicly at the Ministry of the Treasury six times a year in accordance with the drawing plan set out on the reverse side of the bond. The winning of a premium does not exclude the bond from subsequent drawings.

This bond is subject to redemption on 1 February 1941; however, after 1 February 1936 the Minister of the Treasury shall have the right to order early redemption.

The redemption of this bond and the payment of interest and premiums shall be effected in dollars of the United States of America or in złoty at the rate of: 1 dollar equals the sum of 8.914 złoty. The choice of currency belongs to the holder of the bond.

This bond becomes statute-barred on 1 February 1971, or after the lapse of 30 years from the day on which its payment falls due in the case of early redemption. Coupons of the bond become statute-barred after 5 years from the day on which they become payable, and premiums after the lapse of 10 years from the day of their drawing.

*Warsaw, 1 February 1931.*

title: Republic of Poland Dollar Premium Loan Bond, Warsaw, 1931
description: This is a bearer premium-loan bond (obligacja) of Series III of the Dollar Premium Loan (Premjowa Pożyczka Dolarowa) issued by the Republic of Poland (Rzeczpospolita Polska) through its Ministry of the Treasury. The face value is five United States dollars, rendered also as 44.57 złotych, with attached coupons each worth 0.10 dollar (0.89 złoty). Issued at Warsaw on 1 February 1931, under the authorising statute of 1 February 1930, it carried prize draws alongside scheduled redemption. Dollar denomination here speaks to a young republic seeking hard-currency credit and the confidence of holders amid the deflationary strains then settling over Europe; pairing fixed coupons with lottery premia was a familiar device for coaxing small savers into public debt. The restrained, symmetrical engraving and plain lettering favour sober official assurance over ornament.
creator: Republic of Poland
notes: Republic of Poland, Obligacja Serji III Premjowej Pożyczki Dolarowej. No. 1450669. $5 = zł 44.57. Warsaw, February 1, 1931. Same issue as goetzmann0715. Three coupons (nos. 16, 19, 20) attached at bottom.

title: Portugal External Debt Provisional Certificate
description: This is a provisional certificate of Three per Cent. Deferred Stock issued in the conversion of the external funded debt of Portugal, denominated in pounds sterling and printed in English for circulation in the London market. It certifies that the holder is entitled to a residual sum of deferred stock — here a mere "Three pence" entered by hand — bearing interest from 1 January 1863, with the promise that such fractional balances may be exchanged, once accumulated to a sufficient amount, for permanent bonds of £50, £100, £200, or £500. Drawn up by the Financial Agent of the Portuguese Government in London under decrees of 18 December 1852 and 23 March 1853, and printed by Whiting, the instrument records the unglamorous machinery of sovereign debt restructuring: the tidy ledger detail of a few pence held over speaks to a conversion that left small odd balances which only a paper acknowledgement could carry until they grew tradable. Its restrained engraved border and copperplate lettering project the sober creditworthiness London investors expected.
creator: Financial Agency of the Portuguese Government in London
notes: Conversion of the External Debt of Portugal, Decree of December 18, 1852. Provisional Certificate of Deferred Stock. No. 1463. Three Per Cent. Deferred Stock, interest from January 1, 1863. Amount: Three Pence. Signed by Financial Agent of the Portuguese Government in London. Printed by Whiting, London.
transcription:
# Transcription

**CONVERSION OF THE EXTERNAL DEBT OF PORTUGAL,**
**UNDER THE DECREE OF 18th DECEMBER, 1852.**

---

**PROVISIONAL CERTIFICATE OF DEFERRED STOCK.**

---

No. [1463]                                                                                          [Date]

In conformity with Article 25th of the Regulations approved of by a Decree of Her Most Faithful Majesty the Queen of Portugal, under date of 23d March, 1853, by which the Financial Agency in London is authorised to carry out the Conversion of the External Funded Debt of Portugal, in pursuance of the previous Decree of 18th December, 1852:

This is to Certify THAT the HOLDER hereof is entitled to the Sum of

**Three Pounds** [amount in handwriting - illegible]

**THREE PER CENT. DEFERRED STOCK,** bearing Interest from the 1st of January, 1863, which Sum proceeds from the above Conversion.

---

This Certificate will be exchanged on presentation with others, making up a sufficient amount, for Bonds of any of the following Series, viz. £50, £100, £200, or £500, into which the said Stock is divided; and for any remaining Balance a fresh Certificate will be given.

---

FINANCIAL AGENT OF THE PORTUGUESE GOVERNMENT IN LONDON.

WHITING, LONDON.

---

- Date field and signature field visible but largely unfilled/illegible
- Printed by: Whiting, London (bottom left)
- Multiple circular medallion ornaments integrated into border design

title: Portuguese External Fund Zero-Coupon Bond, Lisbon, 1902
description: This is a single zero-coupon bond (Título Especial Sem Juro) of the Fundo Externo Portuguez, third series, issued by the Junta do Crédito Público at Lisbon and dated 31 December 1902. It carries a face value of 308$000 réis, set out in parallel as £6 12s 8d, 166.67 francs, 135.34 marks, and 79.34 florins — the multi-currency tariff of a debt placed across the European exchanges. Bearing no interest, the title promised only its eventual reimbursement, drawn by public lottery from a total emission of 477,517 obligations; the holder, as the text concedes, gained "nenhuma vantagem além do seu opportuno reembolso." Such interest-free, sinking-fund paper let a hard-pressed treasury spread redemption over time under the debt-conversion law of 14 May 1902, the Junta do Crédito Público being governed by the decrees of 1893 and 1900. A French one-per-cent fiscal stamp and the inked note "rembᵗ juillet 1903" record its circulation and retirement in Paris. The engraved Portuguese arms within laurel and acanthus lend sober official dignity.
creator: Junta de Crédito Público
notes: Fundo Externo Portuguez, Título Especial sem Juro, 3.ª Série (Portuguese External Fund, Special Zero-Coupon Bond). No. 303486. Réis 308$000 / Libras 6-12-8 / Francos 166,67 / Marcos 135,34 / Florins 79,34. Emission of 477,517 obligations. Lisbon, Junta do Crédito Público, December 22, 1902. Revenue stamp 1% dated 1903.
transcription:
# Special Non-Interest-Bearing Bond of the Portuguese External Fund, 3rd Series — Transcription

## Original:

(Top-right corner, handwritten in blue ink): remb.ᵗ · juillet 1948 / Present

(Top-right, circular French fiscal stamp): **FONDS D[ES] ÉTATS ÉTRANGERS · 1 % · PLEIN TARIF · PARIS · 18 (11) 1903**

(Engraved Portuguese royal arms within laurel at centre-top)

**TITULO ESPECIAL SEM JURO**
**DO**
**FUNDO EXTERNO PORTUGUEZ**
**3.ª SÉRIE**

Emissão de 477:517 obrigações, especiaes sem juro, amortizaveis ao par por sorteio, em 198 semestres conforme a tabella inscripta no verso, quando o forem as obrigações do juro de 3 por cento da 3.ª série, que tiverem os mesmos numeros d'estes titulos especiaes sendo, 387:517 titulos de 1 obrigação n.ºˢ 1 a 387:515, 477:516 e 477:517 e 18:000 titulos de 5 obrigações n.ºˢ 387:516 a 477:515 conforme a Obrigação Geral adeante transcripta e traduzida no verso.

**Titulo de 1 Obrigação**
**N.º 303:486**
**Réis 30$000 — Libras 6-12-8 — Francos 166,67 — Marcos 135,34 — Florins 79,34**

**⁕ OBRIGAÇÃO GERAL ⁕**

Tendo sido auctorizado o Governo Portuguez, pela carta de lei de 14 de maio de 1902, a converter a divida externa, consolidada e amortizavel, em 3 séries de titulos do typo unico de juro de 3 %, amortizaveis em 198 semestres, e devendo, em relação á 3.ª série de 477:517 obrigações destinadas á conversão da divida amortizavel externa de 4 ½ %, ser creadas outras tantas obrigações especiaes, sem juro, amortizaveis ao par, por sorteio, quando o forem as obrigações do juro de 3 % da referida série que tiverem os mesmos numeros d'estes titulos especiaes:

Declaro, em nome do dito Governo, eu, Fernando Mattozo Santos, Ministro e Secretario de Estado dos Negocios da Fazenda e interino dos Estrangeiros, o seguinte, com fundamento na citada lei e no decreto de 9 de agosto ultimo:

A Nação Portugueza constitue-se devedora de 477:517 obrigações, sem vencimento de juro, do capital cada uma de réis 30$000, £.ª 6-12-8, francos 166,67, marcos 135,34, e florins 79,34 amortizaveis ao par, por sorteio, quando o forem as obrigações do juro de 3 % da 3.ª série que tiverem os mesmos numeros d'estes titulos especiaes.

Estas obrigações são consideradas para todos os effeitos titulos de divida publica fundada.

A amortização por sorteio d'estes titulos far-se-ha nos termos acima, a contar do 1.º de janeiro de 1903, e conforme a tabella no verso, sendo os titulos sorteados pagos em Portugal, Amsterdam, Anvers, Berlim, Bruxellas, Francfort, Londres e Paris.

O Capital d'estes titulos especiaes é isento de qualquer imposto ou taxa, tanto no presente como no futuro.

Para occorrer ao encargo da amortização d'estes titulos especiaes, o Governo, até integral pagamento dos mesmos titulos, fará inserir no orçamento annual do Estado as sommas para isso necessarias, applicando especialmente e de preferencia a esses encargos, nos termos do disposto na base II annexa á carta de lei de 14 de maio de 1902, os rendimentos aduaneiros no continente do reino na Europa, exceptuando os dos tabacos e cereaes, conforme os termos seguintes da mesma lei:

1.º Os thesoureiros das alfandegas entregarão todos os dias á Junta do Credito Publico quantia sufficiente para perfazer a tricentesima parte, em ouro, do total necessario para os encargos annuaes (juro e amortização) da divida externa actual que for convertida, nos termos d'esta lei, e para as despesas do serviço da mesma divida;

2.º No caso em que as receitas aduaneiras de um dia sejam inferiores á quantia necessaria, o deficit será preenchido com as receitas do dia ou dias seguintes;

3.º Logo que, no decurso de um semestre, a Junta do Credito Publico tiver recebido quantia igual á metade, em ouro, da necessaria para os encargos annuaes (juro e amortização) da referida divida externa actual que for convertida, nos termos d'esta lei, e para as despesas do respectivo serviço, cessarão n'esse semestre, quaesquer entregas dos thesoureiros das alfandegas á Junta do Credito Publico, recomeçando só no semestre seguinte;

4.º Se, por qualquer circumstancia imprevista, as entregas feitas na Junta do Credito Publico, durante qualquer semestre, não tiverem preenchido a metade da quantia total, em ouro, necessaria para os encargos annuaes da divida de que trata esta lei, o Governo preencherá o deficit pelas mais receitas e rendimentos do Thesouro Portuguez;

5.º A Junta do Credito Publico deverá transferir todos os 15 dias, pelo menos, para os estabelecimentos encarregados do serviço da divida publica portugueza nos paizes estrangeiros, as quantias que tiver em cofre, a fim de que o annuncio do pagamento dos coupons se faça 15 dias antes dos seus respectivos vencimentos, e a amortização dos titulos seja effectuada pontualmente.

Fica, porém, declarado para todos os effeitos que as disposições acima contidas, e de que trata a referida base II, annexa á carta de lei de 14 de maio de 1902, de modo algum affectarão ou poderão prejudicar a autonomia financeira, economica e administrativa da Nação Portugueza.

Os numeros dos titulos que forem amortizados serão publicados pela Junta do Credito Publico na folha official portugueza e em dois jornaes de cada paiz em que os mesmos titulos sejam ou venham a ser cotados.

O presente titulo, que não tem nenhuma vantagem além do seu opportuno reembolso, por não vencer juro, não vae munido de coupons e leva as assignaturas do Ministro e Secretario de Estado dos Negocios da Fazenda, de dois membros da Junta do Credito Publico e de um funccionario superior da Direcção Geral da Contabilidade Publica ou da Direcção Geral da Thesouraria, podendo todas as assignaturas ser de chancella menos uma que será pessoal.

Declaro mais, em nome do Governo Portuguez, que, até integral pagamento dos titulos d'esta série, serão mantidas e vigorarão as disposições dos decretos de 14 de agosto de 1893 e de 8 de outubro de 1900, que regulam a constituição, funcções e attribuições da actual Junta do Credito Publico.

Em fé do que eu, Fernando Mattozo Santos, Ministro e Secretario de Estado dos Negocios da Fazenda e interino dos Estrangeiros, assignei e sellei em Lisboa, aos 27 de dezembro de 1902, a presente Obrigação Geral, que vae tambem assignada pelo Director Geral da Thesouraria. — O Ministro da Fazenda, *Fernando Mattozo Santos* — O Director Geral da Thesouraria, *Luiz Augusto Perestrello de Vasconcellos.*

Está conforme. — Pela Junta do Credito Publico, *Alberto Antonio de Moraes Carvalho.*

Visto, Tribunal de Contas, 31 de dezembro de 1902, *Gualdino Alfredo Lobo de Gouveia Valladares.*

O portador do presente titulo, que não tem nenhuma vantagem além do seu opportuno reembolso, por não vencer juro, é credor de Réis 30$000 — Libras 6-12-8 — Francos 166,67 — Marcos 135,34 — Florins 79,34 em conta da 3.ª série de obrigações de 3 %, emittidas conforme a obrigação geral acima transcripta.

Este titulo, quando amortizado, será pago em ouro nas praças estrangeiras, mencionadas na dita obrigação geral acima.

Lisboa, Junta do Credito Publico, em 31 de dezembro de 1902.

(Left signature block): **O Ministro da Fazenda** — [handwritten signature: *J. S. Mattozo Santos*]

(Right signature block): **Os Membros da Junta do Credito Publico** — [two handwritten signatures, one reading approximately *José ... Lima* and *Antonio C. do Carmo de [illegible]*]

(Bottom border, engraver credits): COSTA MOTTA SOBR.º (left) · LALLEMANT. (right)

---

## English Translation:

(Top-right, handwritten): reimb.ᵗ · July 1948 / Present

(French fiscal stamp): **FOREIGN STATES FUND · 1 % · FULL TARIFF · PARIS · 18 (11) 1903**

**SPECIAL NON-INTEREST-BEARING BOND**
**OF THE**
**PORTUGUESE EXTERNAL FUND**
**3RD SERIES**

Issue of 477,517 special non-interest-bearing bonds, redeemable at par by lottery, over 198 semesters according to the table printed on the reverse, at such time as the 3-per-cent interest bonds of the 3rd series bearing the same numbers as these special titles are themselves redeemed — comprising 387,517 titles of 1 obligation, Nos. 1 to 387,515, plus 477,516 and 477,517, and 18,000 titles of 5 obligations each, Nos. 387,516 to 477,515, in accordance with the General Obligation transcribed and translated hereafter on the reverse.

**Title of 1 Obligation**
**No. 303,486**
**Réis 30$000 — Pounds 6-12-8 — Francs 166.67 — Marks 135.34 — Florins 79.34**

**⁕ GENERAL OBLIGATION ⁕**

The Portuguese Government having been authorized, by the charter-law of 14 May 1902, to convert the external debt, consolidated and redeemable, into 3 series of titles of the single type bearing 3 % interest, redeemable over 198 semesters; and it being required, with respect to the 3rd series of 477,517 bonds destined for the conversion of the external redeemable debt at 4 ½ %, that an equal number of special bonds be created, without interest, redeemable at par by lottery, at such time as the 3 % interest bonds of the said series bearing the same numbers as these special titles are redeemed:

I declare, in the name of the said Government — I, Fernando Mattozo Santos, Minister and Secretary of State for the Affairs of the Treasury and acting Minister for Foreign Affairs — the following, on the basis of the aforesaid law and of the decree of 9 August last:

The Portuguese Nation constitutes itself debtor of 477,517 bonds, bearing no interest, each of the capital of réis 30$000, £ 6-12-8, francs 166.67, marks 135.34, and florins 79.34, redeemable at par, by lottery, when the 3 % interest bonds of the 3rd series bearing the same numbers as these special titles are redeemed.

These bonds are considered, for all purposes, as titles of the funded public debt.

The redemption of these titles by lottery shall be effected on the terms above, beginning on the 1st of January 1903, and according to the table on the reverse, the drawn titles being paid in Portugal, Amsterdam, Antwerp, Berlin, Brussels, Frankfurt, London, and Paris.

The Capital of these special titles is exempt from any tax or duty, both in the present and in the future.

To meet the charge of the redemption of these special titles, the Government, until full payment of the same titles, shall enter in the annual budget of the State the sums necessary for that purpose, allocating specially and preferentially to those charges, on the terms provided in Base II annexed to the charter-law of 14 May 1902, the customs revenues of the continental territory of the kingdom in Europe, excepting those on tobacco and cereals, according to the following terms of the same law:

1st. The treasurers of the customs-houses shall deliver each day to the Public Credit Board a sum sufficient to make up the three-hundredth part, in gold, of the total needed for the annual charges (interest and redemption) of the present external debt to be converted, on the terms of this law, and for the expenses of the service of the same debt;

2nd. In the event that the customs receipts of one day are less than the required sum, the deficit shall be made up with the receipts of the following day or days;

3rd. As soon as, in the course of a semester, the Public Credit Board has received a sum equal to one half, in gold, of that required for the annual charges (interest and redemption) of the said present external debt to be converted, on the terms of this law, and for the expenses of the respective service, all deliveries by the customs treasurers to the Public Credit Board shall cease for that semester, resuming only in the following semester;

4th. If, by any unforeseen circumstance, the deliveries made to the Public Credit Board during any semester have not made up one half of the total sum, in gold, required for the annual charges of the debt dealt with by this law, the Government shall make up the deficit from the other receipts and revenues of the Portuguese Treasury;

5th. The Public Credit Board shall transfer at least every 15 days, to the establishments charged with the service of the Portuguese public debt in foreign countries, the sums it holds in its coffers, so that the announcement of the payment of the coupons is made 15 days before their respective maturities, and the redemption of the titles is effected punctually.

It is, however, declared for all purposes that the provisions contained above, and dealt with by the said Base II annexed to the charter-law of 14 May 1902, shall in no way affect or be able to prejudice the financial, economic, and administrative autonomy of the Portuguese Nation.

The numbers of the titles that are redeemed shall be published by the Public Credit Board in the Portuguese official gazette and in two newspapers of each country in which the same titles are or come to be quoted.

The present title, which carries no advantage beyond its timely reimbursement, since it bears no interest, is not furnished with coupons and bears the signatures of the Minister and Secretary of State for the Affairs of the Treasury, of two members of the Public Credit Board, and of a senior official of the Directorate-General of Public Accounting or of the Directorate-General of the Treasury, all the signatures being permitted to be by stamp except one which shall be personal.

I declare further, in the name of the Portuguese Government, that, until full payment of the titles of this series, the provisions of the decrees of 14 August 1893 and of 8 October 1900, which govern the constitution, functions, and powers of the present Public Credit Board, shall be maintained and remain in force.

In witness whereof I, Fernando Mattozo Santos, Minister and Secretary of State for the Affairs of the Treasury and acting Minister for Foreign Affairs, have signed and sealed at Lisbon, on the 27th of December 1902, the present General Obligation, which is also signed by the Director-General of the Treasury. — The Minister of Finance, *Fernando Mattozo Santos* — The Director-General of the Treasury, *Luiz Augusto Perestrello de Vasconcellos.*

Certified correct. — For the Public Credit Board, *Alberto Antonio de Moraes Carvalho.*

Seen, Court of Auditors, 31 December 1902, *Gualdino Alfredo Lobo de Gouveia Valladares.*

The bearer of the present title, which carries no advantage beyond its timely reimbursement, since it bears no interest, is creditor of Réis 30$000 — Pounds 6-12-8 — Francs 166.67 — Marks 135.34 — Florins 79.34 on account of the 3rd series of 3 % bonds, issued in accordance with the general obligation transcribed above.

This title, when redeemed, shall be paid in gold in the foreign markets mentioned in the said general obligation above.

Lisbon, Public Credit Board, 31 December 1902.

(Left): **The Minister of Finance** — [signature: *J. S. Mattozo Santos*]

(Right): **The Members of the Public Credit Board** — [two signatures]

(Engraver credits): COSTA MOTTA SOBR.º (left) · LALLEMANT. (right)

title: Hope & Co. Certificate of Russian 6% Bank Assignations, Amsterdam, 1825
description: This bilingual Dutch-and-French certificate entitles its bearer to an inscription of 1,000 roubles, bearing interest at 6 per cent, in Russian Bank Assignations (Bank-Assignatiën / Assignations de Banque) — the assignat paper currency of the Imperial Russian debt — registered in the Groot-Boek (Grand-Livre, or Great Book) of the public debt at St. Petersburg in the name of the administrative office at Amsterdam. It was issued at Amsterdam on 11 May 1825 by the houses of Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, and the widow (Wed. / Veuve) W. Borski, the Dutch bankers who managed Russia's foreign borrowing. Ten semi-annual coupons accompanied it, running to 1 July 1829. The document attests to Amsterdam's role as the financing hub for Russian sovereign credit, repackaging the Tsar's rouble debt into denominated certificates that Dutch investors could hold and trade. The engraved frame and restrained classical lettering convey the sober respectability the issuing houses sought to project.
creator: Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Wed. W. Borski
notes: Certificaat van 6 per Cents Russische Fondsen in Bank-Assignatien / Certificat d'Inscription Russe à 6 pour Cent en Assignations de Banque. No. 1326, R° 1000 rubles. Inscribed in the Grand Livre at St. Petersburg. Managed by Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, and Wed. W. Borski. Amsterdam, May 11, 1825. Bilingual Dutch/French. Ten half-yearly coupons to July 4, 1829.

## English Translation:

CERTIFICATE OF INSCRIPTION
Of 6 Percent Russian Funds in Bank Assignations / Inscription Certificate in Russian 6 Percent Bank Assignations

The holder of this is entitled to an inscription of ONE THOUSAND RUBLES at 6 percent Russian Funds, payable in BANK ASSIGNATIONS, inscribed in the Debt-Book at St. Petersburg, in the name of the ADMINISTRATION OFFICE, established in Amsterdam, under the direction of

title: USSR State Loan Bond
description: A bearer bond (облигация) of the State Loan for the Restoration and Development of the National Economy of the USSR, with a face value of twenty-five rubles, issued in 1946. The first of Stalin's postwar mass subscription loans, it was floated to fund reconstruction after the devastation of the Great Patriotic War, channelling the savings of citizens—often through compulsory payroll subscription—into the state treasury rather than the consumer market, thereby financing recovery while damping inflationary pressure on a goods-starved economy. The engraved design is austere and statist: the Soviet emblem, with hammer, sickle, globe and encircling wheat, crowns a frame of fine guilloche lining, while heavy slab lettering announces the denomination in figures and words. The visual language is one of bureaucratic solemnity rather than ornament, presenting public debt as an act of patriotic participation in rebuilding the war-ravaged Soviet state.
creator: Government of the USSR
notes: USSR, State Loan for Restoration and Development of the National Economy (Государственный Заем Восстановления и Развития Народного Хозяйства СССР), 1946. No. 012594, Series 33, Category 98. 25 rubles. Soviet state emblem design.

title: Russian State Internal 4½% Lottery Loan Bond, Provisional Government, 1917
description: This is a bearer lottery bond (билетъ) of the Государственный внутренній 4½% выигрышный заемъ 1917 года — the Russian State Internal 4½% Lottery Loan of 1917 — with a face value of two hundred rubles (въ двѣсти рублей), bearing 4½ per cent interest and entitling the holder to participate in the loan's prize drawings. The heading states that it was issued on the authority of the resolution of the Provisional Government of 11 August 1917, with the final coupon dated 16 January 1928. Floated in the desperate fiscal summer that followed the February Revolution, the loan sought to draw private savings toward a treasury drained by war and political collapse, its lottery feature an inducement to a public unwilling to lend on patriotism alone; within months the Bolshevik seizure of power would repudiate such obligations. An engraved vignette of a draped allegorical woman attended by putti crowns the certificate, lending an air of established credit to a state already near dissolution.
creator: Imperial Russian Government (Provisional Government)
notes: Russian Imperial State Internal 4½% Prize Loan of 1917 (Государственный Внутренний 4½% Выигрышный Заем 1917 года). Series No. 15800, Bond No. 32, Category 2. 200 rubles nominal. Bearer. Issued per decree of August 11–19, 1917 (Provisional Government). Last coupon January 16, 1928.
transcription:
# Russian State Internal 4½% Lottery Loan Bond of 1917, 200 Rubles (Second Series) — Transcription

## Original:

**Разрядъ второй.**

(upper centre: engraved allegorical vignette — a draped female figure attended by putti; engraver's mark **ABNCo** at lower right of the vignette)

*(left, in red)* Серія номеръ
**15800**

*(right, in red)* Билетъ номеръ
**32**

**ГОСУДАРСТВЕННЫЙ ВНУТРЕННІЙ 4½% ВЫИГРЫШНЫЙ ЗАЕМЪ 1917 ГОДА,**
выпущенный на основаніи постановленія Временнаго Правительства отъ 11-го Августа 1917 года.

**БИЛЕТЪ**
**ВЪ ДВѢСТИ РУБЛЕЙ НАРИЦАТЕЛЬНЫХЪ**
**НА ПРЕДЪЯВИТЕЛЯ.**

Заемъ сей внесенъ въ Государственную Долговую Книгу подъ названіемъ "Государственный внутренній четырехъ съ половиной процентный выигрышный заемъ 1917 года".

Билеты сего займа выпускаются именные и на предъявителя, достоинствомъ въ 200 рублей нарицательныхъ.

Владѣлецъ настоящаго билета участвуетъ въ займѣ суммою въ 200 рублей, приносящей четыре съ половиной процента въ годъ до полнаго погашенія билета вышедшаго въ тиражъ выигрышемъ.

Доходъ по билетамъ второго разряда уплачивается два раза въ годъ: 16 Іюля и 16 Января по предъявленіи купоновъ въ Конторахъ и Отдѣленіяхъ Государственнаго Банка, Казначействахъ, а равно и въ Государственныхъ Сберегательныхъ Кассахъ.

Теченіе процентовъ по билетамъ настоящаго разряда начинается 16 Января 1918 года.

Тиражи выигрышей, происходящіе публично въ мѣстахъ, назначаемыхъ Министромъ Финансовъ, производятся для настоящаго разряда 16 Іюля и 16 Января каждаго года.

Первый тиражъ выигрышей для второго разряда займа назначается на 16 Іюля 1918 года.

Выплата выигрышей производится въ Конторахъ и Отдѣленіяхъ Государственнаго Банка спустя три мѣсяца послѣ тиража выигрышей, по предъявленіи билетовъ со всѣми купонами, срокъ оплаты коихъ наступаетъ послѣ срока тиража, на которые палъ выигрышъ на предъявляемый билетъ; стоимость недостающихъ купоновъ будетъ удержана изъ суммы выигрыша.

Приносящій означенный заемъ доходъ въ размѣрѣ 4½% въ годъ, а равно уплачиваемые по займу выигрыши, освобождаются навсегда отъ сбора съ доходовъ отъ денежныхъ капиталовъ.

Оплата выигрышей производится въ теченіе 30 лѣтъ со дня, назначеннаго для начала таковой уплаты, а купоновъ — въ теченіе 10 лѣтъ по наступленіи по нимъ сроковъ.

Билеты сего займа принимаются по цѣнамъ, устанавливаемымъ на каждое полугодіе Министромъ Финансовъ и публикуемымъ черезъ Правительствующій Сенатъ, въ залоги: а) по казеннымъ подрядамъ и поставкамъ, б) по обезпеченію таможенныхъ пошлинъ.

*(signature block, right)* Управляющій Государственною Коммиссіею Погашенія Долговъ [signature — illegible]
*(left)* Бухгалтеръ [signature — illegible] (red control stamp overprinted)

**Срокъ послѣдняго купона 16 Января 1928 года.**

---

## English Translation:

**Second Series.**

*(left)* Series number **15800**
*(right)* Ticket number **32**

**RUSSIAN STATE INTERNAL 4½% LOTTERY LOAN OF THE YEAR 1917,** issued on the authority of the resolution of the Provisional Government of 11 August 1917.

**BOND**
**FOR TWO HUNDRED RUBLES NOMINAL**
**TO BEARER.**

This loan has been entered in the State Debt Register under the name "State Internal four-and-one-half per cent Lottery Loan of the year 1917."

The bonds of this loan are issued both registered and to bearer, in the denomination of 200 nominal rubles.

The holder of the present bond participates in the loan in the sum of 200 rubles, yielding four and one-half per cent per year until the full redemption of the bond drawn for prize.

Income on second-series bonds is paid twice a year: on 16 July and 16 January, upon presentation of the coupons at the Offices and Branches of the State Bank, at the Treasuries, and likewise at the State Savings Banks.

The running of interest on the bonds of the present series begins on 16 January 1918.

The prize drawings, held publicly in places designated by the Minister of Finance, are conducted for the present series on 16 July and 16 January of each year.

The first prize drawing for the second series of the loan is set for 16 July 1918.

Payment of prizes is made at the Offices and Branches of the State Bank three months after the prize drawing, upon presentation of the bonds together with all coupons whose payment date falls after the date of the drawing, on which bond the prize was drawn; the value of any missing coupons will be deducted from the prize sum.

The said loan income at the rate of 4½% per year, as well as the prizes paid on the loan, are forever exempt from the tax on income from monetary capital.

Payment of prizes is made within 30 years from the day appointed for the commencement of such payment, and of coupons — within 10 years from the falling due of their terms.

The bonds of this loan are accepted, at prices established for each half-year by the Minister of Finance and published through the Governing Senate, as security: (a) for state contracts and supplies, and (b) for the guaranteeing of customs duties.

*(right)* Manager of the State Commission for the Redemption of Debts [signature]
*(left)* Accountant [signature] (red control stamp)

**Term of the last coupon: 16 January 1928.**

title: City of Baku 5% Municipal Loan Bond, 1910
description: A bearer bond (Облигація) of the 5 per cent Municipal Loan of the City of Baku, dated 1910 and printed trilingually in Russian, French, and English so as to court foreign capital. The certificate carries a face value of 189 rubles, fixed for international subscribers at 504 francs or £20 sterling, against a total issue of nearly 26,000,000 rubles, and promises a five per cent annual return. Issued by the municipality of Baku—then the booming oil port of the Russian Empire on the Caspian, in present-day Azerbaijan—the loan funnels European savings into the city's rapid expansion. The restrained engraved border and the civic coat of arms framing the central denomination present the obligation in a sober, mercantile guise, the visual rhetoric of creditworthiness rather than ornament: a provincial city addressing the Paris and London markets in their own terms.
creator: City of Baku
notes: 5% Loan of the City of Baku, 1910 (5% Заем Города Баку 1910 года). No. 20783. 189 rubles / 504 francs / 20 shillings sterling. Total issue: 25,999,973 rubles = £2,857,140 = Fr. 71,999,928. Trilingual Russian/French/German. Bearer. Red official stamp.

title: Imperial Russian Three Per Cent Loan Bond
description: This is a bearer bond of the Imperial Russian Three per Cent Loan of 1859, an inscription in the Great Book of the Public Debt of Russia representing a capital of One Hundred Pounds Sterling (L.St. 100). The holder draws three per cent annually, paid half-yearly in London by Messrs. J. Thomson, T. Bonar & Co. on 16 October and 16 April as two warrants of one pound ten shillings each, or at the holder's option in Berlin by F. Mart. Magnus at six and three-quarter Thalers per pound; coupons run to 16 April 1869, after which a talon yields a fresh sheet. Floated abroad in sterling and serviced through London and Berlin houses, the loan reveals an autocracy reaching into Western capital markets to fund obligations its own savings could not bear. The bond cites the Imperial Ukase of 20 March 1859 and a sinking fund amortising at one and a quarter per cent yearly. The engraved frame, with its restrained scrollwork and copperplate script, projects the probity such cross-border borrowing demanded; affixed Danish revenue stamps mark a later transfer.
creator: Imperial Russian Government
notes: Imperial Russian Three Per Cent Loan 1859. No. 41150. £100 Sterling. Inscription in the Great Book of the Public Debt of Russia. Interest payable halfyearly in London via Thomson, Bonar & Co. and in Berlin via F. Mart. Magnus at 6¾ thalers/£. Talon with dividend warrants. Two Danish revenue stamps at top left.
transcription:
# Transcription of Imperial Russian Three Per Cent Loan 1859 Bond

**IMPERIAL RUSSIAN THREE PER CENT LOAN 1859.**
**ONE HUNDRED POUNDS STERLING**

Inscription in the Great Book of the Public Debt of Russia representing a Capital of **ONE HUNDRED Pounds Sterling**.

Entered the _______ Book _______ folio 1546

The bearer of this Inscription is entitled to an annual Interest of **THREE per CENT**, to be paid half-yearly in London, One Pound ten shillings on the **1st OCTOBER** and **1st NOVEMBER** and One Pound ten shillings on the **1st MAY** and **1st JUNE** by Messrs **J. Thomson, T. Bonar & Co.**, or at the option of the holder in Berlin by **Mr. F. Mart, Magnus in Thalers** at the rate of six and three quarter Thalers per Pound Sterling on the said dates.

Attached to this Inscription are half-yearly dividend warrants up to **18 APRIL 1869**, with a talon. After the expiration of that date new dividend warrants will be delivered in exchange for the talon.

Extract from the Regulations of the **IMPERIAL COMMISSION OF THE SINKING FUND** (Code of Russian laws Tom. XI edit. 1857.)

§. 89. The payment of interminable annuities by the State, as well as in times of war as of peace, without any distinction whatsoever, whether the creditor be of a friendly or of a hostile nation.

§. 90. If a foreigner, proprietor of an Inscription, shall die, the Inscriptions shall pass to his heirs, in the order of succession established by the laws of the country, of which he was a subject.

§. 91. Investments in the irredeemable funded debt, constituting the inviolable property of the depositor are exempt from all imposts whatsoever.

Extract of the **IMPERIAL UKASE** of the **20 March 1859**, addressed to the Minister of Finance.

For the amortization of this Loan a special sinking fund, to be kept separate from the other Loans, will be assigned to begin from the year 1859, which fund will form annually 1½ % of the capital of the Loan. On the closure of this fund, for the purpose of buying in the inscriptions at the exchange of the day. If the holder of an inscription of this Loan should wish to have the same repurchased at any time at such price as he may require, the inscription to the bearer will be then changed into an inscription in the name of the holder, without dividend warrants, and the interest due on such inscriptions will be paid at the **IMPERIAL COMMISSION OF THE SINKING FUND** at **St. Petersburg** every half year, at the period fixed here above.

Countersigned: **THOMSON, BONAR & CO.**
per pro. **F. MART MAGNUS,**
Contractors.

Director of the Commission [**signature - illegible**]

---

## Notable Markings:

- Serial number: **No. 41150** (appears three times on document)
- Denomination: **U.S. $100** (shown in decorative oval)
- Two Danish revenue stamps affixed to upper left (pink/red colored, denominations visible: "12 KRONER" and "30 ØRE")
- Ornamental borders throughout in classical engraved style
- Decorative corner elements and scroll work
- Handwritten folio reference: "1546"
- Signature of Director of the Commission (handwritten, illegible)
- Text indicates bond No. 41150

title: City of Bari Prize Loan Bond
description: This is a bearer prize-loan bond (Obbligazione al Portatore) of 100 lire, redeemable at 150 lire, issued by the Consiglio Comunale of the Città di Bari delle Puglie under its Prestito a Premi. The loan was resolved by municipal ordinance of 31 December 1867 and approved by Royal Decree of 11 June 1868; the printed margin records an Italian emission of 9,000,000 lire in 90,000 bonds (900 series of 100 numbers each), with amortisation set at 27,350,000 lire, divided between 13,500,000 for redemption and 13,850,000 for the payment of premi. Issued at Bari on 1 May 1869 over the signature of the Sindaco, with Francesco Compagnoni of Milan as assuntore. A printed plan of drawings and an attached Annotazione dei Premi sheet govern the periodic lottery extractions through which the obligation is amortised. Such municipal prize loans let a southern comune raise public-works capital cheaply by selling the chance of a prize in place of ordinary interest, blending civic borrowing with the appeal of the lottery. The restrained engraved frame and classicising allegorical vignette announce municipal authority rather than commercial enterprise.
creator: Consiglio Comunale di Bari delle Puglie
notes: Città di Bari delle Puglie, Prestito a Premi (Prize Loan). Deliberated December 31, 1867; approved by Royal Decree June 11, 1868. Emission of L. 3,500,000. Serie 317, No. 049. Obbligazione di Lire 100 al portatore, rimborsabile con Lire 150. Lottery/amortization schedule (Piano delle Estrazioni) and prize payment sheet (Annotazione dei Premi) at right. Bari, May 11, 1869. Signed by Il Sindaco.

title: Principality of Bulgaria Gold Loan Bond
description: A bearer bond (Облигация / Obligation) of the Principality of Bulgaria, one of 212,000 issued under the Bulgarian Government 5% Gold Loan of 1902. Its face value is 500 francs, equal to 500 leva, with equivalences printed for sterling (£18 16s 0d), Austrian crowns (476) and Dutch guilders (240); it bears five per cent in gold and is redeemable at par over fifty years. Issued at Sofia and dated 1/14 January 1903 under the Old- and New-Style calendars, it is signed for the Minister of Finance, M. K. Sarafov, in four languages — Bulgarian, French, German and English — and is expressly secured on the state's tobacco duties ("Gagé sur les droits des tabacs"). For a principality only recently autonomous from the Ottoman Porte and still nominally tributary, the bond shows a young treasury reaching into Western capital markets, pledging a dependable excise to foreign holders to fund its gold obligations. The engraved frame answers that ambition with dense guilloche and arabesque borders in the security-printing manner of the period, centred on a medallion bearing the crowned shield and rampant lion of the Bulgarian arms — an assertion of sovereign credit as much as of statehood.
creator: Principality of Bulgaria

title: Principality of Bulgaria 5% Gold Loan (conditions)
description: This sheet sets out the conditions governing a 5% gold loan of the Principality of Bulgaria, headed by a Bulgarian banner (УСЛОВИЯ НА ЗАЕМА) and laid out in four parallel columns — French, Russian, German and English — with a half-yearly amortisation table. The bonds bear five per centum yearly interest on their nominal value, paid against two equal coupons due on 1/14 March and 1/14 September, each worth 12.50 gold leva or its equivalent in francs, roubles, reichsmark, sterling, Austrian crowns or Dutch florins, payable across a dozen capitals from Sofia and Paris to St. Petersburg, Berlin, London and Amsterdam. The whole issue of 212,000 bonds is redeemable at par by drawings over fifty years, the first set for 1/14 August 1903 and the last in 1953, with early redemption reserved from 1/14 September 1913; the loan is served through the Russian State Bank and the Banque de Paris et des Pays-Bas, and the French text governs. The multilingual frame and Franco-Russian backing reveal a young Balkan principality binding its credit to several hard currencies at once to borrow on foreign markets.

title: Province of Buenos Aires Consolidated Internal Debt Bond, La Plata, 1935
description: This is a bearer bond of one hundred pesos moneda nacional, part of the consolidated internal debt (Deuda Interna Consolidada) of the Province of Buenos Aires, Argentina, issued under provincial Law Nº 4293 of 27 June 1935 and dated at La Plata, 1 July 1935. The title earns 5% annual interest with an additional 1% of cumulative annual amortisation, interest payable quarterly in February, May, August and November against the attached coupons, a near-complete sheet of which survives. Issued amid the fiscal strains of the early 1930s, the bond reflects a province consolidating earlier obligations into a single internal loan placed with domestic holders in national currency rather than borrowing abroad. The engraved frame is conventionally academic: classical allegorical figures flank the provincial arms above the denomination, the restrained ornament projecting the sober creditworthiness such an instrument was meant to convey.
creator: Provincia de Buenos Aires
notes: Provincia de Buenos Aires, Deuda Interna Consolidada, Serie A, Ley N° 4293, 100 Pesos (Cien Pesos), 5% annual interest; large attached coupon sheet; No. 133,050

title: Province of Buenos Aires Consolidated Internal Debt Coupon Sheet, Law No. 4393
description: This is a sheet of detachable interest coupons belonging to a registered bond of the Consolidated Internal Debt (Deuda Interna Consolidada, Serie A) of the Province of Buenos Aires, issued in pesos moneda nacional under Article 9 of provincial Law No. 4393, with the public-debt provisions of Articles 50, 59 and 80 of Law No. 4225, and put into circulation by executive emission decrees dated 11 September 1925, 20 November 1926 and 16 December 1926. The reproduced decree texts authorise consolidation issues reaching into the hundreds of millions of pesos. More than a hundred small pink coupons, each bearing an engraved number from 19 onward, fill the sheet; the opening run of lower numbers has already been clipped away, marking interest periods drawn down against the principal. The dense guilloché lathe-work and medallioned frames are the standard anti-forgery engraving of the era's securities, asserting the document's negotiability and the province's pledge of payment. The sheet documents a Buenos Aires province leaning on long-term funded debt to consolidate and refinance its obligations through the central provincial loan office.
