title: Province of Nova Scotia 3½% Redeemable Stock, Coupon Sheet, payable in London
description: A sheet of detachable interest coupons for the Province of Nova Scotia Government 3½% Redeemable Stock, part of the Dominion of Canada's sterling-denominated borrowing on the London market. Each coupon represents one half-year's dividend of seventeen pounds ten shillings (£17.10.0), less income tax, on a £1,000 certificate — the semi-annual half of the 3½% rate — payable at the National Provincial Bank of England, 112 Bishopsgate Street, London. The coupons fall due successively on 1 January and 1 July; those surviving on this sheet (the second of four) run through the early-to-mid 1920s. Signed by the Provincial Secretary, G. H. Murray, the coupons bear a small heraldic device and a statutory caveat that the revenues of the Province alone are liable for the stock (40 & 41 Vict., ch. 59, sec. 19). The restrained engraved borders and sober typography befit a colonial government raising long-term capital in the imperial money market.
creator: Province of Nova Scotia
notes: Interest coupon sheet (p.2/4) for goetzmann0984 (Province of Nova Scotia £1,000 stock); large sheet of £17 10s. semi-annual interest coupons; No. C 1515

title: Province of Nova Scotia Government Redeemable Stock (Conditions)
description: This sheet forms the conditions-and-coupon portion of a Province of Nova Scotia Government Redeemable Stock certificate, a sterling loan of one thousand pounds bearing interest at three and one-half per cent per annum. The detachable coupons each promise a half-yearly dividend of seventeen pounds ten shillings, less income tax, due 1 January and 1 July, payable in London at the Bank of England, Limited, and countersigned for the National Provincial Bank as registrar. The reverse conditions state pointedly that the revenues of the Province of Nova Scotia alone answer for the stock and its dividends, the Consolidated Fund of the United Kingdom and His Majesty's Treasury being expressly not liable, citing the statute 40 & 41 Vict. Floated on the London market, it shows a colonial government borrowing in imperial sterling on its own credit. The dense engine-turned border in red ochre, framing a thistle vignette, signals security against forgery rather than any artistic programme.
creator: Province of Nova Scotia
notes: Conditions/reverse (p.3/4) for goetzmann0984 (Province of Nova Scotia stock); conditions text panels with remaining coupons at bottom; No. C 1515

title: Province of Nova Scotia Government Redeemable Stock Stubs
description: These are the binding-spine stubs (counterfoils) of registered certificates for the Province of Nova Scotia Government Redeemable Stock, an inscribed colonial loan bearing interest at 3½ per cent per annum on a principal of £1,000 sterling. The two engraved stub labels are headed "Dominion of Canada — Province of Nova Scotia — Government Redeemable Stock," with principal repayable and coupons payable half-yearly, the first instalment falling due 1st January. Issued in London around 1910 under Imperial colonial-stock arrangements, the loan let a Canadian province borrow on the sterling market at rates far below colonial terms. The recurring legend on the lower coupon panels disclaims any Imperial guarantee: the revenues of Nova Scotia alone answer for the stock, while the Consolidated Fund of the United Kingdom and the Lords Commissioners of His Majesty's Treasury are "not directly or indirectly liable." The restrained foliate engraving in terracotta ink serves trust rather than display.
creator: Province of Nova Scotia
notes: Talon (p.4/4) for goetzmann0984 (Province of Nova Scotia £1,000 stock); two Nova Scotia Government Redeemable Stock certificate stubs side by side, both No. C 1515; with remaining interest coupons

title: Banque Industrielle de Chine Share
description: An ordinary bearer share (action ordinaire au porteur), fully paid, for a face value of 500 French francs, issued by the Banque Industrielle de Chine (中法實業銀行, the Sino-French Industrial Bank), a société anonyme with a capital of 150,000,000 francs and its registered office in Paris. The text records statutes deposited before notaries Lavoignat and Moyne on 15 March 1913 and amended by shareholders' general assemblies of 19 May 1919, 28 November 1919, and 7 May 1920; the share therefore dates from about 1920, in the bank's last and most strained phase before its suspension of payments in 1921. The certificate speaks to the ambitions of Franco-Chinese finance on the eve of collapse: founded to channel European capital into Chinese railways, ports, and industry, the bank had over-extended, and shares such as this funded a venture soon ruined. The engraved frame turns that ambition into ornament — a Chinese gateway, paired guardian lions, and a pagoda-studded skyline in ochre and grey present China as a picturesque field for investment, a decorative orientalism characteristic of European concession finance.
creator: Banque Industrielle de Chine

This bank is a bearer (registered-to-no-name) company. Its capital is 150,000,000 francs, divided into 300,000 shares, each share of 500 francs; of these, 297,000 are ordinary shares and 3,000 are founders' preference shares. title: Banque Industrielle de Chine Share (Reverse)
description: The reverse of an ordinary share in the Banque Industrielle de Chine (中法實業銀行), a Paris-domiciled joint-stock bank denominated in francs. The upper panel reproduces the "Extraits des Statuts": Article 3 defines the object as all banking and finance operations "en tous pays," and especially the carrying-out of agreements concluded with the Government of the Chinese Republic; Article 4 fixes the head office at Paris; Article 5 sets a duration of ninety years; Article 6 places the share capital at 150 million francs. Below, a sheet of detachable dividend-coupon stubs is printed only with the bank's name and the words 息票 ("dividend coupon"). Established in 1913, the bank was a vehicle of Franco-Chinese financial diplomacy, channelling French capital toward concessions and public works in republican China; the bilingual statutes and Chinese coupons register that hybrid ambition before the bank's collapse in 1921.
creator: Banque Industrielle de Chine
notes: Reverse (p.2/2) of goetzmann0988 (Banque Industrielle de Chine share); Extraits des Statuts (corporate statutes) in French; Chinese dividend coupon sheet at bottom (Zhongfa Shiye Yinhang interest coupons)
transcription:
# Banque Industrielle de Chine — Share Certificate (Reverse: Extracts from the Articles of Association) — Transcription

## Original:

(Handwritten collector's annotation, upper right corner: "9 60" [uncertain])

**EXTRAITS DES STATUTS**

(Left column)

Art. 3. — La Société a pour objet :

Toutes opérations de Banque et de Finance généralement quelconques, en tous pays.

Et spécialement la mise en œuvre des accords intervenus et à intervenir avec le Gouvernement de la République Chinoise.

En général, faire toutes opérations de banque, de prêts, de dépôts, d'escompte, d'assurances et de prévoyance, d'avance de fonds, en vue de favoriser toutes entreprises utiles telles que peuvent le comporter la mise en valeur et l'exploitation des richesses naturelles de la Chine.

Art. 4. — Le Siège social est établi à Paris.

Art. 5. — La durée de la Société est fixée à quatre-vingt-dix ans, à partir du jour de sa constitution, sauf prorogation ou dissolution anticipée.

Art. 6. — Le fonds social est fixé à 150 millions de francs.

Il est divisé en 300.000 actions de 500 francs chacune.

Ces actions comprennent deux catégories, savoir :

3.000 actions dites de fondateur, numérotées de 1 à 3.000, dont le nombre ne pourra jamais être augmenté ou diminué ni les droits modifiés, même au cas d'augmentation du capital social. Toutefois, en cas d'augmentation de capital, il pourra être alloué aux nouvelles actions émises, concurremment avec les actions de fondateur et les actions ordinaires composant le capital social actuel, le premier dividende de 6 % stipulé à l'article 49 ci-après.

Et 297.000 actions dites ordinaires, numérotées de 1 à 297.000.

Les droits respectifs de ces deux catégories d'actions sont les suivants :

1° Dans toutes assemblées générales autres que celles prévues aux articles 51 et 54, les Actionnaires possédant des actions de fondateur ont droit à six voix par action, et les Actionnaires possédant des actions ordinaires à une voix pour dix actions.

2° Les droits des actions ordinaires et des actions de fondateur dans le partage des bénéfices sont fixés à l'article 49 ci-après ; leurs droits respectifs en cas de liquidation sont déterminés à l'article 52 ci-après.

Art. 7. — Le fonds social pourra être élevé jusqu'à deux cent cinquante millions de francs, en une ou plusieurs fois, par simple décision du Conseil d'Administration, et aux taux, clauses et conditions qu'il fixera, sans qu'il soit besoin d'une autorisation de l'Assemblée générale des Actionnaires, laquelle sera seulement appelée à vérifier la sincérité de la déclaration de souscription et de versement.

En cas d'augmentation de capital par voie d'émission de nouvelles actions ordinaires à souscrire en numéraire, les propriétaires des actions de fondateur et les propriétaires des actions ordinaires, au moment où se fera cette augmentation, auront, sauf ce qui sera dit ci-après, et aussi sous la réserve des droits spéciaux du Gouvernement Chinois à ce sujet, un droit de préférence à la souscription de la totalité des actions émises et ce, dans les proportions respectives de moitié du nombre des actions nouvelles en faveur des propriétaires d'actions de fondateur et de moitié du nombre de ces mêmes nouvelles actions en faveur des propriétaires des actions ordinaires.

Toutefois, au moment de l'augmentation de capital, il pourra être décidé, soit par le Conseil d'Administration, s'il use de la faculté à lui accordée par le premier paragraphe du présent article, soit par l'Assemblée générale dans tous autres cas, qu'une fraction du nombre des actions nouvelles à émettre — sans que dans aucun cas cette fraction puisse être supérieure à la moitié — sera laissée à la disposition du Conseil pour en opérer le placement au mieux des intérêts de la Société, et ainsi qu'il avisera.

Le Conseil d'Administration fixera les conditions, les délais et les formes, dans lesquels le bénéfice des dispositions qui précèdent pourra être réclamé.

Art. 11. —

La possession d'une action emporte, de plein droit, adhésion aux Statuts de la Société et aux décisions de l'Assemblée générale.

Art. 19. — La Société est administrée par un Conseil composé de sept membres au moins et de vingt et un au plus.

La gestion des affaires de la Société en Chine est confiée à un Directeur général.

Art. 20. — Les Administrateurs sont nommés par l'Assemblée générale.

Le Gouvernement Chinois aura dans le Conseil un représentant avec voix consultative, mais qui aura, bien entendu, voix délibérative lorsqu'il aura été nommé Administrateur par l'Assemblée générale. La durée des fonctions de ce représentant sera fixée par le Gouvernement Chinois et à son gré. Le représentant du Gouvernement Chinois aura droit aux mêmes indemnités et avantages de toute nature que chacun des Administrateurs.

Art. 21. — Chaque Administrateur doit, en entrant en fonctions, et pendant toute la durée de son mandat, être propriétaire de 50 actions de l'une ou l'autre catégorie.

Art. 33. — L'Assemblée générale régulièrement constituée représente l'universalité des Actionnaires.

Elle se compose de tous les Actionnaires possédant depuis trois mois, au moins, dix actions ordinaires ou une action de fondateur. Tous propriétaires d'un nombre inférieur d'actions ordinaires à celui spécifié pourront, soit se réunir pour former ce nombre d'actions ou un nombre supérieur et se faire représenter par l'un d'eux, soit se faire représenter par un autre Actionnaire déjà par lui-même membre de l'Assemblée.

Le Conseil pourra toujours, par mesure générale, réduire le délai de trois mois qui vient d'être énoncé et accepter les dépôts faits dans les limites qu'il fixera lui-même.

Il est délivré à ces déposants en échange un récépissé nominatif qui leur sert de carte d'entrée à l'Assemblée générale.

Art. 35. — L'Assemblée générale se réunit chaque année avant le 30 Septembre au Siège social ou au lieu désigné par le Conseil d'Administration.

Des Assemblées générales extraordinaires peuvent être convoquées toutes les fois que le Conseil d'Administration en reconnaît l'utilité.

Art. 36. — Les convocations aux Assemblées tant ordinaires qu'extraordinaires sont faites par un avis inséré, vingt jours au moins avant l'époque de la réunion, dans un des journaux d'annonces légales du Siège social.

(Right column)

Pour les Assemblées générales extraordinaires, cet avis doit contenir l'indication sommaire de l'objet de la réunion.

Art. 39. — L'ordre du jour est arrêté par le Conseil d'Administration.

Il n'y sera porté que les propositions émanant de ce Conseil et celles qui auront été communiquées quinze jours au moins avant la convocation de l'Assemblée générale, avec la signature d'Actionnaires possédant ensemble un sixième au moins du fonds social.

Aucun autre objet que ceux à l'ordre du jour ne peut être mis en délibération.

Art. 40. — L'Assemblée est présidée par le Président du Conseil d'Administration ou, à son défaut, par un des Vice-Présidents ou par l'Administrateur que le Conseil désigne.

Les deux plus forts actionnaires présents, soit par eux-mêmes, soit comme mandataires et, sur leur refus, ceux qui les suivent dans l'ordre de la liste, jusqu'à acceptation, sont appelés à remplir les fonctions de scrutateurs.

Le Secrétaire est choisi par le Bureau et peut être pris en dehors des Actionnaires.

Art. 41. — Les délibérations sont prises à la majorité des voix.

Chaque membre de l'Assemblée a autant de voix qu'il possède de fois dix actions ordinaires soit en son nom, soit comme mandataire, et autant de fois six voix qu'il possède d'actions de fondateur, soit en son nom, soit comme mandataire, sans toutefois qu'un même Actionnaire, tant en son nom que comme mandataire, puisse réunir plus du tiers des voix des membres présents ou représentés à l'Assemblée.

Art. 45. — Les délibérations de l'Assemblée générale sont constatées par des procès-verbaux inscrits sur un registre spécial et signés par les membres composant le bureau ou la majorité d'entre eux.

Art. 46. — La justification à faire vis-à-vis des tiers, des délibérations de l'Assemblée générale, résulte des copies ou extraits certifiés conformes par le Président du Conseil d'Administration ou un Vice-Président ou deux Administrateurs.

Art. 47. —

L'année sociale commence le 1er Janvier de chaque année et finit le 31 Décembre.

Le premier exercice comprendra le temps écoulé entre le jour de la constitution définitive et le 31 Décembre 1913.

Art. 49. — Les produits nets, déduction faite de toutes les charges, constituent les bénéfices.

Sur les bénéfices il est prélevé :

1° 5 % pour former le fonds de réserve légale. Ce prélèvement deviendra facultatif lorsque le fonds de réserve aura atteint le dixième du capital social ; il reprendra son cours si la réserve vient à être entamée.

En dehors de cette réserve, l'Assemblée générale, sur les propositions du Conseil d'Administration, peut prélever, en outre, avant toute distribution de bénéfices, une somme destinée à la création d'un fonds de prévoyance dont elle détermine le montant.

2° Somme suffisante pour payer à chaque action ordinaire ou de fondateur un premier dividende égal à 6 % du montant versé sur chaque action, sans que, si les bénéfices d'une année ne permettaient pas ce paiement, les Actionnaires puissent le réclamer sur les bénéfices des années subséquentes. Toutefois, cet intérêt de 6 % pourra être prélevé ou complété sur le fonds de prévoyance ou sur toutes réserves extraordinaires.

Sur l'excédent, il sera attribué :

1 % au Président du Conseil d'Administration ;

1 % à l'Administrateur délégué ;

1 % au Directeur Général ;

1/2 % à chacun des autres Administrateurs et à chacun des membres du Comité de Direction.

Le solde, sous déduction de tous prélèvements pour la formation de réserves extraordinaires ou de fonds d'amortissement et pour report à nouveau, se répartira comme suit :

80 % aux actions ordinaires ;

20 % aux actions de fondateur.

Le Conseil d'Administration peut décider la distribution d'un acompte sur le dividende aussitôt après la clôture de l'exercice ; le solde de dividende est payé à l'époque fixée par l'Assemblée générale.

Art. 51. — A toute époque et dans toutes circonstances, l'Assemblée générale constituée comme il est dit à l'article 44, peut, sur la proposition du Conseil d'Administration, prononcer la dissolution anticipée de la Société.

En cas de perte des trois quarts du capital social, le Conseil d'Administration ou, faute par lui de le faire, les Commissaires des Comptes sont tenus de provoquer la réunion de l'Assemblée générale de tous les Actionnaires à l'effet de statuer sur la question de savoir s'il y a lieu de prononcer la dissolution de la Société ; dans tous les cas la résolution de l'Assemblée générale, qui est souveraine, est rendue publique.

Art. 52. — Lors de la dissolution de la Société, l'Assemblée générale, sur la proposition du Conseil d'Administration, détermine le mode de liquidation à suivre et nomme les Liquidateurs, dont elle détermine les pouvoirs et attributions.

Toutes les valeurs provenant de la liquidation, après l'extinction du passif, serviront à rembourser au pair le montant libéré des actions ordinaires et de fondateur sans distinction et l'excédent, s'il en existe, appartiendra et sera réparti savoir :

80 % aux actions ordinaires ;

20 % aux actions de fondateur.

Art. 54. — La présente Société ne sera définitivement constituée qu'après l'accomplissement des formalités prescrites par la loi. Les Assemblées constitutives auxquelles les souscripteurs pourront se faire représenter par un mandataire étranger à la Société, ainsi que toutes Assemblées constitutives d'augmentation du capital, devront être tenues dans les conditions déterminées par la loi du 24 juillet 1867.

A ces Assemblées, tout Actionnaire peut prendre part et a autant de voix qu'il possède d'actions ordinaires ou de fondateur, soit en son nom, soit comme mandataire, sans pouvoir avoir plus de dix voix.

Par exception, les Assemblées générales constitutives pourront être convoquées par une insertion faite dans un journal d'annonces légales de Paris, un jour à l'avance pour la première Assemblée et six jours à l'avance pour la deuxième Assemblée.

En cas d'augmentation du capital social, l'Assemblée générale, appelée à rendre cette augmentation définitive, pourra n'être convoquée que six jours seulement à l'avance.

## English Translation:

(Handwritten collector's annotation, upper right corner: "9 60" [uncertain])

**EXTRACTS FROM THE ARTICLES OF ASSOCIATION**

(Left column)

Art. 3. — The object of the Company is:

All banking and finance operations whatsoever, in all countries.

And especially the carrying-out of the agreements concluded and to be concluded with the Government of the Chinese Republic.

In general, to conduct all operations of banking, of loans, of deposits, of discounting, of insurance and of provident funds, of advances of funds, with a view to promoting all useful enterprises such as may be involved in the development and exploitation of the natural resources of China.

Art. 4. — The registered head office is established at Paris.

Art. 5. — The duration of the Company is fixed at ninety years, from the day of its constitution, save for extension or early dissolution.

Art. 6. — The share capital is fixed at 150 million francs.

It is divided into 300,000 shares of 500 francs each.

These shares comprise two categories, namely:

3,000 shares called founder's shares, numbered from 1 to 3,000, the number of which may never be increased or decreased nor their rights modified, even in the event of an increase of the share capital. However, in the event of a capital increase, the newly issued shares may be granted, concurrently with the founder's shares and the ordinary shares composing the present share capital, the first dividend of 6 % stipulated in article 49 below.

And 297,000 shares called ordinary shares, numbered from 1 to 297,000.

The respective rights of these two categories of shares are as follows:

1° In all general meetings other than those provided for in articles 51 and 54, Shareholders holding founder's shares are entitled to six votes per share, and Shareholders holding ordinary shares to one vote for ten shares.

2° The rights of the ordinary shares and of the founder's shares in the distribution of profits are fixed in article 49 below; their respective rights in the event of liquidation are determined in article 52 below.

Art. 7. — The share capital may be raised up to two hundred and fifty million francs, in one or more times, by simple decision of the Board of Directors, and at the rates, clauses and conditions which it shall fix, without there being any need for authorization from the General Meeting of Shareholders, which shall only be called upon to verify the sincerity of the declaration of subscription and payment.

In the event of a capital increase by way of the issue of new ordinary shares to be subscribed in cash, the holders of founder's shares and the holders of ordinary shares, at the time this increase takes place, shall have, subject to what is stated below, and also subject to the special rights of the Chinese Government in this regard, a preferential right to subscribe for all the shares issued, in the respective proportions of one half of the number of new shares in favour of the holders of founder's shares and one half of the number of these same new shares in favour of the holders of ordinary shares.

However, at the time of the capital increase, it may be decided, either by the Board of Directors, if it uses the power granted to it by the first paragraph of the present article, or by the General Meeting in all other cases, that a fraction of the number of new shares to be issued — without this fraction being in any case greater than one half — shall be left at the disposal of the Board to place it in the best interests of the Company, and as it shall see fit.

The Board of Directors shall fix the conditions, time-limits and forms in which the benefit of the foregoing provisions may be claimed.

Art. 11. —

The possession of a share entails, by full right, adherence to the Articles of Association of the Company and to the decisions of the General Meeting.

Art. 19. — The Company is administered by a Board composed of at least seven members and at most twenty-one.

The management of the Company's affairs in China is entrusted to a General Manager.

Art. 20. — The Directors are appointed by the General Meeting.

The Chinese Government shall have on the Board a representative with a consultative vote, but who shall, of course, have a deliberative vote once he has been appointed Director by the General Meeting. The duration of the functions of this representative shall be fixed by the Chinese Government at its discretion. The representative of the Chinese Government shall be entitled to the same indemnities and advantages of every kind as each of the Directors.

Art. 21. — Each Director must, upon taking office and throughout the term of his mandate, be the owner of 50 shares of one or the other category.

Art. 33. — The General Meeting duly constituted represents the entire body of Shareholders.

It is composed of all the Shareholders holding, for at least three months, ten ordinary shares or one founder's share. Any holders of a number of ordinary shares lower than that specified may either combine to form this number of shares or a greater number and be represented by one of them, or be represented by another Shareholder who is already himself a member of the Meeting.

The Board may always, as a general measure, reduce the three-month period just stated and accept deposits made within the limits it shall fix itself.

These depositors are given in exchange a registered receipt which serves as their entry card to the General Meeting.

Art. 35. — The General Meeting is held each year before 30 September at the registered office or at the place designated by the Board of Directors.

Extraordinary General Meetings may be convened whenever the Board of Directors recognizes their usefulness.

Art. 36. — Convocations to Meetings, both ordinary and extraordinary, are made by a notice inserted, at least twenty days before the date of the meeting, in one of the legal-announcement newspapers of the registered office.

(Right column)

For Extraordinary General Meetings, this notice must contain a summary indication of the object of the meeting.

Art. 39. — The agenda is settled by the Board of Directors.

It shall include only the proposals emanating from this Board and those which shall have been communicated at least fifteen days before the convocation of the General Meeting, with the signature of Shareholders holding together at least one sixth of the share capital.

No object other than those on the agenda may be put to deliberation.

Art. 40. — The Meeting is presided over by the President of the Board of Directors or, in his absence, by one of the Vice-Presidents or by the Director whom the Board designates.

The two largest shareholders present, either in person or as proxies and, on their refusal, those following them in the order of the list, until acceptance, are called upon to act as tellers.

The Secretary is chosen by the Bureau and may be taken from outside the Shareholders.

Art. 41. — Deliberations are taken by majority of votes.

Each member of the Meeting has as many votes as he holds multiples of ten ordinary shares, whether in his own name or as proxy, and as many multiples of six votes as he holds founder's shares, whether in his own name or as proxy, without, however, any one Shareholder, whether in his own name or as proxy, being able to command more than one third of the votes of the members present or represented at the Meeting.

Art. 45. — The deliberations of the General Meeting are recorded in minutes entered in a special register and signed by the members composing the bureau or the majority of them.

Art. 46. — The justification to be furnished to third parties of the deliberations of the General Meeting results from copies or extracts certified as true by the President of the Board of Directors or a Vice-President or two Directors.

Art. 47. —

The corporate year begins on 1 January of each year and ends on 31 December.

The first financial year shall comprise the time elapsed between the day of the definitive constitution and 31 December 1913.

Art. 49. — The net products, after deduction of all charges, constitute the profits.

From the profits there is deducted:

1° 5 % to form the legal reserve fund. This deduction shall become optional once the reserve fund has reached one tenth of the share capital; it shall resume if the reserve comes to be drawn upon.

Apart from this reserve, the General Meeting, on the proposals of the Board of Directors, may in addition, before any distribution of profits, set aside a sum intended for the creation of a contingency fund, the amount of which it determines.

2° A sum sufficient to pay to each ordinary or founder's share a first dividend equal to 6 % of the amount paid up on each share, provided that, if the profits of one year did not permit this payment, the Shareholders may not claim it on the profits of subsequent years. However, this 6 % interest may be deducted or supplemented from the contingency fund or from any extraordinary reserves.

On the surplus, there shall be allocated:

1 % to the President of the Board of Directors;

1 % to the Managing Director;

1 % to the General Manager;

1/2 % to each of the other Directors and to each of the members of the Management Committee.

The balance, after deduction of all sums set aside for the formation of extraordinary reserves or of a sinking fund and for carry-forward, shall be distributed as follows:

80 % to the ordinary shares;

20 % to the founder's shares.

The Board of Directors may decide to distribute an interim payment on the dividend immediately after the close of the financial year; the balance of the dividend is paid at the time fixed by the General Meeting.

Art. 51. — At any time and in all circumstances, the General Meeting constituted as stated in article 44 may, on the proposal of the Board of Directors, pronounce the early dissolution of the Company.

In the event of loss of three quarters of the share capital, the Board of Directors or, failing action by it, the Auditors are bound to convene the meeting of the General Meeting of all the Shareholders for the purpose of deciding whether there are grounds to pronounce the dissolution of the Company; in all cases the resolution of the General Meeting, which is sovereign, is made public.

Art. 52. — Upon the dissolution of the Company, the General Meeting, on the proposal of the Board of Directors, determines the mode of liquidation to be followed and appoints the Liquidators, whose powers and duties it determines.

All the assets arising from the liquidation, after the extinction of the liabilities, shall serve to reimburse at par the paid-up amount of the ordinary and founder's shares without distinction, and the surplus, if any, shall belong and be distributed, namely:

80 % to the ordinary shares;

20 % to the founder's shares.

Art. 54. — The present Company shall not be definitively constituted until after the accomplishment of the formalities prescribed by law. The constitutive Meetings, at which the subscribers may be represented by a proxy who is a stranger to the Company, as well as all constitutive Meetings for a capital increase, must be held under the conditions determined by the law of 24 July 1867.

At these Meetings, every Shareholder may take part and has as many votes as he holds ordinary or founder's shares, whether in his own name or as proxy, without being able to have more than ten votes.

By way of exception, the constitutive General Meetings may be convened by an insertion made in a legal-announcement newspaper of Paris, one day in advance for the first Meeting and six days in advance for the second Meeting.

In the event of an increase of the share capital, the General Meeting, called upon to render this increase definitive, may be convened only six days in advance.

title: East Egyptian Railways Bond
description: This is a 3½ % bearer bond (obligation au porteur) for £20 sterling, equal to 504 French francs, issued by the Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien, Société Anonyme, with its registered office in Cairo. The bond belongs to an emission of 12,500 obligations of £20 each, representing a total of £250,000 or 6,300,000 francs, raised against the company's authorised capital of £200,000 and sanctioned by Khedival decree of 8 June 1897. Interest of £0.14.0 (17.64 francs) is payable yearly in gold, half on 1 January and half on 1 July, redeemable by annual drawings within a maximum of seventy years, with coupons honoured in gold at Cairo, Alexandria, Paris, London, Amsterdam, Brussels and Geneva. Dated Cairo, 1 July 1897, and signed by two directors. The bilingual French and English text, gold-clause interest and seven European and Egyptian paying centres locate the issue within the foreign-financed railway building that knit Khedival Egypt into the capital markets of London and Paris. The dense blue guilloche border and ornate engraved lettering follow the protective convention of the period's securities, intended as much to deter forgery as to dignify the obligation.
creator: Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien
notes: Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien (Egyptian Eastern Light Railways), Société Anonyme; Capital £200,000; 12,500 bonds of £20 each; 3.5% Obligation au Porteur / Bond to Bearer; 504 Francs / Twenty Pounds Sterling; interest payable in gold; Cairo, July 1, 1897; No. R 10,592

title: East Egyptian Railways Bond (Conditions)
description: The reverse of a 3½% bond issued in pounds sterling by the Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien, presenting under twin headings "Garantie" and "Guarantees," in French and English, the security underpinning the loan. The conditions describe the concession: the construction and working of narrow-gauge agricultural railways, electric or steam, in the Egyptian provinces of Charkieh, Dakahlieh, and Kalioubieh, some 350 kilometres in extent. Under article 5 of the concession act of 21 May 1896, the Egyptian Government guarantees a net annual receipt of £36 sterling per kilometre, making good any shortfall. Below, a sinking-fund table ("Tableau d'Amortissement") schedules redemption from 1 January 1900 through 1960. The text speaks to the financing of light railways serving the Delta's cotton lands, where a sovereign guarantee was the instrument that drew European capital into Egyptian infrastructure.
creator: Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien
notes: Reverse (p.2/4) of goetzmann0990 (Egyptian Eastern Light Railways bond); Garantie / Guarantees conditions in French and English; Tableau d'Amortissement / Sinking Fund amortization table; No. 10,592
transcription:
# TRANSCRIPTION

## GARANTIE / GUARANTEES

**Original (French):**

La concession a pour objet la construction et l'exploitation des chemins de fer agricoles, a voie étroite a traction électrique ou a vapeur, dans les provinces de Charkich, Dakahlieh et Kaloubieh.

Les lignes concédées représentent actuellement 350 kilomètres environ.

Aux termes de l'article 5 de l'acte de concession du 31 mai 1896, le Gouvernement Égyptien garantit pour l'ensemble de toutes les lignes du Réseau concédé, une recette annuelle calculée a raison de livres sterling/tranches (£ 36) par kilomètre, et s'il s'engage a parfaire, jusqu'a concurrence de cette somme, toute insuffisance de ladite recette réelle réalisée dans une année pour atteindre le chiffre ci-dessus de £ 36 par kilomètre.

Le montant des recettes brutes, comprenant les recettes quelconques en bonne matière dues chemins de fer agricoles et leurs dépendances, après avoir été diminué du 90 /1, pour frais et dépenses d'entretien, dépublication et d'administration, a compris l'amortissement annuel en 90 ans du capital leur et miroisons paragraphe de l'article 2, constitue la recette nette des chemins de fer agricoles et de leurs dépendances pour l'application de la garantie mentionnée ci-dessus.

Le dit paragraphe prévoit en outre, pour chaque année de l'exercice de la concession, la mise en exploitation générale des lignes concédées et spécifiées a l'acte de concession (350 kilomètres), et a partir de la 1re mise en exploitation générale pour les lignes qui auraient été concédées ultérieurement au termes du même acte.

Audit paragraphe il est reçu en générale des lignes pour leur mise en exploitation générale sera par section de 25 kilomètres et la garantie commencera a s'en acquitter a partir pour chaque des sections au fur et a mesure de la mise en exploitation générale.

L'audit garantie sera payable dans le mois qui suivra clôt de la clôture de l'exercice financier de l'exploitation.

---

**English translation:**

The object of the concession is to build and work agricultural narrow gauge railways with either electric or steam traction, in the provinces of Charkich, Dakahlieh and Kaloubieh.

The conceded lines have at present a length of about 350 kilometres.

In accordance with article 5 of the Act of concession dated May 31, 1896, the Egyptian Government guarantees for the whole system of conceded lines, annual net receipts at the rate of thirty-six pounds sterling (£ 36) per kilometre and undertakes to supply, up to that amount, any deficiency in the said annual net receipts effected in the course of 13 months.

The amount of gross receipts, including various receipts of all kinds from the agricultural railways and their dependencies, after deducting 60/, for repairs etc., working and management expenses, including the annual sum to provide for the redemption in 90 years of the capital as per § 3 of article 2, constitutes the net receipts of the agricultural railways and their dependencies for the application of the above mentioned guarantees.

The said guarantee shall only from the commencement of the general working of the conceded lines mentioned in article 1 of the Act of concession (350 kilometres) and from the date of the general working of the lines which may hereafter be conceded in conformity with this said article.

The examination of the lines previous to their general working shall be proceeded with, by sections of 25 kilometres, and the guarantee shall be available from the date of such examination for each of the sections respectively at their general working commences.

The said guarantee shall be payable in the course of the month following that closing the financial year of the working.

title: East Egyptian Railways Bond Coupon Sheet
description: This is a sheet of attached interest coupons from a bond (obligation no. 10592) of the Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien, the company chartered to build and work light railway lines in the eastern Egyptian delta. The eleven coupons here, numbered 110 to 120, each entitle the bearer to eight francs and eighty-two centimes (8.82 fr.), payable on the first of January and the first of July in successive half-years; the dates run from 1 January 1953 (no. 111) through 1 July 1957 (no. 120). Each coupon may be presented at Cairo, Alexandria, Paris, London, Amsterdam, Brussels, or Geneva, the breadth of paying agents marking the international syndicate that financed Egyptian railway construction in francs. The printed terms beside the coupons set out the concession's working by sections of twenty-five kilometres. The plain guilloche frames and engraved lettering announce a fiduciary instrument whose value lay in its promise, not its ornament.
creator: Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien
notes: French interest coupon sheet (p.3/4) for goetzmann0990-0991 (Egyptian Eastern Light Railways bond); Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien coupons of 8 Francs 84c. each; coupons numbered 110-120; payable in Cairo, Alexandria, Paris, London, Amsterdam, Brussels and Geneva; No. 10,592

title: Coupon Sheet of the Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien, Egypt
description: This is the final sheet of interest coupons detached from a bond of the Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien (Company of Economic Railways of Eastern Egypt). Printed in French and English, the sheet ranges twelve identical coupons in a grid, each redeemable for £0.7.0 — seven shillings sterling — and each "Payable" on the first of January or the first of July across the years 1952 to 1958, the company's semi-annual dividend cycle. Payment is offered at a cosmopolitan roster of agencies — Cairo, Alexandria, Paris, London, Amsterdam, Brussels, and Geneva — mapping the European capital that financed Egyptian railway building and the international market in which the loan circulated. The remnant of the parent certificate, visible at the right edge with its company seal, anchors these coupons to the underlying obligation. As the last of several sheets, the document records the long tail of a fixed-income security maturing in the mid-twentieth century.

title: Esterhazy Central Casse Partial Bond
description: This is a partial bond (Partial-Schuldverschreibung) for twenty gulden in Conventions-Münze on the 20-fl. standard, issued by the central treasury of Count Casimir Esterházy von Galántha, an imperial chamberlain and proprietor of lordships in Hungary and Carinthia. The certificate represents one of 50,000 equal partial obligations into which a one-million-gulden loan was divided, bearing five percent interest and redeemed by lottery drawings over forty years beginning in 1848, with the underlying principal deed dated Vienna, 6 November 1847; a treasury stamp records 15 December 1847. The loan was placed through the Vienna wholesale house Hammer & Karis and secured on the count's Carinthian estates, among them the lordship of Tarvis with its forest districts. A magnate borrowing against landed property here taps the public credit market through a banking house rather than private lenders, the engraved comital arms with griffin supporters and coronet lending aristocratic assurance to a paper obligation.
creator: Gräflich Casimir Esterhazy'sche Central-Casse
notes: Partial-Schuldverschreibung (partial bond certificate), 20 Gulden Conventions-Münze; loan of 1,000,000 guilden issued against estate of Count Casimir Esterhazy von Galantha; managed by banking house Hammer & Koch; with lottery repayment recapitulation table; No. 14,725
transcription:
# Partial Bond of 20 Gulden Conventions-Münze — Loan of Count Casimir Esterházy von Galántha — Transcription

## Original (German):

**(Upper sheet — repayment schedule, printed sideways relative to the bond below)**

**Rekapitulation der Rückzahlungen.**

*(Table with columns:* **Verlosung** *(drawing date) —* **Stücke** *(pieces) —* **Rückzahlungs-Betrag fl.** *Opening rows:)*

| Verlosung | Stücke | Rückzahlungs-Betrag fl. |
|---|---|---|
| 15. Mai 1848 | 260 | 54800 |
| 16. Nov. 1848 | 260 | 53800 |
| … | … | … |
| **(Summe)** | **50000** | **fl. 2.371,900** |

*(Below the summary column stands a dense grid of individual half-yearly drawing tables, each headed by a redemption date — e.g.* „16. November 1877", „16. November 1887" *etc. — listing* Stücke *(1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 30, 40 …) against per-draw amounts and a column total. The full numeric grid of drawing dates and prize/redemption figures is a printed repayment schedule and is not enumerated cell-by-cell here.)*

**(Lower half — the bond certificate proper, within an ornate blue border; engraved comital arms with griffin supporters and coronet at top; embossed/blind seals to left and right.)**

**(ornamental cartouche:)** № **14725** •

**Partial-Schuldverschreibung**

über

***Zwanzig Gulden Conventions-Münze im 20 fl. Fusse,***

welche als Antheil an der von mir **Grafen Casimir Esterházy von Galántha**, k. k. Kämmerer, Herrschaften- und Realitäten-Besitzer in ***Ungarn*** und ***Kärnthen***, bei dem hiesigen k. k. priv. Grosshandlungshause **Hammer & Karis** contrahirten Anleihe von **Einer Million Gulden Conv.-Mze. im 20 fl. Fusse** auf 50,000 gleiche Partial-Obligationen vertheilt, mit den in dem jenseits hier angefügten Rückzahlungsplane zugerechneten Zinsen, innerhalb 40 Jahren, vom Jahre 1848 angefangen, in sechs halbjährigen und zweiundzwanzig planmässig weiters vorzunehmenden öffentlichen Ziehungen, dem Ueberbringer dieser Partial-Schuldverschreibung gegen deren Rückgabe sechs Monate nach erfolgter Verlosung derselben mit der nach dem Rückzahlungsplane darauf entfallenden Summe in Conv.-Mze., ausschliesslich jeden Papiergeldes, durch mich, meine Erben und Nachfolger, bei dem Grosshandlungshause **Hammer & Karis** oder ihren Rechtsnachfolgern baar ausbezahlt werden. Es steht übrigens dem Inhaber dieser Partial-Schuldverschreibung frei, den darauf entfallenden Betrag auch vor Verlauf des festgesetzten Verfalltages von sechs Monaten, unter Abzug von **vier pro Cent** jährlichem Disconto zu beheben, wenn sich derselbe **vier Wochen** früher bei dem Grosshandlungshause **Hammer & Karis** meldet.

Zur vollsten Sicherheit der Herren Darleiher ist die, diesem Anlehen zur Basis dienende Hauptschuldurkunde dd. Wien den 6. November 1847 über **Eine Million Gulden C.Mze.** auf die zusammen auf **C.M.fl. 3,700,893. 16 kr.**, Sage **Drei Millionen siebenmalhunderttausend achthundert dreiundneunzig Gulden und 16 kr. C.Mze.**, in Ungern gerichtlich geschätzten **Herrschaften, Wälder,** montan **Entitäten** und **Realitäten**, als: auf meine im Herzogthume Kärnthen, im Villacher Kreise liegende Herrschaft **Federaun**, und die Waldamts-Herrschaft **Tarvis** mit den Forstdistricten **Goggau, Greuth,**

**(The printed text ends here at the ornamental lower border; the sentence is left grammatically open — any continuation/signature is not present within the visible certificate.)**

---

## English Translation:

**(Upper sheet — repayment schedule, printed sideways relative to the bond below)**

**Recapitulation of the Repayments.**

*(Table with columns:* **Drawing** *(date) —* **Pieces** *—* **Repayment amount, fl.** *Opening rows:)*

| Drawing | Pieces | Repayment amount fl. |
|---|---|---|
| 15 May 1848 | 260 | 54,800 |
| 16 Nov. 1848 | 260 | 53,800 |
| … | … | … |
| **(Total)** | **50,000** | **fl. 2,371,900** |

*(Below the summary column is a dense grid of individual half-yearly drawing tables, each headed by a redemption date — e.g. "16 November 1877", "16 November 1887" — listing numbers of pieces against per-draw amounts and totals. This full numeric grid is a printed repayment schedule and is not enumerated cell-by-cell here.)*

**(Lower half — the bond certificate proper, within an ornate blue border; engraved comital arms with griffin supporters and coronet at top; embossed blind seals to left and right.)**

**(ornamental cartouche:)** No. **14725** •

**Partial Bond Certificate**

for

***Twenty Gulden Conventions-Coin at the 20-fl. standard,***

which is hereby recognized as a share of the loan of **One Million Gulden Conventions-Coin at the 20-fl. standard** contracted by me, **Count Casimir Esterházy von Galántha**, Imperial-Royal Chamberlain, proprietor of lordships and realties in ***Hungary*** and ***Carinthia***, with the local Imperial-Royal privileged wholesale house **Hammer & Karis**, divided into 50,000 equal partial obligations. With the interest reckoned in the repayment plan appended on the reverse, within 40 years, beginning from the year 1848, in six half-yearly and twenty-two further regularly-to-be-held public drawings, the bearer of this partial bond certificate — upon returning it, six months after its drawing — shall be paid the sum falling due upon it according to the repayment plan, in Conventions-Coin, to the exclusion of any paper money, in cash by me, my heirs and successors, at the wholesale house **Hammer & Karis** or their legal successors. It is furthermore open to the holder of this partial bond certificate to draw the amount falling due even before the elapse of the fixed maturity term of six months, under deduction of **four per cent** annual discount, provided he presents himself **four weeks** earlier at the wholesale house **Hammer & Karis**.

For the fullest security of the lenders, the principal debt-deed serving as the basis of this loan, dated Vienna, 6 November 1847, over **One Million Gulden Conventions-Coin**, [is secured] upon the lordships, forests, mining entities and realties judicially appraised in Hungary together at **C.M.fl. 3,700,893. 16 kr.**, say **Three Million seven hundred thousand eight hundred and ninety-three Gulden and 16 kr. Conventions-Coin**, namely: upon my lordship of **Federaun**, situated in the Duchy of Carinthia, in the Villach district, and the Forest-Office lordship of **Tarvis** with the forest districts **Goggau, Greuth,**

**(The printed text ends here at the ornamental lower border; the sentence is left grammatically open — no continuation or signature appears within the visible certificate.)**

title: Esterházy Central-Casse 20-Gulden Partial Debenture, Vienna, 1847
description: The reverse of a partial debenture (Partial-Schuldverschreibung) issued by the Gräflich Casimir Esterházy'sche Central-Casse, the central treasury of the comital estate of Count Casimir Esterházy, for a sum of 20 Gulden Conventionsmünze. It belongs to a loan of 1,000,000 florins C.M. raised in 50,000 such 20-florin debentures, secured on the count's domains. The page sets out the Rückzahlungs-Plan, a table of dated drawings by which the bonds were progressively redeemed, followed by the printed conditions, which enumerate the pledged estates and forests and bind the central treasury to repay. Dated Vienna, 15 December 1847, it bears the engraved Esterházy arms, the manuscript signature of Count Casimir Esterházy, and a countersignature. Such estate-backed amortizing loans let a great landed house tap public credit on the security of its lands rather than through a bank, on the eve of the 1848 upheavals.
creator: Gräflich Casimir Esterhazy'sche Central-Casse
notes: Reverse (p.2/2) of goetzmann0996 (Count Esterhazy bond); Rückzahlungs-Plan (repayment plan) for the gräflich Casimir Esterhazy'schen Anlehen von fl. 1,000,000 C.M. in 20 fl. Piecen; signed by trustees Werner Morris and Hoffmann Berliner; Die gräflich Casimir Esterhazy'sche Central-Casse; Vienna, 1848
transcription:
# Repayment Plan of Count Casimir Esterházy's fl. 1,000,000 C.M. Loan (in 50,000 debentures of 20 fl.) — Transcription

## Original (German):

*(The upper half of the sheet, printed inverted relative to the text below, carries the drawing/repayment table. The lower half carries the conditions and signatures, printed the right way up.)*

**Rückzahlungs-Plan**
**der gräflich Casimir Esterházy'schen Anleihe von fl. 1,000,000 C.M. im 20 fl. Fusse**
in 50,000 Schuldverschreibungen à fl. 20 Conv.-Mze. im 20 Gulden-Fusse.

Die Ziehungen finden in Wien unter Aufsicht zweier k. k. Notare statt.

*(Table of drawings — "Ziehungen". 18 numbered drawings, arranged in three horizontal bands of six. Each drawing is headed by a running number and a date; beneath it three sub-columns give: the number of debentures drawn (Anzahl), the amount of each (fl.), and the total sum (fl. zusammen), followed by a column total of debentures and of florins. The drawing dates read:)*

| Nr. | Datum | Nr. | Datum |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. | 15. Mai 1848. | 10. | 16. November 1854. |
| 2. | 16. November 1848. | 11. | 16. November 1855. |
| 3. | 15. Mai 1849. | 12. | 16. November 1856. |
| 4. | 16. November 1849. | 13. | 16. November 1857. |
| 5. | 15. Mai 1850. | 14. | 16. November 1859. |
| 6. | 16. November 1850. | 15. | 16. November 1861. |
| 7. | 16. November 1851. | 16. | 16. November 1863. |
| 8. | 16. November 1852. | 17. | 16. November 1865. |
| 9. | 16. November 1853. | 18. | 16. November 1867. |

*(Representative column — drawing No. 1, "15. Mai 1848." — reads as printed:)*

```
Anzahl   à fl.     fl. zusammen
  1      40000     fl. 40000
  1       4000         4000
  1       1000         1000
  2        500         1000
  5        100          500
 10         50          500
 10         40          400
230         20         [figures per image]
—————————————————————————————
260                fl. [total per image]
```

*(The remaining drawings Nos. 2–18 follow the same three-column scheme, each with one large redemption prize at the head — 40000, then 25000, 20000 fl. etc. in later drawings — descending through 4000, 2000, 1000, 500, 100, 50, 40 and 20 fl. amounts, the column totals of debentures rising from ~260 in the early drawings to 800 and 1200 in the last band, so that all 50,000 debentures / fl. 1,000,000 are amortised by 16. November 1867. Individual prize figures are a dense printed numeric grid; see image for exact amounts. [illegible where noted])*

---

**[Conditions — lower half of sheet]**

*(The printed conditions begin, after a blank space, with the enumeration of the pledged estates:)*

Raibl, Flitschl, Tarvis, Seifnitz, Wolfsbach, Uggowitz, Malborghet, Lussnitz, Leopoldskirchen und Pontafel (zusammen 44,403 ⁷⁹/₁₀₀ Joch productiver Waldboden, in einem Flächenraume von sieben Quadratmeilen), mit allen Grundstücken, Zugehörungen und Schuldigkeiten der Unterthanen, wie selbe in dem hochgräflichen Schätzungsprotocoll vom 26. Juni bis 19. September 1847 verzeichnet wurden und enthalten sind; dann meine fünf Eisenhammerwerke zu Malborghet No. I., III., IV., V. und VI., ferners mein Eisenhammerwerk zu St. Kathrein, und mein Stahlhammerwerk zu Unter-Tarvis sammt allen zugehörigen Liegenschaften im Canalthale gesetzförmlich intabulirt, und mit Rücksicht auf den Werth für dieses Anlehen nicht nur die, einer pupillarmässigen Sicherheit gleichkommende Deckung erreicht, sondern dasselbe überhaupt auch vorzüglich nur in der Absicht contrahirt wurde, um die früheren Lasten meiner Güter successive zu vermindern, die Güter selbst zu melioriren, deren Werth somit möglichst zu erhöhen und überdies meiner herrschaftlichen Güter-Central-Direction einen hinlänglichen baaren Betriebsfond zuzuwenden, um durch grössere Ausdehnung ihres commerziellen Geschäftsverkehrs die grosse Ertragsfähigkeit meiner diesem Anlehen verpfändeten Herrschaften und Besitzungen zu verwirklichen, und aus den Revenüen vorzugsweise die einfallenden Anlehe-Rückzahlungen auszuscheiden und berichtigen zu können.

Zu dem Ende erkläre ich noch hiemit, dass ich gegenwärtiger Partial-Schuldverschreibung, nach Massgabe des darauf entfallenden Antheils, alle jene Rechte und Sicherheiten, wie sie, aus derselben zukommend, in der oberwähnten Hauptschuldurkunde speziell aufgeführt sind, gewähre, daher namentlich die aus derselben bestellten Hypothekar- und Exekutions-Rechte dergestalt einräume, dass jeder Inhaber der gegenwärtigen Partial-Schuldverschreibung allein schon, ohne im Besitze der Hauptschuldurkunde zu seyn, alle diese Rechte, unbeschadet der gleichen Rechte der übrigen Theil-Schuldverschreibungen, ausüben dürfe und könne, wesswegen auch bei der seinerzeitigen Verfallszeit zur Aushändigung dieser Theil-Schuldverschreibung, als Quittung gelten und mich sodann als in meinen Händen befindlich, mithin allein zur Einleitung der Löschung eines Antheils von zwanzig Gulden Conv.-Mze. der intabulirten Anlehens-Summe nach den diessfälligen näheren Bestimmungen der Haupt-Schuldverschreibung berechtigen soll.

Beglaubigte Abschriften der zur Sicherheit aller Theile bei dem hochlöbl. k. k. Landrechte in Kärnthen deponirten oberwähnten Hauptschuldurkunde und sämmtliche Schätzungs- und Intabulations-Documente können jederzeit bei dem k. k. priv. Grosshandlungshause Hammer & Karis beliebig eingesehen werden.

Als Gerichtsstand des Vertrages in Betreff dieses Anlehens erkenne ich für mich, meine Erben und Nachfolger im Besitze der dafür verpfändeten Realitäten mit Verzichtleistung auf den königl. ungarischen Gerichtsstand das löbliche k. k. nied. öst. Landrecht.

**Wien, den 15. December 1847.**

*(Left signature:)* [Hammer & Karis]  *(Right signature:)* **Graf Casimir Esterházy**

*(Below, registration note in smaller type:)*

Gegenwärtige Urkunde ist in den Büchern der gräflichen Central-Cassa gehörig eingetragen worden.
**Die gräflich Casimir Esterházy'sche Central-Cassa.**

*(signature:)* [A. Sestan]

---

## English Translation:

*(The upper half of the sheet, printed upside down relative to the text below, carries the drawing/repayment table; the lower half carries the conditions and signatures.)*

**Repayment Plan**
**of the Count Casimir Esterházy loan of fl. 1,000,000 Convention-money on the 20-florin standard,**
in 50,000 debentures of fl. 20 Convention-coin each, on the 20-Gulden standard.

The drawings take place in Vienna under the supervision of two Imperial-Royal notaries.

*(Table of drawings. 18 numbered drawings in three bands of six. Each has a running number and a date; three sub-columns give the number of debentures drawn, the amount of each in florins, and the total, with column totals of debentures and of florins. The drawing dates are:)*

| No. | Date | No. | Date |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. | 15 May 1848 | 10. | 16 November 1854 |
| 2. | 16 November 1848 | 11. | 16 November 1855 |
| 3. | 15 May 1849 | 12. | 16 November 1856 |
| 4. | 16 November 1849 | 13. | 16 November 1857 |
| 5. | 15 May 1850 | 14. | 16 November 1859 |
| 6. | 16 November 1850 | 15. | 16 November 1861 |
| 7. | 16 November 1851 | 16. | 16 November 1863 |
| 8. | 16 November 1852 | 17. | 16 November 1865 |
| 9. | 16 November 1853 | 18. | 16 November 1867 |

*(Representative column — drawing No. 1, 15 May 1848:)*

```
Number   each fl.   total fl.
  1      40000      fl. 40000
  1       4000          4000
  1       1000          1000
  2        500          1000
  5        100           500
 10         50           500
 10         40           400
230         20          [figures per image]
——————————————————————————————
260                 fl. [total per image]
```

*(Drawings 2–18 follow the same scheme, each headed by one large redemption prize — 40,000, then 25,000, 20,000 fl. etc. in the later drawings — descending through smaller denominations down to fl. 20; the number of debentures per drawing rises to 800 and 1,200 in the final band, so that all 50,000 debentures / fl. 1,000,000 are redeemed by 16 November 1867. The individual prize amounts form a dense printed numeric grid; see the image for exact figures.)*

---

**[Conditions]**

*(The printed conditions open, after a blank space, with the list of pledged estates:)*

Raibl, Flitschl, Tarvis, Seifnitz, Wolfsbach, Uggowitz, Malborghet, Lussnitz, Leopoldskirchen and Pontafel (together 44,403 ⁷⁹/₁₀₀ Joch of productive forest land, over an area of seven square miles), with all lands, appurtenances and the obligations of the subject peasants, as these are recorded and contained in the high-comital valuation protocol of 26 June to 19 September 1847; further my five iron hammer-works at Malborghet Nos. I, III, IV, V and VI, moreover my iron hammer-work at St. Kathrein, and my steel hammer-work at Unter-Tarvis, together with all appurtenant real property in the Canal Valley, all lawfully registered (intabulated) — [these] being, with regard to their value, such that they afford this loan not merely a coverage equal to trustee-grade (pupillary) security, but rather the loan was contracted chiefly with the aim of successively reducing the earlier burdens upon my estates, of improving the estates themselves and thereby raising their value as far as possible, and moreover of providing my seignorial estate central-directorate with a sufficient cash working fund, so that, through a greater extension of its commercial business, the great productivity of the lordships and possessions pledged to this loan may be realised, and the maturing loan repayments may be met and settled preferentially out of the revenues.

To that end I hereby further declare that, in respect of the present partial debenture, according to the share falling upon it, I grant all those rights and securities which, accruing from it, are specifically set out in the above-mentioned principal debt instrument; in particular I concede the mortgage and execution rights created thereby in such a way that every holder of the present partial debenture alone, without being in possession of the principal debt instrument, may exercise all these rights, without prejudice to the equal rights of the other part-debentures; wherefore also, at the future date of maturity, upon the surrender of this part-debenture it shall serve as a receipt, and — the debenture being then deemed to be in my hands — shall alone entitle to the initiation of the cancellation of a share of twenty Gulden Convention-coin of the intabulated loan sum, according to the relevant closer provisions of the principal debenture.

Certified copies of the above-mentioned principal debt instrument, deposited for the security of all parties with the honourable I.-R. Provincial Court (Landrecht) in Carinthia, together with all valuation and intabulation documents, may at any time be freely inspected at the I.-R. privileged wholesale house Hammer & Karis.

As the legal venue for the contract concerning this loan, I acknowledge for myself, my heirs and successors in possession of the realties pledged therefor — renouncing the Royal Hungarian jurisdiction — the honourable Imperial-Royal Lower-Austrian Provincial Court (Landrecht).

**Vienna, 15 December 1847.**

*(Left signature:)* [Hammer & Karis]   *(Right signature:)* **Count Casimir Esterházy**

*(Registration note:)*

The present instrument has been duly entered in the books of the comital Central Treasury.
**The Count Casimir Esterházy Central Treasury.**

*(signature:)* [A. Sestan]

title: USSR Third State Loan for Reconstruction Bond, 1948
description: A state loan bond with a face value of one hundred rubles (Облигация на сумму Сто Рублей), issued under the Third State Loan for the Reconstruction and Development of the National Economy of the USSR (Третий Государственный Заём Восстановления и Развития Народного Хозяйства СССР) and dated 1948. The bond belongs to the cycle of mass subscription loans by which the Soviet state, in the wake of wartime devastation, drew savings from the population to finance the recovery of industry and agriculture under the Fourth Five-Year Plan; such loans were promoted as patriotic duty and largely deducted from wages. The engraved frame is unambiguously didactic: the state emblem with hammer, sickle, and wheat crowns a panorama of a factory, a hydroelectric dam, and combine harvesters working a grain field, fusing heavy industry and mechanized agriculture into a single image of planned reconstruction.
creator: USSR (Soviet Government)
notes: USSR Third State Loan for Reconstruction and Development of the National Economy (Tretiy Gosudarstvennyy Zaym Vosstanovleniya i Razvitiya Narodnogo Khozyaystva SSSR), 100 Rubles; 1948; Grade (Razryad) 150; Series No. 055726, Obligation No. 23

# USSR Third State Loan for the Reconstruction and Development of the National Economy — 100-Ruble Bond, 1948 — Transcription

## Original:

*(Top-left corner label)* **Облигация**

title: USSR Third State Loan Bond (Conditions)
description: This sheet sets out the conditions (Условия) of the Third State Loan for the Restoration and Development of the National Economy of the USSR (Третий Государственный Заём Восстановления и Развития Народного Хозяйства СССР), a lottery bond issued by the Soviet state and dated 1 October 1948 with a twenty-year term to 1 October 1968. The dense Russian text fixes the loan's terms: bonds of 100 and 200 rubles, each made up of two coupons; servicing not by fixed interest but by periodic lotteries, whose payout schedule fills the tabulated grid of prize amounts and winning-bond counts. Closing clauses pledge that the bonds, prizes, and interest are exempt from state and local taxes and levies. Issued in the third postwar year amid reconstruction, the loan channelled household savings to the Soviet treasury, the lottery substituting the chance of a windfall for the certainty of a coupon. Austere and wholly typographic, the layout treats the financial instrument as a public notice rather than an ornamented security; the imprint reads Госзнак. 1948.
creator: USSR (Soviet Government)
notes: Reverse (p.2/2) of goetzmann0998 (USSR Third State Reconstruction Loan, 100 Rubles); Usloviya Tretego Gosudarstvennogo Zayma (loan conditions) in Russian; lottery repayment terms with drawings scheduled 1953-1968; 1948
transcription:
# Conditions of the Third State Loan for the Restoration and Development of the National Economy of the USSR — Transcription

## Original:

**УСЛОВИЯ ТРЕТЬЕГО ГОСУДАРСТВЕННОГО ЗАЙМА ВОССТАНОВЛЕНИЯ И РАЗВИТИЯ НАРОДНОГО ХОЗЯЙСТВА СССР**

Третий государственный заем восстановления и развития народного хозяйства СССР выпущен на 20 лет — с 1 октября 1948 года по 1 октября 1968 года — в облигациях достоинством в 500, 200, 100, 50 и 25 рублей.

Облигации достоинством в 500 и 200 рублей состоят соответственно из пяти или двух сторублевых облигаций одной серии с пятью или двумя номерами и дают право на пять или два выигрыша, которые одновременно падают на каждый из номеров, обозначенных на облигации.

Облигации достоинством в 50 и 25 рублей являются частями сторублевых облигаций и дают право на соответствующую долю (половину, четверть) выигрыша, выпавшего на сторублевую облигацию.

Заем делится на разряды по 100 миллионов рублей в разряде. Каждый разряд состоит из 20000 серий. Серии каждого разряда имеют номера с № 40001 по № 60000. Облигации в каждой серии имеют номера с № 01 по № 50.

По облигациям займа весь доход выплачивается в виде выигрышей. Выигрыши установлены в размере 50.000, 25.000, 10.000, 5.000, 1.000, 500 и 200 рублей на облигацию в 100 рублей, включая нарицательную стоимость облигации (сто рублей).

В течение двадцатилетнего срока займа выигрывает 1/3 всех облигаций, а остальные 2/3 облигации погашаются по их нарицательной стоимости. Выигравшая облигация погашается и исключается из дальнейших тиражей. Общая сумма выигрышей установлена в среднем за двадцатилетний срок займа из расчета 4 процента в год.

В течение всего срока займа производится 40 тиражей выигрышей — по два тиража ежегодно.

(Right column — heading above table:) В тиражах выигрышей на каждый разряд займа, т. е. на каждые 100 миллионов рублей, разыгрывается следующее количество выигрышей:

(Table)

| Размер выигрышей на сторублевую облигацию, включая нарицательную стоимость облигации | **КОЛИЧЕСТВО ВЫИГРЫШЕЙ** в 1—10 тиражах разыгрывается в каждом | в 11—20 тиражах разыгрывается в каждом | в 21—30 тиражах разыгрывается в каждом | в 31—40 тиражах разыгрывается в каждом | Итого во всех 40 тиражах разыгрывается на каждые 100 миллионов рублей займа |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 50.000 рублей | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 40 |
| 25.000 рублей | 2 | 2 | 2 | 2 | 80 |
| 10.000 рублей | 5 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 200 |
| 5.000 рублей | 10 | 10 | 10 | 10 | 400 |
| 1.000 рублей | 100 | 100 | 100 | 100 | 4.000 |
| 500 рублей | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 40.000 |
| 200 рублей | 7.507 | 7.257 | 7.007 | 6.757 | 285.280 |
| Общее количество выигрышей | 8.625 | 8.375 | 8.125 | 7.875 | 330.000 |
| Общая сумма выигрышей (в рублях) | 2.301.400 | 2.251.400 | 2.201.400 | 2.151.400 | 89.056.000 |

Невыигравшие облигации займа погашаются (выкупаются) по их нарицательной стоимости с 1 октября 1953 года в течение 15 лет, остающихся до конца срока займа. Из каждых 100 миллионов рублей займа выкупается: в 1953—1958 гг. — по 2.500.000 рублей, в 1959—1963 гг. — по 4.200.000 рублей, в 1964—1968 гг. — по 6.200.000 рублей ежегодно.

Облигации займа, подлежащие выкупу в 1953—1967 гг., определяются ежегодными тиражами погашения. Облигации, не вышедшие в тиражи погашения, выкупаются с 1 октября 1968 года.

Облигации, на которые пали выигрыши, а также облигации, подлежащие выкупу по их нарицательной стоимости, могут быть предъявлены для оплаты до 1 октября 1969 года; по истечении этого срока облигации, не предъявленные к оплате, утрачивают силу и оплате не подлежат.

Облигации займа и выигрыши по ним освобождаются от обложения государственными и местными налогами и сборами.

(Bottom-left imprint:) Гознак. 1948.

---

## English Translation:

**CONDITIONS OF THE THIRD STATE LOAN FOR THE RESTORATION AND DEVELOPMENT OF THE NATIONAL ECONOMY OF THE USSR**

The Third State Loan for the Restoration and Development of the National Economy of the USSR was issued for a term of 20 years — from 1 October 1948 to 1 October 1968 — in bonds of denominations of 500, 200, 100, 50 and 25 rubles.

Bonds of 500 and 200 rubles consist, respectively, of five or two hundred-ruble bonds of one series, with five or two numbers, and carry the right to five or two winnings, which fall simultaneously on each of the numbers indicated on the bond.

Bonds of 50 and 25 rubles are parts of hundred-ruble bonds and carry the right to the corresponding share (a half, a quarter) of the winning that falls on the hundred-ruble bond.

The loan is divided into categories (razryady) of 100 million rubles each. Each category consists of 20,000 series. The series of each category bear numbers from No. 40001 to No. 60000. The bonds in each series bear numbers from No. 01 to No. 50.

On the loan's bonds the entire income is paid out in the form of winnings. Winnings are set at 50,000, 25,000, 10,000, 5,000, 1,000, 500 and 200 rubles per 100-ruble bond, including the nominal value of the bond (one hundred rubles).

Over the twenty-year term of the loan, 1/3 of all bonds win, while the remaining 2/3 of the bonds are redeemed at their nominal value. A winning bond is redeemed and excluded from further drawings. The total sum of winnings is set on average, over the twenty-year term of the loan, at a rate of 4 percent per year.

Over the entire term of the loan, 40 drawings of winnings are held — two drawings annually.

(Right column — heading above table:) In the drawings of winnings for each category of the loan, that is, per each 100 million rubles, the following number of winnings is drawn:

(Table)

| Amount of winning per hundred-ruble bond, including the nominal value of the bond | **NUMBER OF WINNINGS** — in drawings 1—10, drawn in each | in drawings 11—20, drawn in each | in drawings 21—30, drawn in each | in drawings 31—40, drawn in each | Total in all 40 drawings, drawn per each 100 million rubles of the loan |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 50,000 rubles | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 40 |
| 25,000 rubles | 2 | 2 | 2 | 2 | 80 |
| 10,000 rubles | 5 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 200 |
| 5,000 rubles | 10 | 10 | 10 | 10 | 400 |
| 1,000 rubles | 100 | 100 | 100 | 100 | 4,000 |
| 500 rubles | 1,000 | 1,000 | 1,000 | 1,000 | 40,000 |
| 200 rubles | 7,507 | 7,257 | 7,007 | 6,757 | 285,280 |
| Total number of winnings | 8,625 | 8,375 | 8,125 | 7,875 | 330,000 |
| Total sum of winnings (in rubles) | 2,301,400 | 2,251,400 | 2,201,400 | 2,151,400 | 89,056,000 |

Non-winning bonds of the loan are redeemed (bought back) at their nominal value from 1 October 1953 over the 15 years remaining until the end of the loan's term. Out of each 100 million rubles of the loan there is bought back: in 1953—1958, 2,500,000 rubles annually; in 1959—1963, 4,200,000 rubles annually; in 1964—1968, 6,200,000 rubles annually.

The loan's bonds subject to buy-back in 1953—1967 are determined by annual redemption drawings. Bonds not drawn in the redemption drawings are bought back from 1 October 1968.

Bonds on which winnings have fallen, as well as bonds subject to buy-back at their nominal value, may be presented for payment until 1 October 1969; after the expiry of this period, bonds not presented for payment lose their force and are not subject to payment.

The loan's bonds and the winnings on them are exempt from taxation by state and local taxes and levies.

(Bottom-left imprint:) Goznak. 1948.

title: USSR State Internal Lottery Loan Bond
description: This is a fifty-ruble bond of the State Internal Lottery Loan of 1982 (Государственный внутренний выигрышный заём 1982 года), issued by the government of the USSR. The face bears the denomination spelled in full — «Облигация на сумму пятьдесят рублей» — beside a large numeral 50 and the Soviet state emblem, with «СССР» on the shield below. As a выигрышный (lottery) loan, it carried no fixed coupon; holders were rewarded instead through periodic prize draws, the printed terms extending the loan's drawings across 1982–1990. Issued in the late Brezhnev years, the bond illustrates how the Soviet state mobilised household savings domestically, dressing public borrowing as a chance at winnings rather than guaranteed interest. The restrained green guilloche printing and heraldic crest project official solidity and discourage forgery.
creator: USSR (Soviet Government)
notes: USSR State Internal Lottery Loan 1982 (Gosudarstvennyy vnutrenniy vyigryshnyy zaym 1982 goda); 50 Rubles (Pyatdesyat Rubley); Grade (Razryad) 28; Series No. 269867, Obligation No. 087

title: USSR State Internal Lottery Loan Bond (Conditions)
description: The reverse of a fifty-ruble (ПЯТЬДЕСЯТ РУБЛЕЙ) bond of the State Internal Lottery Loan of 1982 (Государственный внутренний выигрышный заём 1982 года), issued by the government of the USSR and printed entirely in Russian. The face value of fifty rubles recurs in the corner medallions, while the body sets out the loan's conditions: a twenty-year term, with drawings held annually beginning in 1982, repayment and purchase at nominal value through the state savings banks, and exemption of both bonds and winnings from state taxation. Its substance is the prize schedule, listing per drawing one award of 10,000 rubles, five of 5,000, twenty of 2,500, fifty-five of 1,000, two hundred fifty of 500, twenty-five hundred of 250, and 17,169 of 100, totaling 20,000 winnings. Such lottery loans let the Soviet state borrow from household savings by substituting the chance of a prize for ordinary interest, channeling personal funds into the planned economy. The restrained guilloche borders and plain typography reflect a utilitarian official aesthetic rather than any decorative program.
creator: USSR (Soviet Government)
notes: Reverse (p.2/2) of goetzmann1000 (USSR 1982 State Internal Lottery Loan, 50 Rubles); loan conditions in Russian; lottery prize table and redemption schedule
transcription:
# Fifty-Ruble Bond of the USSR State Internal Lottery Loan of 1982 (Reverse — Conditions of the Loan) — Transcription

## Original:

(Reverse side of the bond. Large denomination lettering runs across the top-left; the numeral "50" appears in a medallion at the upper right and in a rosette at the lower center.)

**ПЯТЬДЕСЯТ РУБЛЕЙ**

**50**

(Central printed text block:)

**УСЛОВИЯ ГОСУДАРСТВЕННОГО ВНУТРЕННЕГО ВЫИГРЫШНОГО ЗАЙМА 1982 ГОДА**

1. Государственный внутренний выигрышный заем 1982 года выпускается на срок с 1 января 1982 г. до 1 января 2002 г.

2. Облигации займа свободно продаются и покупаются сберегательными кассами.

3. Заем выпускается отдельными разрядами, по 500 миллионов рублей в каждом разряде. Каждый разряд займа состоит из 100 000 серий с номера 200001 по номер 300000. Облигации в каждой серии имеют номера с 01 по 100.

4. Облигации займа выпускаются достоинством в 100, 50 и 25 рублей. Облигация достоинством в 100 рублей состоит из двух пятидесятирублевых облигаций одной серии с двумя номерами. Облигация достоинством в 25 рублей является половиной пятидесятирублевой облигации.

5. По облигациям займа весь доход выплачивается в форме выигрышей. Общая сумма выигрышей установлена в среднем за двадцатилетний срок займа из расчета 3 процента в год.

В течение двадцатилетнего срока займа выигрывает 32 процента всех облигаций, а остальные 68 процентов облигаций по истечении срока займа выкупаются по их нарицательной стоимости. Выигравшие облигации погашаются и исключаются из дальнейших тиражей выигрышей.

Выигрыши по займу устанавливаются в размере 10000, 5000, 2500, 1000, 500, 250 и 100 рублей на пятидесятирублевую облигацию, включая нарицательную стоимость облигации.

При выпадении выигрышей в одинаковом размере — 100, 250, 500 рублей — на все номера выигрышной серии по сторублевой облигации, имеющей два номера, выплачивается соответственно 200, 500, 1000 рублей; при выпадении на один из номеров облигации выигрыша в размере 500, 1000, 2500, 5000 или 10000 рублей, на второй номер выпадает выигрыш в размере 100 рублей и по сторублевой облигации выплачивается соответственно 600, 1100, 2600, 5100 или 10100 рублей. По облигации достоинством в 25 рублей выплачивается половина выигрыша, выпавшего на пятидесятирублевую облигацию.

6. В течение двадцатилетнего срока займа проводится 160 тиражей выигрышей — 8 тиражей ежегодно. Тиражи проводятся начиная с 1982 года в сроки, устанавливаемые Министерством финансов СССР.

7. В тиражах выигрышей на каждый разряд займа разыгрывается следующее количество выигрышей:

(Table. Stub column heading: "Размер выигрыша на пятидесятирублевую облигацию (включая нарицательную стоимость облигации)". Column group heading: "Разыгрывается", divided into "в одном тираже" and "во всех 160 тиражах"; each divided into "количество выигрышей" and "сумма выигрышей (в рублях)".)

| Размер выигрыша на пятидесятирублевую облигацию (включая нарицательную стоимость облигации) | в одном тираже — количество выигрышей | в одном тираже — сумма выигрышей (в рублях) | во всех 160 тиражах — количество выигрышей | во всех 160 тиражах — сумма выигрышей (в рублях) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 10000 рублей | 1 | 10.000 | 160 | 1.600.000 |
| 5000 рублей | 5 | 25.000 | 800 | 4.000.000 |
| 2500 рублей | 20 | 50.000 | 3.200 | 8.000.000 |
| 1000 рублей | 55 | 55.000 | 8.800 | 8.800.000 |
| 500 рублей | 250 | 125.000 | 40.000 | 20.000.000 |
| 250 рублей | 2.500 | 625.000 | 400.000 | 100.000.000 |
| 100 рублей | 17.169 | 1.716.900 | 2.747.040 | 274.704.000 |
| **Всего . . .** | 20.000 | 2.606.900 | 3.200.000 | 417.104.000 |

8. Облигации, на которые пали выигрыши, а также невыигравшие облигации, подлежащие выкупу по их нарицательной стоимости, могут быть предъявлены к оплате до 1 января 2004 г. По истечении этого срока облигации, не предъявленные к оплате, утрачивают силу и оплате не подлежат.

9. Продажа облигаций производится по ценам, устанавливаемым Министерством финансов СССР, а покупка — по нарицательной стоимости.

10. Облигации Государственного внутреннего выигрышного займа и выигрыши по ним освобождаются от обложения государственными налогами.
