title: Deli Railway Company Share
description: This is a bewijs van aandeel (share certificate) for one share of one thousand Gulden in the Naamlooze Vennootschap Deli-Spoorweg-Maatschappij (Deli Railway Company), a limited company capitalised at ten million Gulden. Issued at Amsterdam on 5 May 1914 and signed by the President-Commissaris and a Director, it traces the firm to its founding deed of 28 June 1883 and a chain of statutory amendments confirmed by Royal Decree through 5 September 1913. The certificate funded a railway across the Deli district of East Sumatra in the Dutch East Indies, the artery serving the celebrated tobacco and rubber estates whose thin-leaf wrapper tobacco fetched premium prices abroad. The engraved Dutch royal arms and restrained typographic border, printed by Joh. Enschedé en Zonen of Haarlem, lend the instrument the sober authority of metropolitan finance underwriting a colonial enterprise.

title: Société Belge d'Exportation (Jules Van der Laat & Cie) Action de Fondation, Antwerp
description: This is a founder's share (action de fondation) of the Société Belge d'Exportation, a commandite par actions—a limited partnership by shares—trading under the raison sociale Jules Van der Laat & Cie, with its siège social at Anvers (Antwerp). The capital social of 5,000,000 francs is divided into 10,000 shares of 500 francs each. Issued sans détermination de valeur, this founder's share carries no fixed par; the present coupure represents one-tenth of a full action de fondation. Constituted by authentic act of 29 July 1881 for a term of thirty years, the partnership unites the gérant Jules Van der Laat with two commissaires whose signatures, beside the red company seal, authenticate the title. Founder's shares of this kind rewarded the organizers and contributors of non-monetary assets with a claim on surplus profits—a characteristic device of Belgian and French company law that channelled Antwerp's export trade into share form.

title: Banque Royale (John Law) Tournois Banknote
description: A printed bearer banknote for ten livres tournois, payable to the holder on sight in silver coin ("en Especes d'Argent"). The text reads "LA BANQUE promet payer au Porteur à vüe Dix livres Tournois en Especes d'Argent, valeur reçeüe," dated at Paris the first of July 1720 ("le premier Juillet mil sept cens vingt") and marked "Division." It bears the manuscript-style countersignatures of the bank's officers: examined by Fenellon/Giraudeau, signed by Bourgeois/Delanauze, and controlled by Durevest/Granet. Issued by John Law's Banque (the Banque Royale, successor to the Banque Générale of 1716), the note belongs to the great paper-money experiment that financed and inflated the Mississippi venture. Its July 1720 date places it at the height of the collapse, after the bank had begun suspending silver redemption in May; the very promise of payment in silver "on sight" had by then become unenforceable. The leaf is austere and purely typographic, its authority resting on text and signatures rather than ornament.
creator: Banque Royale
notes: Banque Royale (John Law), 10 livres Tournois "Division" banknote No. 495,360, Paris July 1, 1720; issued during collapse of Mississippi bubble

title: Compagnie Française du Haut & du Bas Congo Ordinary Share
description: This is a bearer share certificate (action ordinaire au porteur, entièrement libérée) for one ordinary share of 5,000 francs in the Compagnie Française du Haut & du Bas Congo, a société anonyme agricole, industrielle et commerciale de l'Afrique équatoriale with its siège social in Paris. The engraved text records a capital social of 850,000,000 francs, divided into 170,000 shares of 5,000 francs each — 34,000 priority shares and 136,000 ordinary shares — with statutes deposited at the étude of Maître Aubron, notary in Paris, and the sheet signed by J. Cousin as président-directeur général and a delegate of the board. Printed in blue by Morax of Paris, the certificate belongs to the concessionary enterprises that exploited French Equatorial Africa: the colossal nominal capital signals an ambitious extractive venture in the Congo basin. The frame makes the colonial premise explicit — two elephant heads flank the title, and vignettes of riverine landscapes, plantation labour, and a steam launch render the territory as a field of resources to be worked.

title: Banco giro di Venezia 100-ducat cedula, Venice, 1798
description: This is a printed cedula, or credit note, of the Banco giro di Venezia for one hundred ducats (Ducati Cento), valued at eight lire per ducat. Its text declares that the note shall be received as effective money (dinaro effettivo) by all royal and public treasuries in payment of customs, land, and any other contributions, and likewise by the bank's own cashier for exchange in account (cambio in partita). It is issued in correlation with the proclamation of the first of October 1798. Founded in 1619, the Banco del Giro was among Venice's earliest public transfer banks; this note belongs to its twilight, issued under the Habsburg administration that followed Bonaparte's extinction of the Republic in 1797. The double eagle crowning the sheet announces Austrian sovereignty, while the winged lion of Saint Mark below clings to the city's older identity, the engraved emblems registering a transfer of dominion as much as of credit.
creator: Banco Giro di Venezia
notes: Banco Giro di Venezia, 100 Ducat cedula No. 1175, issued per proclamation of October 1, 1798 under Austrian administration; signed by Giovannelli and Schiantavello

title: Overend, Gurney and Company Limited Share Certificate
description: This is a share certificate of Overend, Gurney and Company, Limited, recording that John Metcalfe Smith, Esq., of Leeds is the registered proprietor of one share of £50, of which fifteen pounds had been paid up. Issued under the Common Seal at London and dated the fifteenth of November 1865, it followed the firm's registration and incorporation on the twelfth of July 1865 under the Companies Act 1862. Overend, Gurney & Company, the foremost discount house in the City and styled "the bankers' bank," adopted limited liability to raise fresh capital and offload mounting bad debts onto new shareholders. The partly paid structure left holders liable for the unpaid balance, a liability called in when the house suspended payments on 10 May 1866, precipitating the panic remembered as Black Friday. The certificate's restrained engraved script projects the probity its proprietors no longer commanded.
creator: Overend, Gurney and Company Limited
notes: Overend, Gurney and Company Limited, £50 share No. 72249 (£15 paid), John Metcalfe Smith, Leeds, November 15, 1865; company failed May 1866 causing "Black Friday"

UNDER THE PROVISIONS OF THE COMPANIES ACT, 1862.

*of* **Leeds,**

*is at the date hereof the registered*

**Proprietor of ONE SHARE of £50 numbered 72249** *in the*

*Capital Stock of* **Overend, Gurney and Company, Limited,** *subject to the Articles of Association of the Company, and that up to this day there has been paid up in respect of such Share the sum of* **Fifteen Pounds.**

*Given under the Common Seal of the said Company this 15th day of November, 1865.*

**Wm. Bos**
SECRETARY.

title: Eagle Bank of New-Haven Stock Transfer Receipt
description: This printed receipt records a transfer of stock in the Eagle Bank of New-Haven, a Connecticut state-chartered commercial bank. The form acknowledges that the named holder has, at the date inscribed, one share of the Capital Stock—of one hundred dollars—standing to his credit on the books of the bank, the share "this day transferred" and the transaction completed over the Cashier's signature. The printed note below insists that "Transfers of Stock must be made at the Bank in Person or by Attorney," a procedural safeguard typical of the period, when ownership existed only as an entry in the bank's ledger rather than as a freely circulating instrument. Dated 10 August 1824 at New Haven, the document belongs to the wave of small state banks that multiplied across New England after the War of 1812, and it captures the everyday mechanics—par value, in-person registration, manuscript authentication—by which early republican equity changed hands.
creator: Eagle Bank of New-Haven
notes: Eagle Bank of New-Haven, 1 share $100, W.A. Brockett, August 10, 1824; Connecticut state-chartered bank

title: St. Petersburg Electric Lighting Preference Share
description: This engraved certificate represents one fully paid-up preference share (Action Privilégiée, entièrement libérée) of 250 francs in the Société Anonyme de l'Éclairage Électrique de St. Pétersbourg, a Brussels-domiciled company (Siège Social: Bruxelles). It was constituted by notarial deed before the Brussels notaries Dedoncker and Van Halteren, published in the special supplement to the Moniteur Belge of 11 June 1891. The capital of ten million francs was divided into 40,000 preference shares of 250 francs and 20,000 ordinary shares. The instrument is a vehicle of Belgian capital export: investors in Brussels financed electric street and urban lighting in the Russian imperial capital, a pattern of foreign-controlled utility concessions then common across the Russian Empire. A round French foreign-securities tax stamp dated 1920 records its later circulation in Paris. The vignette frames a panoramic view of the St. Petersburg waterfront between two ornate lamp-standards crowned by lit globes, the lighting motif and flowing botanical ornament drawing on the Art Nouveau idiom of 1890s Brussels.
creator: Société Anonyme de l'Éclairage Électrique de St. Pétersbourg

title: Hungarian Mortgage Credit Bank Prize Bond
description: This is a bearer prize bond (Nyeremény-Kötvény / Obligation à Primes / Prämien-Obligation) of one hundred florins, bearing interest at four and one-half per cent, issued by the Magyar Jelzálog-Hitelbank — the Royal Hungarian Mortgage Credit Bank (Société de Crédit Hypothécaire du Royaume de Hongrie / Ungarische Hypotheken-Bank) — at Budapest, dated 1884. The text is printed in parallel Hungarian, French and German, payable to the bearer (bemutatóra / au porteur / an Überbringer). Founded in 1870 as the kingdom's principal vehicle for long-term mortgage lending, the bank funded itself through such instruments, which married a fixed coupon to periodic prize drawings — a lottery-loan device favoured across the Habsburg lands to broaden a security's appeal among small savers. The engraved frame, with the Hungarian crowned arms above and restrained classical scrollwork, projects the sober institutional authority the offering required.
creator: Magyar Jelzálog-Hitelbank
notes: Magyar Jelzálog-Hitelbank, 4½% 100 Florin Prize Bond, Series 2884, No. 061, Budapest July 1, 1880; trilingual Hungarian/French/German
transcription:
# Hungarian Mortgage Credit Bank 4% Prize Bond for 100 Florins — Transcription

## Original:

*(Trilingual bond — French / Hungarian / German — printed in three parallel columns. Left margin, printed vertically:)*

**MAGYAR JELZÁLOG-HITELBANK**

*(Header band, across the top:)*

**SERIE  2884  SOROZAT**   **NUMMER  061  SZÁM**

*(Central crowned Hungarian coat of arms. Round embossed stamp at upper right:)* K.K. CENTRAL-STEMPEL-AMT · WIEN

**SOCIÉTÉ DE CRÉDIT FONCIER DU ROYAUME DE HONGRIE.**   |   **MAGYAR JELZÁLOG-HITELBANK.**   |   **UNGARISCHE HYPOTHEKEN-BANK.**

**Obligation à Primes  4%**   |   **Nyeremény-Kötvény  4%**   |   **Prämien-Obligation  4%**

au porteur de   |   bemutatóra   |   auf Ueberbringer über

**CENT FLORINS  100**   |   **EGYSZÁZ O.É. FORINTRÓL  100**   |   **HUNDERT GULDEN  100**

---

*(Left column — French:)*

Cette obligation forme une part de l'émission de Quarante millions de florins, valeur autrichienne, en obligations 4% à primes, effectuée par la Société de Crédit Foncier du Royaume de Hongrie pour faire face aux prêts consentis par elle, conformément à ses statuts, aux Municipalités, Villes, Communes et Personnes legales en tant qu'elles sont autorisées par la loi à contracter ces emprunts.

Cette obligation rapporte 4 florins val. autr. d'intérêts par an jusqu'au terme de son remboursement. Ces intérêts sont payables chaque semestre le 1. Juin et le 1. Décembre de chaque année à raison de 2 florins val. autr. à la caisse de la Société de Crédit Foncier du Royaume de Hongrie à Budapest et aux places de paiement qui seront désignées à cet effet.

La Société de Crédit Foncier du Royaume de Hongrie s'engage à rembourser en cinquante années au plus tard le capital de cette obligation ainsi que les primes éventuelles à échoir conformément au tableau des tirages ci-annexé et approuvé par le Ministère des Finances du Royaume de Hongrie.

Le remboursement des obligations sorties au tirage ainsi que le paiement des lots se fait dans les conditions énoncées d'autre part.

Budapest, le 1. Juillet 1884.

---

*(Centre column — Hungarian:)*

Ezen kötvény részét képezi azon osztr. ért. 40 millió frtnyi összegben kibocsátandó 4%-os nyeremény-kötvényeknek, melyek a Magyar Jelzálog-Hitelbank által Budapesten, az alapszabályok értelmében Törvényhatóságoknak, Városoknak, Községeknek és más jogi személyeknek — a mennyiben ezek kölcsönök fölvételére törvény szerint jogosítva vannak — engedélyezett kölcsönök alapján adatnak ki.

Ezen kötvény a lejáratig évenkint osztr. ért. 4 frttal kamatoztatik és e kamat félévenkint minden év junius 1-én és deczember 1-én osztr. ért. 2—2 frttal a Magyar Jelzálog-Hitelbank pénztára által Budapesten vagy más közhirré teendő helyeken fizettetik ki.

A Magyar Jelzálog-Hitelbank kötelezi magát arra, hogy ezen részlet-kötvény tőkeösszegét és a netán reá eső nyereményeket 50 év alatt, a magy. kir. pénzügyministerium által jóváhagyott, a hátlapon foglalt sorsolási tervhez képest fogja kifizetni.

A kihuzott kötvények beváltása és a nyeremények kifizetése a túloldalon levő határozmányok értelmében történik.

Budapesten, 1884. julius 1-én.

---

*(Right column — German:)*

Diese Schuldverschreibung bildet einen Theil der Emission von 40 Millionen Gulden öst. Währg. 4%-ger Prämien-Obligationen, welche von der Ungarischen Hypotheken-Bank in Budapest gegen auf Grund ihrer Statuten gewährte Darlehen an Municipien, Städte, Gemeinden und andere juristische Personen, soweit dieselben zu deren Aufnahme gesetzmässig berechtigt erscheinen, hinausgegeben wird.

Diese Schuldverschreibung wird bis zur Fälligkeit jährlich mit fl. 4.— öst. Währg. verzinst, und werden diese Zinsen halbjährig am 1. Juni und am 1. December jedes Jahres mit je fl. 2.— öst. Währg. an der Casse der Ungarischen Hypotheken-Bank in Budapest und bei anderen kundzumachenden Zahlstellen ausbezahlt.

Die Ungarische Hypotheken-Bank verpflichtet sich den Capitalsbetrag dieser Theilschuldverschreibung und die etwa hierauf entfallenden Prämien binnen 50 Jahren nach Massgabe des beigefügten, vom kön. ungarischen Finanzministerium genehmigten Verlosungsplanes zu bezahlen.

Die Einlösung der gezogenen Schuldverschreibungen, sowie die Auszahlung der Gewinnste erfolgt in Gemässheit der angefügten Bestimmungen.

Budapest, am 1. Juli 1884.

---

*(Signature block, three languages:)*

**French (left):** Le président. — Le directeur. / La couverture requise par les statuts de cette obligation, est par ces présentes certifiée. / Pour le contrôle. / Le président du conseil de surveillance.

**Hungarian (centre):** Elnök. *(signature)* — Igazgató. *(signature)* / Ellenőrizte: *(signature)* / A jelen kötvény alapszabályszerű fedezetének bizonyságául / a felügyelő bizottság elnöke *(signature)*

**German (right):** Der Präsident. — Der Director. / Die statutenmässige Bedeckung dieser Schuldverschreibung wird hiemit bestätigt. / Für die Controlle. / Der Präsident des Aufsichtsrathes.

*(Bottom centre, printer's imprint:)* LÉGRÁDY TESTVÉREK, BUDAPEST

---

## English Translation:

*(Header:)*

**HUNGARIAN MORTGAGE CREDIT BANK** (Société de Crédit Foncier du Royaume de Hongrie / Ungarische Hypotheken-Bank)

**SERIES 2884   NUMBER 061**

**4% Prize Bond** — to bearer — of **ONE HUNDRED FLORINS / ONE HUNDRED FORINT Austrian value / ONE HUNDRED GULDEN — 100**

*(Round stamp:)* Imperial-Royal Central Stamp Office, Vienna

---

This bond forms part of the issue of Forty million florins, Austrian currency, in 4% prize bonds, made by the Land Credit Society of the Kingdom of Hungary (Hungarian Mortgage Credit Bank) to cover the loans granted by it, in conformity with its statutes, to Municipalities, Towns, Communes and legal persons, insofar as they are authorized by law to contract such loans.

This bond yields 4 florins Austrian currency in interest per year until the term of its redemption. This interest is payable each half-year, on 1 June and on 1 December of each year, at the rate of 2 florins Austrian currency, at the treasury of the Land Credit Society of the Kingdom of Hungary in Budapest and at the paying places designated for that purpose.

The Land Credit Society of the Kingdom of Hungary undertakes to repay, within fifty years at the latest, the capital of this bond as well as any prizes that may fall due, in accordance with the schedule of drawings annexed hereto and approved by the Ministry of Finance of the Kingdom of Hungary.

The redemption of bonds drawn in the lottery, as well as the payment of the prizes, is made under the conditions stated on the other side.

Budapest, 1 July 1884.

*(The Hungarian and German columns state the same terms: the bond is one of 40 million florins/gulden of Austrian value in 4% prize bonds issued by the Hungarian Mortgage Credit Bank in Budapest against loans granted under its statutes to authorities, towns, communes and other juristic persons entitled to take up such loans; it bears interest of 4 fl. Austrian value annually, paid half-yearly at 2 fl. each on 1 June and 1 December at the Bank's treasury in Budapest or other publicly announced paying offices; the Bank binds itself to pay out the capital of this partial bond and any prizes falling to it within 50 years according to the drawing plan approved by the Royal Hungarian Ministry of Finance and set out on the reverse; drawn bonds are redeemed and prizes paid per the provisions on the other side.)*

*(Signature block:)*

**French:** The President. — The Director. / The coverage required by the statutes of this bond is hereby certified. / For the control (audit). / The President of the Supervisory Council.

**Hungarian:** President *(signature)* — Director *(signature)* / Audited by: *(signature)* / In witness of the statutory coverage of this bond / Chairman of the Supervisory Committee *(signature)*

**German:** The President. — The Director. / The statutory coverage of this debt instrument is hereby confirmed. / For the control (audit). / The President of the Supervisory Board.

*(Printer:)* Légrády Brothers, Budapest

title: El Callao Consolidated Gold Mining Company Limited Share Warrant for Five Shares
description: A bearer share warrant for five fully-paid ordinary shares of £1 sterling each in the El Callao Consolidated Gold Mining Company Limited, registered under the Companies Act, 1862, with a nominal capital of £800,000 divided into 800,000 shares of £1. Issued under the company's common seal in London on 22 August 1903 and printed bilingually in English and French, the warrant signals a security floated for an international, cross-Channel investing public. El Callao, in the Bolívar region of Venezuela, ranked among the richest gold districts of the nineteenth century, and this consolidated undertaking gathered its claims under London capital. The transferable-by-delivery bearer form, the prominent denomination, and the radiating sunburst that fills the central panel — framed by dense purple scrollwork — present the certificate as a liquid, tradable instrument of speculative mining finance.

title: Société Anonyme d'Exploitation de Phu-Quoc Share
description: A bearer share (action au porteur) of 100 francs, fully paid (entièrement libérée), in the Société Anonyme d'Exploitation de Phu-Quoc, a Paris-domiciled company formed to work concessions on the island of Phu Quoc in Cochinchina (now southern Vietnam). The certificate states a capital of 6,000,000 francs divided into 60,000 such shares, with statutes filed before Maître Robert Aubron, notary in Paris, and a registered office in Paris (13, rue Lafayette). The face documents French colonial enterprise channelled through the Paris bourse: small-denomination paper invited metropolitan savers to fund the exploitation of a tropical possession prized for pepper and fish-sauce production. The engraved frame reinforces that promise, ringing a central silhouette map of the island with lush coastal panoramas of palms and sea, an idealized vision of the colony as a productive garden. Printed by Marcel Charles Verneau, Paris.

title: Imperial Russian Government Perpetual Bond
description: A perpetual bearer bond (Билетъ … Безсрочный) of the Imperial Russian Commission for the Repayment of State Debts, recorded in the State Debt Book in the name of its Administration. Its face capital is 5,000 rubles, stated expressly in assignats (Ассигнаціями Пять тысячъ Рублей), yielding a perpetual income of six per hundred per year — 300 rubles annually, as the side margins confirm — payable in semi-annual portions of 150 rubles each January and July. Issued in 1839 and registered that January, it falls at the moment of the Kankrin currency reform, when the depreciated paper assignat was being pegged to the silver ruble; that the instrument still reckons in assignats marks it as a creature of the old paper standard about to pass. The crowned double-headed eagle and the restrained engraved wave-and-line border project sovereign credit without ornamental excess, the document's authority residing in its lettering rather than allegory.
creator: Imperial Russian Government, State Commission for Debt Repayment
notes: Imperial Russian Government 6% Perpetual Bond. 5,000 rubles. Issued by the State Commission for Debt Repayment. 1859.
transcription:
# Bond of the Imperial Russian Commission for the Repayment of State Debts — Perpetual Capital (Lit. A, 1839) — Transcription

## Original (Russian):

(Upper corners: two oval guilloché medallions, each headed **№**. Left medallion handwritten serial: **145821**. Right medallion: serial repeated, largely illegible.)

(Centre top: crowned Imperial double-headed eagle.)

**Билетъ Государственной Коммиссіи погашенія долговъ**

title: Ferro-Carril y Almacenes de Depósito de Santiago de Cuba Share
description: This engraved share certificate (cédula de propiedad) conveys title to one share—una acción—of one hundred pesos in the Ferro-Carril y Almacenes de Depósito de Santiago de Cuba, the railroad-and-warehouse company of Santiago de Cuba. Issued at Santiago de Cuba on 20 April 1861 in favour of Gabriel de Llamas, it carries the signatures of the president, J. M. de Llaguno, the contador, and the secretary. The undertaking yoked rail transport to bonded storage depots (almacenes de depósito), an arrangement suited to moving and holding the sugar and coffee that flowed through the island's second port. At the certificate's heart a wood-vignette of a steam train crossing a mountainous coast announces the modernity the venture promised, framing the locomotive as the engine of Cuba's export commerce in the last decades of Spanish colonial rule.

title: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania Five Per Cent Stock
description: This engraved certificate of public stock, headed "Five per cent. stock of 1825," records a hundred-dollar loan owed by the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania to James P. Parker, in trust, of Philadelphia. It bears interest at five per centum per annum from the first day of August 1829, payable half-yearly at the Bank of Pennsylvania on the first of February and the first of August, the principal reimbursable fifteen years after the first day of January 1825. Issued by the Treasury Department at Harrisburg and dated November 18, 1829, the stock was created under an act of Assembly of April 11, 1825 entitled "An act authorizing a loan." Such funded debt underwrote the Commonwealth's ambitious internal-improvement program of canals and roads then taking shape as the Main Line of Public Works. At the upper left a seated allegorical figure of Liberty with the state arms lends republican dignity to an instrument of borrowing, presenting state credit as a public virtue rather than a mere fiscal expedient.
creator: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania
notes: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, Five Per Cent Stock of 1825, $100, No. 71, James P. Parker in trust, November 18, 1829; printed by Mowry & Amnon, Harrisburg

bearing interest at **Five per centum per annum**, from the **first day of August** 1829, payable semi-annually at the **Bank of Pennsylvania**—that is to say, one-half part thereof on the first day of February, and the remaining half part on the first day of August, in each year, being **STOCK created in pursuance of an act of Assembly passed the eleventh day of April, 1825**, entitled "An act authorizing a loan;"—the principal of which Stock is reimbursable and payable in Fifteen Years after the first day of January, 1825: which debt is recorded in this Department, and is transferable on the books of the Auditor General or at the Bank of Pennsylvania, by appearance in person or by attorney, according to the rules and forms instituted for that purpose.

title: Fosses à Charbon de M. le Marquis de Traisnel, fractional share (France, 1781)
description: This engraved and manuscript share certificate is an "action d'un denier dans vingt-cinq sols d'intérêt" issued by the Fosses à Charbon de Monsieur le Marquis de Traisnel, a coal-mining enterprise in the Hainaut–Valenciennes coalfield of northern France. Drawn up at the assembly of the said mines and dated 13 March 1781, it certifies the bearer's proprietorship of a single denier out of twenty-five sols of interest — one three-hundredth of the venture — entitling him to share proportionally in its profits and losses, subject to assessments for loans (emprunts) raised by the company; transfer requires the directors' consent. Signed by the directors De Wavrechin and Dehault, the document predates France's general mining legislation and shows how seigneurial coal concessions on the eve of the Revolution were financed by dividing the works into transferable fractional interests. The engraved crowned escutcheon of fleurs-de-lis at the head asserts the noble standing under which the concession was held.
creator: Compagnie des Fosses à Charbon de M. le Marquis de Traisnel
notes: Fosses à Charbon de M. le Marquis de Traisnel, Interest Share No. 263, signed by La Marsche and Dehault, June 13, 1781; pre-revolutionary French coal mining share
transcription:
# Document Transcription

## Original (French):

**Fosses à Charbon de M. le Marquis de Craisnel; Action d'un Denier dans vingt cinq sols d'intérêt. N°. 263**

Nous Soussignés Directeur, associés de la Compagnie des Fosses à Charbon de M. Le Marquis de Traisnel, reconnaissons que Monsieur D'. Montmoreuil Pelletier D'en Suanne D. est propriétaire d'in Denier d'intérêt dans l'entreprise de dites Fosses et, qu'en consequence, il participera dans la proportion audit Denier aux benéfices et aux pertes de la D. Compagnie, a condition qu'il sera soumis aux emprunts et aux mises faites à faire, et, qui ont de ou seront cy après délibérés, à charge, en outre de ne pouvoir vendre allener, ni céder la présente action dans l'agrément de la Compagnie, laquelle en conformité de son acte de societé, sera libre de retraire ladite action à un profit à en cas de vente ou de trasport. Le nouveau propriétaire devra se presenter dans l'année pour obtenir une nouvelle reconnoissance sous son nom, après avoir Donné, ou hypotheque, ou caution à l'appaisement des Directeurs associés, pour la surete des emprunts faits et à faire, fait à l'assemblée deD. Fosses, à Anniéd ___ Le 13 Juin 1781

[Signatures: La Marsche and Dehault]

---

## English Translation:

**Coal Mines (Fosses) of the Marquis de Craisnel; Share of One Denier in Twenty-five Sols of Interest. No. 263**

We, the undersigned Directors and associates of the Company of Coal Mines of the Marquis de Traisnel, acknowledge that Monsieur D'. Montmoreuil Pelletier D'en Suanne D. is the proprietor of one Denier of interest in the enterprise of said Coal Mines and, consequently, he shall participate in the proportion of said Denier in the profits and losses of the said Company, on the condition that he shall be subject to the loans and contributions made or to be made, and which have been or shall hereafter be deliberated, charged furthermore that he shall be unable to sell, transfer, or cede the present share without the approval of the Company, which in conformity with its deed of association, shall be free to redeem said share at a profit in case of sale or transfer. The new proprietor shall present himself within the year to obtain a new acknowledgment under his name, after having provided, mortgaged, or pledged security to the satisfaction of the Associated Directors, for the security of the loans made and to be made, executed at the assembly of the said Fosses, at Anniéd ___ June 13, 1781

[Signatures: La Marsche and Dehault]

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Serial number:** N°. 263
- **Coat of arms:** Ornate heraldic shield at top center (fleur-de-lis design, indicating French nobility)
- **Signatures:** Two signatures at bottom - "La Marsche" and "Dehault" (Directors)
- **Date:** Le 13 Juin 1781 (June 13, 1781)
- **Reference mark:** "FB" in top left corner
- **Archival mark:** Small notation in top right corner

title: Fukuyama-Han (Bingo Province) Silver Note, 1 Mon-me
description: This is a hansatsu, a domain paper note issued in the name of the Fukuyama fief of Bingo Province (後備, present-day eastern Hiroshima Prefecture, Japan), redeemable in silver to the value of one monme (銀壹匁), the monme being a weight measure of silver rather than a coin. The tall, narrow strip is woodblock-printed in the standard vertical hansatsu format: a seated deity of fortune crowns the sheet, the great denomination characters fill the center, and the issuing exchange house, 濱口屋源左衛門, is named at the foot beside its seals. The date 享保十五 (Kyōhō 15, 1730) appears at left, with a later 寶曆 (Hōreki-era) notation marking a re-authorization of the issue. Such notes, valid only within their own domain, let a daimyo stretch scarce silver and finance local obligations; the guardian deity above the sum quietly underwrites trust in paper backed by a lord's word.
creator: Fukuyama-Han (Fukuyama Domain)

title: General Investment Corporation Stock Purchase Warrant
description: This stock purchase warrant of the General Investment Corporation, a company incorporated under the laws of Delaware with its principal office in Boston, Massachusetts, entitles the bearer to purchase four shares of the corporation's common stock at a fixed price of $30 per share, exercisable at any time on or after September 16, 1929, without limit of duration. Dated June 13, 1938, the instrument is a detachable warrant of the kind investment trusts of the era issued—often as a "sweetener" attached to securities—granting the right, but not the obligation, to subscribe to shares at a predetermined strike. By 1938 the $30 exercise price stood well above the depressed market valuations that followed the 1929 crash, so the warrant likely traded as a speculative claim on future recovery. The engraved border and bank-note lettering, in the restrained steel-engraved style of the issuing security printer, signal trustworthiness and resistance to forgery.
creator: General Investment Corporation
notes: General Investment Corporation (Delaware), Stock Purchase Warrant No. NCW5418 to purchase common stock, Boston, June 13, 1938
transcription:
# Stock Purchase Warrant Transcription

**No NCW5418**

---

**STOCK PURCHASE WARRANT**

**TO PURCHASE COMMON STOCK OF**

# GENERAL INVESTMENT CORPORATION

*Incorporated under the Laws of the State of Delaware*

**WARRANT TO PURCHASE ___/___ SHARES**

---

THIS CERTIFIES that the bearer is entitled to purchase at any time on or after September 14, 1929 (without limit) all or any part of

_____ SHARES

full-paid and non-assessable shares of COMMON STOCK of GENERAL INVESTMENT CORPORATION, a corporation of the State of Delaware (herein called the "corporation"), as such stock may be constituted at the time of purchase, at the price of $8 per share, upon surrender of this warrant accompanied by full payment of the purchase price in cash at the principal office of the corporation in Boston, Massachusetts. This warrant does not entitle the holder to any rights whatsoever as a stockholder of the corporation. It is expressly agreed that the shares of common stock of the corporation agreed to be sold hereunder are shares of the common stock of the corporation as such stock may be constituted at the time of purchase and that delivery of the number of shares of common stock called for herein, in good delivery hereunder whether or not the claim is made, shall constitute full performance of all the obligations of the corporation hereunder with respect to the exercise of the rights or interests represented by the shares of common stock of the corporation authorized and outstanding at the date hereof or at any other time prior to such purchase and irrespective of any dividends, sales, exchanges or other disposition of stock of the corporation of any class, any change of authorized capital or reclassification thereof, the sale or lease by the corporation of all or any part of its property, or any change whatsoever; provided, however, that if while this warrant is in effect the corporation shall have recapitalized by subdivision or combination of its outstanding shares of common stock into a greater or lesser number of shares, then, in each such case, the number of shares of common stock purchasable upon exercise of this warrant shall be increased or decreased (as the case may be) in proportion to the change through such subdivision or combination in the number of outstanding shares of common stock of the corporation, and provided further, that if while this warrant is in effect the corporation shall consolidate or merge with or into any other corporation, then, as a condition of such consolidation or merger, adequate provision shall be made whereby the holder of this warrant shall thereafter be entitled to purchase, in lieu of the shares of common stock of the corporation theretofore purchasable upon exercise of this warrant, such shares of stock, securities or assets as would have been purchasable upon the exercise of this warrant had such consolidation or merger not taken place. The existence of this warrant shall not prevent the lawful dissolution of the corporation. Whenever in this warrant the corporation is referred to, it shall be deemed to include any corporation into or to which the corporation may have been merged or consolidated. This warrant is exchangeable at the option of the bearer at the principal office of the corporation in Boston, Massachusetts for other similar warrants of different denominations entitling the bearer thereof to purchase an equal aggregate number of shares of common stock of the corporation as specified in this warrant. Every holder of this warrant, by accepting the same, consents and agrees with the corporation and with every subsequent holder of this warrant as follows: Title to this warrant shall be registered by the absolute owner of this warrant and is hereby granted power to transfer absolute title thereto by delivery thereof to a purchaser thereof for value (present or accrued); without notice of prior defenses or equities or claims enforceable against his transferor; every bearer of this warrant shall be presumed to be such a purchaser thereunder unless the contrary be established; every bearer of this warrant waives and renounces all his equities and rights herein in favor of every such purchaser and every such purchaser shall acquire title hereto and to all rights represented hereby; the corporation and any warrant agent shall treat any bearer who is a purchaser hereof of the character above described, as the absolute owner thereof for all purposes without being affected by any notice to the contrary; the corporation and any warrant agent shall be entitled to accept the surrender of this warrant from the bearer hereof and such surrender shall constitute full and complete discharge of all liability of the corporation and any warrant agent hereunder; the corporation does not make any contract with any bearer hereof except as contained in this warrant and any performance by the corporation with respect to the bearer hereof of any agreement contained with respect to this warrant, shall be due performance of such agreement. On the back of this warrant is a form of subscription which must be used upon exercise thereof.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the corporation has caused this warrant to be signed in its behalf by the undersigned warrant agents.

**GENERAL INVESTMENT CORPORATION**
*(Boston, Mass.)*

Dated **JUN 14 1928** [handwritten]

By _____________________________ [signature]

Warrant Agent _____________________________ [signature]

---

**CENTRAL BANKNOTE COMPANY** [printer's mark at bottom]

---

- **Signatures:** Two manuscript signatures present (one by "By" line, one by "Warrant Agent" line) [illegible]
- **Printer:** Central Banknote Company (bottom border)
- **Decorative Border:** Blue ornamental border frame with geometric patterns

- **Expiration Date:** September 14, 1929 (noted as "without limit" suggesting possible extension)

title: Imperial Japanese Government 4% Loan Bond, Paris, 1910
description: This green-printed certificate is a 500-franc bearer bond (obligation) of the 4% Imperial Japanese Government Loan of 1910 (Emprunt de Quatre Cent Cinquante Millions de Francs), issued under Law No. 6 of 1906 and the Finance Minister's Ordinance No. 19, promulgated 30 April 1910. The text records its issue at the head office, dated 15 May 1910, with coupons payable in Paris, as the red overprint "Coupons délivrés, Paris" attests. Such franc-denominated bonds carried Japanese sovereign debt deep into the Paris market, where France stood among the largest holders, the proceeds servicing the costs of the wars against China and Russia and the public works that followed them. A finely engraved vignette of Mount Fuji crowns the sheet, lending a familiar emblem of the lender's distant sovereign to reassure the European subscriber.
creator: Gouvernement Impérial du Japon
notes: Gouvernement Impérial du Japon, 4½% Emprunt de 1910 (450 Million Francs), 500 Franc bond, Series 30, No. 403578, May 15, 1911; Mount Fuji vignette
transcription:
# Imperial Japanese Government 4% Loan of 1910 — 500-Franc Bearer Bond — Transcription

## Original:

(Vignette at top: Mount Fuji within an ornate green Art Nouveau border; imperial chrysanthemum crest in the top-center cartouche.)

**GOUVERNEMENT IMPÉRIAL DU JAPON**

**EMPRUNT 4% DE 1910**

**DE QUATRE CENT CINQUANTE MILLIONS DE FRANCS.**

CRÉÉE EN VERTU DE LA LOI N° 6 DE 1906 ET DE L'ORDONNANCE N° 19 DU MINISTRE DES FINANCES, PROMULGUÉE LE 30 AVRIL 1910.

**OBLIGATION DE 500 FRANCS**

**Série 30     N° 408279**

(Round black tax handstamp at left:) FONDS D'ÉTATS ÉTRANGERS / 2% / PLEIN TARIF / 26 – PARIS – 1ER / 1911

(Red overprint at right:) du 15 Nov. 19[..] au 15 Mai 19[..] / COUPONS DÉLIVRÉS à PARIS

---

En vertu de la Loi et de l'Ordonnance Ministérielle ci-dessus, le Gouvernement Impérial du Japon est autorisé à émettre, dans les conditions indiquées ci-après, des obligations en représentation d'un Emprunt de Quatre Cent Cinquante Millions de Francs, divisées en quarante-cinq séries numérotées consécutivement, de Fr. 10.000.000 chacune.

En conséquence, je soussigné Baron S. Kurino, Ambassadeur Extraordinaire et Plénipotentiaire de S. M. l'Empereur du Japon à Paris, déclare par les présentes au nom du Gouvernement Impérial du Japon engager et obliger ledit Gouvernement à la due observation et à l'exécution ponctuelle des conventions, clauses et conditions ci-après, savoir :

1° Le Gouvernement Impérial du Japon remboursera, comme il est dit plus loin, au porteur, le capital du présent titre, soit la somme de Cinq Cents Francs (Fr. 500) et lui paiera, en outre, semestriellement, depuis le 15 Mai 1910, les intérêts au taux de quatre pour cent (4%) l'an sur cette somme.

2° Lesdits intérêts seront payables le 15 Mai et le 15 Novembre de chaque année.

3° Le paiement des coupons semestriels et le remboursement du capital du présent titre s'effectueront à Paris, chez M.M. de Rothschild Frères, en Francs pour les montants indiqués respectivement sur le titre et les coupons; à Londres, aux guichets de la Yokohama Specie Bank, Limited, au change du jour sur Paris; à Bruxelles, à la parité du change sur Paris, aux guichets de la Maison que désigneront M.M. de Rothschild Frères; au Japon, aux mêmes dates qu'à Paris, sur la base de 100 Yens or pour 258 Francs exigibles.

4° Sauf le cas de remboursement anticipé prévu plus loin, le capital du présent titre sera remboursé le 15 Mai 1970.

5° Les intérêts seront payés contre remise des coupons échus et le capital contre remise du présent titre muni de tous ses coupons non échus. Le montant des coupons non échus manquants sera déduit du capital à rembourser au porteur.

6° Le ou à partir du 15 Mai 1920 le Gouvernement Impérial du Japon pourra, moyennant un préavis de six mois au moins, donné par voie d'insertion au "Journal Officiel" de la République Française et dans deux autres journaux de Paris, rembourser au pair, soit la totalité de l'Emprunt, soit une ou plusieurs séries entières.

7° Au cas où le Gouvernement Impérial du Japon rembourserait par anticipation, comme il est dit au paragraphe 6 ci-dessus une partie du présent Emprunt, les séries à rembourser seront désignées par le sort.

8° Les numéros des séries sorties aux tirages seront publiés à Paris dans le "Journal Officiel" et dans deux autres journaux.

9° La date du remboursement des obligations amorties en vertu du paragraphe 6 ci-dessus coïncidera avec l'échéance d'un coupon semestriel. A partir de cette date, les obligations ainsi amorties cesseront de porter intérêts.

10° Les 45 séries entre lesquelles sont réparties les obligations du présent Emprunt sont numérotées de 1 à 45 et comprennent chacune :

(table:) 14.000 Obligations de 500 Fr.    700 Obligations de 2.500 Fr.    250 Obligations de 5.000 Fr.

Ces obligations sont numérotées de 1 à 672.750, savoir :
N°s 1 à 630.000 pour les obligations de 500 Fr.
N°s 630.001 à 661.500 pour les obligations de 2.500 Fr.
N°s 661.501 à 672.750 pour les obligations de 5.000 Fr.

11° Le paiement des coupons de la présente obligation et le remboursement de son capital s'effectueront en temps de guerre de même qu'en temps de paix, que le porteur soit sujet d'une puissance amie ou ennemie.

12° Les titres et coupons du présent Emprunt appartenant à des Étrangers ne résidant pas au Japon sont exempts à tout jamais de tout impôt japonais présent ou futur.

**POUR CONTRÔLE**
L'AGENT FINANCIER DU GOUVERNEMENT IMPÉRIAL DU JAPON
PAR DÉLÉGATION SPÉCIALE.
(signature)

Fait le 15 Mai 1910.
(signature) S. Kurino
AMBASSADEUR EXTRAORDINAIRE ET PLÉNIPOTENTIAIRE DE
S.M. L'EMPEREUR DU JAPON À PARIS.

(printer's imprint, bottom center:) WATERLOW & SONS LIMITED, LONDON WALL, LONDON, E.C.

---

## English Translation:

**IMPERIAL GOVERNMENT OF JAPAN**

**4% LOAN OF 1910**

**OF FOUR HUNDRED AND FIFTY MILLION FRANCS.**

Created by virtue of Law No. 6 of 1906 and of Ordinance No. 19 of the Minister of Finance, promulgated on 30 April 1910.

**BOND OF 500 FRANCS**

**Series 30     No. 408279**

(Round black tax handstamp at left:) FOREIGN STATE SECURITIES / 2% / FULL TARIFF / 26 – PARIS – 1st / 1911

(Red overprint at right:) from 15 Nov. 19[..] to 15 May 19[..] / COUPONS DELIVERED at PARIS

---

By virtue of the Law and the Ministerial Ordinance above, the Imperial Government of Japan is authorized to issue, under the conditions indicated hereafter, bonds representing a Loan of Four Hundred and Fifty Million Francs, divided into forty-five series numbered consecutively, of Fr. 10,000,000 each.

Consequently, I the undersigned Baron S. Kurino, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary of H.M. the Emperor of Japan at Paris, declare by these presents, in the name of the Imperial Government of Japan, to bind and oblige the said Government to the due observance and punctual execution of the covenants, clauses and conditions hereafter, namely:

1. The Imperial Government of Japan shall repay, as stated further on, to the bearer, the capital of the present bond, that is the sum of Five Hundred Francs (Fr. 500), and shall pay him besides, half-yearly, from 15 May 1910, interest at the rate of four per cent (4%) per annum on that sum.

2. The said interest shall be payable on 15 May and 15 November of each year.

3. The payment of the half-yearly coupons and the repayment of the capital of the present bond shall be effected at Paris, at Messrs. de Rothschild Frères, in Francs for the amounts respectively indicated on the bond and the coupons; at London, at the counters of the Yokohama Specie Bank, Limited, at the day's rate of exchange on Paris; at Brussels, at the par of exchange on Paris, at the counters of the House that Messrs. de Rothschild Frères shall designate; in Japan, at the same dates as at Paris, on the basis of 100 gold Yen for 258 Francs payable.

4. Save in the case of early redemption provided for further on, the capital of the present bond shall be repaid on 15 May 1970.

5. The interest shall be paid against surrender of the matured coupons and the capital against surrender of the present bond furnished with all its unmatured coupons. The amount of any missing unmatured coupons shall be deducted from the capital to be repaid to the bearer.

6. On or after 15 May 1920 the Imperial Government of Japan may, on at least six months' notice given by insertion in the "Journal Officiel" of the French Republic and in two other Paris newspapers, repay at par either the whole of the Loan or one or more entire series.

7. Should the Imperial Government of Japan repay in advance, as stated in paragraph 6 above, part of the present Loan, the series to be repaid shall be designated by lot.

8. The numbers of the series drawn shall be published at Paris in the "Journal Officiel" and in two other newspapers.

9. The date of repayment of the bonds redeemed by virtue of paragraph 6 above shall coincide with the due date of a half-yearly coupon. From that date, the bonds thus redeemed shall cease to bear interest.

10. The 45 series among which the bonds of the present Loan are distributed are numbered from 1 to 45 and each comprise:

(table:) 14,000 Bonds of 500 Fr.    700 Bonds of 2,500 Fr.    250 Bonds of 5,000 Fr.

These bonds are numbered from 1 to 672,750, namely:
Nos. 1 to 630,000 for the bonds of 500 Fr.
Nos. 630,001 to 661,500 for the bonds of 2,500 Fr.
Nos. 661,501 to 672,750 for the bonds of 5,000 Fr.

11. The payment of the coupons of the present bond and the repayment of its capital shall be effected in time of war as in time of peace, whether the bearer be a subject of a friendly or an enemy power.

12. The certificates and coupons of the present Loan belonging to Foreigners not residing in Japan are exempt for ever from all Japanese tax present or future.

**FOR CONTROL**
THE FINANCIAL AGENT OF THE IMPERIAL GOVERNMENT OF JAPAN
BY SPECIAL DELEGATION.
(signature)

Done the 15 May 1910.
(signature) S. Kurino
AMBASSADOR EXTRAORDINARY AND PLENIPOTENTIARY OF
H.M. THE EMPEROR OF JAPAN AT PARIS.

(printer's imprint, bottom center:) WATERLOW & SONS LIMITED, LONDON WALL, LONDON, E.C.

title: Gouvernement de la Nouvelle-Calédonie et Dépendances 4% Bond, Paris, 1931
description: A bearer bond (obligation au porteur) of 1,000 francs bearing 4% interest, free of present and future taxes ("Net d'Impôts Présents et Futurs"), issued by the Gouvernement de la Nouvelle-Calédonie et Dépendances under its "Emprunt 4% 1931." The certificate records that this is one of 34.020 obligations of 1,000 francs each, amortizable over fifty years, forming part of a larger loan of 95 million francs authorized by the law of 22 February 1931 and approved by decree of 30 July 1931. It is dated Paris, 31 August 1931, and carries an attached coupon sheet. The issue speaks to the fiscal reach of a French Pacific colony turning to the Paris market to fund its administration and public works at the onset of the Depression, as world prices for nickel, the territory's principal export, were already falling. The restrained typographic frame and the interlaced NCG monogram convey official sobriety rather than ornamental display.
creator: Gouvernement de la Nouvelle-Calédonie et Dépendances
notes: Gouvernement de la Nouvelle-Calédonie et Dépendances, 4% Emprunt 1931, 1,000 Franc bond No. 008,679, Paris, August 31, 1931; 34,500 total bonds
transcription:
# Government of New Caledonia and Dependencies — 4% Loan of 1931, 1,000-Franc Bearer Bond — Transcription

## Original:

(Handwritten in pen, top-left corner:) 8679

(Faint printed marks, top-right corner:) 2 · c

(Interlaced monogram at centre top: **NCG**)

**GOUVERNEMENT**
DE LA
**NOUVELLE-CALÉDONIE ET DÉPENDANCES**

**EMPRUNT 4 %  1931**
NET D'IMPOTS PRÉSENTS ET FUTURS
*Approuvé par Décret du 3o Juillet 1931.*
REPRÉSENTÉ PAR **34.020** OBLIGATIONS DE **1.000** FRANCS CHACUNE, AMORTISSABLES EN CINQUANTE ANS, FAISANT PARTIE
DE L'EMPRUNT DE **95** MILLIONS DE FRANCS, AUTORISÉ PAR LA LOI DU 22 FÉVRIER 1931.

**Obligation de Mille Francs au Porteur  N° 008,679**

(left) LE GOUVERNEUR DE LA NOUVELLE-CALÉDONIE
ET DÉPENDANCES,
(right) VU POUR CONTROLE,

*(two handwritten signatures)*

PARIS, le 31 Août 1931.

(Red circular hand-stamp overlapping the text and coupons, reading around its rim:) GOUVERNEMENT DE LA NOUVELLE CALÉDONIE ET DÉPENDANCES *(with NCG monogram at centre)*

(Large pale diagonal underprint / watermark across the body:) GOUVERNEMENT DE LA NOUVELLE-CALÉDONIE … PENDANT

---

**EXTRAITS DE LA CONVENTION**
passée entre le Gouvernement de la Nouvelle-Calédonie et Dépendances, représenté par le Ministre des Colonies, le Trésor Public,
représenté par le Ministre des Finances, et la Banque de l'Indochine.

*(Two columns of fine print follow.)*

*(Left column:)*

ART. 2. — L'annuité nécessaire au service de l'emprunt sera inscrite parmi les dépenses obligatoires de la Colonie et le paiement en est garanti par le Gouvernement de la République Française en vertu de la loi d'autorisation de l'emprunt. Mention en sera imprimée sur les titres.

ART. 3. — Le taux nominal de l'intérêt annuel est fixé à 4 o/o, payable par moitié aux échéances des 30 Avril et 31 Octobre de chaque année.

Les obligations seront placées dans le public à 982 francs, jouissance du 31 Août 1931. Le premier coupon, à l'échéance du 31 Octobre 1931, sera exceptionnellement de 6 fr. 65 c., représentant l'intérêt couru depuis le 31 Août 1931.

Ces obligations seront amortissables en 50 ans, comptés à partir du 30 Avril 1931, soit au pair par tirages au sort semestriels, conformément au tableau d'amortissement qui sera imprimé sur les titres, soit par rachats en Bourse au-dessous du pair, compte tenu de la fraction courue du coupon et à concurrence de l'emploi total de l'annuité prévue pour le service de l'emprunt.

Les titres amortis supplémentairement de ce chef seront imputés sur le ou les derniers tirages.

Les tirages au sort s'effectueront, s'il y a lieu, à Paris, les 25 Mars et 25 Septembre de chaque année et pour la première fois le 20 Octobre 1931.

Au cas où le Gouvernement de la Nouvelle-Calédonie et Dépendances viendrait à émettre de nouvelles obligations assimilables à celles qui font l'objet du présent emprunt, les tirages au sort et les rachats en Bourse pourraient s'effectuer indistinctement sur l'ensemble des titres émis.

Le Gouvernement de la Nouvelle-Calédonie et Dépendances se réserve le droit de procéder à l'amortissement anticipé de tout ou partie des obligations en circulation:

*(Right column:)*

1o Soit, à tout moment, par rachats en Bourse au-dessous du pair, compte tenu de la fraction courue du coupon;

2o Soit, postérieurement au 1er Janvier 1934, par remboursement au pair majoré des intérêts courus, moyennant un préavis d'un mois, des tirages au sort supplémentaires étant effectués dans le cas d'un remboursement partiel.

Les titres amortis par anticipation seront imputés sur le ou les derniers tirages.

Les obligations dont les numéros sortiront au tirage seront remboursables et cesseront de porter intérêt à partir de la première échéance qui suivra le tirage.

Tout titre dont le numéro est sorti au tirage et qui sera présenté au remboursement devra être muni de tous les coupons non échus à la date fixée pour le remboursement. Dans le cas où il en manquerait un ou plusieurs, leur montant sera déduit du capital à payer.

Les dispositions relatives à l'amortissement et au remboursement seront imprimées sur les titres.

ART. 4. — Le paiement des coupons et le remboursement des obligations seront effectués sans aucune déduction, même pour le droit de transmission, respectivement sur la base de 20 francs et de 1.000 francs, tous impôts dont les titres et les coupons sont ou seraient frappés dans le présent ou dans l'avenir, soit en France, soit en Nouvelle-Calédonie et Dépendances, étant à la charge de la Colonie.

ART 6. — Les coupons qui n'auraient pas été présentés à l'encaissement dans un délai de 5 ans à partir de leur échéance seront prescrits; de même seront prescrites les obligations sorties aux tirages qui n'auraient pas été présentées à l'encaissement dans un délai de 30 ans de la date fixée pour leur remboursement.

*(Article numbering jumps from ART. 4 to ART 6 as printed; no ART. 5 appears in this extract.)*

*(Italic line running the full width below the two columns:)*
*Les coupons échus et les titres amortis seront payés et remboursés en France et en Nouvelle-Calédonie, au choix des porteurs.*

(Bottom margin, printer's imprint:) IMPRIMERIE CHAIX, RUE BERGÈRE, 20, PARIS. — 12473-8-31. — (Encre Lorilleux)

(Bottom-right corner:) MODÈLE DÉPO[SÉ]

*(Red NCG circular hand-stamps are struck across several coupons.)*

(Handwritten in pen, top-left corner:) 8679

(Faint printed marks, top-right corner:) 2 · c

(Interlaced monogram at centre top: **NCG**)

**GOVERNMENT**
OF
**NEW CALEDONIA AND DEPENDENCIES**

**4% LOAN  1931**
FREE OF PRESENT AND FUTURE TAXES
*Approved by Decree of 30 July 1931.*
REPRESENTED BY **34,020** BONDS OF **1,000** FRANCS EACH, REDEEMABLE OVER FIFTY YEARS, FORMING PART
OF THE LOAN OF **95** MILLION FRANCS, AUTHORIZED BY THE LAW OF 22 FEBRUARY 1931.

**One Thousand Franc Bearer Bond  No. 008,679**

(left) THE GOVERNOR OF NEW CALEDONIA
AND DEPENDENCIES,
(right) SEEN FOR VERIFICATION,

*(two handwritten signatures)*

PARIS, 31 August 1931.

(Red circular hand-stamp:) GOVERNMENT OF NEW CALEDONIA AND DEPENDENCIES *(with NCG monogram at centre)*

---

**EXTRACTS FROM THE AGREEMENT**
entered into between the Government of New Caledonia and Dependencies, represented by the Minister of the Colonies, the Public Treasury,
represented by the Minister of Finance, and the Bank of Indochina.

*(Two columns of fine print follow.)*

*(Left column:)*

ART. 2. — The annuity required to service the loan shall be entered among the obligatory expenditures of the Colony, and its payment is guaranteed by the Government of the French Republic under the law authorizing the loan. A note to this effect shall be printed on the certificates.

ART. 3. — The nominal annual interest rate is fixed at 4%, payable in halves on the due dates of 30 April and 31 October each year.

The bonds shall be placed with the public at 982 francs, with interest accruing from 31 August 1931. The first coupon, falling due on 31 October 1931, shall exceptionally amount to 6 fr. 65 c., representing the interest accrued since 31 August 1931.

These bonds shall be redeemable over 50 years, counted from 30 April 1931, either at par by semi-annual drawings by lot, in accordance with the amortization table that will be printed on the certificates, or by repurchases on the Stock Exchange below par, account being taken of the accrued fraction of the coupon and up to the full employment of the annuity provided for servicing the loan.

Certificates redeemed additionally on this account shall be charged to the last drawing or drawings.

The drawings by lot shall take place, if occasion arises, at Paris, on 25 March and 25 September each year, and for the first time on 20 October 1931.

Should the Government of New Caledonia and Dependencies come to issue new bonds assimilable to those forming the object of the present loan, the drawings by lot and the Stock Exchange repurchases could be carried out indiscriminately over the whole body of certificates issued.

The Government of New Caledonia and Dependencies reserves the right to proceed with the early redemption of all or part of the bonds in circulation:

*(Right column:)*

1st. Either, at any time, by repurchases on the Stock Exchange below par, account being taken of the accrued fraction of the coupon;

2nd. Or, after 1 January 1934, by repayment at par increased by accrued interest, subject to one month's notice, supplementary drawings by lot being carried out in the case of a partial repayment.

Certificates redeemed in advance shall be charged to the last drawing or drawings.

The bonds whose numbers come out in the drawing shall be redeemable and shall cease to bear interest from the first due date following the drawing.

Any certificate whose number has come out in the drawing and which is presented for repayment must be furnished with all coupons not yet due at the date fixed for repayment. Should one or more be missing, their amount shall be deducted from the capital to be paid.

The provisions concerning amortization and repayment shall be printed on the certificates.

ART. 4. — The payment of the coupons and the repayment of the bonds shall be effected without any deduction, even for the transfer duty, respectively on the basis of 20 francs and of 1,000 francs, all taxes with which the certificates and coupons are or may be charged, now or in the future, whether in France or in New Caledonia and Dependencies, being borne by the Colony.

ART. 6. — Coupons that have not been presented for collection within a period of 5 years from their due date shall be time-barred; likewise, bonds drawn in the lotteries that have not been presented for collection within a period of 30 years from the date fixed for their repayment shall be time-barred.

*(The article numbering jumps from ART. 4 to ART. 6 as printed; no ART. 5 appears in this extract.)*

*(Italic line running the full width below the two columns:)*
*Coupons that have fallen due and redeemed certificates shall be paid and repaid in France and in New Caledonia, at the choice of the bearers.*

title: Republic of Texas Government Bond
description: A government bond of the Republic of Texas, headed "Government Bond, payable to holder. Receivable for all Government Dues," promising the bearer One Hundred Dollars with interest at eight per cent per annum from the first day of January 1840, payable semi-annually at the Treasury Department in gold or silver upon presentation. Dated at the Treasury Department, Houston, and signed by John G. Chalmers as Secretary of the Treasury and David G. Burnet as President; ten semi-annual interest coupons run along the foot. Issued by the young republic in the interval between San Jacinto and annexation, the note is an instrument of frontier sovereign credit, its specie-payable terms and receivability for public dues an effort to lend value to the debt of a cash-poor government. The engraved vignettes—a seated youth amid implements of labour, grazing cattle, and a steamboat—voice the era's faith in industry, husbandry, and river commerce.
creator: Republic of Texas
notes: Republic of Texas Government Bond, $100, 8% semi-annual interest, signed by John F. Chalmers (Secretary); receivable for all government duties, with coupon strips

title: Société des Grands Hôtels Indochinois Action au Porteur de 50 Piastres
description: This is a fully paid-up bearer share (action au porteur, entièrement libérée) of 50 piastres in the Société des Grands Hôtels Indochinois, a société anonyme established by deed received before Maître Mathieu, notary at Saïgon. Its capital of 600,000 piastres was divided into 12,000 such shares, and the certificate was printed in Saïgon by the firm A. Portail. The instrument belongs to the wave of French colonial ventures that, in the early twentieth century, raised capital to build hotels and travel infrastructure across French Indochina. Denomination in piastres rather than francs anchors the enterprise firmly in the colonial monetary economy of the peninsula. The green-and-orange engraved frame, with its dense foliate scrollwork and the interlaced company monogram, follows the conventional decorative idiom of French share certificates of the period, signalling permanence and commercial respectability to the bearer.
creator: Société des Grands Hôtels Indochinois

title: Great Atlantic & Pacific Tea Company stock certificate, Maryland, 1972
description: An engraved certificate for one hundred shares of the common stock of The Great Atlantic & Pacific Tea Company, Inc., each share carrying a par value of one dollar, issued under the laws of the State of Maryland and dated 24 January 1972. The transfer agent named is First National City Bank and the registrar Morgan Guaranty Trust Company of New York; a CUSIP identifier is printed beside the owner line, marking the post-1968 era of standardized securities. By this date A&P, long the dominant American grocery chain, was a publicly traded enterprise whose shares circulated as ordinary investment instruments rather than the closely held stock of its founding decades. The olive-toned border frames a vignette of two winged allegorical female figures attending a medallion bearing the company's monogram, a classicizing motif that lends mercantile commerce an emblem of dignified abundance.

title: Hungarian Fund One-Dollar Bond, New York, 1852
description: A one-dollar bearer bond of the Hungarian Fund, dated at New York on 2 February 1852 and bearing the engraved signature of Lajos Kossuth (L. Kossuth). The text promises that, on demand one year after the establishment in fact of an Independent Hungarian Government, the holder shall receive one dollar at the National Treasury or its agencies at London or New York, or may exchange sums of fifty dollars or over for certificates bearing four per cent interest, repayable in ten equal annual installments. Issued during Kossuth's American tour to fund a renewed bid against Habsburg rule after the failed 1848-49 revolution, the note set its face value low to invite contributions from ordinary sympathizers. Its motifs - a victorious figure standing over a vanquished foe and a helmeted Athena - cast the cause as righteous and embattled. The hoped-for republic never came, leaving the certificate a contingent claim on a state that never existed.
creator: Hungarian Fund (Kossuth exile government)
notes: Hungarian Fund $1 bearer bond. New York, February 2, 1852. Issued during Lajos Kossuth's American fundraising tour for Hungarian independence.

title: Imperial Russian Government 3% State Loan, London, 1859
description: This is an inscribed bearer bond for a capital of one hundred pounds sterling in the Imperial Russian Government's three-per-cent State Loan of 1859, issued by the State Commission for the Redemption of Debts (Государственная Коммиссія Погашенія Долговъ) and entered in the State Debt Book. The holder receives continuous income at three per hundred per year, paid half-yearly in London in two instalments of one pound ten shillings, on the dates 20 October / 1 November and 19 April / 1 May (Old and New Style), through the bankers J. Thomson and T. Bonar. Raised on the London market and denominated in sterling, the loan drew British capital into a Russia that, in the wake of the Crimean War and on the eve of emancipation, was reorganising its finances and funding railway and state expenditure. The engraved frame, crowned by the imperial double-headed eagle, asserts sovereign credit to a foreign creditor.
creator: Imperial Russian Government
notes: Imperial Russian Government 3% State Loan of 1859. £100 bearer bond. No. 31016.

# Certificate of the Russian 3% State Loan of 1859, for a capital of 100 pounds sterling (No. 31016) — Transcription

## Original:

*(Document is entirely in Russian, printed in pre-reform Cyrillic. Faint reversed/offset English text bleeds through from the reverse or an adjacent sheet — it is NOT part of this face and is not transcribed here except where noted.)*

**(Ornamental top header, flanking the Imperial double-headed eagle:)**

**БИЛЕТЪ**

**ГОСУДАРСТВ: КОММИССIИ**

**ПОГАШЕНIЯ ДОЛГОВЪ.**

*(In oval cartouches at left and right of the header:)* **№ 31016** … **№ 31016**

**(Main title block, below the header:)**

**РОССІЙСКАГО ТРЕХПРОЦЕНТНАГО ЗАЙМА 1859 г.**

**НА КАПИТАЛЪ СТО ФУНТ. СТЕРЛ.**

записанный въ Государственную долговую книгу.

Книга *[handwritten: 7]* стр. *[handwritten: 124]*

*(Body paragraph 1:)*

Владѣлецъ сего билета получаетъ непрерывнаго дохода по ТРИ на СТО въ годъ, платимые по полугодно въ Лондонѣ, а именно. одинъ фунтъ стерл. десять шил. на срокъ 20 ОКТЯБРЯ / 1 НОЯБРЯ и одинъ фунтъ стерл. десять шил. на срокъ 19 АПРѢЛЯ / 1 МАЯ, у банкировъ И. Томсонъ, Т. Бонаръ и Кº, или, по желанію, въ Берлинѣ, у банкира Ф. Март. Магнусъ, Прусск. курант. десять талер. три сер. грош. девять пфен. въ тѣ же самые сроки.

*(Body paragraph 2:)*

При семъ билетѣ находятся полугодовые купоны, для полученія процентовъ по 19 АПРѢЛЯ / 1 МАЯ 1869 г., по истеченіи же сего срока будутъ выданы новые купоны, по представленіи принадлежащаго къ сему билету талона.

**Выписка изъ Устава Коммиссіи Погашенія Долговъ (Св. Зак. Т. XI изд. 1857 г.)**

Ст. 89. Безсрочный непрерывный доходъ, равно какъ и платежъ срочныхъ долговъ, производится и въ мирное и въ военное время, безъ всякаго различія, къ дружественной ли или непріязненной націи принадлежитъ вкладчикъ.

— 90. Въ случаѣ смерти иностраннаго вкладчика безъ завѣщанія, безсрочный долгъ имѣетъ поступать къ его наслѣдникамъ, по порядку и правамъ наслѣдія того Государства, къ которому онъ принадлежалъ.

— 91. Капиталы, въ безсрочный долгъ помѣщенные, составляя ненарушимую собственность вкладчика, ни въ какомъ случаѣ не подлежатъ налогамъ.

**Выписка изъ Высочайшаго Указа, даннаго 20 Марта 1859 г. на имя Министра Финансовъ.**

На погашеніе сего займа, съ 1859 г., назначается особый, не смѣшиваемый съ другими займами, фондъ погашенія, который долженъ составлять ежегодно полтора процента съ нарицательнаго капитала займа. Фондъ сей, который будетъ увеличиваться присовокупленіемъ въ послѣдствіи процентовъ, какіе будутъ причитаться на погашенные билеты, имѣетъ быть обращаемъ на выкупъ билетовъ, по биржевому ихъ курсу. Если кто изъ владѣльцевъ билетовъ сего займа пожелаетъ оные переписать на Россійскія деньги, то для сего расчета опредѣляется цѣна шесть сотъ сорокъ рублей за сто фунт. стерл. и билетъ съ тѣмъ вмѣстѣ долженъ быть обращенъ въ именный, безъ купоновъ, а проценты по оному имѣютъ быть выдаваемы въ С. Петербургѣ Коммиссіею Погашенія Долговъ, въ вышеозначенные сроки.

*(Signatures at foot:)*

Управляющій *[handwritten signature]*     Бухгалтеръ *[handwritten signature]*

*(In the ornamental panel at bottom center:)* **1859.**

**(Vertical text in left border panel:)** Капиталъ 100 ф. Ст.

**(Vertical text in right border panel:)** Доходъ 3 ф. Ст.

- Red circular collector/exchange stamp over the title: "BILLETS AU PORTEUR, ACTIONS &c — BRIEFS AAN DRAGER, AANDEELEN, ENZ. — 2000 F.A. 3000 — 3 FR."

## English Translation:

**(Header:)** CERTIFICATE — STATE COMMISSION FOR THE REDEMPTION OF DEBTS. No. 31016 … No. 31016

**OF THE RUSSIAN THREE-PER-CENT LOAN OF 1859, ON A CAPITAL OF ONE HUNDRED POUNDS STERLING**, entered in the State Debt Book.

Book *[7]*, page *[124]*.

The holder of this certificate receives a perpetual income at THREE per HUNDRED per year, payable half-yearly in London, namely: one pound sterling ten shillings for the term of 20 October / 1 November, and one pound sterling ten shillings for the term of 19 April / 1 May, at the bankers J. Thomson, T. Bonar & Co.; or, if desired, in Berlin, at the banker F. Mart. Magnus, [in] Prussian currency, ten thalers three silver groschen nine pfennig, at the same terms.

Attached to this certificate are half-yearly coupons, for the collection of interest through 19 April / 1 May 1869; upon the expiry of that term new coupons will be issued, on presentation of the talon belonging to this certificate.

**Extract from the Statute of the Commission for the Redemption of Debts (Digest of Laws, Vol. XI, 1857 ed.):**

Art. 89. The perpetual continuous income, as well as the payment of term debts, is made both in time of peace and in time of war, without any distinction as to whether the depositor belongs to a friendly or a hostile nation.

— 90. In the event of the death of a foreign depositor without a will, the perpetual debt shall pass to his heirs, according to the order and rights of inheritance of the State to which he belonged.

— 91. Capitals placed in perpetual debt, constituting the inviolable property of the depositor, are in no case subject to taxes.

**Extract from the Imperial Decree, given on 20 March 1859 in the name of the Minister of Finance:**

For the redemption of this loan, from the year 1859, a special sinking fund is established, not to be mixed with other loans, which shall amount annually to one and a half per cent of the nominal capital of the loan. This fund, which will be augmented by the subsequent addition of the interest that would fall due on the redeemed certificates, shall be applied to the buying-back of certificates at their exchange rate. Should any of the holders of certificates of this loan wish to convert them into Russian money, then for this calculation the price is fixed at six hundred forty rubles per one hundred pounds sterling, and the certificate must thereby be converted into a registered [inscribed] one, without coupons, while the interest thereon shall be paid out in St. Petersburg by the Commission for the Redemption of Debts, at the above-mentioned terms.

Manager *[signature]*     Accountant *[signature]*

1859.

title: Romanian Public Debt Internal Consolidation Loan, Lei 5,000
description: A bearer bond of 5,000 lei issued by the Kingdom of Romania under its public debt ("Datoria Publică a României") as part of the Internal Consolidation Loan of 1935 ("Împrumutul Intern de Consolidare 3% 1935"). The instrument bears 3% annual interest — 150 lei a year, payable in two semi-annual installments on 15 May and 15 November — and was emitted by authority of the law published in the Monitorul Oficial No. 156 of 10 July 1934, a Council of Ministers decree of 21 June 1935, and article 19 of the budget law for the 1935/1936 fiscal year, signed by the Minister of Finance. Issued at Bucharest in 1935, it belongs to the wave of domestic debt consolidations by which European states refinanced obligations strained by the slump of the early 1930s. The Romanian eagle spreads above the title and the crowned royal arms anchor the foot, framing the loan in the heraldry of the monarchy that guaranteed it.
creator: Romania. Ministry of Finance
notes: Romanian Public Debt, Internal Consolidation Loan 3% 1935. Lei 5,000 bearer bond. No. 242914. With coupon sheet.

title: Nippon Kangyō Bank (Hypothec Bank of Japan) Premium Savings Bond, 50 yen, Tokyo, 1938
description: This printed premium savings bond (割増金附貯蓄債券, warimashikin-tsuki chochiku saiken) carries a face value of fifty yen (金五拾圓) and forms the 88th issue (第捌拾八回) of the Nippon Kangyō Ginkō (日本勸業銀行), the government-linked Hypothec Bank of Japan, dated Shōwa 13, November 1938 (昭和十三年十一月). It was sold to the public at a discounted price of ten yen (割引賣出價格金拾圓), the gap between issue price and redemption value standing in for interest, while an attached prize-drawing (割増金) offered lottery-style bonuses to holders. Issued as Japan's economy was being mobilised for war in China, the instrument exemplifies the state's drive to absorb household cash into public finance, dressing compulsory thrift in the appeal of a lottery. The dense red border of stylised scrollwork and the small vignette of figures at lower right lend the certificate an air of official solidity.
creator: Japan Industrial Bank (日本勧業銀行, Nippon Kangyo Ginko)

title: Nippon Kangyō Bank Premium Wartime Patriotic Bond, Japan, August 1942
description: A printed premium-bearing wartime patriotic bond (割増金附戦時報國債券, warimashikin-tsuki senji hōkoku saiken) with a face value of five yen (金五圓), issued in its fourth series (第四回) and dated Shōwa 17, August (昭和十七年八月), that is August 1942. The issuer named at left is the Nippon Kangyō Ginkō (株式會社日本勸業銀行), the semi-official mortgage bank through which the government placed small-denomination war loans rather than the central treasury directly. The 'premium' (割増金) terms printed at right attach a lottery to the instrument: a detachable coupon (附票) at the foot entered the bearer in periodic prize drawings, sweetening an otherwise modest return and widening the bond's appeal among wage-earners and small savers. The whole sheet militarizes saving: a long-range bomber crosses the upper field, a battleship and the rising-sun naval ensign flank the central oval, and a red seal reading 大東亞戰爭 ('Greater East Asia War') with Mount Fuji binds the purchase to imperial purpose. Palm fronds at left allude to the newly seized southern theatre. The object captures how the Pacific War financed itself by recasting thrift as patriotic duty and folding chance into civic obligation.

title: Japanese Wartime Patriotic Bond (Nippon Kangyō Bank), 10 Yen, 1942
description: A printed Japanese wartime patriotic bond (戦時報国債券, Senji Hōkoku Saiken) with a premium feature (割増金附), of ten yen face value (金拾圓), issued in the fourth series in the eighth month of Shōwa 17 (August 1942). Though the instrument funded the imperial treasury under the authority of the Ministry of Finance (大蔵省), as the legend on the right panel states, it was placed through the Nippon Kangyō Bank (日本勧業銀行), whose name and seal appear at left; the bond was repaid with a premium awarded by periodic lottery drawing, the detachable coupon (附票) below carrying the matching number for that purpose. Sold during the Greater East Asia War (大東亜戦争), named on the central seal, it formed part of a tiered savings campaign meant to absorb household income across all classes. The engraved imagery is martial and expansionist: a bomber crosses the upper field, a warship rides the sea below, while tropical palms and a rising sun over Mount Fuji evoke the southward conquests the loan was meant to sustain.

title: Kahetian Railway Company Bond
description: This printed bearer bond (облигація) of the Kahetian Railway Company (Общество Кахетинской желѣзной дороги) belongs to a 4½% loan of 1912, struck on a nominal capital of 13,230,000 rubles, or 1,400,000 pounds sterling. Each certificate carries a face value of 189 rubles, fixed at 20 pounds sterling, and runs in parallel Russian and English text. Issued at St. Petersburg in 1912 with the express sanction of the Imperial Russian Government, the loan financed a railway through the Kakheti wine country of eastern Georgia, joining Tiflis to its vineyards. The twin denomination and bilingual face reveal an instrument courting London capital while resting on the security of a sovereign endorsement. The sober typographic frame, the imperial double eagle, and the embossed red seal lend the paper the gravity of a state-backed obligation rather than a speculative venture.
creator: Kahetian Railway Company

title: Imperial Russian Government 4% State Loan Bond, 1902
description: This printed bearer bond (облигація) of 1,000 Imperial German marks, bearing 4 percent annual interest, belongs to the Imperial Russian Government's 4% State Loan of 1902. The face equates the marks to 463 rubles 50 kopecks, 590 Dutch guilders, and 49 pounds sterling, registering the holder in the State Debt Book and signalling a security pitched simultaneously at German, Dutch, and British savers. The loan, authorised by the Highest Ukase of 1 March 1902, was raised expressly to realise the indemnity owed to Russia by China following the events of 1900 to 1901, converting a diplomatic claim into tradable European paper. The nominal capital reached 181,950,000 rubles, or 393,000,000 marks. The double-headed Romanov eagle and dense guilloche borders assert imperial creditworthiness; the multilingual denominations reveal how heavily Tsarist finance leaned on foreign capital markets before 1914.
creator: Imperial Russian Government
notes: Imperial Russian Government 4% State Loan 1902. 1,000 German marks. No. 116485.
