на нарицательный капиталъ въ **181.950.000 рублей**

= 393.000.000 имп. герм. марокъ = 231.870.000 голл. гульд. = 19.257.000 фунт. стерл.

(строки о количествѣ листовъ, частью подъ штампами:) въ количествѣ 22000 листовъ по 5000 имп. герм. мар. съ № 000001 по № 022000, 75000 листовъ по 2000 имп. герм. мар. съ № 022001 по № 097000, 110000 листовъ по 1000 имп. герм. мар. съ № 097001 по № 207000 и 46000 листовъ по 500 имп. герм. мар. съ № 207001 по № 253000.

**ОБЛИГАЦІЯ** № 116485

**ВЪ ТЫСЯЧУ ИМПЕРСКИХЪ ГЕРМАНСКИХЪ МАРОКЪ**

463 руб. = 590 голл. гульд. = 49 фунт. стерл.,

вписанная въ Государственную Долговую Книгу, кн. _____ стр. _____

**НА ПРЕДЪЯВИТЕЛЯ.**

(Основной текстъ:)

На основаніи ВЫСОЧАЙШАГО Указа 1 Марта 1902 года для реализаціи причитающагося Россіи вознагражденія въ возмѣщеніе убытковъ, понесенныхъ вслѣдствіе смутъ въ Китаѣ, выпущенъ настоящій 4% Государственный заемъ на нарицательный капиталъ въ 181.950.000 рублей, равныхъ 393.000.000 германскихъ марокъ, равныхъ 231.870.000 голландскихъ гульденовъ, равныхъ 19.257.000 фунтовъ стерлинговъ, ежегодные платежи по коему находились бы въ точномъ соотвѣтствіи съ причитающимися отъ Китайскаго Правительства ежегодными уплатами процентовъ и погашеній по слѣдуемой Россіи части вознагражденія, которое Китай обязался выплатить согласно эдикта Китайскаго Императора отъ 29 Мая новаго стиля 1901 года.

Заемъ сей внесенъ въ Государственную Долговую Книгу подъ наименованіемъ: «Россійскій 4% Государственный заемъ 1902 г.», выпущенный для реализаціи причитающагося Россіи отъ Китая вознагражденія. Облигаціи сего займа выпускаются именныя и на предъявителя, достоинствомъ въ 500, 1000, 2000 и 5000 имп. герм. марокъ, считая каждая 1000 имп. герм. марокъ = 463 рублямъ = 590 голл. гульд. = 49 фунт. стерл. Владѣлецъ сей облигаціи имѣетъ право на сумму въ ТЫСЯЧУ ИМПЕРСКИХЪ ГЕРМАНСКИХЪ МАРОКЪ = 463 руб., приносящую ЧЕТЫРЕ процента въ годъ дохода до погашенія оной тиражемъ или выкупомъ; теченіе процентовъ начинается съ 19 Декабря 1901 г./1 Января 1902 г.

Облигаціи сіи освобождены навсегда отъ всякихъ русскихъ налоговъ.

Проценты уплачиваются два раза въ годъ: 18 Іюня/1 Іюля и 19 Декабря/1 Января, по предъявленіи купоновъ, по желанію владѣльца:

(два столбца платежныхъ мѣстъ:)

въ РОССІИ — въ Конторахъ и Отдѣленіяхъ Государственнаго Банка — рублями;

въ ГЕРМАНІИ — имперскими германскими марками —
въ Берлинѣ: у гг. Мендельсонъ и К°,
„  „  у г. С. Блейхредеръ,
„  „  въ Дирекціи Учетнаго Общества,
„  „  въ Берлинскомъ Торговомъ Обществѣ,
„  „  у г. Роберта Варшауеръ и К°,
во Франкфуртѣ н/М., въ Дирекціи Учетн. Общества;

въ ГОЛЛАНДІИ — голландскими гульденами — у гг. Липпманъ, Розенталь и К° въ Амстердамѣ;

въ АНГЛІИ — фунтами стерлинговъ — въ Агентствѣ Русскаго для внѣшней торговли Банка въ Лондонѣ.

Облигаціи сего займа погашаются по нарицательной цѣнѣ въ теченіе 39 лѣтъ ежегодными тиражами по плану, на оборотѣ напечатанному. Тиражи будутъ производиться въ Сентябрѣ каждаго года, начиная съ 1902 г. Капиталъ по вышедшимъ въ тиражъ облигаціямъ уплачивается въ слѣдующій за тиражемъ срокъ уплаты процентовъ.

До 19 Декабря 1914/1 Января 1915 года отчисленіе на погашеніе займа не будетъ увеличиваемо сверхъ предусмотрѣннаго планомъ погашенія суммы, а равно до того же срока не будетъ приступлено ни къ выкупу, ни къ конверсіи настоящаго займа.

Уплата капитала по облигаціямъ производится въ тѣхъ же городахъ и въ тѣхъ же валютахъ, какъ и уплата купоновъ, по предъявленіи самой облигаціи съ талономъ и всѣми купонами, срокъ коихъ истечетъ послѣ срока уплаты капитала; сумма недостающихъ купоновъ будетъ удержана при выдачѣ капитала по выкупаемымъ облигаціямъ.

Облигаціи сего займа сохраняютъ платежную силу въ теченіе 30 лѣтъ отъ сроковъ, назначенныхъ для ихъ оплаты, а купоны сего займа — въ теченіе 10 лѣтъ отъ срока ихъ уплаты.

Каждая облигація сего займа снабжена купонами и талономъ для полученія новыхъ купоновъ по истеченіи старыхъ. Облигаціи сего займа выпускаются именныя и на предъявителя. Правила относительно именныхъ облигацій, ихъ обмѣна изъ одного вида въ другую, ихъ обмѣна на предъявительскія и обратно утверждаются Министромъ Финансовъ.

Облигаціи сего займа будутъ приниматься по цѣнамъ, устанавливаемымъ на каждое полугодіе Министромъ Финансовъ, въ публичныхъ торгахъ Правительственныхъ Палатъ, въ залогъ: а) по казеннымъ подрядамъ и поставкамъ, б) по разсрочиваемому акцизу и в) въ обезпеченіе таможенныхъ пошлинъ. Указанныя цѣны будутъ не ниже 90% нарицательной цѣны облигацій.

**Управляющій Государственною Коммиссіею Погашенія Долговъ** [подпись / signature]

**Бухгалтеръ** [подпись / signature]

(на документѣ имѣется нѣсколько красныхъ и фіолетовыхъ печатей; въ верхнихъ углахъ — двуглавый орелъ и знакъ; часть текста перекрыта штампами)

## English Translation:

**IMPERIAL RUSSIAN GOVERNMENT.**

**RUSSIAN**

**4% STATE LOAN OF 1902**

issued to realise the indemnity due to Russia from China, [word hidden under a red stamp / illegible]
**by the Supreme Ukase of 1 March 1902.**

on a nominal capital of **181,950,000 rubles**

= 393,000,000 Imperial German marks = 231,870,000 Dutch guilders = 19,257,000 pounds sterling

(lines on the number of sheets, partly under stamps:) in the quantity of 22,000 sheets of 5,000 Imperial German marks, Nos. 000001 to 022000; 75,000 sheets of 2,000 Imperial German marks, Nos. 022001 to 097000; 110,000 sheets of 1,000 Imperial German marks, Nos. 097001 to 207000; and 46,000 sheets of 500 Imperial German marks, Nos. 207001 to 253000.

**BOND** No. 116485

**FOR ONE THOUSAND IMPERIAL GERMAN MARKS**

463 rubles = 590 Dutch guilders = 49 pounds sterling,

entered in the State Debt Book, vol. _____ page _____

**TO THE BEARER.**

(Main text:)

By virtue of the Supreme Ukase of 1 March 1902, for the realisation of the indemnity due to Russia in compensation for the losses sustained on account of the disturbances in China, the present 4% State Loan is issued on a nominal capital of 181,950,000 rubles, equal to 393,000,000 German marks, equal to 231,870,000 Dutch guilders, equal to 19,257,000 pounds sterling, the annual payments upon which would correspond exactly to the annual payments of interest and amortisation due from the Chinese Government on the portion of the indemnity owed to Russia, which China undertook to pay in accordance with the edict of the Chinese Emperor of 29 May, New Style, 1901.

This loan is entered in the State Debt Book under the designation: "Russian 4% State Loan of 1902," issued for the realisation of the indemnity due to Russia from China. The bonds of this loan are issued as registered and to bearer, in denominations of 500, 1,000, 2,000, and 5,000 Imperial German marks, reckoning each 1,000 Imperial German marks = 463 rubles = 590 Dutch guilders = 49 pounds sterling. The holder of this bond is entitled to the sum of ONE THOUSAND IMPERIAL GERMAN MARKS = 463 rubles, yielding FOUR percent per annum in income until its redemption by drawing or repurchase; the running of interest begins on 19 December 1901 / 1 January 1902.

These bonds are exempt forever from all Russian taxes.

Interest is paid twice a year: 18 June / 1 July and 19 December / 1 January, on presentation of the coupons, at the holder's option:

(two columns of places of payment:)

in RUSSIA — at the Offices and Branches of the State Bank — in rubles;

in GERMANY — in Imperial German marks —
in Berlin: at Messrs. Mendelssohn & Co.,
"  "  at Mr. S. Bleichröder,
"  "  at the Direction of the Discount Company,
"  "  at the Berlin Trade Company,
"  "  at Mr. Robert Warschauer & Co.,
in Frankfurt am Main, at the Direction of the Discount Company;

in HOLLAND — in Dutch guilders — at Messrs. Lippmann, Rosenthal & Co. in Amsterdam;

in ENGLAND — in pounds sterling — at the Agency of the Russian Bank for Foreign Trade in London.

The bonds of this loan are redeemed at par over the course of 39 years by annual drawings according to the plan printed on the reverse. The drawings will be conducted in September of each year, beginning in 1902. The capital of bonds drawn is paid on the interest-payment date following the drawing.

Until 19 December 1914 / 1 January 1915 the amount set aside for the amortisation of the loan shall not be increased beyond the sum provided by the amortisation plan, and likewise until the same date no recourse shall be had either to repurchase or to conversion of the present loan.

Payment of the capital of the bonds is made in the same cities and in the same currencies as the payment of the coupons, upon presentation of the bond itself with its talon and all coupons whose maturity falls after the date of payment of the capital; the amount of any missing coupons will be deducted upon payment of the capital on the bonds being redeemed.

The bonds of this loan retain their payment force for 30 years from the dates fixed for their payment, and the coupons of this loan for 10 years from the date of their payment.

Each bond of this loan is furnished with coupons and a talon for obtaining new coupons upon the expiry of the old ones. The bonds of this loan are issued as registered and to bearer. The rules regarding registered bonds, their exchange from one type into another, their exchange for bearer bonds and back, are established by the Minister of Finance.

The bonds of this loan will be accepted, at prices fixed for each half-year by the Minister of Finance, at public tenders of the Government Chambers, as security: a) for treasury contracts and supplies, b) for excise payable by instalments, and c) as guarantee of customs duties. The said prices shall not be below 90% of the nominal value of the bonds.

**Manager of the State Commission for the Redemption of Debts** [signature]

**Accountant** [signature]

(the document bears several red and violet seals; the double-headed eagle and an emblem appear in the upper corners; part of the text is overlaid by stamps)

title: Imperial Russian Government 4½% State Loan Bond, 1905
description: This printed bearer bond (облигація) belongs to the Imperial Russian Government's 4½% Russian State Loan of 1905, authorised by the Imperial Ukase of 15 December 1904. Its face value of 500 imperial German marks is restated, in the manner typical of a loan placed simultaneously across several European exchanges, as 231 roubles 50 kopecks, 294 Dutch guilders, and 24 pounds 7 shillings 6 pence sterling; the whole issue totalled 231,500,000 roubles. Payable to bearer and entered in the State Debt Book, it was floated chiefly on German capital while the autocracy was financing the Russo-Japanese War and confronting the upheavals of 1905. The 4½% coupon, above the rate of earlier prewar issues, registers the heightened risk lenders attached to Russian credit. The imperial double-headed eagle and dense engine-turned guilloche borders project sovereign solidity and guard against forgery.
creator: Imperial Russian Government
notes: Imperial Russian Government 4½% State Loan 1905. 500 German marks. No. 319459.

title: Kettingverklaring for a De Woning Maatschappij Batavia Share, Amsterdam, 1941
description: This printed Dutch form is a kettingverklaring (chain declaration) for the trading of foreign securities in regular exchange dealings, registering a 100-guilder (f 100,-) share in De Woning Maatschappij Batavia, a housing company in the Netherlands East Indies. It was issued at Amsterdam and dated 24 January 1941, certified by the Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel (Association for Securities Trading) and requested by P. Boeken Jr., a member of that Amsterdam body; the alternative Rotterdam and 's-Gravenhage memberships are struck through. The form invokes the decree of the Secretary-General of the Department of Finance of 18 September 1940 (Verordening No. 155/1940) and article 10 of the Deviezenverordening 1940, the foreign-exchange ordinance imposed under German occupation. The document attests that the security was registered and so could pass between residents without separate declaration. Its bureaucratic restraint records how wartime currency controls bound even colonial shares into an unbroken, certified chain of ownership.
creator: De Woning Maatschappij Batavia (shareholder / Dutch notary)
notes: Dutch Kettingverklaring (chain declaration) No. 1499 for De Woning Maatschappij Batavia shares. January 24, 1941. Related to collection item No. 355.

Van dit effect behoeft geen aangifte als bedoeld in art. 2 lid 1 van vorengenoemd besluit te worden gedaan, indien het effect tusschen ingezotenen in regelmatig beursverkeer wordt verkregen.

Onder verkrijging in regelmatig beursverkeer wordt in dit verband verstaan verkrijging overeenkomstig de Beursvoorschriften 1940 en de reglementen van de Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel te Amsterdam, van de Vereeniging van Effectenhandelaren te Rotterdam en van den Bond voor den Geld- en Effectenhandel in de Provincie te 's-Gravenhage, voorzover de verkrijging geschiedt door of door tusschenkomst van leden van de genoemde vereenigingen.

title: Royal Dutch Petroleum Company Share-Subscription Warrant, The Hague, 1937
description: This printed warrant (inschrijvingsrecht) is a transferable subscription right issued by the Naamlooze Vennootschap Koninklijke Nederlandsche Maatschappij tot Exploitatie van Petroleumbronnen in Nederlandsch-Indië — the Royal Dutch Petroleum Company — at 's-Gravenhage (The Hague), dated February 1937. Against surrender of the warrant, the bearer may purchase one new share of F. 1,000 nominal for F. 4,500 up to 31 March 1940, or for F. 5,000 from 1 April 1940 until the right expires on 31 March 1943. The escalating exercise price rewards early conversion and prices the option of waiting. Issued by a firm chartered in 1890 and by then the Dutch partner in the Royal Dutch/Shell combination, the instrument documents a rights offering through which the oil concern tapped existing holders for fresh capital. The green guilloché frame, the company seal, and the crowned Netherlands arms assert chartered respectability rather than any decorative programme.
creator: Koninklijke Nederlandsche Maatschappij tot Exploitatie van Petroleumbronnen in Nederlandsch-Indië (Royal Dutch Petroleum)
notes: Royal Dutch Petroleum Company (Koninklijke Nederlandsche Maatschappij) stock purchase warrant. No. 015281. The Hague, February 1937. Subscription price F. 4,500 (until March 31, 1940) or F. 5,000 (until March 31, 1943).

title: Austrian Third War Loan Bond
description: A printed bearer bond (Schuldverschreibung) for ten thousand kronen (Zehntausend Kronen) bearing 5½ percent annual tax-free interest, issued by the Imperial-Royal treasury at Vienna and dated 1 October 1915. It belongs to the Third Austrian War Loan (Dritte fünfeinhalbprozentige österreichische Kriegsanleihe), one of the successive loans floated by Austria-Hungary to fund its part in the European war. By this third subscription the conflict was proving costlier than foreseen, and the 10,000-krone face value placed the note among the largest denominations, aimed at banks and men of means rather than ordinary savers. Coupons fell due each 1 January and 1 July, with repayment promised from 1930. The double-headed Habsburg eagle crowns the sheet, an emblem of imperial credit, while a stylized allegorical female head set within ornamental framing at the foot reflects the decorative Viennese idiom of the period.
creator: Austro-Hungarian Government
notes: Austrian Third War Loan (Dritte Österreichische Kriegsanleihe), 5½% amortisable. 10,000 kronen, Serie E, No. 100356. Vienna, October 1, 1915.

# Austrian Third War Loan Bond - Transcription

**Original (German):**

10.000                                                                                                    10.000

SERIE E                                                                                        № 100.356

DRITTE FÜNFEINHALBROZENTIGE
ÖSTERREICHISCHE

KRIEGSANLEIHE
ZEHNTAUSEND KRONEN

Die k. k. Staatsschulden-Kasse in Wien zahlt an den Inhaber dieser auf Grund der kaiserlichen Verordnung vom 4. August 1914 R. G. Bl. Nr. 202 ausgegebenen Staatsschuldverschreibung am 1. Oktober 1930 den Betrag von Zehntausend Kronen. Diese Staatsschuldverschreibung wird mit jährlich fünfeinhalb Prozent verzinst; die Zahlung der Zinsen erfolgt ohne jeden sonstigen Gebühren- oder sonstigen Abzug am 1. Jänner und 1. Juli jeden Jahres nachmitten an den Inhaber der zu dieser Staatsschuldverschreibung gehörigen Coupons bei der k. k. Staatsschulden-Kasse in Wien. Die Staatsverwaltung behält sich das Recht vor, diese Staatsschuldverschreibung gegen [unclear] zu verlautbarten Kündigungsfrist auch vor dem 1. Oktober 1930 zurückzuzahlen. Im Falle unter die Verzinsung mit dem früheren Rückzahlungstermme. Der Anspruch aus dieser Staatsschuldverschreibung erlischt durch Verjährung in Ansehung des Kapitals binnen dreißig Jahren, in Ansehung der Zinsen binnen fünf Jahren von den Fälligkeitstermine. In diese Staatsschuldverschreibung ist mit 30 Coupons versehen, von denen der letzte am 1. Oktober 1930 fällig ist. Der letzte Coupon ist ein vierteljähriger, die ihrigen sind halbjährige; die am 1. Jänner 1916 fälligen Zinsen werden im Verzugswege beglichen.

Wien, am 1. Oktober 1915.                                Der k. k. Finanzminister:

The Imperial and Royal State Debt Office in Vienna pays to the bearer of this State Debt Certificate issued by virtue of the Imperial Ordinance of August 4, 1914, R. G. Bl. No. 202, on October 1, 1930, the sum of Ten Thousand Kronen. This State Debt Certificate bears annual interest of five and a half percent; the payment of interest is made without any deductions for fees or otherwise on January 1 and July 1 of each year to the bearer of the coupons belonging to this State Debt Certificate at the Imperial and Royal State Debt Office in Vienna. The State Administration reserves the right to repay this State Debt Certificate against [unclear] notice also before October 1, 1930. In the event of [unclear] the interest rate [unclear] with the earlier repayment deadline. The claim arising from this State Debt Certificate lapses through expiration of time in respect of the capital within thirty years, in respect of interest within five years from the due date. This State Debt Certificate is furnished with 30 coupons, of which the last falls due on October 1, 1930. The last coupon is quarterly; the others are semi-annual; the interest due on January 1, 1916, will be settled through the ordinary channels.

Vienna, October 1, 1915.                                The Imperial and Royal Finance Minister:

This debt certificate is entered in the principal ledger of the state debt.

For the State Debt Control Commission of the Imperial Council:

title: Russian-American Fur Company Share
description: This printed bearer share certificate (action de capital au porteur) represents one hundred francs of the capital of La Pelleterie Russo-Américaine, a société anonyme seated at 1 Place des Victoires in Paris and incorporated under French law before the notary Maître Dufour. The total capital of five million francs is divided into 4,000 actions de jouissance and 46,000 actions de capital, of which this is one. It is dated Paris, 31 July 1926. The firm, trading in furs drawn from Russia and North America, sat at the meeting point of the Soviet fur trade — which, under the New Economic Policy, exported pelts to earn hard currency — and the Western luxury market. The engraving is restrained and typographic: a rising-sun medallion bearing the company's seal anchors a finely guilloched border, the sober house style of inter-war Parisian share design rather than any pictorial flourish.

title: La Platense Flotilla Company Share Warrant (Glasgow, 1886)
description: This printed share warrant to bearer certifies its holder as proprietor of one fully paid share of £10 in La Platense Flotilla Company, Limited, a concern capitalised at £1,000,000 divided into 100,000 such shares. The text runs in parallel English and French, and the warrant is given under the company's common seal at Glasgow, dated 20 March 1886, with a three-shilling impressed duty stamp cancelled by a Scottish revenue postmark of 25 March 1886. The firm was floated to work a steam flotilla on the Río de la Plata and its tributaries, the commercial artery draining the pastoral hinterlands of Argentina and Uruguay toward Buenos Aires, an enterprise typical of the British capital then financing River Plate shipping. The engraved cartouche of a scallop shell flanked by dolphins above a river landscape announces the maritime venture in restrained neoclassical ornament.
creator: La Platense Flotilla Company Limited
notes: La Platense Flotilla Company Limited. Share warrant to bearer, £10. No. 15821. One of 100,000 shares. River flotilla, Río de la Plata. Ca. early 1900s.

title: Little Miami Rail Road Company Share Certificate
description: This engraved certificate records ownership of ten shares, of fifty dollars each, in the capital stock of the Little Miami Rail Road Company, chartered under the State of Ohio and transferable only on the company's own books upon surrender of the instrument. It was issued at Cincinnati on June 17th, 1862, in the name of James G. King's Sons, the New York banking house here acting as Trustees. Incorporated in 1836 to link Cincinnati with the interior toward Springfield, the Little Miami was among Ohio's pioneering railroads, and by the early 1860s its stock had become a stable, dividend-bearing holding suited to fiduciary investors. The vignette of a passenger carriage drawn by a smoking locomotive announces the enterprise it funds, while the lower scene of a farmer ploughing behind horses ties the railroad to the agricultural country it was built to serve.
creator: Little Miami Rail Road Company
notes: Little Miami Rail Road Company, Ohio. 10 shares at $50 each. No. 4644. Issued to James G. King's Sons, Trustees. Cincinnati, June 17, 1862.

title: Republic of China Lung-Tsing-U-Hai Railway Treasury Bill
description: This printed bearer treasury bill (schatkistbiljet aan toonder), face value F. 1,000 in Dutch currency at 8 percent interest, belongs to the 8% Lung-Tsing-U-Hai Railway (隴秦豫海鐵路) Treasury Bill loan of 1923 floated by the Government of the Chinese Republic. The total issue of F. 16,667,000 was divided into 16,667 bills of F. 1,000 each, the round figure betraying its origin as a fixed sterling-or-franc equivalent rendered into guilders for the Netherlands market. The line, conceived to run from Gansu in the northwest across Shaanxi and Henan to the Jiangsu coast, was financed piecemeal through separate European tranches; warlord rivalry and recurrent civil conflict left it only partly built and its service uncertain. A bridge spanning a mountain river crowns the certificate, while bilingual Dutch and Chinese text and vermilion seals mark the meeting of two fiscal worlds, the bond a token of European capital lent against China's still-unrealized railway future.
creator: Government of the Republic of China
notes: 8% Lung-Tsing-U-Hai Railway Treasury Bill Loan 1923. F. 1,000 Dutch guilders. No. 11638. Total issue F. 16,667,000. Issued by Republic of China.

title: Merchants Insurance Company Stock Transfer, Providence, 1858
description: This printed and hand-completed instrument records the transfer of twelve shares of the capital stock of the Merchants Insurance Company, executed "for value received" by the assigning shareholder in favor of James S. Ham and attested by a witness. The decorative left border fixes the par value at fifty dollars per share, placing the holding's nominal worth at six hundred dollars. Dated at Providence, April 1, 1858, it issues from one of the marine and fire insurers serving the maritime trade and textile mills of antebellum Rhode Island. The form's very existence speaks to an active secondary market in insurance shares, where such equities passed readily from hand to hand as a store and conduit of mercantile wealth. The restrained engraved heading and ornamental vertical panel reflect the plain, type-driven commercial printing typical of New England financial stationery of the period.
creator: Merchants Insurance Company
notes: Merchants Insurance Company (Providence, Rhode Island). Transfer of 12 shares to James S. Ham. No. 178. April 1, 1858.

title: The Mexican Telephone Company Share Certificate, New York City, 1905
description: This engraved certificate records ownership of twenty-five shares of capital stock in The Mexican Telephone Company, each share carrying a par value of ten dollars. The company was organized under the laws of the State of New York with an authorized capital of $2,000,000 divided into 200,000 shares. Issued at New York City and dated February 28, 1905, the certificate names Harry E. Jacobs of Cambridge as holder and required countersignature by the Boston Safe Deposit & Trust Company as transfer agent, binding the New York charter to Boston capital. The legend "Issued for Property Purchased" marks these shares as consideration paid in kind rather than cash, a common device for capitalizing assets contributed to a venture. The enterprise belonged to the wave of foreign-financed telephone concerns extending lines through Mexico during the long presidency of Porfirio Díaz, when overseas capital was courted to build the country's communications. The plain bank-note-style border and bold roman titling favor legibility and the assurance of a financial instrument over ornament.
creator: Mexican Telephone Company
notes: Mexican Telephone Company. 25 shares at $10 each. No. 16621. Issued to Harry E. Jacobs. Countersigned by Boston Safe Deposit & Trust Co., February 28, 1905. CANCELLED.

title: Minas Geraes Goldfields Limited Share Warrant, London, 1912
description: This printed share warrant to bearer certifies that its holder is the registered proprietor of twenty-five fully paid shares of £1 each in Minas Geraes Goldfields Limited, a company incorporated under the Companies Acts 1862 to 1900 with a capital of £150,000. It was given under the common seal of the company at London in July 1912, and it bears an impressed seven-shilling stamp duty surmounted by a crown. The warrant was issued to finance British exploitation of gold-mining ground in the Brazilian state of Minas Gerais (spelled "Minas Geraes" in the period), a region celebrated since its eighteenth-century gold rush for mineral wealth. As a bearer instrument, it allowed shares to pass by simple delivery, easing trade on the London market where Edwardian and Georgian investors funded numerous overseas mining ventures. The bold scarlet border, laurel sprays, and twinned heraldic shields lend the document a confident, prosperous air.

title: Mines d'Or de Nam Kok Share
description: This is a fully paid-up bearer share (action de cent francs au porteur, entièrement libérée) in the Société Anonyme des Mines d'Or de Nam Kok, a Paris-domiciled gold-mining company capitalised at 30,000,000 francs, its statutes deposited with Maître Ader, notary in Paris. The certificate is denominated at 100 francs and entitles the holder to a full sheet of dividend coupons printed below. The company's name points to gold workings in French colonial Asia, financed through the Paris bourse, where syndicates floated extractive ventures on distant terrain to draw small savers into speculative mining capital. The engraved frame, cloud-filled vignette and dense guilloche watermark spelling the company name are the conventional security devices of the period's French share certificates, lending a borrowed solidity to an inherently uncertain promise. A stamp-duty notice records the issue's authorisation in the Journal Officiel of 6 November 1929.
creator: Mines d'Or de Nam Kok
notes: Mines d'Or de Nam Kok. Action au porteur, 100 francs. Capital 30,000,000 francs. No. 059,820. Paris. Ca. early 1900s. Gold mining in Indochina or southern China.

title: Moscow-Kazan Railway Company Bond
description: This printed bearer bond (облигація) belongs to the 4% bond loan of 1901 of the Moscow-Kazan Railway Company (Общества Московско-Казанской Желѣзной Дороги), issued under the perpetual guarantee of the Imperial Russian Government. Its face value of 2,000 German imperial marks is restated in the principal markets of issue—925 rubles 80 kopecks (at 1 ruble = 2.16 marks), 1,180 Dutch guilders, and 97 pounds 18 shillings—against a total loan capital of 35,880,000 marks. Printed at Moscow in 1901 in parallel Russian, German, and Dutch columns, the document was placed simultaneously in the German and Netherlands capital markets, where French and German savers had become the chief financiers of Russian railway expansion. The sovereign guarantee, foregrounded in the text, converts a provincial trunk line to Kazan and the Volga into a near-government security; the multilingual frame and quiet engraved borders address a cautious rentier abroad rather than a domestic speculator.
creator: Moscow-Kazan Railway Company
notes: Moscow-Kazan Railway Company 4% Bond Loan of 1901. 2,000 German marks. No. 06181. Trilingual Russian/German/Dutch. Imperial Russian Government guarantee.
transcription:
# Moscow-Kazan Railway Company — 4% Bond of the 1901 Loan (2,000 German Marks, Bearer) — Transcription

## Original (Russian):

(Corner denomination numerals in all four corners: **2000**)

(Top-left stamp box:) Stempel der Kaiserlich Russischen Regierung
(Top-right stamp box:) Stempelabgabe bezahlt

**4% ОБЛИГАЦІОННЫЙ ЗАЕМЪ 1901 года**

**ОБЩЕСТВА МОСКОВСКО-КАЗАНСКОЙ ЖЕЛѢЗНОЙ ДОРОГИ**

на нарицательный капиталъ въ 35.880.000 марокъ германской имперской валюты =16.608.852 руб. (1 руб. = ¹⁄₁₅ империала) =21.169.200 голл. гульд. =1.756.326 фунт. стерл., съ безусловною гарантіею ИМПЕРАТОРСКАГО Россійскаго Правительства въ уплатѣ процентовъ и погашеніи.

**ОБЛИГАЦІЯ**

**№06181**

**ВЪ ДВѢ ТЫСЯЧИ МАРОКЪ ГЕРМАНСКОЙ ИМПЕРСКОЙ ВАЛЮТЫ**

=925 руб. 80 коп. (1 руб. = ¹⁄₁₅ империала) =1180 голл. гульд. =97 фунт. стерл. 18 шилл.

**НА ПРЕДЪЯВИТЕЛЯ.**

Владѣлецъ сей облигаціи участвуетъ ДВУМЯ ТЫСЯЧАМИ марокъ германской имперской валюты въ займѣ, заключенномъ въ размѣрѣ 35.880.000 марокъ германской имперской валюты на условіяхъ, на оборотѣ сего означенныхъ, и обезпечивается всѣмъ имуществомъ и всѣми доходами Общества и имѣющимъ преимущество предъ всѣми будущими займами.

Независимо отъ сего облигаціи сего займа даруется безусловная гарантія ИМПЕРАТОРСКАГО Россійскаго Правительства въ уплатѣ процентовъ и погашеніи со дня ихъ выпуска. Платежи процентовъ по купонамъ и погашеніе по облигаціямъ навсегда освобождаются отъ всякихъ русскихъ налоговъ.

Проценты изъ 4% годовыхъ выплачиваются по предъявленіи купоновъ ежегодно въ сроки 18 Іюня/1 Іюля и 19 Декабря/1 Января. Москва, 1901 г.

Правленіе Общества Московско-Казанской желѣзной дороги:

(three engraved signatures — [illegible])

Главный бухгалтеръ (signature — [illegible])

Уполномоченный отъ Правительства (signature — [illegible])

---

(Parallel German column — heading block:)

**4% OBLIGATIONS-ANLEIHE VON 1901 DER MOSKAU-KASAN EISENBAHN-GESELLSCHAFT**

im Nominalbetrage von 35.880.000 Mark D. R.-W. = 16.608.852 Rubel (1 Rubel = ¹⁄₁₅ Imperial) = 21.169.200 Holl. Guld. = 1.756.326 Pfund Sterling, mit absoluter Garantie der KAISERLICH Russischen Regierung für Verzinsung und Tilgung.

**OBLIGATION VON ZWEITAUSEND MARK D. R.-W.**

= 925 Rubel 80 Kop. (1 Rubel = ¹⁄₁₅ Imperial) = 1180 Holl. Gulden = 97 Pfund Sterling 18 Schilling

**AUF DEN INHABER.**

(Body paragraph parallels the Russian text. Captions:) Hauptbuchhalter — Der Bevollmächtigte der Kaiserl. Russ. Regierung. Moskau, 1901.

---

(Parallel Dutch column — heading block:)

**4% OBLIGATIE-LEENING VAN 1901 DER MOSKAU-KASAN SPOORWEG-MAATSCHAPPIJ**

tot een nominaal bedrag van Rijksmark 35.880.000 = Roebel 16.608.852 (1 Roebel = ¹⁄₁₅ Imperiaal) = Gulden 21.169.200 Nederl. Court. = 1.756.326 Sterling, onvoorwaardelijk gegarandeerd door de KEIZERLIJK Russische Regeering voor rente en aflossing.

**OBLIGATIE VAN TWEE DUIZEND RIJKSMARK**

= 925 Roebel 80 Kop. (1 Roebel = ¹⁄₁₅ Imperiaal) = 1180 Gulden Nederl. Court. = 97 Ponden Sterling 18 Shilling

**AAN TOONDER.**

(Body paragraph parallels the Russian text. Captions:) Hoofdboekhouder — De Gemachtigte der Keizerlijk Russische Regeering. Moskau, 1901.

---

## English Translation:

(Corner denomination: 2000)

(Top-left stamp:) Stamp of the Imperial Russian Government
(Top-right stamp:) Stamp duty paid

**4% Bond Loan of the Year 1901**

**of the Moscow-Kazan Railway Company**

on a nominal capital of 35,880,000 marks of German imperial currency = 16,608,852 rubles (1 ruble = ¹⁄₁₅ of an imperial) = 21,169,200 Dutch guilders = 1,756,326 pounds sterling, under the unconditional guarantee of the Imperial Russian Government for the payment of interest and redemption.

**BOND**

**No. 06181**

**FOR TWO THOUSAND MARKS OF GERMAN IMPERIAL CURRENCY**

= 925 rubles 80 kopecks (1 ruble = ¹⁄₁₅ of an imperial) = 1,180 Dutch guilders = 97 pounds sterling 18 shillings

**TO THE BEARER.**

The holder of this bond participates with TWO THOUSAND marks of German imperial currency in the loan contracted in the amount of 35,880,000 marks of German imperial currency, upon the conditions set out on the reverse hereof, and it is secured by all the property and all the revenues of the Company and holds priority over all future loans.

Independently of this, the bonds of this loan are granted the unconditional guarantee of the Imperial Russian Government for the payment of interest and redemption from the day of their issue. Payments of interest on the coupons and redemption of the bonds are forever exempt from all Russian taxes.

Interest at 4% per annum is paid upon presentation of the coupons annually on the terms of 18 June/1 July and 19 December/1 January. Moscow, 1901.

The Board of the Moscow-Kazan Railway Company:

(three engraved signatures — [illegible])

Chief Accountant (signature — [illegible])

The Authorized Representative of the Government (signature — [illegible])

(The German and Dutch columns are parallel translations of the same text: "4% Bond Loan of 1901 of the Moscow-Kazan Railway Company... Bond for Two Thousand Marks... To the Bearer," with the captions "Chief Accountant / Bookkeeper" and "The Authorized Representative of the Imperial Russian Government," Moscow, 1901.)

title: Morris Canal and Banking Company Stock Transfer Receipt
description: A single sheet bearing two engraved stock-transfer assignments of the Morris Canal and Banking Company, executed at its Agency at the Phenix Bank in the City of New York and dated June 28 and July 10, 1845. Each is a printed "For Value Received" form by which a holder assigns and transfers shares in the capital stock of the company's "OF 1844" issue, standing in the holder's name on the company's books, to be subscribed before a witness. The Morris Canal and Banking Company, chartered in New Jersey in 1824 to operate the Morris Canal, joined a public work to banking privileges in a manner characteristic of the era's mixed canal-and-banking corporations. Drawn during the company's troubled reorganization following its mid-1840s difficulties, the forms document a brisk secondary trade in its shares handled through New York's financial houses. The plain typographic frame, free of allegory, befits a routine instrument of transfer rather than a presentation certificate.
creator: Morris Canal and Banking Company (Agency at Phenix Bank, NYC)
notes: Morris Canal and Banking Company. Two stock transfer receipts: (1) June 28, 1845, to Hopkins & Berton; (2) July 10, 1845, to E.S. Map. Phenix Bank agency, New York City.

title: New York Central and Hudson River Railroad Company Debt Certificate
description: This engraved certificate is a debt certificate for one thousand dollars, bearing interest at four per cent per annum payable semi-annually, issued by The New York Central and Hudson River Railroad Company and dated December 1892. Its terms specify that principal shall not fall due until the first day of May 1902, and that both principal and interest are payable in gold coin of the United States "of the standard of weight and fineness" then prevailing, a gold clause that reflects the hard-money anxieties of the early 1890s, when fears over silver agitation and the security of the dollar pressed investors to demand specie guarantees. The instrument represents a form of medium-term borrowing the road used alongside its bonded debt and equity. The vignette, in the restrained engraver's classicism favored for American securities, sets two seated allegorical women, emblems of commerce and abundance, about a central globe, asserting the enterprise's reach and solidity.
creator: New York Central and Hudson River Railroad Company
notes: New York Central and Hudson River Railroad Company. Debt Certificate No. 1390. $1,000 at 4%. Due May 1, 1902. December 24, 1892.
transcription:
# Transcription

**FOR A VALUABLE CONSIDERATION RECEIVED BY EACH.**

**IT IS MUTUALLY AGREED BY:**

**The New York Central and Hudson River**
**Railroad Company**

**and the holder of the foregoing Debt Certificate No. 1390**
**of the New York Central Railroad Company for**

**ONE THOUSAND DOLLARS**

that the principal hereof shall not be due nor payable until the first day of May 1902, with interest at the rate of Four percent per annum, payable semi-annually on the first of twenty-four and shall both principal and interest shall be payable in gold coin of the United States of America of the standard of weight and fineness of Decem[ber] 24, 1892. Registered certificates of $1,000 or multiples thereof will be issued in exchange for certificates of the same, under such regulations as the said Company may prescribe. The assent to this agreement by the holder of said Debt Certificate is evidenced in its name, be for the purpose of having this agreement registered therewith, and by the acceptance of the accompanying coupons.

**Witness the seal of the Company**
**and [signature] at of December 1892**

[Signature: illegible/cursive]
**TREASURER**

title: New York, New Haven and Hartford Railroad Harlem River–Port Chester First Mortgage Gold Bond
description: This is a registered first mortgage gold bond of $10,000 bearing four per cent annual interest, issued by the New York, New Haven and Hartford Railroad Company under its Harlem River–Port Chester Branch First Mortgage. Its fifty-year term runs from the first day of November 1904 to maturity on the first day of May 1954, with principal and interest payable in United States gold coin at the company's New York office. Signed at New York in November 1904, the bond funded the New Haven's costly four-tracking and grade-separation of the corridor linking the Harlem River with Port Chester, the spine of its commuter traffic into Westchester and southern Connecticut. The gold-coin pledge and senior mortgage lien advertise the security's conservative grade to savings-bank investors. The engraved vignette of seated classical female figures flanking the company arms follows the allegorical convention of period bank-note engraving, lending an emblem of probity. Stamped SURRENDERED.
creator: New York, New Haven and Hartford Railroad Company
notes: New York, New Haven and Hartford Railroad. Harlem River–Port Chester First Mortgage 4% 50-Year Gold Bond. $10,000. No. 331. 1912. SURRENDERED.

title: Nuovo Monte Sussidio Vacabile della Città di Firenze share certificate, Florence, 1706
description: This handwritten and letterpress instrument is a share certificate (luogo) in the Nuovo Monte Sussidio Vacabile della Città di Firenze, the New Vacatable Subsidy Fund of the City of Florence, issued by its Protettori, Deputati and Amministratori under the Most Serene Grand Duke of Tuscany. It records the holder as creditor for one hundred ducati of Florentine money per luogo, yielding six per cent yearly ("ducati sei per cento l'Anno"). Dated at Florence on 11 August 1706, it rests on the fund's founding provision of 4 July 1692 and a decree of amplification of 8 August 1692. The Florentine monti were among Europe's earliest transferable public debts; in a monte vacabile the income reverted to the fund on the holder's death, blending bond and life annuity. The engraved Medici palle crowning the sheet bind sovereign credit to dynastic authority.
creator: Città di Firenze (City of Florence, Grand Duchy of Tuscany)
notes: Nuovo Monte Sussidio Vacabile della Città di Firenze. Public debt certificate at 6%. Florence, 1693. Grand Duke Cosimo III de' Medici. Among the earliest transferable public debt instruments in Europe.
transcription:
# Share Certificate (luoghi) of the New Vacatable Subsidy Fund of the City of Florence — Transcription

## Original (Italian):

(Three engraved heraldic emblems across the top of the sheet, the central one bearing the Medici palle; an archival number written in the upper-right corner.)

**NOI Protettori, Deputati, & Amministratori** del Nuovo Monte Sussidio Vacabile della Città di Firenze, eretto d'ordine del Serenifs. Gran Duca di Toscana nostro Signore, per Provvisione ottenuta nell' Amplissimo Senato de' Quarant'otto, sotto dì 4. Luglio 1692.

A Tutti quelli, che vedranno la presente nostra faciamo piena, & indubitata fede, come

(handwritten insertion:) [Ill.]mo Sig.re Balì Zanobi [illegible] [e l'Ill.]mo D. Balì Piero Girolami

apparisce Creditore al Libro di detto Nuovo Monte Sussidio Segnato B, a c. ⸻ *di Luoghi* [Tre] *(handwritten, right margin)* di valuta di ducati Cento di lire sette per ducato moneta Fiorentina per ogni luogo, con li frutti a suo benefizio a ragione di ducati sei simili per cento l'Anno per ogni luogo dal dì [primo d'Agosto corrente] *(handwritten)* in avvenire, risegnatili [dal conto cane... nel ... — handwritten interlinear, largely illegible]

come per partito nostro di questo giorno al Gior. di Permute, &c. segn. C, a c. [illegible — handwritten reference]— cessione, traslazione, e risegna, per tenore della presente facciamo, e dichiariamo d. [Ill.]mo Balì Zanobi Girolam[i] *(handwritten)* Creditore di detto Monte di detti luoghi [Tre] *(handwritten)* per luogo, come sopra pagabili di tre Mesi in tre Mesi per rata con annuo reddito di ducati sei [simili]. Però ammettendo Noi detta [cessione ...]

e con li privilegj, prerogative, condizioni, e dichiarazioni di che in detta Provvisione, e nel nostro Decreto d'ampliazione, e dichiaraz. del dì 8. Agosto 1692. al che tutto si abbia sempre relazione: Ordinando in virtù di questa al nostro Camarlingo Generale presente, e che per li tempi sarà, che gli paghi dd. frutti di tre mesi in tre mesi, come sopra. Et in fede abbiamo fatto la presente, quale sarà firmata da uno di Noi Protettori, e sottoscritta dal nostro Canc.e sigillata con il solito sigillo di d. Monte.

Data in Firenze nella nostra solita Residenza questo dì [Undici Agosto 1706] *(date handwritten)*

(handwritten signature, lower right:) Girolamo [Fidensini — surname uncertain] Canc.re

(A large wax/paper seal impression at the lower left.)

---

## English Translation:

(Three engraved coats of arms across the top, the central one bearing the Medici balls; an archival number in the upper-right corner.)

**WE, the Protectors, Deputies, & Administrators** of the New Vacatable Subsidy Fund (Monte Sussidio Vacabile) of the City of Florence, erected by order of the Most Serene Grand Duke of Tuscany our Lord, by a Provision obtained in the Most Ample Senate of the Forty-eight (Quarant'otto), under the date of 4 July 1692.

To all those who shall see this present [instrument] we give full and undoubted assurance, that

(handwritten:) the Most Illustrious Signore Balì Zanobi [illegible] [and the Most Illustrious] Lord Balì Piero Girolami

appears as Creditor in the Book of the said New Monte Sussidio, marked B, at folio ⸻, *for [Three] luoghi (places)* to the value of one hundred ducati of seven lire per ducato of Florentine money for each place, with the income to his benefit at the rate of six similar ducati per hundred per Year for each place, from the [first of August current] onward, re-assigned to him [by the account ... — handwritten, largely illegible],

as by our resolution of this day in the Journal of Transfers (Permute), etc., marked C, at folio [illegible]— [being] a cession, transfer, and re-registration, by the tenor of the present [instrument] we make and declare the said Most Illustrious Balì Zanobi Girolami Creditor of the said Monte for the said [Three] places per place, as above, payable every three months by installment, with an annual return of six [similar] ducati. However, We admitting the said [cession ...]

and with the privileges, prerogatives, conditions, and declarations set forth in the said Provision, and in our Decree of amplification and declaration of the 8th of August 1692, to all of which reference shall always be had: Ordering by virtue of this to our Camerlengo General now in office, and to him who shall hold the office in the future, that he pay the said income every three months, as above. And in witness we have made the present, which shall be signed by one of Us Protectors, and countersigned by our Chancellor and sealed with the customary seal of the said Monte.

Given in Florence in our usual Residence this day [the Eleventh of August 1706].

(handwritten signature:) Girolamo [Fidensini — surname uncertain], Chancellor.

(A large seal impression at lower left.)

---

Notes on conflicts vs. the description/verified facts: None. The image confirms the ledger reference **"Segnato B"** (the old transcription's "E" was wrong), the value of **ducati Cento (100)** per luogo (the old "270"/"two hundred seventy" was wrong), the founding provision **"Senato de' Quarant'otto, sotto dì 4. Luglio 1692"** (the old garbled/duplicated "Senato de' Quadrini" header was wrong), the amplification decree of **8 August 1692**, **di Luoghi Tre (3)**, and the date **Undici Agosto 1706 (11 August 1706)**.

title: Compañía del Ferro-Carril de Palencia á Ponferrada (Noroeste de España) Bond, Madrid 1862
description: A bilingual (Spanish/French) bearer obligation of the Compañía del Ferro-Carril de Palencia á Ponferrada, ó del Noroeste de España (Compagnie du Chemin de Fer de Palencia à Ponferrada ou du Nord-Ouest de l'Espagne), a joint-stock company sanctioned by royal decree of 18 May 1862 under a ninety-nine-year concession authorised by the laws of 21 April 1858 and 5 June 1859. The bond forms part of an emission of 68,420 obligations of 1,900 reales vellón (500 francs) each, bearing 57 reales (15 francs) of annual interest payable each 15 June and 15 December, redeemable at par by annual lottery drawings over ninety-two years from 1 July 1867; the headline total is 65,000,000 reales. Dated at Madrid, 15 June 1862, and signed by two administrators. The line spanning the Cantabrian range linked the Castilian grain plains to the coal of El Bierzo, and the dual-currency text registers the heavy dependence of Spanish railway promotion on French capital. The restrained engraved frame, with a small vignette of the works, subordinates ornament to the language of credit.
creator: Compañía del Ferro-Carril de Palencia a Ponferrada
notes: Compañía del Ferro-Carril de Palencia a Ponferrada (Nord-Ouest de l'Espagne). Bilingual Spanish/French bearer bond No. 32,105. 1,900 Reales = 500 Francs. 68,420 obligations in total. Madrid, ca. 1860s.
transcription:
# Palencia to Ponferrada Railway Company (North-West of Spain) — 1,900-Reales / 500-Francs Bearer Obligation No. 52,105 — Transcription

## Original (Spanish):

**COMPAÑÍA DEL FERRO-CARRIL DE PALENCIA Á PONFERRADA**
**ó DEL**
**NOROESTE DE ESPAÑA.**

Sociedad anónima aprobada por real decreto de 18 Mayo de 1862.

Concesion de 99 años,
AUTORIZADA POR LAS LEYES DE 21 DE ABRIL DE 1858 Y 5 DE JUNIO DE 1859.

**1.ª SÉRIE.**

**EMISION DE 68,420 OBLIGACIONES DE 1,900 REALES CADA UNA,**

Reembolsables á la par, por sorteos anuales, en 92 años, desde 1.º de Julio de 1867.

Emitidas al tipo de 950 Rvn., que hacen un total efectivo de Rvn. 65,000,000.

**INTERES ANUAL: 57 REALES VELLON,**
pagaderos por semestres, el 15 de Junio y el 15 de Diciembre de cada año.

**N.º 52,105**

*(printed, right of centre:)* Dos Administradores.

*(Right column — French:)*

**ou du**

Société anonyme approuvée par décret royal du 18 Mai 1862.

AUTORISÉE PAR LES LOIS DES 21 AVRIL 1858 ET 5 JUIN 1859.

**1.re SÉRIE.**

**ÉMISSION DE 68,420 OBLIGATIONS DE 500 FRANCS CHACUNE,**

Remboursables au pair, par tirages annuels, en 92 ans, à partir du 1.er Juillet 1867.

**INTÉRÊT ANNUEL: 15 francs,**
payables par semestres, le 15 Juin et le 15 Décembre de chaque année.

**UNE OBLIGATION**

*(Bottom — detachable interest coupons in a row; each headed:)*

**ó DEL NOROESTE DE ES[PAÑA]**
**OBLIGACION N.º 52,105**
Cupon de interes de 28,50 Rvn., pagadero[...] de 1878 en MADRID Y PARIS.

*(coupon payment years differ from box to box; other coupon boxes repeat the same heading with their own dates — most values [illegible] at this resolution)*

---

**or of the**

Joint-stock company approved by royal decree of 18 May 1862.

*Note on conflicts with the catalogue description:*
- The description states the company was sanctioned "by royal decree of 18 May **1861**." The image reads clearly, in both languages, **18 Mayo de 1862 / 18 Mai 1862**.
- All other verified facts (laws of 21 April 1858 and 5 June 1859; emission of 68,420 obligations of 1,900 reales; redeemable at par by annual drawings over 92 years from 1 July 1867; issue price 950 Rvn., total 65,000,000 Rvn.; annual interest 57 reales vellón; serial No. 52,105; "Madrid, 15 de Junio de 1862," Two Administrators) match the image exactly.

title: Norwich & Worcester Rail Road Company Bond
description: A printed coupon bond for $1,000 of the Norwich & Worcester Rail Road Company, chartered in the State of Connecticut, part of an issue whose whole authorized amount is $500,000. It bears six per cent interest, payable semi-annually on presentation of the attached coupons, the principal payable to the Treasurer of the State of Connecticut at the New England Trust Company in Boston. The form is dated at Hartford, its manuscript day and year left blank. That the bond runs to the State Treasurer marks it as security for Connecticut's aid to the line linking Norwich, on the Thames, with Worcester in Massachusetts. The engraved border crowds together emblems of the road's traffic and region: a locomotive at a station, a coasting vessel, a seated figure of Justice with sword and scales, mariners, harvesters with sheaves, and the state's arms, the route's commercial promise made ornament around the obligation it secures.
creator: Norwich & Worcester Rail Road Company
notes: Norwich & Worcester Rail Road Company (State of Connecticut). $1,000 bearer bond. No. 468. Total authorized issue $500,000. Printed by Evans, Hartford. Ca. 1840s–1850s.
transcription:

United States of America

Norwich & Worcester
RAIL ROAD COMPANY

**Subtitle Text:**
WHOLE AMOUNT AUTHORIZED $500,000 — INTEREST PAYABLE SEMI-ANNUALLY

**Body Text:**
On the first day of March, 1851, the Norwich and Worcester Rail Road Company, formed to pay by Loan, bearing the date thereof, Connecticut Bank, a Bank Incorporated under the Laws of Connecticut, State of Connecticut, State of $1000, at the New England Bank, Exchange, in the City of Boston, with interest at the rate of $25 per cent per annum, payable semi-annually on the presentation of Dividend Warrant, under the seal and signature of the Directors of said Company, at said Bank until the 1st day of March, 1877. This Bond is transferable, and the holder is entitled to receive from the Treasurer of the said Company, at fixed periods, the interest due thereon. title: Omnium Français du Film Share
description: This printed bearer share (action au porteur, Série A) of 100 francs belongs to the Omnium Français du Film, a société anonyme based in Paris with a capital of three million francs. That capital was divided into 30,000 shares of 100 francs each—26,000 of Série A and 4,000 of Série B—alongside 12,000 founder's shares (parts de fondateur) carrying no nominal value. Dated 10 May 1928 and certified before Maître Baudrier, notary in Paris, the share was issued at the close of the French silent cinema's most prosperous decade, just as the arrival of synchronized sound threatened to upend production economics and demand fresh capital. The stylized geometric monogram "OFF" and the crisp sans-serif lettering of the corners reflect the Art Deco idiom then fashionable in French commercial design, lending a modern, industrial confidence to a speculative venture in a youthful entertainment trade.
creator: Omnium Français du Film
notes: Omnium Français du Film, S.A. Action au porteur Série A, 100 francs. Capital 3,000,000 francs (26,000 Série A + 4,000 Série B). No. 022884. Paris, May 10, 1928.

title: Ostend Company Dividend Receipt
description: A printed and manuscript dividend receipt of the Generale Keyserlycke Indische Compagnie, the Imperial East India Company chartered at Ostend, signed by its cashier Joan Baptista Cogels and acknowledged by the shareholder C.J. De Witte at Antwerp on 11 August 1734. The form records payment of 360 guilders in wissel-gelt (bank money of exchange) as a repartition of six per cent, sixty guilders per share, on six shares held in the holder's name. The engraved vignette crowns the company's arms with the double-headed Habsburg eagle, asserting the imperial charter granted by Charles VI in 1722 that so provoked the maritime powers. Issued three years after Britain, the Dutch Republic, and France forced the company's suppression in 1731, the receipt belongs to its prolonged liquidation: the imperial insignia presides over a venture already being wound up, its capital returned rather than employed in trade.
creator: Generale Keyserlycke Indische Compagnie (Ostend Company)
notes: Generale Keyserlycke Indische Compagnie (Ostend Company). Dividend receipt, 360 guilders (60 per share × 6 shares). Signed by Joan Baptista Cogels, Cassier. Folio 247:B. Antwerp, August 11, 1734.

title: Norbertine Fathers' Order 8% First Mortgage Bond, Gödöllő (Budapest), 1926
description: This printed bearer bond (obligatie luidend aan toonder) of 1,000 Dutch guilders, bearing 8 percent annual interest payable by half-yearly coupons, belongs to the First Mortgage Loan (1e Hypothecaire Leening) of the Order of Norbertine Fathers, the Praemonstratensian Canons of Jászó (Jassauer Praemonstratenser Koorheeren-Orde), seated at Gödöllő near Budapest. The whole loan amounts to 1,600,000 guilders, divided into 1,200 bonds of 1,000 guilders and 800 of 500, secured as a first mortgage on the order's properties and guaranteed by the Royal Hungarian Government. Dated Budapest, 1 August 1926, and administered by the Hollandsche Garantie & Trust Compagnie of Amsterdam, the instrument shows how a Catholic religious house in post-Trianon Hungary reached Dutch capital markets, the sovereign guarantee and guilder denomination together reassuring foreign lenders. The sober typographic frame, unadorned but for the order's seal, presents the loan as an institution of standing rather than speculation.
creator: Orde der Paters Norbertynen (Jassauer Praemonstratenser Koorheeren-Orde)
notes: Orde der Paters Norbertynen (Jászó Premonstratensian / Norbertine Fathers) 8% First Mortgage Bond. F. 1,000 Dutch guilders. No. 0.274. Budapest, August 1, 1928. Total issue F. 1,600,000. Royal Hungarian Government guarantee. Administered by Hollandsche Garantie & Trust Compagnie.
transcription:
# ORIGINAL (Dutch):

ORDE DER PATERS NORBERTYNEN
(JASSAUER PRAEMONSTRATENSER
KOORHEEREN-ORDE)
TE GÖDÖLLŐ BY BUDAPEST.

8% 1e HYPOTHECAIRE LEENING

GEGARANDEERD DOOR DE RON. HONGAARSCHE REGEERING

GROOT
F.1.600.000

VERDEELD IN 1.200 OBLIGATIES VAN F 1.000 EN 800 OBLIGATIES VAN F 500.

OBLIGATIE
LUIDEND AAN TOONDER

GROOT
F 1.000 NED. CT.

No. 0.274

De Jassauer Praemonstratenser Koorheeren-Orde te Gödöllő heeft krachtens een haar door de Kon. Hongaarsche Regeering verleende machtiging (Min. besluit 1872-1928 van 28 Juni 1926) het volgende leening te Gödöllő aangegaan ter grootte van F 1.600.000 (een miljoen zeshonderd duizend hollandsche guldens). Voor deze leening heeft de Jassauer Praemonstratenser Koorheeren-Orde te Gödöllő 1200 obligaties van F 1000 en 800 obligaties van F 500 Hollandsche guldens uitgegeven waarvan deze obligatie deel uitmaakt. De Obligatie verplicht niet tot betaling en terugbetaling op de volgende voorwaarden.

Voor de stipte betaling der rentecoupons en voor de terugbetaling dezer leening is de Jassauer Praemonstratenser Koorheeren-Orde met haar geheele roerend en onroerend vermogen aansprakelijk.

Als bijzondere zekerheid voor de rente-betaling en de terugbetaling dezer leening is een eerste hypotheek ingeschreven ten gunste der HOLLANDSCHE GARANTIE & TRUST COMPAGNIE te Amsterdam op de aan de Jassauer Praemonstratenser Koorheeren-Orde toebehoorende eigendommen te Gödöllő en verder op het grondbezeit a) Alsónovai, Felsonovai en Istaros, b) Vadaskesku, en Leopoldst.

Verder is de rente-dienst en de terugbetaling dezer leening verzekerd door den Kon. Hungarischen Staat, die volgens § V der tristacie van 19 Juli 1928, voor hoofdsom en rente volijdig borg blijft. Iedere obligatie draagt 8½% rente van 1 augustus 1928 tot aan den dag, dat zy terugbetaald wordt. De rente-betaling heeft telkens halfjaarliks plaats op 1 Februari en 1 Augustus en is betaalbaar te Amsterdam volgens de bepalingen der tristacie van 19 Juli 1928.

De obligatien worden terugbetaald volgens het plan en op de voorwaarden zoals die heiden gekeyde zijn afgedrukt.

De verschenen coupons vervallen in 5 jaren en de aflosbare gestelde obligaties na 30 jaar, beiden gereekend van af den dag, dat zy betaalbaar gesteld zyn.

Alle tristacie is gededoceerd by Notaris Franz Lázár te Budapest; een uittreksel is aan keerzyde afgedrukt.

Voor de Obligatiehouders:                    Budapest, 1 Augutus 1926.
Hollandsche Garantie & Trust Compagnie.    Jassauer Praemonstratenser Koorheeren-Orde.

[Signatures]                                 Probst-Praelat von Jassen.

Hierby een couponblad met 20 halfjaarlyskche coupons en een talon.

---

# ENGLISH TRANSLATION:

ORDER OF THE NORBERTINE FATHERS
(JÁSZÓ PREMONSTRATENSIAN
CANONS REGULAR ORDER)
AT GÖDÖLLŐ NEAR BUDAPEST.

8% FIRST MORTGAGE BOND

GUARANTEED BY THE ROYAL HUNGARIAN GOVERNMENT

TOTAL AMOUNT
F.1,600,000

DIVIDED INTO 1,200 BONDS OF F 1,000 AND 800 BONDS OF F 500.

BOND
PAYABLE TO BEARER

AMOUNT
F 1,000 DUTCH GUILDERS

No. 0.274

The Jászó Premonstratensian Canons Regular Order at Gödöllő has, pursuant to an authorization granted to it by the Royal Hungarian Government (Ministry decision 1872-1928 of 28 June 1926), contracted the following loan at Gödöllő in the amount of F 1,600,000 (one million six hundred thousand Dutch guilders). For this loan, the Jászó Premonstratensian Canons Regular Order at Gödöllő has issued 1,200 bonds of F 1,000 and 800 bonds of F 500 Dutch guilders, of which this bond is a part. The Bond obliges [the issuer] not to payment and repayment under the following conditions.

For the punctual payment of interest coupons and for the repayment of this loan, the Jászó Premonstratensian Canons Regular Order is liable with all its moveable and immoveable property.

As particular security for the payment of interest and the repayment of this loan, a first mortgage has been registered in favor of the HOLLANDSCHE GARANTIE & TRUST COMPANY in Amsterdam on the property belonging to the Jászó Premonstratensian Canons Regular Order at Gödöllő and furthermore on the real estate a) Alsónovai, Felsónovai and Istaros, b) Vadaskesku, and Leopoldst.

Furthermore, the payment of interest and the repayment of this loan is secured by the Royal Hungarian State, which according to § V of the deed of trust of 19 July 1928, remains fully liable for principal and interest. Each bond bears 8½% interest from 1 August 1928 until the day it is repaid. Interest payment occurs semi-annually on 1 February and 1 August and is payable in Amsterdam according to the provisions of the deed of trust of 19 July 1928.

The bonds are repaid according to the plan and under the conditions as printed on the reverse.

The matured coupons expire in 5 years and the scheduled redeemable bonds in 30 years, both calculated from the day they become payable.

All deed of trust is authenticated by Notary Franz Lázár at Budapest; an extract is printed on the reverse.

For the Bondholders:                        Budapest, 1 August 1926.
Hollandsche Garantie & Trust Company.       Jászó Premonstratensian Canons Regular Order.

[Signatures]                                 Abbot-Primate of Jászó.

Hereby a coupon sheet with 20 semi-annual coupons and a talon.

---

# NOTABLE MARKINGS:

- **Serial Number:** No. 0.274
- **Ornamental Border:** Decorative blue floral border framing entire document
- **Seal/Stamp:** Orange/tan colored official seal (Hungarian coat of arms) in upper right
- **Official Stamp:** Purple circular institutional seal at bottom center
- **Handwritten Signatures:** Two signatures present at bottom
- **Date:** Budapest, 1 August 1926 (printed)
- **Issue Date:** 28 June 1926 (referenced in Ministry decision)
- **Coupon Sheet:** Reference to attached coupon sheet with 20 semi-annual coupons and talon strip

title: Oregon and Transcontinental Company Share Certificate
description: This engraved certificate represents one hundred shares of one hundred dollars each in the capital stock of the Oregon and Transcontinental Company, whose authorized capital of fifty million dollars is proclaimed across the top of the sheet. Dated the twelfth day of September 1888 at New York and signed by Edward Ede as Assistant Secretary and by the President, it issued during the company's post-collapse years. The Oregon and Transcontinental Company was the holding vehicle Henry Villard assembled in 1881 to bind the Oregon Railway and Navigation Company, Pacific steamships, and a controlling interest in the Northern Pacific into a single Northwestern transportation system; Villard's overreach broke the structure in 1883, and certificates of this date belong to the chastened reorganization that followed. The central vignette, engraved by the American Bank Note Company, sets two native figures on a bluff above a river valley crossed by a railroad and a bridge, an allegory of the transcontinental line advancing through the Pacific Northwest.
creator: Oregon and Transcontinental Company
notes: Oregon and Transcontinental Company. 100 shares at $100 each. Capital $50,000,000. No. 10583. September 1888. President: Mayor Fiske. Secretary: Edward Ede.

title: Austrian Building Lottery Bond
description: This printed premium debenture (Prämienschuldverschreibung), the "Österreichisches Bau-Los, Em. 1926," carries a face value of zehn Schilling (10 Austrian Schilling). It forms one lot in a lottery loan authorised by the federal law of 27 July 1926 (BGBl. Nr. 199): a total of 6,000,000 Schilling divided into 600,000 such lots, guaranteed by the Bund and entered in the land register for housing purposes (für Wohnungszwecke). Issued at Vienna in August 1926 by the Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds (Federal Housing and Settlement Fund), it answered the acute postwar housing shortage by pairing a modest fixed claim with the lure of periodic prize drawings, a savings device long familiar in Central Europe. The vignette of a steep-roofed settlement cottage above a terraced garden makes the purpose literal, the security itself depicting the dwelling its subscriptions would build.
creator: Bundeswohn- und Siedlungsfonds (Austrian Federal Housing and Settlement Fund)
notes: Österreichisches Bau-Los, Emission 1926. 10 Schilling. Serie 2,485, No. 049. Vienna, August 1926. Bundeswohn- und Siedlungsfonds (Federal Housing and Settlement Fund). Lottery bond for housing purposes.
transcription:
# Austrian Building Lottery Bond (Österreichisches Bau-Los, Em. 1926) — Transcription

## Original (German):

**(Top header line, across the sheet):**
Vom Bunde garantiertes und grundbücherlich einverleibtes Lotterieanlehen von 1926 für Wohnungszwecke.

**(Red boxes, left and right):**
Serie 2,485.  N⁰ 049.

**(Ornamental title):**
Österreichisches Bau-Los
Em. 1926

**ZEHN SCHILLING**

*(Vignette: photograph of a steep-/mansard-roofed settlement cottage with a terraced garden and steps leading to the entrance.)*

**(Body text beneath the vignette):**

Diese Prämienschuldverschreibung bildet einen Teil des auf Grund des Gesetzes vom 27. Juli 1926, B. G. Bl. Nr. 199, ausgegebenen Lotterieanlehens vom Jahre 1926 des Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds im Betrage von S 6,000.000.—, eingestellt in 600.000 Lose à S 10.— Nominale, und wird nach Maßgabe des beigefügten Tilgungs- und Prämienziehungs-Planes innerhalb 40 Jahren durch Verlosung zur Rückzahlung aufgerufen werden.

Die Gewinste des Prämienanlehens sind ohne jeden Abzug von Steuern und Gebühren auszuzahlen und in die Bemessungsgrundlage der Einkommensteuer nicht einzubeziehen.

Der Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds haftet für den Dienst dieses Prämienanlehens mit seinem gesamten Vermögen und insbesondere mit den aus dem Erträgnisse dieses Anlehens gewährten Hypothekardarlehen, die als Kaution für die Befriedigung der Ansprüche der Besitzer der Prämienschuldverschreibungen zu bestellen sind. Subsidiär haftet für diese Verbindlichkeiten des Fonds der Bund als Bürge.

Die Teilschuldverschreibungen dieses Prämienanlehens sind für Anlagen gemäß § 23 des Bundesgesetzes vom 15. April 1921, B. G. Bl. Nr. 252, und zur fruchtbringenden Anlegung von Kapitalien der Stiftungen, der unter öffentlicher Aufsicht stehenden Anstalten, des Postsparkassen-Amtes, dann von Pupillar-, Fideikommiß- und Depositengeldern und zu Dienst- und Geschäftskautionen verwendbar.

Wien, im August 1926.

**Bundes-Wohn- und Siedlungsfonds.**

*(Lower left, circular seal:)* BUNDES-WOHN- und SIEDLUNGSFONDS · IM BUNDES MINISTERIUM FÜR SOZIALE VERWALTUNG

*(Handwritten signature to the right of the seal — cursive, [illegible]; appears to read "S. Verbo[...]")*

*(Centered at bottom edge, printer's imprint:)* „STEYERMÜHL" WIEN

---

**(Top header line):**
Lottery loan of 1926 for housing purposes, guaranteed by the Federation and inscribed in the land register.

**(Red boxes):**
Series 2,485.  No. 049.

**(Title):**
Austrian Building Lottery Bond
Emission 1926

**TEN SCHILLING**

**(Body text):**

This premium debenture forms part of the lottery loan of the year 1926 of the Federal Housing and Settlement Fund, issued on the basis of the Law of 27 July 1926, Federal Law Gazette No. 199, in the amount of S 6,000,000.—, divided into 600,000 lots of S 10.— nominal each, and will be called for repayment within 40 years by lot drawing (durch Verlosung) in accordance with the attached redemption and prize-drawing plan.

The winnings of the premium loan are to be paid out without any deduction of taxes or charges and are not to be included in the assessment basis of the income tax.

The Federal Housing and Settlement Fund is liable for the servicing of this premium loan with its entire assets and in particular with the mortgage loans granted out of the proceeds of this loan, which are to be pledged as security (Kaution) for satisfying the claims of the holders of the premium debentures. Subsidiarily, the Federation is liable as guarantor for these obligations of the Fund.

The partial debentures of this premium loan are eligible for investments pursuant to § 23 of the Federal Law of 15 April 1921, Federal Law Gazette No. 252, and for the profitable investment of the capital of foundations, of institutions under public supervision, of the Postal Savings Bank Office, further of ward (trust), fideicommissary, and deposit moneys, and as service and business securities/bonds.

Vienna, in August 1926.

**Federal Housing and Settlement Fund.**

*(Handwritten signature — illegible)*

*(Printer's imprint at bottom:)* "Steyermühl", Vienna

title: Café de la Paix Béziers Founder's Share
description: A founder's part (part de fondateur) issued by the Café de la Paix, a société anonyme seated at 1, Allées Paul Riquet in Béziers, in the Hérault of southern France. The company's statutes, deposited with Maître Vidal, notary at Béziers, on 11 December 1920, fixed its capital at 1,200,000 francs, fully paid, divided into 2,400 shares of 500 francs each; the certificate is dated Béziers, 1 January 1921, and bears the signatures of two administrators. Unlike ordinary shares, a part de fondateur carried no nominal value and was granted to a venture's promoters as a claim on profits above a set dividend, capitalising the goodwill of those who launched the enterprise. The sheet is conceived in a confident Art Nouveau idiom: sinuous display lettering and an allegorical draped woman lifting a cup preside over a precise elevation of the grand café itself, fusing commercial portrait and decorative allegory.
creator: Café de la Paix (Béziers)

title: Peruvian Republic Congressional War Loan Certificate
description: A handwritten subscription certificate of the nascent Republic of Peru, struck with the official seal, recording one share in the 100,000-peso emergency loan (empréstito) authorized by the Sovereign Congress (Soberano Congreso) at Lima in January 1823. The treasury promises to repay the holder—here Santiago Campos, for his part of a 1,000-peso action—within one month of date, with interest "al cinco por ciento mensual," five per cent monthly. The tally below sets principal at 66 pesos and interest at 3 pesos 2 reales 4, totalling 69 pesos 2 reales 4. The Congress raised the money "para subvenir á las necesidades públicas, y dar al ejército un impulso capaz de terminar la guerra"—to meet public needs and impel the army to end the war—and declared principal and interest irremissible national debt ranking before all other obligations. The crushing monthly rate measures the fiscal desperation of the independence war: Peru had proclaimed independence in 1821, yet royalist arms held out until Ayacucho in 1824.
creator: Peruvian Republic (Soberano Congreso)
notes: Peruvian Republic Congressional War Loan certificate. Lima, 1823. 100,000-peso loan authorized by the Soberano Congreso. 5% monthly interest. Irremissible national debt with priority claim. Independence war finance.

title: Philadelphia & West Chester Turnpike Road Company Blank Stock Certificate
description: This is a blank, unissued stock certificate of the Philad'a & West Chester Turnpike Road Co. of Philadelphia, with a par value of 25 dollars per share, as stated in the engraved left margin. No shareholder name, number of shares, or date has been entered; the printed line reads only "Philadelphia, the ___ day of ___ 18__," leaving the year open. The form was printed by Evans, Fourth & Library Streets, Philadelphia. Chartered in 1803, the turnpike was among Pennsylvania's earliest toll roads, joining the city to the borough of West Chester and financed, like many such roads, by subscription shares sold to local investors. The central vignette presents a winged figure of Fame sounding a trumpet, reclining against a globe beside an eagle and shield; a separate corner vignette shows a covered Conestoga wagon, and a sheaf of wheat sits at the foot. The motifs bind commerce, agriculture, and national fortune to the road's promise of overland trade.
creator: Philadelphia & West Chester Turnpike Road Company
notes: Philadelphia & West Chester Turnpike Road Company. Unissued/blank stock certificate. $25 per share. Printed by Evans, Printer, Philadelphia. Ca. 1850s–1860s.

title: Société des Plantations d'Agaves de l'Annam Share (Saigon, 1924)
description: This printed bearer share (action au porteur, entièrement libérée) of 500 francs belongs to the Société des Plantations d'Agaves de l'Annam, a French colonial agricultural company seated at Saigon. The text records an original capital of 2,750,000 francs in 5,500 shares of 500 francs each, with statutes deposited before a Saigon notary on 27 March 1924; the certificate itself is dated Saigon, 31 December 1924. Later overstamps tell the company's financial history: a blue overprint raises the capital to 6,875,000 francs, while a red overprint reduces the face value to 100 francs by resolution of an extraordinary general meeting. The firm planted agave in central Annam for sisal fibre destined for rope and cordage, part of the interwar drive to extract commercial crops from Indochina. The red border, with key-fret meanders, confronted beast-heads, and a central I-Ching trigram, adopts a chinoiserie idiom signalling the venture's Far-Eastern setting.
creator: Société des Plantations d'Agaves de l'Annam

title: Polish-American Oil Company "Columbia" Share Certificate
description: The certificate represents fifty shares of 1,000 Polish marks each, a block totalling 50,000 marks, in the Polsko Amerykańskie Towarzystwo Naftowe "Columbia" (Polish-American Petroleum Company "Columbia"), a joint-stock company whose statute the sheet records as approved on 6 February 1922. Issued at Warsaw and signed by the President of the Council (Prezes Rady), the board (Zarząd), the bookkeeper (Księgowy) and the treasurer (Skarbnik), it belongs to the brief flowering of oil ventures in the recently reunified Polish state, whose Galician fields ranked among the oldest worked in Europe. A photographic vignette of wooden derricks at the "Columbia" field crowns the sheet, framed by engraved oil towers and foliate borders, a naturalistic iconography that trades older allegory for the company own working landscape. Its denomination in the depreciating Polish mark fixes the issue within the inflation that would soon consume such paper.
creator: Polsko Amerykańskie Towarzystwo Naftowe "Columbia" S.A.

title: Compagnie Privilégiée des Ports de Cadix Mortgage Bond
description: This bearer mortgage bond (obligation hypothécaire au porteur) for 400 francs belongs to the Compagnie Privilégiée des Ports, Débarcadère Maritime et Terrains de Cadix, a French société anonyme constituted before the Paris notary Me Bourget, with its registered office at 15, Rue de la Chaussée d'Antin, Paris. Against a capital of ten million francs, the company raised a loan of twenty million, represented by 71,429 mortgage bonds issued at 280 francs and redeemable at 400 over twenty years — the gap between issue and redemption price serving as a built-in premium that lifted the effective yield above the nominal 24 francs annual interest, paid in quarterly six-franc coupons on 15 January, April, July and October. Dated Paris, 31 August 1869, it shows French Second Empire capital financing Spanish port infrastructure at Cádiz. The engraved vignette, framed in Renaissance-revival strapwork and a chain border evoking maritime mooring, sets an allegorical figure of commerce beside a putto with a globe, voicing the venture's trading ambitions.

title: Portuguese External Debt Interest Certificate
description: This printed Certificate of Interest, lettered "B," attaches to a £100 bond of the Three Per Cent Portuguese External Debt, issued under the Portuguese royal Decree of 18th December, 1852. The certificate, countersigned in London by the Financial Agent of the Portuguese Government (José Jorge Loureiro), records the holder's entitlement to an additional one per cent per annum, granted under Article 2 of the Law of 26th July, 1856, and confirmed by royal decree dated at the Palace of Cintra, 28th July, 1856; it derives from an arrangement entered into in London on 13th December, 1855. The instrument belongs to the protracted settlement of Portugal's foreign obligations after the Crown had defaulted on its London-held debt, the original 1852 conversion being sweetened here with a contingent interest top-up to coax bondholders into accepting the restructured terms. The text appears in English with a parallel Spanish translation, addressing the international market that held the paper.
creator: Portuguese Government (Financial Agent in London)
notes: Portuguese External Debt, Certificate of Interest on £100 Three Per Cent Bond (Letter B). Decree of December 18, 1852. Countersigned London, December 13, 1855. Financial Agent: José Jorge Loureiro.
transcription:
# Transcription: Portuguese External Debt Certificate of Interest

**LETTER B.**

**Portuguese External Debt D°.6944**

## CERTIFICATE OF INTEREST

ON THE SUM OF £100, CAPITAL OF THE THREE PER CENT. BOND NUMBER AS ABOVE.

---

By virtue of the authority granted to the undersigned by the Decree of His Majesty the King of Portugal, of which the following is a Translation:

**"DECREE.**

"Making use of the authority granted to my Government by Article 2nd of the law of 26th instant: I am pleased to grant to the holders of Three per Cent. Bonds of the External Debt issued under the Decree of 18th December, 1852 (the documents of the Deferred Debt not included) the eventual right to an increase of Capital: the right to an increase of such Bonds, to which they are to correspond—and to take place only under the hypothesis mentioned in said article. The Board of Public Credit will cause to be signed by the Financial Agency in London the Certificates alluded to in the present Decree, which are to be signed by the Councillor Guterres Candido of the War Department, and will have it thus method, and cause the same to be carried into effect. *Palace at Cintra on the 28th July, 1856.*" THE KING.

"Countersigned, JOSÉ JORGE LOUREIRO."

---

I, the Financial Agent of the Portuguese Government in London, do hereby certify that the holder of the Bond Letter B, No. 6944..........., of £100, issued under the Decree of 18th December, 1852, will be entitled to receive an additional annual interest at the rate of one per cent. per annum, or any smaller sum, upon the capital of said Bond, in terms of and in the case provided for in Article 2nd of the Law of 26th July, 1866, transcribed below, and of the agreement entered into in London on the 13th December, 1855.

---

We, Dom Pedro, by the grace of God, King of Portugal, and Algarves, make known to all our subjects that the Cortes have decreed, and we have sanctioned the following law:—

**ART. 1st.**

title: Royal Serbian Government Tobacco-Monopoly Prize Loan Bond, Belgrade, 1888
description: This printed prize-loan bond (Prämien-Obligation / Обвезница са догоцима / Obligation à prime) is a bearer obligation of the Royal Serbian Government for ten gold francs (dinars), one unit of a ten-million-franc gold loan divided into one million such bonds (ten thousand series of one hundred numbers each). Trilingual in German, Serbian and French, it was issued at Belgrade and dated 3/15 June 1888 under the law of 20 April (2 May) 1888, which created a state tobacco monopoly. The text pledges the monopoly's revenues to the loan's interest, amortization and prize draws over sixty-five years, payable at Belgrade and Vienna. Combining a fixed return with periodic lottery premiums, the instrument courted small savers while monetizing a new fiscal resource. Its dense Gründerzeit border of medallions and arabesques frames the obligation in the ornate register of late-nineteenth-century European state finance.
creator: Kingdom of Serbia, Finance Ministry
notes: Kingdom of Serbia Prize Loan (Prämien-Anleihe). Trilingual German/Serbian/French. 10 gold francs/dinars. Series 1922, No. 35. Signed by Serbian Royal Finance Ministry.
