### Main bond — body text (three parallel columns)

**German column (left):**
Diese Obligation bildet einen Theil des seitens der königl. Serbischen Regierung auf Grund des Gesetzes vom 20. April (2. Mai) 1888 zum Zwecke der Geldbeschaffung für die Ablösung des Tabak-Monopols und für den Betrieb desselben emit[tirten Prämien-Anlehens].

Die königl. Serbische Regierung verpflichtet [sich], die auf diese Prämien-Obligation laut umstehend abgedruckte[m] plan entfallende Prämie baar in Gold auszuzalen und dieselbe in Gemässheit des gleichfalls beigedruck[ten] planes innerhalb 65 Jahren zu tilgen.

Die zur Tilgung gezogene Obligation nimmt [nicht mehr] an den Prämien-Ziehungen Theil. — Dem Inhaber [dieser Oblig]ation wird desshalb der, dieser Obligation anhaf[tende] Coupon zurückgestellt.

Die mit einer Prämie gezogene Obligation [nimmt an den fol]genden Prämien-Ziehungen nicht weiter mit; sie [nimmt] auch fernerhin an den Tilgungs-Ziehungen Theil.

Die Zalung der zur Tilgung gezogenen Ob[ligationen und] Prämien erfolgt seitens der königl. Serbischen [Staatscassa binnen ...] Wochen nach der betreffenden Ziehung.

Zur Sicherstellung des gegenwärtigen Prämie[n-Anlehens von Zehn] Millionen Francs (Dinars) hat die königl. Serbi[sche Regierung] im Sinne des Gesetzes vom 20. April (2. Mai) 188[8 das Tabak-Mono]pol im Königreiche Serbien als Pfand best[ellt und wird] daher dessen Erträgnisse nach Deckung der für [die Verzinsung] und Amortisation des Renten-Anlehens vom Jahre [1885 erforder]lichen Annuität, vor Allem zur planmässigen Auszal[ung ...] und zur planmässigen Tilgung dieses Prämien[-Anlehens ver]wenden.

Die für den Dienst der Verzinsung und der Am[ortisation des] Tabak-Renten-Anlehens vom Jahre 1885 errichtete [Central-]Cassa wird auch den Dienst dieses Prämien-Anleh[ens versehen].

Die Auszalung der gezogenen Obligationen un[d Prämien erfolg]t in Belgrad bei den königl. Serbischen Staats[cassen sowie] in Wien und an etwaigen anderen Plätzen bei den v[on der königl. Serbi]schen Regierung jeweilig bekannt zu gebende[n Stellen].

Die Prämien- sowie die Tilgungsbeträge sin[d von jeder] wie immer gearteten gegenwärtigen oder zukünftigen [Abgabe oder] Steuer in Serbien befreit; die königl. Serbische Regie[rung ver]pflichtet [sich], die Prämien- und Tilgungsquoten [während der ganzen Tilgungsdauer ohne Abzug] auszuzalen.
BELGRAD, am 3./15. Juni 1888.
Der königl. Serbische Finanz-Min[ister]:
*[signature]* M. Rakitch

**Serbian Cyrillic column (centre)** *(partly under the eagle underprint):*
Ова је обвезница део зајма, који је узела српска краљевска влада на основу закона од 20. априла (2. маја) 1888. године, ради откупа и даљег вршења Дуванског Монопола.

Српска краљевска влада обвезује се, да ће исплатити у готову и у злату погодак, који буде припао овој обвезници према плану о вучењу, који је на другој страни, и да ће поред тога још и обвезницу исплатити у року од 65 година, према приложеном плану за исплаћивање.

Обвезница, која се извуче на исплату учествује и даље у вучењу за поготке. Имаоцу извучене обвезнице ради исплате вратиће се с тога купон за поготке, који је придружен овој обвезници.

Обвезница, која је извучена с каквим поготком не учествује у потоњим вучењима за поготке, али вреди и даље при вучењима ради исплате.

Поготке и за исплату извучене обвезнице исплаћиваће српска влада четири недеље после дотичног вучења.

За обезбеђење овога зајма на десет милиона динара, залаже српска краљевска влада Дувански Монопол у Краљевини Србији, према закону од 20. априла (2. маја) 1888.

С тога ће се приход од монопола првенствено употребљавати за исплату погодака и извучених обвезница, према плановима за исплату погодака и извучених обвезница, и то пошто се намири годишња потреба за плаћање интереса и отплате Дуванске Ренте од 1885. године.

Каса, која је установљена да врши исплату интереса и амортизацију Дуванске Ренте од 1885. године, примиће [се] да врши исту службу и овоме зајму.

Исплата извучених обвезница и погодака вршиће се у Београду на Главној државној благајници, као и у Бечу и на другим местима, које ће означити према потреби, краљевска српска влада.

Погоци и суме потребне за исплату обвезница биће ослобођени од свих садашњих и будућих такса и пореза у Србији; с тога ће бити дужна краљевска српска влада, да исплаћује за цело време отплаћивања овога зајма на поготке, све поготке и амортизационе суме, без икаквог одбитка.
У БЕОГРАДУ 3/15. Јуна 1888. год.
Министар финансије,
*[signature]* М. Ракић

**French column (right):**
Cette Obligation fait partie intégrante de l'emprunt contracté par le Gouvernement Royal de Serbie aux termes de la loi du 20 Avril (2 Mai) 1888 en vue du rachat et de l'exploitation du monopole des Tabacs.

Le Gouvernement Royal de Serbie s'engage à payer au comptant et en or la prime échéant à cette Obligation d'après le plan des tirages ci-contre et de la rembourser en outre conformément au plan d'amortissement également ci-contre dans le délai de 65 ans.

L'Obligation sortie au tirage d'amortissement prend part dans la suite aux tirages à primes; à cet effet le talon de l'Obligation amortie sera remis au porteur de cette dernière.

L'Obligation sortie avec prime ne concourra plus aux tirages des primes, mais continuera à participer aux tirages d'amortissement.

Le payement par le Gouvernement Royal de Serbie des Obligations sorties au remboursement et des primes aura lieu quatre semaines après les tirages y relatifs.

En nantissement du présent emprunt de Dix Millions de Francs (dinars) le Gouvernement Royal de Serbie a engagé dans le sens de la loi du 20 Avril (2 Mai) 1888 le monopole des Tabacs dans le Royaume de Serbie, dont le revenu est avant tout destiné au payement des primes et Obligations à amortir d'après les plans ci-annexés, après déduction toutefois de l'annuité pour le service de l'emprunt d'état de l'année 1885.

La caisse de l'emprunt des Tabacs établie pour le service de l'emprunt de la rente des Tabacs de l'année 1885 sera également chargée du service de cet emprunt à primes.

Le payement des primes et des Obligations amorties a lieu à Belgrade aux caisses de l'état du Royaume de Serbie, ainsi qu'à Vienne et dans les autres places éventuelles aux établissements à désigner chaque fois par le Gouvernement Royal de Serbie.

Les primes ainsi que les montants d'amortissement sont francs de tous droits et impôts présents et à venir dans le Royaume de Serbie; le Gouvernement Royal Serbe est donc tenu à payer les primes et sommes à rembourser sans aucune déduction pendant toute la durée de l'emprunt.
BELGRADE, le 3/15 Juin 1888.
Le Ministre des finances du Royaume de Serbie:
*[signature]* M. Rakitch

**Printer's imprint (bottom centre):** WIEN, „STEYERMÜHL".

---

## English Translation:

**Series 1922 · No. 35**

**Premium loan of the Royal Serbian Government** of Ten Million Francs (Dinars) in gold, divided into One Million Premium Obligations of Ten Francs (Dinars) each (ten thousand series of one hundred numbers), issued under the law of 20 April (2 May) 1888.

**PREMIUM OBLIGATION** to bearer for **Ten Francs (Dinars) in gold — 10.**

**Body:**

This Obligation forms part of the loan raised by the Royal Serbian Government under the law of 20 April (2 May) 1888 for the purpose of raising money for the redemption of the Tobacco Monopoly and for its operation.

The Royal Serbian Government undertakes to pay in cash and in gold the premium falling to this Obligation according to the drawing plan printed overleaf, and to redeem the Obligation within 65 years in accordance with the likewise appended amortization plan.

An Obligation drawn for redemption no longer takes part in the premium drawings; the coupon attached to the Obligation is therefore returned to the holder for payment.

An Obligation drawn with a premium no longer competes in the subsequent premium drawings, but continues to participate in the amortization drawings.

Payment of the Obligations drawn for redemption and of the premiums is made by the Royal Serbian Government four weeks after the relevant drawing.

As security for the present premium loan of Ten Million Francs (Dinars), the Royal Serbian Government has, under the law of 20 April (2 May) 1888, pledged the Tobacco Monopoly in the Kingdom of Serbia. Its revenues will therefore be applied first of all to the payment of the premiums and the drawn Obligations according to the plans, after deduction, however, of the annuity required for the service of the interest and amortization of the Tobacco Rente (state loan) of the year 1885.

The fund established to service the interest and amortization of the Tobacco Rente loan of 1885 will also handle the service of this premium loan.

Payment of the drawn Obligations and premiums takes place at Belgrade at the caisses of the state of the Kingdom of Serbia, as well as at Vienna and at any other places to be designated from time to time by the Royal Serbian Government.

The premiums as well as the amortization amounts are free of all present and future duties and taxes in the Kingdom of Serbia; the Royal Serbian Government is therefore bound to pay the premiums and sums to be repaid without any deduction throughout the entire term of the loan.

Belgrade, 3/15 June 1888. — The Minister of Finance of the Kingdom of Serbia: *[signed]* M. Rakić

**Detachable coupon:** Premium Coupon / Coupon for prizes — [relating to the loan] raised by the Royal Serbian Government under the law of 20 April (2 May) 1888 for the redemption and operation of the Tobacco Monopoly. The premium will be paid to the holder of this coupon upon a favourable drawing, against surrender of the coupon; the right to a premium whose payment is not claimed within five years lapses by prescription. Belgrade, 3/15 June 1888. — The Minister of Finance: M. Rakić

*(Austrian revenue stamp: 7 Kreuzer, Imperial-Royal Austrian stamp duty, cancelled Vienna. Printer: Vienna, "Steyermühl.")*

title: Adelsverein Priority Bond
description: This printed Prioritäts-Obligation (priority bond) of 500 Gulden, reckoned in the 24½-Gulden standard and bearing interest at four per cent (zu vier vom Hundert), was issued by the Verein zum Schutze deutscher Einwanderer in Texas — the Adelsverein, or association of German nobles — at Wiesbaden on 1 July 1850. The bearer holds a 500-Gulden share of a 1,600,000-Gulden total, ranking ahead of the ordinary Stamm-Actien upon the Verein's assets, under statutes approved by the Herzoglich Nassau'sche Landes-Regierung (Biebrich, 23 July; statutes of 16 October 1847). Signed by Das Comite. The instrument is a salvage measure: the Adelsverein's costly project of settling thousands of German emigrants in Texas, founding New Braunfels and Fredericksburg, had left it heavily encumbered, and this senior, interest-bearing claim was meant to attract fresh capital while subordinating the founders' own shares. The sheet is wholly typographic, its authority resting on Fraktur display type rather than emblem or vignette.
creator: Verein zum Schutze deutscher Einwanderer in Texas (Adelsverein)
notes: Verein zum Schutze deutscher Einwanderer in Texas (Adelsverein). Priority Bond, 500 gulden at 4%. Wiesbaden, July 1, 1850. Total issue 1,600,000 gulden. Priority over common shares. Chartered by Nassau government. Founded New Braunfels and Fredericksburg, Texas.

Juli 1850 an gemäss §. 26 der am 16. October 1847 von der Herzoglich Nassau'schen Landes-Regierung genehmigten Vereins-Statuten, d. d. Biebrich, 23. Juli 1847 die Priorität vor den Stamm-Actien an dem Vereinsvermögen nach Maassgabe des beigedruckten Reglementes zusteht.

title: French Directory Promesse de mandat territorial, 25 francs, 1796
description: This printed note is a Promesse de mandat territorial — a promise of a territorial mandate — for twenty-five francs, issued by the Trésorerie Nationale of the French Republic and created, as its text declares, by the law of 28 Ventôse Year 4 (18 March 1796). Bearing the signatures of Bugarel and Chevallier and the notation "Vu au Contrôle," it circulated in 1796 under the Directory as a stopgap before the full mandats territoriaux were printed. The mandats were meant to supplant the collapsing assignats, the land-backed paper drawn on confiscated biens nationaux; like them they were nominally redeemable in national property, and like them they depreciated to near nothing within months. The note's restraint is telling: an engraved double frame and the small vignette of a seated figure with the legend "NATIONALE" lend the authority of the state to paper whose value the public already doubted, a faith in the printed promise that the Revolution's monetary experiments repeatedly tested.
creator: Trésorerie Nationale (French Republic)
notes: Trésorerie Nationale. Promesse de Mandat Territorial, 25 francs. No. 19891, Série 30. Law of 28 Ventôse An IV (March 18, 1796). Signed by Mugaret and Chevallier. French Revolutionary paper currency.

title: Reconstitution of French Royal 3% Rentes, Paris, 1763
description: A notarial deed of reconstitution of rentes, executed before the king's counsellors and notaries at the Châtelet de Paris and dated 20 July 1763. Printed on a partly engraved form and completed in manuscript, it re-establishes title to 3 percent perpetual and hereditary rentes (rentes effectives) created by the royal Edict of May 1760, registered in the Parlement on the 23rd of that month, which authorized 1,800,000 livres of such annuities. The income was secured on the Droit sur les Cuirs, the duty on tanned leather established by an earlier Edict of August 1759, and on the king's other revenues. Issued amid the ruinous costs of the Seven Years' War, it shows the crown borrowing against an earmarked excise, while the reconstitution form attests to a paper market in which lost titles could be juridically restored without extinguishing the claim. A small royal arms vignette heads the sheet; the engraving stays plain and administrative, subordinating ornament to legal force.
creator: France. Trésor Royal (Royal Treasury)
notes: Reconstitution de Rentes à 3 Pour Cent. Royal French annuity certificate. Paris, July 20, 1763. Secured on royal leather tax (Denier sur les Cuirs, Edit d'Avril 1759) and other royal revenues. Executed before notaries at the Châtelet de Paris. Seven Years' War era fiscal debt.
transcription:
# RECONSTITUTION DE RENTES À 3 POUR CENT

**Original:**

20; Juillet, 1763.

[Coat of Arms - Royal Seal]

RECONSTITUTION DE RENTES À 3 POUR CENT, créées par Édit du mois de Mai 1760, sur les deniers provenans du Droit sur les Cuirs, établi par autre Édit du mois d'Avril 1759, & sur les autres Revenus du Roi.

N° [number unclear] 4283

PARDEVANT les Confeilers du Roi, Notaires, Garde-notes & Gardes-scel de Sa Majesté au Châtelet de Paris, soulsignés, furent prêsens M. [several handwritten names - partially illegible: "Janneau? d'Orles Vendaux?" and others]

Tous Commisaires nommés par Sa Majesté, par ses Lettres patentes données à Versailles le pour l'exécution de l'Édit du mois de mai 1760, ci-après énoncé.

LESQUELS ont dit que le Roi, par son Édit du mois de mai 1760, enregistré au Parlement le 23 [handwritten: "Jambarde? pou"] du même mois, a créé & émis trois millions livres de rentes effectives & héréditaires à trois pour cent, [handwritten notation], à les avoir à prendre par privilège & préférence sur les deniers provenans du Droit sur Sa Majesté [handwritten] sur les Cuirs établi par l'Édit d'Avril 1759, enregistré au Parlement le 11.2 [handwritten: "payms sim Penfre"] septembre suivant, & qu'Elle s'pécialement & par privilège affectés, obligés & hypothéqués, ensembles [handwritten] tous les autres revenus, tant au payement des arrrérages desdites rentes qu'au remboursement des capitaux [handwritten notations] de la manière ci-après énoncée, après notamment fait la facilité dudit droit formé & delicences au payement des arrérages & au rembourcement des capitaux des liquidations des Offices supprimés par ledit Édit d'aoît, conformément à l'article 111 dudit Édit qui seroit executé de la forme & teneur.

Que lesdites rentes feroient vendues & aliénées par lesdits feigneurs Commisaires, sur les quitances [handwritten] du leur Garde du Trésor royal, & les contrats de constitution desdites rentes conséquence par les Notaires que les Acquéreurs voudroient choisir, lesquelles rentes feroient exemptes de la retenue du [handwritten: "trait dit pours"]; Deux tiers pour livre de Droitun, & de toutes autres impositions générallement quelconques, sous quelque dénomination que pu être.

Que chaque constitution particulière desdites rentes ne pourroit être moindre de mille livres de capital, pour faire levées livres de rente, dont les arrérages commenceroient à courir du jour dudit Acquereurs, du premier jour du quartier courant de la date desdites quitances de finance, & feroient payés de six en six mois sur les premiers jours des mois de Janvier & de Juillet de chaque année, par les simples quitances des Rentiers, à commencée du premier Janvier 1761.

Qu'il faudroit annuellement fait fondés dans l'État de la régie dudit Droit sur les Cuirs & des autres revenus de Sa Majesté, tant que lesdites rentes subsisteroient en tout ou en partie, de la somme de trois millions de livres, qui seroit annuellement remise par les Régisseurs ou Fermiers desdits droits, entre les mains des Gardes du Trésor royal, chacun en l'année de son exercice, pour etre employée au payement des arrérages & au remboursement des capitaux desdites rentes, sans que lesdits payts puissent être distraits ou entranchés au l'entendre reimbourse desdites rentes, sans précédent du prélèvement ci-dffus mentionné.

Que les capitaux desdites rentes feroient remboursés en derniers compans, à raison de la forme de douce cents mille livres pour la première année, de pareille forme pour les années suivantes, & par augmentation de la forme à laquelle se trouveroient monter les arrérages des capitaux qui auroient été remboursés durant l'année, en forte qu'il seroit employé tous les trois millions de fonds jusqu'à parfait reimbourrement desdites rentes, sans pouvoir difiaire aucune partie de ce fonds, sous quelque prétexte que ce fût.

Que ce remboursement seroit fait par le Garde du Trésor royal en exercice, aux premiers jours de Janvier & de Juillet de chaque année, par la voie du fort en forme de Loterie, à commencer du premier Janvier 1762, & ainsi de suite de six en six mois, à l'effet de quoi les contrats de constitution desdites rentes feroient numérotés, & les numéros mis publiquement, dans les derniers jours de Juin & de Décembre de chaque année, dans une roue en la grande salle de l'Hôtel de ville de Paris.

---

**English Translation:**

**20 July, 1763**

[Royal Coat of Arms]

**RECONSTITUTION OF 3% ANNUITIES**, created by Edict of the month of May 1760, on the monies derived from the Right on Hides [leather tax], established by another Edict of the month of April 1759, & on the other Revenues of the King.

No. 4283

BEFORE the Councillors of the King, Notaries, Keeper of Records & Keeper of Seals of His Majesty at the Châtelet of Paris, undersigned, appeared M. [names partially illegible]

All Commissioners appointed by His Majesty, by his Letters Patent given at Versailles for the execution of the Edict of the month of May 1760, hereinafter stated.

WHICH [Commissioners] have declared that the King, by his Edict of the month of May 1760, registered in Parliament on the 23rd of the same month, has created & issued three million livres in effective & hereditary annuities at three per cent, to be drawn by privilege & preference on the monies derived from the Right on Hides established by the Edict of April 1759, registered in Parliament on the 12th of the following September, & that it [the Crown] particularly & by privilege affected, obliged & mortgaged, together with all other revenues, both for the payment of arrears of the said annuities & for the reimbursement of the capital, in the manner hereinafter stated, after having made provision for the facility of said right formed of delicts in the payment of arrears & the reimbursement of capitals of the liquidations of Offices suppressed by said Edict of August, in conformity with article 111 of said Edict which would be executed in the form & tenor.

That the said annuities would be sold & alienated by the said Commissioners, on the receipts of their Treasurer of the Royal Treasury, & the contracts of constitution of said annuities by consequence of the Notaries which the Purchasers would wish to choose, which annuities would be exempt from the retention of [amount]; Two thirds per pound of Rights, & from all other impositions generally whatsoever, under whatever denomination it might be.

That each particular constitution of said annuities could not be less than one thousand livres of capital, to produce five livres of income, of which the arrears would commence to run from the day of said Purchase, from the first day of the quarter current of the date of said quitances of finance, & would be paid every six months on the first days of the months of January & July of each year, by simple receipts of the Rentiers, to commence from the first of January 1761.

That it would be necessary to annually establish in the Account of the management of said Right on Hides & of the other revenues of His

title: Reichsbank 1,000-Mark Reichsbanknote, Berlin, 1910
description: This is a one-thousand-mark Reichsbanknote ("Ein Tausend Mark"), a bearer note of the Reichsbank, the central bank of the German Empire. The text promises that the Reichsbank's principal cashier in Berlin will pay the bearer without verification of identity ("ohne Legitimationsprüfung dem Einlieferer dieser Banknote"), dated Berlin, 21 April 1910, and issued under the authority of the Reichsbankdirektorium, whose directors and deputies sign at the foot. As one of the largest denominations in ordinary circulation before 1914, the note belongs to the era of the classical gold standard, when the mark was convertible into gold and such paper functioned as a trusted claim rather than fiat. The design fills the field with dense Fraktur lettering and elaborately interlaced strapwork, flanked by red seals bearing the imperial eagle—an aesthetic of weighty, ornamental officialdom asserting the solidity of the imperial currency.
creator: Reichsbank (German Imperial Bank)
notes: Deutsches Reich Reichsbanknote. 1,000 Marks (Ein Tausend Mark). No. 6125588N. Berlin, April 21, 1910. Reichsbankdirektorium.

title: Reichsbank 1,000-Mark Reichsbanknote, Berlin, 1922
description: This printed banknote is a one-thousand mark (Tausend Mark) Reichsbanknote, issued by the Reichsbankdirektorium and payable by the Reichsbankhauptkasse in Berlin to the bearer. It is dated Berlin, 15 September 1922. A printed notice warns that from 1 January 1923 the note may be called in and exchanged for other legal tender, while a second clause threatens up to two years' imprisonment for counterfeiting or altering it. The issue belongs to the early stage of the German monetary collapse of 1921 to 1923: a thousand marks, once a substantial sum, was already being eroded as the Reichsbank financed government deficits by printing currency. The provisional redemption clause and the absence of figural allegory speak to wartime austerity and haste; the note relies instead on a dense green guilloche field, the bold numeral 1000, and two eagle-bearing seals, where engraved intricacy itself serves as the guarantee of value the design no longer needs to illustrate.
creator: Reichsbank (German Weimar Republic Bank)
notes: Weimar Republic Reichsbanknote. 1,000 Marks (Tausend Mark). Pa 688969, KH. Berlin, September 15, 1922. Hyperinflation era. Note callable from January 1, 1923.

title: Chinese Republic 5% Gold Bond, London, 1925
description: A bilingual French/English bearer bond (au porteur / to bearer) of the République Chinoise, headed "Bon 5% Or 1925 / 5% Gold Bond 1925," for fifty United States gold dollars at five per cent annual interest, dated London, 27 May 1925. Interest of $1.25 was payable half-yearly, the principal redeemable by annual drawings each December. Despite the gold-dollar denomination, the instrument is not a development loan but a settlement security: its text binds it to the Final Protocol of 7 September 1901 and the Chinese Indemnity, with service drawn from revenues of the Chinese Maritime Customs and channelled through the Société Française de Gérance de la Banque Industrielle de Chine, the body managing that bank's affairs after its 1921 failure. The engraved border, customs stamps, and Schröder counting-house imprint frame a sober claim on indemnity revenues rather than any promise of new enterprise.
creator: Government of the Republic of China
notes: République Chinoise 5% Gold Bond 1925. $50 U.S. gold dollars. No. 350,356. London, May 27, 1925.
transcription:
# Transcription of Republic of China 5% Gold Bond of 1925

## Header Text

**PAYMENT OF COUPONS AND OF DRAWN BONDS SHALL ALSO BE MET IN LONDON**
**AT THE COUNTING-HOUSE OF J. HENRY SCHRODER & CO.**

---

## Main Bond Text

**RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE**

**BON 5% OR 1925**
**5% GOLD BOND 1925**

**DE CINQUANTE DOLLARS OU FIFTY**
**FIFTY U.S.A GOLD DOLLARS**

**AU PORTEUR / TO BEARER**

No. 350,356

---

## French Text (Left Column)

**Original:**

Faisant partie d'un maximum de 877,878 Bons
Émis en vertu des Protocoles conclus:
1. Protocole final du 7 Septembre 1901
2. Arrangement du 1 Juillet 1902
3. Obligations de l'Indemnité Chinoise à 5% Or 1901,
signés respectivement par le Ministre des Affaires Étrangères et le Ministre des Finances de la République Chinoise, d'une part, et le Baron de Rothschild, Président de la Banque Industrielle de Chine, d'autre part.

4. Accord du 1 Février 1925
5. Lot Française du 8 Février 1925
6. Accord du 15 Avril 1925

Le présent Bon jouit d'un droit de priorité absolue sur les revenus affectés à l'Indemnité Chinoise, et il produit un intérêt de 5% l'an, payable semestriellement, les 15 Décembre de chaque année et le 15 Janvier suivant.

Le porteur du présent Bon aura droit:
1. Au remboursement de la valeur nominale à raison de Dollars U.S.A., lors de l'amortissement, chaque année, à partir du Janvier 1926 jusqu'à celui de Décembre 1953, ou au jour du tirage s'il est antérieur.

2. À la jouissance d'un intérêt semestriel de 5% l'an.

Le remboursement annuel lieu le 15 Janvier selon le premier tirage se fera le Décembre 1925.

Le présent Bon est transférable.

À New York, en Dollars Or des États-Unis d'Amérique, et à Londres, par l'intermédiaire de la Banque Industrielle de Chine (Établissement Franco-Chinois) et à Shanghai, Saïgon, aux guichets de la Société Générale (Établissement Franco-Chinois) et sur toutes autres places qui pourraient être désignées par cette Société ou du Gouvernement Chinois, les intérêts et le capital seront payés en or ou son équivalent.

La présentation au guichet payeur.

Conformément à l'accord à et suite à l'accord intervenu entre le Gouvernement Français et le Gouvernement Chinois, l'inspecteur Général de la Douane Chinoise, Le Ministre des Affaires Étrangères et le Directeur Général de la Banque Industrielle de Chine (Établissement Franco-Chinois) sont investis des pouvoirs pour la présentation du présent Bon.

Conformément à l'article 3 de l'Arrangement de 1902, le Gouvernement Français a accepté par le Ministre des Affaires Étrangères la responsabilité de la présentation de ce Bon et de la mise en œuvre des mesures nécessaires pour l'encaissement.

Au cas où les revenus affectés à l'Indemnité Chinoise seraient insuffisants pour le paiement des coupons, le Gouvernement Français s'engage à effectuer sur ses revenus affectés à l'Indemnité de 1901 un prélèvement supplémentaire de la difference.

**LONDON, MAY 27, 1925**

---

## English Text (Right Column)

**Translation:**

Being one of a total issue of 877,878 Bonds created in accordance with the following Protocols and Agreements:
1. Final Protocol of September 7th, 1901
2. Agreement of July 1st, 1902
3. Chinese Indemnity 5% Gold Bonds of 1901, signed respectively by the Minister for Foreign Affairs and the Minister of Finance of the Chinese Republic, on the one hand, and by Baron de Rothschild, President of the Banque Industrielle de Chine, on the other hand.

4. Agreement of February 1st, 1925
5. French Law of February 8th, 1925
6. Agreement of April 15th, 1925

The bearer of this Bond is entitled to receive interest at 5% per annum payable half-yearly on December 15th and January 15th of each year, the first coupon falling due on July 15th, 1925.

The bearer shall be entitled to:
1. Repayment of the face value in U.S.A. Gold Dollars at the time of redemption, which shall take place on December 15th of each year, commencing December 15th, 1925, and ending December 15th, 1953, or on the date of drawing should that date be earlier.

2. Receipt of interest at 5% per annum payable half-yearly.

The annual redemption shall commence on December 15th, 1925.

This Bond is transferable.

In New York, in U.S.A. Gold Dollars, at any financial institution to be named by the Banque Industrielle de Chine (Franco-Chinese Establishment).

In London, at the office of J. Henry Schroder & Co.

In Peking, Shanghai, Canton, or in Saigon, at the branches of the Société Générale (Franco-Chinese Establishment) or at such other agencies as may be designated by the Société Générale or by the Chinese Government.

Payment of interest and principal shall be made in gold or its equivalent.

In accordance with the Agreement concluded on April 15th, 1925, between the French Government and the Chinese Government, the Inspector General of the Chinese Maritime Customs, the Minister for Foreign Affairs, and the Director General of the Banque Industrielle de Chine (Franco-Chinese Establishment) are hereby vested with full power for presentation and encashment of this Bond.

In accordance with Article 3 of the Agreement of 1902, the French Government has undertaken through its Minister for Foreign Affairs to assume responsibility for presentation and for taking all necessary steps for collection and payment of interest and principal.

In the event that the revenues pledged for the Indemnity should prove insufficient for the payment of the interest and principal on this Bond, the French Government undertakes to make up the deficit from the revenues pledged for the 1901 Indemnity.

**LONDON, MAY 27, 1925**

---

## Notable Markings:

1. **Serial Number:** No. 350,356
2. **Red Stamp (top right):** Four shillings (British postal/tax marking)
3. **Red Circular Stamp (center-bottom):** "GENERAL / SEAL" with Chinese characters, dated 1925
4. **Handwritten Signature:** "P. Mantoux" (or similar - partially legible) in lower left
5. **Printer Mark (bottom center):** "VIEILLARD" / "IMP. PARIS"
6. **Additional Red Seals and Chinese Characters:** Multiple official seals throughout the lower portion of the document
7. **Decorative Border:** Ornate Art Deco style border in brown/gold with Chinese characters at bottom corners (left: 民國十四年 - Republic Year 14; right: 民國十四年 - Republic Year 14)
8. **Company/Bank Name:** "J. HENRY SCHRODER & CO." prominently displayed at top

title: Chinese Republic Lung-Tsing-U-Hai Railway Bond, Brussels, 1921
description: This printed bearer treasury bond (bon du trésor) bears a face value of 500 francs and an 8% annual yield, one of 100,000 such bonds making up a loan of 50,000,000 francs floated by the Government of the Chinese Republic to finance the Lung-Tsing-U-Hai Railway (隴秦豫海鐵路). Issued at Brussels and dated 1 July 1921, it was placed through the Belgian-domiciled Compagnie Générale de Chemins de Fer et de Tramways en Chine and bears the seals of the Chinese legation and a Chinese-character endorsement at lower right. The bond documents China's reliance on European money markets, here Belgian francs, to fund an ambitious east-west trunk line across Gansu, Shaanxi, Henan and Jiangsu. Its restrained green guilloche border and engraved classical lettering project the sober probity of European rentier finance rather than any Chinese ornamental vocabulary, signalling that the paper was made to reassure Continental investors.
creator: Government of the Republic of China
notes: République Chinoise 8% Lung-Tsing-U-Hai Railway (隴秦豫海鐵路) Treasury Bond 1921. 500 francs. No. 012918. Brussels, July 1, 1921. Total issue 50,000,000 francs / 100,000 bonds. Compagnie Générale de Chemins de Fer et Tramways en Chine.
transcription:
# Government of the Chinese Republic — 8% Treasury Bond of 1921, Lung-Tsing-U-Haï Railway (500 Francs) — Transcription

## Original:

**GOUVERNEMENT DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE**

**BON DU TRÉSOR 8% DE 1921**

**CHEMIN DE FER LUNG-TSING-U-HAÏ**

**FRANCS 50,000,000**

**DIVISÉS EN 100,000 BONS DE 500 FRANCS**

(Printed body paragraph, upper block:)

CES BONS SONT ÉMIS PAR LES SOINS DE LA COMPAGNIE GÉNÉRALE DE CHEMINS DE FER ET DE TRAMWAYS EN CHINE EN VERTU D'UNE CONVENTION CONCLUE LE 1er MAI 1920 ENTRE LE GOUVERNEMENT DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE, LA COMPAGNIE GÉNÉRALE DE CHEMINS DE FER ET DE TRAMWAYS EN CHINE ET LE NEDERLANDSCH SYNDICAAT VOOR CHINA. CETTE CONVENTION A ÉTÉ NOTIFIÉE PAR LE MINISTRE DES AFFAIRES ÉTRANGÈRES DE CHINE AUX LÉGATIONS DE BELGIQUE, DE FRANCE ET DE HOLLANDE A PÉKIN.

LE PAIEMENT DES INTÉRÊTS ET LE REMBOURSEMENT DU CAPITAL CONSTITUENT UNE OBLIGATION DIRECTE DU GOUVERNEMENT DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE. ILS SONT EN OUTRE GARANTIS PAR UNE HYPOTHÈQUE DE PREMIER RANG SUR LE CHEMIN DE FER DU KANSUH A LA MER (LUNG-TSING-U-HAÏ), LE MATÉRIEL FIXE ET ROULANT, LES DÉPENDANCES ET LES PRODUITS.

UNE SOMME SUFFISANTE SERA CONSIGNÉE ENTRE LES MAINS DE LA COMPAGNIE GÉNÉRALE DE CHEMINS DE FER ET DE TRAMWAYS EN CHINE POUR ASSURER LE SERVICE FINANCIER DES BONS JUSQU'AU 1er JUILLET 1922.

**BON DE 500 FRANCS**

**No 012913**

(Engraved cursive body:)

Le porteur du présent Bon a le droit de recevoir du Gouvernement de la République Chinoise la somme de Frs. 500.– le 1er Juillet 1931, à moins de remboursement antérieur, et les intérêts sur cette somme au taux de huit pour cent (8%) par an.

Les intérêts seront payables le 1er Janvier et le 1er Juillet de chaque année contre remise du coupon correspondant ci-attaché.

Le capital sera payable contre remise du titre muni de tous les coupons non échus.

Les Bons sont remboursables au pair à raison de 20.000 Bons chaque année, à partir du 1er Juillet 1927, de façon que le remboursement intégral soit terminé le 1er Juillet 1931. Les titres à rembourser seront déterminés par un tirage au sort qui sera fait par les soins de la Compagnie Générale de Chemins de fer et de Tramways en Chine.

Les intérêts sur le présent Bon cesseront de courir à partir de la date où le capital en sera remboursable et où la provision aura été faite pour son remboursement.

Les Bons remboursables et les coupons échus sont payables à Bruxelles en monnaie ayant cours légal.

Tous les titres, tous les coupons et tous les paiements faits ou reçus se rapportant au service des présents Bons seront exempts de tous impôts et taxes chinois présents ou futurs.

Les coupons échus et les titres remboursables qui n'auraient pas été présentés au paiement dans un délai de 30 ans à dater de leur exigibilité, seront sans valeur.

Ce Bon est émis au nom du Gouvernement Chinois par la Compagnie Générale de Chemins de fer et de Tramways en Chine, en foi de quoi le Ministre de Chine à Bruxelles a fait apposer ci-dessous le fac-similé de son sceau officiel et le facsimile de sa signature et la Compagnie Générale de Chemins de fer et de Tramways en Chine, a fait apposer la signature d'un de ses Délégués.

(Signature area, three blocks:)

**LE MINISTRE DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE A BRUXELLES**
[handwritten signature; red square Chinese seal / chop at left]

Bruxelles, le 1er Juillet 1921.
**LA COMPAGNIE GÉNÉRALE DE CHEMINS DE FER ET DE TRAMWAYS EN CHINE**
**Le Délégué :**
[handwritten signature]

**LE DIRECTEUR GÉNÉRAL DU CHEMIN DE FER LUNG-TSING-U-HAÏ**
[handwritten signature; large red Chinese seal / chop at right]

(Overprints: blue circular stamp "BELGIQUE — 5 F." at upper left; two brown/black circular Antwerp stamps at upper right; pink/red control overprints across the face.)

(Bottom left imprint:) J. VERSCHUEREN – ANVERS-BRUXELLES

(Bottom right:) Dessin déposé.

---

## English Translation:

**GOVERNMENT OF THE CHINESE REPUBLIC**

**8% TREASURY BOND OF 1921**

**LUNG-TSING-U-HAÏ RAILWAY**

**50,000,000 FRANCS**

**DIVIDED INTO 100,000 BONDS OF 500 FRANCS**

These bonds are issued through the agency of the Compagnie Générale de Chemins de Fer et de Tramways en Chine (General Company of Railways and Tramways in China) by virtue of an agreement concluded on 1 May 1920 between the Government of the Chinese Republic, the Compagnie Générale de Chemins de Fer et de Tramways en Chine, and the Nederlandsch Syndicaat voor China. This agreement was notified by China's Minister of Foreign Affairs to the Legations of Belgium, France, and Holland at Peking.

The payment of the interest and the repayment of the capital constitute a direct obligation of the Government of the Chinese Republic. They are further guaranteed by a first-rank mortgage on the railway from Kansuh to the Sea (Lung-Tsing-U-Haï), its fixed and rolling stock, its appurtenances, and its products.

A sufficient sum shall be deposited into the hands of the Compagnie Générale de Chemins de Fer et de Tramways en Chine to ensure the financial service of the bonds until 1 July 1922.

**BOND FOR 500 FRANCS**

**No. 012913**

The bearer of the present Bond has the right to receive from the Government of the Chinese Republic the sum of Frs. 500.– on 1 July 1931, unless redeemed earlier, together with interest on that sum at the rate of eight per cent (8%) per annum.

The interest shall be payable on 1 January and 1 July of each year against surrender of the corresponding coupon attached hereto.

The capital shall be payable against surrender of the certificate furnished with all unexpired coupons.

The Bonds are redeemable at par at the rate of 20,000 Bonds each year, beginning 1 July 1927, so that full redemption shall be completed by 1 July 1931. The certificates to be redeemed shall be determined by a drawing by lot carried out through the agency of the Compagnie Générale de Chemins de Fer et de Tramways en Chine.

The interest on the present Bond shall cease to run from the date on which the capital becomes redeemable and on which provision has been made for its repayment.

The redeemable Bonds and the matured coupons are payable at Brussels in legal-tender currency.

All certificates, all coupons, and all payments made or received relating to the service of the present Bonds shall be exempt from all Chinese taxes and duties, present or future.

Matured coupons and redeemable certificates which have not been presented for payment within a period of 30 years from the date they became due shall be void.

This Bond is issued in the name of the Chinese Government by the Compagnie Générale de Chemins de Fer et de Tramways en Chine, in witness whereof the Minister of China at Brussels has caused to be affixed below the facsimile of his official seal and the facsimile of his signature, and the Compagnie Générale de Chemins de Fer et de Tramways en Chine has caused to be affixed the signature of one of its Delegates.

**THE MINISTER OF THE CHINESE REPUBLIC AT BRUSSELS**
[handwritten signature; red square Chinese seal at left]

Brussels, 1 July 1921.

title: Kingdom of Bulgaria State Gold Loan Bond
description: This is a bilingual (Bulgarian and French) bearer bond — Облигация / Obligation — for 500 gold francs of the amortizable 4½% Gold State Loan of the Kingdom of Bulgaria of 1909. The face value is stated as 500 gold francs, equal to 500 Bulgarian gold leva, and rendered in the leading gold-standard currencies of the day: 476 Austrian crowns, 405 Reichsmark, 19.163 pounds sterling, and 240 Dutch florins. The whole loan comprised 200,000 such bonds. Issued at Sofia in 1909, the loan came in the wake of Bulgaria's proclamation of full independence from the Ottoman Empire in 1908, which obliged the young kingdom to settle its inherited tribute and to raise fresh capital abroad. The vignette pairs two women in Bulgarian folk costume bearing roses and grain above a scene of factories and a railway, joining the agrarian wealth of rose-oil and wheat to the promise of industry the loan was meant to finance.
creator: Kingdom of Bulgaria, Finance Ministry
notes: Royaume de Bulgarie 4½% Or State Loan 1909. 500 gold francs = 500 leva zlatni. No. 055,199. 200,000 bonds total. Multiple-currency equivalences stated.
transcription:
# Kingdom of Bulgaria 4½% Amortizable Gold State Loan of 1909 — Bond for 500 Gold Francs — Transcription

## Original:

(top corners, large numerals) **500**  ...  **500**

(vignette across the top: two women in Bulgarian folk costume bearing roses and grain, flanking the crowned Bulgarian coat of arms, above a landscape of factories and a railway)

(red/blue revenue stamp at upper left) **5 K**

(serial band) **✳ ✳ № 055.199 ✳ ✳**

**ЦАРСТВО БЪЛГАРИЯ.**

**Български държавенъ 4½% погасяемъ заемъ въ злато отъ 1909 год.**

издаденъ въ основа на закона отъ 27 ноемврий 1909 година, утвърденъ съ царски указъ № 7 отъ 13 декемврий 1909 година и обнародванъ въ „Държавенъ Вѣстникъ", брой 279 отъ 19 декемврий 1909 година, който законъ потвърждава контракта, сключенъ на 3 декемврий 1909 година н. ст. между Българското Царско Правителство и Винеръ-Банкъ-Ферайнъ въ Виена.

**ПРЕДСТАВЛЯВАНЪ ОТЪ 200.000 ОБЛИГАЦИИ**

за една сума отъ номинални

**100.000.000 лева златни**

= 95.200.000 австрийски корони = 81.000.000 германски марки = 3.960.000 лири стерлинги = 48.000.000 холандски флорини

**ОБЛИГАЦИЯ**

на приносителя отъ

**ПЕТСТОТИНЪ ЛЕВА ЗЛАТНИ**

= 476 австрийски корони = 405 германски марки = 19,16,0 лири стерлинги = 240 холандски флорини.

Настоящия заемъ, който съставлява прѣко задължение за българската държава, е издаденъ:

въ 60.000 отрѣзки № 1— 60.000 по 1 облигация,
„ 10.000 „ № 60.001—110.000 „ 5 облигации,
„ 5.000 „ „ 110.001—160.000 „ 10 „
„ 2.000 „ „ 160.001—200.000 „ 20 „

Българското правителство се задължава до като трае заема да внася ежегодно въ бюджета на държавата потрѣбната сума за изплащане лихвитѣ и излѣзлитѣ въ тиражъ облигации.

Годишната лихва отъ четири и половина на сто (4½%) върху номиналния капиталъ ще се изплаща на двѣ равни части срѣщу шестмѣсечни купони съ падежъ 1 юний и 1 декемврий н. ст. всѣка година.

Падежътъ на първия купонъ е на 1 юний 1910 година н. ст.

(bottom left, large numeral) **500**

---

(right-hand / French column)

**ROYAUME DE BULGARIE.**

**Emprunt amortissable de l'État Bulgare 4½% Or 1909**

créé en vertu de la loi du 27 Novembre 1909 v. s., sanctionnée par l'Oukase Royal No. 7 du 13 Décembre 1909 v. s. et publiée dans le Journal Officiel No. 279 du 19 Décembre 1909 v. s., ratifiant le contrat intervenu à la date du 3 Décembre 1909 n. s. entre le Gouvernement Royal de Bulgarie et le Wiener Bank-Verein de Vienne

**REPRÉSENTÉ PAR 200.000 OBLIGATIONS**

portant sur un capital nominal de

**100.000.000 de Francs or**

= 95.200.000 Couronnes d'Autriche = 81.000.000 de Reichsmark = 3.960.000 Livres Sterling = 48.000.000 de Florins de Hollande

**OBLIGATION**

au porteur de

**CINQ CENTS FRANCS OR**

= 476 Couronnes d'Autriche = 405 Reichsmark = 19,16,0 Livres Sterling = 240 Florins de Hollande.

Le présent Emprunt constituant une dette directe de l'Etat Bulgare est émis:

en 60.000 coupures No. 1— 60.000 à 1 Obligation,
„ 10.000 „ „ 60.001—110.000 „ 5 Obligations,
„ 5.000 „ „ 110.001—160.000 „ 10 „
„ 2.000 „ „ 160.001—200.000 „ 20 „

Le Gouvernement Bulgare s'engage à inscrire tous les ans, et ce, pendant toute la durée de l'Emprunt, au budget de l'Etat la somme nécessaire pour le paiement des intérêts et le remboursement des obligations.

L'intérêt annuel, de quatre et demi pour cent (4½%) du montant nominal, sera payé en deux parties égales contre les coupons semestriels aux échéances des 1er Juin et 1er Décembre n. s. de chaque année.

L'échéance du premier coupon est fixée au 1er Juin 1910 n. s.

(bottom right, large numeral) **500**

(printer's imprint, bottom center) Société anon. d'impr. et libr. anciens R. v. WALDHEIM, JOS EBERLE & Co., Vienne.

(side borders, vertically) **4½%**

## English Translation:

(Serial) ✳ ✳ No. 055.199 ✳ ✳

**KINGDOM OF BULGARIA.**

**Bulgarian State 4½% Amortizable Gold Loan of 1909**

issued on the basis of the law of 27 November 1909, confirmed by Royal Decree (Ukase) No. 7 of 13 December 1909 and promulgated in the "State Gazette" (Darzhaven Vestnik), issue No. 279 of 19 December 1909, which law ratifies the contract concluded on 3 December 1909 (New Style) between the Bulgarian Royal Government and the Wiener Bank-Verein of Vienna.

**REPRESENTED BY 200,000 BONDS**

for a total nominal sum of

**100,000,000 gold leva**

= 95,200,000 Austrian crowns = 81,000,000 German marks = 3,960,000 pounds sterling = 48,000,000 Dutch florins

**BOND**

to the bearer, for

**FIVE HUNDRED GOLD LEVA** (Bulgarian) / **FIVE HUNDRED GOLD FRANCS** (French)

= 476 Austrian crowns = 405 German marks (Reichsmark) = £19 16s 0d sterling = 240 Dutch florins.

The present loan, which constitutes a direct obligation of the Bulgarian State, is issued:

in 60,000 coupures Nos. 1–60,000 of 1 bond each,
10,000 coupures Nos. 60,001–110,000 of 5 bonds,
5,000 coupures Nos. 110,001–160,000 of 10 bonds,
2,000 coupures Nos. 160,001–200,000 of 20 bonds.

The Bulgarian Government undertakes, for the whole duration of the loan, to enter each year in the State budget the sum necessary for the payment of the interest and the redemption of the drawn bonds.

The annual interest of four and one-half per cent (4½%) on the nominal capital shall be paid in two equal parts against semi-annual coupons falling due on 1 June and 1 December (New Style) each year.

The first coupon falls due on 1 June 1910 (New Style).

(Printer's imprint) Joint-stock printing and publishing company, formerly R. v. Waldheim, Jos. Eberle & Co., Vienna.

(Marginal) 4½%

Romanization of key names: Царство България = Tsarstvo Bulgaria (Kingdom of Bulgaria); Държавенъ Вѣстникъ = Darzhaven Vestnik (State Gazette); Винеръ-Банкъ-Ферайнъ = Wiener Bank-Verein; лева златни = leva zlatni (gold leva); петстотинъ = petstotin (five hundred).

Note: The per-bond sterling value is printed as "19,16,0" (i.e. £19 16s 0d in the £/s/d convention); the catalogue's "19.163 pounds" is a decimal approximation of this.

title: Russian General Oil Corporation Share Warrant to Bearer, London, 1912
description: A printed share warrant to bearer of The Russian General Oil Corporation (Société Générale Naphthifère Russe) Limited, entitling its holder to one fully paid share of £1 sterling. The text is set trilingually in English, French and Russian, and the capital is stated as £2,500,000 in 2,500,000 shares of £1 each. It was issued under the company's common seal in London and dated 1 October 1912. The corporation was registered in England to hold and work petroleum properties in the Russian Empire, one of many British vehicles channelling sterling capital into the Baku and Caucasus oilfields in the years before the First World War; the three languages address an investor base spanning London, Paris and Russia itself. The seated allegorical woman holding a caduceus, emblem of commerce, set against shipping and industry, frames the venture in the conventional grammar of prosperity. Later stamps record the company's voluntary liquidation (1949), a 1952 winding-up order, and a return of capital to warrant-holders.

title: Imperial Russian Government State 4% Rente Certificate, St. Petersburg
description: This printed bearer certificate (свидѣтельство) registers an entry in the State Debt Book for the Imperial Russian Government's State 4% Rente (Государственная 4% Рента), a perpetual government annuity. The face capital is one thousand rubles (тысяча рублей), yielding forty rubles of fixed annual income, and the certificate belongs to a series whose total value is ten million rubles. The subtitle invokes the chain of imperial ukases and Finance Committee resolutions that authorised the loan, from an ukase of 8 April 1894 through the imperially-confirmed resolution of 22 March 1902; the final coupon is dated 1 December 1924. Issued at St. Petersburg under the Commission for the Redemption of State Debts, the rente was modelled on French and British consolidated annuities and marketed at home and abroad as a conservative income security underpinning Russia's gold-standard finances. The engraved frame, in pink and brown with the crowned double eagle, deploys the sinuous, plant-like linework characteristic of turn-of-the-century Art Nouveau, lending sovereign debt an air of stability and modern refinement.
creator: Imperial Russian Government
notes: Imperial Russian Government State 4% Rente (Государственная 4% Рента). Certificate No. 02756. 1,000 rubles (40 rubles annual income). Series 191. Bearer certificate. Art Nouveau design.

долговую книгу, кн. [handwritten, illegible] стр. [handwritten, illegible]

**НА ПРЕДЪЯВИТЕЛЯ.**

(Body text — dense fine print, partially legible; transcribed to best reading:)

Государственная 4% рента выпускается свидѣтельствами въ 100, 200, 500, 1000, 5000 и 25.000 рублей, съ купонами на 10 лѣтъ, по истеченіи каковыхъ свидѣтельства будутъ подлежать обмѣну на новыя свидѣтельства, съ купонами на дальнѣйшее десятилѣтіе. Свидѣтельства распредѣляются на серіи по 10.000.000 рублей въ каждой.

Свидѣтельства ренты приносятъ 4% годоваго дохода, уплачиваемаго, съ удержаніемъ сбора съ доходовъ отъ денежныхъ капиталовъ, 1 Марта, 1 Іюня, 1 Сентября и 1 Декабря.

Государственная 4% рента можетъ быть во всякое время погашена какъ посредствомъ покупки, такъ и путемъ выплаты нарицательнаго капитала всей ренты или отдѣльныхъ ея серій, назначаемыхъ по жребію [...] черезъ 3 мѣсяца по назначеніи серій къ выкупу [выплачивается] нарицательный капиталъ свидѣтельствъ.

При выкупаемыхъ свидѣтельствахъ должны быть всѣ купоны, подлежащіе оплатѣ послѣ срока уплаты капитала. Стоимость недостающихъ купоновъ, за вычетомъ сбора съ доходовъ отъ денежныхъ капиталовъ, удерживается изъ капитала.

Купоны и выкупаемыя свидѣтельства оплачиваются во всѣхъ учрежденіяхъ Государственнаго Банка, а гдѣ таковыхъ нѣтъ — въ Казначействахъ. Оплата за границею [производится въ мѣстахъ, назначенныхъ] Министерствомъ Финансовъ по курсу [...]: 100 рублей — 266,67 франк., — 216 [имп.] герм. марк. — [...] гульд. — 51 [англ.] долл. золотомъ. [частично illegible]

Свидѣтельство ренты выпускается имен[ное и на предъявителя]. [...] допускаются въ свободному обращенію [...] [частично illegible]

Государственною Коммиссіею Погашенія Долговъ [...] на основаніи особыхъ правилъ, утвержденныхъ Министромъ Финансовъ [...] [далѣе перечень условій пунктами 1)…4), фактически нечитаемо / mostly illegible]

The State 4% Rente is issued in certificates of 100, 200, 500, 1,000, 5,000 and 25,000 rubles, with coupons for 10 years, upon the expiry of which the certificates are to be exchanged for new certificates with coupons for a further decade. The certificates are distributed into series of 10,000,000 rubles each.

title: Russian-Norwegian Timber Industry Company Share
description: A bilingual Russian-Norwegian bearer share for 1,000 rubles in the Русско-Норвежское Лесопромышленное Акционерное Общество (Russian-Norwegian Timber Industry Joint-Stock Company / Russisk-Norsk Skogindustri A/S), one part of a subscribed capital of 3,000,000 rubles. The charter was confirmed by imperial sanction (ВЫСОЧАЙШЕ утверждёнъ) on 29 December 1916, and the certificate itself was issued at Petrograd in 1917 to Herr Jørgen Bjaaland. The company was organised to work the forests of the Russian north, where Norwegian capital had long traded in timber. Printed on the eve of revolution, the parallel Cyrillic and Norwegian text and the ruble denomination record a cross-border partnership whose footing in Russian soil would soon be swept away by nationalisation. The restrained green guilloche border and twin-language symmetry project the sober respectability expected of a chartered joint-stock enterprise.

title: City of Vienna Municipal Bond
description: A printed bearer debenture (5%ige Schuldverschreibung lautend auf den Überbringer) for 10,000 kronen, drawn on the larger 5% loan of the federal capital city of Vienna of the year 1921, issued at a total nominal sum of 1,000 million kronen and redeemable within sixty years. The certificate is dated Wien, am 1. März 1921, and bears the signatures of the mayor and the municipal council, among them the finance councillor Hugo Breitner. Issued under the new Austrian republic in the wake of the First World War, the loan let the city raise capital amid the runaway inflation that was eroding the krone, the vast headline figure and long term betraying a currency already losing its footing. An engraved oval panorama of Vienna crowns the sheet, framed by ornamental scrollwork and allegorical figures in a restrained Secessionist manner that ties civic borrowing to municipal pride.
creator: Stadtgemeinde Wien (Municipality of Vienna)
notes: Stadtanleihe Kremnitz (Kremnica Municipal Bond). 5% bond, 10,000 kronen. Serie 000513, No. 011. Austria-Hungary / Kingdom of Hungary. Art Nouveau design with city panorama. Ca. 1910.
transcription:
# City of Vienna 5% Bearer Bond for Ten Thousand Kronen (1921) — Transcription

## Original (German):

(Top corners, in ornamental ovals flanking the engraved Vienna panorama vignette:) **10000**  ...  **10000**

(Beneath the vignette, above the double-headed eagle bearing the Vienna civic shield — white cross on red:)

**Lit. A.**

**Serie 000519**   **Nummer 011**

(Small line of type introducing the issue:)

5%ige Anleihe der Bundeshauptstadt Wien vom Jahre 1921 im Gesamtnennbetrage von 1000 Millionen Kronen, rückzahlbar innerhalb 60 Jahren.

(Large display title:)

**5%ige Schuldverschreibung**
**lautend auf den Überbringer über**
**Zehntausend Kronen.**

(Main body text:)

Diese Schuldverschreibung bildet einen Bestandteil des auf Grund des Beschlusses des Gemeinderates der Bundeshauptstadt Wien vom 18. Jänner 1921, Pr.-Zl. 600, aufgenommenen, mit jährlich 5% verzinslichen, in längstens 60 Jahren rückzahlbaren Anlehens der Stadt Wien im Betrage von 1000 Millionen Kronen. Die Schuldverschreibungen werden in Abschnitten von 10.000, 5000, 2000 und 1000 Kronen ausgegeben. Die Zahlung der Zinsen erfolgt in halbjährigen, am 1. März und 1. September jeden Jahres im nachhinein fälligen Raten an den Überbringer des zu dieser Schuldverschreibung gehörigen jeweils fälligen Zinsscheines gegen Rückstellung des letzteren. Das Anlehen ist durch 10 Jahre, vom Tage der Ausstellung dieser Schuldverschreibung an gerechnet, unkündbar. Vom 1. März 1931 angefangen erfolgt die Rückzahlung durch eine alljährlich am 1. März stattfindende Auslosung der Schuldverschreibungen; diese findet bei der Stadt Wien öffentlich nach Maßgabe des beigedruckten Tilgungsplanes statt. Die Rückzahlung der ausgelosten Schuldverschreibungen erfolgt 6 Monate nach der Auslosung. Die Stadtgemeinde Wien behält sich jedoch vom 1. März 1931 an das Recht vor, in dem einen oder anderen Jahre im Laufe der planmäßigen Tilgungsperiode wann immer auch eine größere Anzahl von Schuldverschreibungen, als nach dem Tilgungsplane entfallen würde, auszulosen oder auch sämtliche noch nicht ausgelöste Schuldverschreibungen mit mindestens dreimonatiger Frist auf einen Couponzahlungstermin aufzukündigen. Die Nummern der ausgelosten Schuldverschreibungen und die Rückzahlungstermine sowie die eventuelle Kündigung werden in der „Wiener Zeitung" kundgemacht. Die Rückzahlung dieser Schuldverschreibung erfolgt an den Überbringer zum vollen Neuwert gegen Rückstellung derselben sowie der bis zum festgesetzten Rückzahlungstermine noch nicht verfallenen Zinsscheine und des Erneuerungsscheines. Mit dem Eintritte des Rückzahlungstermines hört jede weitere Verzinsung der fälligen Schuldverschreibungen auf; es werden daher bei der Einlösung der fehlenden, erst nach diesem Zeitpunkte fälligen Zinsscheine vom Kapitalsbetrage in Abzug gebracht. Sowohl die Rückzahlung der Zinsen als auch die Rückzahlung des Kapitals dieser Schuldverschreibung findet in der durch das Gesetz vom 2. August 1892, R.-G.-Bl. Nr. 126, beziehungsweise durch Vollzugsanweisung des Staatsamtes für Finanzen vom 25. März 1919, St.-G.-Bl. Nr. 191, festgesetzten Kronenwährung oder zum gesetzlichen Umrechnungskurse in einer etwaigen neuen Währung bei der Hauptkasse der Stadt Wien, der Anglo-österreichischen Bank, dem Wiener Bank-Verein, der Allgemeinen österreichischen Boden-Credit-Anstalt, der Österreichischen Credit-Anstalt für Handel und Gewerbe, der Österreichischen Länderbank, der Niederösterreichischen Escompte-Gesellschaft, der Allgemeinen Depositen-Bank, der Bank- und Wechselstuben-Aktien-Gesellschaft „Mercur", der Allgemeinen Verkehrsbank, der Wiener Lombard- und Escompte-Bank und der Zentralbank der deutschen Sparkassen in Wien statt. Bei diesen Stellen erfolgt auch die Einreichung der Erneuerungsscheine zur kostenfreien Behebung der neuen Zinsscheinbogen. Fällige Zinsen verjähren zugunsten der Stadt Wien nach 3, fällige Schuldverschreibungen nach 30 Jahren vom Verfalltage an gerechnet. Von den Schuldverschreibungen und deren Zinsscheinen sind keine Stempel und unmittelbaren Gebühren zu entrichten. Die österreichische Rentensteuer in ihrer gegenwärtigen und zukünftigen Höhe samt deren gegenwärtigen und zukünftigen Zuschlägen übernimmt die Gemeinde Wien mit eigener Zahlung. Die Schuldverschreibungen dieser Anleihe genießen die Mündelsicherheit, sie können somit zur fruchtbringenden Anlegung von Kapitalien der Stiftungen, die unter öffentlicher Aufsicht stehenden Anstalten, des Postsparkassenamtes, dann von Pupillar-, Fideikommiss- und Depositengeldern und zum Börsenkurse — jedoch nicht über den Neuwert — zu Dienst- und Geschäftskautionen verwendet werden. Für die aus dieser Schuldverschreibung hervorgehenden Rechte und Verbindlichkeiten ist der vorstehende deutsche Text allein maßgebend.

Wien, am 1. März 1921.

(Three signature columns beneath the date:)

**Der Vizebürgermeister:**   **Der Bürgermeister:**   **Der Stadtrat:**
(signed) Georg Emmerling   (signed) Jakob Reumann   (signed) Breitner

(Allegorical figures and floral/wave ornament fill the lower border. No printer imprint is clearly legible in the margins. [illegible])

---

## English Translation:

(Top corners:) **10,000** ... **10,000**

**Lit. A.**

**Series 000519**   **Number 011**

(Introductory line:)

5% loan of the Federal Capital City of Vienna of the year 1921, in a total nominal amount of 1,000 million Kronen, repayable within 60 years.

(Title:)

**5% Debenture**
**payable to the bearer, for**
**Ten Thousand Kronen.**

(Body:)

This debenture forms a part of the loan of the City of Vienna, taken up on the basis of the resolution of the Municipal Council of the Federal Capital City of Vienna of 18 January 1921, Presidial No. 600 — bearing interest at 5% per annum and repayable within at most 60 years — in the amount of 1,000 million Kronen. The debentures are issued in denominations of 10,000, 5,000, 2,000 and 1,000 Kronen. Payment of interest is made in half-yearly instalments falling due in arrears on 1 March and 1 September of each year, to the bearer of the respective matured interest coupon belonging to this debenture, against surrender of that coupon. The loan is non-callable for 10 years, reckoned from the day of issue of this debenture. Beginning on 1 March 1931, repayment is effected through a drawing (lottery) of the debentures held annually on 1 March; this takes place publicly at the City of Vienna in accordance with the appended amortization plan. Repayment of the drawn debentures is made 6 months after the drawing. The Municipal Community of Vienna, however, reserves the right, from 1 March 1931 onward, in any one or another year in the course of the scheduled amortization period, at any time to draw a larger number of debentures than would fall due under the amortization plan, or also to give notice of redemption of all not-yet-drawn debentures with at least three months' notice for a coupon payment date. The numbers of the drawn debentures and the repayment dates, as well as any notice of redemption, are announced in the "Wiener Zeitung." Repayment of this debenture is made to the bearer at full face value against surrender of the debenture itself as well as of the interest coupons not yet matured up to the fixed repayment date and of the renewal coupon (talon). Upon the arrival of the repayment date, all further accrual of interest on the matured debentures ceases; accordingly, upon redemption, the missing coupons falling due only after that point in time are deducted from the capital amount. Both the repayment of interest and the repayment of the capital of this debenture take place in the Krone currency established by the Law of 2 August 1892, R.G.Bl. No. 126, respectively by the Enforcement Ordinance of the State Office for Finance of 25 March 1919, St.G.Bl. No. 191, or at the statutory conversion rate into any new currency, at the Central Treasury of the City of Vienna, the Anglo-Austrian Bank, the Wiener Bank-Verein, the General Austrian Land-Credit Institution, the Austrian Credit Institution for Trade and Industry, the Austrian Länderbank, the Lower Austrian Escompte Company, the General Deposit Bank, the Bank and Exchange-Office Joint-Stock Company "Mercur," the General Traffic Bank, the Vienna Lombard and Escompte Bank, and the Central Bank of the German Savings Banks in Vienna. At these offices the renewal coupons may also be submitted for the free collection of the new interest-coupon sheets. Matured interest lapses in favor of the City of Vienna after 3 years, and matured debentures after 30 years, reckoned from the due date. No stamp duties and direct fees are payable on the debentures and their interest coupons. The Austrian income (rent) tax, at its present and future rate together with its present and future surcharges, is assumed by the Community of Vienna at its own cost. The debentures of this loan enjoy trustee-eligibility (Mündelsicherheit); they may therefore be used for the fruitful investment of the capital of foundations, of institutions under public supervision, of the Postal Savings Bank Office, and of ward, entail (fideicommissum) and deposit moneys, and — at the stock-exchange rate, but not above face value — as official and business securities/deposits. For the rights and obligations arising from this debenture, the foregoing German text alone is authoritative.

Vienna, 1 March 1921.

(Signatures:)

**The Vice-Mayor:** (signed) Georg Emmerling   **The Mayor:** (signed) Jakob Reumann   **The Councillor (Stadtrat):** (signed) Breitner

title: Walchensee Loan Bond, Munich, 1923
description: A printed bearer bond (Schuldverschreibung) for ten thousand marks in Reich currency, issued jointly and severally by three Munich electricity utilities — Walchenseewerk A.G., Mittlere Isar A.G. and Bayernwerk A.G. — as the Walchensee-Anleihe. Interest is not fixed but floating: the Reichsbank discount rate less two percent, capped at fifteen and floored at seven percent, with semi-annual coupons from 1 March 1923 and repayment at par beginning 1928. Issued at Munich on 20 February 1923, the loan funded the Walchensee hydroelectric works rising in the Bavarian Alps, underwritten by authorizations of the Bavarian Landtag. The variable, inflation-chasing coupon and the Reich-currency denomination betray issuance at the threshold of the great 1923 inflation, when fixed nominal terms had become untenable. The alpine vignette of lake and powerhouse above ties the security to the engineering it financed.
creator: Walchenseewerk A.G. / Mittlere Isar A.G. / Bayernwerk A.G.
notes: Walchensee-Anleihe. 10,000 marks, min. 7% p.a. Buchstabe E, No. 419133. Munich, February 28, 1922. Issued by Walchenseewerk A.G., Mittlere Isar A.G., Bayernwerk A.G. Finances Walchensee hydroelectric power plant, Bavaria.
transcription:
# Walchensee Loan Bond, Munich, 1923 — Transcription

## Original:

(Vignette at top: engraved alpine landscape of the Walchensee reservoir with mountains and the Walchensee hydroelectric power station.)

**WALCHENSEE-ANLEIHE**

**Anleihe der Aktiengesellschaften**

**WALCHENSEEWERK · MITTLERE ISAR · BAYERNWERK**

Zum jeweiligen Reichsbank-Diskontsatz abzüglich zwei Prozent, jedoch mit höchstens 15 Prozent und mindestens 7 Prozent verzinsliches Anlehen vom Jahre 1923. Vom Jahre 1928 ab zum Nennwerte rückzahlbar.

**10000 M.**          **10000 M.**

**SCHULDVERSCHREIBUNG**

Buchstabe E          über          **№ 419133**

**Zehntausend Mark**

Die Aktiengesellschaften Walchenseewerk, Mittlere Isar und Bayernwerk in München schulden unter samtverbindlicher Haftung dem Inhaber dieser Schuldverschreibung

**ZEHNTAUSEND MARK IN REICHSWÄHRUNG.**

Sie verpflichten sich, diese Summe zum jeweiligen Reichsbank-Diskontsatz abzüglich zwei Prozent für das Jahr, jedoch mit höchstens 15 und mindestens 7 vom Hundert, in halbjährigen — je am 1. März und 1. September — fälligen Teilbeträgen gegen Einreichung der fälligen Zinsscheine zu verzinsen. Die Verzinsung beginnt am 1. März 1923. Die Septemberzinsscheine werden nach Maßgabe des am letzten Tage des vorangegangenen Februar geltend gewesenen Reichsbank-Diskontsatzes, die Märzfälligkeiten unter Zugrundelegung des Reichsbank-Diskontsatzes vom 31. August des vorangegangenen Jahres eingelöst. □ Die Schuldverschreibungen werden vom Jahre 1928 an zum Nennwert im Wege der Auslosung oder Kündigung in dem Ausmaße heimgezahlt, daß die gesamte Anleihe bis zum Jahre 1963 zurückgezahlt ist. Die Auslosungen erfolgen im Monat März zum 1. September eines jeden Jahres; mit diesem Tag endet die Verzinsung der ausgelosten Schuldverschreibungen. Das Ergebnis der Verlosungen wird im Reichsanzeiger und im Bayerischen Staatsanzeiger bekanntgegeben. Vom 1. März 1928 ab können die Schuldner verstärkte Auslosungen vornehmen oder auch die sämtlichen, noch ausstehenden Schuldverschreibungen mit einer Frist von 6 Monaten zur Rückzahlung kündigen. □ Die Zinsscheine, sowie die ausgelosten und gekündigten Schuldverschreibungen werden durch die Bayerische Staatsbank München und die übrigen Niederlassungen der Bayerischen Staatsbank eingelöst. □ Zur Sicherheit der Inhaber der Schuldverschreibungen dient der gegenwärtige und der künftige Besitz der drei Gesellschaften. Die Bayerische Staatsregierung hat auf Grund der ihr vom Bayerischen Landtag mit Beschlüssen vom 28. Januar 1921 und 21. Dezember 1921 erteilten Ermächtigung durch Vertrag vom 1. Februar 1923 die Bürgschaft für Kapital und Zinsen übernommen. □ Das Staatsministerium für Handel, Industrie und Gewerbe hat mit Entschließung vom 31. Januar 1923 die Genehmigung zur Ausgabe der Schuldverschreibungen erteilt. □ Die Schuldverschreibungen sind im gesamten Gebiete des Deutschen Reichs zur Anlegung von Mündelgeldern geeignet. Mit Min.-Bek. vom 3. Februar und 12. April 1921 wurde die Anlegung von Geldern der Gemeinden und Ortschaften, der gemeindlichen und örtlichen Stiftungen, dann der Kultusstiftungen und der Kirchengemeinden in diesen Schuldverschreibungen gestattet.

**MÜNCHEN, den 20. Februar 1923.**

**Walchenseewerk A.G. ·· Mittlere Isar A.G. ·· Bayernwerk A.G.**

Der Vorsitzende des Aufsichtsrates:  ·  Der Vorsitzende des Aufsichtsrates:  ·  Der Vorsitzende des Aufsichtsrates:

*(three signatures)*

Der Vorstand:  ·  Der Vorstand:  ·  Der Vorstand:

*(three signatures)*

(Printer's imprint at bottom margin:) GEBR. PARCUS A.-G. MÜNCHEN

---

## English Translation:

**WALCHENSEE LOAN**

**Loan of the joint-stock companies**

**WALCHENSEEWERK · MITTLERE ISAR · BAYERNWERK**

Loan of the year 1923, bearing interest at the current Reichsbank discount rate less two percent, but at most 15 percent and at least 7 percent. Redeemable at par value from the year 1928 onward.

**10,000 M.          10,000 M.**

**BOND (DEBENTURE)**

Letter E          for          **No. 419133**

**Ten Thousand Mark**

The joint-stock companies Walchenseewerk, Mittlere Isar and Bayernwerk in Munich owe, under joint and several liability, to the bearer of this bond

**TEN THOUSAND MARK IN REICH CURRENCY.**

They undertake to pay interest on this sum at the respective Reichsbank discount rate less two percent per year — but at most 15 and at least 7 percent — in semi-annual installments falling due on 1 March and 1 September, against surrender of the matured interest coupons. Interest begins on 1 March 1923. The September interest coupons are redeemed according to the Reichsbank discount rate in effect on the last day of the preceding February; the March maturities on the basis of the Reichsbank discount rate of 31 August of the preceding year. □ From the year 1928 onward the bonds are repaid at par value by way of drawing (lottery) or call, to such an extent that the entire loan is repaid by the year 1963. The drawings take place in the month of March, effective 1 September of each year; on that day interest on the drawn bonds ceases. The result of the drawings is announced in the Reichsanzeiger and in the Bavarian State Gazette. From 1 March 1928 onward the debtors may carry out increased drawings, or may also call all the still-outstanding bonds for repayment upon 6 months' notice. □ The interest coupons, as well as the drawn and called bonds, are redeemed through the Bavarian State Bank Munich and the other branches of the Bavarian State Bank. □ The present and future property of the three companies serves as security for the holders of the bonds. The Bavarian State Government, on the basis of the authorization granted to it by the Bavarian Landtag by resolutions of 28 January 1921 and 21 December 1921, has by contract of 1 February 1923 assumed the guarantee for principal and interest. □ The State Ministry for Trade, Industry and Commerce granted, by decree of 31 January 1923, the approval to issue the bonds. □ The bonds are suitable throughout the entire territory of the German Reich for the investment of trust/ward funds (Mündelgelder). By ministerial announcements of 3 February and 12 April 1921, the investment of funds of the municipalities and localities, of municipal and local foundations, then of religious foundations and of parish congregations in these bonds was permitted.

**MUNICH, 20 February 1923.**

**Walchenseewerk A.G. ·· Mittlere Isar A.G. ·· Bayernwerk A.G.**

title: Seville–Jerez–Cadiz Railway Company Bearer Share, 1857
description: This printed bearer share (acción al portador / action au porteur) of the Compañía Anónima de los Ferro-Carriles de Sevilla–Jerez–Cadiz, issued bilingually in Spanish and French, represents one participation of 500 francs, stated equivalently as 1,900 reales vellón. It was authorized by Royal Decree of the Queen of Spain dated 4 March 1857. The company's capital is given as 70,000,000 francs (266,000,000 reales vellón), and the society's duration is fixed at ninety-nine years. The instrument belongs to the first generation of Andalusian railway promotion, linking the sherry-producing district of Jerez de la Frontera—rendered in French as Xérès, the source of the wine's name—to the ports of Seville and Cádiz. Its dual-language form, with coupons payable in Madrid and Paris, reflects the heavy reliance of mid-nineteenth-century Spanish railway ventures on French capital. The lithographed border, with restrained scrollwork and corner cartouches, follows the sober commercial idiom of the period.
creator: Compañía del Ferro-Carril de Sevilla–Jerez–Cadiz

title: Shanghai-Nanking Railway Net Profit Sub-Certificate
description: This printed bearer instrument is a Net Profit Sub-Certificate of the Shanghai-Nanking Railway, issued in London by the British & Chinese Corporation, Limited, under its common seal on the second day of December, 1904. It certifies that the holder is entitled, as one of not more than 32,500 such sub-certificates, to a share in the net profits of the line, the benefits being secured by a Declaration of Trust of 10th July, 1904, between the Corporation and the Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation as trustee. A printed note warns that the certificates cannot run beyond 5th July, 1953, or earlier redemption, and that presentation entitles the holder to inspect the trust deed at the Corporation's London office. The British & Chinese Corporation, a vehicle of the Hongkong and Shanghai Bank and Jardine, Matheson & Co., was formed to finance Chinese railway concessions; the document records how the profits of a trunk line through the Yangtze delta were parcelled into transferable claims sold in the London market. The plain ruled border and copperplate scriptwork eschew pictorial ornament, presenting the security as a sober legal title rather than a decorative share.
creator: British & Chinese Corporation Limited
notes: Shanghai-Nanking Railway Net Profit Sub-Certificate. No. E3036. British & Chinese Corporation Limited. Declaration of Trust, July 10, 1904 (with Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation). London, December 2, 1904. One of max. 32,500 sub-certificates.

# Shanghai-Nanking Railway Net Profit Sub-Certificate

**No. E3036**

Shanghai-Nanking Railway

ISSUE by the
BRITISH & CHINESE CORPORATION LIMITED,
of a Series of Net Profit Sub-Certificates.

title: Sherman & Barnsdall Oil Company Stock Certificate (New York)
description: An engraved share certificate of the Sherman & Barnsdall Oil Company, with a capital stock of $750,000 divided into shares of two and 50/100 dollars ($2.50) each, issued at New York on 8 May 1865 and affixed with a twenty-five-cent United States Internal Revenue stamp of the Civil War era. The reverse-of-issue transfer records the stock passing to James H. Arbona from James Irving at New York on 27 June 1868, witnessed by H.C. Brolasky. Sherman & Barnsdall worked the western Pennsylvania oil regions, opened by Edwin Drake's 1859 strike at Titusville, and the certificate belongs to the feverish flotation of petroleum companies that followed the war, when low-par shares spread speculative ownership widely. The vignette renders an oil-field at work—a timber derrick rising among storage tanks, barrels, laboring hands, and a teamster's wagon—advertising the productive enterprise behind the paper.
creator: Sherman & Barnsdall Oil Company
notes: Sherman & Barnsdall Oil Company. Capital $750,000. New York, May 1865. With U.S. Civil War revenue stamp. Transfer from James Irving to James H. Arbona, June 27, 1868, by H.C. Brolasky. Pennsylvania oil boom era.

title: Commercial Bank of Siberia Share
description: A printed bearer share (одна акція) for two hundred fifty rubles in the Сибирскій Торговый Банкъ — the Commercial Bank of Siberia, styled in parallel as Sibirische Handels-Bank and Banque de Commerce de Sibérie — whose statutes are noted as confirmed on 28 June 1872. The certificate is dated at St. Petersburg, 1905, and a later blue oval overstamp marks it "1910 г. ИСПОЛЬЗОВАНО" (redeemed in 1910). Quadrilingual text in Russian, German, French, and English announces the bank's reach toward foreign capital, fitting for an institution channelling money into Siberian and Trans-Baikal trade in the years of the Trans-Siberian Railway's expansion. The engraved frame sets allegorical figures of industry and commerce above a heraldic shield bearing Siberian provincial emblems and a caduceus, the era's conventional sign of mercantile prosperity binding the empire's eastern resources to its capital.

title: Société Anonyme des Verreries d'Extrême Orient Share, Tokyo, 1907
description: This bilingual French and Japanese bearer share certifies title to one fully paid share of one hundred yen in the Société Anonyme des Verreries d'Extrême Orient (Far East Glassworks Company), a joint-stock company (株式會社) capitalised at ¥1,500,000. The French text records that the society was registered under the Japanese Commercial Code on 26 February and 27 March 1907 at the Tokyo registry, and the certificate was delivered under the company's seal on 25 November 1907, signed by two administrators (取締役), one bearing the name L. Millward. A French corporate form operating under Japanese law reflects the foreign capital and management drawn into Japan's expanding glass trade in the late Meiji years. The engraved frame, with its symmetrical pediment, sunburst, and dense scrollwork, follows the ornate Belle Époque convention of European share certificates of the period.
creator: Société Anonyme des Verreries d'Extrême Orient
notes: Société Anonyme des Verreries d'Extrême Orient. Action No. 8339. Capital ¥1,500,000. Incorporated under Japanese jurisdiction, March 1927. Bilingual French/Japanese. Glass manufacturing in the Far East.

title: Société Anonyme Minière des Aïmaks de Touchetoukhan et de Tsetsenkhan en Mongolie — St. Petersburg, 1911
description: A bearer share (акція / action) for fifty roubles in the Акціонерное Общество Руднаго Дѣла Тушетухановскаго и Цеценхановскаго Аймаковъ въ Монголіи — the Société Anonyme Minière des Aïmaks de Touchetoukhan et de Tsetsenkhan en Mongolie — issued at St. Petersburg in 1911 under statutes imperially sanctioned on 13 February 1907. The trilingual face (Russian, French, and a Chinese vertical border reading 蒙古郭爾河等處礦務股票, "Mongolia, Goer river and other places, mining share") registers a joint-stock venture floated to work mineral deposits in the Khalkha aimaks of Tushetu Khan and Tsetsen Khan. Capitalised in Russian roubles yet glossed in Chinese, the certificate maps the commercial reach of St. Petersburg finance into a Mongolia still held under Qing suzerainty on the eve of the 1911 autonomy movement. The restrained typographic frame, devoid of allegory, presents the instrument as a sober financial title rather than a decorative object.

title: Société Atlantique de Réassurances Share, Tangier, 1952
description: This printed bearer share (action de mille francs au porteur, entièrement libérée) represents one fully paid-up share of 1,000 francs in the Société Atlantique de Réassurances, a société anonyme with capital of 270,000,000 francs divided into 270,000 such shares, its registered office at 17, Rue Goya in Tangier. It is dated July 1952. The choice of seat is itself the document's significance: the International Zone of Tangier (1923–1956), administered jointly by several European powers alongside Morocco, levied light taxation and imposed few exchange or company controls, drawing holding and reinsurance ventures that sought a neutral, lightly regulated harbour for capital. The central vignette—a lateen-rigged sailing vessel riding a calm sea, framed by flowering shore—announces the firm's maritime and transatlantic ambitions, the ship serving as a conventional emblem of commerce and risk carried across the ocean. A sheet of twenty coupons for successive dividend claims remains attached.
creator: Société Atlantique de Réassurances
notes: Société Atlantique de Réassurances. Action au porteur, 1,000 francs. No. 69141. Capital 270,000,000 francs / 270,000 shares. Siège social: 17 Rue Goya, Tanger (International Zone). With 18-coupon sheet. Ca. 1952.
