title: Belgian Enterprise Company Share
description: This printed bearer share (action de 500 francs au porteur) belongs to the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine, a société anonyme constituted by notarial act before Maître Pierre De Doncker of Brussels, with its registered office (siège social) in that city. The company's capital stands at 3,000,000 francs, divided into 6,000 shares of 500 francs each; the statutes, the certificate notes, were modified by a later act published in the annexes to the Moniteur Belge. Issued at Brussels on 1 March 1924, the share dates from the years when Belgian capital pursued railway finance and concessions at Tianjin and elsewhere in Republican China. The engraved frame, dense with foliate scrollwork and shaded gothic display lettering, follows the conventional ornamental idiom of Belgian commercial share printing rather than evoking the Chinese venture it underwrites.

title: Bulgarian Red Cross Society Premium Loan Bond, Sofia, 1912
description: This trilingual printed bond — headed Облигация / Obligation / Облигация in Bulgarian, French and Russian — is an interest-free premium (lottery) obligation of 20 gold leva (LEVA-OR 20), issued by the Société Bulgare de la Croix Rouge, the Bulgarian Red Cross Society, under the high patronage of the King of the Bulgarians. It forms one of 300,000 obligations in a 6,000,000 gold-leva loan, divided into 12,000 series of 25 numbers and authorized by royal ukase of 11/24 November 1912. Dated at Sofia, 15/28 November 1912, the issue coincides with the First Balkan War: bearing no interest, it offered subscribers only the chance of cash premiums drawn periodically and amortized over sixty-six years, channeling patriotic charity toward wartime medical relief. The red cross at the base and the engraved scene of soldiers tending the wounded fix the loan's humanitarian purpose, while the trilingual face speaks to Bulgaria's ties to Russia and the wider Red Cross movement. A coupon sheet survives at right.
creator: Българско дружество "Червен Кръст" (Bulgarian Red Cross Society)
notes: Bulgarian Red Cross Society (Дружество "Червен Кръст"). Lottery bond, 20 gold leva. No. 05331. Total loan 4,000,000 gold leva. Trilingual Bulgarian/French/Russian. With coupon sheet.
transcription:
# Bulgarian Red Cross Society Premium (Lottery) Loan Bond of 20 Gold Leva, Sofia 1912 — Transcription

## Original:

*(Trilingual bond — Bulgarian, French and Russian — printed in three parallel columns. Left of the columns: an engraved vignette of nurses and soldiers tending the wounded on a battlefield, with a Red Cross tent-flag, above a large red cross surmounted by a royal crown. Top center: the Bulgarian lion coat of arms under a crown, flanked by interlace medallions and red crosses. A perforated coupon panel runs down the right edge. The outer margins carry repeating vertical border text of the society's name in the three languages: "Българско дружество „Червенъ Кръстъ"" / "Société Bulgare de la „Croix Rouge"" / "Болгарское Общество „Красный Крестъ"".)*

---

### Column 1 — Bulgarian (left)

**ОБЛИГАЦИЯ**

Серия / Série / Серія } **05.331** — **№ 20**

**Българско дружество „ЧЕРВЕНЪ КРЪСТЪ",**
находяще се подъ Височайшето покровителство на Негово Величество Царя на Българитѣ.

**БЕЗЛИХВЕНЪ ЗАЕМЪ СЪ ПРЕМИИ отъ 6,000,000 лева златни,**

прѣдставляванъ отъ 300.000 облигации, по 20 лева златни всѣка една облигация, разпрѣдѣлени на 12.000 серии, съдържащи по 25 номера всѣка една серия. (Височайши указъ отъ 11/24 ноемврий 1912 година, № 16.)

**ОБЛИГАЦИЯ**

*(sub-heading over the value, with the numeral "20" set large in red between the words:)*
**ЛЕВА ЗЛАТНИ 20 ЛЕВА ЗЛАТНИ**

Тая облигация участвува въ тиражитѣ за премиитѣ, съгласно плана за това и ще бѫде изплатена-погасена въ продължение на 75 години, съгласно плана за погашението на тоя заемъ.

Плана за тиража на премиитѣ и таблицата за погашението на заема сѫ отпечатани на отвѣдната страница.

Излѣзлата въ тиражъ облигация и подлежаща на изплащане продължава да участвува въ всичкитѣ послѣдващи тиражи на премиитѣ, за която цѣль тукъ приложения купонъ за премиятѣ се възвръща на носителя на облигацията.

Излѣзлата въ тиражъ съ премия облигация прѣстава понататъкъ да участвува въ тиражитѣ на премиитѣ, а участвува само въ тиражитѣ за погашение на облигациитѣ.

Изплащането на облигациитѣ и премиитѣ е гарантирано отъ българската държава.

Облигациитѣ се освобождаватъ за винаги отъ гербовъ налогъ, а излѣзлитѣ въ тиражъ отъ всѣкакви даньци държавни или общински, настоящи и бѫдещи.

Изплащанията ще ставатъ отъ Българската Народна Банка единъ мѣсецъ слѣдъ тиража.

СОФИЯ, 15/28 ноемврий 1912 година.

représenté par 300.000 obligations de L-or 20 chacune, divisées en 12.000 séries à 25 numéros. (Ukase royal du 11/24 novembre 1912, № 16.)

**OBLIGATION**

**LEVA-OR 20 LEVA-OR** *(with "de" above the numeral)*

La présente Obligation prend part aux tirages des primes établis par le plan y relatif, et a droit, d'après le plan d'amortissement, au remboursement dans une période de soixante-quinze ans.

Les plans de tirage des primes et d'amortissement sont imprimés au verso.

L'Obligation sortie au remboursement (amortissement) continuera à participer aux tirages des primes. A cet effet le Coupon à prime ci-annexé sera restitué au porteur de l'Obligation remboursé.

L'Obligation sortie avec prime ne concourra plus aux tirages des primes, mais continuera à prendre part aux tirages d'amortissement.

Le payement des Obligations et des primes est garanti par l'État Bulgare.

Les Obligations seront exemptes à tout jamais de droits de timbre et celles sorties aux tirages de tous impôts publics ou communaux présents et à venir.

Les payements ont lieu, un mois après les tirages y relatifs, à la Banque Nationale de Bulgarie.

SOFIA, le 15/28 novembre 1912.

состоящій изъ 300.000 облигацій, по 20 левъ зол. каждая, распредѣленныхъ на 12.000 серій, содержащихъ по 25 номеровъ каждая. (Высочайшій указъ отъ 11/24 ноября 1912 года, № 16.)

**ОБЛИГАЦІЯ**

**ЛЕВЪ ЗОЛ. 20 ЛЕВЪ ЗОЛ.**

Эта облигація участвуетъ въ тиражахъ для премій по соотвѣтствующимъ правиламъ и будетъ выплачена и погашена въ теченіе 75 лѣтъ, на основаніи правилъ о погашеніи этого займа.

Планъ тиража для премій и таблицы о погашеніи займа напечатаны на слѣдующей страницѣ.

Облигація, вышедшая въ тиражъ и долженствующая быть выплаченной, продолжаетъ участвовать во всѣхъ послѣдующихъ тиражахъ для премій; для этой цѣли, приложенный къ сему купонъ для преміи возвращается подателю облигаціи.

Облигація, вышедшая въ тиражъ съ преміей, перестаетъ больше участвовать въ тиражахъ для премій, а участвуетъ только въ тиражахъ погашенія облигацій.

Выплата по облигаціямъ и преміямъ гарантирована Болгарскимъ государствомъ.

Облигаціи освобождены навсегда отъ гербоваго сбора, а вышедшія въ тиражъ отъ всѣхъ нынѣ взимаемыхъ, такъ и впредь устанавливаемыхъ государственныхъ и общинскихъ налоговъ и сборовъ.

Выплата производится въ Болгарскомъ Національномъ банкѣ по истеченіи одного мѣсяца со дня тиража.

This bond takes part in the drawings for premiums, in accordance with the relevant plan, and, under the amortization plan, will be repaid and redeemed over a period of seventy-five years, according to the redemption plan of this loan.

The payment of the bonds and of the premiums is guaranteed by the Bulgarian State.

The bonds are forever exempt from stamp duty, and those drawn in the drawings are exempt from all state or municipal taxes, present and future.

title: Société de Lovitch des Produits et Engrais Chimiques (Łowicz) Share, Warsaw 1896
description: This is a printed bearer share (акція / action au porteur) of 250 rubles in the Ловичское Общество Химическихъ Продуктовъ и Землеудобрительныхъ Веществъ — the Société de Lovitch des Produits et Engrais Chimiques, a chemical-products and fertilizer concern at Łowicz in Russian-ruled Congress Poland. Issued against a share capital of 600,000 rubles, its statutes were sanctioned by the Emperor on 9 June 1895, and the document itself was printed at Warsaw (Варшава / Varsovie) in 1896. The bilingual Russian-and-French text addresses both the imperial administration and the Western capital markets on which such ventures depended. Its restrained engraved frame, geometric corner rosettes and disciplined typography eschew allegory, the sober visual grammar of a chartered joint-stock undertaking marketing fertilizers to an agricultural countryside rather than appealing to sentiment.
creator: Société de Lovitch des Produits et Engrais Chimiques
notes: Société de Lovitch des Produits et Engrais Chimiques. 1st emission. Action No. 2106, 250 rubles. Capital 600,000 rubles. Charter approved June 9, 1895. Warsaw. Chemical fertilizers company.
transcription:
# Share of 250 Roubles — Société de Lovitch des Produits et Engrais Chimiques (Łowicz), Warsaw 1896 — Transcription

title: Société de Lovitch des Produits et Engrais Chimiques Share, Warsaw, 1895
description: This is a bilingual (Russian and French) bearer share of 250 rubles, the second emission (2-й выпускъ / 2ème émission), in the Ловичское Общество Химическихъ Продуктовъ и Землеудобрительныхъ Веществъ — the Société de Lovitch des Produits et Engrais Chimiques, a chemical-products and fertiliser concern at Łowicz. The face proclaims a registered capital of 1,000,000 rubles and bears the imperial Ministry of Finance cachet, the company seal, and the autograph signatures of its directors, cashier, and book-keeper. Issued at Warsaw in 1895, under a charter sanctioned by the Emperor, the certificate documents capital-raising in Russian Poland's fertiliser industry, where chemical manure was tied to agricultural improvement. Its restrained engraved frame — green guilloche borders enclosing star-and-floral rosettes — follows the sober conventions of late nineteenth-century security printing, signalling probity rather than ornament.
creator: Société de Lovitch des Produits et Engrais Chimiques
notes: Société de Lovitch des Produits et Engrais Chimiques. 2nd emission. Action No. 3276, 250 rubles. Capital 1,000,000 rubles. Charter approved June 9, 1895. Warsaw. Companion to 1st emission No. 2106 (row 446).

title: St. Petersburg Capital Lighting Company Share
description: This bilingual Russian-and-French bearer share, denominated at 100 silver rubles, represents one part-ownership in the Общество Столичнаго Освѣщенія, styled in French as the Société d'Éclairage de la Capitale, the gas-lighting concern of the imperial capital, St. Petersburg. Its charter was sanctioned by imperial decree (Высочайше утвержденное) on 10 October 1858, fixing the company's capital at 4,000,000 silver rubles divided into 40,000 shares of 100 rubles each, with an exclusive privilege running fifty years. Made out by hand to John Blythe Robinson, Esqre., the certificate documents foreign capital flowing into a Russian municipal utility on the eve of the Great Reforms. The decorous engraved frame and restrained classical lettering project the solidity expected of a privileged monopoly, presenting the illumination of the capital's streets as both a public benefit and a secured commercial venture.
creator: Общество Столичнаго Освещения (Capital Lighting Company)
notes: Общество Столичнаго Освещения (Société d'Eclairage de la Capitale). Share No. 39419, 100 silver rubles. Capital 4,000,000 silver rubles / 40,000 shares. Charter approved October 10, 1858. St. Petersburg gas lighting company.

(Left-hand column, Russian:)

**ИЗВЛЕЧЕНІЕ ИЗЪ УСТАВА.**

§ 20. Для приведенія предпріятія въ исполненіе, Обществу предоставляется собрать капиталъ въ четыре милліона (4,000,000) р. сер. посредствомъ выпуска сорока тысячъ (40,000) акцій, каждая во сто (100) р. сер.

§ 57. Каждый акціонеръ можетъ присутствовать въ Общемъ Собраніи, но подаетъ голосъ только тотъ, кто имѣетъ на свое имя не менѣе десяти акцій. Владѣющій пятьюдесятью акціями, имѣетъ два голоса, а ста и болѣе акціями — три голоса. За счета голосовъ, акціи должны быть представлены въ Правленіе по крайней мѣрѣ за недѣлю до собранія, и въ нумерахъ ихъ, съ именами представителей, вносятся въ особо заведенную для сего книгу. Послѣ записки въ книгу передача тѣхъ акцій отъ одного лица другому, до окончанія собранія, воспрещается.

Примѣчаніе. Акціи хотя пишутся на имя первоначальныхъ ихъ пріобрѣтателя, но впослѣдствіи могутъ быть передаваемы отъ одного лица другому и по передаточной надписи, отмѣчаемой въ Правленіи Общества, и съ бланковою надписью.

§ 59. Отсутствующій акціонеръ, имѣющій право голоса, можетъ предоставить права свои другому акціонеру, имѣющему также право голоса; но одно лицо не можетъ имѣть довѣренности отъ двухъ и болѣе лицъ отсутствующихъ.

Примѣчаніе къ § 64. Для полученія дивиденда акціонеры, пребывающіе въ Россіи, должны представлять въ Правленіе Общества подлинные купоны. Для выдачи дивиденда акціонерамъ заграничнымъ будетъ назначенъ особый коммиссіонеръ тамъ, гдѣ представится въ томъ надобность.

Въ Указѣ Правительствующаго Сената отъ 5 Декабря 1864 г. за № 73318, между прочимъ, изъяснено: Правительствующій Сенатъ слушалъ рапортъ Министра Финансовъ за № 8925, слѣдующаго содержанія: ГОСУДАРЬ ИМПЕРАТОРЪ, вслѣдствіе представленія Министра Финансовъ въ Комитетъ Министровъ и по положенію онаго, въ 13-й день минувшаго Ноября, Высочайше повелѣть соизволилъ: допустить въ залогъ по казеннымъ подрядамъ и поставкамъ свидѣтельства на акціи Общества Столичнаго Освѣщенія, равно и самыя акціи, по окончательной ихъ оплатѣ, съ опредѣленіемъ закладной оныхъ оцѣнки по правиламъ, изложеннымъ въ ст. 9 приложенія къ ст. 268 Св. Зак. Т. V Уст. о Пит. Сбор. (изд. 1863 г.), съ соблюденіемъ, относительно пріема въ залогъ свидѣтельствъ на не вполнѣ оплаченныя акціи, порядка, въ примѣчаніи къ п. 18 ст. 2 того же приложенія постановленнаго.

(Right-hand column, French:)

**EXTRAIT DES STATUTS.**

§ 20. Afin de réaliser l'entreprise, la Société a le droit de réunir un Capital de quatre millions (4,000,000) de roubles argent, moyennant l'émission de quarante mille (40,000) actions à cent (100) r. a. chacune.

§ 57. Chaque actionnaire peut assister à l'Assemblée Générale; mais le droit de vote n'appartient qu'à celui qui possède au moins dix actions à son nom. Le détenteur de cinquante actions a deux voix; celui qui en possède cent et plus — trois voix. Pour compter les voix, les actions doivent être présentées à la Direction au moins une semaine avant le jour fixé pour l'assemblée, et les numéros des actions, sont inscrits avec les noms des présentateurs, dans un livre spécial. Après l'inscription des actions dans ce livre leur transfert, avant la fin de l'assemblée, est interdit.

Remarque. Bien que les actions soient écrites aux noms des premiers acquéreurs, elles peuvent être cédées par la suite au moyen d'endossements de transfert annotés à la Direction et par endossements en blanc.

§ 59. En cas d'absence, tout actionnaire ayant droit de vote peut déléguer ses droits à un autre actionnaire ayant également droit de vote; mais personne ne peut être le mandataire de deux actionnaires absents ou plus.

Remarque au § 64. Pour toucher les dividendes, les actionnaires se trouvant en Russie doivent présenter les coupons originaux à la Direction de la Société. Pour le paiement des dividendes aux actionnaires à l'étranger, un commissionnaire spécial sera désigné là, où il y aura besoin.

Dans l'Oukaze du Sénat Dirigeant en date du 5 Décembre 1864 sous le № 73318, il est dit entr'autres: Le Sénat Dirigeant a entendu le rapport du Ministre de Finances sous le № 8925 de la teneur suivante: Sa Majesté l'Empereur, par suite de l'exposé du Ministre des Finances fait au Comité des Ministres et suivant conclusion de celui-ci, a daigné ordonner, le 13 Novembre dernier, admettre, à titre de nantissements pour contrats et fournitures conclus avec la couronne, les certificats provisoires pour actions de la Société d'Éclairage de la Capitale, ainsi que les actions mêmes après leur libération définitive, en fixant leur valeur comme nantissement d'après les règles prescrites par l'art. 9 d'annexe à l'article 268 du Code, tome V, lois des impôts sur les boissons (édition de 1863) et en observant, relativement à l'acceptation comme nantissement des certificats pour actions non encore intégralement libérées, le règlement établi dans l'alinéa 18 de l'article 2 de la même annexe.

§ 20. To carry the enterprise into effect, the Company is authorised to raise a capital of four million (4,000,000) silver roubles by the issue of forty thousand (40,000) shares, each of one hundred (100) silver roubles.

§ 57. Every shareholder may attend the General Assembly, but only one who holds at least ten shares in his own name may cast a vote. A holder of fifty shares has two votes, and one holding one hundred or more shares — three votes. For the counting of votes, the shares must be presented to the Board at least one week before the meeting, and their numbers, together with the names of the presenters, are entered in a book specially kept for the purpose. After entry in the book, the transfer of those shares from one person to another is forbidden until the close of the meeting.

Note. Although the shares are made out in the names of their original acquirers, they may afterwards be transferred from one person to another by a transfer endorsement noted at the Company's Board, and by a blank endorsement.

§ 59. An absent shareholder entitled to vote may delegate his rights to another shareholder likewise entitled to vote; but no one person may hold the proxy of two or more absent persons.

Note to § 64. To receive the dividend, shareholders residing in Russia must present the original coupons to the Company's Board. For the payment of the dividend to shareholders abroad, a special commissioner will be appointed wherever the need arises.

In the Decree of the Governing Senate dated 5 December 1864, No. 73318, it is stated among other things: The Governing Senate heard the report of the Minister of Finance No. 8925, of the following tenor: THE SOVEREIGN EMPEROR, following the submission of the Minister of Finance to the Committee of Ministers and in accordance with its resolution, on the 13th day of last November, was graciously pleased to command: to admit as security for state contracts and supplies the certificates for shares of the Company for the Lighting of the Capital, as well as the shares themselves once fully paid up, with the valuation of such pledged securities determined according to the rules set out in Art. 9 of the Appendix to Art. 268 of the Digest of Laws, Vol. V, Statute on the Beverage Excise (1863 edition), observing, with respect to the acceptance as security of certificates for shares not yet fully paid up, the procedure laid down in the note to point 18 of Art. 2 of the same Appendix.

title: Société des Mines d'Or de Kilo-Moto Founder's Share, Brussels and Kilo, after 1944
description: This bearer founder's share (part bénéficiaire sans désignation de valeur, "au porteur") was issued by the Société des Mines d'Or de Kilo-Moto, a société congolaise à responsabilité limitée whose statutes were approved by decree of 8 February 1926 and amended through 1940. Carrying no nominal value, it conferred a claim on profits rather than on capital. The company seated its registered office at Kilo in the Belgian Congo and its administrative office in Brussels, on a capital of 230,000,000 francs—60,000 privileged shares of 500 francs and 200,000 social parts of 1,000 francs—alongside 1,400,000 of these founder's parts. A handstamp records the certificate as "créé après le 6-10-1944." The engraved vignette of dugout canoes, ore tramways and palm-fringed waterways frames the Kilo-Moto goldfields of the northeastern Congo as a colonial concession, and the profit-only instrument let promoters reward founders without diluting the subscribed capital underwriting the mines.

title: French-Peruvian Mining Company Share
description: This printed share certificate (action) of five hundred francs is drawn on the Société Franco-Péruvienne des Mines de la Province de Castro-Virreyna, a limited partnership (en commandite) trading under the firm name Crosnier et Cie. The company was constituted by deed before Maître Fould, notary at Paris, on 2 April 1853; its capital of one million francs was divided into two thousand shares of five hundred francs each. The certificate was delivered at Paris to François Charles Blacque, a banker residing at Rue Caumartin No. 22, and is dated the first of February 1853. A later inked stamp reads "Remboursé 45 Francs par le Liquidateur," recording a partial repayment on winding-up. The share embodies the mid-century French appetite for South American mineral ventures: Castro-Virreyna in central Peru had yielded silver since the colonial era, and Parisian capital sought to revive such workings. The engraved border and a small allegorical vignette in the manner of contemporary financial printing frame the text without elaborate iconographic argument. The liquidator's stamp tells the venture's outcome: the company was dissolved, returning shareholders a fraction of their subscription.
creator: Société Franco-Péruvienne des Mines de la Province de Castro-Virreyna (Crosnier & Cie.)

ART. 7. Le fonds social est fixé à la somme de 1,000,000 de francs, qui sera divisé en 2,000 actions de 500 fr. chacune.

ART. 9. Les actions seront nominatives ; elles seront détachées d'un registre à souche et numérotées de 1 à 2,000. Elles seront signées par le Gérant et par une ou deux personnes qui seront désignées par l'assemblée générale ; elles seront revêtues du timbre de la Société.
Mention des versements successifs effectués sur les actions sera faite sur les titres et signée par le Gérant et l'une des personnes ci-dessus mentionnées.

ART. 10. — Sur les 2,000 actions représentant le fonds social, 1,000 actions, portant les nᵒˢ 1 à 1,000, sont libérées de tout versement ; elles ne seront détachées de la souche et ne deviendront négociables que dans le délai de deux années, à partir de la signature des présentes.

ART. 14. Les souscripteurs originaires d'actions et les cessionnaires successifs seront solidairement garants, jusqu'à concurrence de la totalité des versements, du montant de chaque action.

ART. 15. La cession des actions aura lieu par voie d'endossement.
Seulement, pour être valable vis-à-vis de la Société, cette cession devra être portée à la connaissance de la Société par le cédant, et visée au siége social ; la Société ne pourra, en aucun cas, être responsable de la régularité du transfert, ni de sa validité.

ART. 30. Tous les ans il y aura, de plein droit, une assemblée générale qui se tiendra au siége social, à Paris, le 1ᵉʳ lundi du mois de mai.
Pour faire partie de l'assemblée générale, il faut être propriétaire d'au moins cinq actions.
Chaque actionnaire aura autant de voix qu'il réunira de fois cinq actions, sans qu'une même personne puisse avoir à elle seule plus de dix voix, quel que soit le nombre de ses actions, soit par elle-même, soit comme mandataire d'un ou plusieurs autres actionnaires.
Tout actionnaire devra, lors de la réunion, signer une feuille de présence.

ART. 9. The shares shall be registered (nominative); they shall be detached from a counterfoil register and numbered from 1 to 2,000. They shall be signed by the Manager and by one or two persons designated by the general assembly; they shall bear the seal of the Company.
A note of the successive payments made on the shares shall be made on the certificates and signed by the Manager and one of the persons mentioned above.

ART. 10. — Of the 2,000 shares representing the company capital, 1,000 shares, bearing the numbers 1 to 1,000, are fully paid up; they shall not be detached from the counterfoil nor become negotiable until a period of two years, counting from the signature of the present [deed].

ART. 14. The original subscribers of shares and the successive transferees shall be jointly and severally liable, up to the total amount of the payments, for the amount of each share.

ART. 15. The transfer of shares shall take place by way of endorsement.
However, to be valid with respect to the Company, this transfer must be brought to the knowledge of the Company by the transferor, and endorsed at the registered office; the Company shall not, in any case, be responsible for the regularity of the transfer, nor for its validity.

title: Egyptian Agricultural & Commercial Company Founder's Share
description: This printed bearer certificate conveys one-tenth of a founder's part (dixième de part de fondateur au porteur), without par value (sans désignation de valeur), in the Société Générale Égyptienne pour l'Agriculture & le Commerce, a société anonyme. The text records that the company was constituted by notarial act before M. Alphonse-Louis-Jean Cols, notary in Antwerp, on 31 May 1905, with founding capital of 12,500,000 francs subsequently raised to 15,000,000 francs, divided into 60,000 shares of 250 francs each alongside 5,500 founder's parts issued in tenths. Founder's parts carried no fixed face value but entitled the bearer to a portion of surplus profits above the ordinary dividend, rewarding the promoters of a Belgian-organised venture directed at Egyptian agriculture and trade. The engraving frames the title between papyriform columns beneath a cavetto cornice, an Egyptian Revival idiom that announces the enterprise's field of operation.

title: Spassky Copper Mine Share Warrant
description: A bearer share warrant ("warrant to bearer" / "titre au porteur") of The Spassky Copper Mine Limited, printed in parallel English and French, entitling the holder to one fully paid share of £1. The company's capital stood at £1,250,000, divided into 1,250,000 shares of £1 each. Issued under the company's common seal at London on 20 March 1917 and bearing a red embossed Inland Revenue duty stamp. The Spassky deposit lay on the Kazakh steppe of central Asia, and the warrant exemplifies the British-domiciled, sterling-capitalised companies through which London financed Russian and Central Asian mining before 1917; the bilingual text and a later Paris foreign-securities listing stamp (1925) attest to its circulation among Continental investors. Its date is freighted: it was sealed within weeks of the February Revolution, after which such enterprises passed to Bolshevik nationalisation. A classical winged allegory seated with a tablet bearing the company monogram frames the issue in conventional Belle Époque ornament.
creator: The Spassky Copper Mine Limited
notes: The Spassky Copper Mine Limited. Share warrant to bearer, £1. No. 68868. Capital £1,250,000. London, May 10, 1917. Bilingual English/French. Spassky mine, Kazakhstan.

title: Sixth Austrian War Loan Bond
description: This printed bearer bond is a State loan certificate (Staatsanleihe) for one thousand kronen, bearing 5½ percent tax-free (steuerfreie) amortisable interest, issued as part of the Sixth Austrian War Loan (Sechste österreichische Kriegsanleihe). It is dated Vienna, 1 April 1917. The bond belongs to the series of eight successive war loans through which Austria-Hungary financed its share of the Great War. By 1917 the Habsburg treasury was straining badly, and subscription rested ever more on patriotic appeal and quasi-compulsion than on confident investment; the dynasty's collapse in 1918 left such paper worthless. The ornament speaks to its moment: drawn in the linear Viennese Secession idiom of Jugendstil, allegorical female figures flank the text above a small cityscape oval, while the crowned double-headed imperial eagle below asserts the authority pledging repayment.
creator: Austro-Hungarian Government
notes: Sechste Österreichische Kriegsanleihe (6th Austrian War Loan). 5½% tax-free amortizable Staatsanleihe. 1,000 kronen. Serie 247, No. 007,886. Vienna, April 1, 1917. Art Nouveau design.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original:**

1000
KRONEN
1000

SERIE 247
SECHSTE ÖSTERREICHISCHE KRIEGSANLEIHE
STEUERFREIE 5½% AMORTISABLE

STAATSANLEIHE
EINTAUSEND KRONEN

Diese Staatsschuldverschreibung bildet einen Bestandteil der auf Grund der kaiserlischen Verordnung vom 4. August 1914, R.G.Bl. Nr. 209, ausgegebenen steuerfreien 5½% amortisablen Staatsanleihe (VI. Kriegsanleihe). Die Anleihe ist in Serien à 50,000,000 Kronen eingeteilt und in Abschnitten zu 50, 100, 500, 1000, 5000, 10.000 und 50.000 Kronen zahlbar. Die Coupons und der Talon versehen mit einem Talon versehen, gegen welchen die weiteren Coupons ohne Anrechnung vom Kronen oder derselben vom k. k. Staatszentralkasse in Wien zeitgerecht ausgelöst werden. Die Schuldverschreibungen dieser Anleihe werden vom 1. April 1917 ab mit 5½% jährlich verzinst. Die jährliche Verzinsung erfolgt am Ende eines jeden Jahres, nachhinein verlangt. Die Anleihe wird zum Nennwerte zurückgezahlt und unter Einhaltung eines anstehend gleichen Zinstermin und Kapitalzahlung umfassenden Annuitätsaufwandes in den Jahren 1928 bis 1957 auf Grund von Auslosungen getilgt. Die Auslosung wird nach Serien (zu 5,000,000 l) vorgenommen und die zurückgezahlten Schuldverschreibungen werden von der k. k. Staatszentralkasse in Wien vernichtet. Die Zahlung erfolgt an dem der Auslosung folgenden 1. April. Die ausgelosten Serien werden alsdann aufgelöst nach Zeichnung nehst einer Liste der Serien, aus welchen noch Restanten ausheilen, in der amtlichen „Wiener Zeitung" verlautbart. Der k. k. Staatsrat verwahrt ist das Recht vorbehalten, vom 1. Januar 1927 an bei einer Kündigung eines Jahres von vorne herein ohne Auslosung unter Einhaltung einer dreimonatigen Kündigungsfrist eine Nenn-werte zurückzuzahlen. In dieser Kündigung wird in der amtlichen Wiener Zeitung verlautbart werden. Die Ausschließung der Zinsen sowie die Rückzahlung des Kapitals der Staatsschuldverschreibungen erfolgt ohne Steuer-, Gebühren- oder sonstigen Abzug. Ein allfälliger Wertverlust der Coupons wird vom Kapitalsbetrag in Abzug gebracht. Die Verschreibungen dieser Anleihe werden bei der k. k. Staatszentralkasse in Wien während der Gültigkeitstage des Kapitalstitels ausgegeben und derselb alle bis dahin noch nicht fällig gewordenen Coupons nebst Talon zurückzustellen. Der Betrag der fälligen Coupons wird vom Kapitalsbetrag in Abzug gebracht. Die Dauer des Kapitals binnen 30 Jahren, in Ansehung der Zinsen binnen 8 Jahren.

Wien, am 1. April 1917.

Der k. k. Finanzminister:
[Signature]

Diese Schuldverschreibung ist in dem Hauptbuche der österreichischen Staatsschuld und [illegible] des Reichsrates eingetragen.

[Two signatures below]

---

**English Translation:**

1000
KRONEN
1000

SERIES 247
SIXTH AUSTRIAN WAR LOAN
TAX-FREE 5½% AMORTIZABLE

STATE BOND
ONE THOUSAND KRONEN

This state debt certificate forms part of the tax-free 5½% amortizable state loan (VI. War Loan) issued on the basis of the Imperial Decree of August 4, 1914, R.G.Bl. No. 209. The loan is divided into series of 50,000,000 kronen each and is payable in denominations of 50, 100, 500, 1,000, 5,000, 10,000, and 50,000 kronen. The coupons and the talon are provided with a talon, against which further coupons without set-off will be timely redeemed by the k.k. State Central Treasury in Vienna. The debt certificates of this loan will be bearing interest at 5½% per annum from April 1, 1917 onwards. Annual interest is paid at the end of each year; capital repayment follows at the end of the year in which the drawing occurs. The loan will be repaid at par value and will be amortized in equal annual installments (annuities) encompassing both interest and capital repayment during the years 1928 to 1957 on the basis of drawings. Redemption is carried out by series (of 5,000,000 l) and the repaid debt certificates are destroyed by the k.k. State Central Treasury in Vienna. Payment occurs on April 1 following the drawing. The drawn series will subsequently be dissolved according to the drawing with a list of the series from which remainders are still outstanding, published in the official "Wiener Zeitung." The k.k. State retains the right to terminate from January 1, 1927 onwards with notice of one year, without drawing, with observance of a three-month notice period, to repay at par value. This termination will be published in the official Wiener Zeitung. The exclusion of interest as well as the repayment of the capital of the state debt certificates occurs without tax, fees, or any other deductions. Any incidental loss of value of the coupons will be deducted from the capital amount. The securities of this loan are issued by the k.k. State Central Treasury in Vienna during the validity period of the capital title, and the same must return all coupons not yet due together with the talon. The amount of due coupons will be deducted from the capital amount. The duration of the capital is 30 years, for interest 8 years.

Vienna, April 1, 1917.

The k.k. Finance Minister:
[Signature]

This debt certificate is entered in the main book of Austrian state debt and [illegible] of the Reich Council.

[Two signatures]

---

- Series: 247

- Denomination: 1,000 Kronen at 5½% interest
- Two official signatures (Finance Minister and another official)
- Handwritten annotation "u/115" in red at top left
- Ornamental Art Nouveau border design with flanking allegorical figures
- Austrian Imperial Double-Headed Eagle coat of arms at bottom center
- Purple/mauve and cream colored decorative elements throughout

title: Eighth Austrian War Loan Vienna June 1, 1918
description: This printed bearer obligation is a State Loan bond (Staatsanleihe) of the Eighth Austrian War Loan (Achte österreichische Kriegsanleihe), with a face value of 100 kronen bearing tax-free amortizable interest at 5½ percent. It was issued at Vienna on 1 June 1918. The eighth and final war loan came to market barely five months before the armistice and the dissolution of the Habsburg monarchy; by mid-1918 the Empire's exhaustion was plain, and the appeal to subscribers rested less on prospect of repayment than on patriotic duty. The engraved design embodies the Jugendstil (Viennese Secession) idiom: a sinuous, ornamented frame surrounds the double-headed imperial eagle, the Austrian shield and the imperial crown at the head, flanked by haloed allegorical figures—an armed male guardian above a cannon at left, a labouring female figure at right—pairing martial defence with productive toil to dignify a loan sought in extremity.
creator: Austro-Hungarian Government
notes: Achte Österreichische Kriegsanleihe (8th Austrian War Loan). 5½% tax-free amortizable. 100 kronen. Serie 117, No. 128,924. Vienna, June 1, 1918. Art Nouveau design. Final Austrian war loan, issued five months before armistice.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (German):**

SERIE 117                                                    № 128.924

100 K
Achte Österr. Kriegsanleihe :: Steuerfreie 5½°/₀amortisable

STAATSANLEIHE
BLÜHDERT KRONEN

Diese Staatsanleihesverschreibung bildet einen Bestandteil der auf Grund des Gesetzes vom 4. Juni 1918, R. G. Bl. Nr. 151 eröffneten achten Kriegsanleihe (VIII. Kriegsanleihe). Die Anleihe ist in Serien zu 5,000.000 Kronen eingeteilt und in Abschnitte zu 100, 200, 500, 1000 und 50.000 Kronen ausgeführt; jede Staatsanleihesverschreibung ist mit 22 Coupons und mit einem Talon versehen, gegen welchen die weiteren Coupons ohne Unterschrift zu 100 Kronen oder Coupons von 50 Kronen bei Einlösung an den k. k. Staatscentralkasse in Wien zeitgerecht ausgehändigt werden. Die Anleihe ist zum Kurse von par 100 begeben mit 5½°/₀ jährlich, und zwar die Abschnitte zu 50 Kronen zu ganzjährigen Raten vom 1. Juni und zum Kurse von 50 Kronen zu halbjährigen Raten vom 1. Juni und 1. Dezember eines jeden halbjährlich verschwindenden Termin. Die Zinsen vom 1. Juni 1918 bis 31. August 1918 werden in Obligationscoupons verrechnet. Die Anleihe wird zum Nennwerte rückzahlbar und unter Beibehaltung der Couponrechte mit 1914 als amortisierbar erklärt. Im Falle einer etwaigen Rückzahlung vor dem Fälligkeitstage wird die Ausstattung auf auflösendst in den Jahren 1921 bis 1958 auf Grund von Auslosungen getätigt. Die Auslosung wird nach [illegible] Worten entschieden und findet im September jeden Jahres statt; die Rückzahlung erfolgt am dem Auslosung folgenden 1. März. Die aufgelösten Serien werden nach Ziehung gestat nach Ziehung gehört eine Liste der Serie. Verfahren wird publiziert. Kontrolle gegen Verlust.

Der k. k. Staatsverwaltung hat das Recht vorbehalten, vom 1. Januar 1927 angefangen die Aus- lösung unabhängig von Ziehungslosung oder noch ungelisten Anleihesfläches einer dringenden Rückgabefirst zum Nennwerte zurückzuschreiben. Die Kündigung wird in der amtlichen "Wiener Zeitung" verlautbart werden. Die Auswahrung der Zinsen sowie die Rückzahlung der Anleihe erfolgt bei der k. k. Staatscentralkasse in Wien oder sonstigen Abzug gegen Hinterlegung der fälligen Zinsencoupons, beziehungsweise Staatsanleihe-verschreibung. Die Rückzahlung erfolgt am Fälligkeitstage des Kapitalbetrages; es sind demzufolge alle bei dahem Neben Fällig gewordene Coupons nebst Talon zurückzustellen. Der Betrag der Staatsanleihesverschreibung erleichted durch Verjährung in Anschung der Kapitalbeträges; es sind demzufolge alle bei dahem noch Fällig gewordene Coupons plus Talon zurückzusetzen. Die Staatsanleihesverschreibung erleichted durch Verjährung in Anschung des Kapitals binnen dreißig Jahren. Im Anschlung der Zinsen sechs Jahre vom Fälligkeitstermine an.

Wien, am 1. Juni 1918.
Der k. k. Finanzminister

[Signature]

Diese Schuldverschreibung ist in der Hauptbuche der Staatsschuld eingetragen.
Für die Staatsschulden-Kontrollkommission des Reichsrates

[Signature]

---

**English translation:**

SERIES 117                                                    No. 128,924

100 K
Eighth Austrian War Loan :: Tax-Free 5½% Amortizable

STATE LOAN
HUNDRED KRONEN

This state loan certificate constitutes a component of the eighth war loan (VIII. War Loan) established pursuant to the law of June 4, 1918, R. G. Bl. No. 151. The loan is divided into series of 5,000,000 kronen each and is issued in denominations of 100, 200, 500, 1,000, and 50,000 kronen; each state loan certificate is provided with 22 coupons and one talon, against which further coupons without signature for 100 kronen or coupons for 50 kronen shall be issued without delay at the k.k. State Central Treasury in Vienna upon presentation. The loan has been issued at par 100 at 5½% per annum, with the denominations of 50 kronen to be redeemed in full annual installments from June 1st and at the rate of 50 kronen in semi-annual installments from June 1st and December 1st in terms that expire every half-year. Interest from June 1, 1918 to August 31, 1918 shall be settled in obligation coupons. The loan shall be repayable at par value and, with retention of coupon rights, is declared amortizable as of 1914. In the event of any redemption prior to the maturity date, the distribution shall be effected in the years 1921 through 1958 on the basis of drawings. The drawing shall be conducted according to [illegible] principles and shall take place in September of each year; redemption shall be effected on March 1st following the drawing. The drawn series shall be published according to the drawing record. Control against loss is maintained.

The k.k. State Administration reserves the right, commencing January 1, 1927, to effect redemption independent of drawing or of any uncalled loan portions at par value upon urgent notice. The notice shall be published in the official "Wiener Zeitung." Interest payments and redemption of the loan shall be effected at the k.k. State Central Treasury in Vienna or other authorized offices upon presentation of the due interest coupons or, respectively, state loan certificates, less any deductions for unpaid coupons. Redemption shall take place on the maturity date of the principal; accordingly, all due coupons and talons must be surrendered with it. The principal of the state loan certificate is subject to prescription after thirty years; interest coupons are subject to prescription six years from their maturity date.

Vienna, June 1, 1918.
The k.k. Finance Minister

[Signature]

This debt certificate has been recorded in the main register of state debt.
For the State Debt Control Commission of the Reichsrat

[Signature]

---

**Notable markings:**

- **Serial designation:** Serie 117, No. 128,924
- **Denomination:** 100 Kronen
- **Interest rate:** 5½% per annum, tax-free
- **Issue date:** Vienna, June 1, 1918
- **Signatures:** Two official signatures (Finance Minister and representative of the State Debt Control Commission)
- **Decorative elements:** Art Nouveau design with Austrian imperial double-headed eagle coat of arms, flanked by allegorical female figures representing war/strength and prosperity; ornamental borders with spiral and floral motifs in green and pink
- **Stamp:** Green postal/official stamp visible in upper left portion
- **Document condition:** Printed bearer bond with characteristic period typography and engraving

title: Austrian Imperial State Bond
description: This printed Staatsschuldverschreibung (state-debt certificate) of the k.k. Direction der Staatsschuld, the Imperial-Royal Directorate of State Debts, acknowledges "Ein Hundert Gulden" in Österreichische Währung (Austrian currency). Its terms state that the bond forms part of the 5% uniform public debt, burdened with an unraised tax of 16⅔%, so that the holder's net yield is 4⅙%; it arose, by the law of 20 June 1868, from the conversion (Umwandlung) of the various classes of funded debt outstanding at the end of 1867. Issued at Vienna on 1 July 1868, it embodies the fiscal consolidation that followed the 1866 defeat by Prussia and the 1867 Compromise dividing the burden between the Austrian and Hungarian halves of the new dual monarchy. The engraved crown, supporting genii and classicising florid frame project imperial solvency at a moment of reconstruction; an Antwerpen handstamp and a 1922 mark record its long circulation among Low-Countries investors.
creator: k.k. Direction der Staatschulden (Austrian Imperial-Royal Directorate of State Debts)
notes: k.k. Staatsschuldverschreibung (Austrian Imperial State Bond). 100 gulden (Österreichische Währung). No. 207,407. Vienna, July 1, 1868. 4½% transfunded state debt. k.k. Direction der Staatschulden. Antwerpen deposit stamp.

title: Massachusetts Commonwealth Tax Certificate and Massachusetts-Bay Bill
description: A single sheet bearing two interest-bearing obligations of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts in the unsettled fiscal aftermath of the Revolution. The upper instrument is a tax certificate from the Treasurer's Office, Boston, dated April 1, 1786, certifying that three pounds (£3.0.0) is due to P. Seek or bearer, receivable in payment of one-third of the Tax (No. 5) granted in March 1786, and declared "equal to the same Sum in Gold and Silver" — a specie-equivalence clause guarding the holder against depreciated paper. It is subscribed by Treasurer Alex Hodgdon. Below it is a State of Massachusetts-Bay bill for five dollars, promising payment of five Spanish milled dollars by December 31, 1786, with interest at five per centum per annum under an act of May 5, 1785, signed by R. Cranch. Together they show a hard-pressed state engineering acceptance of its own paper by tying it to taxes and to silver, the everyday machinery of public credit in the early republic.
creator: Commonwealth of Massachusetts (Treasurer's Office)
notes: Two documents: (1) Massachusetts Commonwealth Tax Certificate No. 1582, £3.13s.0, Boston, April 1, 1786. Signed by Alex Hodgdon, Treasurer. (2) State of Massachusetts-Bay Bill No. 1541(?), $5, ca. 1785. 5% interest; payable Dec 31, 1786. Signed by A. Cranch.

title: State of New York Canal Stock, Albany, 1842
description: A registered certificate of New York State Canal Stock for $1,000, issued by the Comptroller's Office of the State of New York and acknowledging the People of the State as debtor. The principal bears interest at seven per centum per annum from the 31st day of May 1842, payable quarterly on the first days of February, April, July, and October, and is reimbursable at the pleasure of the State at any time after the first day of June 1845. It is issued in pursuance of the directions of chapter 114 of the Laws of 1842 and is dated at Albany, May 31, 1842, signed by the Comptroller. Such stock funded the State's canal enterprise, the Erie Canal and its feeders, whose tolls underwrote a public credit so secure that New York could borrow at home rather than abroad; the seven-per-cent coupon registers the lingering cost of capital in the years after the Panic of 1837. Engraved by Rawdon, Clark & Co. of Albany, the head bears neoclassical allegorical figures whose draped, seated repose lends the instrument the dignity of state authority.
creator: State of New York, Comptroller's Office
notes: State of New York Canal Stock. $1,000 at 3% p.a. Issued to Henry Whiting. Albany, May 31, 1842. Comptroller's Office. Quarterly interest; reimbursable at State's pleasure after 1 year notice. Erie Canal financing.

title: Saint George Textile Company Share
description: A bilingual Bulgarian-French share certificate (акция / action) for ten shares of one hundred leva each, a total of 1,000 leva, in the joint-stock company for textile industry and trade in textile materials "Св. Георги" ("Sv. Guéorgui" / Saint George), issued at Sofia in 1929. The text affirms the holder's proportional right in the company's capital and obligations under its statutes, and the document carries a Bulgarian fiscal stamp and the signatures of the administrative council's president and an administrator. The certificate belongs to the wave of incorporation that accompanied Bulgaria's interwar effort to enlarge domestic manufacturing behind protective tariffs; textiles were among the leading branches of that drive. The dual Bulgarian and French presentation reflects the French commercial-law model favoured by Bulgarian société-anonyme charters of the period, signalling an outward orientation toward foreign capital and trade. The dense rose-coloured engraved border, with its symmetrical scrollwork framing the bold denomination, follows the conventional ornamental idiom of European share scrip.
creator: "Св. Георги" (Saint George) Textile Company
notes: "Св. Георги" Textile Company (Société Anonyme pour l'Industrie Textile). 10 shares at 100 leva each. Shares No. 79881–79890. Sofia, 1929. Bilingual Bulgarian/French.

title: Texas and German Emigration Company Certificate of Stock (Houston)
description: This engraved certificate of stock attests two shares of one hundred dollars each in the joint stock of the Texas and German Emigration Company, an enterprise constituted by indentures of 15 September 1843 to colonize Texas under contract with the Republic. The holder of record is the German Emigration Company itself, the Adelsverein of German noblemen that organized the venture, and the certificate is countersigned at the City of Houston on the fifteenth day of June 1852 by Henry F. Fisher as agent. A memorandum binds the shares to the company's tangible assets: some 1,200,000 acres of Texas land, the rights of the colonization contract, and all debts due the company. The instrument promises redemption with interest from 1 January 1853 at five per cent at a banking house in Galveston. The plain typographic frame, free of allegory, presents the speculation as a sober conveyance of land rather than an appeal to sentiment, even as the underlying colonization scheme drifted toward insolvency.
creator: Texas and German Emigration Company
notes: Texas and German Emigration Company. Certificate of Stock No. 572, Folio 92. 2 shares (No. 2775–2776) at $100 each. Houston, June 15, 1852. Agent: Henry F. Fisher. German immigrant colonization of Texas (Adelsverein). Related to Adelsverein bond (row 424).
transcription:
# TEXAS AND GERMAN EMIGRATION COMPANY
## CERTIFICATE OF STOCK

**No. 572 FOLIO 92**

SHARES OF ONE HUNDRED DOLLARS EACH.

This is to certify, that the German Emigration Company is proprietor of TWOT shares, No. 2775 and 2776, in the Joint Stock of the Texas and German Emigration Company, constituted by articles of association, incepted and agreement, contained in Indentures, bearing date the fifteenth day of September, A.D. 1843.

This Certificate is transferable by delivery or assignment, vesting in the bearer all and assignee all the rights of the original proprietor, to be subject to the provisions of said Indentures, and to the right of said German Emigration Company, as their assigns, to redeem the Stock hereby represented, by paying to him, as bearer, as aforesaid in any Banking or Commercial House in the City of Galveston, for him, as bearer, the said sum with interest from the first day of January, 1844, at the rate of five per cent per annum, on or before the first day of May, A.D. 1855.

Given at the City of Houston, this fifteenth day of June, 1852.

Issued by authority of said Indenture, and in accordance with its requirements, by

Henry F. Fisher

AGENT OF THE GERMAN EMIGRATION COMPANY.

---

## MEMORANDUM.

The JOINT STOCK of the **Texas and German Emigration Company** consists in the lands and tenements, rights, interests and property vested, conveyed, put in, assigned and transferred by the **German Emigration Company** to the **Texas and German Emigration Company**, as per Indenture of September 15th, 1843, to-wit: 1st.—All their right and claim to about 1,200,000 acres of land in Harris & Matagorda Counties, Grant. 2nd.—All their right and title to their private property in and about New Braunfels, Fredericksburg, Indianola, and lands and tenements elsewhere in the State of Texas. 3rd. All the debts, demands and claims due them in any manner in the State of Texas.

---

title: The Common Fund Company Scrip
description: This engraved scrip certificate of The Common Fund Company Limited entitles the holder to call for one hundred shares of £20 each in the company's capital stock, to be exchanged for certificates of full stock upon payment of the £20 par cost per share in one sum. It is dated at London, 20 December 1869, and was issued under the Companies' Acts of 1862–67. Scrip of this kind was an interim instrument, representing the right to acquire fully paid shares rather than the shares themselves. The company styled itself a transatlantic concern, advertising offices in Washington, Berlin, Paris, and London and promising that the scrip was "convertible into stock at par by all the Company's bankers in Europe and America." The imagery presses this cosmopolitan ambition: a winged allegorical figure bears a banner reading "Excelsior," a border carries the United States motto "E Pluribus Unum" amid national flags, and a terrestrial globe is turned to display the Americas across the Atlantic.
creator: The Common Fund Company Limited
notes: The Common Fund Company Limited. Scrip H170, right to call for 100 shares at £20 each. London, December 20, 1869. Registrar: R.W. Millz. Offices: Washington, Berlin, Paris, London. Incorporated under Companies' Acts 1862–67.

title: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania Land Warrant for Survey
description: This is a partly printed, partly manuscript land warrant of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, dated the twenty-fourth day of December in the year 1793 and signed by Governor Thomas Mifflin under the lesser seal of the commonwealth. It authorises Daniel Brodhead, Esquire, Surveyor-General, to survey for Robert Morris a tract within the late purchase made from the Indians, east of the Allegheny River and Conewango Creek, the whole of the purchase money having been paid into the office of the Receiver-General at the rate of five pounds for every hundred acres. The instrument recites the enabling statutes of 1785, 1788, and 1792 that opened the state's unappropriated western lands to sale. Such a warrant was the first step by which Pennsylvania converted its frontier into revenue, pricing raw land and tying payment to survey so as to turn territory into a marketable claim. The grantee, Robert Morris, financier of the Revolution, here appears as a speculator in the western lands whose acquisition would soon ruin him. The sheet is austere, its authority resting on engrossed text, seal, and signature rather than on ornament.
creator: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania (Governor Thomas Mifflin)
notes: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania land warrant. Signed by Governor Thomas Mifflin. For survey of land in the Allegheny River/Conewango Creek purchase (Act of April 7, 1792). Directed to Surveyor-General Daniel Brodhead. For Robert A. Arres(?). 1792.
transcription:

hath applied for, and paid into the office of the Receiver-General, of the Land-Office, the whole of the purchase money for [illegible] thousand acres of land within the last purchase made of the Indians, East of Allegheny river and Conawango creek, at and after the rate of Five Pounds for every hundred acres of the fame, agreeably to an act of Assembly, of the said Commonwealth, passed the 7th day of April, 1792, entitled "An "Act to provide further regulation, whereby to secure fair and equal proceedings in the Land-Office, and in fur-"veying of lands;" to one other Act of the said Assembly dated 3d day of October 1788, entitled "A Supple-"ment to an Act, entitled "An Act to alter and amend an Act, entitled "An Act for granting and dis-"posing of the unappropriated lands within this state;" also to one passed the 3d April 1792, entitled "An "Act for the sale of the vacant lands, within this commonwealth." The said application and warrant are numbered four thousand five hundred [and] twenty [six]

There are therefore to authorize and require you to survey or cause to be surveyed for the said Robert A. Morris

the said quantity of acres, within the said late purchase, agreeably to said acts.

In Witness whereof Thomas Mifflin, Governor of the said commonwealth, hath herunto set his hand, and caused the lefs seal of the said commonwealth to be affixed, the twenty fourth day of December in the year 1792

To Daniel Brodhead, Esquire, }

title: Egyptian Enterprise and Development Company Share Warrant
description: This bilingual English-and-French bearer instrument is a deferred share warrant (Certificat de Part de Dividende au Porteur) of The Egyptian Enterprise and Development Company, entitling its holder to one deferred share. The text records a capital of £E. 160,000 increased to £E. 400,000 by resolution of the General Meeting of Shareholders of 15 December 1905, divided into 40,000 ordinary shares of £E. 10 each, the company having been empowered by Khedivial Decree of 26 November 1904. Issued at Cairo on 5 March 1906 and signed by two administrators, it belongs to the wave of joint-stock promotion that accompanied British-supervised Egypt's land and development boom. The deferred share, ranking for dividend only after the ordinary shares took their fixed return, was a leveraged claim on residual profit. The engraved frame, with its scrolling foliate cartouches and central pastoral vignette of the Egyptian countryside, frames speculative finance in the reassuring imagery of cultivated land.
creator: The Egyptian Enterprise and Development Company
notes: The Egyptian Enterprise and Development Company. Deferred share warrant to bearer No. 08527. Capital £E. 400,000. Khedivial Decree November 26, 1904. Cairo, March 5, 1906. Bilingual English/French.

title: Bank of England Stock Transfer Receipt for the New Four per Cent Annuities
description: A printed and manuscript transfer receipt for stock in the New Four per Cent. Annuities, the perpetual government annuities administered at the Bank of England. Dated the 2nd day of August 1822, it records the transfer of a £6 6s. 2d. interest or share in the joint stock to Philip Davis, Abigail Davis, and Jane Catherine Davis, for a consideration of £6 5s. 7d.—the discount of the market price below the nominal annuity reflecting prevailing yields. The form cites the Act of the third year of George IV that converted Five Pounds per Cent. annuities into Four Pounds per Cent., part of the post-war reduction of debt-service charges that followed the Napoleonic Wars. Marginal notes fix the dividend dates of 5 January and 5 July and the Tuesday-to-Friday transfer days, while a later hand records the account closed 25 November 1836. The plain, ledger-like letterpress, untouched by ornament, embodies the administrative routine of a mature market in public funds.
creator: Bank of England (New Four per Cent. Annuities)
notes: The New Four per Cent. Annuities (Bank of England). Stock transfer receipt for £6 6s. 2d. August 2, 1822. Transfer to Philip Davis, Abigail Davis & Jane Catherine Davis. Witnesses: J. Robinson (1085), H.J. Cosper. Account closed November 25, 1836. Act of 3 Geo. IV.

title: Tontine Royale Subscription Contract
description: A printed subscription contract for the French royal tontine created by the King's Edict of August 1734, executed before the Notaires au Châtelet de Paris and the Prévôt des Marchands and Échevins of Paris, here marked for the second classe, first subdivision. The Crown sold 1,463,000 livres of life annuities (rentes viagères) styled a tontine "sujette à accroissement," divided into fifteen classes of subdivisions, each share carrying three hundred livres of capital. The annuities were secured, by privilege ranking ahead of the Trésor Royal, on the droits d'Aydes et Gabelles and the cinq grosses fermes. On a subscriber's death one quarter of the arrears reverted to the King and three quarters accrued to the surviving members of the subdivision, so its last survivor alone gathered three-quarters of the revenue, paid in two installments yearly from 1 October 1734. The form shows how the monarchy harnessed longevity speculation, openly inviting unnaturalized foreigners and waiving droits d'aubaine, to fund itself amid the fiscal strains of Louis XV's early reign. An engraved cartouche bearing the city's arms lends the borrowing municipal gravity.
creator: Notaires du Châtelet de Paris
notes: French royal tontine, August 1734. Second Class, First Subdivision. 14,063,000 livres of life annuities. Drawn up before Notaires du Châtelet de Paris including Michel Etienne Turgot. Annuities charged on Aides et Gabelles revenues.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (French):**

TONTINE ROYALE Aoüst 1734. 2.e Classe.

M. De Raphanneuf
Subdivision

PARDEVANT les Contrôllers du Roy, Notaires au Chastelet de Paris, souffignez, furent presens Messire MICHEL ESTIENNE TURGOT, Chevalier, Seigneur de Souffons, Bons, Uffy, Potigny, Perciers, Brucourt, & autres lieux, Conseiller du Roy en ses Conseils, President au Parlement & en la seconde Chambre des Requestes du Palais, Prevost des Marchands; Claude-François Petit, Ecuyer, Conseiller du Roy, Quartiniers; Jean-Baptiste de Santeul, Ecuyer; Jean-Baptiste Tripart, Ecuyer, Conseiller du Roy & de la Ville; & François Touvenot, Ecuyer, Conseiller du Roy, Notaire au Chastelet, tous Eichevins de cette Ville.

Lesquels ont dit, que le Roy par son Edit du mois d'Aoüst 1734. registré où besoin a esté, auroit, pour les causes y contenués, ordonne qu'il seroit par les Commissaires de Sa Majesté nommeroît, vendu & alienné quatorze cens soixante-trois mille livres actuelles & effectives de rente viagere, sous le titre de Tontine, fusette à accroissement, distribuée en quinze classes, composées de differentes divisions, dont chaque action feroit de trois cens livres de capital; lesdigtes rentes à prendre sur le produit des droits d'Aydes & Gabelles, & des cinq grosses fermes, que Sa Majesté à [speciellement] & par privilege, affectez, obligez & hypothequez au payement & continuation des arrerages desdites rentes, même par préference à la partie du trésor Royal, dont les constitutions seroient faites à ceux qui defereroient les acquerois sous leurs noms, où autres qu'ils nommerioent, avec faculté à chaque personne de prendre tel nombre de parties de rente qu'il lui plairoit dans chaque subdivision de la classe, pour lesquelles il lui seroit expedié des contrats: Que de la rente de chaque rentier qui viendroit à deceder, un quart des arrearges de fadite rente seroit estenu à la defcharge des revenus de Sa Majesté, & les trois autres quarts appartiendieroît par accroissement aux survivans de la subdivision de la classe, entre lesquels ils seroient distribuée d'année en année jusqu'au dernier mourant; en forte que le dernier vivant de chaque subdivision de chacune desdites classes, recueilleeroit feul les trois quarts du revenu des capitaux qui composeroient ladite subdivision, & que lesdites rentes pourroient auffi estre acquises par les estrangers non naturalizez, même ceux demeùrans hors du Royaume; à l'efect de quoy Sa Majesté auroit renoncé à tous droits d'aubaine, confiscations, & autres qui pourroient lui appartenir: pour, des arrearges desdites rentes, seûoit par les acquéreurs leur vie durant, comme de leur propre chose, vray & loyal acquelt, en vertu des contracts qui seroient faits par lesdits Sieurs Brevolt des Marchands & Eichevins, & estre payé des arrearges desdites rentes à bureau ouvert par chacune année, en deux paiemens, sur lesquittances en parchemin, paffez devant Notaires en la forme portée audit Edit, à commencer du premier Octobre 1734. pour ceux qui auroient fourni leurs fonds avant le premier Janvier 1735. fans que lesdites rentes puiffent estre fujettes à aucunes faites pour quelque caufe que ce soit, même pour les dettes & affaires de Sa Majesté, ni estre reduites ni retranchées fous quelque pretexte que ce puiffe estre: en outre, que les rentes qui seroient acquises par les estrangers, seroient exemptes de toutes lettres de marque & de represaille, ainfi qu'il eft plus au long porté audit Edit; en execution duquel a esté faite vente & alienation par Messieurs les Commiffaires du Conseil, Procureurs speciaux de Sa Majesté auxdits Sieurs Brevolt des Marchands & Eichevins, desdites quatorze cens foixante-trois mille livres de rente, par contract paffé en vertu des Lettres patentes y enoncées, pardevant Bourron & ion conferere

---

# English Translation:

ROYAL TONTINE August 1734. Second Class.

M. De Raphanneuf
Subdivision

BEFORE the Controllers of the King, Notaries of the Châtelet of Paris, undersigned, were present Messire MICHEL ESTIENNE TURGOT, Knight, Lord of Souffons, Bons, Uffy, Potigny, Perciers, Brucourt, and other places, Counselor of the King in his Councils, President of Parliament and in the Second Chamber of Petitions of the Palace, Provost of the Merchants; Claude-François Petit, Esquire, Counselor of the King, Quarternier; Jean-Baptiste de Santeul, Esquire; Jean-Baptiste Tripart, Esquire, Counselor of the King and of the City; and François Touvenot, Esquire, Counselor of the King, Notary of the Châtelet, all Aldermen of this City.

Who declared that the King, by his Edict of the month of August 1734, registered where necessary, would have, for the reasons contained therein, ordered that by the Commissioners of His Majesty appointed, fourteen thousand sixty-three thousand livres of actual and effective life annuities would be sold and alienated, under the title of Tontine, subject to increase, distributed into fifteen classes, composed of different divisions, each share consisting of three hundred livres of capital; said annuities to be charged on the proceeds of the rights of Aides and Gabelles, and the five major farms, which His Majesty had specially and by privilege, affected, obliged, and mortgaged to the payment and continuation of the arrears of said annuities, even in preference to the portion of the Royal Treasury, the constitutions of which would be made to those who would purchase the shares under their names or others whom they would name, with the faculty for each person to take such number of annuity shares as they pleased in each subdivision of the class, for which they would be issued contracts: That from the annuity of each annuitant who should come to die, one quarter of the arrears of said annuity would be retained for the discharge of the revenues of His Majesty, and the three other quarters would belong by accretion to the survivors of the subdivision of the class, among whom they would be distributed year by year until the last survivor; so that the last living member of each subdivision of each of said classes would collect alone the three quarters of the revenue of the capital which would compose said subdivision, and that said annuities could also be acquired by foreigners not naturalized, even those residing outside the Kingdom; for which purpose His Majesty would have renounced all rights of escheat, confiscations, and others which might belong to him: for, from the arrears of said annuities, would be paid by the acquirers for their lifetime, as of their own property, true and loyal ownership, by virtue of the contracts which would be made by said Messieurs Brevolt of the Merchants and Aldermen, and be paid of the arrears of said annuities at open bureau by each year, in two payments, upon receipts on parchment, passed before Notaries in the form contained in said Edict, commencing from the first October 1734 for those who would have provided their funds before the first January 1735, provided that said annuities cannot be subject to any proceedings for any cause whatsoever, even for the debts and affairs of His Majesty, nor be reduced or diminished under any pretext whatsoever: furthermore, that the annuities which would be acquired by foreigners would be exempt from all letters of marque and reprisal, as it is more fully contained in said

title: Moscow & Russia Tramways Company Share
description: This is a 250-franc preferred share (action privilégiée), payable to bearer and entirely paid up, in the Compagnie Générale des Tramways de Moscou & de Russie, a Belgian société anonyme with its registered seat in Brussels. The certificate records the company's constitution by authentic act of 17 January 1885, executed before the notary Van Halteren and published in the Moniteur belge of 4 and 7 February 1885, and it is dated Brussels, 8 February 1885, beneath the signatures of two administrators. The share confers the advantages stipulated in article 44 of the statutes. It exemplifies the wave of Belgian capital that financed and operated urban transport across the Russian Empire in the 1880s, channeling Brussels finance into Moscow's tramways. The dense engraved border of foliate scrollwork and the attached sheet of dividend coupons reflect the conventions of contemporary continental securities rather than any distinct artistic program.
creator: Compagnie Générale des Tramways de Moscou & de Russie
notes: Action Privilégiée No. 00274, 250 francs fully paid. Belgian SA. Registered in Brussels. Contracts dated January 17, 1885, confirmed by M. Van Halteren, notary; published Moniteur belge February 4–7, 1885.

title: Egyptian Government Aswan and Asyut Irrigation Works Payment Warrant, London
description: A mandat de paiement (payment warrant) for £500 sterling issued by the Egyptian Government for the irrigation works of Aswan (Assouan) and Asyut (Assiout) bearing the programme year 1898. By the instrument the government acknowledges, in return for works executed and materials supplied, an absolute and unconditional debt to Messrs. John Aird & Cie.—the British firm that built the first Aswan Dam—or to the bearer of the warrant, the sum payable in London on 1 January 1932 through the Bank of England. The warrant thus converts a contractor's claim into a transferable, long-dated bearer obligation charged on the state, financing capital works against revenue to come. Engraved in red, it pairs a sphinx beneath the title with a winged Egyptian sun-disk flanked by uraei in the upper corners, antiquarian motifs that lend the colonial-era debt the dignity of a pharaonic monument. Dated 10 July 1901.
creator: Egyptian Government, Ministry of Public Works
notes: Mandat de Paiement No. 58/111, £500 Sterling. Travaux d'Irrigation du Gouvernement Égyptien, Assouân et Assiout 1898. Payable to John Aird & Cie. Due London, January 1, 1932, at Banque de l'Afrique. Interest 3½% p.a.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (French):**

NUMÉRO DE SÉRIE: 58
NUMÉRO D'ORDRE: 111

TRAVAUX D'IRRIGATION DU GOUVERNEMENT ÉGYPTIEN

ASSOUAN ET ASSIOUT 1898

MANDAT DE PAIEMENT

£500 STERLING | £500 STERLING

Le Gouvernement Égyptien déclare par les présentes, qu'en retour de travaux effectués et de matériaux fournis pour les travaux d'irrigation susmentsionnés, il reconnaît devoir, absolument, et sans condition aucune, à MM. JOHN AIRD et CIE. la somme de Cinq cents livres sterling (£500).

Le Gouvernement s'engage par les présentes à payer à MM. JOHN AIRD et CIE., ou au PORTEUR DE CE MANDAT, le 1er Janvier 1932 la dite somme de Cinq cents livres sterling.

Ce paiement s'effectuera à LONDRES, par l'intermédiaire de la Banque d'Angleterre, contre la remise de ce mandat.

Ce paiement sera effectué à tout événement AU PORTEUR des présentes, en totalité et sans déduction quelconque et indépendamment de toute contestation qui peut être actuellement ou qui pourrait s'élever dans la suite quelconque. [Underlined text in box: "Gouvernement"]

MM. John Aird et Cie., ou de toute autre contestation quelle qu'elle soit, la dette reconnue par les présentes étant pour une somme certaine et déterminée et constituant une créance liquide et reconnue par le Gouvernement Égyptien.

Le présent mandat confère au porteur, jusqu'à son entier désintéressement, une affectation sur les travaux pour assurer le paiement de la somme indiquée dans ce mandat, le dit porteur pourra, d'accord avec les porteurs de tous mandats sans par rapport aux mêmes travaux (dont le maximum ne devra pas toutefois excéder les limites mentionnées dans la table au dos des présentes) ou d'accord avec la majorité de ces porteurs, nommer ou faire nommer par l'autorité compétente un représentant chargé de mettre à exécution de la manière qu'il appartiendra le dit droit d'affectation, au cas où ce mandat n'aura pas été payé à l'échéance.

Aucune prise de possession par le Gouvernement des travaux ni aucun autre acte quelconque ne seront susceptibles de porter atteinte à la dite affectation.

Le présent mandat et tous autres mandats émis ou à émettre dans les limites susmensionnées auront rang égal et jouiront sans aucun droit de préférence ni de priorité à raison de leur numéro, série, date d'émission ou autre circonstance quelconque.

En date du 30 Juillet 1901 | Pour le Gouvernement,
[Signatures and handwriting] | Le Ministre des Travaux Publics.

Contresigné
[Signature]
Ingénieur du Gouvernement Égyptien.

---

# English Translation:

SERIES NUMBER: 58
ORDER NUMBER: 111

IRRIGATION WORKS OF THE EGYPTIAN GOVERNMENT

ASWAN AND ASSIUT 1898

PAYMENT WARRANT

£500 STERLING | £500 STERLING

The Egyptian Government declares by these presents, that in return for work executed and materials supplied for the above-mentioned irrigation works, it acknowledges owing, absolutely and unconditionally, to MM. JOHN AIRD and CO. the sum of Five Hundred Pounds Sterling (£500).

The Government undertakes by these presents to pay to MM. JOHN AIRD and CO., or to the BEARER OF THIS WARRANT, on 1st January 1932 the said sum of Five Hundred Pounds Sterling.

This payment shall be made in LONDON, through the intermediary of the Bank of England, against the surrender of this warrant.

This payment shall be made in any event TO THE BEARER of these presents, in full and without any deduction whatsoever and independently of any contestation which may currently exist or which may subsequently arise in any manner. [Underlined in box: "Government"]

MM. John Aird and Co., or any other contestation whatsoever, the debt acknowledged by these presents being for a certain and determined sum and constituting a liquid claim recognized by the Egyptian Government.

The present warrant confers upon the bearer, until full satisfaction, a charge upon the works to secure payment of the sum indicated in this warrant. The said bearer may, in agreement with the bearers of all other warrants relating to the same works (the maximum of which shall not, however, exceed the limits mentioned in the table on the reverse of these presents) or in agreement with the majority of such bearers, appoint or cause to be appointed by the competent authority a representative charged with executing in the manner which shall be appropriate the said right of charge, in the event that this warrant shall not have been paid at maturity.

No taking into possession by the Government of the works nor any other whatsoever act shall be capable of affecting the said charge.

The present warrant and all other warrants issued or to be issued within the limits aforesaid shall rank equally and shall enjoy without any right of preference or priority by reason of their number, series, date of issue or other circumstance whatsoever.

Dated 30 July 1901 | For the Government,
[Signatures] | The Minister of Public Works.

Countersigned
[Signature]
Engineer of the Egyptian Government.

---

- **Royal seal:** Five Pound Lion seal in upper right corner
