- **Pink/magenta stamp marks:** Rectangular stamp or approval mark visible in the document
- **White circular marks:** Several white circular markings (possibly from document handling or filing)
- **Language:** French (official language for Egyptian Government international financial documents of the period)

title: Court of Chancery Injunction Bond, Madison County, Tennessee, 1852
description: A handwritten injunction bond, the printed and manuscript instrument by which a complainant and his sureties bind themselves in the penal sum of nineteen hundred dollars before the Court of Chancery at Jackson, for the district of Madison County, Western Division of the State of Tennessee. The obligors—Nathan H. Mallon[?] together with Miles F. Robinson and Jas. M. Sharp—undertake the obligation as a condition of the injunction granted upon the complainant's bill against Thomas W. Farnell and others; the bond is voided if the suit prevails, otherwise the penalty stands, securing the defendants' costs and damages. Sealed and dated the 15th day of April, 1852. The document's substance lies in its security, justified to the court not in land or coin but in two enslaved people: "Boyle, age 52, value 900.00" and "Loren, age 41, value 1100.00"—two thousand dollars in human chattel pledged against a chancery injunction, a stark instance of how the antebellum Southern legal economy capitalized enslaved persons as ordinary collateral for civil suretyship.
creator: Nathan H. Ballon; John E. Bostick
notes: Chancery Court injunction bond, $1,900. Court of Chancery, Jackson, Madison County, Western Division, Tennessee. Nathan H. Ballon plaintiff; John E. Bostick surety. Dated April 10, 1852. Property security approved by Court.
transcription:
# Transcription

Know all men by these presents, that we, Nathan H. Ballon & Mccoy D. Walmores & Deo E. Bostick, Jas W. Cleaph

are held and firmly bound unto Res. a. Farnell & a. Sauncer, Tably. P. W. & Nathaniel Atcherwerds, Jas S. Hargrood Lowry, Jassella Starges Elyan Thos, Jas Millers, Elyard Mary & M'Leay,

in the just and full sum of Nineteen hundred dollars

dollars, for the payment of which, well and truly to be made, we and each of us, bind ourselves, our heirs, executors and administrators, jointly and severally, firmly, by these presents, sealed with our seals, and dated this 10th day of April 1852

The condition of the above obligation is such, that whereas the above bound

N. Ballon

this day filed a Bill of complaint in the Clerk's office of the Court of Chancery, at Jackson, for the district composed of the county of Madison, for the Western Division of the State of Tennessee, against said

Thos Thos Farnell and others)

and prayed for and obtained an Injunction upon the matters set forth in said Bill. Now should the said,

Haltoone

prosecute his suit on said Bill with effect, or in case he shall fail therein, shall pay to the said

Leaph,

all such costs and charges as may accrue in consequence thereof, and stand to, abide by and perform such order, judgment or decree, as may be made by said Court, touching said Injunction; then the above obligation to be void; otherwise, to remain in full force and virtue.

[Handwritten notes at bottom:]
Security for Bond this [illegible]
Slave [illegible] or 52 yales 900. D.N.
Hiren age 41, value 900 D.
Accepted by Court John E. Bostick Ced

---

**Notable markings:**

- Handwritten annotations throughout in cursive script
- Signatures of Nathan H. Ballon and John E. Bostick
- Court approval notation at bottom
- Property valuation notes indicating security ($900+)
- Ages noted: 50 and 41 years
- Dated: 10th day of April 1852
- Handwritten note in upper left corner: "Two Slaves or Bond 1900"

title: Unilever N.V. Option Certificate, Rotterdam, July 1937
description: This is an option certificate (optiebewijs No. 97790) issued by Unilever N.V. of Rotterdam in July 1937, void after 30 June 1942. Eight such certificates, surrendered together, entitle the holder to acquire one certificate for a single ordinary Unilever share of f.1000 nominal, at f.1950 (195 per cent) through 31 December 1938 and f.2150 (215 per cent) from 1 January 1939, denominated in Dutch guilders. The option springs from Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V.'s 3½ per cent loan of 1937, capped at f.30,000,000, granting bondholders an equity sweetener. Issued the year Unilever was renamed Lever Brothers & Unilever N.V. (noted in a later overprint), the instrument captures the Anglo-Dutch margarine and soap combine binding debt to share subscription. The sober blue lithographed border, free of allegory, mirrors the technical, contractual character of the document.
creator: Unilever N.V.
notes: Optiebewijs No. 97790. Unilever N.V., Rotterdam. Eight certificates entitle holder to subscribe to 8 new shares at f.20 each (f.160 total). Valid until June 10, 1942. Related to Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. 3½% Obligatielening, 1937.
transcription:
# Unilever N.V. Option Certificate No. 97790

**Original (Dutch):**

UNILEVER N.V.
GEVESTIGD TE ROTTERDAM.

OPTIEBEWIJS No. 97790

VAN ONWAARDE NA 30 JUNI 1942.

Bij notariële acte dd° 23 November 1937 is de naam der Unilever N.V. veranderd in: „Lever Brothers & Unilever N.V.". De naam der Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. werd bij notariële acte dd° 2 September 1938 gewijzigd in: „Van den Bergh's en Jurgens' Fabrieken N.V.".

De houder van acht dezer optiebewrijzen heeft het recht, tegen inlevering dezer bewijzen van Unilever N.V. een certificaat van een gewoon aandeel in Unilever N.V., groot ƒ 1000.— nominaal, te verwerven tot de prijs van ƒ 1950.— (195%) tot en met 31 December 1938 en van ƒ 2150.— (215%) tot en met 1 Januari 1939 tot en met 30 Juni 1942, op de voorwaarden vervat in de aan de keerzijde van dit bewijs vermelde bepalingen. Overeenkomstig art. 5 dieser bepalingen worden bij de storting 3½%-obligatien, behorende tot de in 1937 ten laste van Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. uitgegeven 3½%-obligatielening, ten hoogste groot ƒ 30.000.000.—, in betaling aangenomen tot haar volle nominale bedrag, vermeererd met opgelopen rente.

ROTTERDAM, Juli 1937.

[Secretary signature]
SECRETARIS.

[Chairman signature]
LID VAN DE RAAD VAN BESTUUR.

JOH. ENSCHEDÉ EN ZONEN - HAARLEM

---

**English Translation:**

UNILEVER N.V.
ESTABLISHED IN ROTTERDAM.

OPTION CERTIFICATE No. 97790

OF NO VALUE AFTER 30 JUNE 1942.

By notarial deed of 23 November 1937, the name of Unilever N.V. was changed to: "Lever Brothers & Unilever N.V.". The name of Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V. was changed by notarial deed of 2 September 1938 to: "Van den Bergh's and Jurgens' Fabrieken N.V.".

The holder of eight of these option certificates has the right, upon return of these certificates to Unilever N.V., to acquire a certificate of an ordinary share in Unilever N.V. with a nominal value of ƒ 1,000.— at the price of ƒ 1,950.— (195%) until and including 31 December 1938 and ƒ 2,150.— (215%) until and including 1 January 1939 and until 30 June 1942, under the conditions set forth in the provisions stated on the reverse of this certificate. In accordance with article 5 of these provisions, upon deposit of the 3½%-bonds belonging to the 3½%-bond loan issued in 1937 by Van den Bergh's Fabrieken N.V., up to a maximum amount of ƒ 30,000,000.—, shall be accepted in payment at their full nominal value, plus accrued interest.

ROTTERDAM, July 1937.

---

**Notable Markings:**

- Two handwritten signatures: one labeled "SECRETARIS" (Secretary) and one labeled "LID VAN DE RAAD VAN BESTUUR" (Member of the Board of Directors)
- Orange circular seal in upper right corner with Dutch coat of arms and text "50 C" (denomination marking)
- Serial number: **No. 97790**
- Ornate blue decorative border frame throughout
- Printer mark at bottom: "JOH. ENSCHEDÉ EN ZONEN - HAARLEM" (Johannes Enschedé and Sons, Haarlem - a prominent Dutch security printer)
- Date: Rotterdam, Juli 1937 (July 1937)

title: United States of Brazil Funding Bond
description: This is a $100 bearer bond of the United States of Brazil, styled the "5 Per Cent 20-Year Funding Bond of 1931," dated October 1, 1931 and due October 1, 1951. It promises to pay the bearer one hundred dollars in gold coin of the United States, with semi-annual coupons at five per cent per annum, through the fiscal agency of the City Bank Farmers Trust Company in New York (successor to Dillon, Read & Co.), and at the holder's option in London. The instrument belongs to Brazil's 1931 funding loan, a depression-era restructuring that consolidated short-term external obligations and capitalized unpaid interest into long-dated paper as coffee revenues collapsed and gold drained away. The engraved arms of the Republic — the celestial sphere ringed by stars beneath the motto "Ordem e Progresso" — flanked by seated allegorical figures of labor and abundance, present sovereign distress in the reassuring vocabulary of order and productive promise.
creator: United States of Brazil
notes: Brazil 5% 20-Year Funding Bond of 1931, $100. No. S2940. Due October 1, 1951. Decree No. 20,251, August 1931. Semi-annual coupons. Payable at City Bank Farmers Trust Company, New York.
transcription:
# Transcription of United States of Brazil 5% 20-Year Funding Bond of 1931

## Main Text Transcription:

**UNITED STATES OF BRAZIL**

**5 PER CENT. 20-YEAR FUNDING BOND OF 1931**

**DUE OCTOBER 1, 1951**

No C
82940                                                                                                82940
No C

United States of Brazil, wheresoever issued the Government for value received, promises to pay to the bearer hereof on the first day of October, 1951 (unless before said date this bond shall have been duly called for redemption), and payment of the accruing upon prior date, pursuant to the principal sum of

**ONE HUNDRED DOLLARS ($100)**

in gold coin of the United States of America of the present standard of weight and fineness existing October 1891, with interest thereon from the first day of October, 1931, at the rate of five per cent. per annum, payable semi-annually on the first and last days of October in each and every year until this bond shall be paid. Principal and semi-annual interest on this bond shall be made only upon presentation and surrender of the attached interest coupons as they severally mature. Both principal and interest shall be paid without exchange or other deduction to any person in London, England, at the office of a paying agent to be nominated by Dalton, Read & Co., in advising of Bankers buying such bonds, or in New York, at the office of the City Bank Farmers Trust Company, or such other paying agent as the United States of Brazil may designate.

Principal of and interest on this Bond shall also be collectible at the offices of the bearer hereof in London, England, at the office of a paying agent to be nominated by Dalton, Read & Co., in advising of bankers buying such bonds, or in New York, at the office of the City Bank Farmers Trust Company, or such other paying agent as the United States of Brazil may designate.

[EXTENSIVE LEGAL TEXT regarding funding loan details, consolidation of prior debts, and financial covenants - much of the fine print is challenging to read with complete accuracy]

The Sterling Bonds and the Time Bonds are similarly entitled to the benefits of an Equivalent Gold Coin provision. [text continues with financial and legal terms]

Dated, October 1,1931.

**United States of Brazil**

title: Westchester & Philadelphia Rail Road Co. Scrip, Philadelphia, 1860
description: This is an interest-bearing scrip certificate of the Westchester & Philadelphia Rail Road Co., authorized by resolution of the board on September 29th, 1860, and issued at Philadelphia on the first day of October, A.D. 1860. The company acknowledges itself indebted to Washington Pastorius in the sum of fifty-eight dollars, which it promises to pay him or his assigns on the first day of October, 1865, with interest at the rate of six per cent per annum from its date, the whole attested by the treasurer's signature and the company seal. Scrip of this kind let a cash-poor Pennsylvania short-line settle obligations with paper rather than coin, postponing payment five years while binding holders to the road's fortunes. The plain engraved heading and modest scrollwork, free of allegory, suit a workaday corporate instrument meant to circulate among contractors and creditors rather than to impress.
creator: Westchester & Philadelphia Rail Road Co.
notes: Scrip Certificate No. 262. Westchester & Philadelphia Rail Road Co. Washington Hastings, $1,865 at 6% interest. Board resolution September 29, 1860. Dated October 10, 1862.

title: Wisconsin Investment Company Class A Common Stock Certificate, Chicago, 1930
description: A certificate for one hundred shares of Class A common stock, without nominal or par value, in the Wisconsin Investment Company, a holding concern incorporated under the laws of Delaware. The face is engraved in orange and black and dated at Chicago, 15 November 1930, countersigned by the Continental Illinois Bank & Trust Company (Chicago Trust Company) as transfer agent. Attached at left is a detachable warrant, bearing the same number, that entitles the holder to receive one-tenth of a share of Class B common stock for each Class A share, without further cost, exercisable on or before May 1, 1934, after which it is void; fractions are settled in scrip. This stock-dividend warrant marks the document as an instrument of late-1920s investment-trust finance, in which leverage and layered share classes promised holders an enhanced participation. Its issuance in November 1930, just past the Crash, situates it at the unraveling of that boom. The vignette of a seated female figure bearing a torch between two terrestrial hemispheres voices a familiar emblem of commerce and enlightenment encircling the globe.
creator: Wisconsin Investment Company
notes: Stock Warrant No. C8081, 100 shares. Wisconsin Investment Company. Issued to Mary E. Carey. Orange-printed certificate with globe vignette and company seal. ca. 1930s.

title: Y. Rofé & Co. Suez Canal Obligation Sale Agreement
description: A blank, preprinted preliminary bond-sale agreement (Acte Préliminaire de Vente d'Obligations), "valable après la confirmation des vendeurs par lettre séparée," issued by the banking and brokerage house Y. Rofé & Cie of Cairo (Le Caire, 5 Sharia El Maghraby), with branches at Alexandrie, Port-Saïd and Mansourah. By its terms the firm undertakes to sell Suez Canal Company 3% bonds, 2nd Series (Oblig. Canal de Suez 3% 2ème Série) at a lump-sum price (prix forfaitaire), with payment due partly on signature and the balance in instalments, definitive certificates to be delivered at the Cairo head office only after full payment, and recourse governed by article 338 of the Code Civil Mixte. The mixed-court framework and multi-city brokerage reflect the cosmopolitan financial milieu of Egypt under the Capitulations, where Suez Canal securities ranked among the most actively traded international stocks. The engraved letterhead, with its allegorical figure and scrolled cartouche, follows conventional finance-printing ornament rather than any single movement.
creator: Y. Rofé & Co., Le Caire
notes: Acte Préliminaire de Vente d'Obligations, Oblig. Canal de Suez 3%, 2ème Série. Y. Rofé & Co., Cairo; offices in Port-Said, Alexandrie, Mansoura. Reference to Code Civil Mixte, Art. 238. Ca. late 19th century.

payable in the following manner:
P.T. _________________ in cash, upon signature of the present deed and the balance in _________________
installments of P.T. _________________ each, the last of which being P.T. _________________ due on the first of
each month, beginning the following month.

2° The present preliminary deed shall bind the House Y. ROFÉ & Co. only after confirmation by it notified to the purchaser by separate letter.

This letter shall concern the principal conditions of the present contract.

The purchaser shall, in case he should find any difference whatsoever between the conditions of the present contract and those which shall be stated in the confirmation letter, notify his claims by registered letter to the House Y. ROFÉ & Co., within 30 days following the date of the Confirmation letter. In the absence of which the conditions stated in this last letter shall be irrevocable to the purchaser.

3° After payment of the final installment of the agreed price, the House Y. ROFÉ & Co. shall deliver to the purchaser, with a regularly receipted note which shall constitute the definitive deed of sale, the bond certificates mentioned, the ownership of which shall be acquired at that moment only and lawfully under Art. 338 of the Mixed Civil Code.

The delivery of the aforementioned bonds shall take place exclusively at the Registered Office of the Social Company Y. ROFÉ & Co., in Cairo, 15 days after payment of the balance of the agreed price.

The purchaser shall return to the House Y. ROFÉ & Co. the present contract together with all related documents, accompanied by a regular discharge signed by him establishing receipt of the bonds.

title: Deutsches Reich Treasury Note
description: A Treasury note (Schatzanweisung) of the German Reich, second issue (Ausgabe II) of 1923, with a face value of five million mark Reichswährung (5 000 000 ℳ). Issued at Berlin on 25 May 1923 by the Reichsschuldenverwaltung (Imperial Debt Administration) under the law of 2 May 1923, it bears interest at a floating rate tied to the Reichsbank discount rate, bounded between a minimum of 8 and a maximum of 15 per cent, and is redeemable from 1 September 1927. An attached strip of half-yearly coupons falls due each 1 March and 1 September of 1926 and 1927, void after 1930–1931. The very device of indexing interest to the discount rate testifies to the runaway depreciation of the mark in the spring of 1923: a fixed coupon had become meaningless, and the Reich could borrow only by promising a yield that would chase inflation upward. The sober, classical frame and the imperial eagle assert continuity and creditworthiness even as the currency they are denominated in dissolved.
creator: Reichsschuldenverwaltung
notes: 8–15% Schatzanweisung des Deutschen Reichs, Ausgabe II, Buchstabe A, No. 73723. 5,000,000 Mark Reichswährung. Issued May 20, 1923. Runs from September 1, 1921; matures December 31, 1930. Attached coupon strip. Weimar hyperinflation era.

title: Eendracht Polder Bond, Zaamslag (Zeeland), 1779
description: This part-printed, part-manuscript bond records a debt undertaken by the Heeren van Zaamslag, acting as administrators of the newly diked Eendracht Polder on the island of Axel in Zeeland. The undersigned acknowledge themselves justly indebted to Pieter Paul van Gelce, or the lawful bearer ("wettigen Toonder"), in the capital sum of One Hundred Flemish Pounds (£100 Ponden Vlaams), borrowed to finance the reclamation and its works, at 3 per cent interest free of charges yearly. Beyond fixed interest, the holder shares by lot in the profits of the polder's produce, blending a loan with a speculative claim on the harvest. Sealed at Sluys, the first of July 1779, the instrument illustrates how Zeeland's water-management ventures drew on transferable, bearer-payable credit to fund the costly business of winning land from the sea.
creator: Heeren van Zaamslag
notes: Eendracht Polder bond, No. 353. Heeren van Zaamslag, island of Axel, Zeeland. Capital: £100 Flemish at 3% p.a. Issued to Pieter Paul van Gelce or bearer. Holders entitled to participate in polder produce profits distributed by lot. Dutch land reclamation finance, ca. 18th century.

# Transcription

[Handwritten at top, partially illegible]
Shat Engel 28 Stanj Gormo July 1779

Wy Ondergeschreven als Heeren van ZAAMSLAG, en Geinteresfecrden in de voltrokken nieuwe Dykagie genaamd *den Eendracht Polder*, in den Eilande van *Axel*, bekennen en verklaren wel en deugdelyk schuldig te wezen aan [handwritten] *Dhr. Pieter Paul van Gelce* — of wettigen Toonder dezes, Eene Capitale Somme van *Een Honderd Ponden Vlaams*, ipruitende ter zake van deugdelyk geleende en op Intrest vertrekkte Penningen, by Ons in voornaemde qualiteit ontfangen en genoten; renunciërende derhalven van de exceptie van onaagetelden gelde; en zulx om die Penningen benevens meerdere Capitaalen tot voltrekking van boveugemelde Bedyking met de dependentien van dien te emploiëeren: Beloovende derhalven het voorschreve Capitaal van £ 100:--:-- met daarenboven den Intrest van dien à 3 per Cento vry Geld jaarlyx en langer loopende na rato des tyds, te zuillen betalen en aflossfen aan voorschreve

title: Russian Gold Loan Coupon Sheet
description: A sheet of detachable interest coupons (talon) belonging to a bond of the Imperial Russian Government's 4% Gold Loan, Sixth Issue of 1894. The underlying obligation carries a face value of 187 roubles 50 kopeks, set as one fifteenth of an imperial and presented alongside its equivalents in French francs, German reichsmark, pounds sterling, Netherlands florins, and United States dollars — a polyglot pricing that reveals the loan's placement simultaneously across Europe's principal financial centres. Each small coupon entitles the holder to a semi-annual interest payment of the 4% rate, redeemable at St. Petersburg, Berlin, Paris, London, and Amsterdam. The text appears in Russian and French on one sheet and in German and English on the other, addressing the international syndicate of subscribers who financed the late tsarist state.

pages.description: The Russian and French face of the talon. A header names the 4% gold loan, sixth issue of 1894, and the 187 rouble 50 kopek obligation, with its franc, reichsmark, sterling, florin, and dollar equivalents. Below, a grid of detachable coupons (numbered roughly 97 to 116) each states amount, due date, and paying cities. Page 1 of 2.

The German and English face of the same talon, headed "Russische 4% Gold-Anleihe, sechste Emission, von 1894" and "Russian 4% Gold Loan, Sixth Issue, 1894." A continuing grid of coupons, German entries left and English right, repeats the obligation's terms and lists redemption at St. Petersburg, Berlin, Paris, London, and Amsterdam. Page 2 of 2.

creator: Imperial Russian Government
notes: Coupon sheet (talon), Russian 4% Gold Loan, Sixth Issue 1894. Denomination: 187 roubles 50 kopeks (= 500 fr. / £20 / 405 marks / $96.25). No. X035911. ~20 detachable coupons, ca. nos. 97–116. Russian text with French headers.

# Russian 4% Gold Loan, Sixth Issue of 1894 — Coupon-Renewal Talon — Transcription

## Original:

(Vertical marginal numerals at the outer top corners: **1913** at left, **1923** at right)

(Serial number printed at both upper corners:) **№ 059944**

**Российскій 4% золотой заемъ, шестой выпускъ 1894 года. ТАЛОНЪ къ облигаціи въ 187 руб. 50 коп. (1 рубль = ¹⁄₁₅ империала).**

**Emprunt Russe 4% or, sixième émission, 1894. TALON de l'obligation de 187 roubles 50 cop. (1 rouble = ¹⁄₁₅ d'impériale).**

(Left-hand paragraph, in Russian:)

По предъявленіи сего талона, съ 19 Декабря 1923 г./1 Января 1924 г. по 18 Іюня/1 Іюля 1925 г., будетъ выданъ безвозмездно, въ мѣстахъ, назначенныхъ для оплаты купоновъ, новый купонный листъ, если, однако, до дня выдачи его не будетъ предъявлена другимъ лицомъ для той же цѣли самая облигація. Въ этомъ случаѣ новый купонный листъ выдается лишь предъявителю облигаціи. Послѣ 18 Іюня/1 Іюля 1925 г. талонъ сей не дѣйствителенъ, и новый купонный листъ будетъ выданъ [лишь по] предъявленіи самой облигаціи. Къ облигаціямъ, вышедшимъ въ тиражъ 18 Сентября/1 Октября 1923 г. и [въ] предыдущіе [тиражи], новые купонные листы не будутъ выдаваемы.

(Right-hand paragraph, in French:)

Contre remise du présent talon, à partir du 19 Décembre 1923/1 Janvier 1924 jusqu'au 18 Juin/1 Juillet 1925, il sera délivré, aux guichets désignés pour le service des coupons, une nouvelle feuille de coupons si, auparavant, le détenteur de l'obligation même n'a point présenté son titre pour ledit objet. Dans ce cas, la nouvelle feuille de coupons est délivrée au détenteur seul. Après le 18 Juin/1 Juillet 1925, le présent talon devient nul et sans valeur, et la nouvelle feuille de coupons ne sera délivrée que sur présentation du titre même. Pour les obligations sorties au tirage le 18 Septembre/1 Octobre 1923 et jusqu'aux tirages antérieurs, il ne sera point délivré de nouvelles feuilles de coupons.

**Директоръ** (Director) — (manuscript signature)

---

(Below the talon: a grid of 24 detachable interest coupons, arranged in 6 rows × 4 columns, numbered 97 to 120. Each coupon is identical in wording; one full coupon — No. 117, the first cell — is transcribed here in full.)

**РОССІЙСКІЙ 4% ЗОЛОТОЙ ЗАЕМЪ, 6й ВЫП. 1894 Г.**

117-й купонъ къ облигаціи **№ 059944**

19 Марта / 1 Апрѣля 1923 г. уплачивается въ Конторахъ и Отдѣленіяхъ Государственнаго Банка 1 рубль 87½ копѣекъ (1 руб. = ¹⁄₁₅ империала). Дѣйствителенъ въ теченіе 10 лѣтъ.

(large ornamental coupon numeral: **117**)

**EMPRUNT RUSSE 4% OR, 6ᵐᵉ EMISSION, 1894.**

117ᵉ coupon de l'obligation, payable le 19 Mars/1 Avril 1923 : à Paris 5 fr., à Berlin 4 m. 4 pf., à Londres 3 sh. 11½ p., à Amsterdam 2 fl. 39 c., à New-York 96¼ c.

(The remaining coupons repeat this format identically, each carrying its own number and its own quarterly payment date given in the Old-Style/New-Style dual calendar. The four columns of each row correspond to the four quarters — 1 April, 1 July, 1 October and 1 January — and the six rows descend by year:
- Row 1 — coupons 117 (19 Марта/1 Апрѣля 1923), 118 (18 Іюня/1 Іюля 1923), 119 (18 Сентября/1 Октября 1923), 120 (19 Дек. 1923/1 Января 1924);
- Row 2 — coupons 113–116 (1922 / 1 Jan 1923);
- Row 3 — coupons 109–112 (1921);
- Row 4 — coupons 105–108 (1920);
- Row 5 — coupons 101–104 (1919);
- Row 6 — coupons 97–100 (1918).
Each coupon bears the serial № 059944, the amount 1 руб. 87½ коп. / 5 fr., and the same five-city payment clause.)

---

## English Translation:

(Marginal corner numerals: **1913** at left, **1923** at right)

(Serial, at both upper corners:) **No. 059944**

**Russian 4% Gold Loan, Sixth Issue of the year 1894. TALON to the bond of 187 roubles 50 kopeks (1 rouble = ¹⁄₁₅ of an imperial).**

**Russian 4% Gold Loan, sixth issue, 1894. TALON of the bond of 187 roubles 50 cop. (1 rouble = ¹⁄₁₅ of an imperial).**

(Left paragraph, from the Russian:)

On presentation of this talon, from 19 December 1923/1 January 1924 to 18 June/1 July 1925, a new coupon sheet will be issued free of charge, at the places designated for the payment of coupons, provided that, before the day of its issue, the bond itself has not been presented for the same purpose by another person. In that case the new coupon sheet is issued only to the presenter of the bond. After 18 June/1 July 1925 this talon is invalid, and the new coupon sheet will be issued [only upon] presentation of the bond itself. For bonds drawn in the redemption drawing of 18 September/1 October 1923 and [in] previous [drawings], no new coupon sheets will be issued.

(Right paragraph, from the French:)

Against surrender of the present talon, from 19 December 1923/1 January 1924 until 18 June/1 July 1925, a new coupon sheet will be delivered, at the counters designated for the coupon service, provided that the holder of the bond itself has not previously presented his title for that object. In that case the new coupon sheet is delivered to the holder alone. After 18 June/1 July 1925 the present talon becomes null and void, and the new coupon sheet will be delivered only upon presentation of the title itself. For bonds drawn in the drawing of 18 September/1 October 1923 and in the earlier drawings, no new coupon sheets will be delivered.

**Director** — (manuscript signature)

**RUSSIAN 4% GOLD LOAN, 6th ISSUE, 1894.**

On 19 March / 1 April 1923 there is paid, at the Offices and Branches of the State Bank, 1 rouble 87½ kopeks (1 rouble = ¹⁄₁₅ of an imperial). Valid for 10 years.

(ornamental numeral: **117**)

(The other coupons repeat this format, each with its own number and quarterly Old-Style/New-Style date: coupons 97–120 run quarterly from 1 April 1918 (row 6, bottom) to 1 January 1924 (row 1, top), all bearing serial No. 059944, the amount 1 rouble 87½ kopeks / 5 francs, and the same five-city payment clause.)

title: Chinese Imperial Government 5% Gold Loan Bond, 1903
description: A 500-franc bearer bond (Obligation au Porteur) from the Imperial Chinese Government's 5% Gold Loan of 1903 (Emprunt Chinois 5% Or 1903), issued at Brussels on 12 April 1905. The loan, totalling 25,000,000 francs in 50,000 bonds of 500 francs, was dedicated exclusively to constructing the railway between Kaifeng-Fou and Honan-Fou in Honan province, undertaken through the Compagnie Impériale des Chemins de Fer Chinois and Belgian railway interests under Imperial edicts of the Kuang-Su reign. Interest of 25 francs runs at 5% per annum, payable each 1 January and 1 July in gold; the bond is redeemable over twenty years by annual drawings from the tenth year. French text dominates the ornate orange border, framed by Chinese imperial seals and signatures, expressing late Qing sovereign borrowing in European capital markets.

pages.description: The bond's face: a pagoda-form orange border crowns the titles "Gouvernement Impérial de Chine, Emprunt Chinois 5% Or 1903," and "Obligation de Cinq Cents Francs au Porteur." French text states the railway purpose, the 25-franc annual interest, and thirty-year redemption. Chinese seals, three authorising signatures, and two attached coupons (57th, 58th) appear. "500 Francs" sits at base. Page 1 of 2.

The reverse, printed in dense French. An "Obligation Générale" preamble is followed by two "Édit de S.M. l'Empereur de Chine" (dated 1899 and 1903) and a "Création de l'Emprunt" extract of the contract. A twenty-column amortisation table (1915–1934) details capital, interest, and totals across the loan's life. A signature closes the text. Page 2 of 2.

creator: Imperial Chinese Government
notes: Gouvernement Impérial de Chine. Emprunt Chinois 5% Or 1903. Obligation de 500 Francs au Porteur, No. 30659. Brussels, April 12, 1907. Capital: 60,000,000 francs. Interest 5% p.a. in gold, semi-annual. Guaranteed by Chinese Imperial Customs revenues. Late Qing dynasty foreign loan.

# Transcription

**Original (French):**

GOUVERNEMENT IMPÉRIAL DE CHINE

EMPRUNT CHINOIS
5% OR 1903

AU CAPITAL DE 25.000.000 DE FRANCS
Représenté par 50.000 Obligations de 500 Francs

Émis en vertu de l'Édit de S.M. l'Empereur de Chine du 20 Octobre 1903.

OBLIGATION DE CINQ CENTS FRANCS
AU PORTEUR

N° 30,659

Reportant les intérêts annuels de 25 francs, payables par semestre le 1er Janvier et le 1er Juillet de chaque année,
remboursable au pair en 40 années par tirage au sort et conversion à la Banque de France et autres banques désignées

LE PREMIER TIRAGE AURA LIEU LE TROISIÈME MERCREDI D'AVRIL 1913

Le présent Emprunt est destiné exclusivement à l'établissement d'une ligne de Chemin de fer entre Kaifong-Fou
et Honan-Fou, villes situées dans la province du Honan.

L'Ordre l'Obligation Générale du Gouvernement Impérial Chinois, en Emprunt, de même que celui dont la création
est prescrite par le Décret Impérial du 20 Octobre 1903, sera organisé en exploitation, à une garantie
spéciale de premier rang sur la ligne de Chemin de fer, matériel roulant et ses et produits.

Le Ministère Plénipotentiaire de Chine à Bruxelles s'est spécialement chargé de signer les titres et de en révéler de
son.

Les Titres sont exempts, à tout jamais, de tout impôt Chinois, présent et à venir, ainsi que de tout autre
prélèvement de la part de l'Etat de Chine.

Le paiement des intérêts et des titres sortis se fera à Bruxelles et à Paris.

Bruxelles, le 12 Avril 1905.

POUR LE GOUVERNEMENT IMPÉRIAL CHINOIS :

LE DIRECTEUR GÉNÉRAL DES CHEMINS DE FER CHINOIS,          LE MINISTRE PLÉNIPOTENTIAIRE DE CHINE À BRUXELLES,

Par délégation de la Compagnie Générale
des Chemins de fer
et du Min. des Finances.

[Signatures and Chinese characters present]

**English Translation:**

IMPERIAL GOVERNMENT OF CHINA

CHINESE LOAN
5% GOLD 1903

WITH A CAPITAL OF 25,000,000 FRANCS
Represented by 50,000 Bonds of 500 Francs

Issued by virtue of the Edict of H.M. the Emperor of China of October 20, 1903.

OBLIGATION OF FIVE HUNDRED FRANCS
TO BEARER

No. 30,659

Bearing annual interest of 25 francs, payable semi-annually on January 1st and July 1st of each year,
redeemable at par over 40 years by drawing and conversion at the Bank of France and other designated banks.

THE FIRST DRAWING SHALL TAKE PLACE ON THE THIRD WEDNESDAY OF APRIL 1913

This Loan is intended exclusively for the establishment of a railway line between Kaifeng and
Honan-Fu, cities located in Honan Province.

The General Obligation of the Imperial Government of China, in Loan, as well as that which the creation
is prescribed by the Imperial Decree of October 20, 1903, shall be organized in operation, with a special
guarantee of first rank on the railway line, rolling stock and its products.

The Minister Plenipotentiary of China in Brussels has been specially charged with signing the titles and verifying them.

The Titles are exempt, for all time, from all Chinese taxes, present and future, as well as from any other
levies on the part of the Chinese State.

Payment of interest and redeemed bonds shall be made in Brussels and Paris.

Brussels, April 12, 1905.

FOR THE IMPERIAL GOVERNMENT OF CHINA:

THE GENERAL DIRECTOR OF CHINESE RAILWAYS,          THE MINISTER PLENIPOTENTIARY OF CHINA IN BRUSSELS,

title: Imperial Russian 4% Gold Loan Bond, Sixth Issue (125 Rubles)
description: An Imperial Russian Government 4% Gold Loan bearer bond, the Sixth Issue of 1894 (Российскій четырехпроцентный золотой заемъ, шестой выпускъ 1894 года), in the Class A denomination of 125 gold rubles, equivalent to 500 French francs. Authorised by Imperial ukase, the loan funded the empire's treasury at four per cent, redeemable by annual drawings over half a century. Surmounted by the Romanov double-headed eagle and framed by dense engraved guilloche borders, the bond renders its obligation in Russian on one face and in French, German, and English on the reverse, with cross-rates in marks, sterling, florins, and dollars. Coupons were payable across the financial capitals of Europe, advertising Russia's reach into Western capital markets on the eve of the Franco-Russian alliance.

pages.description: Russian-language face. The Imperial double-headed eagle crowns the title "ИМПЕРАТОРСКОЕ РОССІЙСКОЕ ПРАВИТЕЛЬСТВО" above "Четырехпроцентный золотой заемъ, шестой выпускъ 1894 года." Centred is the obligation "ОБЛИГАЦІЯ въ сто двадцать пять рублей золотомъ" (125 gold rubles / 500 fr.), marked "НА ПРЕДЪЯВИТЕЛЯ" (to bearer), Class A. A block of fine print lists paying banks and redemption terms above engraved signatures. Page 1 of 2.

Reverse repeating the obligation in four parallel columns: French ("GOUVERNEMENT IMPÉRIAL DE RUSSIE — Emprunt Russe 4% Or"), German ("Kaiserlich Russische Regierung"), and English ("Imperial Government of Russia — Russian 4 per cent Gold Loan, Sixth Issue, 1894"). Each states the 125-rouble value with equivalents of 500 francs, 404 marks, 19 livres 16s 5d sterling, 239 florins, and 96.50 dollars, payable to bearer, followed by dense conditions of the loan. Page 2 of 2.

creator: Imperial Russian Government
notes: Russian Imperial 4% Gold Loan, Sixth Issue 1894. Obligation No. 59944, 125 Rubles Gold (= 500 fr.). Ornate design with Imperial double-headed eagle. Total loan: 125,000,000 rubles at 4%, redeemable over 50 years. Imperial ukase February 3, 1894.
transcription:
# Imperial Russian 4% Gold Loan Bond, Sixth Issue of 1894 — 125 Gold Rubles (Class A, No. 059944) — Transcription

## Original:

(Top-left corner) **125 РУБ. ЗОЛ.** / 6й выпускъ.
(Top-right corner) **500 FR.** / 6me émission.
(Handwritten pencil annotation in top-right margin — [illegible])

(Decorative upper banner, around the double-headed Imperial eagle)
**ИМПЕРАТОРСКОЕ РОССІЙСКОЕ ПРАВИТЕЛЬСТВО**
(Denominations engraved in the left/right border ovals: "500 Franc" and "404 Marks" — decorative guilloche)
(Circular ink stamp at right — [illegible])

**РОССІЙСКІЙ ЧЕТЫРЕХПРОЦЕНТНЫЙ ЗОЛОТОЙ ЗАЕМЪ, ШЕСТОЙ ВЫПУСКЪ 1894 ГОДА,**

на сто тринадцать милліоновъ шестьсотъ тысячъ рублей золотомъ

= 454.400.000 фр. = 367.155.200 имп. герм. марокъ = 17.971.520 фунт. стерл. = 217.203.200 голл. гульд. = 87.472.000 долларовъ золотомъ.

выпущенный на основаніи ВЫСОЧАЙШАГО Указа 9 Февраля 1894 года

въ количествѣ: 399975 листовъ по одной облигаціи (въ 125 р.) съ №000001 по №399975, 91420 листовъ по пяти облигацій (въ 625 р.) съ №399976 по №857075 и 2069 листовъ по двадцати пяти облигацій (въ 3125 р.) съ №857076 по №908800, для замѣны не погашенныхъ еще акцій, дивидендныхъ акцій и учредительскихъ паевъ Главнаго Общества Россійскихъ жел. дорогъ.

**А.**

**ОБЛИГАЦІЯ** — **125 Руб. Зол.**

**№059944**

**ВЪ СТО ДВАДЦАТЬ ПЯТЬ РУБЛЕЙ ЗОЛОТОМЪ**

= 500 фр. = 404 герм. марк. = 19 фунт. ст. 15 ш. 6 п. = 239 голл. гульд. = 96,25 долл. зол.,

записанная въ Государственную Долговую Книгу, кн. [3 — handwritten] стр. [96 — handwritten]

**НА ПРЕДЪЯВИТЕЛЯ.**

Владѣлецъ сей облигаціи имѣетъ право на сумму СТО ДВАДЦАТЬ ПЯТЬ рублей золотомъ, приносящую ЧЕТЫРЕ процента въ годъ дохода до погашенія оной тиражемъ. Облигація сія освобождена навсегда отъ всякихъ русскихъ налоговъ. Проценты уплачиваются четыре раза въ годъ: (20 Марта / 1 Апрѣля — 19 Іюня / 1 Іюля — 19 Сентября / 1 Октября — 20 Декабря / 1 Января) по предъявленіи купоновъ, по желанію владѣльца:

въ С.-ПЕТЕРБУРГѢ: въ ГОСУДАРСТВЕННОМЪ БАНКѢ, золотомъ или кредитными билетами по курсу дня;

въ ПАРИЖѢ: въ Парижско-Нидерландскомъ Банкѣ, Ліонскомъ Кредитѣ, Національной Учетной Конторѣ, въ Отдѣленіи Русскаго для Внѣшней Торговли Банка и у Гг. Готтингеръ и К°, во франкахъ;

въ ЛОНДОНѢ: въ Агентствѣ Русскаго для Внѣшней Торговли Банка, въ фунтахъ стерлинговъ;

въ БЕРЛИНѢ: у Гг. Мендельсонъ и К°, въ герм. маркахъ;

въ АМСТЕРДАМѢ: у Гг. Липманъ, Розенталь и К°, въ голландскихъ гульденахъ;

въ НЬЮ-ЙОРКѢ: у Гг. Берингъ, Магунъ и К°, въ долларахъ золотомъ.

Облигаціи сіи подлежатъ выкупу по нарицательной цѣнѣ въ теченіе 81 года, посредствомъ имѣющихъ быть въ Государственной Коммиссіи Погашенія Долговъ два раза въ годъ, 20 Марта / 1 Апрѣля и 19 Сентября / 1 Октября каждаго года, тиражей, начиная съ 19 Сентября / 1 Октября 1894 года. Для этой цѣли отчисляется въ каждое полугодіе 0,08428[%] съ нарицательнаго капитала займа, съ присоединеніемъ 2% на всѣ погашенныя облигаціи.

До 19 Декабря 1903 г. / 1 Января 1904 г. вышеупомянутое отчисленіе на погашеніе займа не будетъ увеличиваемо, а равно до того же срока не будетъ приступлено и къ выкупу, ни къ конверсіи настоящаго займа.

Уплата капитала по вышедшимъ въ тиражъ облигаціямъ производится за тиражемъ срока платежа процентовъ, въ тѣхъ же городахъ и въ тѣхъ же валютахъ, какъ и уплата купоновъ.

Купоны, срокъ коимъ истекаетъ послѣ срока уплаты капитала, должны быть представлены вмѣстѣ съ облигаціями; въ противномъ случаѣ, сумма недостающихъ купоновъ будетъ удержана при выдачѣ капитала по вышедшей въ тиражъ облигаціи.

Облигаціи сего займа сохраняютъ силу въ теченіе 30 лѣтъ со сроковъ, назначенныхъ для ихъ оплаты, а купоны сихъ облигацій — въ теченіе 10 лѣтъ со срока ихъ оплаты.

Облигаціи сіи снабжены купонами за 19 Декабря 1903 г. / 1 Января 1904 г. и талономъ, для возобновленія купоновъ.

По истеченіи срока купоннаго листа, представляющемъ талонъ отъ облигацій, которыя до тѣхъ поръ не вышли въ тиражъ, будутъ выданы, безплатно, новые купонные листы, въ мѣстахъ оплаты купоновъ.

Облигаціи сіи выпускаются именныя и на предъявителя. Правила относительно обращенія именныхъ облигацій въ предъявительскія и обратно утверждаются Министромъ Финансовъ.

(Signature line, engraved facsimile signature)
Управляющій Государственною Коммиссіею Погашенія Долговъ [signature]
Бухгалтеръ [signature]

## English Translation:

(Top-left) **125 GOLD RUBLES** / 6th issue.
(Top-right) **500 FR.** / 6th issue (émission).
(Handwritten pencil note in the top-right margin — [illegible])

(Upper banner, around the double-headed Imperial eagle)
**IMPERIAL RUSSIAN GOVERNMENT**
(Denominations in the border ovals: "500 Franc" and "404 Marks")
(Circular ink stamp at right — [illegible])

**RUSSIAN FOUR-PER-CENT GOLD LOAN, SIXTH ISSUE OF THE YEAR 1894,**

for one hundred and thirteen million six hundred thousand gold rubles

= 454,400,000 francs = 367,155,200 Imperial German marks = 17,971,520 pounds sterling = 217,203,200 Dutch guilders = 87,472,000 gold dollars.

issued on the basis of the IMPERIAL Ukase of 9 February 1894

in the quantity of: 399,975 sheets of one bond each (of 125 r.) from No. 000001 to No. 399975; 91,420 sheets of five bonds each (of 625 r.) from No. 399976 to No. 857075; and 2,069 sheets of twenty-five bonds each (of 3,125 r.) from No. 857076 to No. 908800, for the replacement of the still-unredeemed shares, dividend shares, and founders' shares of the Main Company of Russian Railways.

**A.**

**BOND** — **125 Gold Rubles**

**No. 059944**

**FOR ONE HUNDRED AND TWENTY-FIVE GOLD RUBLES**

= 500 fr. = 404 German marks = 19 pounds sterling 15 shillings 6 pence = 239 Dutch guilders = 96.25 gold dollars,

entered in the State Debt Book, vol. [3 — handwritten], p. [96 — handwritten].

**TO BEARER.**

The holder of this bond is entitled to the sum of ONE HUNDRED AND TWENTY-FIVE gold rubles, yielding FOUR per cent per annum in income until its redemption by drawing. This bond is forever exempt from all Russian taxes. Interest is paid four times a year (20 March / 1 April — 19 June / 1 July — 19 September / 1 October — 20 December / 1 January) upon presentation of the coupons, at the holder's choice:

in ST. PETERSBURG: at the STATE BANK, in gold or in credit notes at the rate of the day;

in PARIS: at the Banque de Paris et des Pays-Bas, the Crédit Lyonnais, the Comptoir National d'Escompte, at the Branch of the Russian Bank for Foreign Trade, and at Messrs. Hottinguer & Co., in francs;

in LONDON: at the Agency of the Russian Bank for Foreign Trade, in pounds sterling;

in BERLIN: at Messrs. Mendelssohn & Co., in German marks;

in AMSTERDAM: at Messrs. Lippmann, Rosenthal & Co., in Dutch guilders;

in NEW YORK: at Messrs. Baring, Magoun & Co., in gold dollars.

These bonds are subject to redemption at par over the course of 81 years, by means of drawings to be held at the State Commission for the Redemption of Debts twice a year, on 20 March / 1 April and 19 September / 1 October of each year, beginning on 19 September / 1 October 1894. For this purpose there is set aside each half-year 0.08428[%] of the nominal capital of the loan, with the addition of 2% on all redeemed bonds.

Until 19 December 1903 / 1 January 1904 the above-mentioned redemption allocation shall not be increased, and likewise before that same date there shall be no recourse to redemption or to conversion of the present loan.

Payment of the capital on bonds drawn is made, following the drawing, at the date of interest payment, in the same cities and the same currencies as the payment of the coupons.

Coupons whose maturity falls after the date of payment of the capital must be presented together with the bonds; otherwise, the amount of the missing coupons will be withheld upon payment of the capital on the drawn bond.

The bonds of this loan remain valid for 30 years from the dates set for their payment, and the coupons of these bonds for 10 years from the date of their payment.

These bonds are furnished with coupons up to 19 December 1903 / 1 January 1904 and with a talon for the renewal of coupons.

Upon expiry of the coupon sheet, against the talon of bonds which have not by then been drawn, new coupon sheets will be issued free of charge at the places of coupon payment.

These bonds are issued as registered and to bearer. The rules concerning the conversion of registered bonds into bearer bonds and vice versa are confirmed by the Minister of Finance.

(Engraved facsimile signatures)
Manager of the State Commission for the Redemption of Debts [signature]
Accountant [signature]

title: Life-Annuity Lottery Ticket of the City of Hamburg, 1773
description: This printed ticket represents one lot (Loß) in the Leib-Renten-Lotterey der Stadt Hamburg — the Life-Annuity Lottery of the City of Hamburg — published on 23 April 1773. Its bearer (Zeiger dieses) holds the lot under the noted number and motto for Zwey Hundert Mark Banco, two hundred Mark Banco, a sum recorded as transferred on the date of issue to the Cämmerey Lotterey-Conto, the Treasury's lottery account in the Hamburg Bank. The instrument shows how a free Hanseatic city raised funds by yoking the appeal of a lottery to the security of a life annuity: subscribers staked their capital, and the fortunate drew annuities paid from the municipal chamber. The engraved achievement crowning the sheet — a crowned shield bearing the city's towered castle, flanked by two lions and topped with plumes — asserts civic authority and the credit of Hamburg standing behind the obligation.
creator: City of Hamburg (Kämmerei)
notes: Leib-Renten-Lotterey der Stadt Hamburg. No. 7027, 200 Mark Banco. Published April 23, 1773. Credited in Kämmerei Lotterie-Conto in Banco. Life annuity lottery; Hamburg city coat of arms. Municipal finance instrument.

title: Connecticut Comptroller's Interest Receipt, Hartford, 1789
description: This printed-and-manuscript receipt records the payment of interest on Connecticut's Revolutionary War debt. It acknowledges that the bearer has received of Oliver Wolcott, Comptroller of the Public Accounts, one pound fifteen shillings and three pence in lawful money, paid in a single certificate as the interest due on four State Notes whose principal amounts to £29 6s 11¼d, computed to the first of February 1789. It was issued at the Comptroller's Office in Hartford and dated 4 April 1789, the sum being taken for John Douglass and signed on his behalf by Stephen Crosby. The form's plainness is itself eloquent: a young state, lacking specie, met its obligations to noteholders by issuing fresh certificates rather than coin, and such receipts trace the laborious bookkeeping by which the public credit was kept alive in the years before federal assumption of the states' debts.
creator: Oliver Wolcott, Comptroller of Public Accounts, Connecticut
notes: Receipt No. 2435. Oliver Wolcott, Comptroller of Public Accounts, Connecticut. Interest on 4 State Notes (principal £29.6.4½): £1 15s 3d Lawful Money, computed to February 1, 1789. Issued to John Douglas. Hartford, April 4, 1789.

title: Koninklijke Fransche Lyfrente Negotiatie van Maart 1781 — Nominee Register, Amsterdam, 1787
description: A notarial register, headed "Copia," recording nominees in a Dutch life-annuity loan on the French crown — the "Koninglyke Fransche Lyfrente Negotiatie van Maart 1781" directed by Pieter Stadnitski and Hendrik Vollenhoven. Executed at Amsterdam on the first of May 1787 before notary Abraham van Beem, with Abraham Fock and Adriaan Scharff as commissioners, it concerns fifty-nine contracts of 25,000 livres capital each and here lists nominees Nos. 30 through 59, all named women (Jufvrouw). A marginal note records that No. 74, on the life of Elisabeth Judith de Vries, lapsed at her death on 14 February 1787. The document belongs to the speculative Dutch trade in French rentes viagères of the 1780s, in which investors pooled funds to buy royal annuities written on young, long-lived nominees so that survivors absorbed the income of those who died — a wager on longevity that helped finance a French monarchy sliding toward insolvency.
creator: Abraham Fock; Adriaan Schaap (Commissioners)
notes: Subscription list Nos. 30–59 for the Compagnie Française Lijfrente Negotiatie van Maart 1781. Amsterdam, May 1, 1787. Commissioners: Abraham Fock and Adriaan Schaap. Nominees predominantly women. Dutch negotiatie on French royal life annuities (rentes viagères).

En de Wel-Ed. Heeren ABRAHAM FOCK en ADRIAAN SCHARFF, als Commisarissen
In de nasteemde Negotiatie, Comparanten binnen deeze Stad, my Notaris bekend.

Verklarende, dat door de twee Heeren eerte Comparanten, ingevolge het door Hun uitgegeven Bericht, van diverso Participanten, volgens geteekende Reçepissen is gexpedieerd, een Capital van Seven maal Honderd Vyftig Duizend Guldens Hollandsch Courant Geld, te-gen zoodanige Jaarlykshe Interesten, als hier na zal worden gezegd, ten einde dezelve opgeuomen
Penningen tegen een vastgestelden Knop van Een Millioen en Vyfmaal Honderd Duizend
Guilders, ingevolge, gevoegd op de Conditiën, leder van Lyvres genoeg, of te Conditiën, leder
renverende 10 pCt. op een Hoofd, dog belast met de korting van de tiende Penning, en dezelve
te beschermen met Jeugne Participanten binnen deze Stad de Negotiatie der Provisie, Premie, en Courtugie &c. hoe ooft genoeg, en voorts dat dezulve Contraten zouden worden geconstitueerd, op de Ly-ven van gevolg Jongc Juffrouwen, als tuschen de 5 en 8 Jaaren, en geluigid op de mannen en
Notaris GERRIT HOUSMAN en Getuygen, door gemeinde twee Heeren laaste Comparanten en van my Notaris, notaris enz. Heeren eerste Comparanten gepaseerd, staat vermeld, en welcke
Acte, onder de Pariering dezer Negotiatie, ter Viffe van de Eigenars en Gsinteesteerderens , ten
Compagne van my Notaris, berustende blijft.

Ingevolge, en ter voldoening van 't invoege voorneld uitgegeven Bericht, zijn door de twee
Heeren eerste Comparanten, aan my Notaris, ten overftan, en in prefentie van de twee Heeren
laaste Comparanten ter hand getuigid ; Zoo als in Notaris, ook bekende ten Fine genaageld, overge-
Negen-en-Vyftig Contracten, van Vyf-en-Twinttig Duizend Livres Capital, ieder op een
Hoofd, renverende 10 pCt., onder korting van de roode Penning, van de Koninglyke Fransche Lyf-
rente Negotiatie, gearriseert by Edizt van Maart 1781. Staande alle op de Nanmen van ABRA-
HAM FOCK, ADRIAAN SCHARFF en ABRAHAM VAN BEEM, genaomde en belegid op
de Lyven hier na gemeld. Zijnde reeds in de Constitutie, een Contract van £ 250001, beleyd
en Lyve ELISABETI JUDITH de VRIES, N°. 74, door huw overlayden (voorgevellan den
14 Februarij 1787. Waat van aan my Notaris de dood Cedulil is geteekitbeert.) Vervallen, znde

title: Hope & Co. Certificate for Russian 5% Silver Bonds, Amsterdam, 1834
description: This is a certificaat of the fifth series (vijfde reeks) of 5 per cent Russian Funds in Silver (Russische Fondsen in Zilver), representing a capital of five hundred roubles, issued at Amsterdam on 29 August 1834 by the Dutch banking houses Hope & Comp., Ketwich & Voombergh, and the Weduwe Willem Borski. It entitles the bearer to interest at 5 per cent, payable on 1 April and 1 October against coupons, the administration resting with the notarial office of Commelin & Weyland, with whom the original Russian inscriptions were deposited. Such bearer certificates let Amsterdam savers hold Russian sovereign silver debt at one remove, the Dutch houses interposing their own credit between the tsar's treasury and the Dutch rentier. The sober engraved frame, free of allegory, advertises probity rather than splendour, befitting an instrument whose value rested wholly on the standing of the issuing houses.
creator: Hope & Comp.; Ketwich & Voombergh; Weduwe Willem Borski
notes: Certificaat der Vijfde Reeks, 5% Russische Fondsen in Zilver. No. 13, 500 Roubles. Amsterdam, August 29, 1834. Issued by Hope & Comp., Ketwich & Voombergh, Weduwe Willem Borski. Interest 5% p.a., April 1 and October 1. Last coupon April 1, 1839.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

---

CERTIFICAAT
DER VIJFDE REEKS,
van 5 per cent Russische Fondsen
IN ZILVER.

fl. [handwritten: 1/2]

HOUDER dezes is voor een Kapitaal van VIJF HONDERD ROEBELS, geregistrigd tot den mede-eigendom van oorspronkelijke inschuijvingen van Russische Fondijen in ZILVER, à 5 per cent Interest, betaalbaar alle 2de MAANDEN op 1s April en 1s October, ingevolge Regtsgeltje Uittase in dato 2 Juny 1834, welke door het Kantoor van Administratie onder Directie van

HOPE & COMP.,
KETWICH & VOOMBERGH,
EN
WEDUWE WILLEM BORSKI,

zijn gedeponneerd ten Kantore van de Notarissen COMMELIN & WEYLAND.

De Intereesen verschillende 1 April en 1 October, zullen voor rekening en risico van den Houder ontvangen en uitbetaald worden op COUPONS, overeenkomstig Art. 6 van het Bewust, in dato 30 Juny 1824.

De HOUDER of HOUDERS dozer Certificaten, zullen ten allen tijde geregtigd zijn, de Certificaten, welke zij tegen overdracht, tegen intrekking der CERTIFICATEN met de Ontverschenen Coupons het Subject tot zijne in Maart van [Nieuwe Coupons] daaraoe herhoudende, en tegen betaling van de onkosten in Art. 10 van het BEWUST vermeld, mits de Certificaten wij worden ingeleverd bij partijen van 10 stuks, representeerende een Kapitaal van Z. R. 5000.

Amsterdam den 29 Augustus 1834

[Signatures - illegible handwriting]

Serijn by my
Commelin
Notaris.

[Additional signatures]

Hierbij afgegeven Geldparse Coupons la 1s April 1839 en Talon.

## English Translation:

**CERTIFICATE
OF THE FIFTH SERIES,
of 5 per cent Russian Funds
IN SILVER.**

fl. [handwritten: 1/2]

THE HOLDER hereof holds for a Capital of FIVE HUNDRED ROUBLES, registered as joint owner of original subscriptions of Russian Funds in SILVER, at 5 per cent Interest, payable every 2 MONTHS on 1st April and 1st October, according to Legal Deed of 2 June 1834, which has been deposited at the Office of Administration under the Direction of

HOPE & COMP.,
KETWICH & VOOMBERGH,
AND
WIDOW WILLEM BORSKI,

are deposited at the Office of the Notaries COMMELIN & WEYLAND.

The Interest accruing from 1 April and 1 October shall be received and paid out to the Holder at his risk and cost on COUPONS, in accordance with Art. 6 of the Deed, dated 30 June 1824.

The HOLDER or HOLDERS of this Certificate shall at all times be entitled, the Certificates which they have against transfer, against the redemption of CERTIFICATES with unpaid Coupons, the Subject to its right in March of [New Coupons] thereunto binding, and against payment of the costs mentioned in Art. 10 of the DEED, provided the Certificates are delivered by parties of 10 pieces, representing a Capital of Z. R. 5000.

Amsterdam, 29 August 1834

title: Dutch Certificate for Swedish Crown Debt
description: This printed Dutch certificaat documents a creditor's stake in the debt of the Swedish Crown (Zweedsche Kroon) negotiated in Holland. Against a deposit of Swedish obligations of three thousand guilders (Drie duizend Gulden) nominal capital, the holder has received a repartition of fifteen percent of that nominal sum, paid out of the first three installments remitted by His Majesty the King of Sweden toward the discharge of these debts, and retains a proportional claim on future distributions as the remaining installments fall due and the outstanding annuities and obligations (Lijfrenten en Obligatiën) are divided among the creditors. Issued at Amsterdam on 10 October 1816 in the name of W. G. van de Poll, with a handwritten Specificatie of the deposited obligations below. The document reflects the orderly settlement of Swedish sovereign borrowing held by Dutch lenders in the years after the Napoleonic wars, when Amsterdam's capital market parcelled foreign loans into tradeable fractional claims. The plain typographic frame, engrossed sums, and notarial signatures speak to function over ornament.
creator: W.G. van de Poll
notes: Certificaat No. 1475. Dutch certificate for Swedish Crown debt in Holland. Holder: W.G. van de Poll. First three installments paid; remaining nominal capital ~3,000 Dutch guilders. Amsterdam, October 10, 1816. Post-Napoleonic Swedish debt restructuring.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

N°. 1475

CERTIFICAAT.

Houder dezes, ten gevolge van het dépôt van de hier onder gespecificeerde [*handwritten: Mtg ahi*], ten lasten der Zweedsche Kroon in Holland genegotieerd, groot [*handwritten: Jielduizen*] [*handwritten: Drie duizend Guilden*] nominal Kapitaal, ontvangen hebbende de, uit de betaling der drie eerste termijnen, van het geen van weegens Zijne Majesteit den Koning van Zweden tot kwijting dezer schulden in Holland is aangenomen te voldoen, bepaalde repartitie van vijftien percent van gemeld nominal Kapitaal, blijft als nu nog verder geregtigd, om, in evenredigheid tot het zelve nominal Kapitaal Zweedsche [*handwritten: Mtg ahi*] te deelen, in het geen, na uitbetaling van weegens het Zweedsche Gouvernement der drie overige termijnen, russchen de houders der Lijfrenten en Obligatiën ten lasten de Zweedsche Kroon zal blijken te kunnen worden verdeeld.

Zijnde dit Certificaat, ook namens de Heer *W. G. van de Poll*, ingevolge door denzelven ten dien einde special verleende qualificatie, geteekend en afgegeven in Amsterdam den 10. [*handwritten: Oktbr*] – 1816, door [*signature*]
[*handwritten: hag. batavus 10.10.bre 10. 10.1816.*]

SPECIFICATIE:

[*handwritten: g 0.M. twee L°° 1199. g223 & 5043.*]

---

## English Translation:

The holder hereof, in consequence of the deposit of the hereby specified [*amount*], charged to the Swedish Crown negotiated in Holland, for the amount of [*Three Thousand Guilders*] nominal Capital, having received, from the payment of the first three installments, of that which His Majesty the King of Sweden has undertaken to pay in Holland for the discharge of these debts, the determined distribution of fifteen percent of said nominal Capital, remains now further entitled to share, in proportion to the same nominal Capital of the Swedish [*amount*], in that which, after payment by the Swedish Government of the three remaining installments, between the holders of Annuities and Obligations charged to the Swedish Crown shall appear to be able to be distributed.

Being this Certificate, also in the name of Mr. *W. G. van de Poll*, pursuant to the special qualification granted by the same for that purpose, signed and issued in Amsterdam on 10th [*October*] – 1816, by [*signature*]
[*The Hague, October 10, 1816.*]

SPECIFICATION:

[*handwritten: g 0.M. two L°° 1199. g223 & 5043.*]

title: Chinese Imperial Government Gold Loan Bond
description: This is a bilingual English and French bearer bond for £20 Sterling, one of a £5,000,000 issue placed under the Imperial Chinese Government Gold Loan of 1908. Authorised by Imperial Edict of 8 October 1908 and floated through the Banque de l'Indo-Chine, it was signed and dated at Paris on 1 March 1909. The bond bears interest at 5 per cent per annum until 5 October 1923 and thereafter at 4½ per cent until redemption on 5 October 1938, the descending coupon reflecting the lender's expectation of improving Chinese credit over the term. Repayment is unconditionally guaranteed by the Imperial Government and secured by a first charge on the provincial revenues of Chekiang, Kiangsu, Hupei and Chihli, to the extent of 4,250,000 Kuping Taels yearly. The engraved vignette, printed in green within an ornate border, depicts a walled city crowned by a tiered pagoda gate-tower above a river crossed by an arched stone bridge and plied by junks, an image of an old order seeking Western capital on the eve of the dynasty's fall.
creator: Imperial Chinese Government; Banque de l'Indo-Chine
notes: Imperial Chinese Government Gold Loan 1908. £5,000,000 total. Bond No. B106822, £20 Sterling. Paris, March 15, 1908. Banque de l'Indo-Chine. Bilingual English/French. Green ornate border. Backed by Chinese Imperial Customs revenues. Late Qing railway finance.

title: Hollandsche Garantie- & Trust Compagnie German Reich Certificate
description: A 6 percent certificate of inscription (Certificaat van Inschrijving) in the German national debt register, the Reichsschuldbuch (here styled "Grootboek van het Duitsche Rijk"), for 2,000 Reichsmark, maturing 31 March 1943. The bearer holds a share in a 6 percent inscription registered in the name of the Hollandsche Garantie- & Trust Compagnie of Amsterdam, which acted as trustee; the governing conditions were fixed by notarial deed of 19 November 1928 before Notary B. H. Grijseels and printed on the reverse. The certificate is dated Amsterdam, November 1928, and carries an orange Dutch revenue stamp valued at 10, with a sheet of twenty semi-annual coupons and a talon once attached. Issued during the Weimar Republic's post-hyperinflation stabilisation, it shows a Dutch trust house repackaging German sovereign debt for Netherlands savers, intermediating cross-border capital through registered, fiduciary inscription. The plain, ruled typographic frame announces sober financial respectability rather than ornament.
creator: Hollandsche Garantie- & Trust Compagnie
notes: 6% Certificaat van Inschrijving, Grootboek Duitsche Rijk (Reichsschuldbuch). No. 0128, 2,000 Reichsmark, Series per 1943. Amsterdam, November 1928. Hollandsche Garantie- & Trust Compagnie. Notary B.H. Grepels. Dutch investment vehicle for Weimar-era German government bonds.

title: Royal Dutch Petroleum Stock Purchase Warrant, No. 015284
description: This is a stock-purchase warrant issued by the Koninklijke Nederlandsche Maatschappij tot Exploitatie van Petroleumbronnen in Nederlandsch-Indië (Royal Dutch Company for the Working of Petroleum Wells in the Dutch East Indies), a naamlooze vennootschap seated at 's-Gravenhage and founded by deed of 16 June 1890. Dated The Hague, February 1937, it grants the bearer an option on the company's equity: until 31 March 1940 one share of f.1,000 nominal may be bought for f.4,500, and from 1 April 1940 to 31 March 1943 for f.5,000, the instrument becoming void thereafter. The graduated exercise prices, set well above par, register the prized standing of Royal Dutch shares in the late 1930s and the firm's method of raising capital by selling forward claims on stock. Engraved in restrained classical letterforms with a finely guilloché green border, the document bears a 50-cent revenue stamp and the printer's mark of Lankhout, The Hague.

title: Compagnie des Indes Life Annuity (Rente Viagère), Paris
description: This is a notarial act drawn at the Châtelet de Paris, cast in the prévôtal form headed by Gabriel-Jérôme de Bullion, Prévôt of Paris, before whose notaries the parties appeared. By it the Syndic and Directeurs of the Compagnie des Indes (the French East India Company), acting in the Company's name, constitute a rente viagère — a life annuity — of 275 livres, payable yearly in coin from the Company's funds, purchased by Charles-Claude Poyrier, Écuyer, Conseiller-Secrétaire du Roi, and his wife, Dame Élizabeth Louise, but measured on the life of their son, Louis François, who is to enjoy it after the death of his parents. Dated 13 January 1725 at Paris, the act invokes the royal Council edicts of February and 20 June 1724 that had reorganized the Company after the collapse of John Law's System. The annuity forms part of a lottery-tontine (loterie d'accroissement), the shares of surviving annuitants growing as fellow rentiers die. One sees here how the rechartered Company met its obligations not through tradable shares but through such annuities secured on its own revenues — a quieter instrument of credit in the wake of speculative ruin. The engraved notarial cartouche, with its caduceus and bound emblems, asserts the public faith underwriting the deed.
creator: Compagnie des Indes (French East India Company)
notes: Compagnie des Indes legal document, January 13, 1745, Paris. Director: Gabriel-Jérôme de Bullion. Concerns share transfers and Loterie de Saint-Quentin proceeds. References King's Council edict February 3, 1724. Parties include Dame Juliane Elizabeth de Guise Holler.
transcription:
# Compagnie des Indes Life Annuity (Rente Viagère), Paris — Transcription

## Original:

*(Language: French. Notarial deed of the Prévôté / Châtelet de Paris. Printed form with handwritten insertions in the blanks, plus manuscript marginal notes and a fiscal stamp. Handwritten portions are rendered in *italics*; printed portions in roman.)*

*(Top left, manuscript date:)* *13 Janvier 1725.*

*(Upper centre, engraved tax stamp of the généralité:)* **ACT. DES N. — 13 SOLS 4 D. — GEN. DE PARIS** *(caduceus device between two medallions)*

*(Above centre, manuscript:)* *né 27 8bre 1717* *(i.e. "né 27 octobre 1717")*

*(Centre, second inked stamp / cartouche:)* **PREMI. D'EXPE. 4. — FEUIL. DITIONS. LIVR.**

*(Upper right, manuscript file annotations:)* *Mr et Mad.e / Poyrieu. / N° 336.*

---

*(Main printed body, opening initial "A":)*

**A** TOUS ceux qui ces presentes Lettres verront, Gabriel-Jerosme de Bullion, Chevalier Comte Desclimont, Mestre de Camp du Regiment de Provence Infanterie, Conseiller du Roy en tous ses Conseils, Prevost de la Ville, Prevoté & Vicomté de Paris, SALUT. Sçavoir faisons que pardevant M[es] *[handwritten: Et Jean Gaschieu]* Conseillers du Roy, Notaires, Gardes-nottes & Gardes-scel de Sa Majesté au Chastelet de Paris, soussignez, furent presens *[handwritten: Estienne Demeunier fils]* *Escuyer* ...... Syndic de la Compagnie des Indes, Et *[handwritten: sieur François Mouchard & Vincent pierre Fromaget Escuyer]*

Directeurs de ladite Compagnie; lesquels en vertu de l'Arrest du Conseil d'Estat du Roy du 15. Fevrier 1724. par lequel Sa Majesté a accordé à ladite Compagnie le Privilege Exclusif de faire dans l'estenduë du Royaume, differentes Loteries; Et en execution de la Déliberation du 13. Decembre audit an, prise dans l'Assemblée generale d'Administration de ladite Compagnie, au sujet de la Loterie composée de Lots payables, les uns en Argent, & les autres en Rentes Viageres, qui doivent former un corps de Tontine de Vingt-huit mille sept cens cinquante Numero, chacun d'iceux, susceptible d'accroissement, à mesure des decés des Rentiers interessez à ladite Loterie; sçavoir, pour moitié au profit de ladite Compagnie, Et pour l'autre moitié à ceux des Rentiers survivans, que le sort préferera dans les Loteries qui doivent estre tirées annuellement & successivement. Lesquelles Rentes Viageres qui seront assignées sur le fonds de ladite Compagnie, lesdits Sieurs Syndic & Directeurs ont pouvoir de constituer tant au profit des Sujets de Sa Majesté, que des Estrangers, ausquels le payement des Arrerages & des Accroissemens en doit estre fait, quand même ils resideroient hors du Royaume, & même en temps de guerre, sans que lesdites Rentes puissent estre retranchées ou saisies pour quelque cause que ce soit, pas même pour les propres deniers & affaires de Sa Majesté, conformément à l'Arrest du 20. Juin 1724. ainsi qu'il est plus au long porté en ladite Déliberation, par laquelle il est permis aux Acquereurs desdites Rentes, d'en faire passer les Contracts sous le nom de telles personnes qu'ils voudront choisir, pour en joüir, tant par eux, que par ceux qu'ils nommeront sur leurs Quittances, pendant la vie des personnes sur la teste desquelles les Constitutions auront esté mises. Ont lesdits Sieurs Syndic & Directeurs comparans, en vertu desdites Déliberations & Arrests du Conseil des 15. Fevrier & 20. Juin 1724. vendu, créé & constitué, assis & assigné par ces presentes; Et promis pour & au nom de ladite Compagnie des Indes, garentir de tous troubles & autres empêchemens generalement quelconques, A

*[handwritten, filling the blank:] Mre Charles Claude Poyrieu Escuyer Conseiller Secretaire du Roy Receveur general des finances d'Orleans Et Dame Elizabeth Louise Mollée son Epouse, pour joüir par eux et le survivant d'eux de la rente cy après sur la teste et pendant la vie de Louis François Poyrieu leur fils qui la jouira après le decds [decès] de sesd[its] pere et mere, lesd[its] sieur et D[am]e Poyrieu, elle dudit sieur son mary autho[risée] à l'effet des presentes, demeurant à paris Rue Thevenot, à ce jour[d'huy] les acceptant Acquereurs, Deux cent soixante quinze [livres]*

de Rente Viagere; laquelle Rente lesdits Sieurs Syndic & Directeurs, comparans pour & au nom de ladite Compagnie des Indes, promettent de faire bailler & payer en Especes sonnantes par chacun an, à Bureau ouvert, des Deniers de ladite Compagnie, à *[handwritten: usd[its] s[ieu]r Et Dame Poyrieu, Et après eux aud[it] s[ieu]r leur fils]* .......... sous Quittances, qui ne pourront estre passées que *[printed text cut off at foot of leaf]*

*(Foot right, manuscript notary signature:)* *M. Bouttra[ys?]* *(with paraph)*

*(Foot left, faint manuscript:)* *le 1. [illegible]*

*(Left margin, manuscript approbation note, largely illegible:)* *[illegible] ... dix* *(with paraph)*

---

*(Right margin, manuscript accretion notes, in later cursive; readings of the tontine numbers are uncertain:)*

*Inventaire soixante quatorze* *(with paraph)*

*Le present Contrat en r[e]presente d'Trois cent vingt livres de rente viagere, le num[éro] 20749[?] l'un de ceux porté au present Contrat ayant gagné quarante cinq livres de rente viagere par le tirage de la lotterie d'accroissement de lad[ite] Compagnie des Indes du dix neuf juin mil sept cent Cinquante quatre, ainsy qu'il paroit par une liste imprimée des gagnans de lad[ite] lotterie signée des s[ieu]rs Sindic et directeurs de lad[ite] Compagnie, en consequence de laquelle j'ai fait la presente mention et autant sur la minute, et l'un et l'autre porté sur un carnet subrogé à la pratique de m[aîtr]e [illegible]* *(with paraph and flourish)*

*La Rente portée au present Contrat en cette [augmentation] est de trois cent soixante cinq livres, au moyen de ce que le n[umér]o 20748 compris aud[it] Contrat y a gagné quarante cinq livres de rente viagere par le tirage* *[illegible / continues off the torn edge]*

---

## English Translation:

*(Top left, date:)* 13 January 1725.

*(Engraved tax stamp:)* **STAMP OF THE NOTARIES — 13 sols 4 deniers — GENERALITY OF PARIS**

*(Above centre:)* born 27 October 1717

*(Second stamp:)* **First [sheet] of Expedition, 4. — Additional sheets, [per] livre.**

*(Upper right file note:)* Monsieur and Madame Poyrieu. No. 336.

---

**T**O all those who shall see these present Letters, Gabriel-Jérôme de Bullion, Knight, Count Desclimont, Field Colonel (Mestre de Camp) of the Regiment of Provence Infantry, Councillor of the King in all his Councils, Provost of the City, Provostry and Viscounty of Paris, GREETING. We make known that before the Masters *[Et Jean Gaschieu]*, Councillors of the King, Notaries, Keepers of the Records and Keepers of the Seal of His Majesty at the Châtelet of Paris, the undersigned, were present *[Étienne Demeunier the younger]*, Esquire, ...... Syndic of the Company of the Indies, and *[Sieur François Mouchard and Vincent Pierre Fromaget, Esquire]*,

Directors of the said Company; who, by virtue of the Decree of the King's Council of State of 15 February 1724, by which His Majesty granted to the said Company the exclusive privilege of conducting, throughout the extent of the Kingdom, various Lotteries; and in execution of the Resolution of 13 December of the said year, taken in the general Assembly of Administration of the said Company, concerning the Lottery composed of Prizes payable, some in Money and the others in Life Annuities (Rentes Viagères), which are to form a body of Tontine of Twenty-eight thousand seven hundred and fifty Numbers, each of them being subject to accretion (accroissement) in proportion to the deaths of the Annuitants (Rentiers) interested in the said Lottery; that is to say, for one half to the profit of the said Company, and for the other half to those of the surviving Annuitants whom the drawing shall favour in the Lotteries which are to be drawn annually and successively. The which Life Annuities, which shall be charged upon the funds of the said Company, the said Sieurs Syndic and Directors have power to constitute both to the benefit of the Subjects of His Majesty and of Foreigners, to whom the payment of the Arrears and of the Accretions must be made even should they reside outside the Kingdom, and even in time of war, without the said Annuities being liable to be cut down or seized for any cause whatsoever, not even for the King's own moneys and affairs, in conformity with the Decree of 20 June 1724, as is set forth more fully in the said Resolution, by which it is permitted to the purchasers of the said Annuities to have the Contracts made out under the name of such persons as they shall choose, so as to enjoy them, both by themselves and by those whom they shall name upon their Receipts (Quittances), during the life of the persons upon whose head (teste) the Constitutions shall have been placed. The said Sieurs Syndic and Directors, appearing, by virtue of the said Resolutions and Decrees of the Council of 15 February and 20 June 1724, have sold, created and constituted, settled and assigned by these presents; and have promised, for and in the name of the said Company of the Indies, to warrant against all troubles and other hindrances whatsoever, to

*[Master Charles Claude Poyrieu, Esquire, Councillor-Secretary of the King, Receiver-General of the finances of Orléans, and Dame Elizabeth Louise Mollée his wife, to be enjoyed by them and by the survivor of them, upon the head and during the life of Louis François Poyrieu their son, who shall enjoy it after the death of his said father and mother — the said sieur and dame Poyrieu, she being authorized by the said sieur her husband for the purpose of these presents, dwelling at Paris, Rue Thévenot, who this day accept as Purchasers — Two hundred and seventy-five [livres]]*

of Life Annuity; which Annuity the said Sieurs Syndic and Directors, appearing for and in the name of the said Company of the Indies, promise to cause to be delivered and paid in ringing coin (espèces sonnantes) each year, at the open Office (Bureau), out of the moneys of the said Company, to *[the said sieur and dame Poyrieu, and after them to the said sieur their son]* .......... under Receipts, which may only be given *[printed text cut off at the foot of the leaf]*

*(Notary's signature at foot right:)* M. Bouttra[ys?]

*(Left-margin approbation note, largely illegible)*

---

*(Right-margin accretion notes, added later; the tontine numbers cited are uncertain readings:)*

Inventory seventy-four.

The present Contract now represents Three hundred and twenty livres of life annuity, number 20749[?] being one of those carried on the present Contract, having won forty-five livres of life annuity by the drawing of the accretion lottery of the said Company of the Indies of the nineteenth of June one thousand seven hundred and fifty-four, as appears by a printed list of the winners of the said lottery signed by the Syndic and directors of the said Company, in consequence of which I have made the present notation, and the like upon the minute, both being entered in a register, [I being] subrogated to the practice of Master [illegible].

The Annuity carried on the present Contract, in this [increase], is Three hundred and sixty-five livres, by reason that number 20748, comprised in the said Contract, has therein won forty-five livres of life annuity by the drawing *[illegible / continues off the torn edge]*

title: Liverpool Corn Trade Association Perpetual Mortgage Debenture, Liverpool
description: This is a perpetual mortgage debenture, numbered 291, of the Liverpool Corn Trade Association, Limited, issued to George Grossman for £100 sterling and signed by Edward Briggs, Secretary. The printed conditions establish it as one of a series of "C." perpetual debentures securing principal not exceeding £100,000 in all, charged on the Association's property and bearing interest not above £5 per centum per annum. Issued at Liverpool, the great corn-importing port of late-Victorian Britain, the instrument let a self-governing trade association raise long-dated capital from its own members rather than a bank, the perpetual form fixing a permanent charge in place of a maturing loan. Handwritten endorsements trace its passage: on George Grossman's death his sons take it under letters of administration, Ernest Clifford Grossman of Great Crosby receives the transfer in June 1919, and Hannah Jones, spinster, of West Derby acquires it in 1933. The austere engraved frame and blackletter title eschew allegory, presenting the bond as a sober commercial covenant. (Reverse of the certificate, with conditions printed below.)
creator: Liverpool Corn Trade Association, Limited
notes: Reverse of C. Perpetual Mortgage Debenture No. 291, £100, Liverpool Corn Trade Association, Ltd. Shows printed conditions and transfer record: transferred May 25, 1910 to Ernest Clifford Grasman; registered June 1919; further transfer October 9, 1933.
