title: Middelburg Plantation Bond for Essequibo and Demerara
description: A handwritten plantation mortgage bond (No. 90) drawn on the Register of Middelburg in Zeeland and signed by Kornelis van den Helm Boddaert as Directeur for the geld-Opschieters (money-lenders) of various plantations in the colony of Essequibo and Demerary, issued under the printed "Plan" of hypotheken secured upon the colonial estates and authenticated with the Company seal. Dated at Middelburg the first of January 1760, it acknowledges a capital of six hundred guilders courant (Zes Hondered Guldens Courant) at five per cent yearly interest. The instrument belongs to the Dutch system of plantageleningen, by which West Indian mortgages were bundled and sold to investors at home, an early apparatus for turning distant, slave-worked estates into negotiable paper. The plain engrossed text and the small printed form, completed by hand and sealed, embody bureaucratic routine rather than display; later endorsements record its long secondary life.
creator: Kornelis van den Helm Boddaert; Middelburg Administration
notes: Middelburg plantation bond No. 99. Kornelis van den Helm Boddaert, Director/Secretary. Plantations in Essequibo and Demerara (Guyana). Capital ~500 Livres at 5% p.a. January 1760. Dutch plantageleningen system. Endorsements dated 1772, 1793, 1817.
transcription:
# Middelburg Plantation Mortgage Bond No. 90 for Essequibo and Demerary (1760) — Transcription

## Original:

*(Language: Dutch. A printed loan form on the "Register MIDDELBURG" of a Zeeland plantation-mortgage fund, completed by hand and later covered with manuscript coupon-release and Orphans'-Chamber endorsements in several hands and inks. Printed matter is shown in **bold**; hand-written insertions are shown in [square-bracketed italics or plain]. Layout is described in parentheses.)*

*(Top margin — left block, cursive:)*
Afgegeven 10[e] Coupon[blad], 180[9] à 1818 ingesloten — Middelb: verkogt 18[..] *(line end trimmed)*
H. J. van Galen,

*(Top margin — right block, cursive:)*
Afgegeven [aantal illegible] Coupons
van 1819 tot 1828
Middelburg 10 Novb 1819
*(paraph / signature, illegible)*

*(Centre, immediately below the printed heading — middle-right block:)*
afgegeven 10 [op 8?] Coupons
van 1829 tot 1838
Midd: 4 April 1829
*(paraph / signature, illegible)*

*(Centre header, printed:)*
( **1** )

N°. [90] *(hand-written serial)*

**Register MIDDELBURG.**

*(Directly under the printed heading, cursive:)*
Afgegeven 10 coupons van 1839 à 18[48]
Middelburg 2[3] Aug: [1839]
P. Nachtegall.

*(Main printed body, with the four hand-written blanks filled in:)*

**Ik Ondergeschreeve KORNELIS VAN DEN HELM BODDAERT, als Directeur en gequalificeerde van de geld Opschieters, op diverse Plantagien in de Colonie van Essequebo en Demerary, volgens de Hypotheeken ter Secretarie aldaar, in behoorlyke Ordre alle ten behoeve van dit fonds gepasseerd, waar van de Copyen Authenticq met 'sCompagnie's Zegel bezegeld, en door den Secretaris der Colonie geteekend onder my zyn berustende, Bekenne in die qualiteit agtervolgens het gedrukte Project en Conditien (waar van den inhoud hier agter is geinsereerd) Ontfangen te hebben van** [D'Heer S: Elias]

**Eene Somma van** [Zes Hondered Guldens Courant]

**Welke somma ik Ondergeschreeve, beloove aan dezelve of deszelfs Regt en Actie verkrygende, te zullen doen Restitueeren na Expiratie van den tyd in het Plan hier agter vermeld, en inmiddels daar van te zullen uitkeeren, den Interest tot** *Vyf perCento* **in 't Jaar ingaande primo January 1700** [Agtenzestig] **geduerende alzoo van Jaar tot Jaar, tot de volle en Effectueele betaaling der Hypotheeken, waar van de eerste vervaldag zal zyn, op primo January 1700** [Negenenzestig] **en zoo vervolgens alle jaar, en dat van alles vry-geld zonder eenige kortinge aan Capitaal of Interessen te mogen doen, zullende de Interessen werden betaald ten Comptoire van den Ondergeschreevene, tot nakominge van al het welke de Geld-opneemers boven hunne speciale Hypotheeken hebben verbonden, hunne Perzoonen en goederen, ten bedwang als na Regten.**

**Ter Oirkonde dezes by my onderteekend**

Middelburg den [Eersten Januarij] 17[60]

*(large signature, right:)* [K. v. d. Helm Boddaert]

*(Endorsement block, left of the signature, cursive:)*
Afgegeven 10 N: Coupons
van N°. 1799 à 1808 ingesloten
Middelburg 27 July 1805.
F. B. Haringman.

*(Below, running across, cursive:)*
Conform Art: 5 betaald voor 3 Jaar verschenen 1 January 17[71] : 9 — .. — *(final figures set as money columns, ≈ ƒ 9 : — : —)*

*(Foot of sheet — two paired Orphans'-Chamber endorsements:)*

*(left:)*
de Weeskamer Subject
Middelburg 19 Maart 1799.
Als Wees[meester] [gesteld]
[Johan Philipse]

*(right:)*
de Weeskamer Ontslagen.
Middelburg 10 Augustus 1804.
Als Weesmeester.
[P.?] Schorer

---

## English Translation:

*(Top margin, left:)* Delivered [the] 10th coupon[-sheet], 180[9] to 1818 enclosed — sold at Middelburg 18[..]. H. J. van Galen.

*(Top margin, right:)* Delivered [number illegible] coupons, from 1819 to 1828. Middelburg 10 November 1819. *(signature illegible.)*

*(Centre right:)* Delivered 10 [of 8?] coupons, from 1829 to 1838. Middelburg 4 April 1829. *(signature illegible.)*

*(Header:)* ( 1 ) — No. [90] — **Register MIDDELBURG.**

*(Under the heading:)* Delivered 10 coupons from 1839 to 18[48]. Middelburg 2[3] August [1839]. P. Nachtegall.

**Main body:**

I, the undersigned, KORNELIS VAN DEN HELM BODDAERT, as Director and authorized [agent] of the money-advancers, upon divers Plantations in the Colony of Essequibo and Demerary, according to the Mortgages [held] at the Secretariat there, all duly executed in proper order for the benefit of this fund, of which the authentic Copies, sealed with the Company's Seal and signed by the Secretary of the Colony, are in my keeping, acknowledge in that capacity, pursuant to the printed Plan and Conditions (the contents whereof are inserted hereafter), to have received from [Mr. S. Elias]

A Sum of [Six Hundred Guilders Courant]

Which sum I, the undersigned, promise to repay to the same [person], or to whoever acquires his right and title, after expiry of the term stated in the Plan hereafter, and meanwhile to pay out thereon the Interest at Five per cent per year, commencing the first of January [17]68, continuing thus from year to year until the full and effectual payment of the Mortgages, of which the first due date shall be the first of January [17]69, and so on every year thereafter, and all of this as free money without any deduction from Capital or Interest whatsoever, the Interest to be paid at the Office of the undersigned; for the fulfilment of all of which the money-borrowers have bound, over and above their special Mortgages, their persons and goods, to distraint as by law.

In witness whereof, signed by me.

Middelburg, the [first of January] 17[60].

*(signature:)* [K. van den Helm Boddaert]

*(Endorsement, left:)* Delivered 10 N[ew] coupons, from No. 1799 to 1808 enclosed. Middelburg 27 July 1805. F. B. Haringman.

*(Across:)* Pursuant to Art. 5, paid for 3 Years fallen due 1 January 17[71]: 9 — .. — *(money columns.)*

*(Foot, left:)* Subject to the Orphans' Chamber. Middelburg 19 March 1799. As Orphan-master [appointed]. [Johan Philipse]

*(Foot, right:)* Discharged by the Orphans' Chamber. Middelburg 10 August 1804. As Orphan-master. [P.?] Schorer

title: French Life Annuity on Four Million Livres
description: This is a French royal life annuity (rente viagère au denier vingt-cinq), a partly printed and partly manuscript quittance issued by Antoine Pâris, Conseiller du Roy and Garde du Trésor Royal, acknowledging receipt at Paris of a principal of 1,175 livres in exchange for a yearly rente of forty-seven livres. The annuity belongs to the four millions of livres raised under the édit of July 1723, charged on the tailles and other impositions, and is constituted for Simonne Pinault, widow of Claude Rabaroux, upon the life of Simonne Agathe Rabaroux. Dated 1723, in the Regency-to-Louis-XV transition, the instrument shows how the crown, still recovering from the collapse of Law's System, leaned on life rents to draw in private capital. A marginal note records the rente's later reduction to 23 livres 10 sols under the édit of November 1726, evidence of the monarchy's serial repudiations.
creator: Jean Antoine Paris, Conseiller du Roy
notes: Rente Viagère au Denier Vingt-cinq, No. 21839. Quatre Millions de Livres, Édit de Juillet 1723, Tailles & autres Impositions. Nominee: Simonne Jenaille. Jean Antoine Paris, Conseiller du Roy. Paris, 1723. Annual payment ~23 livres 74 sous. French royal life annuity.
transcription:
# Receipt (Quittance) for a Royal Life Annuity on the Four Millions of Livres, Edict of July 1723 — Transcription

## Original:

**Rentes Viageres au Denier Vingt-cinq, Sur les Quatre Millions de livres de l'Edit du mois de Juillet 1723. sur les Tailles & autres Impositions.**

**N.º1** [handwritten:] 5579. (printed "N.º1" followed by manuscript "5579", i.e. No. 15579)

(left margin, manuscript:) La rente cy endroit est reduitte a 23# 10[s] / suivant l'édit de novembre 1726. (below, a paraphe/flourish signature)

**JE ANTOINE PARIS, Conseiller du Roy en ses Conseils, Garde de son Trésor Royal, Confesse avoir reçû en cette Ville de Paris, de** [handwritten:] Simonne Pinault Vᵉ de Claude Rabaroux pour jouir par elle de la rente cy apres sur la teste de Simonne agathe Rabaroux femme de Pierre Charles Quilles [or Quillet] avocat en Parlement demeurant a Dreux

**la Somme de** [handwritten:] Vnze cent soixante quinze livres

**pour le Principal de** [handwritten:] Quarante sept livres **de Rentes Viageres au Denier Vingt-cinq, faisant partie des Quatre Millions de livres de Rentes Viageres créées par Edit du mois de Juillet 1723. Registré où besoin a esté, à prendre sur les Tailles & autres Impositions, tant des Pays d'Election que des Pays d'Estats, pour jouir par** [handwritten:] Lad. Pinault

**pour chacun an, de ladite Rente de** [handwritten:] xLVII ᵗᵗ **sur le pied du Denier Vingt-cinq, ainsi qu'il sera plus au long expliqué par le Contract de Constitution qui sera expedié de ladite Rente par Messieurs les Commissaires du Conseil nommez à cet effet par Lettres Patentes du Roy du 9. Aoust de la présente année ; de laquelle somme de** [handwritten:] xi C Lxxv ᵗᵗ **à moy ordonnée pour employer au fait de ma Charge, je quitte le** [handwritten:] led. Pinault **& tous autres. Fait à Paris le** [handwritten:] trentième **jour de** [handwritten:] septembre **mil sept cens vingt-trois.**

**Quittance du Garde du Trésor Royal, Année mil sept cens vingt-trois.**

[handwritten paraphe/signature, centre-bottom]

**M.** [handwritten:] Larsonnier **N.ᵣₑ** (Notaire)

---

## English Translation:

**Life Annuities at the Denier Twenty-five, On the Four Millions of livres of the Edict of the month of July 1723, on the Tailles and other Impositions.**

**No. 1** [handwritten:] 5579. (i.e. No. 15579)

(left margin, manuscript:) The annuity herein is reduced to 23 livres 10 [sols] / pursuant to the edict of November 1726.

**I, ANTOINE PARIS, Counsellor of the King in his Councils, Keeper of his Royal Treasury, confess to have received in this City of Paris, from** [handwritten:] Simonne Pinault, widow of Claude Rabaroux, to be enjoyed by her of the annuity hereafter, upon the life of Simonne Agathe Rabaroux, wife of Pierre Charles Quilles [or Quillet], advocate in Parlement, residing at Dreux,

**the Sum of** [handwritten:] Eleven hundred seventy-five livres,

**for the Principal of** [handwritten:] Forty-seven livres **of Life Annuities at the Denier Twenty-five, forming part of the Four Millions of livres of Life Annuities created by the Edict of the month of July 1723. Registered where needed, to be drawn upon the Tailles and other Impositions, both of the Pays d'Election and the Pays d'Etats, to be enjoyed by** [handwritten:] the said Pinault

**for each year, of the said Annuity of** [handwritten:] 47 livres, **at the rate of the Denier Twenty-five, as will be more fully explained by the Constitution Contract which will be issued for the said Annuity by the Gentlemen Commissioners of the Council appointed for this purpose by Letters Patent of the King of the 9th of August of the present year; of which sum of** [handwritten:] 1,175 livres **ordered to me to be employed in the execution of my Office, I release the** [handwritten:] said Pinault **and all others. Done at Paris the** [handwritten:] thirtieth **day of** [handwritten:] September **one thousand seven hundred twenty-three.**

**Receipt of the Keeper of the Royal Treasury, Year one thousand seven hundred twenty-three.**

[handwritten signature/paraphe]

**M.** [handwritten:] Larsonnier **Notary.**

title: Royal Life Annuity Contract, Hôtel de Ville of Paris, 1759
description: A partly printed, partly manuscript contract for a French royal life annuity on one head (rente viagère sur une tête), passed before the notaries and gardes-scel of Sa Majesté at the Châtelet de Paris and the Prévôt des marchands et échevins of the city, Jean-Baptiste-Élie Camus de Pontcarré. It executes the edict of November 1758, registered by the Parlement on 12 December following, which authorised the sale of three million six hundred thousand livres of life annuities, charged by preference upon the royal treasury and payable through the garde du trésor royal. Annuities could be constituted on one or two heads, with returns graduated by the holder's age into six classes — from one hundred livres per thousand of capital in the first class to one hundred forty in the sixth (eighty per thousand on two heads). The terms reveal a crown borrowing during the fiscal strain of the Seven Years' War, recruiting even foreigners and members of religious orders with the inducement that the rents be exempt from the two vingtièmes, the deux sols pour livre, and the droits d'aubaine, bâtardise et confiscation. The manuscript docket names the holder as the Comtesse de Pons-Saint-Maurice and dates the act 26 July 1759.
creator: Notaires du Châtelet de Paris; Jean-Baptiste-Élie Canus de Pontcaillé
notes: Rente Viagère Sur une Tête, Édit de Novembre 1738, Première Classe. Nominee: Comtesse de Saint-Hermaurice. Issued July 26, 1739, Paris. Notaires du Châtelet. Marchands et Echevins financing (4,000 livres capital each). French royal life annuity under Louis XV.
transcription:
# Royal Life Annuity Contract on One Head, Hôtel de Ville of Paris (Edict of November 1758) — Transcription

## Original:

(Handwritten docket, top left):
Mᵈᵉ La Comtesse de Pons St Maurice
26. Juillet 1759

(Engraved oval seal, center): **GEN. DE PARIS** / [ornamental arms, part illegible] / [...] 3 SOLS

(Right margin, large handwritten flourish + printed word): 1.ᵉ **Classe.**

**RENTES VIAGÈRES SUR UNE TÊTE.**

**Édit de Novembre 1758.**

(Left margin, handwritten): S.ʳ Grosset / ad plias [illegible marginal note]

**PAR-DEVANT** les Conseillers du Roi, Notaires, Gardes-notes & Gardes-scel de Sa Majesté au Châtelet de Paris, soussignés, furent présens, M.ʳᵉ **JEAN-BAPTISTE-ÉLIE CAMUS DE PONTCARRÉ**, seigneur de Viarme, Seugy, Belloy & autres lieux, Conseiller d'État, Prevôt des Marchands;

(Handwritten insertions naming the échevins):
Jean François Bralles Ecuyer Con.ᵉʳ du Roy au l'Hôtel de Ville
Jean Baptiste Vernay Ecuyer.
Jean Olivier Boutray Ecuyer Con.ᵉʳ du Roy quartenier
Et Jean André [surname illegible] Ecuyer avocat au Parlement et ès Conseils du Roy

**Tous Échevins de cette ville de Paris.**

**LESQUELS** ont dit que Sa Majesté, par son édit du mois de novembre 1758, enregistré au Parlement le 12 décembre suivant, auroit ordonné que par les Commissaires que Sa Majesté nommeroit à cet effet, il seroit vendu & aliéné auxdits sieurs Prevôt des Marchands & Échevins, trois millions six cens mille livres actuelles & effectives de rentes viagères, à prendre sur tous les deniers provenans des droits d'aides, gabelles & cinq grosses fermes, que Sa Majesté a affectés, obligés & hypothéqués par préférence à la partie du trésor royal, au payement des arrérages desdites rentes, qui pourroient être acquises, soit sur une seule tête, à divers taux, suivant l'âge des acquéreurs, ou sur deux têtes indistinctement, à raison de huit pour cent par an.

Que les constitutions particulières desdites rentes, qui ne pourroient être moindres sur une seule tête de cinquante livres, & sur deux têtes de quarante livres de jouissance annuelle, seroient faites par lesdits sieurs Prevôt des Marchands & Échevins, à ceux qui en auroient fourni les capitaux en deniers comptans entre les mains du sieur Savalete de Magnanville, Garde du Trésor royal, pour en jouir par les acquéreurs leur vie durant, soit sur leur tête, soit sur celle ou celles de toutes autres personnes que bon leur sembleroit, & que les contrats seroient passés par-devant tels Notaires du Châtelet de Paris que lesdits acquéreurs voudroient choisir, qui seroient tenus de leur délivrer lesdits contrats sans frais.

Que les rentes qui seroient acquises sur une tête, seroient réparties en six classes, suivant l'âge des acquéreurs; la première, depuis la naissance jusqu'à cinquante ans accomplis; la deuxième, depuis cinquante ans jusqu'à cinquante-cinq; la troisième, depuis cinquante-cinq ans jusqu'à soixante; la quatrième, depuis soixante ans jusqu'à soixante-cinq; la cinquième, depuis soixante-cinq ans jusqu'à soixante-dix; & la sixième, depuis soixante-dix ans jusqu'à soixante-quinze ans & au dessus.

Que les rentes sur deux têtes seroient acquises sans distinction d'âge ni de classe; & dans l'une comme dans l'autre espèce desdites rentes viagères, il seroit justifié de l'âge de ceux sur la tête desquels elles seroient constituées, par des extraits baptistaires ou actes équipolens, suivant & conformément à ce qui a été ci-devant prescrit en pareil cas; & qu'annuellement il seroit payé aux acquéreurs desdites rentes, pendant leur vie ou de ceux sur la tête desquels elles seroient constituées, savoir, pour les rentes sur une seule tête & pour chaque constitution de mille livres en capital dans la première classe, cent livres; dans la deuxième, cent cinq livres; dans la troisième, cent dix livres; dans la quatrième, cent vingt livres; dans la cinquième, cent trente livres; & dans la sixième, cent quarante livres: Et pour les rentes sur deux têtes quatre-vingt livres, aussi pour chaque constitution de mille livres en capital.

Que toutes sortes de personnes indistinctement, de quelque âge, sexe, qualité & condition qu'elles puissent être, même les religieux & religieuses qui, suivant leur Ordre, peuvent avoir quelque pécule, pourroient acquérir lesdites rentes; le survivant des pères & mères qui les auroient acquises sous le nom de leurs enfans, en jouiroit, sans être tenu dans aucun cas de leur en rendre compte; les enfans & autres qui entreroient en religion & feroient profession dans quelque Ordre que ce puisse être, conserveroient par forme de pension alimentaire, celles qui auroient été par eux acquises, ou qui ayant été constituées sur leur tête leur auroient été abandonnées avant leur profession: Comme aussi, que même ceux qui sont demeurans hors du royaume, pays, terres & seigneuries de l'obéissance de Sa Majesté, pourroient, ainsi que ses propres sujets, acquérir lesdites rentes, encore bien qu'ils fussent sujets de Princes & États avec lesquels Elle est ou pourroit être en guerre; en conséquence, Sa Majesté a voulu que lesdites rentes & les arrérages qui en seroient dûs au jour du décès de ces rentiers, soient exemptes de toutes lettres de marque & de représailles, droits d'aubaine, bâtardise, confiscation, ou autres qui pourroient lui appartenir, auxquels Sa Majesté a renoncé, conformément à l'édit du mois de décembre 1674 & autres subséquens.

Que lesdites rentes seroient exemptes de la retenue des deux Vingtièmes, des Deux sols pour livre

(Signature, bottom): M.ᵉ Gervais no.ᵉ [notaire; surname reads "Gervai(s)"]

---

## English Translation:

(Handwritten docket, top left):
Madame the Countess of Pons Saint-Maurice
26 July 1759

(Engraved oval seal, center): GENERALITY OF PARIS / [...] 3 SOLS

(Right margin): 1st Class.

**LIFE ANNUITIES ON ONE HEAD.**

**Edict of November 1758.**

(Left margin, handwritten): Sieur Grosset / [illegible marginal note]

**BEFORE** the Councillors of the King, Notaries, Keepers of the Minutes & Keepers of the Seal of His Majesty at the Châtelet of Paris, the undersigned, were present Maître **JEAN-BAPTISTE-ÉLIE CAMUS DE PONTCARRÉ**, lord of Viarme, Seugy, Belloy & other places, Councillor of State, Provost of the Merchants;

(Handwritten insertions naming the aldermen):
Jean François Bralles, Esquire, Councillor of the King at the Hôtel de Ville
Jean Baptiste Vernay, Esquire.
Jean Olivier Boutray, Esquire, Councillor of the King, quartenier [ward officer]
And Jean André [surname illegible], Esquire, advocate at the Parlement and in the Councils of the King

**All Aldermen of this city of Paris.**

**WHO** stated that His Majesty, by his edict of the month of November 1758, registered by the Parlement on the 12th of December following, had ordered that, through the Commissioners His Majesty should appoint to that effect, there should be sold & alienated to the said Provost of the Merchants & Aldermen three million six hundred thousand livres, actual & effective, of life annuities, to be taken upon all the revenues arising from the duties of the aides, the gabelles & the five great farms, which His Majesty has assigned, bound & mortgaged by preference to the account of the royal treasury, for the payment of the arrears of the said annuities, which might be acquired either upon a single head, at various rates according to the age of the purchasers, or upon two heads without distinction, at the rate of eight per cent per year.

That the individual constitutions of the said annuities, which could not be less than fifty livres upon a single head, & forty livres of annual enjoyment upon two heads, should be made by the said Provost of the Merchants & Aldermen to those who should have furnished the capital in ready money into the hands of the Sieur Savalete de Magnanville, Keeper of the Royal Treasury, to be enjoyed by the purchasers during their lifetime, whether upon their own head, or upon that or those of any other persons as should seem good to them, & that the contracts should be passed before such Notaries of the Châtelet of Paris as the said purchasers might choose, who should be bound to deliver the said contracts to them without charge.

That the annuities acquired upon one head should be divided into six classes according to the age of the purchasers: the first, from birth to fifty years completed; the second, from fifty to fifty-five; the third, from fifty-five to sixty; the fourth, from sixty to sixty-five; the fifth, from sixty-five to seventy; & the sixth, from seventy to seventy-five years & above.

That the annuities upon two heads should be acquired without distinction of age or class; & in the one as in the other kind of the said life annuities, the age of those upon whose head they should be constituted must be proved by baptismal extracts or equivalent acts, according & conformably to what has heretofore been prescribed in like cases; & that annually there should be paid to the purchasers of the said annuities, during their life or that of those upon whose head they should be constituted, namely, for annuities upon a single head & for each constitution of one thousand livres of capital: in the first class, one hundred livres; in the second, one hundred and five livres; in the third, one hundred and ten livres; in the fourth, one hundred and twenty livres; in the fifth, one hundred and thirty livres; & in the sixth, one hundred and forty livres: And for annuities upon two heads, eighty livres, also for each constitution of one thousand livres of capital.

That all sorts of persons without distinction, of whatever age, sex, quality & condition they may be, even religious men & women who, according to their Order, may hold some private means, might acquire the said annuities; the survivor of fathers & mothers who had acquired them in the name of their children should enjoy them, without being bound in any case to render account thereof to them; children & others who should enter religion & make profession in whatever Order it might be, should retain by way of alimentary pension those which had been acquired by them, or which, having been constituted upon their head, had been abandoned to them before their profession: Likewise, that even those who dwell outside the kingdom, lands, territories & lordships under the obedience of His Majesty might, like his own subjects, acquire the said annuities, even though they were subjects of Princes & States with which He is or might be at war; in consequence, His Majesty has willed that the said annuities & the arrears due thereon at the day of the death of these annuitants be exempt from all letters of marque & reprisal, rights of aubaine, bastardy, confiscation, or others that might belong to Him, to which His Majesty has renounced, conformably to the edict of the month of December 1674 & others subsequent.

That the said annuities should be exempt from the withholding of the two Twentieths, of the Two sols per livre

(Signature, bottom): Maître Gervais, notary [surname reads "Gervai(s)"]

title: Republic of New Granada Deferred Bond
description: A £100 sterling bearer bond styled "Deferred," issued in London on 1 June 1845 by the Chargé d'Affaires of the Republic of New Granada (forerunner of Colombia) under an Agreement signed at Bogotá on 15 January 1845 between the Republic's Secretary of Finance, Juan Climaco Ordóñez, and Powles, Illingworth, Willson & Co., agents of the bondholders of the foreign debt of Colombia. By its terms New Granada acknowledges half of the loans contracted by the old Republic of Colombia in 1822 and 1824, exchanging new bonds for one-half of each Colombian bond surrendered and so dissolving the joint liability inherited from Gran Colombia's dissolution. Interest is genuinely deferred: nothing falls due until 1 December 1861, and the rate climbs from one per cent, rising a quarter-point yearly toward six, payable half-yearly at Baring, Brothers & Co. The engraved condor and arms of New Granada crown a document that is less an investment than an admission of insolvency dressed as settlement, the word "Deferred" naming a young republic's chosen instrument for postponing the debts of its revolutionary birth.
creator: Republic of New Granada; Powles, Illingworth, Willson & Co.
notes: Republic of New Granada Deferred Bond, Letter A, No. 7376, £100 Sterling. London, January 1, 1845. Chargé d'Affaires: Juan Climaco Ordóñez. Agents: Powles, Illingworth, Willson & Co. 4% interest deferred; dividends at Runneys, Barings & Co. Signed Bogotá August 15, 1843. Bilingual English/Spanish.
transcription:
# Republic of New Granada Deferred Bond (£100 Sterling) — Transcription

## Original:

(Ornamental arched heading over the national coat of arms; engraver's imprint beneath the arms reads: *Perkins, Bacon & Petch London*)

**REPUBLIC OF NEW GRANADA.**

**Letter A. N.o 7376**                    **£100 Sterling.**

**DEFERRED.**

I, the undersigned Chargé d'Affaires of the Republic of New Granada at the Court of London, in pursuance of the Agreement between the Secretary of Finance of the said Republic and the Agents of the Bondholders of the Foreign Debt of Colombia, signed at Bogota on the 15th January, 1845, of which a copy is subjoined, and in conformity with the instructions received from my Government, as its fiscal Commissioner in London, do hereby declare that the said Republic is indebted to the Bearer hereof in the Sum of One Hundred Pounds Sterling, and do hereby, in the most solemn manner, bind the said Republic to pay the same, and the interest thereof, according to the terms of the said Agreement. The interest of the said Sum of One Hundred Pounds is to be paid Half-yearly, on the first day of June and the first day of December in each year, the first Half-yearly Dividend falling due on 1st December, 1861, according to the Fourth Article of the said Agreement, at the Counting-house of Messrs. Baring, Brothers, and Co., in London, on delivery of the Warrants or Coupons attached hereto. The Government of New Granada engages to deliver additional Dividend Warrants to the Holder hereof, when those attached shall have been paid; and so from time to time until the Principal of this Bond shall have been redeemed, or payment thereof tendered by the said Government.

(Below, the Agreement is printed in two parallel columns — English "TRANSLATION" on the left, Spanish "ORIGINAL" on the right)

**TRANSLATION.**

WE, the undersigned, namely JUAN CLIMACO ORDOÑEZ, Secretary of the Finance Department of the Government of NEW GRANADA, in the name of the said Government, and Powles, Illingworth, Willson, & Co., as Agents of the Bondholders of the Debt of Colombia, have agreed to the following proposals:—

1. New Granada acknowledges as her Debt Fifty per Cent. of the Loans contracted by the Republic of Colombia in 1822 and 1824, and will issue, in London, Bonds for the half of each Colombian Bond presented; the latter being consequently cancelled as respects one half of its value, and New Granada being freed from the joint responsibility which as part of Colombia it was liable to in respect of the said Bonds.

2. The Bonds which New Granada may issue, in conformity with the preceding article, will bear Interest at One per Cent per annum during the first four years, which Interest shall be increased one fourth per Cent. in each of the following years, until it reaches the Six per Cent. assigned for the Original Bonds.

3. In payment of the Interest due and not paid, and of that which shall become payable until the said Bonds bear Six per Cent. Interest, Bonds shall be issued at the rate of Cent. per Cent. of the Principal, so that the said Bonds shall form a new Debt, equal to the original Debt which is acknowledged by New Granada.

4. These last-mentioned Bonds shall not bear Interest during the first sixteen years after their issue. In the seventeenth year they shall bear One per Cent. Interest, which shall increase by one eighth part in each of the following years, until it reaches Three per Cent. which is the maximum of Interest that is to be assigned thereto.

5. The Interest on the New Granada Bonds that may be issued in accordance with the stipulation of Article 1, shall be paid in London every half year as it falls due: the payment of the first half year to be made from the first of June, one-thousand eight-hundred and forty-five, and to this account are to be paid the forty-seven thousand eight-hundred and sixty-five Dollars, five and three quarters Reals, received by the house of Powles, Brothers, of London.

6. The Bonds of both Debts shall be received at par in payment of the purchase of the national properties appropriated by Law to the extinction of the foreign debt.

7. The nett produce of the Tobacco Revenue and the half of the produce of the Customs' Revenue, are chargeable for the execution of this contract.

8. These Proposals shall bind the Government of New Granada, after the assent of the Creditors, and for this purpose the Government of New Granada will immediately give orders that Bonds be issued to the Creditors who accept thereof, and that in due time the payments be made to them as above agreed upon.—*If the Creditors should not accept thereof, this Agreement shall be null and void as regards both parties.*

In faith whereof we sign these presents at Bogota, on the fifteenth of January, one-thousand eight-hundred and forty-five,

JUAN CLIMACO ORDOÑEZ,
POWLES, ILLINGWORTH, WILLSON, and CO.

The above is a Copy
The Secretary of Finance, J. C. ORDOÑEZ.

**ORIGINAL.**

Los infrascritos, á saber, JUAN CLÍMACO ORDOÑEZ, Secretario de Hacienda del Gobierno de NUEVA GRANADA, á nombre de dicho Gobierno, i Powles, Illingworth, Willson, i Cía, como Agentes de los Tenedores de vales de la Deuda de Colombia, hemos convenido en las siguientes proposiciones:—

1.ª La Nueva Granada reconoce como deuda suya cincuenta por ciento de los empréstitos que contrató Colombia en 1822 i 1824, i emitirá en Londres obligaciones por la mitad de cada vale Colombiano que se le presente, cancelándose en consecuencia en la mitad de su valor, i quedando libre la Nueva Granada de la responsibilidad mancomunada que como parte de Colombia tenia respecto de aquellos vales.

2.ª Las obligaciones que emita la Nueva Granada de conformidad con el artículo anterior, ganarán el uno por ciento de interes anual durante los cuatro primeros años, el cual interes se aumentará en un cuarto por ciento en cada uno de los años siguientes, hasta completar el seis por ciento asignado á las obligaciones primitivas.

3.ª En pago de los intereses vencidos i no pagados, i de los que dejarán de pagarse mientras las espresadas obligaciones ganan el seis por ciento, se emitirán billetes á razon de ciento por ciento de capital, de manera que con dichos billetes se formará un nuevo capital igual al primitivo que toca reconocer á la Nueva Granada.

4.ª Estos billetes no ganarán interes alguno en los diez i seis primeros años despues de su emision. En el décimo séptimo ganarán el uno por ciento que se aumentará en una octava parte en cada uno de los años siguientes, hasta completar el tres por ciento, que es el maximum de interes que se les señala.

5.ª Los intereses de las obligaciones Granadinas que se emitan segun lo estipulado en el artículo 1.ro, se pagarán en Londres por semestres vencidos, verificándose el pago del primer semestre desde primero de Junio de mil ochocientos cuarenta i cinco, i abonándose á esta cuenta los cuarenta i siete mil ochocientos sesenta i cinco pesos cinco i tres cuartos reales recibidos por la Casa de Powles Hermanos de Londres.

6.ª Los vales de una i otra deuda serán admitidos á la par en pago de las propiedades nacionales que se vendan, destinadas por las leyes á la Amortizacion de la deuda esterior.

7.ª El producto líquido de la renta del Tabaco i la mitad de los productos de la de Aduanas, quedan afectos al cumplimiento de este contrato.

8.ª Estas proposiciones obligarán al gobierno de la Nueva Granada desde que ellas fueren aceptadas por los acreedores, i al afecto el Gobierno Granadino dará immediatamente sus órdenes para que á los acreedores que las acepten se les espidan los vales i en su oportunidad se les hagan los pagos como queda convenido. *Si los acreedores no las aceptasen, todo este convenio será nulo i de ningun valor respecto de ambas partes.*

En fé de lo cual firmamos el presente en Bogota á quince de Enero de mil ochocientos cuarenta i cinco.

JUAN CLÍMACO ORDOÑEZ.
POWLES, ILLINGWORTH, WILLSON i Cía

Es Copia,
El Secretario de Hacienda, J. C. ORDOÑEZ.

(Below the columns:)

Dated in London, this First day of June, 1845

*(handwritten signature)* M. M. Mosquera

We hereby Certify that the above is the Signature of His Excellency M. M. MOSQUERA.
*(handwritten signature)* Baring Brothers & Co.

Secretario. *(handwritten signature, illegible)*

---

## English Translation:

(The bond and its English "TRANSLATION" column are already in English; see the transcription above. The Spanish "ORIGINAL" column is the same Agreement — its faithful English rendering is the printed "TRANSLATION" column reproduced above. For completeness, a plain English rendering of the Spanish text follows.)

**REPUBLIC OF NEW GRANADA.**

Letter A. No. 7376 — £100 Sterling — DEFERRED.

I, the undersigned Chargé d'Affaires of the Republic of New Granada at the Court of London, pursuant to the Agreement between the Secretary of Finance of that Republic and the Agents of the Bondholders of the Foreign Debt of Colombia, signed at Bogotá on 15 January 1845 (a copy of which is appended), and in conformity with the instructions received from my Government as its fiscal Commissioner in London, hereby declare that the said Republic is indebted to the Bearer of this bond in the sum of One Hundred Pounds Sterling, and hereby, in the most solemn manner, bind the said Republic to pay that sum and its interest according to the terms of the said Agreement. The interest on the said sum of One Hundred Pounds is to be paid half-yearly, on 1 June and 1 December of each year, the first half-yearly dividend falling due on 1 December 1861, in accordance with the Fourth Article of the said Agreement, at the counting-house of Messrs. Baring, Brothers, and Co. in London, upon delivery of the warrants or coupons attached hereto. The Government of New Granada undertakes to deliver additional dividend warrants to the Holder when those attached have been paid, and so from time to time until the principal of this bond has been redeemed, or its payment tendered by the said Government.

**The Agreement (from the Spanish original):**

We, the undersigned — namely Juan Clímaco Ordoñez, Secretary of the Finance Department of the Government of New Granada, on behalf of that Government, and Powles, Illingworth, Willson & Co., as Agents of the Holders of the notes of the Debt of Colombia — have agreed on the following proposals:

1. New Granada acknowledges as her own debt fifty per cent of the loans contracted by Colombia in 1822 and 1824, and will issue in London bonds for half of each Colombian note presented; that note being consequently cancelled as to half its value, and New Granada being freed from the joint liability which, as part of Colombia, she bore in respect of those notes.

2. The bonds that New Granada may issue in conformity with the preceding article shall earn one per cent annual interest during the first four years, which interest shall be increased by one quarter per cent in each of the following years, until it reaches the six per cent assigned to the original obligations.

3. In payment of the interest due and unpaid, and of that which shall go unpaid while the said obligations earn six per cent, notes shall be issued at the rate of one hundred per cent of capital, so that with those notes a new capital shall be formed equal to the original one that New Granada must acknowledge.

4. These notes shall earn no interest during the first sixteen years after their issue. In the seventeenth year they shall earn one per cent, which shall increase by one eighth part in each of the following years, until it reaches three per cent, which is the maximum interest assigned to them.

5. The interest on the Granadan obligations issued as stipulated in Article 1 shall be paid in London in arrears each half-year, the payment of the first half-year being made from 1 June 1845; and to this account shall be credited the forty-seven thousand eight hundred and sixty-five pesos, five and three quarters reales, received by the House of Powles Brothers of London.

6. The notes of both debts shall be admitted at par in payment for national properties sold that are earmarked by the laws for the Amortization (redemption) of the external debt.

7. The net product of the Tobacco revenue and half of the products of the Customs revenue are pledged to the fulfilment of this contract.

8. These proposals shall bind the Government of New Granada from the time they are accepted by the creditors; and to that end the Granadan Government shall immediately give its orders that the notes be issued to the creditors who accept them, and that in due course the payments be made to them as agreed. *If the creditors should not accept them, this whole agreement shall be null and of no value with respect to both parties.*

In witness whereof we sign this document at Bogotá, on the fifteenth of January, one thousand eight hundred and forty-five.

Juan Clímaco Ordoñez.
Powles, Illingworth, Willson & Co.

A true copy. The Secretary of Finance, J. C. Ordoñez.

Dated in London, this first day of June, 1845.
*(signed)* M. M. Mosquera

We hereby certify that the above is the signature of His Excellency M. M. Mosquera.
*(signed)* Baring Brothers & Co.

Secretary. *(signature, illegible)*

title: Rjasan Uralsk Railway Company Certificate
description: This is a 4½% Certificaat A for f. 1,200 (Twaalf Honderd Gulden, twelve hundred Dutch guilders) of the Rjasan Uralsk Spoorweg Maatschappij (Ryazan-Ural Railway Company), the renamed successor of the Rjasan Koslow Spoorweg Maatschappij established at Moscow under the Imperial Ukase of 11 January 1892. The certificate was issued at Amsterdam on 14 July 1903 by the Algemeene Trust Maatschappij against surrender of an original share (Origineel Aandeel) No. 2722 of f. 100, under a deed passed before Notary J. C. G. Pollones on 23 June 1903, and carries twenty coupons and a talon. Such Dutch certificates repackaged Russian railway securities for Amsterdam investors, who financed the lines carrying Volga and Ural grain to export. The restrained engraved border, with its guilloche frame and plain serif lettering, reflects the sober commercial typography of the Dutch trust houses rather than any decorative program.
creator: Rjasan Uralsk Spoorweg Maatschappij; Algemeene Trust Maatschappij
notes: 4½% Certificaat A No. 1977, f. 1,200. Rjasan Uralsk Spoorweg Maatschappij (Ryazan-Ural Railway). Algemeene Trust Maatschappij, Amsterdam, July 14, 1903. Replaces 12× Certificaat A No. 2722 at f. 100 each. Imperial Ukase January 11, 1897.

title: The Rock Island Company Administration Certificate, Amsterdam, 1897
description: This is a Dutch administration certificate (Certificaat) for $1,000 of common stock in The Rock Island Company, issued through the Administratie-Kantoor van Amerikaansche Fondsen at Amsterdam founded by the firms Broes en Gosman, Ten Have en van Essen, and Jarman en Zoonen. The holder is entitled to ten common shares of $100 each (TIEN GEWONE AANDEELEN à $100) deposited with the trust office, whose terms are governed by a notice dated 1 October 1897; dividend coupons run to No. 20 with a renewal talon. The instrument embodies the late-nineteenth-century Dutch appetite for American railway securities: rather than holding the underlying shares abroad, Amsterdam investors received locally administered certificates against deposited stock, the kantoor collecting dividends and managing risk on their behalf. The engraving is sober and typographic, the firm name set in heavy roman capitals over a finely ruled rose ground, conveying probity rather than ornament.
creator: The Rock Island Company; Broes en Goosman; Ten Have en van Essen; Jarman en Zoonen; Plantenga (Amsterdam)
notes: Dutch Certificaat No. 52108, $1,000 Common Stock, The Rock Island Company (Chicago, Rock Island and Pacific Railway). Administered by Broes & Goosman, Ten Have & van Essen, Jarman & Zoonen, Plantenga, Amsterdam. Pink-printed certificate.

at AMSTERDAM

and legalized by the undersigned Notary.

The PAYMENTS on the accumulated shares will be paid for the account and risk of the HOLDER in America, and exclusively only.

The exchange of CERTIFICATES with overvalued Dividend vouchers, against the original Bank note, can be agreed simultaneously effective from 1 October 1897.

Witness by me
Amsterdam,

Company for the management of the Administration Office aforementioned,

Notary,
Hereby [?] send Dividend vouchers of up to 20, with COUPON.

title: Vereeniging tot Bevordering van 's Lands Weerbaarheid Lottery Loan Share, Rotterdam, 1871
description: A lottery-loan share (Loterij-Geldleening) of f 2.50, one of 400,000 making up a total loan of f 1,000,000 raised by the Vereeniging tot Bevordering van 's Lands Weerbaarheid, the Dutch association for the promotion of national defence readiness. Authorised by Ministerial Resolution of 2 July 1870, the loan was secured by a guarantee fund of roughly f 500,000 effectief and bore interest reckoned at 5 percent. Issued at Rotterdam in May 1871, it sets out a drawing plan: twice yearly, on 31 July and 15 January, 12,000 shares are redeemed by lot with premiums headed by f 75,000 and descending through f 30,000, f 25,000, f 20,000 and many smaller prizes down to f 10. The instrument fuses charity, patriotism and speculation, a private patriotic body funding post-1870 military preparedness by selling the public tickets dressed as redeemable bonds. The engraved trophy of arms, flanked by provincial shields and crowned with the royal motto JE MAINTIENDRAI and the banner ALLES VOOR HET VADERLAND, binds the subscriber's hope of a prize to the defence of the fatherland.
creator: Vereeniging tot Bevordering van 's Lands Weerbaarheid
notes: Loterij-Geldleening, f. 1,000,000 / 400,000 shares at f. 2.50. Series 7734, No. 38. Rotterdam, May 1877. Draws: July 31 and January 15 annually; 12,000 shares. Top prize f. 10,000. Vereeniging tot Bevordering van 's Lands Weerbaarheid (national defense association).

## Original:

A ribbon banner beneath the trophy reads **ALLES VOOR HET VADERLAND** (partly obscured by the central shield). The whole certificate is framed by an ornamental border set with provincial coats of arms.)

**Serie 7734** (upper left)      **N.° Acht en Dertig.** (upper right)

**VEREENIGING TOT BEVORDERING VAN 'S LANDS WEERBAARHEID.**

**Loterij=**
**Geldleening groot ƒ 1,000,000,**

Registratie en Domeinen).

handen eener vaste commissie van beheer, gekozen door de Algemeene Vergadering van Aandeelhouders in deze Leening,
gehouden te UTRECHT op 17 April 1871. Dit Kapitaal met de oploopende renten gerekend ad 5 %, verzekert ten
volle de jaarlijksche aflossingen met hooge premiën, overeenkomstig achterstaand plan van trekking.

**Houder dezes is geregtigd tot de som , waarmede dit Aandeel in eene der trekkingen wordt uitgeloot.**

Volgens het Plan van uitloting, aan de keerzijde dezes specifiek vermeld, worden afgelost:

**Reeds op 31 Julij en 15 Januarij aanstaande 12,000 aandeelen,**
waaronder met ZEER HOOGE PREMIËN, als :

In de volgende trekkingen de *resterende* aandeelen, waaronder eveneens met
ZEER HOOGE PREMIËN, als :

(Table 2 — two columns:)
```
Een ............... met ƒ 80,000 ...... ƒ 80,000  | Vijf ................ met ƒ 40,000 ...... ƒ 200,000

Zeven .............   "  –  5,000 ...... – 35,000  | Drie ................   "  –  3,000 ...... –   9,000

Twintig ...........   "  –    500 ...... – 10,000  | Twee honderd twee en twintig " – 200 ...... –  44,400
Honderd vijftig ...   "  –    100 ...... – 15,000  | Honderd zestig ......   "  –     50 ...... –   8,000
Vier honderd ......   "  –     40 ...... – 16,000  | en vele anderen.
```

Tot een gezamentlijk bedrag van ruim Twee Millioen gulden, wordende van deze *resterende* geen enkel met
minder dan ƒ 2.50, de meeste echter met ƒ 2.75, ƒ 3.—, ƒ 3.25, ƒ 3,50 en ƒ 4.— terugbetaald.

De trekkingen hebben plaats ten overstaan van een Notaris. Houders van aandeelen hebben het regt daarbij tegenwoordig te
zijn. De uitslag wordt in de voornaamste Nieuwsbladen bekend gemaakt. De betaling der uitgelootte aandeelen geschiedt

De Raad van Beheer,

*Van deze aandeelen worden alleen voor echt erkend, die, welke voorzien*
*zijn van den Rijks-Zegel-Stempel (regts bovenaan) en van het*

(Printer's imprints along bottom edge:) STEENDR. VAN J. W. v. LEENHOFF & ZOON, ROTT.  (left)   VOGELZANG JR. FECIT  (right)

## English Translation:

(Top engraved trophy of arms with the royal motto **JE MAINTIENDRAI** — "I will maintain"; banner ribbon **ALLES VOOR HET VADERLAND** — "All for the Fatherland".)

**Series 7734**      **No. Thirty-Eight.**

**ASSOCIATION FOR THE PROMOTION OF THE NATION'S DEFENCE READINESS.**

**Lottery**
**Loan totalling ƒ 1,000,000,**
divided into 400,000 shares of ƒ 2.50.

(Authorised pursuant to Ministerial Resolution, dated 2 July 1870, no. 30, Department of
Registration and Domains.)

Guaranteed by a guarantee fund specially pledged for the purpose, amounting to approximately ƒ 500,000 in actual value, deposited in the hands of a permanent management committee chosen by the General Meeting of Shareholders in this Loan, held at UTRECHT on 17 April 1871. This Capital, together with the accruing interest reckoned at 5 %, fully secures the annual redemptions with high premiums, in accordance with the drawing plan set out overleaf.

**The bearer of this is entitled to the sum for which this Share is drawn (redeemed) in one of the drawings.**

According to the Plan of drawing, specified on the reverse hereof, the following are redeemed:

```
and many others of ƒ 200, ƒ 100, ƒ 40, ƒ 20, ƒ 15, ƒ 10, etc.

In the following drawings the *remaining* shares, among which likewise with
VERY HIGH PREMIUMS, namely:

(Table 2 — two columns:)
```

One ............    "  – 30,000 ...... – 30,000  | Seven ...............    "  – 10,000 ...... –  70,000

Four ...........    "  –  2,500 ...... – 10,000  | One hundred twenty ..    "  –  1,000 ...... – 120,000
Twenty .........    "  –    500 ...... – 10,000  | Two hundred twenty-two   "  –    200 ...... –  44,400

The drawings take place before a Notary. Holders of shares have the right to be present at them. The result is made known in the principal Newspapers. Payment of the drawn shares is made immediately after the drawing, without any deduction and at such place as shall be further announced.

title: Imperial Russian 5% Perpetual Income Certificate, St Petersburg, 1822
description: This printed certificate is a registered claim to perpetual income (Свидѣтельство о непрерывномъ доходѣ, "Nepreryvny Dokhod") yielding five per cent, issued through the Imperial Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts (Коммиссія погашенія долговъ) and entered in the state debt book on 1 March 1822 at Pavlovsk. Its capital is stated twice over — 960 silver roubles equal to 148 pounds sterling — the dual face, together with the London paying-agent stamp at upper right, marking it as part of Russia's sterling-denominated foreign loan placed on the Western market. As a non-redeemable instrument paying interest in perpetuity, it functioned as the Russian counterpart of the British consol, converting the empire's borrowing into a permanent charge on treasury revenue. The crowned double-headed eagle clasping orb and sceptre asserts that this perpetual obligation rests on imperial sovereign credit.
creator: Imperial Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts
notes: Russian 5% Perpetual Income Certificate (Непрерывный Доход по 5 на сто), 960 Roubles. Registered State Debt Book, March 1, 1822. Imperial double-headed eagle. Государственная Комиссия погашения долгов. Perpetual 5% income, semi-annual payments. Russian equivalent of consols.
transcription:
# Imperial Russian 5% Perpetual Income Certificate — Transcription

## Original:

(Printed on both left and right vertical side borders, repeated: **Russian 5 PerCent Loan (1822)**)

(Upper left, in a printed box:) **№ 79948**

(Centre top: crowned imperial double-headed eagle with orb and sceptre)

(Upper right, in a printed box:) **№ 15163**   (handwritten to the right: 79)

**ROUBLES 960**   1822 года.   **£148 Sterling**

(handwritten beneath: 528 / 328)

**НЕПРЕРЫВНЫЙ ДОХОДЪ ПО 5 НА СТО.**

Свидѣтельство о непрерывномъ доходѣ на капиталъ, внесенный въ Государственную долговую книгу Коммиссіи погашенія долговъ, состоящій въ девяти стахъ шестидесяти рубляхъ серебромъ, равняющихся 148 фунтамъ стерлинговъ.

Записанный 1го Марта 1822.

(handwritten:) Книга 2. страни. 479. 2 займа Лит. А.

Предъявитель сего Свидѣтельства имѣетъ право на непрерывный доходъ, заключающійся въ сорока [осми] (обозрѣнію мѣшаетъ штемпель) рубляхъ серебромъ, платимый каждые полгода по его произволу въ С. Петербургѣ или Лондонѣ, [то есть] двадцать четыре рубля 1го числа Марта и двадцать четыре рубля серебрянными рублями настоящаго вѣса и пробы, естьли въ С. Петербургѣ; естьли же въ Лондонѣ, по [три шиллинга] одинъ пенсъ стерлинговъ за каждый серебряный рубль: въ томъ и другомъ случаѣ по предъявленіи купона на причитающійся къ платежу непрерывный доходъ.

Предъявитель сего Свидѣтельства можетъ, представивъ оное въ Государственную Коммиссію погашенія долговъ, требовать, чтобы вмѣсто онаго выданъ былъ безсрочный билетъ на туже сумму на его имя, или на имя лица, или лицъ, которыя онъ назначитъ: въ такомъ случаѣ непрерывный доходъ имѣетъ [быть] платимъ токмо въ С. Петербургѣ въ вышепостановленные сроки, и трансфертъ или передача таковыхъ билетовъ должна быть производима по существующимъ правиламъ.

Двадцать четыре купона принадлежатъ къ сему Свидѣтельству. Естьли при наступленіи срока послѣдне[го] изъ нихъ, капиталъ не будетъ выкупленъ, или обращенъ въ безсрочный билетъ Коммиссіи погашенія долговъ; то имѣютъ быть выданы вновь двадцать четыре купона, и такъ далѣе, дабы обезпечить [предъ]явителю полученіе непрерывнаго дохода въ С. Петербургѣ или Лондонѣ.

Прил. Особенный фондъ, или капиталъ, составляющій (ежегодно) одинъ процентъ со всей суммы сего [зай]ма, назначенъ на выкупъ его.

(В правой боковой рамкѣ, въ рамкѣ:) Капиталъ 960 рублей серебромъ равняющійся 148 фун. стерл.

**Выписка изъ Устава Коммиссіи:**

22. Безсрочный непрерывный доходъ, равно какъ и платежъ срочныхъ долговъ [...] въ мирное и военное время безъ всякаго различія, къ дружественной ли или не[пріятельской нации] принадлежитъ вкладчикъ.

23. Въ случаѣ смерти иностраннаго вкладчика безъ завѣщанія, безсрочный долгъ имѣетъ принадлежать его наслѣдникамъ по порядку и правамъ наслѣдія той націи, къ которой онъ принадлежалъ.

24. Капиталы, въ безсрочный долгъ обращенные, составляя ненарушимую собственность вкладчика, не подвергаются секвестру ни по казеннымъ, ни по частнымъ искамъ; изключая, естьли весь или часть капитала представлена въ залогъ по подрядамъ, или другимъ сдѣлкамъ съ казною или частными людьми, или въ обезпеченіе иска; въ такомъ случаѣ оный подлежитъ общимъ правиламъ о залогахъ и взысканіяхъ. Равнымъ образомъ таковые капиталы ни въ какомъ случаѣ не подлежатъ никакимъ налогамъ.

25. Никто не можетъ быть противъ его желанія принужденъ къ обратному полученію всего или части капитала, въ безсрочномъ долгѣ помѣщеннаго. Но дабы съ другой стороны предоставить каждому [возможн]ости обратить оный, когда пожелаетъ, въ наличныя деньги, Коммиссія имѣетъ упо[треблять] на выкупъ оныхъ по курсу особенный капиталъ погашенія, ежегодно сверхъ платежа не[прерывныхъ] доходовъ на предметъ сей назначаемый.

За Управляющаго   (подпись)

Бухгалтеръ   (подпись)

(Наложенные штемпели и печати: два красныхъ штемпеля «TEN SHILLINGS» и «FIVE SHILLINGS»; круглая печать съ цифрами «1900»; французскіе фискальные штемпели «UNIS DES ETATS ETRANGERS — 1% — PLEIN TARIF» и «DROITS DES ETRANGERS EN LONDRES 1834»; круглый штемпель платежнаго агента въ верхнемъ правомъ углу.)

---

## English Translation:

(Printed on both side borders, repeated: **Russian 5 PerCent Loan (1822)**)

(Upper left, boxed:) **No. 79948**

(Centre: crowned imperial double-headed eagle with orb and sceptre)

(Upper right, boxed:) **No. 15163**   (handwritten: 79)

**ROUBLES 960**   in the year 1822.   **£148 Sterling**

(handwritten: 528 / 328)

**PERPETUAL INCOME AT 5 PER HUNDRED.**

Certificate of perpetual income on capital entered in the State Debt Book of the Commission for the Repayment of Debts, amounting to nine hundred and sixty silver roubles, equal to 148 pounds sterling.

Registered 1st March 1822.

(handwritten:) Book 2, page 479, 2nd loan, Letter A.

The bearer of this Certificate has the right to a perpetual income consisting of forty-[eight] (partly covered by a stamp) silver roubles, payable every half-year at his choice in St Petersburg or London, [that is] twenty-four roubles on the 1st of March and twenty-four roubles in silver roubles of proper weight and standard, if in St Petersburg; but if in London, at [three shillings] one penny sterling for each silver rouble: in either case upon presentation of the coupon for the perpetual income falling due for payment.

The bearer of this Certificate may, on presenting it to the State Commission for the Repayment of Debts, demand that in its place a perpetual (registered) bill be issued for the same sum, in his name or in the name of the person or persons he shall designate: in that case the perpetual income shall be payable only in St Petersburg on the aforesaid dates, and the transfer or conveyance of such bills must be carried out according to existing rules.

Twenty-four coupons belong to this Certificate. If, when the last of them falls due, the capital has not been redeemed or converted into a perpetual bill of the Commission for the Repayment of Debts, then twenty-four new coupons shall be issued, and so on, in order to secure to the bearer the receipt of the perpetual income in St Petersburg or London.

Note. A special fund, or capital, amounting (annually) to one per cent of the whole sum of this loan, is designated for its redemption.

(In the right-hand border, boxed:) Capital of 960 silver roubles equal to 148 pounds sterling.

**Extract from the Statute of the Commission:**

22. The perpetual income, as well as the payment of term debts [...] in time of peace and of war without any distinction, whether the depositor belongs to a friendly or a [hostile nation].

23. In the event of the death of a foreign depositor without a will, the perpetual debt shall belong to his heirs according to the order and rights of inheritance of the nation to which he belonged.

24. Capitals converted into perpetual debt, constituting the inviolable property of the depositor, are not subject to sequestration whether for treasury or for private claims; except where the whole or part of the capital has been given as security for contracts, or for other transactions with the treasury or with private persons, or as security for a claim; in that case it is subject to the general rules on pledges and recoveries. Likewise such capitals are in no case liable to any taxes.

25. No one may, against his will, be compelled to take back the whole or part of the capital placed in the perpetual debt. But in order, on the other hand, to allow everyone the possibility of converting it, whenever he wishes, into ready money, the Commission shall employ for their redemption, at the market rate, a special sinking-fund capital, appointed annually for this purpose over and above the payment of the perpetual incomes.

For the Manager   (signature)

Accountant   (signature)

(Overlaid stamps and seals: two red stamps "TEN SHILLINGS" and "FIVE SHILLINGS"; a circular seal bearing the figures "1900"; French fiscal stamps "UNIS DES ETATS ETRANGERS — 1% — PLEIN TARIF" and "DROITS DES ETRANGERS EN LONDRES 1834"; a circular paying-agent stamp in the upper right corner.)

title: Russian Public Debt Inscription, Imperial Sinking-Fund Commission, Saint Petersburg, 1876
description: A French-language inscription in the Grand Livre de la dette publique de Russie, registered through the Commission Impériale d'amortissement (Imperial Sinking-Fund Commission) and dated 1876. It records a capital of 500 roubles in silver, entered in the Grand Livre on 11 October 1876, yielding a perpetual rente of five per cent per year — an annual income of 25 roubles, paid each half-year as twelve roubles fifty copecks between the 1st and 15th of March and of September. The capital stands inscribed to the Amsterdam house under the direction of Hope & Cie, together with Ketwich & Voombergh and the widow W. Borski. Issued from Saint Petersburg for Dutch account, the document belongs to the registered-debt machinery through which the Russian treasury drew foreign capital; the engraved guilloche borders and restrained roundel frame project the fiscal solidity such inscriptions were meant to convey. An Extrait du Règlement, Chap. II, sets out the conditions of inscription and transfer.
creator: Commission Impériale d'amortissement de la dette publique de Russie
notes: Inscription au Grand Livre, dette publique de Russie. Commission Impériale d'amortissement. 1876, Première Série. Capital [?] Roubles silver; annual income 500 Roubles. Amsterdam: Ketwich & Voombergh; Mme. W. Borski. Payable March 1/15 and September 1/15. French-language certificate.
transcription:
# Inscription in the Grand Ledger of the Russian Public Debt, Imperial Sinking-Fund Commission, 1876 — Transcription

## Original:

(Ornamental engraved border framing the whole certificate; the top band carries large decorative letters resembling Cyrillic capitals flanking a central crowned imperial roundel; the outer margins are filled with rows of guilloche roundels.)

(Left roundel, top) **Nº** 717448 *(handwritten)*

(Right roundel, top) **Nº** 58448 *(handwritten)*

(Left panel, engraved box) **Rente**
annuelle
Roubles
25

(Right panel, engraved box) **Capital**
Roubles
500

(Centre heading, in a ruled box) **1876.**

**Inscription au Grand livre de la dette publique de Russie
à la Commission Impériale d'amortissement.**

**PREMIÈRE SÉRIE.**

d'un capital de Roubles en argent,

*cinq cents* *(handwritten)*

porté au Grand livre le *11 Octobre* 1876. Livre *12* fol. *1142* *(handwritten insertions)*

au nom de *l'Administration sous la direction de Hope & Cⁱᵉ,
Ketwich & Voombergh & Vᵛᵉ W. Borski à Amsterdam* *(handwritten)*

rapportant une rente perpétuelle de **CINQ** pour cent par année et pour
chaque semestre de *douze roubles cinquante copecs* *(handwritten)*
payables du **1** au **15** Mars et du **1** au **15** Septembre.

**EXTRAIT DU RÈGLEMENT DE LA COMMISSION CHAP. II.**

(Two columns of fine print:)

§. 22. L'acquittement de la rente perpétuelle, de même que le payement des dettes à termes, s'effectuera en tems de paix, comme en tems de guerre, sans avoir égard, si le créancier appartient à une nation amie ou ennemie.

§. 23. Si un étranger propriétaire d'inscriptions meurt sans faire de testament, les inscriptions passeront à ses héritiers d'après l'ordre de succession établi par les lois du pays dont il était sujet.

§. 24. Les capitaux placés dans la dette perpétuelle étant considérés comme une propriété inviolable, seront exempts du séquestre, tant pour les prétentions de la Couronne que particulières, à moins que ces capitaux, en totalité ou en partie, n'ayent été donnés en nantissement pour contract de fournitures ou pour toute autre convention, soit avec la Couronne, soit avec des particuliers, ou pour cautionner quelque prétention, dans lesquels cas ils sont assujétis, aux lois générales concernant les hypothèques et cautions. Ces capitaux sont de même exemptés pour tous les cas de toute imposition.

§. 25. Personne ne peut être contraint de reprendre la totalité ou une partie du capital placé dans la dette perpétuelle. Mais pour faciliter aux propriétaires d'inscriptions les moyens de les convertir lorsqu'ils le désireront, en argent comptant, la Commission employera annuellement, pour les racheter, selon le cours, un capital d'amortissement qui sera assigné en sus du fonds nécessaire pour payement des rentes perpétuelles.

§. 26. Ce capital sera composé dans le principe d'une somme équivalente à 2 pr. cent du montant total des dettes perpétuelles et augmentera annuellement du montant des intérêts des rentes rachetées.

(Right of centre, below fine print:) Le Directeur de la Commission *[signature — illegible]*

La présente Inscription est cédée à

En foi de quoi signé          à          le

Certifie la signature ci dessus de

Le Consul général de Russie

NOTA. L'acquéreur ne pourra toucher la rente qu'en faisant transférer l'inscription à son nom dans le grand livre de la dette publique à la Commission.

---

## English Translation:

(Left roundel) No. 717448

(Right roundel) No. 58448

(Left panel) **Annual income** — Roubles 25

(Right panel) **Capital** — Roubles 500

**1876.**

**Inscription in the Grand Ledger of the Russian Public Debt
with the Imperial Sinking-Fund Commission.**

**FIRST SERIES.**

of a capital in silver Roubles,

*five hundred*

entered in the Grand Ledger on 11 October 1876. Book 12, fol. 1142

in the name of the Administration under the direction of Hope & Co.,
Ketwich & Voombergh & the Widow W. Borski at Amsterdam

yielding a perpetual income of FIVE per cent per year, and for
each half-year of twelve roubles fifty copecks,
payable from the 1st to the 15th of March and from the 1st to the 15th of September.

**EXTRACT FROM THE REGULATIONS OF THE COMMISSION, CHAP. II.**

§. 22. The payment of the perpetual income, as well as the payment of term debts, shall be effected in time of peace as in time of war, without regard to whether the creditor belongs to a friendly or an enemy nation.

§. 23. If a foreign owner of inscriptions dies without making a will, the inscriptions shall pass to his heirs according to the order of succession established by the laws of the country of which he was a subject.

§. 24. The capitals placed in the perpetual debt, being regarded as inviolable property, shall be exempt from sequestration, both for claims of the Crown and of private parties, unless these capitals, in whole or in part, have been given as pledge for a supply contract or for any other agreement, whether with the Crown or with private parties, or to guarantee some claim, in which cases they are subject to the general laws concerning mortgages and sureties. These capitals are likewise exempt in all cases from any taxation.

§. 25. No one may be compelled to take back the whole or a part of the capital placed in the perpetual debt. But in order to give the owners of inscriptions the means to convert them into ready money whenever they wish, the Commission shall employ annually, to redeem them at the going rate, a sinking-fund capital which shall be assigned in addition to the funds needed for the payment of the perpetual incomes.

§. 26. This capital shall in principle consist of a sum equivalent to 2 per cent of the total amount of the perpetual debts, and shall increase annually by the amount of the interest on the redeemed incomes.

The Director of the Commission *[signature — illegible]*

The present Inscription is transferred to

In witness whereof signed          at          on the

Certified the signature above of

The Consul General of Russia

NOTE. The purchaser may not collect the income except by having the inscription transferred to his name in the grand ledger of the public debt at the Commission.

title: Russian Perpetual Capital Certificate, State Commission for the Repayment of Debts, St. Petersburg, 1839
description: This is a printed certificate (Билетъ) of the Imperial Russian Государственная Коммиссія погашенія долговъ, the State Commission for the Repayment of Debts, registering a Perpetual Capital (Капиталъ Безсрочный) of 5,000 roubles inscribed in the holder's name in the state debt book under letter A, page 589. It yields a continuous income of 300 roubles a year, the rate stated as "по Шести на Сто въ Годъ" — six per hundred annually — disbursed in two coupons of 150 roubles, payable between the 15th and 31st of January and the 15th and 31st of July. Issued at St. Petersburg and dated 16 January 1839, it bears the Imperial double-headed eagle and the manuscript signatures of the Administrator (Управляющій) and Bookkeeper (Бухгалтеръ). A perpetual claim on the Treasury rather than a dated loan, it is Russia's counterpart to the Western consol or rente perpétuelle — a parallel the bilingual document makes explicit in its French inscriptions referring to a "rente perpétuelle" entered in the "dette publique."
creator: Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts
notes: Билет Государственной Комиссии погашения долгов. Perpetual Capital (Капитал Безсрочный), 300 Roubles. 1839. Income 6% p.a., paid January 15 and July 15. Imperial double-headed eagle. Signed by Administrator and Bookkeeper.

title: Russian Perpetual Capital Certificate, 500 Rubles Silver
description: This is a perpetual-debt certificate (Билетъ … на Капиталъ Безсрочный) of the Imperial Russian Commission for the Repayment of Debts, recording a principal of 500 rubles "in Russian silver coin" (Россійскою серебряною монетою) yielding a fixed annual income of 30 rubles, that is, six per hundred (по Шести на Сто) per year, paid in two half-yearly installments in January and July. Issued at St. Petersburg and dated February 1854, under Nicholas I, it is subscribed by the Commission's Administrator (Управляющій) and Bookkeeper (Бухгалтеръ). The instrument has no maturity: the state undertakes perpetual interest rather than repayment of capital, a convenient form for funding standing obligations on the eve of the Crimean War. The crowned double-headed eagle dominating the upper border, flanked by guilloche panels and a punched coupon margin, asserts sovereign credit; the silver-denominated face reflects the Kankrin reform that anchored the ruble to a silver standard.
creator: Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts
notes: Билет Государственной Комиссии погашения долгов. Perpetual Capital (Капитал Безсрочный), 50 Roubles in Russian silver (серебряным монетою). 1854. Income 6% p.a., paid January 15 and July 15. Imperial double-headed eagle. Post-Kankrin monetary reform denomination.

title: Hope & Co. Russian 6% Silver Certificate, Amsterdam, 1857
description: A bilingual Dutch-French certificate for a 500-ruble inscription in the 6% Russian Funds in Silver (Russische Fondsen in Zilver / Inscription Russe en Argent), issued at Amsterdam on 19 May 1857 by the houses of Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, and Weduwe W. Borski. The instrument represents the holder's share of an inscription entered in the Russian State Debt Book at St. Petersburg (Groot Boek / Grand Livre) and administered through a bureau the three firms maintained in Amsterdam; interest of six per cent runs for the holder's account and risk, drawn against half-yearly coupons, and the original inscription may be reclaimed upon surrender of the certificate and its unused tickets. The document exemplifies Amsterdam's enduring role as the conduit through which Dutch savings reached the Russian treasury, the silver denomination assuring investors of a hard-money claim. The engraved frame, with its restrained neoclassical roundels and balanced typographic banners, projects the sober probity of an established merchant-banking partnership rather than ornamental display.
creator: Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Weduwe W. Borski
notes: 6% Certificaat/Certificat d'Inscription Russe, Russische Fondsen in Zilver/en Argent. No. 670, R° 500 Roubles. Amsterdam, November 19, 1857. Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Wed. W. Borski. Interest 6% p.a. Ten half-yearly coupon tickets.

Van 6 per Cents
RUSSISCHE FONDSEN
IN

N°. 670.—

HOUDER dezes is geregtigd tot eene Inscriptie in het GRONDBOEK
nomens ƒ 6 per Cents Russische Fondsen, betaalbaar in ZILVER, ingeschreven in het Groot-Boek te St. Petersburg, ten name van het ADMINISTRATIEBURO, opgericht te Amsterdam, onder directie van

HOPE en COMP.,
KETWICH en VOOMBERGH,
EN
WED. W. BORSKI.

Waarvan de Interessen voor rekening en risico van den HOUDER en ontvangen en uitbetaald zullen worden op Coupons, ingevolge Art. 6 van het uitgegeven Bericht.

De HOUDER van dit Certificaat zal te allen tijde geregtigd zijn de originale INSCRIPTIE terug te vorderen, tegen terugave van dit Certificaat, met de onderwijl afgestelde HALVE JAARLIJKS COUPONS, tegen betaling van de onkosten, in Art. 10 van het Bericht vermeld.

Amsterdam, den 19 der Maand 1857

Gezien bij mij
S. Gimmelman
Notaris

Hierbij afgegeven Halfjarige COUPONS, tot 19 Juli 1859 en Calon.

---

**RIGHT COLUMN (French):**

CERTIFICAT
D'INSCRIPTION RUSSE
à 6 pour Cent
EN
ARGENT.

R° 500:—

LE PORTEUR à droit à une Inscription de Rente CENTRALE nominale capital à 6 pour Cent, de la Dette russe de Suisse, payable en ARGENT, inscrit sur le Grand-Livre à St. Pétersbourg, au nom du BUREAU d'ADMINISTRATION, établi à Amsterdam, sous la direction de

HOPE et COMP.,
KETWICH et VOOMBERGH,
ET
Veuve W. BORSKI.

Dont les Intérêts seront reçus pour compte et risque du PORTEUR et payés contre la remise des Coupons, suivant Art. 6 du Prospectus.

Le PORTEUR de ce Certificat pourra en tout temps réclamer l'INSCRIPTION originale, contre restitution du Certificat, avec les Coupons non échus y appartenant, et la RECONNAISSANCE y annexée pour obtenir le paiement des FRAIS, moyennant le paiement des frais, mentionnées dans l'Art. 10 du Prospectus.

Amsterdam, den 19' der Mei 1857

[Signature]
Ferdinand Mombault
Patr. Wooels.

Délivré COUPONS de Rentes de Six Mois, jusqu'au 19 Juillet 1859 avec Calon.

Of 6 per Cent
RUSSIAN FUNDS
IN

in the name of ƒ 6 per Cent Russian Funds, payable in SILVER, registered in the Grand Book at St. Petersburg, in the name of the ADMINISTRATIVE BUREAU, established in Amsterdam, under the direction of

AND

at 6 per Cent
IN

THE BEARER is entitled to an Inscription of CENTRAL Rent with nominal capital at 6 per Cent, of the Russian Debt of Switzerland, payable in SILVER, inscribed on the Grand-Book at St. Petersburg, in the name of the ADMINISTRATIVE BUREAU, established in Amsterdam, under the direction of

HOPE and COMP.,

title: Hope & Co. Russian 6% Inscription Certificate in Bank Assignations, Amsterdam, 1825
description: A bilingual Dutch and French certificate of Russian inscription ("Certificaat van 6 per Cents Russische Fondsen in Bank-Assignatien" / "Certificat d'Inscription Russe à 6 pour Cent en Assignations de Banque") for 1,000 roubles, bearing interest at six per cent and reckoned in bank assignations, the paper rouble then in circulation. The certificate was issued at Amsterdam on 16 April 1825 by the houses of Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, and the Widow W. Borski, who placed the underlying stock in the holder's name and undertook to procure payment of interest and principal. The document attests to Amsterdam's role as the conduit through which the Russian crown's debt reached the European investing public, the great Dutch merchant banks lending their credit to a sovereign borrower. That the sum is denominated in assignations, rather than silver, fixes the instrument before Kankrin's monetary reform of 1839. The engraving is plain and clerical, its ornament confined to a beaded border, foregrounding contractual exactitude over display.
creator: Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Weduwe W. Borski
notes: 6% Certificaat/Certificat d'Inscription Russe, in Bank-Assignatien/Assignations de Banque. No. 4031, R° 1,000 Roubles. Amsterdam, August 16, 1822. Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Wed. W. Borski. Ten half-yearly coupons (from July 1, 1829). Denominated in Bank Assignations (pre-Kankrin Reform Russian paper currency).

title: Sillem, Benecke & Co. Russian Funds Certificate, Hamburg, 1821
description: A Dutch-language certificaat issued at Hamburg on 13 March 1821, representing an inscription of five hundred roubles (Vyfhonderd Roebels) of 5 per cent Russian funds, payable in metallic coin (Metalick) and entered in the grootboek der Russische Inschryvingen in the name of an administration established at Hamburg. The bureau was directed by the firm Sillem, Benecke & Comp. together with H.J. Stresow; interest at 5 per cent was collected for the holder's account against coupons under Article 5 of the published notice, and the holder could at any time reclaim the original inscription against return of the certificate and unused coupons. The document records how a Hamburg house repackaged Russian sovereign debt for circulation among northern European savers, the city serving as an entrepôt that channelled Dutch and German capital toward the Tsar's treasury after the Napoleonic wars. The typography is austere and mercantile, the engraved 'CERTIFICAAT' heading and the vertical 'KANTOOR VAN ADMINISTRATIE' panel asserting bureaucratic order over a purely financial promise.
creator: Sillem, Benecke & Comp.; H.J. Stresow
notes: Certificaat No. 13549, 500 Roubles 5% Russian Funds payable in Metalick (specie). Sillem, Benecke & Comp. and H.J. Stresow, Hamburg. Grand Ledger inscription. Hamburg, March 13, 1821. Notary: N. Dongkants. Hamburg-issued Dutch certificate for Russian government debt.

No. 13549.

Goed voor eene Inscriptie van Vyfhonderd Roebels 5 pct. Russische fondsen, betaalbaar in Metalick, ingeschreven in het grootboek der Russische Inschrijvingen ten Naame van de administratie opgericht te Hamburg onder directie van de Heeren Sillem, Benecke & Comp. en de Heer H.J. Stresow,

waarvan de Intressen voor Rekening van den Houder ontfangen & uitbetaald zullen worden op Coupons, in gevolge Art 5. van het uitgegeven bericht.

De houder van dit Certificaat zal ten allen tyde en op deszelfs eerste aanvrage, gerechtigd zyn, de voorschreven Som van Vyfhonderd Roebels terug te vorderen, in Originneele Inscriptie, tegen terug gave van dit Certificaat, met de onverscheene Coupons, en het daarby afgegeven bewys voor de afgift van volgende Coupons, alles in gevolge Art. 2. van het bericht.

Hamburg 13 Maart 1821.

[Signature] Sillem Benecke & Co
[Signature] H.J. Stren [or Strem - unclear]
