title: Volksstaat Hessen Brown-Coal-Rye Loan Bond, Darmstadt, 1923
description: This is a Schuldverschreibung (bearer bond) of the Volksstaat Hessen, the People's State of Hesse, issued for the value of one unit (Eine Einheit) of the state's 6% Braunkohle-Roggen-Anleihe — the Brown-Coal-Rye Loan of 1923. The Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung (Hessian State Debt Administration) executed it at Darmstadt on 5 April 1923, attaching thirty interest coupons (Zinsscheine) and one renewal coupon (Erneuerungsschein). Interest of 6% runs annually from 1 May to 30 April, with the loan term beginning 1 May 1923 and redemption opening from 1 May 1926. Crucially, the bond's worth is fixed not in money but in the market value of brown coal and rye. Issued as the Reichsmark dissolved into hyperinflation, it is a Sachwertanleihe, a real-value loan whose denomination in tangible commodities shielded creditors from a currency losing all meaning. The restrained typographic frame, free of allegory, lets the contractual text and the Hessian arms speak plainly to fiscal emergency.
creator: Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung (Volksstaat Hessen)
notes: Schuldverschreibung, Volksstaat Hessen. Eine Einheit, 6% Braunkohle-Roggen-Anleihe 1923. Reihe 37, No. 20002. Darmstadt, April 5, 1923. Value indexed to brown coal and rye prices. 30 interest + 1 renewal coupon. Weimar hyperinflation commodity-indexed bond (Sachwertanleihe).
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (German):**

Reihe 37                           Eine                              № 20002
                                   Einheit

Schuldverschreibung

des

Volksstaats Hessen

über den Geldwert von 1 Einheit der 6% Braunkohle-Roggen-
Anleihe von 1923.

Gegenwärtige Schuldverschreibung ist für jeden Inhaber gültig. 30 Zinscheine
und 1 Erneuerungsschein sind beigefügt.

Das Zinsjahr läuft vom 1. Mai bis 30. April. Die Zinsen werden jährlich
gezahlt am 1. Mai und am 1. November fällig, erstmals am 1. Februar 1924 für die Zeit
vom 1. Mai 1923 bis 30. April 1924.

Die Rückzahlung der Anleihe erfolgt durch Auslosung über freihandigen
Rückkauf vom 1. Mai 1926 an mit jährlich 2 2/10 des ursprünglichen Gesamtnennn-
betrages. Vom 1. Mai 1928 an können die noch umlaufenden Schuldbor-
schreibungen mit halbjährlicher Frist ganz oder teilweise gekündigt werden.
Für die Anleihe haftet der hessische Staat mit seiner Steuerkraft und seinem
gesamten Vermögen.

Im übrigen gelten die auf der Rückseite abgedruckten Bedingungen.

Darmstadt, den 5. April 1923.

Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung.

Ausgefertigt: [signature]

---

**English translation:**

Series 37                          One                               No. 20002
                                   Unit

Debt Certificate

of the

People's State of Hesse

for the monetary value of 1 unit of the 6% Brown Coal-Rye
Loan of 1923.

This debt certificate is valid for any bearer. 30 interest coupons
and 1 renewal coupon are attached.

The interest year runs from May 1 to April 30. Interest is paid annually,
due on May 1 and November 1, first due on February 1, 1924 for the period
from May 1, 1923 to April 30, 1924.

Repayment of the loan occurs by drawing lots via free negotiated
repurchase from May 1, 1926 onward at 2 2/10 percent annually of the original total
principal amount. From May 1, 1928 onward, the still-circulating debt
certificates may be terminated in whole or in part with six-month notice.
The Hessian state guarantees the loan with its tax revenue and its
entire assets.

Moreover, the conditions printed on the reverse side apply.

Darmstadt, April 5, 1923.

Hessian State Debt Administration.

Executed: [signature]

title: Spanish Debt Conversion Passive Stock Certificate, London, 1835
description: This bearer certificate, headed "Conversion of Spanish Debt. Passive Stock," entitles its holder to fourteen pounds, three shillings and four pence (£14.3.4) of Spanish Five per Cent Passive Stock, issued at London on 4 May 1835. It is an interim, fractional claim: on presentation of enough such certificates to make up one of the standard amounts printed below — £42.10, £85, £170, £255, £510, or £1,020 — the signatory engages to deliver in exchange a bond of the Spanish Government. It bears the signature of the Ricardo house, London agents for the operation. The piece belongs to the restructuring of Spain's defaulted external debt under the Martínez de la Rosa government, in which arrears of interest were capitalised into a deferred "passive" class. Plainly typeset within a guilloche border, it speaks less to ornament than to the workaday machinery of the London market in foreign sovereign paper.
creator: Spanish Government (London agent: Ricardo)
notes: Conversion of Spanish Debt, Passive Stock Certificate No. 457, £14 3s 4d. Spanish 5% Passive Stock. Bearer exchanges certificate at standard amounts: £42.10, £85, £170, £255, £510, £1,020 for Spanish government bond. Signed by [Ricardo]. London, May 4, 1835. 1834–35 Spanish debt restructuring.

title: Stadnitski & van Heukelom Russian Loan Certificate, Amsterdam, 1825
description: A bilingual Dutch and French certificate documenting an inscription in the State Debt Book of the Imperial Russian Amortization Commission at St. Petersburg, entitling the bearer to a capital of Duizend Roebels / Mille Roubles (1,000 roubles) in assignats at 6 percent interest. Issued at Amsterdam, dated 30 June 1825, it was administered by a syndicate of Amsterdam banking houses headed by Stadnitski & van Heukelom and including Jacob van Beeck Vollenhoven, Samuel & David Saportas, Lamaison & Bouwer, Johannes Samuel Wurfbain, Hendrik Oyens, and De Lanoy & Burlage. The certificate is drawn up under the prospectus published 30 June 1824, and the holder may at any time reclaim the underlying inscription by surrendering the certificate with its unused coupons; twelve half-yearly interest coupons were attached. The instrument speaks to the early-nineteenth-century channeling of Dutch capital into Russian sovereign debt, with Amsterdam intermediaries repackaging the tsar's St. Petersburg inscriptions into transferable paper for investors in the Low Countries. The typography is plain and commercial, its restrained roman and italic lettering signalling fiscal probity rather than ornament.
creator: Stadnitski & van Heukelom; Jacob van Beeck Vollenhoen; Samuel & David Saportas; Lamaison & Bouwer; Johannes Samuel Wurfbain; Hendrik Oyens; De Lanoy & Burlage
notes: Certificaat L.A. No. 5320, 1,000 Roubles in Assignations. 6% inscription, Imperial Russian State Debt Book (Commission at St. Petersburg). Amsterdam, June 8, 1875. Administered by Stadnitski & van Heukelom et al. Ten half-yearly coupons.

title: London Stock Exchange WWI Good Delivery Certificate
description: This printed certificate, issued under the London Stock Exchange's Temporary Regulations for the Re-Opening of the Stock Exchange, applies Regulation 10 to a single bearer security: a City of Baku 5% 1910 bond for £20. Completed in manuscript and dated 29 April 1916, it bears the signature and inked stamp of the Secretary of the Share and Loan Department, certifying that the bond "has been expressly passed by the Committee as a good delivery, special cause having been shown." Regulation 10 barred bearer or blank-endorsed securities from good delivery unless impressed with the government stamp dated before the 1st of October and accompanied by a banker's or broker's declaration that they had not, since the outbreak of war, passed into enemy ownership. Such rules, framed when the Exchange reopened after its August 1914 closure, sought to keep securities held by enemy nationals out of wartime dealings. The austere, text-only sheet, printed by E. Couchman & Co. of Throgmorton Street, embodies the administrative machinery by which a great market reconciled open trading with the controls of total war.
creator: London Stock Exchange (Share and Loan Department)
notes: London Stock Exchange WWI Regulation 10 good delivery certificate. City of Baker 5% 1910 Bond No. 58707, £20. Signed Secretary, Share and Loan Department. April 29, 1916. WWI wartime enemy-ownership regulations.
transcription:
# Transcription

**TEMPORARY REGULATIONS FOR THE RE-OPENING OF THE STOCK EXCHANGE.**

---

**REGULATION 10.**

No Securities to bearer or endorsed in blank will be a good delivery unless impressed with the Government stamp dated previous to the 1st October and accompanied by a declaration by a Banker, Broker or other responsible party that they have not, since the outbreak of war, been in enemy ownership, except in cases permitted under Regulation 4 (3) or Securities sent for registration abroad under Clause 9 and such other Securities as may be expressly passed by the Committee as a good delivery on special cause being shown.

Title of Security .... *City of Baker 5% 1910* ....

Bond for £20 Numbered .... *58707* ....

I hereby certify that the above Security has been expressly passed by the Committee as a good delivery, special cause having been shown.

Signature .... *[Handwritten signature - illegible]* ....

Secretary .... *SECRETARY, SHARE AND LOAN DEPARTMENT, STOCK EXCHANGE.* ....

Date .... *29 4 1916* ....

---

**REGULATION 4 (3).** — No dealings will be allowed in any new issue, other than an issue of British Government Securities made after the 4th January, 1915, unless specially allowed by the Committee and approved by the Treasury.

**REGULATION 9 (Sentence 3).** — Securities which have been transmitted abroad merely for registration shall for the purpose of this regulation be deemed to have remained in physical possession in the United Kingdom.

**REGULATION 11.** — *The expression "Enemy" in these regulations means any person or body of persons of whatever nationality, resident, or carrying on business in an enemy country, but does not include persons of enemy nationality who are neither resident nor carrying on business in an enemy country. In the case of incorporated bodies, enemy character attaches only to those incorporated in an enemy country.*

COPYRIGHT. — Printed and sold by E. COUCHMAN & CO., LTD., 23, Throgmorton Street, E.C.

---

## Notable markings:

- Handwritten signature (Secretary's signature - illegible)
- Date stamp: "29 4 1916" (29 April 1916)
- Bond number: 58707
- Bond denomination: £20
- Security title: City of Baker 5% 1910
- Printed stamp: "SECRETARY, SHARE AND LOAN DEPARTMENT, STOCK EXCHANGE."
- Copyright attribution to E. Couchman & Co., Ltd.

title: United States Treasury Six Per Cent Deferred Stock Certificate, Philadelphia, 1792
description: This is a registered certificate of United States public stock, issued by the Treasury's Register's Office and dated 16 June 1792. It acknowledges that the United States owes Daniel Crommelin & Sons, merchants of Amsterdam, or their assigns, the sum of ten thousand dollars, bearing interest at six per cent per annum from the first day of January 1801, payable quarter-yearly, and redeemable in any one year only to the proportion of eight dollars upon a hundred of the principal. The stock was created under the Act making Provision for the Debt of the United States of 4 August 1790, and is transferable solely by personal appearance or by attorney at the proper office. Signed by Joseph Nourse, Register of the Treasury, with a notarial attestation begun by Clement Biddle of Philadelphia, the certificate embodies Alexander Hamilton's funding program: the deferred interest start and gentle redemption schedule were designed to consolidate Revolutionary debt on terms the new federal credit could sustain, while its placement with a great Dutch house records the role of Amsterdam capital in financing the early republic. The engraved frame holds the small seal of the Treasury rather than allegorical ornament, a restraint befitting an instrument meant to project fiscal sobriety.
creator: Treasury of the United States; Joseph Nourse, Register
notes: US Treasury 6% Funded Debt Certificate No. 42.94, $10,000. Daniel Ternwiles & Wife. Register Office, June 16, 1792. Signed Joseph Nourse, Register. Notarized by Clement Biddle. Created under Funding Act of August 4, 1790. Hamilton's federal debt consolidation.

bearing Interest at Six per Cent per Annum from the first Day of January A.D. title: Dutch Deferred Debt Compliance Certificate, Amsterdam, 1815
description: This is a Dutch certificate of compliance with the law (Bewijs van Voldoening aan de Wet) for the Uitgestelde Schuld, the deferred class of the national public debt, drawn for a capital of ƒ1400. The Commission named in it was established by decree of His Royal Highness (Z.K.H., the Sovereign Prince Willem) of 7 June 1814, charged with executing the Law of 14 May 1814; the certificate attests that articles 5, 12 and 18 of that law have been satisfied, the sum being inscribed in the Oude Grootboek der Publieke Schuld in the name of the village of Heemstede, for which W.H. Gerlings signs as burgemeester. Issued at Amsterdam in March 1815, the document records how the restored House of Orange consolidated the debts left by the Batavian Republic and French annexation, deferring part of the principal rather than repudiating it. Engraved Gothic blackletter headings lend the printed form the gravity of an official instrument of the new monarchy.
creator: Dutch Government Commission (Commissie voor de Uitgestelde Schuld)
notes: Bewijs van Voldoening aan de Wet, Uitgestelde Schuld No. 6231, f. 500. Pursuant to Besluit Z.K.H. June 7, 1814, No. 23, and Wet of May 14, 1814. Holder: W.F. Holling. Post-Napoleonic Dutch deferred debt restructuring. Recorded in Hollandsche Schuld Grootboek.
transcription:
# Certificate of Compliance with the Law — Deferred Debt (Uitgestelde Schuld), Amsterdam, 1815 — Transcription

## Original:

(Small centered heading in the top ornamental border:) **Uitgestelde Schuld.**

**N.o** _6234_          **Kapitaal** _ƒ 1400_

**Bewijs van Voldoening**
**AAN DE WET.**

(ornamental rule)

**UITGESTELDE SCHULD.**

Alzoo aan de COMMISSIE, bij Besluit van Z. K. H., in dato 7 Junij 1814, N.o 23, belast met de Werkzaamheden voortvloeijende uit de Wet van 14 Mei 1814, gebleken is: dat door _W. H. Geerlings_ als _Burgem.r_ [Burgemeester]

behoorlijk is voldaan aan den inhoud van Art. 5, 12 en 18 van de Wet voornoemd, voor de Kapitalen op het **Oude Grootboek** der **Publieke Schuld**, ingeschreven ten name van _het dorp Heemstede_

blijkens AFSCHRIJVINGS-BILLET N.o _13709_ van het GROOTBOEK zal deze Rekening overeenkomstig Art. 16 der Wet, op het **Nieuwe Grootboek** der UITGESTELDE SCHULD, ten voorschreven hoofde worden overgebragt ter Somma van _veertien honderd guldens_ welke mitsdien reeds zal deelen in de kans der eerste Uitloting van dezelven.

En is hierbij overeenkomstig Art. 14 van het Besluit van Z. K. H., in dato 28 Julij 1814, N.o 23, afgegeven een Restant-Renversaal in blanco, groot _honderd agt en dertig guld: negen st: vier pen:_ [one hundred thirty-eight guilders, nine stuivers, four penningen — cursive struck through with ruled security lines; sub-units uncertain]. Uitgegeven uit kracht van Art. 62 der Wet van 14 Mei l.l., door de COMMISSIE voornoemd.

En is deze ten blijke van Echtheid door ons onderteekend.

(handwritten, center-left:) _Ingeschreven_

Amsterdam, den _2[8?]_ Maart 181_5_          (two ink signatures at lower right, illegible)

(Left-margin handwritten annotations, top to bottom:)
- _Bureau van Acceptatie_
- _8. 2892._
- _[Get.?]_ / _Schutte_ [signature]
- _Coll:_ / _v.d. Haeg_ [?] / _Schutte_ [signature]
- _fol. 250_ / _Vaillant_ [signature]
- (a further abbreviated margin note below fol. 250) [illegible]

---

## English Translation:

(Top border heading:) **Deferred Debt.**

**No.** 6234          **Capital** ƒ 1400

**Certificate of Compliance**
**WITH THE LAW.**

**DEFERRED DEBT.**

Whereas it has appeared to the COMMISSION — established by Decree of His Royal Highness, dated 7 June 1814, No. 23, charged with the tasks arising out of the Law of 14 May 1814 — that by _W. H. Geerlings_, as _Burgomaster_,

due compliance has been made with the content of Articles 5, 12 and 18 of the aforesaid Law, for the Capital sums in the **Old Grand Ledger** of the **Public Debt**, inscribed in the name of _the village of Heemstede_;

as evidenced by WRITE-OFF NOTE No. 13709 of the GRAND LEDGER, this Account shall, in accordance with Article 16 of the Law, be transferred to the **New Grand Ledger** of the DEFERRED DEBT, under the aforesaid heading, for the Sum of _fourteen hundred guilders_, which shall accordingly already share in the chance of the first Drawing (redemption lottery) of the same.

And there is hereby, in accordance with Article 14 of the Decree of His Royal Highness, dated 28 July 1814, No. 23, issued a blank Remainder-Counterbond, amounting to _one hundred thirty-eight guilders, nine stuivers, four penningen_. Issued by virtue of Article 62 of the Law of 14 May last, by the aforesaid COMMISSION.

And in witness of its authenticity this has been signed by us.

title: Continental Loan Office Bill of Exchange, No. 131
description: A bill of exchange, partly printed and partly completed by hand, issued in the name of the United States of America and dated the ninth day of October 1778. Drawn at thirty days' sight upon the Commissioners of the United States at Paris, it directs payment to Anne Brown, or order, of three hundred dollars rendered in fifteen hundred livres tournois at the stated rate of five livres tournois per dollar, for interest due on money borrowed by the United States. It is the third bill of a set of four, payable if the first, second, and fourth remain unpaid. Countersigned by Tho. Smith, Commissioner of the Continental Loan-Office for Pennsylvania, and signed by Francis Hopkinson, Treasurer of Loans. Issued amid the Revolutionary War, the instrument shows how the Continental Congress, lacking specie at home, serviced the interest on its Loan Office certificates by drawing bills on its agents in France, binding the new republic's credit to its French alliance. The engraved capital and restrained type betray no ornamental program, the document's authority resting on signature and form rather than imagery.
creator: United States of America, Loan Office, Pennsylvania
notes: US Bill of Exchange No. 131, $300 / 1,300 Livres Tournois. October 9, 1776. Payable to Annie Brown. Interest on Loan Office Certificates. Pennsylvania Loan Office; signed by H. Atkinson, Treasurer of Loans. Among the earliest-dated Continental bills of exchange.

title: Hessian Rye Loan Bond
description: A 5% Schuldverschreibung (bond) of the Volksstaat Hessen (People's State of Hesse), part of its Roggen-Anleihe (Rye Loan), issued at Darmstadt on 12 December 1923 by the Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung (Hessian State Debt Administration). Its face value is fixed not in money but in kind: the cash value of 10 Zentner (hundredweight) of rye, the bond declaring that the state owes the bearer "den Geldwert von 10 Zentner Roggen." Against it the state pledges its whole property and revenues, with interest and amortisation charged on general receipts and the state domains. Dated to the closing weeks of the great inflation, when the paper Mark had become worthless, the certificate exemplifies the rye-indexed Sachwertanleihen by which German authorities restored confidence by promising real value rather than nominal sums. The dense black Fraktur lettering and the central rye-sheaf cartouche tie fiscal promise to the harvest meant to secure it.
creator: Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung (Volksstaat Hessen)
notes: Roggen-Anleihe, Volksstaat Hessen. 5% Schuldverschreibung, 10 Zentner Roggen. Abteilung 1, Buchst. D, No. 002152. Darmstadt, December 12, 1923. Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung. Commodity-indexed rye bond (Sachwertanleihe). Weimar hyperinflation.

title: Continental Loan Office Bill of Exchange, Massachusetts Bay
description: This is a Continental Loan Office bill of exchange, a partly printed, partly manuscript draft by which the United States of America paid interest on its domestic war debt in hard foreign coin. Dated 12 November 1778, it is the First Bill of a set of four (only one of which need be honoured), promising at thirty days' sight to pay Jesse White or order thirty-six dollars, reckoned at five livres tournois to the dollar, hence one hundred and eighty livres tournois, "for Interest due on Money borrowed by the United States." It is addressed to the American Commissioners at Paris, countersigned by Nathaniel Appleton, Commissioner of the Continental Loan Office for Massachusetts Bay, and signed by Francis Hopkinson, Treasurer of Loans. Issued amid a depreciating paper currency, the instrument let the young confederation reward its lenders in stable specie drawn on French credit, binding the Revolution's finances to its Parisian alliance. The engraved foliate border frames an otherwise plain, businesslike form.
creator: United States of America, Loan Office, Massachusetts Bay
notes: US Bill of Exchange No. 64, $36 / 180 Livres Tournois. November 12, 1778. Payable to Jesse White. Interest on Loan Office Certificates. Massachusetts Bay Loan Office; Nath. Appleton, Commissioner; H. Atkinson, Treasurer of Loans.

title: Continental Loan Office Bill of Exchange, No. 71
description: This is a bill of exchange, partly engraved and partly completed by hand, drawn by the United States of America on the fourth day of November 1778 and numbered as the first of a set of four ("First Bill, Second, Third and Fourth not paid"). At thirty days' sight it directs the American loan commissioners at Paris to pay John Simpkins, or order, one hundred and twenty dollars rendered as six hundred livres tournois at the fixed rate of five livres to the dollar, the sum representing interest due on money borrowed by the United States. It is countersigned by Nathaniel Appleton, Commissioner of the Continental Loan Office in Massachusetts Bay, and signed by Francis Hopkinson, Treasurer of Loans. The draft embodies the young republic's wartime expedient of paying interest on its domestic loan-office debt in hard foreign currency abroad, drawing on credit extended by France to sustain confidence in the Continental cause when paper money was depreciating at home.

title: Suriname Plantation Loan Conditions
description: This is the printed sheet of conditions (Conditien eener Negotiatie) governing a one-million-guilder loan — "een Millioen of Tienmaal honderd Duysend Guldens" — raised under the direction of the Amsterdam merchants Lever en de Bruine, with Samuel en Jacobus Walland as commissioners. The negotiatie is offered to planters in the Colony of Suriname (Colonie Zurinamen), who pledge their plantations, produce, and movable and immovable goods as mortgage security against capital advanced at six percent annual interest, repayable in fixed installments drawn from the estates' yield. Such terms exemplify the Dutch plantation-mortgage funds (plantageleningen) by which Amsterdam capital financed West Indian sugar, coffee, and cotton estates; the document's plain typographic dignity, free of allegory, presents credit as a sober contractual instrument. The articles continue beyond this opening page (signature A).
creator: Lever en de Bruine; Samuel en Jacobus Walland (Commissioners)
notes: Conditien eener Negotiatie, f. 1,000,000. Suriname plantation mortgage negotiatie. Directors: Lever en de Bruine, Amsterdam. Commissioners: Samuel & Jacobus Walland. Dutch plantageleningen system for West Indian colonial plantation finance.
transcription:
# Original:

CONDITIEN EENER NEGOTIATIE
Van een Millioen of Tienmaal honderd Duyfend Guldens, onder Directie van de Heeren LEVER EN DE BRUINE, Kooplieden te Amsterdam, en ten overflaan van de Heeren SAMUEL en JACOBUS WALLAND, als Commisariffen deczer Negotiatie en in alle gevallen Geauthorifeerde Representanten van de Gevolmachtigde Geinterseffeerders in dezelve, ten behoeve van Planlyke Geintereffeerders in de COLONIE ZURINAMEN, welke geneecgen zyn en aaneemlyк bevonden worden daar in Deel te neemen, opgerigt.

Art. I.

De Planters dewelke geadmitteerd zullen worden, uit deeze Negotiatie Penningen op te neemen, zullen hunne Plantagiën (met uytfluitiing van Meubilen en andere Zaaken, onnodig tot het voortzetten derfelven) behoorlyк doen Inventariseeren en door besiglde oin daar toe aangeftelde Prijzeurs doen prijzeeren.

En wyders die Plantagiën met alle derzelver aan en toebehooren Legaal en Speciaal na style Locaal der COLONIE ZURINAMEN, verbinden en verhypothequeeeren aan de Heeren Directeurs dezer Negotiatie, en zulks voor en ten behoeven der Negotiatie, welck Hypotheeck geftarkt zal moeten worden met willinge Condemnatie van den Ed. Hove van Civiel Justitie der gedagte Colonie.

Art. II.

De Planters zullen zig by dat Hypotheecq verpligten, alle de producten profluerende van hunne Plantagien, van tyd tot tyd te Configneren aan de Directeuren dezer Negotiatie en van dezelven hunnen benodigheden voor de Plantagien te doen toekomeli, en wyders overgefte dat ingavallen zy daar pan niet mogten voldoen, of hunne Producten in ZURINAMEN kwamen te verkoopen of op andere wyzen elders te diverteeren, het by hun verschuldigde Capitaal met de Intresten en kosten diretkelijk kan teeren gebruikt en by Executie ingevorderd by Executie ingevorderd zy by dezelven hun verhalt aan hunne Persoonen, Plantagiën, roerenде en onroerenде Goederеn.

Art. III.

De Heeren Directeuren zullen moeten werden geequalificeerd eens voor al tot het laten doen van Affuitantie, zo op de benodigtheeden, als op de af te zenden producten, van welke afzending de Planters ter behooryker tyd advertentie zullen moeten geeven.

Art. IV.

De Planters verbinden zig, om van de verfchuldigde Somma Jaarlycks te betaalen de Intreffеn van zes percent tot de darelycke voldoening en aflossing toe, welke Intreffеn uit de Producten der Plantagiën voor alle anderen schulden zullen moeten werden voldaan.

Art. V.

De Restitutie of aflossing der Capitaalen zal gefchieden in Termynen, duydelyk in het Hypotheeck te expreffeeren, zodanig die door de Planters zullen werden verkoeен, edog welke verfchuldige zodanig zal moeten zyn gereculeerd, dat binnen de tyd van 20 Jaaren de Termynen wel micerder, maar niet minder dan 10 percento zal mogen bedragen.

Art. VI.

De Planters zullen gehoude zyn binnen drie Maanden, na dat zy de Jaarlycke Reckening Courant van de Heeren Directeuren bekomen hebben, dezelve te approbeeren of hunne belanпgen daar tegen op te geeven en aan Directeuren toe te zenden, en zulks niet gedaan hebbende, zal de Reckening als door hun geapprobeert zynde, gehoude worden.

[Followed by signature mark and "A" at bottom]

---

## English translation:

CONDITIONS OF A NEGOTIATION
For one Million or Ten times one hundred thousand Guilders, under the Direction of the Messrs. LEVER AND DE BRUINE, Merchants in Amsterdam, and with the representation of the Messrs. SAMUEL and JACOBUS WALLAND, as Commissioners of this Negotiation and in all cases duly Authorized Representatives of the Authorized Shareholders in the same, for the benefit of Planters and interested Shareholders in the COLONY OF SURINAME, who are willing and found suitable to participate therein, established.

Art. I.

The Planters who shall be admitted to take up monies from this Negotiation shall cause their Plantations (with the exclusion of Movables and other goods unnecessary for the continuation thereof) to be properly inventoried and valued by appointed Appraisers.

And further they shall bind their Plantations with all that belongs thereto legally and specially in the manner customary in the COLONY OF SURINAME, and mortgage them to the Messrs. Directors of this Negotiation, and this for and on behalf of the Negotiation, which Mortgage shall be strengthened with willing Condemnation by the Honored Court of Civil Justice of the said Colony.

Art. II.

The Planters shall obligate themselves by that Mortgage to assign all the products flowing from their Plantations, from time to time, to the Directors of this Negotiation and to receive from the same their necessities for the Plantations; and further that if it should happen that they cannot satisfy this, or that their Products are sold in SURINAME or diverted elsewhere in any other way, the Capital owed by them with the Interest and costs can be directly pursued and recovered by Execution, with recourse against all their Persons, Plantations, movable and immovable Goods.

Art. III.

The Messrs. Directors shall be authorized once and for all to make settlements regarding both the necessary supplies and the products to be sent, of which shipment the Planters shall give proper notice in due time.

Art. IV.

The Planters obligate themselves to pay yearly on the owed Sum the interest of six percent toward the yearly payment and repayment thereof, which Interest from the Products of the Plantations shall be satisfied before all other debts.

Art. V.

The Repayment or redemption of the Capitals shall occur in Terms, clearly expressed in the Mortgage, to be determined by the Planters themselves, provided that such payment obligation shall be so calculated that within the period of 20 years the Terms may become less, but not less than 10 percent per annum.

Art. VI.

The Planters shall be obligated within three Months after they have received the yearly Account Current from the Messrs. Directors to approve the same or give notice of their objections thereto and send them to the Directors, and if they do not do so, the Account shall be held as approved by them and shall be maintained accordingly.

---

- Page marked "A" at bottom
- Printed in Dutch, typical 18th-century typography
- Document appears to be the first page of a longer prospectus

title: Vlaardingen Orphanage Lottery Loan Conditions
description: This printed prospectus sets out the conditions of a negotiatie (loan-lottery) of f. 75,000, headed "Dankbaarheid en Erkentenis" (Gratitude and Acknowledgement) and sanctioned by the Vertegenwoordigend Lichaam des Bataafschen Volks, the representative assembly of the Batavian Republic. Dated 9 May 1800, it was raised to sustain the orphanage (Weeshuys) of the town of Vlaardingen. The loan is divided into 1,500 shares of f. 50 each, allotted by drawing. A prize table runs from a single f. 10,000 down through f. 5,000, f. 1,000, f. 500, f. 250, f. 125, f. 100 and f. 50, with 500 prizes totalling f. 48,000 plus premiums. The 500 highest prizes and 14 premiums (f. 50,000) are paid as 3 percent obligations; the remaining 1,000 prizes (f. 50,000) take the form of expiring life annuities (instervende lyfrentebrieven) graded into five age classes, from f. 300 yearly for holders under twelve to f. 500 for those above forty-eight. The instrument fuses charitable endowment, public lottery and annuity finance in one revolutionary-era civic appeal.
creator: Vertegenwoordigend Lichaam van het Bataafschen Volks; Weeshuys Vlaardingen
notes: Negotiatie f. 75,000 / 1,500 shares at f. 50. For Weeshuys (Orphanage) of Vlaardingen. Batavian Republic, May 9, 1800. Lottery prizes: f.10,000 down to f.125. Annual life annuity interest by age class for non-prize holders.

title: "Voor den Armen" Charitable Bond, City of Haarlem
description: A Dutch municipal charitable bond headed "Voor den Armen" (For the Poor), issued at Haarlem and signed "den eersten January 1805" by M. Bodisco, the Ontfanger van de Stedelyke Belasting (Receiver of the City Tax). Acting under special qualification of the Raad (Council) by resolution of 5 June 1804, the receiver acknowledges f. 500 (Vyf-Honderd Guldens) received from a lender left unnamed on the printed form, placed at interest "tegen den Penning 40," that is one-fortieth, or 2½ per cent, yielding f. 12.10 (twelve guilders, ten stuivers) yearly. A resolution of the Raad of 14 December 1804 pledges the city's incomes and goods, especially the revenues of the former Gilden (guilds), as security, and sixteen coupons of f. 12.10 each, running from January 1806, are appended. The instrument shows a small city financing poor-relief on the credit of its own dissolved corporations during the Batavian Republic, on the eve of the kingdom Napoleon would impose. Manuscript endorsements track coupon renewals from 1822 to a final talon dated 1903, a near-century of unbroken service. Engraved italic and roman capitals frame the text in a sober, restrained late-eighteenth-century manner without allegorical ornament.
creator: City of Haarlem (Ontvanger der Stadslijke Belasting)
notes: Voor den Armen, Haarlem. Charitable bond No. 110, f. 500 at Penning 40 (2½%). Issued by Ontvanger der Stadslijke Belasting, Haarlem. Council resolution June 5, 1804. Signed M. Bodisco. Coupon annotations from 1822–1860s. Municipal charitable finance.

title: Habsburg Imperial Obligation (Deutz Loan), Amsterdam
description: This printed Dutch-language obligation ("Obligatoir Instrument") records a sovereign loan contracted in the name of Charles VI, by the Grace of God elected Holy Roman Emperor, whose full Habsburg titulature opens the text: "WY KAREL de Zesde... Koning in Germanien en Spanjen... Aards-Hertog van Oostenryk... in Opper- en Neder Silesien..." The Emperor acknowledges a borrowing of seven hundred thousand guilders Holland Current money at five per cent interest, raised at Amsterdam through Willem Gideon Deutz as director and agent. The capital is to be amortised within fifteen years, the annual redemptions running from Anno 1737 to Anno 1751 — interest only through 1746, the capital then repaid in five installments of twenty per cent from 1747 to 1751 — charged upon the revenues of the Silesian duchies. Issued at Amsterdam and authenticated by the notary J. Barels, the document exemplifies how a land-rich but cash-poor Habsburg court tapped the deep Dutch capital market, pledging provincial income to foreign rentiers. The dense, unornamented letterpress, framed only by marginal annotations, presents itself as a legal-financial instrument rather than a decorative title, its authority resting on imperial style and notarial form alone.
creator: Habsburg Imperial Government (Charles VI / Karel de Tweede)
notes: Dutch bond/obligation document. "Wy KAREL, de Tweede, door Gods Roome Keyser..." Habsburg imperial bond. Agent: Willem Gideon Donz., Amsterdam. Aandelen and Obligaties in Hollandsch Courant Geld. Likely refers to Emperor Charles VI (1711–1740). Dutch-intermediated Habsburg imperial finance.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

(1)

WY KAREL de Zesde, door Gods gnaden verkooren Rooms Keyzer, ten allerijen en Boheemien, Lands-Hertzog van Oost-enryck, Hertzog van Burgondien, Styrien, Carinthien, Crain, en Wirtemberg, in Opper- en Neder-Silezien, Markgraaf tot Mahren, in Opper en Neder-Lausitz, Graaf tot Habsburg, Tyrol en Gortz &c. beken-nen, voor Ons., onze Vryen en Nakomlingen, in kracht dezes schuldbrief openlijk., en doen door middel en allardeste van Willem Gideon Deutz, tot Amsterdam van verscheydene Persoonen en Particulieren, ten deele onderhet gebied van de Heeren Staaten der Vereenigde Nederlanden, ten deele ook op andere Plaatsen, woonende, ten dienst van onsen van het gemeente weezen genegetieerd, en by forme van beleeninge op-gebragt, zedert in handen van onze Keizerslyke schatkamer in contant geld, tegens de daar voor ontfangende behoorlyke equipanten, voldaan is zekere Capitaal, ende Courant geld, bedragende zevenaaml hondert duyzend gyldens Hollands Courant, en wie dierulyk in de gemeene Somme was vereymaid hondert duyzend Guldens Hollands Courant aen Willem Gideon Deutz., en verdere mede Geinterestlder dens., de welcke Bovenitaande leeningen te zamen hebben gekooten, verichulydt syn gewornen, en van dien aan tegen voor Ons., onze Vryen en Nakomlingen, voorwetens op voldaagdlyck, in de Interessen, voorwetens na voorgaande onderhandelinge daar over met syne daar toe benoemde Gevollmachtigdens gehoueden, tegeevoguiven, en vyt tieden beleeven, ioezeggen, en verrigsdelen, dat wy voor het geneelde for leen opgenoomene Capitaal 's jaarlyks aan Intereilen wy rato tot betalen sullen pro sexe. de vorige nominale opgenomene, en verrigde somme van zevenaal hondert duyzend gyldens Hollands Courant vyftien Jaaren binnen Amsterdam, in gelyke Hollandsche Courante Munt weder sullen betaelen en doen Edgesldo en in derzelvige jaren, synde Anno een duyzend zevenhonderd zeven en dertig, tot Anno een duyzend zevenhonderd zeven en veertig, en twee duyzend zevenhonderd aght en veertig, en twee duyzend zevenhonderd negen en veertig, een duyzend zevenhonderd vyftigh, en een duyzend zevenhonderd-rt een en vyftig, het Capitaal zelis telsens voor een Jaer geplaert wytti pro ratore, gehevens de Interesten pro rata op halve verloopen, algesoi werden val; zullende de vooriz. afslofinge te opzigtige van het Kapitaal van half Jaar tot half Jaar, doch de betaalnge der Interetlen alle vierdeel Jaars geschiedde, en wel in-pleids uyt de middelen door onze Hertzogdommen van Opper en Silzien bewilligd., en vantyd tot tyd iaadande vervallen, gelyk ook ten dien ten byhe trouwe en geneotse houden zullen aldaar, volgens onze genadedie Obligatie, zich in de beste forme van rechten verunbonden zenden, om de gemelde be'lligige, en door baar te voldoende's Lands Inkomsten ten erbelteemder ryd, op de hler vorengeltalte termynen, zo wel de gestipuleerde Inte-

resten, als het Kapitaal zelfs voor z. zevenaal hondert duyzend gyldens Hollands Courant, tot bilyk en welkomen geneogen van hem Willem Gideon Deutz, en der meede mede Geintereslders, zonder weinig manquement afgesiot, en over gelyke mede afslotinge door onze Vorten en Stauden in Silzien deswegens opgemaakt, dat namemtyk de allos-tinge zo wel van het ter leen gelichrickte Kapitaal, als der Interesten pro rata op de bo-ventlande wyze, en in de vattgestelde termynen, zonder cerize exception of uyttel geslcheiden zal, allesins voor aangenaam gehouden, en daar over onze genadigte toe-

WY

# English Translation:

(1)

WE CHARLES the Second, by the Grace of God Elected Roman Emperor, [etc.]. To all and every one, whom this present deed may concern, be it known, that We, together with Our Heirs and Successors, do hereby openly and publicly declare and make known by this letter of obligation and bond, that through the mediation and directed agency of Willem Gideon Deutz, of Amsterdam, from diverse Persons and Individuals, partly within the jurisdiction of the States of the United Netherlands and partly residing in other Places, in service to our commonly administered treasury, and in the form of a loan, has been brought into the hands of Our Imperial Treasury in current money, against appropriate securities, a certain Capital Sum, and Holy Roman money, amounting to seven hundred thousand guilders in Holland Currency, and this common sum having been computed at one hundred thousand Guilders Holland Currency, [which] to Willem Gideon Deutz., and the further and mutual Interested Parties, the which aforementioned loans taken together have been subscribed, obligated and concluded between Us, Our Heirs and Successors, and from that time forth against Us, Our Heirs and Successors, with knowledge and agreement, on the condition and understanding that, having received the aforementioned loan amount of Capital, in yearly Interests We shall pay at a rate to the existing and stipulated Interest pro rata, the previous nominal borrowed sum of seven hundred thousand guilders Holland Currency for fifteen Years within Amsterdam, in like Holland Currency Coinage, shall be repaid and discharged from Anno one thousand seven hundred seven and thirty, until Anno one thousand seven hundred seven and forty, and two thousand seven hundred eight and forty, and two thousand seven hundred nine and forty, one thousand seven hundred fifty, and one thousand seven hundred fifty-one, the Capital itself in each and every Year pro rata to be placed; such being the aforesaid discharge regarding the Capital from half Year to half Year, but the payment of the Interests every quarter Year shall be made, and well through the resources from Our Duchies of Upper and Lower Silesia granted, and from time to time as the aforementioned obligations fall due, as also at that time by his loyalty and promise shall hold themselves, according to our gracious Obligation, bound in the best form of law and of right, to fulfill and to execute's Lands Income for the aforementioned matter and time at the aforementioned terms, so well the stipulated Inte-

rests, as the Capital itself for said seven hundred thousand guilders Holland Currency, to the satisfaction and acceptance of him Willem Gideon Deutz, and the likewise also Interested Parties, without any hindrance whatsoever satisfied, and further over like discharge by Our Princes and States in Silesia for these purposes drawn up, that namely the payment so well of the amount loaned out of Capital, as of the Interests pro rata in the above manner, and in the time fixed, without any exception or evasion whatsoever shall be executed, all in every way for approved held, and thereupon our gracious...

title: German Government International 5½% Loan of 1930, Belgian Issue (Berlin, 1930)
description: This is a bearer bond of the Internationale 5½%ige Anleihe des Deutschen Reichs 1930 — the German Government International 5½ Per Cent. Loan of 1930 — printed trilingually in German, English, and French. It belongs to the Belgian issue (Belgische Ausgabe / Tranche Belge), carrying a face value of 100 Belgas, equal to 500 Belgian Francs, and paying 5½ per cent interest. The bond is dated Berlin, 15 August 1930, and bears the seal and signatures of the Reichsschuldenverwaltung, the Reich Debt Administration. The loan was the financial instrument of the Young Plan, which rescheduled German reparations after the First World War; it was floated simultaneously across several markets and currencies — pounds, French and Belgian francs, kronor, Swiss francs, and dollars — to spread the burden among the creditor powers and lend the settlement an air of commercial normalcy. The red eagle seal asserts the sovereign credit of the Weimar state standing behind a debt born of defeat.
creator: German Government (Reichsschuldenverwaltung)
notes: German Government International 5½% Loan 1930. Belgian Issue. No. A.22399, 100 Belgas = 500 Belgian Francs. Trilingual German/English/French. Reichsschuldenverwaltung. Young Plan international bond issue, simultaneous multi-currency placement.

title: Dutch Essequibo Plantation Negotiatie
description: This printed copy ("Copie") of a notarial deed of obligation and mortgage establishes a plantation negotiatie — a syndicated loan secured on West Indian estates and placed with investors in the Dutch Republic. Drawn before Albertus Bakker and Paulus Cordes, councillors of the Court of Policy, Criminal and Civil Justice of the Essequibo river and its districts, it binds the spouses Johan Gottlieb Detericks and Johanna Maria van de Vyver, owners of the plantation "De Vyver" and its annexed lands (cum annexis), to a capital of 80,000 guilders "Hollands Courant." The fund, raised through 160 bonds of 500 guilders each at six per cent, runs ten years from 1 July 1789, ten per cent of capital redeemed annually by lottery before notary and witnesses. Managed from Amsterdam by Remy & Comp. as directors, the instrument shows how planters mortgaged distant colonial soil to satisfy creditors in the fatherland, channelling Caribbean produce into metropolitan credit.
creator: Remy & Comp. (Amsterdam); Albertus Bakker; Paules Cordes (Notaries)
notes: Dutch Essequibo plantation negotiatie. "De Vyver" plantation and Midland Plantations. Capital f. 13,000. Directors: Remy & Comp., Amsterdam. Notaries: Albertus Bakker, Paules Cordes, Essequibo Colony. July 1789. Dutch colonial plantation mortgage system.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original:**

Copie

Compareeren voor ons ALBERTUS BAKKER en PAULUS CORDES, Raaden in den Edelen Aghtbaren Heeren van Politie, Criminele en Civiele Justitie der Rivier en onderhorige districten van Essequebo,
De Heer JOHAN GOTTLIEB DETERICKS, en Vrouwe JOHANNA MARIA von de VYVER Echtelieden, zynde de Vrouwe Comparante ten deezen met dezelve haare Man geadmitteerd, en door hem tot redeneering en posteering dezes (speciaal gerechtigd[?]eerd;

Te kennen geevendes: dat zy Comparanten waarneemens zynde te Negotatoeren en van diverse Persoonen op te neemen, een Capitale Somma van TAG TIG DUYZEND GULDENS, HOLLANDS COURANT; ter meede voldooge van huune Crediteuers in den Vaderlanden, op Conditien en onder zodanige speciale Hypothecair Verband van de Comparanten nametmelde Plantage De VYVER als in 't vervolg zal werden gemeld; daar over by blisfive van dato 25 Augustus 1788. hadden gefchreeven, aan de Heeren REMY & COMP. Kooplieden t'Amsterdam, en dezelve met de Commisie gechargeerd, om het zelve aan hun Comparanten Crediteuers voortestellen, en unsere het moggelyck deeldens door hunne Interesses te perfeitaisueren, op het deelmeeing door deezen en dezelve Negotiatie byna volleekend was; Edog niet ziin onder de Rihere tyo die Agte van deelneming gemaeld, als:

**Articul 1.**

Dat de voorz. Negotiatie zal gefchieden onder directie van de Heeren REMY & COMP. Kooplieden t'Amsterdam, als Directeuren, en onder 't opgzegvang van de natetoemene Heeren Commissarischen'.

**2.**

Dat de voorz. Negotiatie groot en niet groozer zal mogez zyn, als TAGTIG DUYZEND GULDENS's Capitaal HOLLANDS COURANT;

**3.**

Dat de voorz. Negotiatie gfchied, voor den tyd van TIEN JAAREN, ingaande primo July 1789, op die van de na opgezegene Somma, 's Jaarlyka zal moeten werden afgeslost tien per Cento van 't Capital of AGT DUYZEND GULDENS, waar meede 's jaarlyka Sestien fluks der natetoemene te formeere Obligatien, en overplan van Noaris en genuygen uyrrelooten, zullen moeten worden afgeslost, op de respective verftchyiden, zynde primo July van vder Jaar, en motecende over zulks de certe Allosding eerste inschuiving primo Jaren af 1790, bencyens de Interessen, gereekend tegens zas per Cento in 't Jaar, eerot van 't geheele en vervolgeans van 't retgecende Capitaal;

**4.**

Dat door him Comparanten, direct na dat by hun ryding van de volleeckelung deezes Negotiatie zoude zyn bekoomea, ten behoeven van de respective Deelneemers in behoorfyke forma en orden renuntatiatie van afte hypotheken, als plaatse zoude kunnen en mogen hebben; Dezelve alle specificiid zullen moeten werden gepaasterd, behoorfyke en duuderkeike Acte van Obligatie en Hypothecq Inschrift en voorz. Plantage gemaeld. De VYVER cum annexis, gewergigd, met Intresse van de Conditien waa op de deelneming is gefchicd, en bafotte van Hipte markooning van alle deelche, of the by forme van den Hypotheeck Acte als allen ryden zal mogea worden opgezegd, en de misgaders dat by de voorz. Plantage cum annexis, als eenige geretefcyck Declaratorit, dat dezelve Acle van Hypotheecq, zoude mogea worden gevorgd, een behoorfyke, deugsdelyke en gerechts[?]ck Declaratorit, dat dezelve van Hypotheecq, is, burrijen de natetoemele, deeren Ed. Aghtbare Raaden ten behoeven van voorn. Heeren REMY & COMP; ingecertificeerd' waas van 't' y werelde noch zal worden gemeld, en datzelve met geen andere en meedere Hypothecquaire of Laslen bewaard: —

**5.**

Dat van de voorz. Adte van Obligatie en Hypothoecquarie, zullen worden uysgegeven 160 Copies authentici door een Notaris t'Amsterdam onderteekend, en met een daar onder ftaande Acte van deelgewing, welke groote VIF HONDERD GULDENS Capitaal, behoorly genaamoot van No. 1 tot No. 160, explicatie, en welke blanco in op naam, door de deelneemers refpectivvely te verkezzen, onderteekend door de Heeren REMY & COMP. als Directeuren, en ten einde daarvan geen meedere an de voorz. Houeeren Commissarischen reconnostreeren en door een Notaris t'Amsterdam geprocureerd ingeschi werden, als Directeuren onderteekend door de Heeren REMY & COMP.

**6.**

Dat ten einde de voorz. Allosding van 't Capitaal en betaaling der Interessen, door de voornoemde Heeren REMY & COMP. als Directeuren behoorlyк zal kunnen gefchieden, door hin Comparanten als Achthuseigenares, alle Producten hoe gaanaaml, die van de voorz. Plantage voorkomen, altos een inpoedigllen zullen moeten worder afgezonden, aan de voorz. Heeren REMY & COMP., om dezelve t'Amsterdam te verzoggen, en dan wytz, zoo evenegenoemde Allosding en betaaling als de onkosten van de Premien van Artillante ze zenden van de nodige provisien en benoodigheeden zoo Voor- als Najars, voor de voorz. Plantage te hanenen doen; Edog desze laastte niet o doen, dan met godesvindea en overwieg van de natetoemehe Heeren Commissarischen.

**7.**

Dat de Heeren REMY & COMP., voor hunne moeite voor provisie zullen genieten, zoo veel als in diergelyce Negotiation gebruskely en t'Amsterdam bereekend werd; —

**8.**

Dat de Heeren REMY & COMP. dxog on dat deze Negotiatie tot volkcomen land zal syn gebracht, en de Pentingen (die met de voorn. primo July 1789. in gereechheid zullen moeten zyn,) het zy in contani

Appear before us ALBERTUS BAKKER and PAULUS CORDES, Councillors in the Noble and Honorable Gentlemen of Police, Criminal and Civil Justice of the River and subordinate districts of Essequebo,
The Gentleman JOHAN GOTTLIEB DETERICKS, and the Lady JOHANNA MARIA von de VYVER, Spouses, the Lady Comparant being herein authorized with her Husband, and by him admitted to reason and posting of this (specially authorized);

To make known: that they the Comparants, intending to arrange to receive from diverse Persons, a Capital Sum of THIRTEEN THOUSAND GUILDERS, DUTCH CURRENCY; for the satisfaction of their Creditors in the Fatherland, on Conditions and under such special Mortgage Bond of the Comparants named Plantation De VYVER as in the following shall be stated; whereupon by letter of date

title: Changuion Plantation Mortgage Loan, Essequibo & Demerara, 1816
description: This printed prospectus sets out the Conditien van Negotiatie, the terms of a Dutch mortgage loan fund directed by Daniël Changuion, raising f 400,000 in guilders for planters along the Rio Essequebo and Rio Demerary (Essequibo and Demerara, in present-day Guyana) to continue and improve their estates, at 6 percent interest for a term of ten years. Issued in the Netherlands in 1816, with ten claviers and ten coupons, it belongs to the wave of colonial plantation finance that followed the Napoleonic wars, when Dutch capital again sought returns from the newly British Guianas. The articles bind each planter to mortgage his plantation and its inventory, including the enslaved people held there, valued conservatively at a fifth of assessment, and to consign produce to the directors, with foreclosure on default. Plain and untitled, the sheet's austerity is itself revealing: the security behind the paper was human bondage, stated as routine collateral.
creator: Daniel Changuion (Director)
notes: Conditien van Negotiatie. Director: Daniel Changuion. Plantation loans for Essequibo & Demerara planters. f. 400,000 at 6% p.a. for 10 years. 10 Claviers, 10 Coupons. Ca. 1816. Mortgages on plantations and inventories. Dutch colonial plantation finance, post-Napoleonic period.
transcription:
# Terms of Negotiation for a Loan Fund under Daniel Changuion for Planters in Essequibo & Demerara (1816) — Transcription

## Original:

*(Handwritten note, upper right:)* 1816. Bet: 10 Cl: en uitgegeven 10 Coupons.

**Conditien van Negotiatie, tot een Fonds waar uit, onder Directie van DANIEL CHANGUION, aan eenige Planters in *Rio Essequebo* en *Rio Demmerary*, tot voortzetting en verbeetering hunner Plantagien een Somma van *f* 400000:- voor 10 Jaaren zal werden gefourneert, teegens den Intrest van 6 pC.to 's Jaars.**

**Art. 1.**

De Planters in opgemelde Colonien, welke geneegen zyn eenige Gelden ter verbeeteringe en voortzettinge hunner Plantagien op te neemen, en daar toe door den Directeur zyn geaccepteert, zullen gehouden zyn ten hunnen kosten, gemelde Plantagien door be-ëedigde Priseurs behoorlyk te laten Inventariseeren, zoo de Gronden, Gebouwen, Werken, Slaven en Slavinnen, en generaallyk wat verder daar toe behoord, met uitsluiting van Meubelen en andere Zaken, onnodig tot het bewerken van een Plantagie, en den vollen en onbelasten Eygendom van dien Inventaris door hun beëedigd, door gemelde Taxateurs doen in waarde brengen.

**2.**

Den Inventaris als hier voren bepaald, opgemaakt en gepriseert weesende, zal aan *Daniel Changuion*, moeten werden gezonden, beneevens de behoorlyke Hypotheecq of Verbandbrief, waar uyt zal moeten blyken dat deselve Hypotheecq, ter Secretary van de Colonie behoorlyk geregistreert is, en dat geen andere Speciale of Generale verbanden op deselve Effecten zyn leggende. als meede dat geen verbanden zullen werden geadmitteert of geregistreert, buyten consent van de Geldschieters in deesen.

**3.**

Aan de Planters welke den Inventaris en Verbandbrief als boven zullen overmaken, zal worden geschoten de helft of uyterlyk vyf agtste der waarde waar op derzelver Plantagien, met hun toebehooren, zyn getaxeerd, teegens den Intrest van Ses pCento in 't Jaar, die zy zig verbinden Jaarlyks boven alle andere Lasten te voldoen, op Pœne in de Verbandbrief vermeld.

**4.**

De Geld-opneemers, welke Jaarlyks met Valable bewyzen kunnen aantoonen, dat hunne bezittingen daar door zyn verbeetert, zullen vryheyd hebben, op een nieuwe Prisatie als boven gemeld te doen, van het meerder bedragen der waarde, nog op te neemen de helft van die verbeetering, mits dezelve aan voorn: *Daniel Changuion* meede Verhypothequeerende.

**5.**

De Respective Planters Geld-opneemers, verbinden zig alle hunnen te zenden Producten te addresseeren aan, nodige Provisien te ontbieden van, en de Wisselbrieven te trekken op *Daniel Changuion*, als meede by afzending hunner Producten, aan hem in tyds behoorlyke opgave ter bezorging der nodige assurantie te geeven, met overgifte dat in gevalle zy daar aan niet mogten voldoen of ook hunne Producten in de respective Colonien kwamen te Verkoopen, of op andere wyzen te diverteeren, en in gebreeken bleeven Jaarlyks hunne Intrest te voldoen, het by hun genotene Capitaal met de Intressen directelyk zal konnen worden opgeeyscht, en geheellyk ten kosten van zodanige Planters, by Executie verhaald, aan hunne Persoonen, Plantagien met al derzelver toebehooren, en verdere zoo roerende als onroerende Goederen, zig hier toe voor den Hove van Justitie in de Colonie, of waar het anders zoude mogen vereyscht werden, by vrywillige Condemnatie verbindende.

**6.**

De Geldgeevers behouden aan zig de vryheyd, zodanige en zoo veele Persoonen in de Rivieren van *Essequebo* en *Demerary* aan te stellen en te qualificeeren, als zy zul-

*(catchword at foot:)* zul-   *(sheet signature:)* A

---

## English Translation:

*(Handwritten note, upper right:)* 1816. Paid: 10 Cl[?]: and issued 10 Coupons.

**Terms of Negotiation for a Fund from which, under the Direction of DANIEL CHANGUION, a Sum of f 400,000:- shall be furnished for 10 Years to certain Planters in Rio Essequibo and Rio Demerary, for the continuation and improvement of their Plantations, against Interest of 6 per Cent per Year.**

**Article 1.**

The Planters in the aforesaid Colonies who are inclined to take up some Monies for the improvement and continuation of their Plantations, and who to that end have been accepted by the Director, shall be obliged, at their own cost, to have the said Plantations duly inventoried by sworn Appraisers — namely the Lands, Buildings, Works, Male and Female Slaves, and generally whatever else belongs thereto, excluding Furniture and other Things not necessary for the working of a Plantation — and to have the full and unencumbered Ownership of that Inventory, sworn to by them, brought to valuation by the said Assessors.

**2.**

The Inventory, as determined above, having been drawn up and appraised, must be sent to Daniel Changuion, together with the proper Mortgage or Bond deed, from which it must appear that the said Mortgage has been duly registered at the Secretariat of the Colony, and that no other Special or General liens rest upon the same Assets; likewise that no liens shall be admitted or registered without the consent of the Lenders in this matter.

**3.**

To the Planters who transmit the Inventory and Bond deed as above, there shall be advanced one half, or at the most five eighths, of the value at which their Plantations, together with their appurtenances, are assessed, against Interest of Six per Cent per Year, which they bind themselves to pay Yearly above all other Charges, under the Penalty stated in the Bond deed.

**4.**

The Borrowers who can each Year prove by valid evidence that their possessions have thereby been improved, shall have the liberty, upon a new Appraisal to be made as above, to take up an additional half of the increased amount of the value (the improvement), provided they likewise mortgage the same to the aforesaid Daniel Changuion.

**5.**

The respective Planter-Borrowers bind themselves to address all their Produce to be shipped to, to order the necessary Provisions from, and to draw their Bills of Exchange upon, Daniel Changuion; likewise, upon the shipment of their Produce, to give him timely and proper notice for procuring the necessary insurance. It being understood that should they fail to comply with this, or should their Produce come to be Sold in the respective Colonies, or diverted in any other manner, and should they remain in default of paying their Interest Yearly, the Capital received by them, together with the Interest, may be demanded back directly, and wholly at the cost of such Planters be recovered by Execution against their Persons, Plantations with all their appurtenances, and further both movable and immovable Goods — binding themselves hereto before the Court of Justice in the Colony, or wherever else it might be required, by voluntary Condemnation.

**6.**

The Lenders reserve to themselves the liberty to appoint and qualify such and so many Persons in the Rivers of Essequibo and Demerary as they shall [wish...]

*(text breaks off at foot of page)*

title: Russian Imperial 6% Perpetual Obligation (Fourth Loan), payable at Amsterdam
description: A perpetual obligation of the Russian imperial government, headed «Безсрочный Актъ» (perpetual act) and «БЕЗСРОЧНЫЙ» in large type, acknowledging a principal of one thousand rubles («Тысячу Рублей») inscribed in the State Debt Book and bearing interest «по шести на Сто въ Годъ» (six per hundred per year), paid as thirty rubles each half-year. The text assigns it to the Fourth Loan («Четвертаго Займа») and names Amsterdam («въ Амстердамъ») as the place of payment. Issued in the mid-1850s, it embodies the empire's reliance on the Amsterdam capital market—the channel of Hope & Co.—to fund obligations carried in perpetuity rather than redeemed. The engraved imperial eagle and fine guilloche assert sovereign credit, while the dense overlay of dated semi-annual coupon stamps and Dutch revenue and succession marks (Nederlandsch Plakzegel; Ontvanger der Successie te Amsterdam), running into the early twentieth century, records two generations of Dutch holders drawing income from a single enduring loan.
creator: Russian Imperial Government
notes: "БЕЗСРОЧНЫЙ" = perpetual; multiple stamp cancellations indicating payment history across 1860s–1870s; perpetual government bond
transcription:
# Russian Imperial 6% Perpetual Obligation (Fourth Loan), payable at Amsterdam — Transcription

## Original:

(The document is a large engraved certificate with the Russian imperial double-headed eagle at the top centre, surrounded by fine guilloche work. Almost the entire surface is covered by an overlay of later stamps — see the note on stamps below. The core printed and manuscript text runs down the centre-right of the sheet.)

**БЕЗСРОЧНЫЙ**

(cursive, beneath the large printed heading:)
[Безсроч]ный Актъ

Тысячу Рублей,

[вне]сенную [въ Государ]ственную Долговую книгу [лит.] А [...]

стр. 643. № 36225. Четвертаго Займа

[въ] Амстердамъ.

[...] года по шести на Сто въ Годъ,

[что за] полгода Тридцать рублей.

Управляющій Госу[дарственнымъ ...] [illegible]

[Амо]ртизаціоннымъ [...] [illegible]

[...]родъ лит. Г. стр. 643. Года 18[__]. № 20

(signature, cursive:) Директоръ Штиглицъ

---

**Overlaid stamps (structural note — not part of the original engraved text):**

- Dozens of round black cancellation/coupon stamps carrying half-year dates from the 1850s through the 1890s (e.g. 1858, 1861, 1863, 1864, 1865, 1870, 1873, 1874, 1875, 1877, 1880, 1881, 1882, 1884, 1885, 1892, 1893, 1894), each marked "1 Sem" or "2 Sem" (i.e. 1er/2e Semestre). One fully legible example reads "2 Sem / 1894". (repeated across the whole sheet for successive coupon periods; individual worn values not separately transcribed.)

- Purple/blue rectangular boxed coupon stamps down the right margin, numbered and dated by semester, e.g. "N° 22 / 1 Sem", "N° 24 / 2 Sem 1897", "N° 25 / 2 Sem 1895", "N° 19 / 1 Sem 1896", "N° 1898", "N° 1899", "Semester 1899", "1 Sem 1910", "2 Sem 1910", "1 Sem 1911", "2 Sem 1912", "1 Sem 1913", "1 SEM 14", "2 SEM 15". (repeated for many semesters.)

- Purple/blue coupon-payment stamps in French across the heading: "1 Sem. 1900", "2 Sem. 1900", "2 Sem. 1901", "1 Sem. 1903", "2 Sem. 1904", "2 Sem. 1905", "1 Sem. 1906", "2 Sem. 1906", "1 Sem. 1907".

- Blue Dutch succession-duty stamp (left side, boxed): "Ontvanger [der] Succ[essie]rechten No. 1 / [T]E AMSTERDAM / [...] betaald / EEN GULDEN". A second blue "Ontvanger Suc[cessie] ... T.T. AMS[TERDAM]" mark appears at top right.

- Red stamp, top right: "[illegible] Гульд[еновъ] [...] 1912" (partly legible).

- Various additional purple/red circular and boxed marks, illegible.

---

## English Translation:

(Engraved certificate bearing the Russian imperial double-headed eagle and fine guilloche ornament, its surface almost entirely covered by later payment stamps.)

**PERPETUAL**

Perpetual Act

One Thousand Rubles,

inscribed in the State Debt Book [letter] A [...]

page 643. No. 36225. Of the Fourth Loan

[payable] at Amsterdam.

[...] per year at six per hundred per year,

[which for] a half-year [is] Thirty rubles.

Director of the State [...] [illegible]

[...] of the Amortization [...] [illegible]

[...] letter G, page 643, Year 18[__], No. 20

(signature:) Director Stieglitz

---

**Overlaid stamps (not part of the original text):**

- Dozens of round black coupon-cancellation stamps with half-year ("1st Sem." / "2nd Sem.") dates from the 1850s to the 1890s (e.g. "2nd Sem. 1894"), repeated for successive coupon periods.

- Purple/blue boxed coupon stamps numbered by semester in the right margin (e.g. "No. 24 / 2nd Sem. 1897"; "Semester 1899"; "1st Sem. 1910"; "2nd Sem. 1912"; "1st Sem. 1913"; "1st Sem. 1914"; "2nd Sem. 1915"), repeated for many semesters.

- Purple/blue French coupon-payment stamps across the heading ("1st Sem. 1900" through "1st Sem. 1907").

- Blue Dutch succession-duty stamp: "Receiver of Succession Duties No. 1 / at Amsterdam / [...] paid / ONE GUILDER"; a second "Receiver of Succession ... Amsterdam" mark at top right.

- Red stamp at top right: "[illegible] guilders [...] 1912" (partly legible).

- Additional illegible circular and boxed marks.

(Note: the certificate's serial number is read as No. 36225, but its first digit is partly obscured by a red stamp; the registration year on the final line, "Года 18—," is illegible. These are consistent with the catalogue dating of 1855.)

title: Pekin Syndicate Limited Bearer Share Warrant
description: A bilingual English/French share warrant to bearer issued by the Pekin Syndicate Limited of London, certifying that the holder owns five ordinary shares of £1 each. Incorporated under the Companies Acts 1862 to 1893, the syndicate was formed to work mining and railway concessions in Shansi (Shanxi) province in northern China, and the shares are accordingly styled "Shansi Shares." Printed in green on cream within a finely engraved border, the certificate centres on an engraved vignette of a Chinese walled town set in a river landscape, with the capital then standing at £1,540,000. As a warrant to bearer, ownership passed by delivery and dividends were claimed by detaching the numbered coupons attached below.

pages.description: Recto: the engraved certificate face, headed "Pekin Syndicate Limited," with a central vignette of a Chinese walled town by a river, the £1,540,000 capital, and parallel English and French texts certifying five Shansi shares. Dated November 1919 under the syndicate's seal, signed by directors and secretary, with a 1923 French foreign-securities tax stamp. Below, the coupon sheet opens with a TALON for fresh coupons and dividend coupons numbered down to 1. Page 1 of 2.

Verso: a bilingual English/French notice within an engraved frame stating that the warrant is issued subject to the conditions governing bearer shares as fixed from time to time by the board of directors. The remainder is the continuation of the dividend coupon sheet, the coupons printed in reverse. Page 2 of 2.

creator: Pekin Syndicate Limited
notes: British concession company in northern China (Shanxi); bilingual English/French bearer certificate; No. 835003; coupon sheet coupons 1–33 attached

# Pekin Syndicate Limited — Share Warrant to Bearer for 5 Shansi Shares — Transcription

## Original:

*(Ornate engraved certificate, printed in black on green guilloche border. Bilingual English / French. Large vignette of a Chinese treaty-port harbour scene at centre. "5 / SHANSI SHARES" printed in each of the four corners.)*

**WARRANT Nº**
B35603

**NUMERO DU TITRE**
B35603

*(Central circular seal, top:)* **INCORPORATED · 17TH MARCH 1897** *(with a Chinese character 福 at centre)*

*(Red/orange embossed revenue stamp, upper left:)* ONE SHILLING AND SIXPENCE

**Pekin Syndicate, Limited.**

INCORPORATED UNDER THE COMPANIES ACTS 1862 TO 1897

**CAPITAL £1,540,000**

IN 1,500,000 SHANSI SHARES OF £1 EACH, 399,000 ORDINARY SHARES OF 2/- EACH AND 2,000 DEFERRED SHARES OF 1/6 EACH.

*(Circular device, right side:)* 2% / TITRES ÉTRANGERS / (16) PARIS (10) / 1923

*(Boxed fine-print rights paragraph:)*

THE HOLDERS OF THE SHANSI SHARES are entitled to 10 per cent of the profits of the Syndicate of each year until the sum paid in respect of such percentage shall amount to £1 per Share on the issued Shansi Shares, and to half the surplus profits of each year after providing for the payment of the said percentage whilst it continues payable and afterwards to half the surplus profits of the Syndicate in each year and in the event of a winding-up to 50 per cent of the surplus assets which shall remain after paying off the whole of the Syndicate's paid up Capital.

THE HOLDERS OF THE ORDINARY SHARES are entitled subject to the rights as to dividends to the Shansi Shares to the whole of the remaining profits of the Syndicate until the sums paid in respect of dividends shall amount to 2/ per share and afterwards after distributing the profits applicable to the Shansi Shares to 50 per cent of the surplus of such profits and in the event of a winding-up subject to the rights of the holders of the Shansi Shares to 50 per cent of the balance of the assets after the return of the whole of the paid up Capital of the Syndicate.

THE HOLDERS OF THE DEFERRED SHARES are entitled subject to the rights attached to the Shansi Shares and Ordinary Shares to 40 per cent of the surplus profits of the Syndicate and in the event of a winding-up subject to the rights of the holders of the Shansi Shares, to 50 per cent of the balance of the Assets remaining after the return of the whole paid-up capital of the Syndicate.

**SHARE WARRANT TO BEARER** | **TITRE AU PORTEUR**
FOR | DE
SHANSI **5** SHARES. | ACTIONS **5** SHANSI.

Nºˢ 1143187 to 1143191

*(signature)* Chas Price

*(Watermark faintly visible across body:)* PEKIN SYNDICATE LIMITED

**Pekin Syndicate Limited.**
**TALON** FOR FRESH SUPPLY OF COUPONS FOR SHARE WARRANT TO BEARER
The Bearer of the above Warrant will receive in Exchange For this Talon a fresh supply of Coupons when those below have all fallen due.
SHARE WARRANT Nº B35603 REPRESENTING 5 SHANSI SHARES

**Bearer Certificate — for 5 Shansi Shares**

The Bearer of the present Certificate is the Proprietor of FIVE Shansi Shares, each fully paid up to £1 Sterling, of the PEKIN SYNDICATE LIMITED, bearing the numbers indicated above and subject to the conditions stipulated in the Statutes and other Regulations of the Syndicate.

title: Vlaardingen Orphanage Negotiatie (Lottery-Loan), Batavian Republic, 9 May 1800
description: This printed broadside sets out the conditions of a negotiatie (a public financing scheme) of f. 75,000 in Dutch guilders, authorized by decree of the Vertegenwoordigend Lichaam des Bataafschen Volks (the Representative Body of the Batavian People) and raised for the upkeep of the orphanage (Weeshuys) of the town of Vlaardingen, dated 9 May 1800. The loan is divided into 1,500 shares of f. 50, drawn by lot. A prize table lists 500 prizes totalling f. 48,000 (the top prizes f. 10,000 and f. 5,000); with premiums these 514 winning lots, worth f. 50,000, are paid in obligations bearing three per cent interest, while a further 1,000 lots of f. 50, also f. 50,000, are paid as life annuities graded across five age classes from f. 300 to f. 500. Headed "Dankbaarheid en Erkentenis," it fuses lottery, bond, and annuity in service of charity, a characteristic fiscal improvisation of the early Batavian Republic.
creator: Vertegenwoordigend Lichaam des Bataafsen Volks
notes: Lottery-style charitable negotiatie for Vlaardingen orphanage; f.75,000 total; five age-class life annuity structure; prize structure listed; see also related obligation certificate

title: Portuguese External Debt Certificate
description: This is a certificate of claim ("vordering") issued by the Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel te Amsterdam, the Amsterdam securities-dealers' association, against the Portuguese State. It records a holder's claim to the two-thirds portion of interest withheld on the 3 percent Portuguese Foreign Debt of 1853–1884, the coupon falling due 1 July 1892, and was issued in concert with the Nederlandsch Beschermings-Comité voor Portugeesche Fondsen, the Dutch committee formed to defend bondholders' interests. Dated Amsterdam, 30 September 1892, it documents the Portuguese default of that year, when Lisbon unilaterally reduced foreign-debt interest by two-thirds, and the organised response of Dutch investors who held large quantities of these securities. The sober engraved frame, free of allegory and dense with legal text, reflects the instrument's purpose: not to entice subscribers but to register and protect a creditor's residual rights against a sovereign in distress.
creator: Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel, Amsterdam
notes: Portuguese 3% foreign debt 1853–1884; arrears interest certificate; issued by Amsterdam Securities Exchange; Dutch-Portuguese debt arbitration

VORDERING op den Portugeeschen Staat,
wegens onbetaald gebleven twee derde deel der renten van 3 pCts.
Portugeessche Schuld, vervallen 1 Juli 1892.

Het Bestuur der Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel te Amsterdam certificeert, dat de inleveraar van de op Lijst No. [.....], ingevolge aankondinging van het Bestuur dd. 13 September 1892, ingeleverde Coupons van Obligatiën der 3 pCts. Portugeessche Staatsschuld zijne rechten handhaft op de voldoening van het onbetaald gebleven twee derde deel dier Coupons, ten bedrage van £ .....
en de handhaving dezer rechten opdraagt aan het Nederlandsch Beschermings-Comité voor Portugeessche Fondsen te Amsterdam, of aan zoodanig Comité of zoodanige instelling als, bij ontstentenis van eerstgenoemd Comité, door de Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel met de handhaving dier rechten mocht worden belast.

De uitkeering van een evenredig deel van hetgeen eventueel wegens deze rechten aan de inleveraars mocht te goed komen, zal geschieden aan den houder van dit Certificaat.

HET BESTUUR DER
Vereeniging voor den Effectenhandel te Amsterdam,

AMSTERDAM, 30 September, 1892.

[Signatures]
P. Straderty, Voorzitter.
[illegible], Secretaris.

## English Translation:

3% FOREIGN DEBT OF PORTUGAL,
1853—1884.

Certificate No. 1402

CLAIM against the Portuguese State,
concerning unpaid two-thirds of the interest on 3% Portuguese Debt, due July 1, 1892.

The Board of the Association for Securities Trading in Amsterdam certifies that the depositor of the Coupons from Bonds of the 3% Portuguese State Debt, delivered on the list No. [.....], in accordance with the announcement of the Board dated September 13, 1892, maintains his rights to payment of the unpaid two-thirds portion of those Coupons, amounting to £ .....
and entrusts the enforcement of these rights to the Dutch Protection Committee for Portuguese Funds in Amsterdam, or to such Committee or such institution as, in the absence of the aforementioned Committee, may be entrusted by the Association for Securities Trading with the enforcement of these rights.

The payment of a proportional share of what may eventually be due regarding these rights to the depositors shall be made to the holder of this Certificate.

THE BOARD OF
the Association for Securities Trading in Amsterdam,

AMSTERDAM, September 30, 1892.

[Signatures]
P. Straderty, Chairman.
[illegible], Secretary.

title: New Russia Company Debenture
description: A six per cent first mortgage debenture of the New Russia Company Limited, a London-registered concern operating ironworks and collieries on its estates in the Government of Ekaterinoslav, southern Russia (now Ukraine). Issued for £100 within a total charge of £800,000 and dated 6 April 1910, the bond promises repayment of principal on 1 October 1933, with half-yearly interest at six per cent paid on 1 April and 1 October. The debt is secured under a Trust Deed of 25 March 1910 upon roughly 5,000 acres comprising the Company's principal works. The deed names trustees Alfred Lyttelton, K.C., M.P., and Charles Colin Macrae. Engraved in blue on cream within an elaborate ornamental border, the certificate marks British capital sustaining heavy industry in the Russian Empire.

pages.description: The debenture certificate proper. An ornate engraved heading announces the New Russia Company Limited with capital of £1,200,000 ordinary stock and £800,000 in six per cent first mortgage debentures of £100 each. The cursive body sets out the £100 principal, the 6% terms, the secured estates in Ekaterinoslav, and the Trust Deed, closing with a sealing clause dated 6 April 1910 and a director's signature. Page 1 of 3.

Reverse documentation. At left, a stub repeating the company name and the terms (interest payable 1 April and 1 October at 6%). The centre holds a dense Table of Amortization listing redemption dates and amounts year by year. At right, the printed Conditions of the debenture and, below, a blank ruled Memorandum for registering the debenture as a registered security. Page 2 of 3.

A full sheet of interest coupons arranged in rows and columns, each marked £100 and bearing the certificate number, to be detached and presented for successive half-yearly six per cent payments. The lower right corner of the sheet is blank where coupons have been trimmed away. Page 3 of 3.

creator: New Russia Company Limited
notes: British company; £800,000 total issue; No. 277; 6% first mortgage debenture; land in Ekaterinoslav, southern Russia; repayable 1933

# New Russia Company Limited - 6% First Mortgage Debenture No. 277

## Transcribed Text:

**NEW RUSSIA COMPANY LIMITED**

INCORPORATED UNDER THE COMPANIES ACT, 1862.

CAPITAL ORDINARY STOCK, $1,200,000.

ISSUE OF
£600,000

SIX PERCENT FIRST MORTGAGE DEBENTURES
OF £100 EACH.

NO 277                                                                                                                £100.

NUMBERED FROM 1 TO 6,000 (BOTH INCLUSIVE).

[Extended legal text regarding terms and conditions of the debenture, including details about interest payments, principal repayment, and security provisions - text is dense and partially difficult to read in places]

**SIX PERCENT FIRST MORTGAGE DEBENTURE.**

NEW RUSSIA COMPANY, LIMITED [Hereafter called the Company]
will on the 1st day of October aforesaid and on similar date in the principal amount therein mentioned (if punctually paid) in accordance with the conditions endorsed hereon, pay to the bearer of this Debenture unless then registered and in such case to the registered holder hereof the sum of £100 sterling in London and the Company will pay interest thereon at the rate of 6 per cent per annum, by equal half yearly payments on the 2nd April and the 1st day of October in each year in accordance with the coupons annexed hereto unless forfeited by non-presentation and surrender of such Debenture.

By virtue of the Trust Deed mentioned below these Debentures are specially secured upon a portion of the company's property consisting of estates in the Government of Catherinaslav, Russia; on part of which property are situated the principal Collieries and works of the Company. The said estates comprise an area of about 1,300 acres and are more particularly described in the Deed of Mortgage and are issued upon and subject to the conditions endorsed hereon and to those contained in the Deed of Trust dated the 22nd day of March, 1901, and made between the Company of the one part and Mr. Right Honourable Alfred Lyttleton, M.A.; M. P., and Charles Cohn, Marine Eng'r, by the other part, and on the condition that the bearer or registered holder hereof has the same may do, stand thereby.

Given under the Common Seal of the Company
this sixth day of April 1901.

[Signatures for Director and Scrip Secretary appear to be present but are not clearly legible]

title: New Russia Company 6% First Mortgage Debenture Coupon Sheet
description: An engraved coupon sheet, printed in blue on a single leaf and arranged in three columns of small oval cartouches, each enclosing only a sequential number; the coupons run from 22 through 70, covering the later interest periods of a long-dated security. According to catalogue research the sheet accompanies the New Russia Company Limited 6% First Mortgage Debenture, a £100 sterling obligation of the British-registered firm that operated ironworks and collieries in the Donets region of southern Russia, issued around 1901. Each oval is framed by finely cut acanthus scrollwork and a beaded border, the restrained, repetitive engraving typical of late-Victorian security printing, where ornament served less as decoration than as a deterrent to forgery. The sheet's anonymity, bearing numbers alone, reflects how the value lay not in the slip but in its detachment and presentation for payment.
creator: New Russia Company Limited
notes: Coupon sheet coupons 22–70; later-series coupons for debenture No. 277; blue printed on white
