title: Société Selim et Samaan Sednaoui Registered Shares
description: Registered share certificate of the Société Selim et Samaan Sednaoui, a société anonyme of the République Arabe Unie (United Arab Republic) seated in Cairo (R.C.N 377), the firm behind one of the city's foremost department stores, shown here established in 1907. Printed in red and terracotta with an ornate Arabic display title above a French sub-heading, the certificate represents twenty-five nominative shares of ten Egyptian pounds each (LE 250) within a stated capital of LE 620,000 divided into 12,000 shares. Bilingual Arabic and French text, hand-completed and stamped, attests ownership; a sheet of detachable dividend coupons and a share-transfer register accompany it. Issued in Cairo in 1958, shortly before the company's nationalisation.

pages.description: The certificate face: an ornamental red border frames the Arabic calligraphic company name over the French heading "Société Selim et Samaan Sednaoui, Société Anonyme R.A.U., R.C.N 377 - Le Caire," with founding year 1907. Arabic text states capital of LE 620,000 and grants 25 nominative shares at LE 10 each; manuscript entries, two signatures, and an oval blue stamp. A full sheet of twenty numbered dividend coupons runs across the lower half. Page 1 of 2.

Verso headed "انتقال ملكية الأسهم" (transfer of share ownership): a ruled table with columns for date, name, and signatures of both parties on each side, recording successive transfers. Only one handwritten entry appears at the top; the remaining rows are blank. A faint grid for further coupons or stamps occupies the lower portion. Page 2 of 2.

creator: Société Selim et Samaan Sednaoui S.A.
notes: Famous Cairo department store founded 1886; United Arab Republic era (1958–1961); 25 registered shares at LE.10 each; later nationalized

title: Mexico Consolidated Debt Bond and Ethiopian Railway Share
description: Two unrelated bearer securities filed under one record. The first is a Mexican government bond, the "Deuda Nacional Consolidada al Tres por Ciento," a 5,000-peso certificate of the consolidated national debt bearing the Mexican eagle-and-serpent seal within a dense engraved border, accompanied by its decree text and a sheet of dated interest coupons. The second is an Imperial Ethiopian railway share, an "Action de Cinq Cents Francs au Porteur" of the Compagnie Impériale des Chemins de Fer Éthiopiens, headquartered in Paris, with a romantic vignette of an Ethiopian caravan, bilingual French and Amharic lettering, and its own coupon sheet. The pairing reflects a cataloguing grouping rather than a single instrument.

pages.description: Engraved Mexican bond certificate, "Deuda Nacional Consolidada al Tres por Ciento," "Bono de 5,000 Ps.," with the eagle-and-serpent seal in a vignette, the dateline "Mexico... 1.° de Enero de 1851," lettered Letra F, and a violet handstamp. A second value tablet sits at left. Page 1 of 4.

Imperial Ethiopian railway share, "Chemins de Fer Éthiopiens, Compagnie Impériale, Action de Cinq Cents Francs au Porteur," printed in red-brown with Amharic and French text, a vignette of an Ethiopian caravan with palms, and signatures; capital of 8,000,000 francs noted. Page 2 of 4.

Mexican bond text page: "Memoria Deuda consolidada del Erario federal de la República Mexicana," headed "Secretaría de Estado y del Despacho de Hacienda," with closely set decree articles, two signatures at foot, the value tab "Letra F. Valor $5,000" at left, and a tall column of dated interest coupons at right. Page 3 of 4.

Full coupon sheet for the Ethiopian railway share, forty-eight numbered detachable coupons in a red-brown grid, each lettered "Chemins de Fer Éthiopiens, Compagnie Impériale" with the share number and coupon ordinal. Page 4 of 4.

title: Compagnie Impériale Chemins de Fer Ethiopiens Share
description: This is the reverse of a 500-franc bearer share (action de cinq cents francs au porteur) in the Compagnie Impériale des Chemins de Fer Éthiopiens, the French-backed concession chartered to build the railway from the Gulf port of Djibouti toward Addis Ababa. The face, visible here only as mirror-image bleed-through, carries the title in French and Amharic above a Lion of Judah — the emblem of the Ethiopian crown whose imperial grant underwrote the line. Two violet handstamps record not dividends but the gradual return of capital: a first distribution of 5 francs on the reimbursement of the shares, voted by the general assembly of 30 May 1915, and a second of 5 francs voted on 1 June 1921. Together they trace the slow winding-down of an undercapitalised colonial venture whose financial troubles forced repeated reorganisation, repaying its bearers in small instalments.
creator: Compagnie Impériale Chemins de Fer Ethiopiens
notes: Reverse of share No. T 01,927; first dividend distribution stamp dated 30 May 1908

title: Compagnie Impériale Chemins de Fer Ethiopiens Coupon Sheet
description: This is the detached dividend-coupon sheet that accompanied a share of the Compagnie Impériale des Chemins de Fer Éthiopiens, the French-chartered company formed to build and operate a railway from the Gulf of Tadjoura toward the interior of the Ethiopian Empire. The sheet carries forty-eight numbered coupons arranged in a four-column grid, each headed "COUPON N°" and an "EXERCICE" (financial year) running consecutively from 1900 through 1947, to be clipped and presented as each annual dividend fell due. Printed in plain black on white in austere typographic form, without vignette or allegory, the sheet speaks less of imperial spectacle than of the workaday mechanics of bearer finance. As an instrument it shows how Parisian capital, drawn to a concession on the Red Sea frontier, was packaged for the ordinary investor: a single share promising a near half-century stream of coupons, the railway's projected profits sliced into detachable yearly claims.
creator: Compagnie Impériale Chemins de Fer Ethiopiens
notes: Annual dividend coupon sheet; exercices 1900–1947; 48 coupons; likely separated from a share certificate

title: Chinese Republic 8% Railway Equipment Loan Treasury Note
description: Treasury note of the Government of the Chinese Republic for £20, equivalent to 1,200 Belgian francs, drawn on the 8% Railway Equipment Loan of 1922, First Series of £8,000,000 (40,000 notes at £20, or 48,000,000 Belgian francs). The title appears in parallel French and English across a finely engraved blue border, with an allegorical vignette of a steam locomotive crowned by an eagle at the centre. Issued to fund rolling stock and equipment for Chinese railways and secured on a stated special guarantee, the note bears bilingual text alongside Chinese characters, two vermilion Chinese seal impressions, and the signatures of authorising republic officials.

pages.description: Engraved certificate face in blue. Bilingual French/English headings name the Government of the Chinese Republic and the 8% Railway Equipment Loan of 1922, First Series of £8,000,000; locomotive-and-eagle vignette at centre. Below, the £20 / 1,260 Belgian francs denomination, dense terms in two columns, two red Chinese seals, and three signatures. Page 1 of 3.

Sheet of attached interest coupons printed in blue, arranged in a grid. Each small rectangular coupon repeats the loan title and carries a redemption date and the franc interest amount payable on the £20 note, to be detached as successive payments fall due. Page 2 of 3.

A further sheet of blue interest coupons in the same grid format, continuing the series of later-dated payments. The uppermost row carries an authentication stamp; each coupon states its due date and the franc sum payable against the note. Page 3 of 3.

transcription:
# Government of the Chinese Republic — 8% Railway Equipment Loan of 1922, £20 Treasury Note — Transcription

## Original:

(Blue engraved bond certificate; text set in two parallel columns — French on the left, English on the right. Blue ornamental border with an allegorical central vignette of a steam locomotive. Corner denomination cartouches at upper left and upper right read **£20 / OU / Frs 1200**.)

**GOUVERNEMENT DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE**
**GOVERNMENT OF THE CHINESE REPUBLIC**

*(Left column — French heading:)*
**EMPRUNT 8% DE 1922**
POUR LA FOURNITURE DE — MATÉRIEL DE CHEMIN DE FER
**(AVEC GARANTIES SPÉCIALES)**
**PREMIÈRE SÉRIE DE £800,000.**
**OU FRANCS BELGES 48,000,000.**
EN 40,000 BONS DU TRÉSOR DE £20.- OU FRANCS BELGES 1,200.

*(Right column — English heading:)*
**RAILWAY 8% EQUIPMENT LOAN OF 1922.**
**WITH SPECIAL GUARANTEES.**
**FIRST SERIES OF £800,000.**
**OR BELGIAN FRANCS 48,000,000.**
IN 40,000 TREASURY NOTES OF £20.- OR BELGIAN FRANCS 1,200.

*(Intro paragraph, French, left:)*
CET EMPRUNT DU GOUVERNEMENT DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE 8% DE 1922 (AN. XI DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE) EST ÉMIS PAR LES SOINS DE LA SOCIÉTÉ BELGE D'ENTREPRISES EN CHINE, À BRUXELLES, EN VERTU D'UN CONTRAT INTERVENU EN DATE DU 2 OCTOBRE 1922 ENTRE LE GOUVERNEMENT DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE ET LA SOCIÉTÉ BELGE D'ENTREPRISES EN CHINE.
CE CONTRAT PORTE LE SCEAU ET LA SIGNATURE DU MINISTRE DES COMMUNICATIONS ET DU MINISTRE DES FINANCES DÛMENT AUTORISÉS CONFORMÉMENT À UNE DÉCISION DU CONSEIL DES MINISTRES SANCTIONNÉE PAR LE PRÉSIDENT DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE EN DATE DU 14 SEPTEMBRE 1922, ET A ÉTÉ NOTIFIÉ PAR LE MINISTRE DES AFFAIRES ÉTRANGÈRES DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE AU MINISTRE DE BELGIQUE À PÉKIN LE 24 OCTOBRE 1922.
LE PAIEMENT DES INTÉRÊTS ET LE REMBOURSEMENT DU CAPITAL DE L'EMPRUNT CONSTITUENT UNE OBLIGATION FORMELLE ET SANS RÉSERVES DU GOUVERNEMENT CHINOIS. ILS SONT, EN OUTRE, GARANTIS PAR :
1° UNE HYPOTHÈQUE DE PREMIER RANG SUR LA SECTION À CONSTRUIRE DE PAOTU À NINGHSIA DU CHEMIN DE FER PÉKIN-SUIYUAN.
2° UNE HYPOTHÈQUE DE SECOND RANG SUR LA SECTION DE PÉKIN À PAOTU DU MÊME CHEMIN DE FER.
CES DEUX HYPOTHÈQUES S'ENTENDENT TANT SUR LA LIGNE ELLE-MÊME QUE SUR SON MATÉRIEL FIXE ET ROULANT, SES DÉPENDANCES ET SES PRODUITS.
3° L'EXCÉDENT DISPONIBLE DES RECETTES DU CHEMIN DE FER PÉKIN-HANKOW, CE PRIVILÈGE PRENANT RANG AVANT TOUTE AUTRE CHARGE QUE CE CHEMIN DE FER POURRAIT ASSUMER APRÈS LE 20 SEPTEMBRE 1922.
UNE PROVISION SUFFISANTE SERA CONSIGNÉE ENTRE LES MAINS DE LA SOCIÉTÉ BELGE D'ENTREPRISES EN CHINE POUR ASSURER LE SERVICE DES INTÉRÊTS À PAYER SUR LES BONS JUSQUES ET Y COMPRIS L'ÉCHÉANCE DU 1ER DÉCEMBRE 1924.

*(Intro paragraph, English, right:)*
THIS 8% LOAN OF THE GOVERNMENT OF THE CHINESE REPUBLIC OF 1922 (XIth YEAR OF THE CHINESE REPUBLIC), IS ISSUED BY THE SOCIÉTÉ BELGE D'ENTREPRISES EN CHINE, IN BRUSSELS, BY VIRTUE OF AN AGREEMENT ENTERED INTO ON OCTOBER 2nd 1922 BETWEEN THE GOVERNMENT OF THE CHINESE REPUBLIC OF THE ONE PART AND THE SOCIÉTÉ BELGE D'ENTREPRISES EN CHINE OF THE OTHER PART.
THIS AGREEMENT BEARS THE SEALS AND THE SIGNATURES OF THE MINISTER OF COMMUNICATIONS AND OF THE MINISTER OF FINANCE, DULY AUTHORIZED IN COMPLIANCE WITH A DECISION OF THE COUNCIL OF MINISTERS, SANCTIONED BY THE PRESIDENT OF THE CHINESE REPUBLIC UNDER DATE OF SEPTEMBER 14th 1922, AND HAS BEEN NOTIFIED BY THE CHINESE MINISTER FOR FOREIGN AFFAIRS TO THE BELGIAN MINISTER IN PEKING ON OCTOBER 24th 1922.
PAYMENT OF INTEREST ON THE PRESENT LOAN AND REPAYMENT OF ITS PRINCIPAL ARE AN EXPRESS AND UNCONDITIONAL OBLIGATION OF THE CHINESE GOVERNMENT. THEY ARE FURTHERMORE SECURED BY:
1° A FIRST RANK MORTGAGE SECURITY ON THE SECTION TO BE CONSTRUCTED FROM PAOTU TO NINGHSIA OF THE PEKING-SUIYUAN RAILWAY.
2° A SECOND RANK MORTGAGE SECURITY ON THE SECTION FROM PEKING TO PAOTU, OF THE SAME RAILWAY.
BOTH MORTGAGES ARE MEANT ON THE RAILROAD ITSELF, ITS FIXED PLANT AND ROLLING STOCK, ITS APPURTENANCES AND ITS EARNINGS.
3° THE AVAILABLE SURPLUS OF THE EARNINGS OF THE PEKING-HANKOW RAILWAY, THIS LIEN BEING PRIOR IN RANK TO ANY OTHER CHARGE THAT MIGHT BY ASSUMED BY THE RAILWAY CONCERNED AFTER SEPTEMBER 20th 1922. *(printed "MIGHT BY ASSUMED" — apparent typographical error for "BE")*
AN AMOUNT SUFFICIENT TO COVER INTEREST TO BE PAID ON THE NOTES UNTIL AND INCLUDING DECEMBER 1st 1924, WILL BE DEPOSITED WITH THE SOCIÉTÉ BELGE D'ENTREPRISES EN CHINE.

*(Central heading band, over serial box:)*
**BON DU TRÉSOR DE £20 OU FRANCS BELGES 1,200.-** | **N° 04086** | **TREASURY NOTE FOR £20 OR BELGIAN FRANCS 1,200.**

*(Lower terms, French, left — cursive italic script:)*
Le porteur du présent Bon a le droit de recevoir du Gouvernement Chinois au plus tard le 1er Décembre 1932, sauf amortissement antérieur, la somme de £20.- ou Francs Belges 1200.- au choix du porteur.
Le porteur a droit également aux intérêts sur cette somme au taux de huit pour cent par an, ces intérêts étant payables semestriellement le 1er Juin et le 1er Décembre de chaque année, et pour la première fois le 1er Juin 1923, contre remise des coupons correspondants ci-attachés, soit par 16 Shillings, soit par 18 Francs Belges, au choix du porteur.
Le capital sera payable contre remise du présent Bon, muni de tous les coupons non échus. Les 40,000 Bons représentant cette première série seront à cet effet remboursables au pair à raison de 8,000 Bons chaque année par tirages annuels effectués par les soins de la Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine, le premier remboursement étant effectué le 1er Décembre 1928, de façon à ce que l'emprunt soit intégralement remboursé le 1er Décembre 1932.
Les intérêts sur le présent Bon cesseront de courir à partir de la date où le capital en sera remboursable et où la provision aura été faite pour son remboursement.
Les paiements tant en ce qui concerne le remboursement du capital que les coupons, auront lieu à Bruxelles et à Anvers, ainsi que dans les autres villes qui seront à ce désignées par la Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine, soit en Francs Belges, soit en Livres Sterling au cours à vue sur Londres : les mêmes paiements auront également lieu à Londres.
Tous les Bons, tous les coupons et tous les paiements faits ou reçus afférents au service du présent emprunt seront exempts de tous impôts et taxes chinois.
Les coupons échus et les titres remboursables qui n'auraient pas été présentés au paiement dans un délai de trente ans à dater de leur exigibilité seront sans valeur.
Le présent Bon est émis au nom et pour compte du Gouvernement Chinois en vertu du contrat intervenu en date du 2 Octobre 1922 (an XI de la République Chinoise), entre le Gouvernement Chinois et la Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine, en foi de quoi le Ministre des Communications de la République Chinoise et le Ministre de Chine à Bruxelles ont fait apposer ci-dessous le fac-similé de leur sceau officiel et de leur signature et la Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine a fait apposer ci-dessous son sceau et la signature de son délégué.
Bruxelles, le 1er Décembre 1922.

*(Lower terms, English, right — cursive italic script:)*
The holder of this Note, unless the same be previously redeemed, is entitled to receive from the Chinese Government the sum of £20.- or Belgian francs 1200.- at his option, on and not later than December 1st 1932.
The holder is furthermore entitled to receive interest on aforesaid amount at the rate of 8% per annum, said interest being payable half-yearly on the first day of June and on the first day of December of each year, and for the first time on June 1st 1923, on surrender of the corresponding coupons hereto attached either by Sh. 16 or Belgian francs 18.- at the option of the holder.
Principal shall be paid on surrender of this Note together with all coupons maturing subsequently to the redemption date. The 40,000 Notes representing this First Series, shall be redeemable at par by yearly drawings of 8,000 Notes each effected by the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine, the first payment being made on December 1st 1928, so that the Loan will be entirely paid on December 1st 1932. Interest on this Note will cease on the principal becoming payable and provision having been made for its repayment.
Payment of the principal and of the matured coupons will be made in Brussels and Antverp, & in such other cities as shall be designated by the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine, either in Belgian francs or in Pound Sterling at sight rate on London. The same payments will also take place in London.
All Notes, coupons or payments made or received in connection with this Loan, shall be exempt from all Chinese taxes and imposts. Coupons and drawn Notes not presented for payment within thirty years after the date of their maturity shall be void.
This Note is issued in the name and for account of the Chinese Government by virtue of an agreement entered into on the 2nd October 1922 (XIth year of the Chinese Republic) between the Chinese Government and the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine, in witness whereof the Minister of Communications of the Chinese Republic and the Chinese Minister in Brussels have caused the fac-simile of their official seal and of their signature to be affixed hereto, and the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine has caused their seal and the signature of their agent to be affixed hereto.
Brussels, December 1st 1922.

*(Signature block — three panels along the bottom:)*

Left panel:
LE MINISTRE DES COMMUNICATIONS DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE,
THE MINISTER OF COMMUNICATIONS OF THE CHINESE REPUBLIC.
— manuscript signature "Kao En Hung" [reading uncertain], written over a large red Chinese seal impression.

Centre panel:
POUR LA / FOR THE — **SOCIÉTÉ BELGE D'ENTREPRISES EN CHINE, SOCIÉTÉ ANONYME.**
Red rectangular company seal reading **SOCIÉTÉ BELGE D'ENTREPRISES EN CHINE S.A. — BRUXELLES**, enclosing Chinese characters 比國在華 / 營業公司 (Belgian Enterprises in China Company).
UN ADMINISTRATEUR / DIRECTOR — manuscript signature [illegible, "Vandenven"?].

Right panel:
LE MINISTRE DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE À BRUXELLES,
THE MINISTER OF THE CHINESE REPUBLIC IN BRUSSELS,
— manuscript signature [illegible] written over a red square Chinese seal (seal-script characters).

*(Bottom margin, left:)* J. VERSCHUEREN — ANVERS-BRUXELLES
*(Bottom margin, right:)* Dessin déposé.

*(Upper margin, manuscript:)* circled "IV" (centre); "30.113" (right).
*(Upper right, two struck impressions of Belgian revenue/tax stamps: one reading "…TIMBRE À L'EXTRAORDINAIRE… BRUXELLES" with the Belgian lion; one circular "BELGIQUE — BELGIE" stamp with crowned arms and an "F." value.)*

---

## English Translation:

**GOVERNMENT OF THE CHINESE REPUBLIC**

*(Left heading, translated from French:)*
8% LOAN OF 1922 — For the supply of railway equipment (with special guarantees). First Series of £800,000, or 48,000,000 Belgian francs. In 40,000 Treasury Notes of £20.- or 1,200 Belgian francs.

*(Right heading, as printed in English:)*
RAILWAY 8% EQUIPMENT LOAN OF 1922. With special guarantees. First Series of £800,000, or Belgian francs 48,000,000. In 40,000 Treasury Notes of £20.- or Belgian francs 1,200.

**Preamble (the French and English columns carry the same text; English rendering:)**

This 8% Loan of the Government of the Chinese Republic of 1922 (11th year of the Chinese Republic) is issued through the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine, at Brussels, by virtue of a contract concluded on 2 October 1922 between the Government of the Chinese Republic and the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine.

This contract bears the seal and signature of the Minister of Communications and of the Minister of Finance, duly authorized in accordance with a decision of the Council of Ministers, sanctioned by the President of the Chinese Republic on 14 September 1922, and was notified by the Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Chinese Republic to the Belgian Minister at Peking on 24 October 1922.

Payment of the interest and repayment of the principal of the loan constitute a formal and unreserved obligation of the Chinese Government. They are furthermore secured by:
1. A first-rank mortgage on the section to be constructed from Paotu to Ninghsia of the Peking–Suiyuan Railway.
2. A second-rank mortgage on the section from Peking to Paotu of the same railway.
Both mortgages extend to the line itself as well as to its fixed plant and rolling stock, its appurtenances and its revenues.
3. The available surplus of the receipts of the Peking–Hankow Railway, this privilege ranking ahead of any other charge that this railway might assume after 20 September 1922.

A sum sufficient to service the interest payable on the notes up to and including the 1 December 1924 coupon will be deposited with the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine.

**Central heading:** TREASURY NOTE FOR £20 OR 1,200 BELGIAN FRANCS — No. 04086

**Terms of the note (French and English columns carry the same text):**

The holder of the present Note is entitled to receive from the Chinese Government, no later than 1 December 1932 (save earlier amortization), the sum of £20.- or 1,200 Belgian francs at the holder's option.

The holder is likewise entitled to interest on that sum at the rate of eight per cent per year, such interest being payable half-yearly on 1 June and 1 December of each year, and for the first time on 1 June 1923, against surrender of the corresponding coupons attached, at either 16 shillings or 18 Belgian francs, at the holder's option.

The principal shall be payable against surrender of the present Note together with all unmatured coupons. The 40,000 Notes representing this first series shall accordingly be redeemable at par at the rate of 8,000 Notes each year by annual drawings carried out by the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine, the first repayment taking place on 1 December 1928, so that the loan shall be fully repaid on 1 December 1932.

Interest on the present Note shall cease to accrue from the date on which the principal becomes repayable and provision has been made for its repayment.

Payments, both as regards repayment of principal and the coupons, will be made at Brussels and Antwerp, as well as in such other cities as may be designated by the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine, either in Belgian francs or in Pounds Sterling at the sight rate on London; the same payments will also take place in London.

All Notes, all coupons, and all payments made or received relating to the service of this loan shall be exempt from all Chinese taxes and duties.

Matured coupons and redeemable securities not presented for payment within thirty years of their falling due shall be void.

The present Note is issued in the name and for the account of the Chinese Government by virtue of the contract concluded on 2 October 1922 (11th year of the Chinese Republic) between the Chinese Government and the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine; in witness whereof the Minister of Communications of the Chinese Republic and the Minister of China at Brussels have caused to be affixed below the facsimile of their official seal and signature, and the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine has caused to be affixed below its seal and the signature of its delegate.

Brussels, 1 December 1922.

**Signatures:**
- The Minister of Communications of the Chinese Republic — signed "Kao En Hung" [uncertain], over a red Chinese seal.
- For the Société Belge d'Entreprises en Chine, a limited company (red company seal: "Belgian Enterprises in China Company"): a Director [signature illegible].
- The Minister of the Chinese Republic in Brussels — [signature illegible], over a red Chinese seal.

**Imprint:** J. Verschueren — Antwerp-Brussels. Registered design ("Dessin déposé").

title: Costa Rica Railway Company First Mortgage Debenture (Conditions), London
description: This is the reverse, or conditions, face of a £100 First Mortgage Debenture of The Costa Rica Railway Company, Limited, a British-registered concern formed to build and operate railways in Costa Rica. The printed conditions declare the instrument to be one of a series of 6,550 debentures securing an aggregate principal of £655,000, all ranking pari passu as a first floating charge on the Company's property, with the Company restrained from creating any mortgage in priority. Further clauses govern the register of holders, voting at debenture-holders' meetings, and modification of terms by a two-thirds majority. Issued in London in the mid-1880s, the document shows British capital underwriting tropical railway promotion, its security carefully layered to reassure investors in a distant, speculative venture. A blue CANCELLED stamp and a transfer register recording holders from 1888 onward attest to its later retirement. The framing is restrained, the typography sober rather than ornamental.
creator: Costa Rica Railway Company Limited
notes: First mortgage debenture; £100; CANCELLED; reverse/conditions side; transfer register blank; British company operating Costa Rican railway
transcription:
# Transcription: Costa Rica Railway Company Limited, First Mortgage Debenture £100

## CONDITIONS

1. This Debenture is one of a series of 5,250 Debentures for securing the aggregate principal sum of £525,000 issued or about to be issued by the Company. The debentures of the said series will in each year be puan as a first charge on the property hereby charged, without any preference or priority one over another, and such charge is by the a floating security, but as that, except as hereafter expressly provided, the Company is not to be at liberty to create any subsequent mortgages on the property making prior claim with or in priority to this said Debentures.

2. A register of the Debentures will be kept at the Company's registered office, wherein there will be entered the names, addresses, and descriptions of the holders, and particulars of the Debentures held by them respectively.

3. The registered holder will be regarded as exclusively entitled to the benefit of this Debenture, and all persons may at all times look exclusively to the Company shall not be bound to enter in the register notice of any trust or to recognize any right in any other person, save as herein provided.

4. Every transfer of the Debenture must be in writing under the hand of the registered holder, his executors or administrators. The transfer must be delivered at the registered office of the Company, with a fee of 2s. 6d. and such evidence of identity or title as the Company may reasonably require, and thereupon his transfer will be registered.

5.—In the case of joint registered holders, the principal moneys and interest hereby secured shall be paid to them upon a joint account.

6. The principal moneys and interest hereby secured will be paid without regard to any equities between the Company and the original or any intermediate holder thereof and the receipt of the registered holder for each principal, moneys and interest respectively, shall be a good discharge to the Company.

7.—The principal moneys hereby secured shall immediately become payable at the office of the Company unless default be made in the payment of any interest hereby secured, and the registered holder hereof

before such interest is paid, by notice in writing to the Company, calls in such principal moneys ; (b) If an order is made or an effective resolution is passed for the winding up of the Company.

8. The registered holder of this Debenture will be entitled to receive notices of and to vote at meetings of the Company, as provided by the Articles of Association.

9. The Company may, with the sanction of a majority of not less than two-thirds of such Debenture holders of this issue as are present in person or by proxy at any meeting of Debenture holders of which notice specifying the intention to propose a resolution to such effect shall have been duly given as aforesaid, and at any subsequent meeting of the Company, make a further issue or issues of Debentures either to redeem debentures of the Company as subsisting liens or otherwise ranking pars pass with or in priority to the Debentures of this series, or may otherwise alter or vary any of the terms or conditions upon which this series of Debentures have been issued.

10. In the event of the becoming necessary, either for reason of any requirement of the Government of Costa Rica in that behalf or otherwise, to establish a sinking fund for the purpose of redeeming the Debentures of this series, any question whether in regard to the fixing of the rate of the per centage or otherwise arising in relation to the establishment of such sinking fund, and which may require the sanction or determination of the Debenture holders, shall be referred to a meeting of Debenture holders, of which notice shall be duly given as aforesaid, and the resolution of a majority of Debenture holders present in person or by proxy at such meeting, shall be referred to the decision of, and shall be binding upon all the Debenture holders of this series.

11. A notice may be served by the Company upon the holder of this Debenture either personally or by sending it through the post in an envelope addressed to such holder at his registered address.

12. Any notice served by post shall be deemed to have been served at the time when it was posted, and the postage must serve it shall be sufficient to prove the address on the envelope enclosing the notice and the posting of it.

---

## DOCUMENT HEADER

**THE COSTA RICA RAILWAY COMPANY, LIMITED.**

**FIRST MORTGAGE DEBENTURE.**

**£100** | **No. 5426**

has been registered in the books of the Company this _____ 18_____
No. _____

title: Costa Rica Railway Company Debenture
description: A £100 Second Debenture of the Costa Rica Railway Company, Limited, a British-registered concern raising capital in London for railway works in Costa Rica. The certificate forms part of a £600,000 issue of 6,000 such debentures, each bearing interest at five per cent per annum, payable half-yearly on the first of March and the first of September. Sealed under the company's common seal in February 1888, it secures repayment of principal out of the company's revenues and is transferable on the register. An engraved oval portrait of a moustached man in uniform anchors the design, framed by dense guilloche borders that proclaim both the obligation's worth and its resistance to forgery, a document binding British investors to a distant tropical enterprise.

pages.description: Front face of the debenture. An engraved oval portrait of a uniformed, moustached man sits beneath an arched ribbon reading "The Costa Rica Railway Company Limited," flanked by "£100" panels and the legend "Second Debenture." Engrossed body text states the terms; the lower right bears manuscript signatures of a director and the secretary, and the dated common-seal clause. Page 1 of 2.

Reverse. Printed "Conditions" in two columns at the top govern transfer, interest, and repayment. Below, a registration panel repeats the company name, "Second Debenture," an ornamental "£100" cartouche and "No. 6886," above a ruled grid of transfer-record boxes for successive holders. A diagonal "CANCELLED" stamp marks the instrument as discharged. Page 2 of 2.

The Common Seal of the Company
Witnessed affixed in the presence of

**Signatures:**
[Signature] — DIRECTOR
[Signature] — SECRETARY

**Notable markings:**
- Serial number: No 6886
- Denomination: £100

- Orange/red company seal visible on left side
- Ornate decorative border with Celtic-style patterns throughout
- Intricate engraving work typical of 19th-century bond certificates
- Page marked as "Page 1 of 2" at bottom

# TRANSCRIPTION

## CONDITIONS.

1. This Debenture is one of a series of 5,000 Debentures for securing the aggregate principal sum of £500,000, issued, or about to be issued, by the Company.

2. The Company shall appropriate in each year a sufficient proportion of the net revenue of the Company for that year remaining, after providing for the seven-fold subscription (if necessary) of the First Mortgage Debentures for the time being of the Company, to pay interest at the rate of 5 per cent. for that year upon all the Debentures of this series, and shall apply the same accordingly, or in case the revenue shall not be sufficient for that purpose then shall it split the whole of the revenue equally amongst all the Debentures issued to the holders of the Debentures of this series of interest for that year upon their Debentures.

3. The interest upon the said Debentures shall not be cumulative, and the net revenue of any year shall only be subject to payment of the interest for that year.

4. A register of the Debentures will be kept at the Company's registered office, wherein there will be entered the names, addresses, and descriptions of the holders, and particulars of the Debentures held by them respectively.

5. The registered holder will be regarded as exclusively entitled in benefit of the Debenture, and all persons that act accordingly, and the Company shall not be bound to enter in the register notices of any trust or to recognize any other person as in any other person as in any other person.

6. Every transfer of this Debenture must be in writing under the hand of the registered holder, his executors, or administrators. The transfer must be delivered at the registered office of the Company with a fee of 2s. 6d. and such evidence of identity or title as the Company may reasonably require, and thereupon the transfer will be registered.

7. In the case of joint registered holders the principal moneys and interest payable hereunder shall be deemed to be owing to them upon a joint account.

8. The principal moneys and interest payable hereunder will be paid without regard to any equities existing between the Company and any intermediate holder hereof, and the receipt of the registered holder for such principal moneys and interest shall be a good discharge to the Company.

9. The principal moneys hereby secured shall immediately become payable if an order is made or an effective resolution is passed for the winding up of the Company.

10. The registered holder of this Debenture will be entitled to receive notices of and to vote at Meetings of the Company as provided by its Articles of Association.

11. In the event of its becoming necessary either by reason of any requirement of the Government of Costa Rica in that behalf, or otherwise, to establish a sinking fund for the purpose of redeeming the Debentures of this series, the same shall be established and managed in accordance with, or otherwise, arising in relation to the establishment of such sinking fund and which may require, the direction of a meeting of the Debenture holders, of which notice shall be duly given as aforesaid, and the decision of a majority of Debenture holders present in person or by proxy at such meeting shall be deemed to be the decision and shall be binding upon all the Debenture holders of this series.

12. A notice may be served by the Company upon the holder of this Debenture either personally or by sending it through the post in an envelope addressed to such holder at his registered address.

13. Any notice served by post shall be deemed to have been served at the time when it was posted, and in proving such service it shall be sufficient to prove the address on the envelope enclosing the notice, and the posting of it.

title: Bética Agricultural-Industrial Cooperative Mortgage Bond, Seville, 1931
description: A bearer mortgage bond (Obligación Hipotecaria) of 500 Pesetas nominal, bearing seven per cent annual interest free of taxes, issued by Bética, Sociedad Anónima Cooperativa Agrícola Industrial of Seville. The note belongs to an issue of 6,000,000 Pesetas, divided into 12,000 titles of 500 Pesetas, authorized at the extraordinary shareholders' assembly of 20 June 1931 and formalized by public deed before the notary Don Félix Sánchez Blanco y Sánchez; the company itself had been constituted in Seville on 15 December 1924 with a capital of 17,000,000 Pesetas. Signed at Seville on 1 October 1931, the bond is secured by mortgage and amortized within thirty years through semi-annual lottery draws. A red overprint records that the Azucarera de Sevilla assumed the interest payments, marking the cooperative's absorption into the city's sugar interests amid the financial strain of the early Second Republic. The vignette personifies Commerce with Mercury's winged helmet and caduceus, flanked by mural-crowned towers evoking Seville's civic enterprise.

notes: Agricultural-industrial cooperative; Seville; 4,000,000 pesetas mortgage bond issue; 12,000 titles at 500 pesetas; coupon sheet attached
transcription:
# Document Transcription

## Original (Spanish):

**Header and Title:**
Bética

SOCIEDAD ANÓNIMA COOPERATIVA AGRÍCOLA INDUSTRIAL

Constituido en Sevilla el 15 de Diciembre de 1924 por escritura otorgada ante el Notario D'Ilíc. Sánchez Blanco y Sánchez con un

CAPITAL SOCIAL DE 17.000.000 DE PESETAS

EMISIÓN DE 6.000.000 DE PESETAS EN OBLIGACIONES HIPOTECARIAS DIVIDIDAS EN 12.000 TÍTULOS AL PORTADOR, DE 500 PESETAS NOMINALES CADA UNO, ACORDADA EN JUNTA GENERAL EXTRAORDINARIA DE 30 DE JUNIO DE 1931, Y FORMALIZADA POR ESCRITURA PÚBLICA ANTE EL NOTARIO DON FÉLIX SÁNCHEZ BLANCO Y SÁNCHEZ EN SEVILLA EL 21 DE SEPTIEMBRE DE 1931.

**Bond Details:**
OBLIGACIÓN Nº [number] 500 PESETAS

El portador de esta Obligación tiene derecho al interés del siete por ciento anual, libre de impuestos presentes y futuros. Pagadero en pesetas oro semestres vencidos el 31 de Marzo y 30 de Septiembre de cada año. A contar desde el 31 de Marzo de 1932, hasta el día de su amortización que se hará en el plazo máximo de treinta años. Por sorteos, en la cuantía mínima de doscientas mil pesetas anuales, a partir del 30 de Septiembre de 1934, rendiendo aumentar la cuantía indicada por la amortización y resarcimiento de la Sociedad el derecho de retiramiento todas de la circulación previo pago de las mismas, y sin necesidad de sorteo, coincidiendo con la fecha destinada para el mismo.

EMENDADO A VALLE                                   SEVILLA 1ª DE OCTUBRE DE 1931

EL PRESIDENTE.                    [signature]                    EL SECRETARIO.

La S.A. "Azucarera de Sevilla"

[Additional text stamps and markings below main bond]

**Coupon Sheet:**
Multiple coupon boxes labeled "BETICA S.A. COOPERATIVA AGRÍCOLA INDUSTRIAL" with numbers (50, 51, 52, etc.), serial numbers, and payment dates from September 1931 and March 1932 onwards.

---

## English Translation:

**Bética**

ANONYMOUS COOPERATIVE AGRICULTURAL AND INDUSTRIAL COMPANY

Established in Seville on December 15, 1924 by deed executed before Notary D. Ilíc. Sánchez Blanco y Sánchez with a

SHARE CAPITAL OF 17,000,000 PESETAS

ISSUE OF 6,000,000 PESETAS IN MORTGAGE BONDS DIVIDED INTO 12,000 BEARER TITLES OF 500 PESETAS NOMINAL VALUE EACH, AUTHORIZED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY GENERAL SHAREHOLDERS' MEETING OF JUNE 30, 1931, AND FORMALIZED BY PUBLIC DEED BEFORE NOTARY DON FÉLIX SÁNCHEZ BLANCO Y SÁNCHEZ IN SEVILLE ON SEPTEMBER 21, 1931.

**Bond Details:**
BOND No. [number] 500 PESETAS

The bearer of this Bond is entitled to seven percent annual interest, free of present and future taxes. Payable in gold pesetas in semi-annual installments due on March 31 and September 30 of each year. Starting from March 31, 1932, until the date of amortization, which shall occur within a maximum period of thirty years. By lottery drawing, in a minimum amount of two hundred thousand pesetas annually, beginning September 30, 1934, the Company may increase said amount for amortization and retains the right to retire all bonds from circulation by prior payment of the same, without need for drawing, coinciding with the date set for the same.

AMENDED AT VALLE                                   SEVILLE OCTOBER 1, 1931

THE PRESIDENT.                    [signature]                    THE SECRETARY.

The S.A. "Azucarera de Sevilla"

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Signatures:** Two signatures present for El Presidente and El Secretario, dated October 1, 1931
- **Serial Numbers:** Various coupon numbers visible (50-59 range noted)
- **Decorative Border:** Ornate geometric frame with "500" in corners
- **Allegorical Figure:** Female figure with agricultural/industrial imagery in top section
- **Coupon Sheet:** Attached at bottom with multiple detachable coupons for interest payments
- **Stamps/Seals:** Multiple official stamps and "JUSTIFICADO" marking visible
- **Date Discrepancy:** Document mentions establishment in 1924 but formalization in 1931

title: Bética Agricultural-Industrial Cooperative Mortgage Bond (Reverse)
description: This is the reverse of a 500-peseta mortgage obligation (Obligación Hipotecaria) issued by the Bética Sociedad Anónima Cooperativa Agrícola Industrial of Seville, Spain. Dominating the salmon-pink field is a large latent-image "PESETAS" flanked by the figure 500, set within a finely engraved guilloche frame whose dense rosettes and lathe-work served to deter forgery. A two-column legal notice records the bond's formal standing: the deed of emission, dated 12 November 1931, was inscribed in the Land Registry of Lora del Río (Villanueva del Río, tomo 37, finca 1585) and, on 7 November 1931, in the Mercantile Registry of Seville. The reverse thus supplies the juridical scaffolding behind the security—registries and stamp duty ("Reintegrada del Timbre en la Matriz")—documenting how a Second-Republic agricultural cooperative pledged real property to raise capital from bearer investors.
creator: Bética Sociedad Anónima Cooperativa Agrícola Industrial
notes: Reverse of mortgage bond; "500 PESETAS" latent text; Registro Mercantil de Sevilla registration noted; salmon/pink printed reverse

title: Chinese Republic 5% Gold Industrial Loan, 500-Franc Bond (1914)
description: A 500-franc bearer obligation from the 5% Gold Industrial Loan floated by the Government of the Chinese Republic in Paris on 7 April 1914, part of a 150,000,000-franc issue divided into 300,000 bonds of 500 francs and running fifty years. Authorised by the President of the Republic and exempt from Chinese taxes, it was placed on the French market to fund industrial development. The certificate marries French financial convention with Chinese imperial-derived emblems: a central title cartouche in heavy capitals, vignettes of buildings and works framing the text, vermilion seals, and a signature panel bearing Chinese characters. The pairing of European bond engraving with Chinese officialdom reflects the young republic's reliance on Western capital markets.

pages.description: The bond face. A title cartouche reads "Emprunt Industriel du Gouvernement de la République Chinoise, 5% Or 1914," with "Obligation de 500 Francs au Porteur" below and "République de Chine" on the upper ribbon. Vignettes of buildings and industrial works flank dense French terms; vermilion seals, a "500" roundel, and a signature panel with Chinese characters appear at the foot. Page 1 of 2.

The attached coupon sheet, a full grid of small detachable interest coupons (roughly five across and many rows down) each headed "Obligation N°" and carrying its value and due date. The block represents the bond's thirty-year stream of semi-annual gold-franc interest payments. Page 2 of 2.

notes: 5% Gold Industrial Loan 1914; 150,000,000 francs total; No. 026,111; 500 francs; Paris issue; 30-year term; exempt from Chinese taxes; coupon sheet attached
transcription:
# Industrial Loan of the Government of the Chinese Republic — 5% Gold 1914, 500-Franc Bearer Bond — Transcription

## Original:

(Top decorative banner)
**RÉPUBLIQUE DE CHINE**

(Central medallion monogram: **RC** / **CR** intertwined)

(Circular French stamp-duty seal, upper left:)
FONDS D'ÉTATS ÉTRANGERS · 2% · PLEIN TARIF — PARIS · 9 – 9 · 1915

(Main title cartouche, heavy capitals)
**EMPRUNT INDUSTRIEL**
**DU**
**GOUVERNEMENT**
**DE LA**
**RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE**

**5% OR 1914**
DE 150.000.000 DE FRANCS
DIVISÉ EN 300.000 OBLIGATIONS DE 500 FRANCS CHACUNE
AUTORISÉ PAR LE PRÉSIDENT DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE
Cette autorisation a été officiellement communiquée au Ministre de France à Pékin
EMISSION DE 200.000 OBLIGATIONS 5% OR DE 500 FRANCS
Nᵒˢ 1 à 200.000.

**OBLIGATION DE 500 FRANCS**
AU PORTEUR
Nᵒ **026.144** (in red)

(Body text, left column)
Le contrat de cet Emprunt a été conclu à Pékin, le 9 Octobre 1913, entre le GOUVERNEMENT de la RÉPUBLIQUE CHINOISE d'une part, et la BANQUE INDUSTRIELLE de CHINE, d'autre part, le Gouvernement Chinois autorisant ladite Banque à émettre l'Emprunt pour son compte.

A la date du 28 Octobre 1913, le contrat de l'Emprunt a été notifié par le Ministre des Affaires Étrangères Chinois (Waï-Chiao-Pou) au Ministre de France à Pékin.

**L'EMPRUNT JOUIT DE LA GARANTIE ABSOLUE DU GOUVERNEMENT CHINOIS, TANT EN CAPITAL QU'EN INTÉRÊTS.**
**IL EST EN OUTRE GAGÉ SPÉCIALEMENT PAR :**
1ᵒ Le port de Poukou (ou Pukow)
2ᵒ Les installations et les constructions pour le service des eaux, de l'électricité et des tramways et autres entreprises municipales de Pékin,

(Body text, right column — continuation)
y compris le matériel, les dépendances et les recettes ;
3ᵒ Les taxes municipales présentes et futures de Pékin (non compris les octrois)
4ᵒ L'impôt sur l'alcool dans toutes les provinces du Nord du Yang-tsé-Kiang, exception faite de la Mongolie et la Mandchourie et dont le total est officiellement évalué à 4.000.000 de taëls environ.

**LE PRODUIT DE CET EMPRUNT EST DESTINÉ A L'EXÉCUTION DE TRAVAUX PUBLICS, ÉTANT INDIQUÉ QUE LES PREMIERS DE CES TRAVAUX PRÉVUS SONT :**
1ᵒ La construction du Port de Poukou (ou Pukow) sur le Yang-tsé-Kiang, en face de Nankin ;
2ᵒ Des travaux d'édilité à exécuter dans la ville de Pékin (tramways, éclairage électrique, eaux, etc.)

(Centred, heavy capitals)
**LES TITRES ET COUPONS DE CET EMPRUNT**
**SONT AFFRANCHIS A TOUT JAMAIS DE TOUS IMPÔTS CHINOIS**
Ces Obligations rapportent un intérêt annuel de Frs. 25 payable les 1ᵉʳ Mars et 1ᵉʳ Septembre de chaque année, par Coupons semestriels de Frs. 12,50.
**LA DURÉE DE L'EMPRUNT EST DE 50 ANS.**

(Lower body, left column)
Le remboursement devra s'effectuer au pair, par tirages annuels, de 1930 à 1964. Le premier remboursement aura lieu le 1ᵉʳ Mars 1930.
L'emprunt ne pourra être remboursé par anticipation avant le 1ᵉʳ Mars 1924, à partir de cette date et jusqu'au

(Lower body, right column — continuation)
1ᵉʳ Mars 1934, le Gouvernement aura la faculté d'anticiper le remboursement, mais à 102½% soit Frs. 512,50 par obligation.
A partir du 1ᵉʳ Mars 1934, le remboursement anticipé pourra s'effectuer au pair.

(Signature block)
**LE MINISTRE DES FINANCES** | Pour Contrôle : | **LE MINISTRE DE CHINE A PARIS**
[Chinese seal-name in vertical brush characters at lower left: 周自齊 — Zhou Ziqi] | **BANQUE INDUSTRIELLE DE CHINE** / Par délégation [manuscript signature] | [manuscript signature: **Hoo Weiteh** (Hu Weide)]

(Two vermilion square seals with seal-script characters flank the lower text; the large right-hand seal reads roughly 中華民國財政部關防 — official seal of the Ministry of Finance of the Republic of China)

**PARIS, le 7 Avril 1914.**

(Bottom margin, printer's imprint)
IMPRIMERIE RICHARD, 92, Rue St Lazare, PARIS

(Corner cartouches: **500** lower left; **5%OR** lower right. Oval vignettes of a railway station/gate, a railway bridge over a river, and a large railway yard; laurel wreaths in upper corners.)

FOREIGN STATE FUNDS · 2% · FULL TARIFF — PARIS · 9 – 9 · 1915

**INDUSTRIAL LOAN OF THE GOVERNMENT OF THE CHINESE REPUBLIC**

**5% GOLD 1914**
OF 150,000,000 FRANCS
DIVIDED INTO 300,000 BONDS OF 500 FRANCS EACH
AUTHORISED BY THE PRESIDENT OF THE CHINESE REPUBLIC
This authorisation was officially communicated to the Minister of France at Peking

The contract of this Loan was concluded at Peking on 9 October 1913, between the GOVERNMENT of the CHINESE REPUBLIC on the one part, and the INDUSTRIAL BANK OF CHINA on the other part, the Chinese Government authorising the said Bank to issue the Loan on its account.

On 28 October 1913, the Loan contract was notified by the Chinese Minister of Foreign Affairs (Wai-Chiao-Pou) to the Minister of France at Peking.

**THE LOAN ENJOYS THE ABSOLUTE GUARANTEE OF THE CHINESE GOVERNMENT, BOTH IN PRINCIPAL AND IN INTEREST.**
**IT IS FURTHERMORE SPECIALLY SECURED BY:**
1st The port of Poukou (or Pukow)
2nd The installations and constructions for the water, electricity and tramway services and other municipal enterprises of Peking,

(Right column — continuation)
including the equipment, appurtenances and revenues;
3rd The present and future municipal taxes of Peking (not including the octrois/toll duties)
4th The tax on alcohol in all the provinces north of the Yang-tsé-Kiang, with the exception of Mongolia and Manchuria, the total of which is officially estimated at about 4,000,000 taels.

**THE PROCEEDS OF THIS LOAN ARE INTENDED FOR THE EXECUTION OF PUBLIC WORKS, IT BEING SPECIFIED THAT THE FIRST OF THESE PLANNED WORKS ARE:**
1st The construction of the Port of Poukou (or Pukow) on the Yang-tsé-Kiang, opposite Nanking;
2nd Municipal works to be carried out in the city of Peking (tramways, electric lighting, water supply, etc.)

(Centred)
**THE CERTIFICATES AND COUPONS OF THIS LOAN ARE EXEMPT FOREVER FROM ALL CHINESE TAXES**
These Bonds bear an annual interest of Frs. 25 payable on 1 March and 1 September of each year, by semi-annual Coupons of Frs. 12.50.
**THE DURATION OF THE LOAN IS 50 YEARS.**

(Lower left column)
Redemption shall be effected at par, by annual drawings, from 1930 to 1964. The first redemption will take place on 1 March 1930.
The loan may not be redeemed in advance before 1 March 1924; from that date and until

(Lower right column — continuation)
1 March 1934, the Government shall have the option of anticipating redemption, but at 102½%, that is Frs. 512.50 per bond.
From 1 March 1934, anticipated redemption may be effected at par.

(Signature block)
**THE MINISTER OF FINANCE** [seal-name: Zhou Ziqi] | For Verification: **INDUSTRIAL BANK OF CHINA**, By delegation [signature] | **THE MINISTER OF CHINA AT PARIS** [signature: Hoo Weiteh / Hu Weide]

(Two red official Chinese seals; the larger reads approximately "Official Seal of the Ministry of Finance of the Republic of China")

**PARIS, 7 April 1914.**

(Printer's imprint)
RICHARD PRINTING HOUSE, 92 Rue St Lazare, PARIS

(Corner cartouches: **500** and **5% GOLD**; vignettes of railway works.)

title: Chinese Republic Industrial Gold Loan Bond (Reverse)
description: This is the reverse of a bearer bond of the Chinese Republic Industrial Government Loan, a 5 per cent gold issue denominated at 500 francs and dated 1914. The face is largely blank, as was customary, reserved for authentication: the bond number 026,111 is printed in red (here appearing in mirror image), and two red official seals, or chops, are impressed, one an elaborately bordered rectangular stamp at the lower left and a smaller square mark to the right. The ghostly imprint of the obverse text and its ornamental frame shows through the paper, and the 500-franc denomination is faintly legible in the lower corners. Issued in the early years of the Republic, the loan sought European capital for industrial undertakings; denomination in francs and the bilingual Chinese and French texts mark it as a sovereign instrument floated on the Paris market. The seals stand in for the state's pledge, the manual guarantee behind the printed promise.
creator: Government of the Chinese Republic
notes: Reverse of bearer bond No. 026,111; two Chinese official red seals affixed; bond number printed in red
transcription:
# Chinese Republic Industrial Gold Loan Bond — Reverse — Transcription

## Original (Chinese):

*(This is the reverse (verso) of a bearer bond. The sheet is essentially blank, reserved for authentication. There is no printed regulation or bilingual panel on this side. The only ink on the reverse is the red serial number and two red seal impressions (chops). The obverse text and ornamental frame show through the paper faintly in mirror image.)*

**Serial number (printed in red ink; appears in mirror image on this side, reads correctly when flipped):**
026,144

*(upper-central area, in red)*

**Large official seal — lower left (red square chop, seal script / 篆書, an ornate double-ruled border, roughly a 4×4 arrangement of characters):**
(seal: red square seal in seal script; characters not confidently legible — [illegible]. The impression may include 中 and 國 among its characters, but the seal-script forms cannot be read with certainty.)

**Smaller seal — to the right (red square chop, seal script, roughly a 2×2 arrangement of four characters):**
(seal: red square seal in seal script; four characters, [illegible]. The upper-left character resembles 規, but the reading is not certain.)

*(Show-through from the obverse, visible faintly through the paper — NOT text printed on this reverse side: the ornamental border/frame of the bond, the central title panel, and the denomination "500" faintly legible in the lower corners.)*

---

## English Translation:

This is the reverse (verso) of a bearer bond of the Chinese Republic Industrial Government Loan (Zhōnghuá Mínguó / 中華民國), a 5 per cent gold issue of 500 francs dated 1914. The reverse carries no printed terms or bilingual text; it is blank apart from authentication marks.

- **Serial number:** 026,144 (printed in red ink; it appears reversed on this side and reads correctly when the image is mirror-flipped).
- **Large official seal (lower left):** a red square chop impressed in seal script (zhuànshū / 篆書) within an ornate ruled border. The individual seal-script characters cannot be read with confidence and are transcribed as [illegible].
- **Smaller seal (to the right):** a second red square chop, four characters in seal script, [illegible].

The faint imagery visible across the sheet — the ornamental frame, the central title cartouche, and the "500" (franc) denomination in the lower corners — is show-through of the obverse printing, not text printed on this reverse side. The seals serve as the state's manual guarantee standing behind the printed promise.

*Note: The catalogue facts record the bond number as 026,111, but the image clearly shows 026,144 (the final two digits are unmistakably "4 4"). The image is followed here as ground truth.*

title: Chinese Republic Industrial Gold Loan Bond Coupon Sheet
description: This is a coupon sheet belonging to the Chinese Republic Industrial Government Loan of 1914, the 5 per cent gold loan denominated in French francs and floated for a nominal total of 150,000,000 francs. The sheet carries a grid of detachable semi-annual interest coupons attached to a 500-franc obligation, each cell repeating the obligation's reference number in red. The scan shows the reverse of the sheet, so the number reads through in mirror image and the coupons' printed face — interest text, payment dates, denomination — does not register. Issued in Paris-oriented form for the young Republic, the loan reflects China's reliance on European money markets to fund industrial and administrative needs after 1911, the franc denomination and gold clause reassuring foreign holders against currency risk. The plain ruled grid, devoid of vignette or ornament on this side, is purely functional: a ledger of future claims awaiting clipping and presentation.
creator: Government of the Chinese Republic
notes: 5% Gold Industrial Loan 1914; red-printed coupon sheet; large format; semi-annual coupons
transcription:
# Chinese Republic Industrial Gold Loan (1914) — Coupon Sheet Reverse — Transcription

## Original:

(This is the reverse/back of a bond coupon sheet. The paper is aged cream/off-white. A large black void occupies the lower-left corner where part of the sheet is missing/torn away. The only registering print is a red serial/reference number repeated in a regular grid of cells — five columns across the intact portion, and roughly twenty rows down. Because it is the back of the sheet, every number appears mirror-reversed, i.e. the red printing shows through from the face.)

**Repeated red reference number (printed in each grid cell, showing through mirror-reversed):**

026,144

(No other printed text registers legibly on this side. Faint show-through of the coupons' printed face — interest text, payment dates, denomination — is visible in places but is illegible: [illegible]. No signatures, stamps, or additional serials are legible.)

---

## English Translation:

There is no running prose to translate. The sheet is the reverse of a coupon sheet from the 1914 Chinese Republic Industrial Government (5% gold) Loan. The only legible printed matter is the bond's reference/serial number, No. 026,144 (bond No. 26144), repeated in red within each coupon cell of the grid and showing through in mirror image on this back side. The remaining show-through of the coupons' face text is not legible.

title: Republic of Peru 5% Gold Bond, £10
description: Bearer bond for £10 sterling from the Republic of Peru's 5% Gold Bonds issue of 1920, a sterling loan of £720,620 raised in London and divided into 72,062 bonds of £10 each. Printed bilingually in English and French with the Peruvian coat of arms above twin titles, the bond carries 5% interest payable half-yearly on 1 January and 1 July at the Anglo South American Bank in London and Paris, with principal repayable in English gold on 1 January 1946 unless redeemed earlier through a sinking fund or the Republic's option. Authorised by Law No. 4131 of 7 June 1920, the issue was placed by the Ethelburga Syndicate as agents and dated London, 25 May 1921. The Arts-and-Crafts foliate borders and engraved arms typify early-twentieth-century London financial printing.

pages.description: Bond face in mirrored English and French columns beneath the Peruvian arms, headed "Bond to Bearer for £10 Sterling." Engrossed text sets the terms; signed by the Ethelburga Syndicate's authorised representative and countersigned, dated this 25th day of May 1921. Page 1 of 4.

Upper register repeats the green title cartouche and arms over "£10" and "£720,620"; the lower half prints the full "General Bond," reciting Law No. 4131 of 7 June 1920 and the act of Director General of Finance Dr. Heraclides Perez. Page 2 of 4.

Sheet of French-language interest coupons, "Obligation de £10," each for £0.5.0 payable at the Anglo South American Bank in London or Paris; arranged in a grid by maturity, the visible coupon No. 13 falling due 1 July 1926. Page 3 of 4.

Sheet of English-language interest coupons, "Bond for £10," each marking a half-year's interest of £0.5.0; the grid runs to the later maturities, the visible coupon No. 52 falling due 1 January 1946. Page 4 of 4.

transcription:
# Transcription of Republic of Peru 5% Gold Bonds of 1920

## Left Column (English):

**REPUBLIC OF PERU**

**5 PER CENT GOLD BONDS OF 1920 FOR £720,620.**

**IN 72,062 BONDS OF £10 EACH. NUMBERED 1 TO 72,062 INCLUSIVE.**

[Small text regarding bond details and conditions - partially visible]

**BOND TO BEARER FOR £10 STERLING**

No 71547

The Government of the Republic of Peru will on the 1st day of January 1926 or on such earlier day as they may think fit, redeem these Bonds at par with accrued interest in London in the usual American gold coin or in sterling exchange at the rate of exchange ruling on the day of redemption, and will in the meantime pay interest thereon at the rate of five per centum per annum on the 1st January and 1st July of each year upon presentation of the coupons corresponding to such payment.

This Bond is one of a series of Bonds of like tenor and effect issued and to be issued in the aggregate £720,620 and all the Bonds of this series shall make their bonds jointly and severally binding over another and the holder of this Bond shall be entitled to all such of the registered holders of the other bonds of this series outstanding to the benefit of the General Bond.

The Bonds are issued under and pursuant to the authorization given by the Government of the Republic of Peru shall be charged upon the property forming the security for the Mexican and other equally valid Bonds of this series.

In witness whereof the authorized representatives of the Government of the Republic of Peru have affixed this seal this 25th day of May 1924.

Govts of ETHELIBURGA SYNDICATE LIMITED,
Agents for the Issuer

[Signature]

## Right Column (French):

**REPUBLIQUE DU PEROU**

**OBLIGATIONS 5 POUR CENT OR DE 1920 POUR £720,620.**

**EN 72,062 OBLIGATIONS DE £10 CHACUNE. NUMEROTEES DE LA 72,062 INCLUSIVEMENT.**

[Small text regarding bond details and conditions - partially visible]

**OBLIGATION AU PORTEUR DE £10 STERLING**

N° 71547

Le Gouvernement de la République du Pérou, le 1er Janvier 1926 ou à toute époque antérieure à laquelle il croirait devoir procéder, remboursera ces Obligations au pair avec intérêts courus à Londres soit en espèces d'or américain ou couramment reconnu, soit en sterling au taux du change des cours de Londres à la date du remboursement, et il servira en attendant un intérêt de cinq pour cent l'an payable le 1er Janvier et 1er Juillet de chaque année à la présentation des coupons correspondants au dit paiement.

Cette Obligation fait partie d'une série d'Obligations de la Républica du Pérou montant au total à £720,620 et toutes les Obligations de cette série forment un tout inséparable soumis à la même hypothèque garantissant l'ensemble et le porteur de cette Obligation aura les mêmes droits et privilèges que les porteurs des autres Obligations de la même série.

En foi de quoi le représentant autorisé du Gouvernement de la République du Pérou a appesé sa signature et son cachet sur le présent titre de l'Obligation Générale.

En témoins donné est emprunté qu'autorisé autorisé Gouvernement de la République du Pérou a apposé le contre-seing sur le même papier que les Obligations de cette série.

En fait le quoi le représentant autorisé du Gouvernement de la République du Pérou a apposé les présentes le 25 Mai 1924.

[Signature: Leu R Wallen]

Authorized representative of the Government of the Republic of Peru
Représentant autorisé du Gouvernement de la République du Pérou

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Serial Number:** No 71547
- **Denomination:** £10 Sterling
- **Date:** 25 May 1924
- **Signature:** Leu R Wallen (Authorized representative)
- **Official Seal:** Coat of arms of Peru at top center
- **Ornamental Border:** Elaborate green decorative frame with scrollwork
- **Orange stamp/seal:** Upper left corner
- **Agents:** Etheliburga Syndicate Limited, London

# Transcription of Republic of Peru 5% Gold Bonds of 1920

## Document Overview
This page shows a sheet of bond coupons arranged in a 4×14 grid format (56 coupons total), each representing interest payment vouchers.

## Original Text (French):

**Header on each coupon:**

REPUBLIQUE DU PÉROU
DE L'EMPRUNT 5 % OR
DE 1920 POUR £720,620

[Each coupon contains the following standard text:]

Obligations Gouvernementales à 5 p.c. garanties par le Gouvernement de la République du Pérou, autorisées par Décret du 13 juillet 1920.

Le Gouvernement de la République du Pérou paiera au porteur, le [DATE] à Londres, à Paris ou à Amsterdam, contre la présentation et remise de ce coupon, la somme de [AMOUNT] représentant l'intérêt semestriel à 5 p.c. l'an sur les Obligations de 10 Livres Sterling de son Emprunt 5 p.c. Or de 1920.

Les coupons devront être présentés à l'une des Banques désignées ci-dessus ou à tout Banquier autorisé.

En cas de perte ou de destruction de ce coupon, il ne sera pas remplacé.

OF THE 5% GOLD LOAN
OF 1920 FOR £720,620

[Each coupon contains:]

Government Obligations at 5% guaranteed by the Government of the Republic of Peru, authorized by Decree of 13 July 1920.

The Government of the Republic of Peru will pay to the bearer, on [DATE] in London, Paris or Amsterdam, upon presentation and surrender of this coupon, the sum of [AMOUNT] representing the semi-annual interest at 5% per annum on the Obligations of 10 Sterling Pounds of its 5% Gold Loan of 1920.

Coupons must be presented at one of the designated Banks above or to any authorized Banker.

In case of loss or destruction of this coupon, it will not be replaced.

title: Bulgarian 5% State Gold Loan Bond of 1904
description: A bearer obligation of the Principality of Bulgaria (Княжество България) for its 5% State Gold Loan of 1904, with a face value of 500 Francs rendered in five languages — Bulgarian, French, German, English, and Russian (Пятьсотъ Франковъ). Issued from a loan of 200,000 obligations secured on Bulgarian state revenues, it carries a 5% annual coupon redeemable in gold. The certificate is dated Sofia, 10/23 May 1905, bearing the engraved arms of the Principality (a crowned rampant lion) and signed by the Finance Minister and the Director of the Public Debt. The orange-rust borders, allegorical figures, and parallel-column text reflect the cosmopolitan presentation expected of a small state borrowing on the European capital markets.

pages.description: Face of the bond. A central crowned lion (Bulgarian arms) crowns four parallel columns stating the loan terms in French, Bulgarian/Russian, German, and English, each headed "Obligation / Bond" for 500 Francs. The dated Sofia line, 10/23 May 1905, sits above two engraved signatures. Page 1 of 4.

Reverse text page. The loan conditions ("Conditions de l'emprunt" / "Bedingungen der Anleihe" / "Conditions of this Loan") are printed in parallel columns in four languages, followed by the opening years of the amortization table ("Tableau d'amortissement" / "Tilgungs-plan") listing redemption sums by year. Page 2 of 4.

Continuation of the redemption schedule: dense multi-column tables of drawn obligation numbers and corresponding payment particulars carrying the amortization plan across its remaining years. Page 3 of 4.

Coupon sheet. Rows of detachable interest coupons ("Coupon" / "Купонъ"), each for the Kingdom of Bulgaria 5% loan and numbered to the bond, with descending coupon numbers and their due dates. A larger talon for renewal sits at upper left. Page 4 of 4.

title: Bulgarian 4½% Gold Loan of 1907 Bond
description: A bearer bond of the Kingdom of Bulgaria's 4½% Gold Loan of 1907 (ЦАРСТВО БЪЛГАРИЯ / ROYAUME DE BULGARIE — БЪЛГАРСКИ 4½% ДЪРЖАВЕНЪ ЗАЕМЪ ВЪ ЗЛАТО ОТЪ 1907 ГОД. / EMPRUNT BULGARE 4½% OR 1907), printed in four parallel languages: Bulgarian, French, German, and English. The obligation carries interest at four and a half per cent, paid in two equal half-yearly coupons reckoned in gold and quoted across European money centres (11.25 gold leva = 11.25 francs = 4.22 roubles = 9.11½ marks = 5.40 Dutch florins = 10.71 Austrian crowns = £0.8.11). Principal is repayable at par within sixty years by half-yearly drawings held at Sofia, with redemption schedule and conditions printed in full. The note declares the French text alone authoritative.

pages.description: Reverse of the bond. Quadrilingual conditions of the loan (УСЛОВИЯ НА ЗАЕМА / CONDITIONS DE L'EMPRUNT / BEDINGUNGEN DER ANLEIHE / CONDITIONS OF THE LOAN) set the 4½% rate, the coupon currency equivalents, the cities of payment, and redemption at par over sixty years by drawing. Below runs the amortization table (ТАБЛИЦА ЗА ПОГАШЕНИЕ / TABLEAU D'AMORTISSEMENT / TILGUNGS-PLAN / TABLE OF AMORTISATION), tabulating dates of payment, obligations to be amortized, amortization, and interest from 1909 onward. Page 1 of 3.

A trimmed offcut sheet against a dark ground, repeating in a grid the coupon currency-and-place table: metallic gold leva 11.25 at Sofia, francs 11.25 at Paris, francs at sight-rate on Paris at Brussels/Geneva/Bâle/Zurich, roubles 4.22 at St Petersburg, Reichsmark 9.11½ at Berlin and Frankfurt, sterling 0.8.11 at London, Austrian crowns 10.71 at Vienna, Dutch florins 5.40 at Amsterdam. Page 2 of 3.

Face of the bond. The title block names ЦАРСТВО БЪЛГАРИЯ / ROYAUME DE BULGARIE and the 4½% Or 1907 obligation, beside a TALON entitling the bearer to draw a fresh coupon sheet from the Direction de la Dette Publique. A strip of attached half-yearly coupons follows, each payable in Paris at francs 11.25 on 1 February and 1 August, running into the early 1940s. Page 3 of 3.

creator: Kingdom of Bulgaria
notes: Reverse of Kingdom of Bulgaria bond; quadrilingual conditions and amortization table; "УСЛОВИЯ НА ЗАЕМА" (modernized spelling vs. 1904 Principality bond)

title: Empire of Mexico External Public Debt Bond
description: A 500-franc bearer bond of the External Public Debt (Deuda Pública Exterior) of the Empire of Mexico, dated 1865 and issued under Emperor Maximilian I to fund the imperial regime then sustained by French intervention. The item pairs two parallel sheets of the same loan: a Spanish-language obligation (Obligación de 500 francos al portador) and its French-language counterpart (Obligation de 500 francs au porteur), each belonging to the second series. The franc denomination and bilingual issue reflect the debt's placement on European, especially Parisian, markets rather than within Mexico. Each sheet sets a central engraved obligation amid dense rows of detachable interest coupons, ministerial signatures, and a subsidiary billet repeating the heading.

pages.description: Spanish-language sheet, headed "IMPERIO DE MEXICO 1865 / DEUDA PUBLICA EXTERIOR," for an "Obligacion de 500 francos" of the second series, payable "al portador." The engraved central obligation, with terms of repayment and two ministerial signatures under "EL MINISTRO DE MEXICO," is flanked by grids of detachable interest coupons; a smaller billet at the foot repeats the same loan. Page 1 of 2.

French-language sheet, headed "EMPIRE DU MEXIQUE 1865 / DETTE PUBLIQUE EXTÉRIEURE," for an "Obligation de 500 francs" of the second series (deuxième série), payable "au porteur." Identical layout to the Spanish version: central obligation with repayment terms and signatures under "LE MINISTRE DU MEXIQUE," surrounded by sheets of detachable coupons and a subsidiary billet below. Page 2 of 2.

creator: Imperio de Mexico (Maximilian I)
notes: Maximilian I of Mexico; Spanish-language bond No. 1,085,528; 500 francos; Deuda Publica Exterior 1865; companion Spanish version to French No. 1,086,528
transcription:
# IMPERIO DE MEXICO - DEUDA PUBLICA EXTERIOR
## Obligacion de 500 francos - 1865

**Original (Spanish):**

IMPERIO DE MEXICO
1865                                                                                                1865

DEUDA PUBLICA EXTERIOR

Obligacion de 500 francos.
SEGUNDA SERIE
Número 1,085,428

El Gobierno Mexicano se compromete á pagar

AL PORTADOR

los intereses anuales á la demanda, al tipo de 5 por ciento francos de esta Obligacion, en París, en los meses de Abril y Octubre de cada año, y de devolver el capital en París, por semestrada del portador por la Comisión de Hacienda de Francia, en 1890, según las condiciones siguientes:

Los bonos se numerarán en París, se presentarán del público por la Comisión de Hacienda de Francia, conforme á lo acordado con el Gobierno Mexicano.

[Multiple detailed terms and conditions follow regarding interest payments, redemption, and administrative procedures]

Son á razón de millonarios de francos por año.

Las reclamaciones deberán hacerse bajo las prescripciones de los decretos en vigor [text continues with administrative details]

LE MINISTRE DE MEXICO

[Signatures visible]

---

**English Translation:**

EMPIRE OF MEXICO
1865                                                                                                1865

EXTERNAL PUBLIC DEBT

Obligation of 500 francs.
SECOND SERIES
Number 1,085,428

The Mexican Government commits itself to pay

TO THE BEARER

the annual interest on demand, at the rate of 5 percent francs of this Obligation, in Paris, in the months of April and October of each year, and to return the capital in Paris, by the holder's deadline per the Commission of Treasury of France, in 1890, according to the following conditions:

The bonds shall be numbered in Paris, [presented to the public] by the Commission of Treasury of France, in accordance with what was agreed with the Mexican Government.

[Multiple detailed terms and conditions regarding interest payments, redemption procedures, and administrative regulations follow]

At the rate of millions of francs per year.

Claims must be made under the prescriptions of the decrees in force [administrative details continue]

THE MINISTER OF MEXICO

---

**Notable Markings:**

- Serial Number: **1,085,428** (Segunda Serie/Second Series)
- Denomination: **500 francos**
- Date: **1865**
- Multiple "SEGUNDA SERIE" (Second Series) headers throughout

- Decorative borders and ornamental frames throughout
- Tabular interest payment schedules visible at bottom of page
- Official government seal references
