title: 170 Broadway Building First Mortgage Leasehold Gold Bond
description: A First Mortgage Leasehold 6½% Sinking Fund Gold Bond for $500, issued by Corner Broadway-Maiden Lane, Inc., the company formed to develop the 170 Broadway Building at the southeast corner of Broadway and Maiden Lane in the Borough of Manhattan, New York. The bond is secured not by ownership of the ground but by the leasehold estate in the improved property, a structure typical of Manhattan office-tower finance in the building boom of the late 1920s. Principal falls due May 1, 1949, with interest payable May 1 and November 1 in gold coin, and a sinking fund provided to retire the issue. Manufacturers Trust Company acts as trustee. Engraved in green with a spread-eagle vignette.

pages.description: The bond face. A spread eagle perched on rock crowns the sheet, flanked by the $500 denomination in green panels. Below, the heading names the 170 Broadway Building and Corner Broadway-Maiden Lane, Inc., and sets out the leasehold security, 6½% rate and gold-coin promise. Two signatures, Secretary and President, appear at foot with a corporate seal. Page 1 of 3.

The reverse and registration leaf. A green-bordered coupon-style panel restates the $500 sum, the May 1, 1949 maturity, the May 1/November 1 interest dates and Manufacturers Trust Company as trustee, with an assistant trust officer's countersignature. A ruled table headed for date of registration, registered owner and registrar follows, all blank. Page 2 of 3.

The attached coupon sheet, with the bond's green-bordered redemption text beneath. Rows of $16.25 semi-annual interest coupons, each marked Corner Broadway-Maiden Lane, Inc. and a printed treasurer signature, run from November 1934 through later years toward maturity; coupons for earlier dates have been clipped. The lower panel carries redemption and default provisions and two officers' signatures with seal. Page 3 of 3.

# Transcription

UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
STATE OF NEW YORK

170 BROADWAY BUILDING
CORNER BROADWAY-MAIDEN LANE, INC.
FIRST MORTGAGE LEASEHOLD 4½% SINKING FUND GOLD BOND
No. B25

Secured by Leasehold Estate in the Improved Property at the Southeast Corner of Broadway and Maiden Lane in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York.

CORNER BROADWAY-MAIDEN LANE, INC., a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of New York (hereinafter called the "Company") for value received acknowledges itself indebted and hereby promises to pay to the bearer hereof, or if this bond be registered, to the registered holder hereof, on the first day of May, 1948 [unless this bond shall have been called for previous redemption], the sum of

FIVE HUNDRED DOLLARS ($500)

and to pay interest thereon at the rate of four and one-half per cent (4½%) per annum payable semiannually on the first day of May and the first day of November in each year, and interest hereon until maturity hereof (unless this bond shall have been called for previous redemption) upon presentation and surrender hereof at the office of the Trustee, or at the principal office of the Company in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York, in gold coin of the United States of America or of equal to the present standard of weight and fineness; without deduction for any normal federal income tax (except the excess, if any of any normal federal income tax imposed upon the holders of this bond by the United States government), and without deduction for any other tax, duty or governmental charge whatsoever.

The Trustee hereunder named, or any paying agent of the Company may be required or permitted to pay hereon or to deliver or receive hereunder under any present or future law of the United States of America.

If default shall be made in the payment of any installment of interest due on this bond on the date thereof or in the payment of the principal hereof, then the registered holder hereof may, in the sole discretion thereof, offer mentioned, reimburse such bearer or registered holder hereof who shall be a resident of the District of Columbia or of certain States of the United States of America referred to in said indenture, any and all taxes assessed or to be assessed upon such bond or the interest hereon, provided that no such obligation shall exist nor shall any such reimbursement shall be made to the Company, as provided in said indenture, within thirty (30) days after payment of such and all other charges shall not have become due and payable.

The Company also agrees, in accordance with the provisions of the Sinking Fund Gold Bonds, all of the bonds (save of the outstanding member 2nd debentures) issued and to be issued in an aggregate principal amount of not exceeding one hundred thousand dollars ($100,000) during the continuation of the indenture, under the lien and provisions thereof, dated May 1, 1925 (hereinafter referred to as the "Indenture"), and duly recorded and delivered, and conveying, with Trustees First Trust Company of New York, as Trustee, are upon the Indenture estate securities to hereby made to said indenture, as is full and complete in description of the property mortgaged, the nature and extent of the security hereby owned and the rights of the holders of said bonds and of the Trustee to enforce said security.

[The document continues with extensive legal text regarding bond terms, conditions, redemption provisions, and trustee responsibilities. Due to the density and complexity of the legal language, some passages become increasingly difficult to read clearly in the image.]

This bond shall pass by delivery unless registered as provided in the indenture or unless such registration be made at the office of the Trustee and unless such registration is made and cancelled, in which case registration shall be valid.

This bond may be discharged from registration in the manner provided in said indenture, except that it shall not be entitled to any benefit under said indenture or be valid or obligatory for any purpose until it shall have been authenticated by the execution of the certificate hereon endorsed by Manufacturers Trust Company as Trustee under

IN WITNESS WHEREOF Corner Broadway-Maiden Lane, Inc., has caused this bond to be signed by its president or a vice-president, and its corporate seal to be hereto affixed and attested by its secretary or an assistant secretary, and the date hereof to be written or printed hereon.

CORNER BROADWAY-MAIDEN LANE, INC.,

Dated: [date appears to be around May 1, 1925]

title: 170 Broadway Building First Mortgage Leasehold Gold Bond (Manufacturers Trust Version)
description: A $500 First Mortgage Leasehold 6½% Sinking Fund Gold Bond issued by Corner Broadway-Maiden Lane, Inc., the corporation formed to finance the 170 Broadway Building. Dated as of the first day of May, 1928, it promises to pay the bearer five hundred dollars in gold coin on the first day of May, 1948, with interest at six and one-half per cent per annum payable semi-annually on the first of May and November. The obligation is secured by the leasehold estate on the improved property at the southeast corner of Broadway and Maiden Lane in the Borough of Manhattan, with the Manufacturers Trust Company serving as trustee. Signed by the company's secretary and president, the certificate speaks to the speculative real-estate finance of late-1920s New York, when ground leases on prime lower-Manhattan corners were repackaged into widely sold mortgage bonds. The engraved spread-winged eagle, set within disciplined neoclassical borders, lends the federal dignity that bond houses used to reassure investors of the security's soundness.

transcription:
# 170 Broadway Building (Corner Broadway–Maiden Lane, Inc.) First Mortgage Leasehold 6½% Sinking Fund Gold Bond — Transcription

## Original:

(Top center, above eagle vignette:)

**UNITED STATES OF AMERICA**
**STATE OF NEW YORK**

(Left and right of the eagle vignette, large green denomination panels with dollar-sign motifs:) **$500. ... $500.**

(Main title block:)

**170 BROADWAY BUILDING**
**CORNER BROADWAY–MAIDEN LANE, INC.**
**FIRST MORTGAGE LEASEHOLD 6½% SINKING FUND GOLD BOND**

(To the left and right of the following lines, serial-number boxes:) **No. D25** ... **No. D25**

Secured by Leasehold Estate in the Improved Property at the Southeast Corner of Broadway and Maiden Lane in the Borough of Manhattan City and State of New York.

**CORNER BROADWAY–MAIDEN LANE, INC.**, a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of New York (hereinafter called the "Company") for value received acknowledges itself indebted and hereby promises to pay to the bearer hereof, or if this bond be registered, to the registered holder hereof, on the first day of May, 1949 (unless this bond shall have been called for previous redemption as provided herein),

**FIVE HUNDRED DOLLARS ($500)**

and to pay interest thereon at the rate of six and one-half per cent (6½%) per annum payable semi-annually on the first day of May and the first day of November in each year, said interest hereon until maturity hereof (unless this bond shall have been called for previous redemption) being payable only upon surrender of the interest coupons hereto attached as they severally mature.

Both principal of and interest on this bond are payable at the principal office of Manufacturers Trust Company in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York, in gold coin of the United States of America of or equal to the present standard of weight and fineness without deduction for any normal federal income tax (except the excess, if any, of such normal federal income tax over an aggregate in any one year of two per cent (2%) of the amount payable as such interest) which the Company, its successors or assigns, or the Trustee hereinafter named, or any paying agent of the Company may be required or permitted to pay thereon or to deduct or retain therefrom under any present or future law of the United States of America.

The Company covenants, in the manner, upon the conditions and subject to the limitations provided in the indenture hereinafter mentioned, to reimburse to each bearer or registered holder hereof who shall be a resident of the District of Columbia or of certain States of the United States of America referred to in said indenture, any and all taxes assessed to and paid by such bearer or registered holder by reason of such residence and the ownership of, or receipt of income from, this bond, provided that application for such reimbursement shall be made to the Company, as provided in said indenture, within thirty (30) days after payment of such tax and within three (3) months after such tax shall have become due and payable.

This bond is one of a duly authorized issue of bonds of the Company known as its First Mortgage Leasehold 6½% Sinking Fund Gold Bonds, all of like tenor (except as to designating number and denomination) issued and to be issued in an aggregate principal amount of not exceeding one million three hundred thousand dollars ($1,300,000) at any one time outstanding, under the provisions of and equally secured, irrespective of the actual date of issue, by an indenture of mortgage dated as of the first day of May, 1925 (hereinafter referred to as the "indenture") and duly executed and delivered, mortgaging, transferring and conveying unto Manufacturers Trust Company as Trustee that certain leasehold estate created by lease from 2-4 Maiden Lane Corporation, as Lessor to the Company, as Lessee, recorded or to be recorded in the office of the Register of the County of New York. Reference is hereby made to said indenture for a full and complete description of the property mortgaged, the nature and extent of the security thereby created and the rights of the holders of said bonds and of the Trustee in respect of such security.

This bond is entitled to the benefit of a certain sinking fund established in the indenture and may, through the operation of said sinking fund, in the manner and upon the conditions specified in said indenture, and upon thirty (30) days' published notice, be redeemed on any interest payment day upon payment of the principal amount hereof and accrued interest, plus a premium of three per cent (3%) of said principal amount hereof if such redemption be accomplished on or prior to May 1, 1930, two per cent (2%) of the principal amount hereof if such redemption be accomplished thereafter and on or prior to May 1, 1935, and one per cent (1%) of the principal amount hereof if such redemption be accomplished thereafter and on or prior to November 1, 1948; and at the option of the Company may, together with all, but not less than all, other bonds of this issue then outstanding, be redeemed on any interest payment date, on like notice and in like manner upon payment of the principal amount hereof, plus a premium of one per cent (1%) of said principal amount.

If default shall be made in the payment of any installment of interest due on this bond or in the performance or observance of any of the covenants and agreements in the indenture contained, then the principal hereof may, prior to the express maturity hereof, be declared and caused to become due and payable in the manner, at the time or times and with the effect provided in said indenture.

The faith and credit of the Company are pledged for the payment of this bond and all interest hereon, according to the tenor and effect hereof, but this bond is a corporate obligation only and no recourse whatsoever, either directly or through any trustee, receiver, assignee or other person shall be had for the payment of the principal of or interest on this bond, or for the enforcement of any claim based hereon or on the indenture against any incorporator or any past, present or future stockholder, officer or director of the Company or of any successor corporation by virtue of any statute or constitution or by the enforcement of any assessment, penalty, subscription or otherwise, all individual liability of whatsoever kind or nature of incorporators, stockholders or directors as such being released by the holder hereof, by the acceptance of this bond and being also waived and released by the terms of said indenture.

This bond shall pass by delivery unless registered as to principal in the holder's name at the office of the Trustee and unless such registration be by the Trustee acting as Registrar noted hereon. After such registration no transfer hereof shall be valid unless made by the registered holder in person or by attorney duly authorized in writing and similarly noted hereon, but this bond may be discharged from registration by being in like manner transferred to bearer and thereupon transferability by delivery shall be restored, but this bond may again and from time to time be registered and transferred to bearer as before. The registered holder hereof shall be entitled to receive the principal hereof, but such registration shall not affect the negotiability of the interest coupons hereto attached, which shall continue to be transferable by delivery and payment to the bearer thereof shall discharge the Company with respect to the interest therein mentioned, whether or not this bond shall have been registered.

This bond shall not be entitled to any benefit under said indenture or be valid or obligatory for any purpose until it shall have been authenticated by the execution of the certificate hereon endorsed by Manufacturers Trust Company as Trustee under said indenture or its successor in said trust.

(Red rubber-stamp overprint across the last two paragraphs:) **MANUFACTURERS TRUST COMPANY** / **Trustee**

IN WITNESS WHEREOF Corner Broadway–Maiden Lane, Inc., has caused this bond to be signed by its president or a vice-president, and its corporate seal to be hereunto affixed and attested by its secretary or an assistant secretary, and coupons for such interest bearing the facsimile signature of its treasurer to be hereto attached, all as of the first day of May, 1925.

**CORNER BROADWAY–MAIDEN LANE, INC.,**

(right side:) By [handwritten ink signature, illegible]
**President.**

(left side:) Attest: [handwritten ink signature, illegible]
**Secretary.**

(center bottom: green ornamental seal/rosette)

(Bottom-center imprint:) HAMILTON BANK NOTE CO., NEW YORK.

title: 170 Broadway Building First Mortgage Leasehold Gold Bond Stub
description: The face of a registered First Mortgage Leasehold 6½% Sinking Fund Gold Bond for $500, issued by Corner Broadway-Maiden Lane, Inc.—the 170 Broadway Building company—and secured by the leasehold estate in the improved property at the southeast corner of Broadway and Maiden Lane in the Borough of Manhattan. Principal falls due May 1, 1949, with interest payable May 1 and November 1 at the office of Manufacturers Trust Company, the indenture trustee whose certificate and assistant officer's signature authenticate the instrument. The reverse permits exchange of $500 and $1,000 bonds of the issue for an equal aggregate amount. Such gold bonds financed lower Manhattan office construction by pledging the ground lease itself; the engraved frame, green guilloche, and Roman lettering project the sober permanence on which a long-dated mortgage obligation depended. A blank registration ledger occupies the left panel.

title: Corner Broadway-Maiden Lane, Inc. (170 Broadway Building) First Mortgage Leasehold Gold Bond, New York, 1928
description: This is the coupon-and-conditions sheet for a First Mortgage Leasehold 6½% Sinking Fund Gold Bond issued by Corner Broadway-Maiden Lane, Inc., the corporate vehicle behind the 170 Broadway Building in Manhattan, dated the first day of May, 1928 and maturing 1 May 1949. Each surviving semi-annual coupon, running from May 1933 through May 1949, promises the bearer sixteen and 25/100 dollars in gold coin of the United States at the principal office of Manufacturers Trust Company in the Borough of Manhattan — $32.50 a year, the 6½% yield on a $500 bond. The dense legal legend at right, secured against the building's leasehold and sinking fund and signed in facsimile by the company's president and secretary, embodies real-estate finance in the late 1920s building boom: a single Lower Manhattan tower mortgaged into transferable gold-clause paper on the eve of the 1929 collapse.

**ONE THOUSAND DOLLARS ($1,000)**

In certain bonds and mortgages of or assumed by Nineteen John Street Corporation, a corporation existing under and by virtue of the laws of the State of New York (hereinafter called the "Mortgagor"), for an aggregate principal amount of Four Million Four Hundred Thousand Dollars ($4,400,000), executed and delivered to The New York Trust Company, as Trustee (hereinafter called the "Trustee"), and secured by a mortgage dated as of October 1, 1925, of the real property known as the Tyler Building, given to secure the Nineteen John Street Corporation's First Mortgage Six Per Cent Sinking Fund Gold Loan, bearing date as of October 1, 1925, and bearing interest from October 1, 1925.

title: Maplewood Suburban Home Company Mortgage Sinking Fund Bond
description: Six percent mortgage sinking-fund bond for $500 issued by the Maplewood Suburban Home Company, a real-estate venture incorporated in 1890 with capital stock of $2,000,000. Dated the first day of August 1890 at Nashville, Tennessee, the bond matures August 1, 1900, with interest payable quarterly and secured by a trust mortgage on the company's lands held by the American Loan & Trust Company as trustee. Printed in green, the certificate frames an engraved view of a domed civic building captioned "Nashville, Tenn." within an elaborate scrollwork border, with the denomination repeated as a large screened watermark across the text. Signed by the president and treasurer beneath the corporate seal, it embodies the speculative suburban land-and-home schemes of the era.

Engraved vignette of a civic building labeled "Nashville, Tenn." Body text, embossed seal at lower left, and ink signatures of president and treasurer dated August 1, 1890. Page 1 of 3.

Verso and endorsements. Central panel repeats "Maplewood Suburban Home Company / $500 / Six Per Cent Mortgage Sinking Fund Bond," principal due August 1, 1900, interest payable quarterly. Includes a printed trustee's certificate, a registration assignment clause, and a ruled registration table at right. Page 2 of 3.

Sheet of attached interest coupons, ten rows by four, each headed "Maplewood Suburban Home Company" and payable by the American Loan & Trust Co. for $7.50, being three months' interest, with quarterly due dates running to 1900. Engraved-script signature on each; manuscript notes in the right margin. Page 3 of 3.

title: Dutch East India Company (VOC) Middelburg Chamber Obligation Receipts
description: Two related documents of the Middelburg (Zeeland) Chamber of the Vereenigde Oost-Indische Compagnie, photographed together. The lower sheet is a handwritten obligatie in which the Reecken-meesters (accounting masters) of the Chamber, acting through its receiver Dominicus van Hoontshoerle, acknowledge a loan of £1333:6:8 Flemish (about 8,000 guilders), bearing interest at 6¼ per cent per year and repayable to the bearer forty-four months after its date. It is drawn and signed at Amsterdam, 26 October 1622 (the pre-printed place "Middelburgh" struck out and "Amsterdam" substituted, though the issuing body remains the Middelburgh Chamber), by the Chamber's officers, among them Cornelis Jauckly[?]. The upper sheet is the matching printed payment order of the "Ontfangers der Oost-Indische Compagnie," completed by a manuscript receipt acknowledging full satisfaction of capital and accrued interest, signed by Van Hoontshoerle at Amsterdam, 9 November 1623. Together they document the early VOC's reliance on short-term interest-bearing borrowing from private lenders to finance its trading voyages, and preserve the paper mechanism by which such an obligation was issued, serviced, and discharged.
creator: VOC (Vereenigde Oost-Indische Compagnie), Kamer Middelburg
notes: Dutch East India Company (VOC) – Middelburg Chamber Obligation Receipts, 1622–1623

title: Monte di Pietà of Florence assignment of luoghi, Florence, 1647
description: A printed assignment of luoghi (assegnazione di luoghi) issued by the Officials, Protectors and Administrators of the Monte di Pietà of Florence, conveying to a named holder—here an illustrious Marchese—a stake in the Monte's non-redeemable funded capital. Manuscript entries record four and a half luoghi, each valued at 100 scudi fiorentini reckoned at seven lire to the scudo, yielding five scudi a year, a steady five-per-cent return paid in half-yearly portions. The text grounds the issue in the provision of 23 April 1616 by which the Grand Duke of Tuscany authorised the perpetual capital, while the document itself is dated at Florence on 11 January 1647. Such instruments turned a charitable pawn-bank into a vehicle for funded Tuscan public credit, drawing patrician capital into a secure, interest-bearing endowment. A wax seal, secretarial and ducal signatures, and engraved figures flanking the Medici arms attest its authority.
creator: Monte di Pietà della Città di Firenze
notes: Bond certificate assigning luoghi of the Florentine Monte di Pietà. Interest 5 scudi per luogo per year. Established under Grand Ducal senate ordinance of 1616.
transcription:
# Assignment of Luoghi of the Monte di Pietà of Florence — Transcription

## Original (Italian):

*(top left margin, manuscript):* 1647 11 Genn.o

*(printed body, with manuscript insertions marked)*

**NOI Officiali del Monte di Pietà della Città di Firenze, Protettori, & Amministratori del Monte non vacabile** eretto d'ordine del S. G. D. di Toscana nostro Sig. per la Prouisione publicata nell'Amplissimo Senato de' 48. il dì 23. di Aprile 1616. cediamo, & assegniamo à [manuscript: Ill.mo Sig. Marchese fu B.o [illegible surname]] Luoghi [manuscript: quattro e mezzo] di detto Monte di valuta di scudi cento per ogni luogo di lire sette per scudo moneta Fiorentina con i frutti à ragione di scudi cinque l'anno simili per ogni luogo da hoggi in auuenire. Il prezzo de' quali luoghi di detto Monte ci è stato pagato di contanti in mano al nostro Camarlingo generale in scudi [manuscript: quattrocento cinquanta] come à Entrata D a [manuscript: 309]. E noi l'habbiamo fatto descriuere creditore, e padrone de sudetti luoghi al libro di detto Monte eretto per detta Prouisione segnato D de' Liberti à pagarli i frutti di sei mesi in sei mesi la rata portione, e con i priuilegi, condizioni, e dichiarazioni di che in detta Prouisione, e non altrimenti, nè in altro modo. Et in virtù della presente ordiniamo al Camarlingo generale del nostro Monte, che per i tempi sarà, che paghi detti frutti di sei mesi in sei mesi, & in fede habbiamo fatta fare la presente, quale sarà sottoscritta da vno de' nostri Officiali, e dal nostro Prouedittore, e Cancelliere, e sigillata con il solito sigillo di detto Monte.

**Data in Firenze nell'Offizio di detto Monte di Pietà il** [manuscript: 11 di Genn.o 1647]

*(lower right, manuscript signatures):* [illegible] officiale / Michele [illegible surname] (Cancelliere)

*(layout notes: engraved allegorical male figures at upper left and upper right flanking the crowned Medici arms at center top; red wax seal impression at lower left; the "4" of the year 1647 is written in old open-form and could be misread as "2".)*

---

## English Translation:

*(top left margin):* 1647, 11 January

We, Officials of the Monte di Pietà of the City of Florence, Protectors and Administrators of the non-redeemable Monte erected by order of His Serene Grand Duke of Tuscany our Lord, by the Provision published in the Most Ample Senate of the 48 on the 23rd day of April 1616, cede and assign to [the Most Illustrious Lord Marquis, the late B[.] [illegible surname]] [four and a half] Luoghi of the said Monte, of the value of one hundred scudi for each luogo, at seven lire to the scudo in Florentine money, with interest at the rate of five like scudi per year for each luogo from today onward. The price of which luoghi of the said Monte has been paid to us in cash into the hands of our Treasurer-General in [four hundred and fifty] scudi, as per Entry D at [folio 309]. And we have had him entered as creditor and proprietor of the said luoghi in the ledger of the said Monte erected for the said Provision, marked D of the Liberti, the interest to be paid every six months in its due portion, and with the privileges, conditions, and declarations set forth in the said Provision, and not otherwise nor in any other manner. And by virtue of the present [instrument] we order the Treasurer-General of our Monte, whoever shall hold office from time to time, to pay the said interest every six months; and in witness whereof we have caused the present to be drawn up, to be subscribed by one of our Officials and by our Provveditore and Chancellor, and sealed with the customary seal of the said Monte.

Given in Florence in the Office of the said Monte di Pietà, the [11th of January 1647].

*(signatures):* [illegible] Official / Michele [illegible surname], Chancellor

title: De Woning-Maatschappij, Batavia Bearer Share
description: A bearer share (aan toonder) of f 100 in the Naamlooze Vennootschap De Woning-Maatschappij, a limited liability housing company established at Batavia in the Dutch East Indies. The certificate records an authorised capital of f 500,000 in Dutch guilders, divided into 5,000 shares of f 100 each, and recites the company's legal foundation: incorporated by notarial deed of 27 February 1908 before Notary H. Schotel at Batavia and sanctioned by Government Resolution of 9 March 1908, its statutes wholly amended by deed of 25 January 1909 before Notary E.H. Carpentier Alting and approved by Government Resolution of 18 February 1909. Issued at Batavia and signed by a commissioner and a director, the share speaks to the colonial property market of the Indies, where a chartered company gathered guilder capital to build and let dwellings. The ornate vignette of a villa among palms, framed in a warm terracotta Art Nouveau border, advertises the comfortable tropical residence the enterprise promised to supply.
creator: De Woning-Maatschappij

title: Ostend Company Share Subscription Receipt
description: This subscription receipt records the first installment paid on capital stock of the Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie, the Imperial East India trading company seated at Ostend and chartered by Emperor Charles VI. The directors here order their cashier, Joan Baptist Cogels junior, to receive 250 guilders in bank money (“Wissel gelt”) from the subscriber as the first payment on a share (“actie”) of one thousand guilders, on the conditions set forth more fully in the Imperial Charter (“Octroy”). Done at Antwerp, the thirteenth of August 1723. Endorsements beneath the text acknowledge three further installments (October and December 1723, and 1724–1725), so the printed engraving doubles as a running quittance. Issued at the height of the speculative enthusiasm that greeted the Company's founding, the document shows how Habsburg overseas ambition was financed by drawing the merchant capital of Antwerp into divisible, transferable shares.
creator: Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie
notes: Ostend Company share subscription receipt, 250 guilden, Antwerp 1723. Multiple payment endorsements through 1726. Ostend Company chartered 1722, suppressed 1731.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

O. 1198

De Directeurs van de generale Keyserlyche Indische compagnie, ordonneren aen hunnen cassier Joan Baptist Cogels junior, te ontfangen van D.M. Joseph Bergkin in Wernen de Somme van twee hondert en vijftigh guldens wissel gelt, voor het eerste payement Syndercactie vaneenduijsent guldens in het Capitael vande Selve compagnie, op de conditien in het Octroy breeder Vermelt, Stellende quitantie hier onder, actum in Antwerpen derthien Augusti Seventhien hondert drij en twintiagh

Ontfangenvan D.Heer Jodeph Bergkin de Somme van twee hondert vijftigh guldens Wissel gelt voor het eerste paijement adje 8. October 1723

Solvit als boven het 2de kayement 8. December 1723

Solvit als boven d'Helft van het 3de Bayement 13 decemb. 1724

Solvit als boven onder de helft van het Derde Rayement 2. Novemb. 1725

**English translation:**

The Directors of the General Imperial Indian Company order their cashier Joan Baptist Cogels junior to receive from D.M. Joseph Bergkin in Wernen the sum of two hundred and fifty guilden in exchange money, for the first payment of one thousand guilden in the Capital of the same company, under the conditions more broadly set forth in the Charter, granting receipt hereof, executed in Antwerp the thirteenth of August seventeen hundred and twenty-three.

Received from D. Mr. Joseph Bergkin the sum of two hundred fifty guilden in exchange money for the first payment on 8 October 1723.

He paid as above the 2nd payment 8 December 1723.

He paid as above the half of the 3rd payment 13 December 1724.

He paid as above the remaining half of the third payment 2 November 1725.

**Notable markings:**

- Coat of arms (double-headed eagle with Imperial insignia) at top center
- Reference number "O. 1198" (upper left)
- Multiple signatures in cursive script (right margin):
  - Corveyaard
  - Jacomo de Poli
  - Peroletti
  - B. Bogells Lame (appears multiple times on payment endorsements)
- Handwritten payment endorsements with dates: October 8, 1723; December 8, 1723; December 13, 1724; November 2, 1725
- Aged, cream-colored parchment or paper with visible wear and foxing

title: Ostend Company Engagement Contract for the China Trade
description: A French-language engagement contract (No. 7) by which the Directors of the Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie, the Ostend Company, retain Sieur Jean de Jonghe to conduct the Company's trade to China ("pour faire le Commerce à la Chine"). For all emoluments ("pour tous appointemens") he is granted twelve hundred florins (Mil deux Cens florins), with the customary right to carry goods back on his own account under stated conditions. It was drawn at Antwerp on 31 January 1726, sealed with the Company's arms and subscribed by directors including Jacobus Maelcamp, Louis Bernaert, and Pietro Proli, countersigned "Par ordonnance de Messieurs les Directeurs, Louis Couturier," with a later endorsement of April 1729. The sheet documents the personnel and incentive structure of the short-lived Habsburg-chartered company (1722–1731) that briefly contested Dutch and English dominance of the East India trade from the Austrian Netherlands.
creator: Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie
notes: French-language Ostend Company payment receipt, 300 florins, Antwerp January 24, 1726. See also goetzmann1029 (Dutch-language receipt, 1723).
transcription:
# Engagement Contract of the Ostend Company Retaining Jean de Jonghe as Fifth Merchant for the China Trade — Transcription

## Original:

(top-left corner, above the engraved arms) **N° 7** (an earlier number struck through before it)

(engraved oval vignette at top left: the crowned double-headed Imperial eagle above an escutcheon with crossed anchors, flanked by two furled flags and columns — the arms of the Generale Keyzerlyke Indische Compagnie / Ostend Company)

**Les Directeurs** de la Compagnie G[énéra]le
des Indes, déclarent avoir engagé au service de ladite Compag[nie]
Le S[ieu]r Jean de Jonghe en qualité de Cinquième marchand,
pour faire le Commerce à la Chine ensuite de nos Instructions,
luy accordans pour tous appointemens la somme de Mil deux
Cens florins argent de Change pour le Voyage, es le privilege
de pouvoir rapporter avec lui trois Caisses de la Grandeur
ordinaire des Caisses de Thé Bouÿ pour ses hardes en
Bagages; telle est la convention faite avec lui, es à laquelle
il s'engage par son [Reversael ?].

(left-margin insertion, keyed with a "à" caret, running down the left edge beside the two preceding lines) bien entendu que les-
dites Caisses soient
de la Grandeur de
pouvoir contenir
une pièce de pequine
en Carrée —

Fait au Bureau Général de la Compagnie
à Anvers le 31 Janvier 1726.

(signature block)

(left column, Dutch note above the first signature) Betaelt de gaege als hier
boven vermelt

(signature) Louis Bernaert

(lower-left, Dutch endorsement of receipt, partly struck through) Ontfangen den [inhoud ?] deser
[…] den [… ?] April 1729

(signature) Joan de Jongh[e] Jun[io]r

(right column, director signatures, from top) Jacobus de Maelcamp

Paulo de Kerchove

Pietro Proli

(large flourished signature, illegible)

(lower centre-right, countersignature) Par ordonnance de Messieurs
les Directeurs

(signature) Louis Couturier

(docket, bottom-left corner) Le S[ieu]r Jean de Jonghe ———

---

## English Translation:

(top-left) **No. 7**

(engraved arms of the Imperial / Ostend Company)

**The Directors** of the General Company of the Indies declare that they have engaged in the service of the said Company the Sieur Jean de Jonghe in the capacity of Fifth Merchant, to carry on the trade to China in accordance with our Instructions, granting him for all emoluments the sum of One Thousand Two Hundred florins in money of exchange for the Voyage, together with the privilege of being able to bring back with him three Chests of the ordinary size of chests of Bohea tea for his clothes and baggage; such is the agreement made with him, to which he binds himself by his [reversal-bond ?].

(margin insertion) it being well understood that the said Chests shall be of a size able to contain one piece of pekin [silk] folded square —

Done at the General Bureau of the Company at Antwerp, the 31st of January 1726.

Paulo de Kerchove
