title: Monte di Pietà di Firenze Bond Certificate
description: A printed and manuscript-completed certificate of assignment (assegnazione di luoghi) issued by the Officials, Protectors and Administrators of the Monte di Pietà of the city of Florence, the standing "Monte non vacabile" established by the ordinance of the Senate of the Forty-Eight published 23 April 1616. The form assigns shares (luoghi) in the Monte's capital to a named holder; each luogo is valued at 100 scudi fiorentini, reckoned at seven lire per scudo, and yields frutti of five scudi yearly, a fixed 5 percent return. The holder is entered as creditor and owner in the Monte's register, the income payable in six-monthly instalments. The manuscript date reads 11 January 1627. Such Monte shares let the Tuscan state convert charitable-pawn capital into a funded perpetual debt, offering Florentine investors a secure, transferable rente. The sheet is headed by the engraved Medici grand-ducal arms flanked by classicizing nude figures, and bears a wax seal and manuscript signatures.
creator: Monte di Pietà della Città di Firenze
notes: Second Monte di Pietà bond of same printed type as goetzmann1027; different assignee. Interest 5 scudi per luogo per year.
transcription:
# Assignment of Luoghi in the Monte di Pietà of Florence (Monte non vacabile) — Transcription

## Original (Italian):

*(Engraved heading: Medici grand-ducal arms surmounted by a crown, flanked by two classicizing nude figures. Top-right corner, manuscript:)* {1627. 11. Genn.o}

**NOI** Offiziali del Monte di Pietà della Città di Firenze, Protettori, & Amministratori del Monte non vacabile eretto d'ordine del S. G. D. di Toscana nostro Sig. per la Prouisione publicata nell'Amplissimo Senato de' 48. il dì 23. di Aprile 1616. cediamo, & assegniamo à {Il M.to Ill.e Sig. Marchese Fab. [illegible surname]} Luoghi {quattro e mezzo} di detto Monte di valuta di scudi cento per ogni luogo di lire sette per scudo moneta Fiorentina con i frutti a ragione di scudi cinque l'anno simili per ogni luogo da hoggi in auuenire. Il prezzo de' quali luoghi di detto Monte ci è stato pagato di contanti in mano al nostro Camarlingo generale in scudi di {quattrocento cinquanta} come à Entrata D a {300}. E noi l'habbiamo fatto descrivere creditore, e padrone di sudetti luoghi al libro di detto Monte eretto per detta Prouisione segnato D de' Libri à per pagarli i frutti di sei mesi in sei mesi la rata porzione, e con i priuilegij, condizioni, e dichiarazioni di che in detta Prouisione, e non altrimenti, nè in altro modo. Et in virtù della presente ordiniamo al Camarlingo generale del nostro Monte, che per i tempi sarà, che paghi detti frutti di sei mesi in sei mesi, & in fede habbiamo fatta fare la presente, quale sarà sottoscritta da vno de' nostri Offiziali, e dal nostro Prouedutore, e Cancelliere, e sigillata con il solito sigillo di detto Monte.

**Data** in Firenze nell'Offizio di detto Monte di Pietà il {11 di Genn.o 1627}.

*(Wax seal impression at lower left. Two manuscript signatures at lower right: the first, an Official — [illegible]; the second, beneath it,* {Michele Bona[illegible]} *,* {Canc.} *[Cancelliere].)*

---

## English Translation:

*(Manuscript, top-right: 1627, 11 January.)*

**WE,** Officials of the Monte di Pietà of the City of Florence, Protectors and Administrators of the perpetual Monte (Monte non vacabile) established by order of His Grand-Ducal Highness of Tuscany our Lord, by the Provision published in the Most Ample Senate of the Forty-Eight on the 23rd day of April 1616, do cede and assign to {the Most Illustrious Lord Marquis Fab. [illegible surname]} {four and a half} luoghi (shares) of the said Monte, of the value of one hundred scudi for each luogo, at seven lire per scudo in Florentine money, with the income at the rate of five like scudi per year for each luogo, from today onward. The price of which luoghi of the said Monte has been paid to us in cash into the hands of our general Camerlingo (treasurer) in the sum of {four hundred and fifty} scudi, as per Entrata (receipts book) D, at {300}. And we have caused him to be registered as creditor and owner of the said luoghi in the book of the said Monte set up for the said Provision, marked D of the Books, so as to be paid the income every six months in the due proportion, and with the privileges, conditions, and declarations set out in the said Provision, and not otherwise nor in any other manner. And by virtue of the present [document] we order the general Camerlingo of our Monte, whoever shall hold office for the time being, to pay the said income every six months; and in witness whereof we have caused the present to be drawn up, which shall be subscribed by one of our Officials and by our Provveditore and Chancellor, and sealed with the customary seal of the said Monte.

**Given** in Florence, in the Office of the said Monte di Pietà, on the {11th of January 1627}.

*(Wax seal, lower left. Two manuscript signatures lower right: an Official [illegible]; and beneath, {Michele Bona[illegible]}, {Chancellor}.)*

title: North American Land Company Share Certificate
description: An engraved share certificate of the North American Land Company, certifying that James Greenleaf is entitled to ten shares (numbered 141 to 150) in the entire property of the Company, with a guaranteed dividend of not less than Six Dollars on each share annually, conformable to Articles of Agreement executed at Philadelphia on the twentieth day of February 1795. Signed at Philadelphia on the tenth day of March 1795 by James Marshall as Secretary and Robert Morris as President, with shares transferable only in person or by legal representative at the Company's Philadelphia office. The instrument captures one of the earliest large land-syndication schemes in the young republic: Robert Morris, financier of the Revolution, and his associates consolidated millions of acres of unimproved western land into a single joint enterprise, promising fixed dividends to attract European capital. The promise would prove illusory, for the venture's collapse drove Morris into debtors' prison. The certificate's restrained copperplate engraving and unadorned text reflect a commercial-document tradition prizing legibility and the authority of signature over ornament.
creator: North American Land Company
notes: No. 15, 10 shares, min. $6/share annual dividend. Signed by Robert Morris (President) and James Marshall (Secretary). Morris was a principal financier of the American Revolution.
transcription:
# Transcription

No. 15 representing Witnesses
from 1st to 130.

This is to Certify, that James Greenleaf
is entitled to ten __________ Shares in the entire Property
of the NORTH AMERICAN LAND COMPANY, the Dividend whereof shall not
be less than Six Dollars, on each Share Annually, conformable to
Articles of Agreement Duly Executed, dated at PHILADELPHIA
the twentieth __ day of February 1795, Transferable only at the
Company's Office in that CITY, by the Owner in Person, or by his Executor,
Administrator, Attorney, or legal Representative, Signed in the presence,
and by Order of the Board of Managers, at Philadelphia, this tenth
day of March— One thousand seven hundred and ninety five

James Marshall Secretary B. Morris President—

---

## Notable markings:

- **Signatures**: James Marshall (Secretary), B. Morris/Robert Morris (President)
- **Serial number**: No. 15
- **Date**: March 10, 1795
- **Location**: Philadelphia
- **Company name**: NORTH AMERICAN LAND COMPANY (prominently displayed in decorative script)
- **Decorative header**: Ornate calligraphic flourishes at the top of the document
- **Handwritten entries**: Owner name (James Greenleaf), number of shares (ten), and specific dates filled in manuscript
- **Margin annotation**: Upper right corner shows "No 15 representing Witnesses / from 1st to 130"

title: Compagnie des Indes Life-Annuity Contract, Paris
description: A notarial contract of life annuity (constitution de rente viagère) passed before the King's Notaries at the Châtelet de Paris on 13 January 1726, by which the Syndic and Directors of the Compagnie des Indes constitute a yearly rente viagère of 920 livres in favour of Louis Despioch, conseiller du Roy and trésorier de France at the bureau des finances of the généralité of Montpellier, payable on his head and, after his death, on that of his daughter Antoinette Despioch. The Company binds itself to pay the rente in hard coin (espèces sonnantes), in two terms each year, at an open bureau, against the annuitants' acquittances and proof of life. The act invokes the Arrêt du Conseil d'État of 15 February 1724, which granted the Company the privilege of operating lotteries, and a deliberation of 13 December forming a tontine of twenty-eight thousand numbers whose proceeds, payable in cash and in life annuities, funded such contracts. The document captures the Compagnie des Indes—the reorganised vehicle of John Law's collapsed System—turning to tontine and lottery finance to raise capital from Crown subjects and foreigners alike in the aftermath of the bubble. The engraved fiscal stamps of the généralité of Paris head the sheet; the hand is otherwise plain chancery script without pictorial ornament.
notes: Company constitution/statutes notarial act, Paris January 18, 1785. Company name partially illegible; possibly a trading or insurance company. Mentions Bordeaux.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (French):**

Constitution Viguère
19 Janvier 1785

[ORNAMENTAL SEALS/STAMPS - Two decorative heraldic devices visible]

PAR DEVANT les Conseillers du Roy; Notaires, Gardes-notes & Gardes-scel au Châtelet de Paris, soulsignés, furent prefens Etienne D'Hemeville Garnier blxt [?] 8.

Indes, Et Etienne le Cordier D'Olive Syndic de la Compagnie des Directeurs D'Aguecc [unclear] [several handwritten insertions and corrections]

Directeurs de ladite Compagnie; lesquels en vertu de l'Arrest du Conseil d'Estat du Roy du 17 Fevrier 1724, par lequel Sa Majesté a accordé à ladite Compagnie, le Privilege & la liberation du 13. Decembre audit an, prise dans l'Assemblée generale d'Administration de ladite Compagnie, au fujet de la Loterie composée des Loix payables, les trois en Argent, & les autres en Rentes Viagres, qui devoient former un corps de Tontine de Vingt-huit mille [unclear] Ecu chacun à Numero, chacun d'Ecus, lesquelles Rentes Viagres, devoient estre tirees annuellement & inocesamment, lesquelles Rentes Viagres, dont affligées sur le fonds de ladite Compagnie, lesdits Sieurs Syndic & Directeurs pouvoient de constituer un à-profit des Sujets de Sa Majesté, que des Etfrangiers, uniquement des Arrearages & des accroissemens doit estre fait, quand même ils redieroleient hors du Royaume, & meme en tems de guerre, que lesdites Rentes puissent estre retranchées ou fiées pour quelque cause que ce soit, pas meme pour les propres deniees affaires de Sa Majesté conformement à l'Arrest du 20. Juin 1724. ainsi qu'il est plus au long porté [several handwritten additions]

[Lower section with handwritten text:]

M. M. Louis Despioch con

de Roy tresoreir & finances au bureau de l'Infrance à la generalité à Montpelliers pour l'oir par lui à l'esrante o [unclear] apres la lateste et pendant la l'r d'Antoinette Despioch l'Afle qui enjoyunt apres la decesir des 2e Impiees Denestreint à Paris anse d'Richelieu funeire d't Rochon cognement & acceptant

Neuf cents Vingtliure [?]

de Rente Viagere; laquelle Rente lesdits Sieurs Syndic & Directeurs, comparans pour & au nom de ladite Compagnie des Indes, s'pronettent de faire paiier & couronner ce Jonnaaties par chacun an, à Bureau ouvert, des Deniers de ladite Compagnie, lace l Despioch lales Sorrant & len l'Danile la Afle.

Sous Quittances qui ne pourront estre panées que parle devant Notaires à Paris, [unclear], avant les memes Notaires que auront recû les Contracts, lesquels certifieront la vie & l'existence des Rentiers; Et à l'egard des sinistres domiciliez en la Ville de Paris, on se pratique pour les Rentes Viageres dues par Sa Majesté: Et ce en deux termes, l premiere [unclear]

M. Cappe No

**English Translation:**

Constitution in Force
January 19, 1785

[HERALDIC SEALS]

BEFORE the Counselors of the King; Notaries, Keepers of Notes & Keepers of the Seal at the Châtelet of Paris, undersigned, appeared Etienne D'Hemeville Garnier [and others] 8.

The Indies, And Etienne le Cordier D'Olive Syndic of the Company of Directors of [unclear]

Directors of the aforesaid Company; which by virtue of the Order of the Council of State of the King of February 17, 1724, by which His Majesty granted to the aforesaid Company the Privilege & the liberation of December 13th of that year, taken in the General Assembly of Administration of the aforesaid Company, regarding the Lottery composed of Annuities [Rentes] payable, three in Cash, & the others in Life Annuities, which were to form a body of a Tontine of twenty-eight thousand [unclear] Ecu each, each numbered at Ecus, which Life Annuities were to be drawn annually & incessantly, which Annuities, thus charged upon the fund of the aforesaid Company, the said Sieurs Syndic & Directors could constitute an assignment for the benefit of the Subjects of His Majesty, as well as Foreigners, solely from Arrearages & increases thereof, must be established when even they might fall outside the Kingdom, & even in time of war, so that the said Annuities might be curtailed or stopped for whatever cause, not even for the proper needs of the affairs of His Majesty conformably to the Order of June 20, 1724, as it is more fully stated [...]

[Lower handwritten section discusses:]

M. M. Louis Despioch, Treasurer and [Court official] at the Bureau of Finance at the Generality of Montpellier, to hear by him [the terms] after [the decease?] and during the death of Antoinette Despioch the daughter who enjoys it after the decease of the Second [term?] Registered in Paris with Richelieu, furnished by Mr. Rochon, competent and accepting.

Nine hundred Twenty Livres

of Life Annuity; which Annuity the said Sieurs Syndic & Directors, appearing for & in the name of the aforesaid Company of the Indies, promise to pay & crown this [annuity] Jonnaites by each year, at an open Bureau, from the Funds of the aforesaid Company...

Under Receipts which can only be passed before Notaries in Paris [before] the same Notaries that shall have received the Contracts, which shall certify the life & existence of the Annuitants; And regarding the unfortunate domiciled in the City of Paris, as is practiced for the Life Annuities due by His Majesty: And this in two terms, the first [unclear]

M. Cappe No.

---

**Notable Markings:**

- Two ornamental heraldic seals/stamps at top of document (royal coat of arms and company seal)
- Multiple handwritten annotations and insertions throughout the printed text
- Signature of "M. Cappe No" at bottom
- Date: "19 Janvier 1785" [January 19, 1785]
- Reference to "Châtelet de Paris" (royal notarial office)
- Several illegible passages due to age, handwritten overlays, and document condition

title: Middelburg Plantation Mortgage Bond for Essequebo and Demerara
description: This printed and hand-completed bond, struck on a standard form headed "Register MIDDELBURG," records a money obligation in the Zeeland mortgage-lending trade that financed plantation agriculture in the Dutch colonies of Essequebo and Demerary (now Guyana). The undersigned, Kornelis van den Helm Boddaert, styles himself director and qualified agent of the geld-opschieters (money-advancers) on diverse plantations there, whose advances were secured by hypothecation (mortgage) registered at the colonial secretariat. Against a principal of one thousand guilders courant, received here from Mejuffr. Anna Susanna van Citters, he promises repayment with interest at five per cent yearly, the holder ranking with other lenders upon the special mortgages. Dated at Middelburg in January 1794, the instrument exposes the machinery of colonial credit at the close of the Dutch West India trade: a metropolitan investor's capital bound, through a notarised intermediary, to the soil and enslaved labour of distant plantations. The form is austere and typographic, its authority resting on the printed text, the company seal invoked in the body, and the clustered manuscript signatures below rather than on ornament.
notes: Plantation bond No. 502, Register Middelburg, January 1794. Secured on plantations in Essequibo and Demerara (Dutch Guiana). Part of Dutch colonial plantation mortgage system.
transcription:
# Middelburg Plantation Mortgage Bond for Essequebo and Demerary (Register Middelburg, No. 572) — Transcription

## Original:

(Manuscript coupon-renewal notes fill all four corners around a central printed form. Handwritten insertions in the printed body are shown in **bold**.)

(Top-left corner, manuscript:)
Afgegeeven tien p.s Coupons
van 1819 tot 1828.
Middelburg 10 Nov: 1819.
(paraph / signature)

(Top-right corner, manuscript — upper note:)
Afgegeeven 10 p.s Coupons
van 1829 tot 1838
Midd: 4 April 1829
(signature) [illegible]

(Top-right corner, manuscript — lower note:)
[Afgegeeve]n 10 Coupons van 1839 à 1848.
Middelburg 2[8?] Aug: 1839
(paraph)

(Center top, printed mark:) **( I )**   (with an inked flourish/paraph above)

**N.o 572.**

**Register MIDDELBURG.**

**Ik** Ondergeschreeve **KORNELIS VAN DEN HELM BODDAERT**, als Directeur en gequalificeerde van de geld Opschieters op diversche Plantagien in de Colonie van Essequebo en Demerary, volgens de Hypotheeken ter Secretarie aldaar, in behoorlyke Ordre alle ten behoeve van dit fonds gepasseerd, waar van de Copyen Authenticq met 'sCompagnie's Zegel bezegeld, en door den Secretaris der Colonie geteekend onder my zyn berustende, Bekenne in die qualiteit agtervolgens het gedrukte Project en Conditien (waar van den inhoud hier agter is geïnsereert) Ontfangen te hebben

van **Mejuffr. Anna Susanna van Citters**

Eene Somma van **Een duijzend Gulden Cour.t** [= one thousand guilders courant]

Welke somma ik Ondergeschreeve, beloove aan dezelve of deszelfs Regt en Actie verkrygende, te zullen doen Restitueeren na Expiratie van den tyd in het Plan hier agter vermeld, en inmiddels daar van te zullen uitkeeren, den Intrest tot *Vyf* perCento in 't Jaar ingaande primo January 1700 **Een en Zeventig** geduurende alzoo van Jaar tot Jaar, tot de volle en Effectueele betaaling der Hypotheeken, waar van de eerste vervaldag zal zyn, op primo January 1700 **twee en Zeventig** en zoo vervolgens alle jaar, en dat van alles vry-geld zonder eenige kortinge aan Capitaal of Interessen te mogen doen, zullende de Interessen werden betaald ten Comptoire van den Ondergeschreevene, tot nakominge van al het welke de Geld-opneemers boven hunne speciale Hypotheeken hebben verbonden, hunne Perzoonen en goederen, ten bedwang als na Regten.

Ter Oirkonde dezes by my onderteekend
Middelburg den **Eersten** Januarij 17**71**   (signature: *[K.] van den Helm Boddaert*, with large paraph)

(Note: the printed "1700" in the two interest clauses is a blank form-placeholder; the actual years are supplied in manuscript as "Een en Zeventig" (seventy-one → 1771) and "twee en Zeventig" (seventy-two → 1772). The bottom date field is printed "17__" completed in manuscript to "1771".)

(Bottom-left corner, manuscript:)
Afgegeeven 10 p.s Coupons
van A.o: 1799 à 1808 ingesloten
Middelburg 24 Aug: 1800.
F.B. Haringman

(Bottom-right corner, manuscript, beneath the main signature:)
Afgegeeven 10 p.s Coupons
over A.o 1809 à 1818 ingesloten.
Middelburg 27 July 1810.
P.J. Boddaert

---

## English Translation:

(Top-left corner, manuscript:)
Delivered ten p[iece]s Coupons
from 1819 to 1828.
Middelburg, 10 November 1819.
(paraph / signature)

(Top-right corner — upper note:)
Delivered 10 p[iece]s Coupons
from 1829 to 1838
Middelburg, 4 April 1829
(signature) [illegible]

(Top-right corner — lower note:)
[Delivere]d 10 Coupons from 1839 to 1848.
Middelburg, 2[8?] August 1839
(paraph)

(Center top, printed mark:) **( I )**

**No. 572.**

**Register MIDDELBURG.**

I, the undersigned, **KORNELIS VAN DEN HELM BODDAERT**, as Director and qualified agent of the money-advancers on various Plantations in the Colony of Essequebo and Demerary, according to the Mortgages [registered] at the Secretariat there, all duly passed in good order for the benefit of this fund — of which the authentic Copies, sealed with the Company's Seal and signed by the Secretary of the Colony, are held in my keeping — do acknowledge, in that capacity, in accordance with the printed Prospectus and Conditions (the text of which is inserted here below), to have received

from **Miss Anna Susanna van Citters**

A Sum of **One Thousand Guilders Courant**

Which sum I, the undersigned, promise to the same or to whoever acquires her right and claim, to cause to be repaid after the expiration of the term stated in the Plan set out here below, and meanwhile to pay out on it the Interest at *Five* per cent per Year, commencing the first of January 17[**71**], continuing thus from year to year, until the full and effective repayment of the Mortgages, whereof the first due date shall be the first of January 17[**72**], and so on every year thereafter; and all of this as free money, without any deduction being made from Capital or Interest, the Interest to be paid at the Office of the undersigned. For the fulfilment of all which the Money-borrowers have pledged, over and above their special Mortgages, their persons and goods, subject to enforcement as by law.

In witness whereof signed by me,
Middelburg, the **First** of January 17**71**   (signature: *[K.] van den Helm Boddaert*)

(Bottom-left corner, manuscript:)
Delivered 10 p[iece]s Coupons
for the years 1799 to 1808 inclusive
Middelburg, 24 August 1800.
F.B. Haringman

(Bottom-right corner, manuscript, beneath the main signature:)
Delivered 10 p[iece]s Coupons
for the years 1809 to 1818 inclusive.
Middelburg, 27 July 1810.
P.J. Boddaert

title: French Royal 3% Rente (Droit sur les Cuirs)
description: A constitution of rente at 3 percent on the French crown, passed before the King's Notaries at the Châtelet of Paris and dated 3 October 1761, the rentier's capital standing at 10,000 livres. The printed heading ties the annuity to the Édit of May 1760, which created these rentes upon the proceeds of the duty on hides (Droit sur les Cuirs), itself established by an Édit of August 1759, together with the King's other revenues. Its substance lies in the redemption scheme: rather than a true perpetual, the principal is repaid in cash, beginning at 1,200,000 livres the first year and rising as freed arrears accrue toward a yearly fund of three millions, the holders chosen twice a year by lot. The numbered rentes are placed in a wheel in the great hall of the Hôtel de Ville and drawn each January and July before the Prévôt des Marchands and Échevins, the first drawing set for January 1762. The document shows a crown financing the Seven Years' War by pledging a fresh consumption duty and sweetening the loan with a lottery-like sinking fund. Signed by the notary Gervais.
creator: Kingdom of France
notes: No. 46460, 3% French royal rente, Paris October 1761. Derives from 1739 state debt refinancing. Part of pre-revolutionary French government bond market.
transcription:
# French Royal 3% Annuity Bond - Transcription

**Original (French):**

d. Octobre 1761.                                    M. Vaudeuil d'Harteville Sig.
                                                    10000

RENTES À 3 POUR CENT, créées par Édit du mois de Mai 1760, sur les derniers provenans du Droit sur les Cuirs, établi par autre Édit du mois d'Août 1759, & sur les autres Revenus du Roi.
No. 46460

PARDEVANT les Conseillers du Roi, Notaires, Garde-notes & Gardes-scel de Sa Majesté au Châtelet de Paris, soussignés, furent présens M.rs [followed by handwritten names and text which are largely illegible in the cursive script]

Tous Commissaires nommés par Sa Majesté, par les Lettres patentes données à Versailles le pour l'exécution de l'Édit du mois de mai 1760, ci-après énoncé:

Lesquels ont dit que le Roi par son Édit du mois de mai 1760, enregistré le 23 du même mois, a créé dix-huit cents mille livres de rentes effectives & héréditaires à Trois pour cent, à les over & percevoir par privilege & préférence sur les deniers provenans du Droit que Sa Majesté a établi par les Curs tannés & apprêtés, par un Édit d'août 1759, enregistré au Parlement le 11 septembre suivant, & qu'Elle a spécialement & par privilege affectés, obligés & hypothéques, ensemble tous les autres revenus, tant au payement des arrérages desdites rentes qu'au remboursement des capitaux de la manière ci-après énoncée, après néanmoins le prélèvement sur lesdits droits, des sommes définies au payement des arrérages & au remboursement des capitaux les liquidations des Offices supprimés par ledit Édit d'août, conformément à l'article 111 dudit Édit qui feroit exécuté félon la forme & teneur.

Que lesdites rentes feroient vendues & aliénées par lesdits seigneurs Commissaires, sur les quittances du sieur Garde de son Trésor royal, & les contrats de constitution passés en conséquence par tels Notaires que les Acquéreurs voudroient choisir; lesquelles rentes seroient exemptes de la retenue des Vingtiemes, Deux fois livre du Dixième, & de toutes autres impositions généralement quelconques, tous quelconque imposition que ce put être.

Que chaque constitution particulière desdites rentes ne pourroit être moindre de mille livres de capital, pour faire trente livres de rente, dont les arrérages commenceroient à courir au profit des Acquéreurs, au premier jour du quartier courant de la date desdites quitances de finances, & feroient payés de fix en ix mois, aux premiers jours des mois de Janvier & de Juillet de chaque année, par le sieur Garde du Trésor royal en exercice, sur les simples quittances des Rentiers, à commencé du premier Janvier 1761.

Qu'il seroit annuellement fait fonds dans l'État de la régie dudit Droit sur les Curs & autres revenus de Sa Majesté, en que lesdites rentes substitutiroient en tout ou partie, de la somme de trois millions de livres, qui seroit annuellement remise par les Régisseurs ou Fermiers desdits droits, entre les mains des Curs du Trésor royal, chacun à l'année de son exercice, pour être par eux employé au payement des arrérages & au remboursement des capitaux desdites rentes, sans que lesdits fonds puissent être diminués ni retranchés jusqu'à l'entier remboursement desdites rentes, dans préjudice du prélèvement ci-dessus mentionné.

Que les capitaux desdites rentes feroient remboursés en derniers compans, à raison de la somme de deux cents livres par mille livres pour la première année, de pareille somme pour les années suivantes, & par augmentation de la somme à laquelle le trouveroient monter les arrérages des capitaux qui auroient été remboursés chaque année, en sorte qu'il seroit employé nous les ans de fonds jusqu'à parfait remboursement desdites rentes, dans pouvoir distraire aucune partie de ce fonds, sous quelque prétexte que ce fut.

Que les remboursemens feroient faits par le Garde du Trésor royal en exercice, aux premiers jours de Janvier & de Juillet de chaque année, par la voie du sort en forme de Loterie, à commencer du premier Janvier 1762 & ainsi de suite de fix en six mois; à l'effet de quoi les contrats de constitution desdites rentes feroient numérotés, & les numéros mis publiquement dans les derniers jours de Juin & de Décembre de chaque année, dans une roue en la grande Roue de l'Hôtel de ville de Paris, pour, en présence de Messieurs les Prévôt des Marchands & Échevins de la même ville, être tiré

[Signature] M. Garnier Bp.

---

## English Translation:

**October 2, 1761**                                M. Vaudeuil d'Harteville, Sig.
                                                    10000

**3 PERCENT ANNUITIES (RENTES), created by Edict of the month of May 1760, on the latest proceeds of the Tax on Hides, established by another Edict of the month of August 1759, & on the other Revenues of the King.**

No. 46460

BEFORE the Counselors of the King, Notaries, Record-Keepers & Seal-Keepers of His Majesty at the Châtelet of Paris, undersigned, appeared M.rs [followed by handwritten names largely illegible]

All Commissioners appointed by His Majesty, by Letters patent issued at Versailles for the execution of the Edict of the month of May 1760, hereinafter stated:

Who declared that the King, by his Edict of the month of May 1760, registered on the 23rd of the same month, has created eighteen hundred thousand livres of effective & hereditary annuities at Three percent, to be recovered & collected with privilege & preference on the funds deriving from the Tax which His Majesty has established on tanned & dressed hides, by an Edict of August 1759, registered in Parliament on September 11 following, & which He has specially & by privilege assigned, obligated & mortgaged, together with all other revenues, both for the payment of arrears of said annuities and for the reimbursement of capital in the manner hereinafter stated; provided nevertheless that there shall first be deducted from said revenues, the sums defined for the payment of arrears & for the reimbursement of capital for the liquidations of Offices suppressed by the said Edict of August, in conformity with article 111 of the said Edict which shall be executed according to its form & tenor.

That said annuities shall be sold & transferred by said gentlemen Commissioners, upon receipts from the Keeper of His Royal Treasury, & contracts of constitution passed in consequence by such Notaries as the Purchasers shall choose; which annuities shall be exempt from the assessment of the Twentieths, Two-fold book of the Tenth, & from all other general impositions whatsoever, any imposition whatsoever that might be.

That each individual constitution of said annuities shall not be less than one thousand livres of capital, to yield thirty livres of annual income, whose arrears shall commence to accrue for the benefit of Purchasers, on the first day of the quarter current from the date of said financial receipts, & shall be paid every six months, on the first days of the months of January & July of each year, by the Keeper of the Royal Treasury in office, upon simple receipts of the Annuitants, beginning from

title: Russian Imperial Perpetual Income Bond
description: A Russian Imperial perpetual-income certificate (Свидѣтельство о непрерывномъ доходѣ, "непрерывный доходъ по 5 на сто") promising 5 per cent in perpetuity on a principal of 960 silver rubles, rendered also as £148 sterling — a fixed parity of three shillings one penny per silver ruble that announces its intended London market. The capital is recorded in the State Debt Book of the Commission for the Reduction of Debts (Коммиссія погашенія долговъ), the imperial sinking-fund body, and the instrument is dated 1 March 1822 at St Petersburg. It belongs to the celebrated Russian 5 per cent loan of 1822, the first sovereign issue Nicholas-era Russia floated through N. M. Rothschild of London, whose embossed seal appears at lower right; the dense Russian and French conditions on payment and redemption, and the central seal of Justice bearing the tablet LOI, advertise a bond drawn for foreign creditors as much as domestic ones, binding imperial finance to the western capital markets.
creator: Russian State Loan Bank (Государственный Заёмный Банк)
notes: Russian 5% Perpetual Income bond, 960 rubles = £43 Sterling, Class 1, issued March 1, 1822 by Russian State Loan Bank. Bilateral ruble/sterling denomination targets foreign investors.
transcription:
# Russian Imperial 5 % Perpetual-Income Certificate, 1822 — Transcription

## Original:

*(Ornamental heading band, left to right:)*

**ROUBLES 960**    *(centre: Imperial double-headed eagle above the date* **1822 ГОДА** *)*    **£148 Sterling**

*(Printed serial at left of band:)* **№ 69374.**
*(Printed serial at right of band:)* **№ 15172.**   *(handwritten beside it:)* 90

*(Centred heading:)*

**НЕПРЕРЫВНЫЙ ДОХОДЪ ПО 5 НА СТО.**

Свидѣтельство о непрерывномъ доходѣ на капиталъ, внесенный въ Государственную долговую книгу Коммиссіи погашенія долговъ, состоящій въ девяти стахъ шестидесяти рубляхъ серебромъ, равняющійся 148 фунтамъ стерлинговъ.

Записанный 1го Марта 1822.

Книга *1.* стран. *204.* 2 займа Лит. *С.*   *(the book-number, page-number and series letter are handwritten fill-ins)*

Предъявитель сего Свидѣтельства имѣетъ право на непрерывный доходъ, заключающійся въ сорока восьми рубляхъ серебромъ, платимый чрезъ каждые полгода по его произволу въ С. Петербургѣ или Лондонѣ, а именно: двадцать четыре рубля серебромъ 1го числа Марта и двадцать четыре рубля серебромъ 1го числа Сентября; если въ С. Петербургѣ, по серебрянымъ рублямъ настоящаго вѣса и пробы, если же въ Лондонѣ, по три шиллинга и одинъ пенсъ стерлинговъ за каждый серебряный рубль: въ томъ и другомъ случаѣ по предъявленіи купона на причитающійся къ платежу непрерывный доходъ.

Предъявитель сего Свидѣтельства можетъ, представивъ оное въ Государственную Коммиссію погашенія долговъ, требовать, чтобы вмѣсто онаго выданъ былъ безсрочный билетъ на туже сумму на его имя, или на имя лица, или лицъ, которыя онъ назначитъ: въ такомъ случаѣ непрерывный доходъ имѣетъ быть платимъ токмо въ С. Петербургѣ въ вышепостановленные сроки, и трансфертъ или передача таковыхъ билетовъ должна быть производима по существующимъ правиламъ.

Двадцать четыре купона принадлежатъ къ сему Свидѣтельству. Если при наступленіи срока послѣднему изъ нихъ, капиталъ не будетъ выкупленъ, или обращенъ въ безсрочный билетъ Коммиссіи погашенія долговъ; то имѣютъ быть выданы вновь двадцать четыре купона, и такъ далѣе, дабы обезпечить предъявителю полученіе непрерывнаго дохода въ С. Петербургѣ или Лондонѣ.

*Прим.* Особенный фондъ, или капиталъ, составляющій (ежегодно) одинъ процентъ со всей суммы сего займа, назначенъ на выкупъ его.

**Выписка изъ Устава Коммиссіи:**

§. 22. Безсрочный непрерывный доходъ, равно какъ и платежъ срочныхъ долговъ, производится и въ мирное и военное время безъ всякаго различія, къ дружественной ли или непріязненной націи принадлежитъ вкладчикъ.

§. 23. Въ случаѣ смерти иностраннаго вкладчика безъ завѣщанія, безсрочный долгъ имѣетъ принадлежать его наслѣдникамъ по порядку и правамъ наслѣдія той націи, къ которой онъ принадлежалъ.

§. 24. Капиталы, въ безсрочный долгъ обращенные, составляя ненарушимую собственность вкладчика, не подвергаются секвестру ни по казеннымъ, ни по частнымъ искамъ; изключая, если весь или часть капитала предъставлена въ залогъ по подрядамъ, или другимъ сдѣлкамъ съ казною или частными людьми, или въ обезпеченіе иска; въ такомъ случаѣ оный подлежитъ общимъ правиламъ о залогахъ и взысканіяхъ. Равнымъ образомъ таковые капиталы ни въ какомъ случаѣ не подлежатъ никакимъ налогамъ.

§. 25. Никто не можетъ быть противъ его желанія принужденъ къ обратному полученію всего или части капитала, въ безсрочномъ долгѣ помѣщеннаго. Но дабы съ другой стороны предоставить каждому болѣе удобности обратить оный, когда пожелаетъ, въ наличныя деньги, Коммиссія имѣетъ употреблять на выкупъ оныхъ по курсу особенный капиталъ погашенія, ежегодно сверхъ платежа непрерывныхъ доходовъ на предметъ сей назначаемый.

За Управляющаго *[handwritten signature — illegible]*

Бухгалтеръ *[handwritten signature — illegible]*

*(Vertical printed labels in the margins repeat the register data: left margin* Непрерывный доходъ*; right margin* Капиталъ … равняющійся 148 фунтамъ стерлинговъ*.)*

*(Applied stamps and later annotations:)*
- Top left, black registration stamp: **BUREAU DE [illegible] / (ACTES JUDICIAIRES)**
- Top left, red German revenue stamp: **REICHS-STEMPEL [ELSASS-…] · 10 ZEHN 10 · Pf**
- Top right: several lines of French handwriting *[illegible cursive annotations, apparently receipts in francs]*
- Left margin, blue oval stamp: **CONSULAT DE RUSSIE À HAMBOURG**, with handwritten **№ 76**
- Right side, blue framed stamp: **Les coupons jusqu'[au …] ont été détachés au Consulat gén[éral …]** *[partly illegible]*
- Right side, black oval seal: **COUPONS DELIVERED IN LONDON · 1834**
- Centre, French fiscal stamp for foreign securities (allegory of Law holding tablet): **LOI / 1 F 50 POUR MILLE / TITRES ÉTRANGERS AU-DESSUS DE 1000 [F]**
- Lower right: embossed (blind) N. M. Rothschild seal

---

## English Translation:

*(Heading band:)* **ROUBLES 960** — (centre: Imperial double eagle, above the date **1822**) — **£148 Sterling**

*(Serial numbers:)* No. 69374 / No. 15172 (with handwritten "90")

**PERPETUAL INCOME AT 5 PER HUNDRED.**

Certificate of a perpetual income upon capital entered in the State Debt Book of the Commission for the Redemption of Debts, consisting of nine hundred and sixty silver rubles, equal to 148 pounds sterling.

Registered 1 March 1822.

Book *1*, page *204*, 2nd loan, series *С.* *(handwritten entries)*

The bearer of this Certificate is entitled to a perpetual income amounting to forty-eight silver rubles, payable every half-year at his choice in St Petersburg or London, namely: twenty-four silver rubles on the 1st of March and twenty-four silver rubles on the 1st of September; if in St Petersburg, in silver rubles of the present weight and standard, but if in London, at three shillings and one penny sterling for each silver ruble: in either case upon presentation of the coupon for the income falling due.

The bearer of this Certificate may, by presenting it to the State Commission for the Redemption of Debts, demand that in its stead a perpetual (registered) bond be issued for the same sum, in his name or in the name of the person or persons he designates: in that case the perpetual income shall be payable only in St Petersburg at the aforesaid dates, and the transfer or conveyance of such bonds shall be effected according to the existing regulations.

Twenty-four coupons belong to this Certificate. If, when the last of them falls due, the capital has not been redeemed or converted into a registered bond of the Commission for the Redemption of Debts, then twenty-four new coupons shall be issued, and so on, so as to secure to the bearer the receipt of the perpetual income in St Petersburg or London.

*Note.* A special fund, or capital, constituting (annually) one per cent of the whole sum of this loan, is appropriated to its redemption.

**Extract from the Statute of the Commission:**

§ 22. The perpetual income, as well as the payment of term debts, is carried out in both peacetime and wartime without any distinction, whether the depositor belongs to a friendly or a hostile nation.

§ 23. In the event of the death of a foreign depositor without a will, the perpetual debt shall belong to his heirs according to the order and laws of inheritance of the nation to which he belonged.

§ 24. Capitals converted into perpetual debt, constituting the inviolable property of the depositor, are not subject to sequestration for either state or private claims; except where the whole or part of the capital has been given as security for contracts or other transactions with the treasury or with private persons, or as surety for a suit; in that case it is subject to the general rules on pledges and levies. Likewise such capitals are in no case liable to any taxes.

§ 25. No one may be compelled against his will to take back the whole or part of the capital placed in perpetual debt. But so that, on the other hand, each may have greater convenience to convert it, whenever he wishes, into ready money, the Commission shall employ for their redemption, at the going rate, a special redemption capital, appropriated annually for this purpose over and above the payment of the perpetual incomes.

For the Manager *[signature — illegible]*

Accountant *[signature — illegible]*

*(Later stamps and endorsements:)* French registration stamp "BUREAU DE [illegible] / (JUDICIAL ACTS)"; German revenue stamp of Alsace(-Lorraine) "IMPERIAL STAMP … 10 (TEN) Pf"; French handwritten receipts [illegible]; blue oval "CONSULATE OF RUSSIA AT HAMBURG", handwritten No. 76; blue framed "The coupons up to [date] have been detached at the Consulate General [illegible]"; black oval seal "COUPONS DELIVERED IN LONDON 1834"; central French stamp-duty seal for foreign securities "LAW / 1 fr. 50 per thousand / FOREIGN SECURITIES ABOVE 1000 [fr.]"; embossed N. M. Rothschild seal at lower right.

title: Republic of Texas Texian Loan Bond
description: This is a subscription certificate for the Texian Loan, acknowledging receipt of thirty-two dollars as the first instalment upon a loan of three hundred and twenty dollars made to the Government of Texas for a term of five years, bearing interest at eight per centum per annum, payable annually. It is dated at the City of New-Orleans, the eleventh day of January 1836, and subscribed by the three Texas commissioners sent to solicit funds in the United States: Stephen F. Austin, Branch T. Archer, and William H. Wharton. Issued to finance the revolt against Mexico, the instrument doubles as a land warrant, promising that sums paid may be redeemed for Texas land at fifty cents per acre. The vignette of stacked arms and the marginal device proclaiming a 'Certificate of Texas' for 'Six Hundred and Forty Acres of Land' bind credit to conquest, offering the unconquered soil itself as the security of a government not yet recognized.
creator: Provisional Government of Texas
notes: No. 43, $320, 5-year term, 8% interest. Signed by Texas Commissioners authorized November 1835 to seek funds for the Texas Revolution.
transcription:
# TEXIAN LOAN

**TEXIAN GOVERNMENT AND GOVERNMENT LOAN**

**CERTIFICATE No. 43**

Received, of the General Thirty-two Dollars, the First Installment on a Loan of Three Hundred and Twenty Dollars, made by him this day to the Government of Texas for FIVE YEARS, bearing Interest at the rate of EIGHT PER CENTUM PER ANNUM, payable annually. Public Notice to be given, according to contract, when the other Instalments will be required, at which time they may be paid, or not, at the option of the said

For the amount paid, Land in Texas may be taken at FIFTY CENTS per Acre, with the guarantees and conditions of the contract aforesaid.

In Testimony whereof, we have hereunto set our hands, and affixed our seals, in the City of NEW-ORLEANS, this Eleventh day of January, one thousand eight hundred and thirty-six.

---

## Notable markings:

- **Three signatures:**
  - J. T. Austin
  - B. T. Archer
  - Wm H. Wharton

- **Serial Number:** No. 43
- **Date:** January 11, 1836
- **Location:** New Orleans
- **Decorative border:** Ornamental frame with floral and geometric designs
- **Printer attribution:** "Printed by BENJAMIN LEVY—NEW ORLEANS" (at bottom)
- **Heraldic seal/emblem:** Central eagle design with flourishes

title: Continental Bill of Exchange
description: This engraved bill of exchange, drawn on the 25th day of November 1778, obliges the Commissioners of the United States of America at Paris to pay thirty Spanish-milled dollars — reckoned at five livres tournois to the dollar, or one hundred and fifty livres tournois — to Moses Frazier or order, at thirty days' sight. It is the First of a set of four (First, Second, Third, Fourth), the duplicate form that guarded a remittance against loss at sea. The bill answers interest "due on Money borrowed by the United States," countersigned by Nathaniel Appleton at the Continental Loan-Office for Massachusetts Bay and signed by Francis Hopkinson, Treasurer of Loans. It is an artifact of the infant Republic's wartime finance: lacking hard coin at home, Congress drew on its agents in France — credit secured by the Franco-American alliance — to service the loan-office certificates by which patriots had lent their specie to the cause. The plain engraved frame, free of allegory, befits a workaday fiscal instrument rather than a public proclamation.
creator: Continental Congress / Continental Loan Office
notes: No. 85, $30 = 150 Livres Tournois. Interest on US war debt. Signed by Francis Hopkinson (Treasurer of Loans) and Nathaniel Appleton (Massachusetts Loan Commissioner). Payable in Paris.
transcription:
# Continental Bill of Exchange No. 85 Transcription

**Printed for 30 Dollars at five Sums, Journous Dollar, Numb. 85**

United States of AMERICA, 25. Day of November 1778

AT Thirty Days Sight of this First Bill, Second, Third and Fourth not paid, pay to Mr. Moses Frazier or Order, Thirty Dollars, in One Hundred and Fifty Livres Tournois, for Interest due on Money borrowed by the United States.

To the Commissioner or Commissioners of the
United States of America, at Paris.

Countersigned, Nathaniel Appleton
Commissioner of the Continental Loan-Office in the
State of Massachusetts Bay

S. Hopkinson
(Treas. of Loans.)

---

**Notable markings:**

- Serial number: **85** (appears in top right)
- Signature: **S. Hopkinson** (Francis Hopkinson, Treasurer of Loans)
- Countersignature: **Nathaniel Appleton** (Commissioner of Continental Loan-Office, Massachusetts Bay)
- Ornamental borders and decorative elements on left margin
- Handwritten annotations and stamps on left side [partially illegible]
- Standard format indicating this is the "First" of four bills issued simultaneously
